facts: In 2023, more than 82,000 Georgia families saved more than $148 million on clean energy and energy efficiency investments.

The U.S. Department of the Treasury released new data from the IRS and analysis by the Office of Economic Policy showing that more than 82,000 Georgia families benefitted from more than $148 million in tax credits to lower the costs of clean energy and energy efficiency upgrades to their homes during 2023.
Today’s announcement marks the first public release of data from 2023 tax filings showing the benefit of the Inflation Reduction Act’s clean energy tax incentives for consumers.

Nationwide, the number of families benefiting from expanded credits for investments that lower their utility bills increased almost one third compared to tax year 2021, prior to passage of the Inflation Reduction Act.
The aggregate value of the credits increased by almost two thirds compared to tax year 2021.

Recent research from U.S. national labs and analysis from Treasury’s Office of Economic Policy shows that after consumers adopt these upgrades, they can save hundreds or thousands of dollars per year on their utility bills, depending on the upgrades made to their homes.
Households installing residential solar have saved a median of $2,230 annually.

Households that install efficient heat pumps and improve building efficiency are expected to save up to $600, $1,200, or $3,100 per year, based on the type of heating and cooling system that is being replaced.

U.S. Secretary of the Treasury Janet L. Yellen stated the Inflation Reduction Act is advancing the goal of lowering costs for American families by making home energy upgrades more affordable and cutting monthly utility bills.
Senator Reverend Warnock stated that investments in clean energy will help Georgia families save money and that he fought to pass the IRA to ensure historic tax credits for Georgia.

Representative Nikema Williams (GA-05) stated the IRA is the largest ever federal investment to combat the climate crisis and is already paying dividends for Georgians by lowering costs for hardworking families.
Georgia families have claimed more than $97 million in credits for residential clean energy investments on 2023 tax returns filed and processed through May 23, 2024.
The residential clean energy credit helps families afford investments in solar electricity generation, solar water heating, and battery storage, among others.
Families have also claimed more than $51 million for energy efficient home improvements—which include heat pumps, efficient air conditioners, insulation, windows, and doors—during the same time span.
These estimates are expected to increase as additional returns are filed and processed.
Nationwide, more than 750,000 families claimed investments in residential solar electricity.
Nationwide, more than 250,000 families claimed investments in electric or natural gas heat pumps.
Nationwide, more than 100,000 families claimed investments in heat pump water heaters.
Nationwide, nearly 700,000 families claimed investments in insulation and air sealing.
The Inflation Reduction Act provides incentives for consumers to lower their energy bills and shield themselves from future spikes in fossil energy prices.

The IRA extended and expanded the Energy Efficient Home Improvement Credit, providing a total credit of up to $1,200 annually for investments like installing insulation or efficient windows and doors.
There is a special credit of as much as $2,000 for electric heat pumps that provide super-efficient heating and cooling.
The IRA provides a $150 credit for a home energy audit conducted by an inspector to help determine the best home improvement options.

The IRA extended the Residential Clean Energy Credit, ensuring that households will be able to continue receiving a tax credit to cover up to 30% of the costs of installing, among others, rooftop solar and battery storage through 2034.
source_title: U.S. Department of the Treasury Releases New Data on Georgia Consumer Energy Savings Under Inflation Reduction Act | U.S. Department of the Treasury

entity_name: NSF Program Solicitation 23-530 (DMREF) and DOE EERE Teaming Partner List
canonical_name: Designing Materials to Revolutionize and Engineer our Future (DMREF) Program Solicitation 23-530
facts: The announcement serves as a Teaming Partner List to help prospective applicants for NSF Program Solicitation 23-530 identify potential DOE National Laboratory partners within DOE-EERE, including the Energy Materials Network consortia.
Applicants can select "Cross-Cutting" for the Area of Expertise if no specific option fits their needs.
The Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP) Subcommittee on the Materials Genome Initiative (MGI) is a federal partnership between NSF and DOE.
The MGI vision aims to enable discovery, development, manufacturing, and deployment of advanced materials at least twice as fast at a fraction of the cost compared to traditional methods.
DOE's EERE is building a clean energy economy that encompasses energy efficiency, renewable energy, and sustainable transportation.
EERE's primary interface with DMREF is through the Energy Materials Network (EMN), which accelerates solutions to materials challenges in the energy sector.
Teams partnering with this option must agree to participate in technical review meetings, and funds may be budgeted for this purpose.
"EERE" must be included as a Key Word in the Project Summary.
DMREF is the principal NSF program responsive to the MGI.
Over its inaugural decade, the MGI has driven a transformational paradigm shift in materials research philosophy.

DMREF supports the 2021 MGI Strategic Plan goals: unifying the materials innovation infrastructure; harnessing the power of materials data; and educating, training, and connecting a world-class materials R&D workforce.
The DMREF solicitation is open to Institutions of Higher Education.

This solicitation enables DMREF teams to collaborate with MGI-related efforts to bolster scientific/engineering aspects of the iterative feedback loop for accelerated materials research, facilitate translation of fundamental research toward application, and provide education, training, and workforce development opportunities.
Federal partners must not be listed on the Cover Page of proposals.
Letters of Collaboration are not required from DMREF's federal partners, but the collaborating PI must be identified in the Project Description to explain how collaboration benefits both parties.
Proposals collaborating with personnel from DMREF's federal partners like EERE may budget funds for student support or travel to enable these activities.
No funds may be budgeted or sub-awarded to federal partners, as these personnel will participate as unfunded collaborators.
Proposals partnering with EERE may budget funds specifically for travel to technical review meetings.
EERE and DMREF share common interests through the EMN in developing materials, processes, and advanced manufacturing technologies for the energy sector.

Specific topics of interest to both EERE and DMREF include catalysis; materials for extreme environments; accelerated materials design and discovery by artificial intelligence and autonomous experimentation; multiscale modeling; photovoltaic materials; and lightweight and durable materials.
The Teaming Partner List allows EERE partners, including the EMN, to network with prospective DMREF applicants.
Proposals are due March 13, 2023, closing by 5 PM submitter's local time.
source_title: DOE: CMEI eXCHANGE: Funding Opportunities

facts: Between July 1, 2024, and June 30, 2025 (Fiscal Year 2025), GDEcD’s Global Commerce team supported 423 facility expansions and new locations.
These FY25 commitments represent more than $26.3 billion in investment, a new record for the state.
The projects will create 23,200 new private-sector jobs over the next few years.
Expansions at existing Georgia companies accounted for 74% of FY25 projects, building on an estimated 50,000 jobs already employing Georgians.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Georgia has been the No. 1 state for business for over a decade due to its unmatched ability to attract new companies and support existing employers.

Lt. Governor Burt Jones highlighted ongoing investments in workforce programs with the University System of Georgia, the Technical College System of Georgia, and College Career Academies to ensure a steady talent pipeline for current and future industry partners.
77% of all FY25 expansions and new locations occurred in communities outside the 10-county Atlanta region.

Metro Atlanta remains one of the Southeast’s top business hubs, with companies like TriNet, A.I.G., CRH, Duracell, and Mercedes-Benz establishing or expanding key corporate functions in the 10-county region.
Georgia attracted more than 6,500 new jobs from international companies expanding or locating in the U.S., with the Republic of Korea, Japan, and Canada as top investment sources.
International companies committed to more than $3 billion in investment, with 64% slated for expansions within the state.
Notable international investments include Irving Tissue’s expansion in Macon, GreenBox Systems’ new location in Butts County, and KISS USA’s expansion in Bryan County.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that Georgia’s stable, reliable business climate gives companies confidence to plan for the long term.
Georgia is investing over $18 million to help rural communities prepare new industrial sites through the Rural Site Development Initiative.
These grants support site improvements and add locations to the “Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development” (GRAD) Program, which now includes more than 70 certified sites.

Deputy Commissioner of Global Commerce Misti Martin stated that programs like the Rural Site Development Initiative and site-readiness certification help communities attract new jobs by offering speed-to-market solutions.
Georgia remains the No. 1 state for business according to Area Development magazine, a title held for over a decade.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Economic Development Investments Break New Record | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: Wire 3 announced plans to bring its symmetrical, high-speed network to Warner Robins and Centerville.

Through a fully funded $38 million investment, the company’s expansion will deliver faster, more reliable internet and future-ready connectivity to nearly 38,000 households and businesses across Houston County.
Construction is slated to begin in the coming weeks.
Wire 3 anticipates connecting its first customers in the area as early as this fall.
Jai Ramachandran, Wire 3 CEO, stated that the response in Georgia has been tremendous and expressed excitement about growing just south into Warner Robins and Centerville.
The company is rapidly expanding across its second state with a $60 million Macon expansion and a $37 million Albany expansion both currently under construction.
Delivering speeds up to 10 gigabits per second, ten times faster than traditional cable, Wire 3 continues expanding its 100% fiber network across the Southeast.
Based in Daytona Beach, Florida, Wire 3 provides symmetrical, multigigabit speeds scaling up to 10-Gig with free Wi-Fi equipment and flexible no-contract plans.
Founded in 2021, Wire 3 aims to create 10-Gig cities across Florida and soon Georgia and South Carolina.

links.context: Pre-sale and construction updates / learn more about offerings

source_title: Wire 3 Accelerates Georgia Expansion With $38M Warner Robins and Centerville Investment

entity_name: Toyota Industries Electric Systems North America, Inc.
canonical_name: Toyota Industries Electric Systems North America, Inc.
facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Toyota Industries Electric Systems North America, Inc., (TIESNA) will create more than 250 new jobs while expanding the Toyota Industries Corporation campus in Pendergrass with a new manufacturing facility valued at $69 million in investment.
TIESNA broke ground at the site on November 14, 2023.
Japanese Consul General Mio Maeda and local representatives joined the announcement.
Governor Brian Kemp stated Georgia has distinguished itself as a leader in the automotive industry and thanked Toyota for choosing to expand there.
TIESNA is a subsidiary of Toyota Industries Corporation, which is headquartered in Japan.
Toyota Industries develops and manufactures automobile-related products, including car air-conditioning compressors and car electronics components for worldwide carmakers.
The Toyota Industries Group has operated in Pendergrass since 2004.
The group initially started with TD Automotive Compressor Georgia, LLC (TACG).
The group added Toyota Industries Compressor Parts America, Co. (TICA) in 2012.
TACG and TICA currently support more than 400 jobs in Jackson County.
TIESNA’s new facility will be located at the 1700 Valentine Industrial Parkway campus in Pendergrass.
The new facility will produce DC-to-DC converters, which convert high voltage to low voltage to supply power to auxiliary equipment.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in 2025.
County Commission Chair Tom Crow welcomed TIESNA as Toyota Industries' third related company to Jackson County.
Griff Lynch, President and CEO of the Georgia Ports Authority, commented on the expansion adding to Georgia’s growing automaking sector.
Griff Lynch stated significant investments from Toyota Industries and other automotive suppliers are delivering career opportunities for Georgians.
Kristen Miller, Senior Regional Project Manager, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on the project.
The project was a partnership with Jackson County Area Chamber of Commerce, Georgia Power, the Georgia Ports Authority, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated Toyota Industries has been part of the Jackson County community fabric since 2004.
Pat Wilson noted maintaining 50 years of representation in Japan allows direct communication with corporate HQs like Toyota Industries’.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in Georgia’s automotive industry increased by 324 percent compared to fiscal year 2021.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s OEMs resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
Toyota Industries Corporation was established in 1926 in Japan for the manufacturing and sales of Automatic Looms.
TICO has more than 280 consolidated subsidiaries around the world.
TICO holds the largest world market share in three products: air-jet looms, car air conditioning compressors, and lift trucks.
Toyota Industries Electric Systems North America, Inc. was established in 2013 in Novi, Michigan.
TIESNA is a sales company handling automotive electronics components such as AC inverters, voltage stabilizers, and motor controllers.
TIESNA’s customers include major OEMs in North America.

links.context: Press contact email for Governor Brian P. Kemp

source_title: Toyota Industries Corporation Expands Georgia Presence, Creating 250 N | Georgia

facts: The Department of Energy (DOE) established a program for the integration of electric vehicles (EVs) onto the electric grid and to conduct and report on an Assessment Study of Vehicle Grid Integration (VGI).
The Transportation Technologies Office published the report on January 13, 2025.
Vehicle Grid Integration (VGI) is critical to the electrification of the transportation sector and the modernization of the electric grid.

The Department of Energy (DOE) is prepared to conduct research, development, demonstration, and deployment needed to establish U.S. leadership in VGI, create good paying U.S. jobs, and contribute to combating climate change.
Through the VGI Assessment Study DOE details a 10-year roadmap for DOE’s VGI work.
Within this workplan, DOE will conduct research on specific aspects of VGI covering impacts on EVs and the grid, grid services, codes and standards, and cybersecurity.

This roadmap was developed as the result of work performed across the DOE complex, consultations with National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST), review of reports and studies by non-DOE researchers and VGI stakeholders, and input received directly from stakeholders.
source_title: Vehicle Grid Integration Assessment Report | Department of Energy

facts: In 2020, the current owners purchased the company with the intent to modernize, streamline, and make the company more profitable, while also maintaining jobs for the existing workforce.
COO Hans Pfunder met John Zegers, Georgia Manufacturing Extension Partnership (GaMEP) at Georgia Tech’s Northwest Georgia region manager, and explained their plans.
GaMEP at Georgia Tech is part of the MEP National Network™ and helped the company become a better manufacturer to offer its capabilities to others.

Zegers connected the Trenton Pressing team with Sean Madhavaraman, a GaMEP technology project manager, who saw an opportunity to connect legacy machinery to a Manufacturing Execution System (MES) using sensors and other Industry 4.0 technologies.
The company had various types of stamping presses, robotic welding cells, and other machinery that needed to be monitored in real-time to understand output, efficiency, and downtime.
Pfunder worked with Madhavaraman to create a roadmap to connect the machinery and train employees on how to use the new data.
The engineering and maintenance teams installed new monitoring equipment and utilized software to allow real-time data tracking, helping predict equipment failure before it happened.
The software was also used to optimize production planning and improve employee experience.
Improvements allowed the company to ship substantially more units and freed up additional capacity to take on new customers.
Director of business development Marco Linner told Zegers about an idea to diversify their business by taking on more complex, lower-volume projects that could develop into long-term partnerships.
Within a week, Zegers learned of an agricultural equipment company in Sandersville, Georgia, looking for a contract manufacturer.
That company had been working on product design with Bob Wray, a GaMEP technology project manager, and asked for help sourcing a manufacturing partner who could jump in quickly to start production.
Zegers connected the two companies, as it sounded like a perfect fit for Trenton Pressing.
Linner met with the company and found that the product was indeed the type of business opportunity they were seeking.
The product is now in production at Trenton Pressing.
The first units were delivered just three months after their initial introduction, which was ahead of schedule.
The partnership is going well and both companies hope to produce more products together in the future.
source_title: Manufacturer Connects to New Technology and Strategic Customers | NIST

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced the purchase of a more than 1,100-acre economic development site in Peach County on June 16, 2022.
The Middle Georgia Megasite was acquired through a partnership between the State of Georgia and the Development Authority of Peach County.
The site is strategically located adjacent to GA Hwy 96 with immediate access via Interstate 75 to multiple major metro areas across the American South and Midwest.
The site is less than three hours by truck from the Port of Savannah, which is the single-largest and fastest-growing container terminal in the U.S. and has two Class I rail facilities on-site.
Both the Port of Savannah and Port of Brunswick can be reached in less than 5 hours by rail.

Rail service to the site is provided by Norfolk Southern Class I rail on its north side and an additional rail line on its west side, creating multiple access and design possibilities for loading cars.
The site is a 90-minute drive from Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport, which is described as the busiest and most efficient airport in the world.
Two regional airports are located within 25 minutes of the Middle Georgia Megasite.
Industrial utilities are adjacent to the site, and extensive due diligence reports have been completed to reduce barriers to speedy operations.
The purchase was funded using proceeds from the sale of the Chatham County Economic Development Site, which Amazon purchased in 2021 for a new major distribution facility.
The Middle Georgia Megasite includes four parcels of land, with approximately 1,100 acres designated as developable.

Martin Moseley, Chairman of the Peach County Board of Commissioners, stated that the megasite will position the Middle Georgia region for significant investment and new jobs, calling it transformative for a rural community.

Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that preparing sites of this scale to meet company timelines keeps Georgia competitive and creates a ripple effect of growth across county lines.

Governor Kemp emphasized that Georgia’s superior infrastructure, including its robust highway system, rail lines, record-setting port, and top-ranked airport, makes the state a competitive choice for companies worldwide.
source_title: Governor Kemp Announces Purchase of Middle Georgia Megasite | Georgia

facts: Georgia Power is an investor-owned utility (IOU) that operates in Georgia.
The page provides a summary of the types of incentives provided by the utility related to alternative fuels and vehicles.
Residential EV Time-of-Use (TOU) Rate incentive is available for fuel prices.
Residential EV Level 2 Charging Station Purchase Rebate incentive is available for infrastructure.
No residential vehicle incentives are currently offered.
No non-residential fuel price incentives are currently offered.
Non-residential EV Level 2 Charging Station Purchase Rebate incentive is available for infrastructure.
Non-residential Make-Ready or Pre-Wiring Rebate for EV Chargers incentive is available for infrastructure.
No non-residential vehicle incentives are currently offered.
Georgia Power operates in the Atlanta-Sandy Springs-Alpharetta Metropolitan Statistical Area.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Georgia Power

entity_name: U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) AMMTO and WPTO Domestic Large Component Manufacturing Funding Program
canonical_name: Advanced Materials & Manufacturing Technologies Office (AMMTO) and Water Power Technologies Office (WPTO) Domestic Large Component Manufacturing to Enable a Clean and Competitive Economy Funding
facts: As of February 2024, the U.S. Department of Energy’s (DOE) Advanced Materials and Manufacturing Technologies Office (AMMTO) announced $29.9 million for two selected projects to develop and commercialize novel manufacturing processes and methods capable of producing large metallic manufacturing components for renewable power systems.
The first project received $14.9 million in federal funding from AMMTO in September 2023 and has the potential to help reduce overall hydropower costs by 20% and lead times by four months.
The second project received a total of $15 million in federal funding: $13 million from AMMTO and $2 million from Water Power Technologies Office (WPTO) in February 2024.
Wind and water power both rely on large (10+ ton) metallic components, which can be produced through conventional manufacturing processes such as casting and forging.
The domestic manufacturing base is currently unable to competitively produce these large components using conventional methods alone.

The inability to domestically produce these components has resulted in increased production costs, longer lead times, reliance on foreign supply chains, and, ultimately, an inhibited ability to manufacture large and complex systems.

This incapacity affects a large cross-section of the manufacturing sector including clean electricity generation, transportation, industrial machinery, tooling, durable goods, and domestic infrastructure.
These two projects directly support the innovation required to boost the domestic production of large metallic manufacturing components.
Project Title: Development of Advanced Casting Cell and Digital Foundry to Enable Cost Effective Domestic Manufacturing of Large Clean Energy Castings
Institution: GE Research
Project Partners: GE Hydro, GE Onshore Wind, GE Offshore Wind, Voxeljet, Clemson University, Oak Ridge National Laboratory (ORNL), and Hodge Foundry
Location: Niskayuna, New York
Federal Funding: $14.9 Million
GE Research will develop, build, and refine the advanced casting cell (ACC), a sand binder jet 3D printer for printing large sand-casting molds.
The digital creation of mold designs via a digital foundry (an end-to-end suite of tools that include process monitoring and topology optimization through multi-faceted models).
The completion of a techno-economic analysis that will study the true costs and supply chain challenges that need to be overcome for full-scale implementation of these systems.
The casting of a 16-ton rotor hub using ACC molds.
The development of a robotic welding process for the assembly of a more than 10-ton hydropower Francis runner (including turbine blades, plates, and rotating shaft pieces).
A corresponding workforce development component that will create a curriculum for worker training and involvement.
Project Title: Net Shape Multi-Agent Robotic Hybrid Additive Manufacturing for Large-Scale Metal
Institution: Oak Ridge National Laboratory
Project Partners: Tennessee Valley Authority
Location: Oak Ridge, Tennessee
Federal Funding: $15 Million ($13 Million from AMMTO; $2 Million from WPTO)
Oak Ridge National Laboratory plans to develop a net shape multi-agent robotic hybrid additive manufacturing (AM) process for large-scale metallic structures.
Fabricating full-scale Francis hydropower runners, the largest of which will be 4.7 meters in diameter and 42,000 kilograms.
Halving the existing lead time of this manufacturing process to enable more efficient domestic hydropower production.
Achieving 100% domestic production of a hydropower turbine runner in the United States.
Enabling the efficient production of complex near net shape metallic manufacturing components, specifically for hydropower technology.
Combining robotic welding-based directed energy deposition (DED) processes with conventional machine tools in a hybrid process.
source_title: Funding Selections: Domestic Large Component Manufacturing to Enable a Clean and Competitive Economy | Department of Energy

facts: Volvo Cars has been granted a specific authorization from the Office of Information and Communications Technology and Services under the 'Securing the Information and Communications Technology and Services Supply Chain: Connected Vehicles' rule in the United States.
Under the rule Volvo Car USA was required to follow a process with the US Department of Commerce to obtain a specific authorization for the continued import and sale of connected cars in the US.

The process is carried out on a case-by-case basis and the issuance of a specific authorization follows constructive discussions with the US Department of Commerce and other US officials regarding Volvo Cars' governance, technology and data security.
With this specific authorization, Volvo Cars can continue its growth plans in the US.
The US is one of the largest markets for Volvo Cars and home to its state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Charleston, South Carolina.
Volvo Cars has invested over 1.3 billion USD to date at its Charleston, South Carolina facility.
The Charleston, South Carolina facility has created over 2,000 jobs.
At the end of September 2025, Volvo Cars announced additional investments in South Carolina to bring two additional vehicles into production before 2030.
The company's US headquarters is based in New Jersey with around 400 people employed.
An additional 200 corporate employees are located across the country.
Last year, the company celebrated 70 years in the US.
Currently, Volvo Car USA has 281 dealers in 48 states.
The dealer network employs approximately 11,500 people.
source_title: Volvo Cars receives a specific authorization in the United States under the ICTS Connected Vehicles Rule

facts: German multi-metal provider and recycling company Aurubis is among an influx of ancillary companies forming to support Georgia's booming closed-loop electric mobility supply chain.
Aurubis is one of the world’s leading recyclers of copper, precious metals, and non-ferrous materials.
In November 2021, Aurubis announced it would build a recycling and secondary smelting facility for multi-metal recycling in Augusta, Georgia.
Leaders from the Georgia Department of Economic Development were at Aurubis's headquarters in Hamburg, Germany, when the investment was announced.
The announcement followed a series of meetings between the company and local and state officials that occurred on the home soil of each partner.
In December 2022, Aurubis increased its investment total for the Augusta facility to €640 million.
Aurubis projects it will double its throughput volume from 90,000 tons to 180,000 tons of complex recycling materials per year.
The facility is expected to create 200 jobs.
The announcement marked the single-largest investment of a German company in Georgia.
The Augusta site is the first facility of its kind in the U.S. and Aurubis's first greenfield project in the country.
Aurubis plans to produce materials that can be used in lithium-ion batteries and other electronics valuable in the EV industry.

Shortly after the December 2022 announcement, Georgia Governor Brian Kemp and state leaders traveled to Germany to celebrate the company’s commitment to Georgia's e-mobility and renewable energy ecosystems.
Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that the state's ports system, highly skilled workforce, and pro-business environment attracted Aurubis to Augusta.
Governor Kemp noted that Aurubis will join more than 500 German companies with operations in Georgia.
Aurubis CEO Roland Harings said today’s high recycling demand cannot be met by existing facilities.
Harings stated that metals such as copper can be recycled without loss of quality or performance for use in cars, mobile phones, batteries, cables, and computers.
Harings described the company's increased commitment to the U.S. as a game-changer for the environment, manufacturers, and the transition to a renewable economy.
Aurubis broke ground on the recycling plant in June 2022.
The first phase is expected to come online in mid-2024, with the expansion to be completed in 2026.
The company was able to quickly start work on the site due to its status as a 'Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certified' site.
GRAD certification is a proactive way for landowners and local developers to catalyze economic growth and increase speed to market for companies.
Cal Wray, president of the Augusta Economic Development Authority, praised the speed at which Aurubis is building the facility and executing further expansions.
Wray stated that Aurubis will provide great jobs and be a wonderful addition to the Augusta community.
Aurubis's presence in Georgia highlights the state's status as a national leader in electric mobility and sustainable battery production.
Germany is one of Georgia’s top three investment partners and was Georgia’s second-largest job-creating country for Foreign Direct investment in FY22.
At least 513 German facilities operate in the state, employing more than 25,000 Georgians.
Georgia is celebrating its 50th year of continuous representation in Germany.

links.context: Georgia's expanding EV supply chain information

source_title: Investment in Recycling Facility Strengthens Georgia's Position in EV | Georgia

facts: Volvo Group North America evaluates proposed capital projects using standardized methods including Net Present Value (NPV), Internal Rate of Return (IRR), Modified IRR (MIRR), and Simple Payback.
Profitability calculations such as NPV, IRR, and MIRR are used for larger investments, while Simple Paybacks are used for minor investments.
Previously, some energy efficiency projects faced resistance due to long payback periods and the challenge of estimating energy costs over time.
Implementing the Volvo Group Guideline for Energy-Efficiency Investment Calculations addresses these barriers by providing clear guidance on factoring in the risk of sharp rises in energy costs.
The new guideline levels the playing field for energy efficiency projects and helps prioritize Volvo Group’s vision and environmental responsibility, one of the company’s core values.
Volvo Group’s vision is to become the world leader in sustainable transport solutions.
Reducing energy consumption at manufacturing sites is a key method by which the company is working towards this vision.
Objectives for U.S. manufacturing sites include reducing energy intensity by 25% by 2020 from a 2009 baseline.
Volvo Group sites are striving towards CO2 neutrality.
Objectives include reducing idling losses (system losses during non-production hours) by 50%.
Reducing system losses when a system is idle has become an important consideration.
The new guideline was developed in 2013 by Volvo’s Environmental Director and members of the Executive Management, Environmental Council, and Corporate Standards teams.
This guideline is an update of the Guidelines for Energy Saving Investments guideline developed in 2006.
The Guideline for Energy-Efficiency Investment Calculations was disseminated throughout Volvo Group by the Corporate Standards team after its development.
Energy cost projections used in the guideline were developed in collaboration between the Volvo Environmental Council and Executive Management team and are reviewed annually.
Maintenance and Energy staff have been essential in executing the guideline throughout the new project approval process.
All PPE investments are reviewed according to the Volvo Group Investment Policy.
Once approved, environmental and energy professionals are involved in collecting, reporting, and analyzing data on the implementation and impact of each energy savings project.
There are two metrics for measuring the impact of the new guideline: the number of new energy savings projects and a reduction in company energy intensity.
Volvo Group Trucks Operations tracks proposed and implemented energy savings projects.
Energy consumption data is tracked at the corporate level.
Both sets of data are featured in the annual Volvo Group Environmental Data Report.
Since the guideline was launched, numerous energy savings projects have been approved that may not have received capital funding otherwise.
The guideline provides a powerful tool for the company’s energy, maintenance, and environmental managers to obtain funding for strategic energy projects.
This has helped Volvo make significant strides in energy efficiency at its U.S. plants.
The company met its initial 25% energy intensity reduction target in 2014.
The company followed up by setting a new 25% target.
The New River Valley truck plant in Dublin, Virginia used the guideline to gain approval for an energy-efficient lighting project.
Volvo’s New River Valley plant uses the Group guideline in all energy project financial calculations.

A case study shows the use of the Group guideline in replacing 400w High Intensity Discharge (HID) metal halide bulbs with energy-efficient Light-Emitting Diode (LED) lights with occupancy sensors at the New River Valley plant.
Traditionally, HID lights are used for high bay plant lighting, parking lot lights, wall packs, and outdoor flood lights.
LED bulbs use less power relative to the amount of light generated and help reduce greenhouse gas emissions by saving energy.
The comparison shows 400w HID metal halide bulbs use 465 input watts versus 215 for LED bulbs with occupancy sensors.
HID bulbs operate 21 hours/day while LED bulbs with occupancy sensors operate 20 hours/day due to dimming during lunch and breaks.
The yearly cost for HID bulbs is $213.95 compared to $21.67 for LED bulbs, based on a $/KWH rate of $0.07.
Fixture costs are $250.00 for HID bulbs and $1,043.00 for LED bulbs.
Maintenance costs are $30.00 for HID bulbs and $0.00 for LED bulbs.
The simple payback period for the HID setup is 4.01 years.
Total fixture costs for the plant project are $72,250.00 for HID and $260,750.00 for LEDs.
The project yields yearly cost savings of $65,084.63.
The project yields yearly energy savings of 805,923 KWH (or 806 MWH).
The project yields yearly CO2 savings of 620 tons.
Graphs 2 and 3 show a hypothetical comparison of ROI results using the Guideline versus not using it.
The comparison is based on an actual project proposal at New River Valley to replace HID bulbs with LEDs with occupancy sensors.
The Guideline allows for a 10% projection in electricity prices in Year 1 to 3, while the second calculation assumes a 5% increase in Y1 to Y3.
The comparison shows a payback period of four to five years for the LEDs with or without the Guideline.
The return on investment (ROI) is much higher in year 4 and year 5 respectively when the 5%/10%/15% increase specified in the Guideline is used.
Using the Guideline, a 5%/10%/15% increase in electricity price cuts a year off of the projected payback period.
source_title: Volvo Group North America: Giving Energy Projects a Boost with New Investment Guidelines | Better Buildings & Better Plants Initiative

facts: Wabash National Corporation is a diversified industrial manufacturer and North America’s leading producer of semi-trailers and liquid transportation systems.
Wabash National entered into a definitive agreement to acquire all outstanding shares of Supreme Industries, Inc. in a cash tender offer for $21 per share.
The acquisition represents an equity value of $364 million and an enterprise value of $342 million.
Supreme Industries was founded in 1974 and is the second largest U.S. manufacturer of truck bodies with 2016 sales of $299 million.
Supreme primarily manufactures light- and medium-duty truck bodies at seven facilities throughout the United States.
Dick Giromini, Wabash National’s chief executive officer, stated that Wabash formally entered the final mile space in 2015 with the launch of dry and refrigerated truck bodies.

The acquisition combines Supreme’s medium- and light-duty commercial vehicle portfolio, distribution network, and regional manufacturing locations with Wabash National’s advanced composite technologies, lean manufacturing expertise, engineering proficiency, and supplier relationships.
Supreme provides Wabash National with reduced dependence on dry van trailer demand, reduced cyclicality, and new segments for growth.
Wabash National intends to preserve Supreme’s heritage of excellence in serving customers while accelerating its organic truck body growth initiative.

Dick Giromini noted that combining with Supreme allows Wabash to accelerate organic growth with innovative DuraPlate®, honeycomb panel and molded structural composite (MSC) truck bodies, as well as provide a broader conventional product offering.
Wabash National expects to deliver at least $20 million in annual run-rate cost synergies by 2021, primarily driven by corporate and procurement expenditures and operational improvement savings.
Wabash National expects to achieve significant incremental revenue opportunities over time that neither company could obtain on a standalone basis.
Mark Weber, Supreme Industries’ Chief Executive Officer, commented that combining with Wabash will enhance their ability to innovate more quickly and create more value for customers.
Wabash formed an acquisition subsidiary, Redhawk Acquisition Corporation, to commence the tender offer.
Following the tender offer, Wabash expects to consummate a merger of Redhawk Acquisition Corp. and Supreme, converting remaining shares into the right to receive the same cash price per share.

The transaction is subject to customary closing conditions, including acquiring a majority of Supreme’s outstanding shares and expiration/termination of the Hart-Scott-Rodino Antitrust Improvements Act waiting period.
The closing of the acquisition is expected to occur no later than the fourth quarter of 2017.
The transaction is not subject to any financing condition, with Wabash obtaining committed bridge financing from Morgan Stanley Senior Funding, Inc. and Wells Fargo Bank.
The purchase price is expected to be funded by a combination of notes and cash.
Supreme’s board of directors approved the agreement and recommended that stockholders accept the offer.
Baird acted as financial advisor to Supreme, and Haynes & Boone provided legal advice.
Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC acted as financial advisor to Wabash, and Hogan Lovells provided legal advice.
Wabash National will conduct a conference call to discuss the transaction on August 9, 2017, at 8:00 a.m. EDT.
Wabash National Corporation was established in 1985 in Lafayette, Indiana.

Wabash National manufactures dry freight and refrigerated trailers, platform trailers, bulk tank trailers, dry and refrigerated truck bodies, truck-mounted tanks, intermodal equipment, aircraft refueling equipment, structural composite panels and products, trailer aerodynamic solutions, and specialty food grade and pharmaceutical equipment.

Wabash National’s products are sold under brand names including Wabash National®, Beall®, Benson®, Brenner® Tank, Bulk Tank International, DuraPlate®, Extract Technology®, Garsite, Progress Tank, Transcraft®, Walker Engineered Products, and Walker Transport.
Supreme Industries’ customers include national rental fleets, national and regional leasing companies, truck dealers, and fleet operators.
