SEC website for tender offer documents

source_title: Wabash National Corporation Announces Agreement to Acquire

facts: The Georgia Department of Labor (GDOL) coordinates the federal Work Opportunity Tax Credit Program (WOTC).
The program is a federal tax credit available to employers who hire and retain eligible individuals from certain targeted groups with significant barriers to employment.
Employers claim about $1 billion in tax credits each year under the WOTC program.

WOTC reduces an employer's cost of doing business by decreasing their federal income tax liability between $1,200.00 and $9,600.00 per employee, depending on the target group identified and hours worked during the first year of employment.
For employers to earn the tax credit, the new employee must work at least 120 hours during the first year of employment and have not worked previously for the employer.
Employers can claim the WOTC on an unlimited number of qualified employees each year.
The Work Opportunity Tax Credit (WOTC) is authorized through December 31, 2025 (Section 113 of Division EE of P.L. 116-260, Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2021).
The legislative authority for all WOTC targeted groups will expire on December 31, 2025, unless reauthorized by Congress.
At this time, the U.S. Department of Labor (DOL) Employment and Training Administration (ETA) has not indicated whether Congress will pass timely legislation to reauthorize WOTC.

In the event of a lapse in WOTC authorization, Georgia WOTC will accept and fully process all timely submitted WOTC certification requests for individuals with start dates on or before December 31, 2025.

Georgia WOTC will accept, date stamp, log, and retain certification requests submitted using the required IRS and ETA WOTC processing forms (Forms 8850 and 9061) for individuals with start dates on or after January 1, 2026, provided they are submitted within the DOL 28-day timeliness requirement.
Georgia WOTC will not release certification request determinations for individuals with start dates on or after January 1, 2026, until after Congressional reauthorization.
The GA WOTC Online System will remain functional and will accept certification requests submitted within the DOL 28-day timeliness requirement.
No determinations will be released for WOTC applications with a start date on or after January 1, 2026.
Employers can register for an account at the GA WOTC Employer Portal and will receive a confirmation email upon approval.
Upon approval, employers can log in to enter WOTC applications, upload supporting documentation, check application status, and print determinations once processed.

By submitting an application electronically, the employer is the designated legal forms holder and must maintain original signature documents (IRS Form 8850, ETA Form 9061), any supporting documentation, and certification letters received from the GA WOTC Coordinator.
As the legal forms holder, employers are authorized to enter data from the original signature documents and submit this information electronically to the Georgia WOTC Program.

Consultants submitting WOTC applications on behalf of an employer must have a completed Form 9198 (Employer Representative Declaration Form) on file before entering applications per USDOL TEGL 16-20, change 1, effective 10/1/2024.
Form 9198 can be uploaded through the GA WOTC Consultant Portal for staff review and approval.
Employers must complete IRS Form 8850, 'Pre-Screening Notification and Certification Request'.
Employers must complete ETA Form 9061, 'Individual Characteristics Form.'
Employers must submit completed and signed Forms 8850 and 9061 electronically within 28 calendar days of the new employee start date.
Hard copies of Forms 8850 and 9061 are retained by employers for their records.
If mailing WOTC applications, the postmarked date must be within 28 calendar days of the new employee’s start date.
The mailing address is Georgia Dept of Labor, WOTC Unit, 148 Andrew Young International Blvd. NE, Atlanta, GA 30303.
Access to WOTC forms and additional program information includes IRS Form 8850 and instructions, USDOL ETA Form 9061, USDOL Form 9175, USDOL ETA Form 9198, and IRS Form 5884.
A Georgia WOTC Flyer (DOL-4468) is available.
After receiving a certification, employers may file for the tax credit with the IRS.
Generally, an employer elects to take the credit by filing IRS Form 3800 and IRS Form 5884.
A tax-exempt organization that hires an employee in the WOTC veteran target group should use IRS Form 5884-C.
Employers must meet requirements for the Minimum Employment Period, which is at least 120 hours in the first year of employment before they can file and qualify for the tax credit.
WOTC applies only to new employees; rehires do not qualify.
Temporary Assistance for Needy Family / Qualified IV-A recipients qualify for a $2,400 credit if they are members receiving TANF for any 9 months during the 18-month period ending on the hiring date.

Veterans qualify if they served on active duty for more than 180 days or were discharged/released for a service-connected disability, and did not have active duty (excluding training) of more than 90 days ending in the 60-day period before hiring.
Veteran/SNAP recipients qualify for $2,400 if they received SNAP benefits for at least a 3-month period during the 15 months ending on the hire date.
Unemployed Veteran/4 Weeks qualifies for $2,400 if unemployed for at least 4 weeks in the 1-year period ending on the hiring date.
Unemployed Veteran/6 Months qualifies for $5,600 if unemployed for at least 6 months in the 1-year period ending on the hiring date.
Disabled Veteran qualifies for $4,800 if entitled to compensation for a service-connected disability and hired not more than 1 year after discharge/release.
Disabled Unemployed Veteran qualifies for $9,600 if entitled to compensation for a service-connected disability and unemployed for at least 6 months in the 1-year period ending on the hiring date.
Recently released Ex-felons / Qualified ex-felon qualify for $2,400 if convicted of a felony under federal or state law and hired not more than 1 year after conviction or release from prison.
Designated community residents qualify for $2,400 if at least age 18 but not yet 40 on the hiring date and live within an empowerment zone (EZ) or rural renewal county (RRC).
Currently, Randolph and Stewart counties are the only RRCs located in Georgia.

Vocational Rehabilitation referrals qualify for $2,400 if they have a physical or mental disability resulting in a substantial handicap to employment and were referred by an approved rehabilitation agency, Ticket to Work employment network, or Department of Veterans Affairs.

Summer Youth Employees qualify for $1,200 if they perform services between May 1 and September 15, are at least age 16 but not yet 18 on the hiring date (or May 1), have never worked for the employer before, and live within an empowerment zone.

SNAP Recipients qualify for $2,400 if they are 18-39 years old on the hiring date and received SNAP benefits for the 6-month period ending on the hiring date, or were ineligible under section 6(o) but their family received SNAP for at least 3 months of the 5-month period ending on the hiring date.
Supplemental Security Income/SSI recipients qualify for $2,400 if receiving SSI benefits for any month ending during the 60-day period ending on the hiring date.

Long-term family assistance recipients qualify for $9,000 if they received TANF payments for at least 18 consecutive months ending on the hiring date, or received TANF for any 18 months after August 5, 1997, with the earliest period ending in the past 2 years, or stopped being eligible due to federal/state law limits and are hired not more than 2 years after eligibility ended.

Long-term unemployment recipients qualify for $2,400 if in a period of unemployment not less than 27 consecutive weeks that includes a period receiving unemployment compensation under state or federal law.
A tax credit may not be claimed for relatives.
A tax credit may not be claimed for individuals who have previously worked for the employer.
A tax credit may not be claimed for federally subsidized, on-the-job training, though wages paid after the subsidy expires can qualify.
A tax credit may not be claimed for a new employee who works less than 120 hours in the first year of employment.
The program offers a $1,200 - $9,600 tax credit for each new hire meeting certification and working requirements.
Employers make the hiring decision.
There is no limit to the number of new hires who can qualify for the tax credit.
The program requires minimal paperwork.
Applications are submitted electronically.
source_title: Learn About Tax Credits and Incentives | Georgia Department of Labor

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) announced an investment of $25 million across 11 projects to advance materials, processes, machines, and equipment for domestic manufacturing of next-generation batteries.
The announcement was made on December 19, 2024, in Washington, D.C.
These projects will advance platform technologies upon which battery manufacturing capabilities can be built, enabling flexible, scalable, and highly controllable battery manufacturing processes.
Batteries are critical for next-generation technologies that will enhance energy affordability and increase America’s overall energy security and independence.

For grid storage, these innovations will advance the resilience of homes and businesses, the electrification of the transportation sector, and the success of the domestic industrial manufacturing sector.
U.S. Secretary of Energy Jennifer M. Granholm stated that under the Biden-Harris Administration, a foundation has been built to keep momentum growing into the next generation of battery innovation.
Granholm noted that the announced projects build on this success, enhancing national security while delivering jobs for American workers for decades more to come.
Since President Biden took office, companies have announced more than $140 billion in investments in battery and critical mineral supply chains.
DOE recently announced over $3 billion for selected projects to boost the domestic production of advanced batteries and battery materials nationwide.
Those previously selected projects will retrofit, expand, and build new domestic facilities for battery-grade processed critical minerals, battery components, battery manufacturing, and recycling.
The next-generation batteries projects are managed by DOE’s Advanced Materials and Manufacturing Technologies Office (AMMTO).
Subtopic 1 focuses on advanced processes and/or high-performance processing machines for low cost, large-scale, sustainable, commercial manufacture of sodium-ion batteries.
AM Batteries, Inc. (Billerica, Massachusetts) received $2.8 million under Subtopic 1.
Argonne National Laboratory (Lemont, Illinois) received $1.5 million under Subtopic 1.
Clean Republic SODO LLC d/b/a Dakota Lithium Materials (Seattle, Washington) received $2 million under Subtopic 1.

Subtopic 2 focuses on design and manufacturing of flow battery membranes, as well as system design and manufacturing for scale-up of flow battery production and cost-effective integration of flow battery systems.
Quino Energy, Inc. (San Leandro California) received $2.6 million under Subtopic 2.
Arkema, Inc. (King of Prussia, Pennsylvania) received $2.1 million under Subtopic 2.
University of Akron (Akron, Ohio) received $1.6 million under Subtopic 2.
Subtopic 3 focuses on scalable manufacturing processes and equipment for nanolayered films.
University of Maryland (College Park, Maryland) received $2.6 million under Subtopic 3.
Illinois Institute of Technology / Spraying System Co. (Chicago, Illinois / Glendale Heights, Illinois) received $2.6 million under Subtopic 3.

Smart Manufacturing Platforms for Battery Production emphasizes development of broadly applicable smart manufacturing platforms that can be leveraged to improve the production of a variety of battery technologies.
Charge CCCV (Vestal, New York) received $2.6 million under Smart Manufacturing Platforms.
American Lithium Energy Corp. (Carlsbad, California) received $2.6 million under Smart Manufacturing Platforms.
Titan Advanced Energy Solutions (Salem, Massachusetts) received $2.6 million under Smart Manufacturing Platforms.
Selection for award negotiations is not a commitment by DOE to issue an award or provide funding.
Before funding is issued, DOE and the applicants will undergo a negotiation process, and DOE may cancel negotiations and rescind the selection for any reason during that time.
Award amounts are rounded and subject to change pending negotiations.
source_title: U.S. Department of Energy Selects 11 Projects to Advance Domestic Manufacturing of Next-Generation Batteries | Department of Energy

entity_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America
canonical_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America (YMMC)
facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America (YMMC) and Yamaha Motor Corp., USA (YMUS) celebrated the production of 4 million vehicles with the assembly of a new 2021 Wolverine RMAX2 1000 Side-by-Side at its U.S. manufacturing facility in Newnan, Georgia.

The milestone celebration took place at the Newnan factory on August 12 to recognize employees and thank them for their dedication to superior manufacturing processes and high-quality vehicle production.
YMMC serves as Yamaha’s hub for 4-wheel off-highway vehicle manufacturing, producing all of the company’s Side-by-Sides, full-size ATVs, WaveRunners, and Golf Cars for worldwide distribution.
Over the past 20 years, Yamaha has invested more than $380 million into U.S. manufacturing to create a world-class facility in Newnan, according to Taka Imanishi, president of YMMC.
The company strives to maintain the highest quality of production and environmental standards while giving back to the community through comprehensive corporate social responsibility programs.
Since its founding in 1988, YMMC has made a significant impact within its community, covering 280 acres with more than 1.3 million square feet of manufacturing facilities in Newnan.
Between the YMMC facility and YMUS corporate offices in Marietta and Kennesaw, Georgia, Yamaha now employs over 2,000 people in the state alone.
This Georgia workforce encompasses more than half of the 4,000+ individuals the company directly employs in the United States.
The city of Newnan named May 18th “Yamaha Day” in 2011 to celebrate the company’s positive economic impacts and ongoing job creation.
Many of Yamaha’s community impact messages were highlighted in the season 19 premiere of “Modern Marvels: Machines” on the HISTORY® Channel, which went behind-the-scenes at the factory.

Mike Martinez, YMUS vice president of motorsports, stated that Yamaha continues to add personnel and strengthen operations in Georgia as the Side-by-Side line expands with popular new models like the Wolverine RMAX 1000.
With increasing demand for outdoor recreation, Yamaha expects continued growth of its operations in Georgia and across the country.
The YMMC facility holds the highest certifications for quality, safety, and environmental manufacturing for producing golf cars, personal watercraft, Side-by-Side vehicles, and ATVs.
The factory is ISO9001 Quality Management System and ISO14001 Environmental Management System certified.
YMUS maintains a corporate office in California, two corporate offices in Georgia, facilities in Wisconsin and Alabama, and factory operations in Tennessee and Georgia.

Additional U.S.-based subsidiaries include Yamaha Marine Systems Company with divisions Bennett Marine (Florida) and Kracor Systems (Wisconsin), Skeeter Boats (Texas) with division G3 Boats (Missouri), and Yamaha Precision Propeller (Indiana).

links.context: Full lineup of vehicles assembled in the USA

source_title: Yamaha Celebrates 4 Million Units Assembled in the USA

facts: Leads federal efforts to enable the commercial viability of hydrogen and fuel cell technologies.
Conducts a broad portfolio of research, development, and demonstration activities for hydrogen and fuel cell technologies.
Conducts activities in manufacturing, safety, codes and standards, and workforce development to further support deployment and market adoption.
Efforts help support domestic energy security and resilience.
Efforts strengthen American competitiveness and innovation in the rapidly growing global hydrogen and fuel cell industry.
Published a fact sheet titled 'Progress in Hydrogen and Fuel Cells' in March 2025 describing how DOE funding has spurred significant progress in several areas.
source_title: Hydrogen and Fuel Cell Technologies Accomplishments and Progress | Department of Energy

facts: The National Laboratory of the Rockies (NLR, formerly NREL) is sharing an update regarding the future of the Community Power Accelerator (CPA) platform.
Beginning in March 2026, the CPA platform will transition to the Milken Institute’s Community Infrastructure Center (CIC).
CPA will continue to operate as a free, public-facing platform while benefiting from expanded resources, partnerships, and long-term support.
The integration of CPA into CIC is designed to preserve tools users rely on today while expanding access to capital, expertise, and knowledge across a broader range of infrastructure projects.
All core CPA functionality will remain intact.
Existing project information and user activity will be preserved.
Continued support for distributed solar and storage projects under 5 megawatts.
Free access to the platform for all users.
A broader national network of capital providers, technical assistance partners, and project sponsors will be added.
Enhanced grant and financing matching tools will be available.
An expanded knowledge base will include new templates, case studies, and resources.
Opportunities to participate in financing forums and deal-focused convenings will be offered.
Ongoing platform enhancements and feature development will continue.
The Community Infrastructure Center supports infrastructure projects nationwide—well beyond solar and storage—and connects communities to a wide range of capital pathways.
In early March 2026, users will receive instructions to reset their password and activate their account on the CIC platform.
A live onboarding and platform demo will be held on April 7, 2026, at 12:00 PM MT.
Registration details for the live session will be shared soon.
All existing project information, user profiles, and activity histories will be preserved and available on the CIC platform.
CPA will continue to support distributed solar and storage projects under 5 MW.
Access to the CPA platform through CIC remains free.
Users will receive instructions to reset their password and activate their existing account in CIC rather than creating a new one.
Users gain access to a larger national network of funders and partners, improved matching tools, expanded learning resources, and new financing convening opportunities.
source_title: Home | Community Power Accelerator

facts: The median range of all-electric vehicles (EVs) for model year (MY) 2024 reached a record high of 283 miles per charge.
The median EV range gained 13 miles from the previous model year.
The median EV range is more than four times higher than the median range for MY 2011.
The highest maximum range for an EV certified by the EPA was the Lucid Air sedan in MY 2022, rated at 520 miles per charge.
Data was sourced from the U.S. Department of Energy and U.S. Environmental Protection Agency Fuel Economy Website Datafiles, accessed October 10, 2024.
source_title: FOTW #1375, December 30, 2024: Median EV Range in Model Year 2024 Reached a Record High of 283 Miles per Charge | Department of Energy

facts: Archer is making its proprietary, dual-use advanced powertrain technology (currently used on its Midnight eVTOL aircraft) available to third parties for the first time, introducing a new revenue stream.
Archer strategically vertically integrated the design and manufacturing of its battery pack to meet rigorous aviation safety, performance, and reliability standards.
The Omen, a hover-to-cruise autonomous air vehicle co-developed by Anduril and EDGE Group in the UAE, will be the first third-party product to receive Archer's powertrain technology.
Archer manufactures its electric powertrain across nearly 1,000,000 square feet of manufacturing and test facilities in the U.S., applying automation to key areas for reliable, scalable production.

Archer Aviation Inc. (NYSE: ACHR) announced an agreement to supply its dual-use electric powertrain technology to Anduril Industries and EDGE Group to accelerate development and scaled production of the Omen autonomous air vehicle system.
The UAE has committed to an initial acquisition of 50 Omen systems, creating a demand signal for both the vehicle and Archer's powertrain technology.
Archer has invested heavily in developing a proprietary, best-in-class electric powertrain highly optimized for commercial and defense aviation use cases.
Archer's expansion into supplying electric powertrains for defense applications builds on its accumulated experience in battery and electric engine technologies.

Archer founder and CEO Adam Goldstein stated that the company views its Midnight eVTOL as a platform for various aerospace technologies and expects the Anduril powertrain deal to be the first of many similar collaborations.

Anduril SVP of Engineering Shane Arnott noted that combining Archer's powertrain expertise with five years of internal work has matured their propulsion solution to meet required ranges, speeds, and payload capacity for dual-use missions.
Archer announced an exclusive agreement with Korean Air to commercialize aerial mobility (AAM) technology across multiple markets and use cases.
The cities of Osaka and Tokyo have selected Archer and its local partner to lead aerial mobility deployments in Japan.
Archer's Midnight aircraft is a piloted design capable of carrying four passengers with rapid back-to-back trips and minimal charge times between flights.
The Midnight aircraft aims to replace 60–90 minute car commutes with 10–20 minute electric air taxi flights that are safe, sustainable, low-noise, and cost-competitive with ground transportation.

EDGE Group, headquartered in Abu Dhabi, capital of the UAE, consolidates more than 35 entities into five core clusters: Platforms & Systems, Missiles & Weapons, Space & Cyber Technologies, Technology & Innovation, and Homeland Security.

links.context: SEC filings and Annual Report on Form 10-K

source_title: Archer to Supply Its Proprietary Electric Powertrain to Third Parties, Starting with Anduril and EDGE Group for their Recently Announced Omen Autonomous Air Vehicle

facts: Archer Aviation selected a site in Covington, Georgia adjacent to the Covington Municipal Airport for its manufacturing facility.
The site selection represents a key next step as Archer works towards being first in the industry to bring up a high-capacity eVTOL production facility.
Archer plans to initially build out a 350,000 square-foot facility on a 96-acre site.
The initial facility will be capable of producing up to 650 aircraft per year.
Construction is expected to be completed in the first half of 2024.
Initial production is expected to begin in the second half of 2024.
The facility will be capable of being expanded by an additional 550,000 square feet.
The expansion is estimated to support production of up to 2,300 aircraft per year.
Archer intends to invest $118M over the next 10 years.
Archer will receive an incentive package totaling 33% of its capital investment commitment.
The incentive package includes land conveyance, tax incentives, and a Georgia REBA grant.
Archer has entered into a letter of intent with Synovus Bank to obtain financing for the project.
Synovus Bank is the largest bank headquartered in Georgia.
Archer completed a comprehensive survey of potential manufacturing sites across the United States for its first facility.
Key site selection considerations included local labor market talent availability, utility availability, ability to conduct seamless flight test operations, construction costs, and logistics.
The planned eVTOL aircraft manufacturing facility is expected to create over 1,000 jobs over the long term.
Adam Goldstein, Archer's CEO, stated that after many months of discussion and evaluation, they are excited to select Georgia for their manufacturing facility.
Goldstein noted that the U.S. has long led the world in aerospace technology and Georgia has played a vital role in that industry.

Goldstein added that eVTOL technology can transform how urban and rural communities live and commute, and the factory can create pathways to highly skilled manufacturing jobs and other ladders of social and economic opportunity.
Governor Brian P. Kemp commented that Georgia has made nurturing innovation a priority as it works with businesses to define the future.
Kemp stated that Georgia's established leadership in the aerospace industry was essential to landing Archer Aviation in Newton County.
Kemp welcomed Archer to Georgia, noting they can take advantage of the state's signature workforce training program Quick Start to help fill over 1,000 jobs in Covington long-term.
Georgia is home to more than 800 aerospace companies and maintains a robust aerospace education pipeline.

The local education pipeline includes a dozen high schools that offer training in the field, five technical colleges with aviation programs, and several universities offering degrees in aerospace engineering.

The Georgia Center of Innovation for Aerospace, a strategic arm of the Georgia Department of Economic Development, has been cultivating opportunities for innovative companies like Archer for more than 10 years.
Archer is designing and developing electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft for use in urban air mobility networks.
Archer's team is based in Santa Clara, California.
source_title: Archer Aviation Selects Site in Georgia Adjacent to the Covington Municipal Airport for its Manufacturing Facility

facts: All forms of electric vehicles (EVs) can help improve fuel economy, lower fuel costs, and reduce air quality impacts.
Using electricity as a power source for transportation improves public health and the environment, provides safety benefits, and contributes to a secure transportation system.
Advanced batteries in electric vehicles are designed for extended life but will wear out eventually.
Several manufacturers of electric vehicles are offering 8-year/100,000-mile battery warranties.
Predictive modeling by the National Laboratory of the Rockies indicates that today’s batteries may last 12 to 15 years in moderate climates (8 to 12 years in extreme climates).
Battery life is impacted by climate, driving and charging patterns, battery cell chemistry and design, and the vehicle-battery-environment thermal system.
Manufacturers have not published pricing for replacement batteries, but some are offering extended warranty programs with monthly fees.
Replacing batteries outside the warranty may be a significant expense.
Battery prices are expected to continue declining as battery technologies improve and production volumes increase.
Energy costs for EVs are generally lower than for similar conventional vehicles, but purchase prices can be significantly higher.
EV purchase prices are likely to equalize with conventional vehicles as production volumes increase and battery technologies continue to mature.
Initial costs can be offset by fuel cost savings and state and utility incentives.
Some states and electric utilities also offer incentives, many of which can be found in the Laws and Incentives database.
Electric vehicles can reduce fuel costs dramatically because of the high efficiency of electric-drive components.
All-electric vehicles and PHEVs rely in whole or part on electric power, so their fuel economy is measured differently than conventional vehicles.
Miles per gallon of gasoline equivalent (MPGe) and kilowatt-hours (kWh) per 100 miles are common metrics.
Today's light-duty all-electric vehicles (or PHEVs in electric mode) can exceed 130 MPGe and can drive 100 miles consuming only 25–40 kWh.
HEVs typically achieve better fuel economy and have lower fuel costs than similar conventional vehicles.
The 2024 Toyota Corolla Hybrid has an EPA combined city-and-highway fuel economy estimate of 50 miles per gallon (MPG).
The estimate for the conventional 2024 Corolla (four cylinder, automatic) is 35 MPG.
The fuel economy of medium- and heavy-duty all-electric vehicles and PHEVs is highly dependent on the load carried and the duty cycle.
In the right applications, all-electric vehicles maintain a strong fuel-to-cost advantage over their conventional counterparts.
All-electric vehicles and PHEVs have the benefit of flexible charging because the electric grid is near most locations where people park.
An EV charging station, sometimes referred to as electric vehicle supply equipment (EVSE), is needed to safely deliver energy from the electric grid to a vehicle’s battery.
Drivers can charge overnight at a residence, including multifamily housing, as well as the workplace or a public charging station when available.
PHEVs have added flexibility because they can also refuel with gasoline or diesel (or possibly other fuels in the future) when necessary.
Public charging stations are not as ubiquitous as gas stations.

Charging equipment manufacturers, automakers, utilities, Clean Cities and Communities coalitions, states, municipalities, and government agencies are rapidly establishing a national network of public charging stations.
The number of publicly accessible charging stations in the United States reached more than 60,000 in 2024.
These stations offer more than 162,000 charging ports, according to the Alternative Fueling Station Locator.
The transportation sector accounts for approximately 30% of total U.S. energy needs and 70% of U.S. petroleum consumption.
Using more energy efficient vehicles like hybrid and electric vehicles supports the U.S. economy and helps diversify the U.S. transportation fleet.
The multiple fuel sources used to generate electricity results in a more secure energy source for the electrified portion of the transportation sector.
All of this strengthens national energy security by increasing flexibility during natural disasters and fuel supply disruptions.

HEVs typically use less fuel than similar conventional vehicles because they employ electric-drive technologies to boost vehicle efficiency through regenerative braking—recapturing energy otherwise lost during braking.
PHEVs and all-electric vehicles, also referred to as battery electric vehicles (BEVs), are both capable of being powered solely by electricity.
Electricity is produced in the United States from natural gas, coal, nuclear energy, wind energy, hydropower, and solar energy.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Electric Vehicle Benefits and Considerations

facts: Atlanta and Savannah serve as primary global gateways for people and products through air, sea, and rail.
State leadership under then-Governor Jimmy Carter began to establish Georgia’s physical presence abroad fifty years ago.
Coryn Marsik serves as Director of International Investment at the Georgia Department of Economic Development.
The State of Georgia’s international office in Tokyo was first established in 1973, providing direct access to rapidly developing markets in East Asia.
Japanese companies continue to be the top international employer in Georgia.
Georgia maintains active ties with organizations such as the Japan-America Society of Georgia and the Japan External Trade Organization.
Georgia is a charter member of the Southeast U.S.-Japan Alliance.
The State of Georgia’s Europe Office was established on the heels of opening its Japan Office in 1973.
Professionals for the Europe Office serve the European market from Munich, Germany.
A mission to Europe in 2020 propelled bringing mobility of the future to the forefront of Georgia’s manufacturing recruitment plans.
Governor Brian Kemp and a State of Georgia delegation met with automotive companies headquartered in Germany, where electrification and other alternative fuels were repeatedly discussed.
Following the 2020 mission, the team came back with a new goal to identify opportunities at every step of the supply chain.
Just three years later, the mobility and battery supply chain have announced more than $25.5 billion in investments.
These announced investments are estimated to create 30,700 new jobs over the next few years.
GEDIA Automotive, Aurubis AG, and Heliox are European-based companies that are enhancing Georgia’s mobility ecosystem.
The success of Georgia’s mobility and clean tech industries thrust the state to platforms like the 2023 World Economic Forum in Davos-Klosters, Switzerland.

Governor Brian Kemp served on a panel at the 2023 World Economic Forum, stating that 'one thing you can count on is stability and a great economy, a great business environment in the State of Georgia.'
Ellen Kraft serves as Managing Director of the Europe Office.
The Europe Office highlights expanding ties with European partners, including the new North Rhine-Westphalia (NRW) office in Atlanta.
Commissioner Pat Wilson explains how Georgia is reaping the seeds sown decades ago by visionary leaders.
source_title: Georgia: 50 Years in Japan and Europe | Georgia

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE), in coordination with the U.S. Department of Labor (DOL), announced the release of the Battery Workforce Initiative’s National Guideline Standards for registered apprenticeships for battery machine operators on March 26, 2024.

The DOL-certified guidelines were created in partnership with battery manufacturers, community colleges, and unions to lay out rigorous training requirements for the skilled workforce needed as electric vehicle sales have quadrupled.
Nearly 300 new or expanded battery facilities have been announced since President Biden took office.

Energy Secretary Jennifer M. Granholm, Acting Labor Secretary Julie Su, Michigan Governor Gretchen Whitmer, U.S. Representative Elissa Slotkin, and UAW President Shawn Fain attended the announcement at UAW Local 652 Union Hall in Lansing, MI.
The announcement location was near the Ultium factory being constructed with support from DOE’s Loan Program Office.

U.S. Secretary of Energy Jennifer M. Granholm stated that over 400 EV and battery manufacturing facilities have been announced in the U.S. in just three years under President Biden’s Investing in America agenda.

Acting Secretary of Labor Julie A. Su noted that in just under a year, the Battery Workforce Initiative has built a strong partnership between government, industry, technology, and labor to expand pathways into good jobs through Registered Apprenticeships.
U.S. Secretary of Education Miguel Cardona described the initiative as an example of how agencies and community college partners can create new career pathways into the clean energy workforce.

The new Standards will inform Battery Workforce Initiative training materials for companies and training providers, with curricula currently under development to support DOE’s broader workforce education activities for battery manufacturing.
The initiative includes the BWI Pilot Training Project and the Battery Workforce Challenge Program Regional Workforce Training Hubs Project.
DOE's National Energy Technology Lab will launch the BWI Pilot Training in April.
Argonne National Laboratory will launch the Battery Workforce Challenge’s Regional Workforce Training Hubs in Summer 2024.
The new Standards facilitate upfront industry engagement and alignment, speeding up the DOL approval process for new battery training programs and providing a foundation for national credentials.
BWI materials create a clear pathway for training sponsors to leverage public funds for workforce development, meet battery manufacturers' needs, and provide career-track pathways for workers.

Bob Galyen, Chief Technology Officer for NAATBatt and SAE International Fellow, stated that after 48 years in the battery industry working in the world’s largest lithium battery manufacturing operations, training is key to a successful battery industry and BWI will accelerate the return to global technology leadership while creating more middle-class jobs.
Since 2022, the Battery Workforce Initiative has convened meetings and engaged with hundreds of stakeholders representing established and emerging manufacturers, academia, and labor groups.
The initiative's next agenda item is to examine other jobs in the battery supply chain.
BWI has already started convening stakeholders working on battery-grade materials processing and recycling to map out skills requirements for that work.

BWI’s strategic partnerships across agencies, including with the Department of Education, leverage historic investments in clean energy industries as a critical pillar of President Biden’s whole-of-government decarbonization strategy.
source_title: DOE and DOL Announce New Effort to Support and Expand America’s Battery Workforce | Department of Energy

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Boston Scientific will construct a new manufacturing and supply chain facility in Johns Creek.
The $62.5 million investment is expected to bring roughly 340 new jobs to Fulton County over the next seven years.
Boston Scientific is headquartered in Massachusetts.
Boston Scientific designs, develops, and manufactures a wide range of medical devices and therapies used in less-invasive procedures across multiple medical specialties.
Its solutions and technologies are used by healthcare providers in endoscopy, cardiology, peripheral interventions, oncology, urology, and neurology.
The company has more than 17,000 products that treat approximately 30 million patients around the world each year.
Brad Sorenson, Executive Vice President of Global Operations at Boston Scientific, stated the new site in Johns Creek will enable continued growth as they fulfill their mission to transform lives.
In 2016, Boston Scientific purchased Georgia-based EndoChoice.
Boston Scientific currently employs approximately 300 individuals in Georgia and more than 41,000 individuals globally.
The new facility will be located at 11350 Johns Creek Parkway in Johns Creek.
Johns Creek Mayor John Bradberry stated that locating in the Town Center underscores Johns Creek as a leading destination for healthcare innovation and wellness.
Marty Turpeau, Chairman of the Development Authority of Fulton County, called the announcement a game changer for Fulton County that adds a world-class brand to its life science ecosystem.

Katie Kirkpatrick, President and CEO of the Metro Atlanta Chamber, noted that Metro Atlanta has rich assets to support the life sciences industry, including medical schools, universities, a large cluster of life science businesses, and a highly educated workforce.
EJane Caraway, Director of Life Sciences, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was developed in partnership with the City of Johns Creek, the Fulton County Development Authority, the Metro Atlanta Chamber, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson welcomed the expansion and highlighted collaborative relationships between the state’s universities, technical college systems, and companies as inspiring innovation.
There are more than 2,200 clinical trials recruiting or underway in Georgia.
From 2014 through 2017, there were 547 patents awarded to applicants from Georgia for drugs and pharmaceuticals.
In 2021, Georgia exported $3.2 billion in immunological products and $1.9 billion in medical devices and pharmaceuticals.
source_title: Boston Scientific Expands Research Capabilities in Georgia | Georgia

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Daechang Seat Savannah Corp., a supplier specializing in automotive seat frames, will invest $72.5 million in the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.
The facility is located in close proximity to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA).
The project will create over 500 jobs.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that suppliers for the HMGMA project alone reach at least nine counties.
Governor Brian Kemp noted the growing manufacturing base is centered around Chatham County and the Savannah region.
Daechang Seat Corp. (DSC) was established in 1979 in Kyungju, South Korea.
DSC has become one of the largest seat frame producers in the world.
Globally, DSC employs more than 5,000 people across seven countries.
The company’s production process includes fully automatic lines with arc welding and spot-welding machines.
CEO Jinsuk Lee expressed heartfelt appreciation to the State of Georgia, its local communities, and the City of Savannah.
DSC maintains a prominent partnership with Hyundai Transys and Hyundai Mobis at HMGMA.
The company's primary objective is to drive growth through facility expansions to transform into a global entity.
The new facility will be located at the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.
The company will hire assembly operators, equipment technicians, mechanical engineers, and electrical engineers.
Hiring will occur through local job fairs and website postings.
Georgia Ports Authority President and CEO Griff Lynch stated DSC is only minutes from the Port of Savannah.
DSC is ideally located to take advantage of Georgia’s world-class connectivity for U.S. supply chains.

Savannah Economic Development Authority President and CEO Trip Tollison announced DSC is the third Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America supplier locating in the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.
The project brings 500 new jobs and $72.5 million in capital investment to Chatham County.
Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy Alyce Thornhill represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was developed in partnership with the Savannah Economic Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that countless partners are helping transform Georgia into a center of the automotive and mobility industry.
As the market transitions to electric vehicles, Georgia has pursued the entire supply chain.
Georgia has created more than $25 billion in investments and 30,000 jobs since 2018.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent when compared to FY21.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
Daechang Seat Co. recently ventured into the electric vehicle sector and continues to expand its scope.
DSC anticipates greater opportunities for growth with the completion of new manufacturing facilities, providing larger installation spaces.
The announcement was published on Oct. 23, 2023.
source_title: Automotive supplier Daechang Seat Savannah Corp. to create 500 jobs in Chatham County – Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority

facts: On October 14, 2025, RISE released results of a Childcare Demand Forecast and Market Analysis examining demand for childcare services across the eight-county region of Bryan, Bulloch, Candler, Chatham, Effingham, Evans, Liberty, and Screven.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) officially began production the week of October 1, 2024, ahead of its projected schedule and less than two years after breaking ground.
The first electric vehicle to roll off HMGMA's assembly line was an IONIQ 5.
HMGMA will produce several models of Hyundai, Genesis, and Kia vehicles at its Bryan County facility.

On August 13, 2024, contributions to the Well Mitigation Fund reached $1 million, with each of the Development Authority of Bulloch County, Development Authority of Bryan County, Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority, and Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America contributing $250,000.

On February 29, 2024, Governor Brian Kemp and First Lady Marty Kemp joined Hyundai Motor Company President and Global COO José Muñoz to celebrate Hyundai's impact on Georgia and confirm the facility would officially start vehicle production in the fourth quarter of 2024.

On December 8, 2023, automotive supplier Kyungshin America Corp. announced it would establish a new distribution facility in Rincon, creating an estimated 70 new jobs and over $22 million in investment in Effingham County.

On November 14, 2023, the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority released a Regional Workforce Study conducted by Wadley Donovan Gutshaw Consulting, LLC to identify labor supply challenges for Savannah region industrial employers and develop initiatives to minimize future supply shortfalls.
The November 2023 workforce study scope included a market analysis, identification of needs and challenges, and a strategic workforce plan.

On October 23, 2023, Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that automotive supplier Daechang Seat Savannah Corp. would invest $72.5 million in the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center to create over 500 jobs.
Daechang Seat Savannah Corp.'s new facility will be located in close proximity to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America and will specialize in automotive seat frames.

On September 6, 2023, Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that DAS Corp. would establish a new manufacturing facility in Metter, creating 300 new jobs and generating more than $35 million in investment in Candler County, involving collaborative indirect investments.

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Denkai America, the only North American manufacturer of high-quality electrodeposited (ED) copper foil used in printed electronics, will locate its new manufacturing facility and North American Headquarters in Richmond County.
The company’s initial investment will be $150 million to establish an ED copper foil production facility.
The facility will focus on electric vehicle (EV) battery foil.
The project is located on a 115-acre site.
The goal is to invest a total of $430 million over the next five years.
The goal includes creating 250 new jobs over the next five years.

Governor Kemp stated Georgia is proud to be selected as the home of Denkai America, further strengthening the state's status as a leader in the emerging electric mobility and existing aerospace, defense, and technology industries.
Governor Kemp emphasized the focus on bringing quality jobs to highly-trained Georgians across the state.
Governor Kemp highlighted Georgia’s top-ranking pro-business environment.
Denkai America’s materials have traditionally been used in printed circuit board (PCB) applications, primarily for the aerospace and defense industries.
The materials are now also used in lithium-ion batteries powering electric vehicles.
Denkai has operated for more than 60 years.
Denkai has team members worldwide with operations in Japan and North America.
Heday Nakajima, Ph.D., Denkai Group President and CEO, stated the investment highlights their commitment to leading ED battery foil manufacturing in North America for many years to come.
Heday Nakajima expressed gratitude to the teams in Richmond County and the State of Georgia for making the project possible.
Nobu Masuda, Denkai America’s President and CEO, stated they are thrilled to expand their manufacturing presence into Augusta.
Nobu Masuda noted Augusta has the skilled and talented workforce needed for company growth.
Construction is expected to begin in September 2022 as the first part of a three-phase investment.
The facility will be fully operational by December 2024.
The company will be hiring for skilled industrial technicians, production managers, and engineers.
Steven Kendrick, Chairman of the Augusta Economic Development Authority, stated the authority is excited to welcome Denkai America and its North American Headquarters to the Augusta Corporate Park.
Steven Kendrick noted they will bring high-skilled jobs to the growing list of companies in Augusta and the Augusta Corporate Park.
Steven Kendrick thanked the Augusta Commission and the Augusta Utilities Department for their support and hard work.
Barton Lowrey, Global Commerce Director of Corporate Solutions and Cyber Security at GDEcD, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development on this competitive project.
The project was in partnership with the Augusta Economic Development Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
Georgia Quick Start is a nationally ranked workforce training program by the Technical College System of Georgia.
Georgia Quick Start has provided specialized training tailored to business needs for more than 40 years.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that as electric mobility adoption becomes widespread, Georgia is committed to harnessing this opportunity to ensure Georgians in the automotive industry have access to future jobs at home.
Pat Wilson noted Denkai America plays a vital role in the EV ecosystem.

Pat Wilson highlighted Georgia’s commitment to building lasting international relationships driving economic partnerships across the state, including recruiting the wider electric mobility value chain.
Georgia’s prime location, extensive infrastructure, skilled workforce, and business-friendly climate have made it attractive for diverse industries focused on a sustainable future.
Georgia is cultivating a vertically integrated supply chain to help companies increase efficiencies by reducing reliance on imported materials.
EV-related projects have contributed more than $12.6 billion in investments to Georgia since 2020.
EV-related projects have contributed more than 17,800 new jobs to Georgia since 2020.
Augusta Corporate Park is a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certified park.
To earn GRAD certification, a property must undergo due diligence and complete specific requirements prior to a prospective business visit.
Completing the GRAD certification program with GDEcD helps catalyze economic growth and industrial development for regions.
Denkai America is a leader in manufacturing high-quality electrodeposited copper foils for PCB, industrial, and energy storage applications.
Denkai America delivers application-specific copper foils required by technology-leading companies.
Denkai America operates with the strength of domestic manufacturing backed by a global presence.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Copper Foil Manufacturer Denkai America to Locate New Manufacturing Facility, North American Headquarters in Richmond County | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: Global corporations throughout the clean-energy sector are spending billions to build facilities to manufacture a wide array of solar, e-mobility, and innovative battery products.
The State of Georgia ranks as a top-choice destination for these projects, which are literally creating tens of thousands of jobs in the state.
Georgia Quick Start is a 56-year-old program providing company-specific workforce training.
The program is routinely named the top state workforce development program in the country.
Quick Start has helped prepare more than 1.8 million Georgians for new jobs since its creation in 1967.
Companies benefiting from Quick Start include Rivian, Hyundai Motor Group, and SK Battery America.
Quick Start customizes training and replicates the actual processes that will be used to create a workforce ready to start production on Day One.
The program provides free-of-charge job training for qualified companies.
While utilized by a diverse group of manufacturing sectors, the majority of Quick Start's recent clients are clean energy companies and their suppliers.
In June 2023, the U.S. Secretary of Education spoke at Agnes Scott College and praised Quick Start as one of Georgia’s unique workforce-training assets.

Robert Howey was hired as a 'tabber operator' at the Hanwha Qcells solar manufacturing plant in Dalton to watch over machines that solder silicon wafers together, an early stage in making a solar module.
Before receiving Quick Start training, Howey felt nervous and uncertain about how his task fit into the overall process.
Quick Start provided guidance during a week of on-ramping, walking him through all steps of making a solar panel to give him confidence for his new career.
Howey described Quick Start as "a lifesaver."
Quick Start currently operates five training centers.
The program is expanding its advanced manufacturing center.
Quick Start is designing three new facilities for clean energy manufacturers and suppliers.

One new facility will be located in Bryan County, where Hyundai Motor Group is investing $7.59 billion in an electric vehicle (EV) and battery manufacturing "metaplant" with aggressive production timelines.
The state-of-the-art Quick Start facility in Bryan County will train 300 to 400 new Hyundai hires a week.
source_title: Quick Start: Meeting the Demands of Georgia’s Clean Energy Boom | Georgia

entity_name: U.S. Department of Energy Office of Electricity
canonical_name: U.S. Department of Energy Office of Electricity
facts: The U.S. Department of Energy’s Office of Electricity (OE) announced nine projects selected to receive approximately $20 million through its Flexible Innovative Transformer Technologies (FITT) funding opportunity announcement.
OE also announced eight winners of the Silicon Carbide (SiC) Packaging Prize Phase 1 who will receive $50,000 each in cash prizes from a $400,000 total prize pool.

The total $2.25 million SiC prize is awarded in three phases as part of the American-Made Challenges Program, which fosters collaboration between entrepreneurs, innovators, DOE’s National Labs, and the private sector.

Gene Rodrigues, Assistant Secretary for the Office of Electricity, stated that the selected projects address advanced technology and supply chain challenges to drive innovation for the 21st century grid.
Advanced transformers and semiconductor packaging will help the power grid better serve residents and businesses by ensuring resilient, reliable, secure, and affordable electricity.
Transformers regulate voltage levels as power enters homes and businesses and are critical for ensuring reliability and security of power delivery.
Selected FITT projects address technical challenges of advancing transformers through research, development, and demonstration across distribution to transmission scale applications.
DOE’s Office of Electricity and the Office of Cybersecurity, Energy Security, and Emergency Response (CESER) co-fund the FITT FOA, contributing $18 million and $2 million respectively.

Clemson University in Clemson, SC received $2.47 million for the Smart Hybrid transformer for Abnormal Power Events (SHAPE) project, which integrates medium voltage power electronics and battery storage into a distribution transformer.

Eaton Corporation in Menomonee Falls, WI received $2.94 million to research and test a Compact and Reliable Voltage Regulating Hybrid Transformer for utility distribution networks and industrial sites.

Electric Power Research Institute, Inc. in Palo Alto, CA received $2.88 million for Solid State PowerHub Development and Demonstration to accelerate commercialization of an innovative solid-state transformer accommodating input voltage variations.

Electric Research and Manufacturing Cooperative, Inc. in Dyerberg, TN received $1.84 million for the Distribution Transformer Extender (DTE) Project to develop devices using power electronics to combine solid-state transformer functionality with small battery energy storage.

GE Vernova Operations LLC in Schenectady, NY received $1.99 million for Full-scale Alternating Current Solid-State Transformer Demonstration (FASST) to develop and deploy a one megavolt-amperes 3-winding solid-state transformer to a public utility-owned substation.

GE Vernova Operations LLC in Schenectady, NY received $2.99 million for topic area 2 to design and deploy a single-phase 2-winding power transformer and solid-state tap changer enabling dynamic load balancing, power flow control, and fault current limiting.

Resilient Power Systems, Inc in Austin, TX received $1.47 million for Direct Current-as-a-Service SolidFlex Power Hub to develop and demonstrate solid-state transformer technology enhancing efficiency, reliability, and flexibility in power distribution.

Transforma Energy, Inc. in Albuquerque, NM received $1.45 million for Drop-In Replacement Electronically Controlled Transformer (DIRECT) proposing a solid-state transformer architecture to increase system robustness across voltage and power ratings.

University of Pittsburgh in Pittsburgh, PA received $2.5 million for Enabling Technology and Grid-Tied Testbed for Efficient, Flexible, Intelligent Distribution Transformers to leverage industry partners for evaluating and maturing next-generation intelligent distribution transformers.
Silicon carbide is used as raw material for semiconductors because it can withstand high electricity voltage fluctuations and temperatures.

SiC Prize Phase 1 winners will design, build, and test state-of-the-art SiC semiconductor packaging prototypes to enable devices to work more effectively in high-voltage environments such as energy storage.
Board Breakers from Fargo, North Dakota is creating a replacement for traditional power electronic modules using additive manufacturing to print 3D ceramic packaging.
LincolnX from Lincoln, Nebraska plans to develop novel ultrafast and scalable SiC modules featuring dual orthogonal cooling to meet prize goals and metrics.
Marel Power Solutions from Plymouth, Michigan plans to make packaging improvements through thermal management, three-dimensional mechatronic design, and a scalable arrangement of power switches.

NC Solar Inverters from Cary, North Carolina will utilize a designed symmetric layout to maximize parasitic flying capacitance and minimize parasitic inductance using commercially available topside cooled discrete devices.
NoMIS-Lux-QPT-UA from Albany, New York will combine technology and products in Smart Metal Core SiC power blocks to create high voltage chip-scale packaging.

Stony Brook Power Packaging Team from Stony Brook, New York will develop high-voltage, high-current, fast-switching, and cost-effective modules and create a business entity for engineering sampling and commercialization.
Superior SiC Power Module Team from Gainesville, Florida plans to develop an interdisciplinary approach for SiC power modules with high-speed, high-energy efficiency, and low EMI.
Team Raiju - University of Arkansas from Fayetteville, Arkansas will embed 128 SiC die in LTCC controlled by an active dV/dt voltage balancer and cooled with integrated microchannel busbars.
In Phase 2, winning teams from Phase 1 will develop a physical prototype meeting Phase 2 metrics and send it to a national lab for testing validation.
At the end of Phase 2, up to four winning teams will receive $250,000 each and become eligible to compete in Phase 3.
source_title: Energy Department Advances U.S. Electric Grid Resilience and Reliability with New Transformer and Silicon Carbide Packaging Projects | Department of Energy
