facts: Carpool Logistics was cofounded by Michael Malakhov and Eric Morris and housed in Atlanta Tech Village (ATV), a tech hub that functions as an incubator, accelerator, and coworking space for entrepreneurs.
ATV offers month-to-month leases requiring no upfront capital, which was a key consideration for the cofounders who had just quit their day jobs.
David Cummings sold his marketing automation company Pardot and created ATV in 2012.
Atlanta Ventures, the venture capital arm of ATV, invested $2 million in Carpool Logistics in 2022.
The seed round allowed Carpool, which was just a year old at the time, to boost its customer base and staff.
Carpool Logistics aims to optimize vehicle shipping by enabling multiple customers including consumers, dealers, carmakers, and auction houses to ship cars on one truck.
Combining smaller shipments saves money for customers and helps lower emissions by reducing the number of partially filled trucks on the road.
Carpool’s tech platform allows customers to track their shipments.
Atlanta has a major stake in the supply-chain economy and hosts Cox Automotive, which owns Autotrader and Dealer.com among other brands.
Georgia serves as a logistics hub especially for cars, with the majority of imported vehicles in the U.S. coming into the Port of Brunswick and Jacksonville.
Automotive supply chains are moving down to the South, particularly when it comes to electric vehicles.
Costs to launch a business and for owners and employees to live in Georgia are much lower than on the West Coast.
In 2021, startups in Georgia raised more than $4 billion, setting a new record that beat the previous $2 billion set in 2020.
Atlanta is the state’s epicenter for tech startups while other emerging hubs include logistics and supply chain in Savannah, fintech and robotics in Columbus, and ag tech in Macon and Middle Georgia.
Technology Association of Georgia (TAG) president and CEO Larry Williams notes the state has a balanced portfolio of companies and industry sectors which helps overall during economic slowdowns.

Some layoffs occurred in 2022 at fast-growing startups including highly touted unicorns valued at $1 billion or more like cybersecurity firm OneTrust and digital experience intelligence company FullStory.

TAG president Larry Williams says founding and growing a company outside Atlanta is harder because angel and early-stage investors are concentrated there but it makes sense to go where the action is for specific sectors like ag tech.
ATDC (Advanced Technology Development Center) was founded in 1980 as one of the oldest incubators in the country and has graduated more than 190 companies.

Headquartered at Georgia Tech but funded by the state, ATDC serves as the state’s official incubator with staff in Athens, Augusta, Savannah, multiple spots in Metro Atlanta, Macon, Columbus, and Albany.
Ben Andrews serves as ATDC’s statewide program manager and notes the organization wants to make it possible to create startups where people are living rather than forcing everyone into big cities.
It makes sense for logistics companies to be in Savannah and cybersecurity startups in Augusta, with Atlanta companies often having satellite offices in other Georgia cities.
Being close to customers can be as important as being close to investors, especially for startups that make sense to be near rural areas where their customers are.
ATDC offers tiered resources starting with ATDC Educate for basics like customer discovery and financial literacy, followed by ATDC Accelerate for early-stage companies.
ATDC Accelerate companies must have at least one full-time and one part-time person dedicated to the business, a minimally viable product, and may be pre-revenue seeking initial funding.
ATDC Signature companies have business models and leadership teams in place, scalable products or services, and industry-changing technology or intellectual property.
Carpool Logistics is an ATDC Signature company that joined because ATDC built out a new supply-chain vertical.
Most startups at ATV are probably SaaS-platform related software-as-a-service companies that deliver applications over the web.
There are usually between 30 and 40 Signature companies in the incubator at a time.

Startups graduate from ATDC after hitting $1 million in annual recurring revenue from the product side for two years in a row, with a little over 90% still in business or having successful exit events five years later.
Do Process is an HR tech company growing in South Georgia and Albany that uses video to optimize employee onboarding and training.
Cofounder Blake Cook describes Do Process as like a learning-management system and TikTok had a baby, mapping out company processes into video-based formats for faster training and access.
Using written employee manuals is inefficient for employers and employees especially for small businesses according to Cook.
Do Process opened an Atlanta office at ATDC but remains Albany-based with cofounders Blake Cook and Tracy Goode being Albany natives.
Cook notes having a tech company in Albany is an advantage because they see where the biggest needs are including people struggling to hire and train.
Zach Hodges cofounded Cyber Security Solutions in Tampa in 2015 with two other Air Force veteran cofounders before opening a satellite office in Augusta’s Georgia Cyber Innovation & Training Center.
The startup moved after hearing an announcement at the Augusta airport name-checking the city as the soon-to-be cyber capital of the world.
Cyber Security Solutions grew by adding employees using a grant program that provides salary reimbursements for three to six months.
The company partnered with the Georgia Cyber Center on the Georgia AI Manufacturing (GA-AIM) grant project designed to speed integration of AI technologies in industries across the state.
The GA-AIM grant and project is led by the Georgia Tech Research Corporation with a number of initiatives.

The Cyber Center’s role is to perform cybersecurity assessments for small, underserved rural manufacturing companies according to Rob Dennis executive director of corporate relations for Augusta University.
Cyber Security Solutions leverages knowledge gained doing cybersecurity assessments for companies contracting with the U.S. Department of Defense to partner with the center.

They will work with Augusta University and Augusta Technical College to train students to do streamlined assessments using technology that scans configurations so human questions focus on physical and personnel security.
The Cyber Center does not house an incubator but Clubhou.se a coworking space aiming to encourage interaction among entrepreneurs has space in the center.

Michael Shaffer executive vice president strategic partnerships and economic development for the Georgia Cyber Center says conversations are ongoing about creating an entrepreneurship center in downtown Augusta in partnership with Augusta Tech.
The plan is to take advantage of everything from university IP to the talent pool that comes from Fort Gordon.
Michael Shaffer states the Georgia Cyber Innovation and Training Center showed what an ecosystem can do and how important it is to build something.
Coming off the 2021 record year, Georgia saw a decrease in venture capital investment of about 50% in 2022 with companies raising $2.25 billion.
TAG president Larry Williams says investors are currently in implementation mode focusing on profitability rather than growth but always look for good quality deal flow.
Providing great companies and deal flow will help Georgia’s venture capital funds grow locally and attract more venture capital from around the country to keep tech growing.
source_title: Growing Tech in Georgia - Georgia Trend Magazine

entity_name: Hyundai Motor Group and SK On Georgia EV Battery Facility
canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Group / SK On Georgia EV Battery Joint Venture
facts: Hyundai Motor Group (HMG) and South Korean battery maker SK On have selected a site in Bartow County, Georgia, for a new electric vehicle (EV) battery manufacturing facility that will supply HMG’s plants in the United States.
The project is considered one of the largest economic development projects in Georgia state history.
Stakeholders estimate the project will create more than 3500 new jobs through approximately US$4 billion to US$5 billion of investment in Bartow County.
HMG and SK On recently signed a memorandum of understanding (MOU) regarding the partnership for a new EV battery facility in the US, with details of the partnership still in development.
SK On was established in 2021 as the lithium-ion battery subsidiary branch of SK Innovation.
SK On currently employs more than 2000 people at its SK Battery America facility in Commerce, Georgia.
Bartow County Sole Commissioner Steve Taylor stated that Bartow County is very pleased with the decision of Hyundai Motor Group and SK On.
Commissioner Steve Taylor thanked the owners of the former Bartow Centre Industrial property and noted they pursued this extraordinary economic development project together.
Commissioner Steve Taylor stated the project will have regional benefit for job seekers in the area and establishes Bartow in Georgia’s EV ecosystem in a significant way.

Commissioner Steve Taylor highlighted that with Bartow’s strong history and talent-base built upon advanced manufacturing, the automotive industry, and innovation, the battery partnership should thrive in this environment.
Commissioner Steve Taylor expressed looking forward to welcoming HMG and SK in meaningful ways in the community and enjoying a long-term partnership together.
Director of Korean Investment Yoonie Kim represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The GDEcD partnered with Georgia EMC, Development Authority of Bartow County, the Cartersville-Bartow County Department of Economic Development, and Georgia Quick Start on the project.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that HMG and SK have been pioneering partners for Georgia for decades as one of the major drivers for Georgia’s automotive renaissance and as the first Korean manufacturer to locate in the state, respectively.

Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that by supporting cooperation and partnerships across Georgia's growing EV ecosystem, they are creating a fully integrated supply chain for automotive OEMs while connecting battery manufacturers with recyclers to close the loop on battery manufacturing.
Commissioner Pat Wilson expressed excitement for the jobs of the future this will create for Bartow County and northwest Georgia, and gratitude for support from community and utility partners.

Georgia’s prime location, major ports system, extensive infrastructure, skilled workforce, and pro-business climate have made it an attractive location for a diverse array of rapidly developing industries focused on creating a sustainable future.
Building on the assets that make the automotive industry successful, Georgia is positioned as a hub for the electric transportation industry.
Excluding this announcement, EV-related projects announced in Georgia since 2020 total approximately US$17 billion in investment and more than 22,800 new jobs in Georgia.
HMG separately announced in May its plans to invest US$5.54 billion in Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, an EV manufacturing facility in Bryan County.
HMG broke ground on the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America facility in October.
source_title: Hyundai, SK ON For Georgia EV Manufacturing Facility – Energy Transition Insider

canonical_name: Georgia AI Manufacturing (GA-AIM) Technology Corridor Grant
facts: Aaron Stebner and Tom Kurfess are among the leaders of GA-AIM, a $65 million grant that focuses on manufacturing and AI in the state of Georgia.
Aaron Stebner outlined an aggressive plan for artificial intelligence and manufacturing when he applied for a faculty position in 2019.

In his cover letter, Stebner promised to establish the Georgia Institute of Technology as a world leader in additive manufacturing of solid materials (ceramics & metals) R&D, especially in the fusion of data sciences and AI to create new, world-leading technologies.
Stebner originally thought it would take 10-15 years of incremental steps and funding to achieve his goal.
The project is funded by a $65 million grant from the U.S. Department of Commerce’s Economic Development Administration.
The grant was announced by President Joe Biden.
Stebner's plan will begin to become a reality two years after he arrived on campus.
The initiative covers the entire state of Georgia and all of its manufacturing sectors, ranging from agriculture to airplanes.
This $65 million grant is the largest of the nine projects within the larger Georgia AI Manufacturing (GA-AIM) technology corridor grant.
The grant will allow Stebner and Georgia Tech to transform the Advanced Manufacturing Pilot Facility (AMPF) into the Artificial Intelligence Manufacturing Pilot Facility (AI-MPF).
The 24,000 square-foot facility is located on 14th Street.
The facility will more than double in size after Georgia Tech and statewide GA-AIM partners were selected as one of 21 Phase II awardees.
The selection was part of the $1 billion Build Back Better Regional Challenge (BBB) competition.
The BBB competition is part of the Investing in America’s Communities initiative under the American Rescue Plan Act of 2021.
AMPF had previously been a shell waiting for funding to fill it out, according to Stebner.
Aaron Stebner is an associate professor at the George W. Woodruff School of Mechanical Engineering and the School of Materials Science and Engineering.
The transformed facility will be one of the nation’s first manufacturing labs designed for autonomy.
The goal of the new lab is to help the state and the nation become world AI manufacturing leaders.

links.context: Link to read the entire story

source_title: $65M Grant to Build AI Manufacturing in Georgia | News Center

facts: GSC Steel Stamping, LLC is a minority- and family-owned automotive parts manufacturer.
The company has acquired Dixien, LLC's assets.
GSC Steel Stamping continues a 43-year history of automotive parts stamping.
The company is led by CEO Vincent Henderson, who is known for turning around award-winning operations.
GSC is committed to excellence, investing in technology, and maintaining a highly experienced staff.
The company operates a 200,000 sq. ft. facility located in Lake City, GA.
GSC's Lake City, GA facility is claimed to be the largest minority-owned automotive stamping plant globally.
The press release was published on January 30, 2024.
source_title: GSC Steel Stamping Press Releases & News - PR.com

entity_name: Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc. Georgia Plant
canonical_name: Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc. Georgia Plant
facts: Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc. Georgia Plant is located at 1000 Unisia Dr, Monroe, GA 30655.
The facility operates 24 hours a day, seven days a week.
The plant holds an overall rating of 2.9 stars based on 33 reviews.
One employee reports working in the VDP department under supervisor Shannon Walker.
Workers have reported being asked to work a 7 days a week schedule for 12 hours a day, despite their official schedule being 4 days a week with 10 hour shifts.
Multiple reviews cite management issues, including claims that leadership lied about compensation and does not appreciate hard work.
One reviewer states that management fails to intervene when fights break out between employees and only sends the non-initiating person to handle it.
A reviewer claims the workforce consists of "ghetto people" while criticizing management's handling of workplace conflicts.
The business directory classifies the Monroe location as a manufacturer.
source_title: Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc. Georgia Plant in Monroe, GA 30655  - 770-207...

entity_name: Japanese Companies and Investors in Georgia
canonical_name: Japanese Companies and Investors in Georgia
facts: Georgia hosted a blockbuster reception in Tokyo in October to mark a half-century of engagement through its economic development office there.
Japan Day at the Capitol was a half-day celebration held on Tuesday of the economic and cultural impact of Japanese companies in Georgia.
Japanese companies are responsible for an estimated 40,000 jobs in Georgia.
The Consulate General of Japan opened in Atlanta in 1974.
Then-Gov. Jimmy Carter decided to put a physical presence in Japan in response to budding investment from companies like YKK and Murata.
Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson highlighted how Japanese firms have spread to "every single corner" of Georgia.
Current Gov. Brian Kemp praised Consul General Mio Maeda and his team for their persistent presence in the state.
Gov. Kemp presented the country’s top diplomat in the Southeast U.S. with a proclamation.
International investment accounted for 47 percent of the jobs and 65 percent of the capital pledged to Georgia in the latest fiscal year, according to Gov. Kemp.
Georgia broke its previous record for total trade in 2022 by some $5 billion, reaching $196 billion in imports and exports.
Consul General Mio Maeda was presented with resolutions in both the House and Senate celebrating the 50th anniversary of the consulate.
The event was part of activities organized by the Japanese community in partnership with the Georgia-Japan Legislative Caucus.
The event included exhibition tables featuring products and brochures from Georgia-invested Japanese companies.
Guests received bottles of made-in-Georgia Marukan rice vinegar and Yakult yogurt drinks, which will soon be manufactured in a $300 million-plus factory in Cartersville.
An orange Kubota vehicle was displayed outside the Capitol.
An Indy Car sponsored by Hitachi Astemo and a Yamaha side-by-side were displayed under a white tent.
Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that each job created represents hope and opportunity for a family in the state.
Consul General Mio Maeda credited the companies and the Japanese community they’ve helped build for the staying power of the consulate.

Consul General Mio Maeda offered condolences to victims of the earthquake in western Japan’s Ishikawa prefecture that killed more than 200 and left more than 20,000 people displaced on New Year’s day.
Gov. Brian Kemp echoed the sentiment regarding the earthquake.

Key members of the Georgia-Japan Legislative Caucus offered remarks, including State Rep. Teri Anulewicz (D-Smyrna), State Sen. Kay Kirkpatrick (R-Cobb) and State Rep. David Knight (R-Griffin), who chairs the group.

Students from the International Charter Academy of Georgia, a state charter school in Gwinnett that offers dual immersion in Japanese and English, closed the day with a song on the outside steps of the capitol building.

Former Gov. Nathan Deal was on hand for the event and was set to be honored during a conferment ceremony for his Order of the Rising Sun designation during a reception at Consul General Maeda’s residence later in the evening.
The Order of the Rising Sun decoration, which recognizes contributions to the U.S.-Japan relationship, was announced in December.

Japanese companies with a presence at the Capitol included Marukan Vinegar Inc., Miura America Co., Ltd., Mizuno USA, MUFG Bank, TOTO USA, Asahi Biosciences, Inc., Canon Solutions America, Inc., Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc., Hoshizaki America, Kubota Manufacturing of America Corp., Shimizu America, Yakult USA, Yamaha Motor Corp. U.S.A, Yamato Transport U.S.A., Inc., and YKK.
source_title: 'Japan Day' at Capitol Celebrates Impact of Japanese Investors, Consulate’s 50 Years in Georgia - Global Atlanta

facts: Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas Inc. is a global supplier of automotive parts.
Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas Inc. announced a massive, multi-year expansion of its manufacturing plant in Monroe, Georgia.
The Monroe facility is located approximately 45 miles east of Atlanta.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp announced on Friday, May 17 that Hitachi will invest anywhere from $100 million to $330 million in expanding its existing facility.
The expansion project is expected to bring an additional 100 jobs to Walton County.
Hitachi's parent company, Hitachi Automotive Systems Ltd., is headquartered in Tokyo.
Hitachi Automotive Systems Ltd. manufactures engine powertrain systems, electric powertrain systems, and integrated vehicle control systems.
Hitachi opened its first facility in Georgia in 1997.
Hitachi currently employs more than 900 people in the Peach State (Georgia).
Hitachi is the largest employer in Walton County.
The expansion project will be unveiled in phases over the next five years.
Parts produced at the Monroe plant will continue to be sold to major automotive companies including Ford, General Motors, Honda, and Nissan.
Hitachi cited larger growth in the automotive industry and the strength of the local workforce as key incentives behind the expansion project.

Pat Wilson, commissioner of the Georgia Department of Economic Development, stated that partnering with a company like Hitachi helps bring jobs and investment opportunities to Georgia while taking an active role in the community.
A timeline for the initial groundbreaking of the project was not released.
source_title: Hitachi Automotive Undertakes $100M Plant Expansion in Monroe, Georgia - REBusinessOnline

facts: Tim Clark has been appointed CEO and president of Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc.
Clark is a senior vice president and executive officer of Hitachi Astemo, Ltd.
Clark previously served as president of Hitachi Astemo Europe, based in Munich.
Paul Carroll recently was named global account manager for Hitachi America, Ltd., succeeding Clark.
Clark previously served as vice president of sales and marketing for Hitachi Automotive Systems in Farmington Hills, Michigan.
Clark previously was general manager in charge of international sales for Hitachi Automotive Systems based in Tokyo.
Prior to joining Hitachi in 2003, Clark was general manager of Unisia North America.
Clark previously held executive positions at Nissan North America and with Mazda in Japan.
Clark is fluent in Japanese and holds a bachelor’s degree in Japanese Studies from Brigham Young University.
Hitachi Astemo Americas has more than 20 manufacturing plants, research-and-development centers and sales offices in the U.S., Canada, Mexico and Brazil.
Hitachi Astemo was created in 2021 by the merger of Hitachi Automotive Systems with three former Honda affiliates: Nissin Kogyo, Keihin and Showa.
Hitachi Astemo is a joint venture between Hitachi, Ltd. and Honda Motor Co. headquartered in Tokyo.
Hitachi Astemo develops, manufactures, sells, and services automotive and transportation components as well as industrial machinery and systems.
Hitachi Astemo Americas manufactures and markets engine management, electric powertrain and integrated vehicle controls for major automotive manufacturers worldwide.
Hitachi Astemo adheres to ISO/TS:16949:2009 industry standards.
source_title: Clark Named CEO, President of Hitachi Astemo Americas - The BRAKE Report

facts: Hitachi Astemo is a global automotive technology company formed in 2021 by the merger of Hitachi Automotive Systems with Honda’s Keihin, Showa and Nissin Kogyo groups.
The company has approximately 90,000 employees.
Hitachi Astemo holds leadership positions in AD/ADAS, software and powertrain, as well as chassis and motorcycle systems.
Its Americas headquarters is located in Farmington Hills, Michigan.
Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc. manufactures and markets engine management, electric powertrain and integrated vehicle controls for major automotive manufacturers worldwide.
The company adheres to ISO/TS:16949:2009 industry standards.
Hitachi Astemo is a technology partner with Chevrolet in the development of its highly successful INDYCAR engine program.
Hitachi Astemo provides fuel-system components for all of Chevrolet's 2.2-liter, twin-turbocharged INDYCAR racing engines.
source_title: News Releases : July 27, 2022 : Hitachi Astemo, Ltd.

facts: Hitachi Astemo (Astemo), a leading automotive industry supplier, continues its NTT INDYCAR SERIES partnership with Team Penske for a 14th consecutive year in 2025 as a sponsor of the No. 2 Dallara/Chevrolet driven by Josef Newgarden.
Astemo supplies fuel-system components for all of the 2.2-liter twin-turbocharged Chevrolet race engines in the series.
Hitachi Astemo is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan, and is a joint venture between Hitachi, Ltd., Honda Motor Co., and JIC Capital, Ltd.
The company has 80,000 employees worldwide with operations in the Americas, Asia, China, Europe, and Japan.
Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc. manufactures and markets engine management, electric powertrain, and integrated vehicle controls for major automotive manufacturers worldwide.
Rob Sharpe serves as senior vice president of sales and marketing at Hitachi Astemo Americas.
source_title: Hitachi Astemo Continues INDYCAR Partnership with Team Penske and Chevrolet - Roastbrief US

entity_name: Hitachi Automotive Electric Motor Systems America, Inc.
canonical_name: Hitachi Automotive Electric Motor Systems America, Inc.
facts: The Hitachi Automotive Electric Motor Systems America, Inc. plant in Berea plans to manufacture motors for electric vehicles.
Production could begin between April 2022 and March 2023, in part based on the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic.
The operation currently employs 18 people and is expected to grow to approximately 200 in 2023.
Established in Kentucky in March 2020, Hitachi Automotive Electric Motor Systems America will supply motors to Honda of America Manufacturing, Inc.

Shingo Nakamura, president of Hitachi Automotive Electric Motor Systems America, Inc., stated that the company is pleased to establish this new business in Berea, where Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas has forged a robust, ongoing relationship with the local community and state officials over the years.

Shingo Nakamura noted that the new venture is a critical piece in strengthening Hitachi’s foundation in the fast-growing electric vehicle market and supporting the revitalization of the economy in Kentucky through job creation in response to COVID-19.

Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear stated that the new venture, along with the jobs and wages it will create, adds true momentum to economic recovery and points to a bright future as they build a better Kentucky.

Gov. Andy Beshear emphasized that from an automotive workforce and industry perspective, it is crucial that Kentucky continues to attract forward-looking projects as the auto industry undergoes a years-long tectonic shift to electric vehicles.
The new subsidiary joins more than 500 other automotive suppliers and four automotive assembly plants operating in Kentucky.
Kentucky’s geographic position in the middle of the country’s automotive corridor provides suppliers quick access to dozens of automotive assembly plants within a day’s drive.
In Kentucky, Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas operates four facilities.
Two of these facilities are manufacturing plants in Berea making brake and suspension components.
The other two facilities are manufacturing and warehousing operations in Harrodsburg producing electronic control systems, actuators and fuel system components.

Mike Williams, president and CEO of Blue Grass Energy, stated that his company is proud to supply electricity for Hitachi Automotive Electric Motor Systems America, Inc. in Berea and is excited to see the development of electric vehicle technology and production in their service area.
Mike Williams added that their community is fortunate to count Hitachi as one of their own.
On April 1, 2021, Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas, Inc. will change its name to Hitachi Astemo Americas with its subsidiaries also changing their names to follow the new naming convention.
source_title: Hitachi’s electric future - Kentucky Living

facts: Foresight announced signing an agreement with Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc. (“Hitachi Astemo”) to test and evaluate autonomous driving vision systems (ADAS).
Hitachi Astemo is a global Tier One supplier of engine management, electric powertrain and integrated vehicle-control systems for the auto industry.
The paid joint proof of concept project (POC) with Hitachi Astemo calls for the evaluation and testing of simulated and real-life autonomous driving scenarios.
The program will pair Foresight QuadSight® and Mono2Stereo™ technologies with existing Hitachi Astemo camera systems in an effort to improve distance measurement and object detection.
The project with Hitachi Astemo follows the successful evaluation of a QuadSight vision system prototype reported by the Company in March 2021.

Foresight’s proprietary Mono2Stereo software-based solution uses the overlapping views of existing cameras with different fields of vision to create a three-dimensional stereo-vision system to improve object detection.
Haim Siboni, CEO of Foresight, stated the company is excited to be involved in another auto industry POC project.

Siboni noted that being chosen by Hitachi Astemo underlines the importance of stereoscopic technology in the enhancement of existing ADAS systems and its ability to amplify performance, provide better distance accuracy, and offer more robust active safety features.
Foresight’s solutions can be readily deployed in vehicles equipped with Level 2 and Level 2-plus autonomy systems without requiring additional hardware and design changes.
Siboni believes that teaming up with Hitachi Astemo could potentially lead to further collaboration with other automotive OEMs.
source_title: Foresight, Hitachi Astemo Americas to Test and Evaluate Autonomous Driving Systems - Self Drive News

facts: Hollingsworth & Vose (H&V) is a global materials company focused on filtration and energy storage solutions.
The company uses advanced materials across industries to support cleaner, healthier, and more sustainable environments.
It operates from 13 facilities worldwide, with a footprint across the Americas, Europe, and Asia to meet global and regional customer needs efficiently and cost-effectively.
The company emphasizes research and development and innovation to raise standards for filtration and separator materials.
Applications for its advanced materials include cleanrooms, indoor air quality, drinking water protection, face masks and protective gear, battery separators, and start-stop engine systems.
The company is headquartered at 112 Washington Street, East Walpole, Massachusetts.
Hollingsworth & Vose employs between 501 and 1000 people.
Recent activity includes expanding manufacturing capacity at its Mysuru, India plant.
The company launched PlusZero PFAS-free products.
H&V partnered with ArcActive on the GEM battery plate architecture.
The GEM battery plate architecture earned the 2026 BCI Innovation Award recognition.
source_title: Hollingsworth & Vose Company Overview, Contact Details & Competitors | LeadIQ

facts: Hollingsworth & Vose is a global leader in filtration, battery separator and energy storage solutions.

The company is family-owned for seven generations, with origins dating back to 1728 when the “Act for the Encouragement of Making Paper” was passed by the General Court of the Province of Massachusetts Bay.
Hollingsworth & Vose’s advanced materials contribute to powering hybrid, start-stop and electric vehicles.
The company’s battery separator and energy storage solutions are helping the world transition to a more sustainable future.
Hollingsworth & Vose’s filtration solutions are used in vehicles and heavy-duty equipment, among other applications like clean rooms, hospitals, computers, commercial buildings, and homes.
Hollingsworth & Vose operates 13 manufacturing and nine research sites strategically located in the Americas, Europe, and Asia.
The company employs more than 2,200 people globally, including more than 40 PhDs on its research and development team.
Hollingsworth & Vose holds more than 280 patents and applications related to filtration and separator materials.
The company is headquartered at its plant site in East Walpole, Massachusetts, USA.
source_title: Company | Hollingsworth & Vose

facts: Hollingsworth & Vose is investing over $40 million to expand its operation in Floyd County, Virginia.
The expansion will create 25 jobs.
The funds will be used to add over 28,000 square feet to the company's facility.
The facility is located at 365 Christiansburg Pike Northeast in Floyd County, Virginia.
The added space will make room for new production equipment to meet increased demand.
Governor Glenn Youngkin stated that Hollingsworth & Vose has generated positive economic impact and job opportunities in Floyd County for more than four decades.
Governor Glenn Youngkin noted that the significant investment further solidifies the company’s commitment to Virginia.
Governor Glenn Youngkin highlighted that businesses with a long history of expansion in the Commonwealth offer powerful testimonials on why a Virginia location is a foundation for success.
Josh Ayer, CEO of Hollingsworth & Vose, stated the company has been part of the Floyd, Virginia community since 1976.
Josh Ayer said the facility is essential to serving both global and domestic customers.
Josh Ayer explained that Virginia was chosen for the expansion due to its positive business environment and strong support from the Commonwealth of Virginia and Floyd County.
Hollingsworth & Vose is a global manufacturer of advanced materials used in filtration, battery, and industrial appliances.
source_title: Hollingsworth & Vose expanding operation in Floyd County

facts: Hollingsworth & Vose is a global manufacturer of advanced materials used in filtration, battery and industrial applications.
The company operates manufacturing and research & development facilities in the Americas, Europe, China and India.
H&V's battery separators went to support medical equipment and contribute to backup power sources for emergency vehicles, hospitals and other facilities.
H&V’s materials can be found in batteries used mainly in telecom and hybrid vehicle applications.
H&V’s materials can be found in filters for cars, trucks and heavy-duty equipment.
Hollingsworth & Vose became a leading supplier of automotive start-stop battery separator material just a few years ago.

Hollingsworth & Vose recently announced several distribution partnerships, with I.W. Tremont and Superior Felt & Filtration, to expand the reach of the Trupor microporous filter media both in the U.S. and globally.
Trupor enables enhanced flow compared to traditional membranes, which improves the ability to filter novel liquid filter streams.
H&V is developing new filtration solutions that provide better air quality with lower energy consumption.
Filtration will continue to play a huge role as we continue to move toward more efficient cars, hybrids and EVs.
Josh Ayer serves as Hollingsworth & Vose’s president and chief operating officer.
H&V operates 13 R&D and manufacturing facilities around the world.
The company is family-owned for seven generations, with origins dating back to 1728.
Hollingsworth & Vose is headquartered in East Walpole, Massachusetts USA.
source_title: More than 200 years in, Hollingsworth & Vose continues its pursuit of a ‘cleaner, healthier, more sustainable world’ - International Filtration News

facts: Hollingsworth & Vose Company is a leading global manufacturer of advanced materials used in filtration, battery, and industrial applications.
The company has been in business for over 170 years.
Its products are used in a wide range of industries, including healthcare, automotive, aerospace, and energy.
The product portfolio includes filter media, battery separator materials, and industrial fabrics.
The company specializes in producing high-performance materials that offer superior strength, durability, and reliability.
Products are designed to meet the specific needs of customers for applications including air and liquid filtration, energy storage, and industrial manufacturing.
Hollingsworth & Vose offers value-added services including technical support, testing and analysis, and custom design services.
The company works closely with customers to understand unique needs and offer tailored solutions.
It operates state-of-the-art research and development facilities to innovate and develop new products.
Manufacturing capabilities include advanced weaving, coating, and lamination technologies.
These manufacturing capabilities enable the production of materials to the highest quality standards.
The company maintains a global network of sales and distribution partners to ensure worldwide product availability.
source_title: Hollingsworth & Vose Company | Air Filters

facts: Is a supplier of advanced materials for filtration, battery separator, and industrial applications.

Offers a range of filtration media, battery separators, and several industrial products to various industries such as transportation, indoor air quality, industrial and manufacturing, heavy equipment, energy generation and storage, apparel and home furnishings, and aerospace.
Offers its products under various brands including Technostat, ViatMat, NanoWave, Hovowipe, Capaceon, PerForm, NanoWebANOWEB, EnergyGuard, Hi-Sep, PowerFill, PowerLock, TruporHovoliner, and Hovotex.
Operates manufacturing sites and research and development centers in the Americas, Asia, and Europe.
Is headquartered in East Walpole, Massachusetts, the US.

facts: Honda works to build the future of mobility in communities all across North America.
Honda provides tens of thousands of jobs that help power the economy.
In 2025, more than 60% of Honda vehicles sold in America were manufactured in the U.S. using globally and locally-sourced parts and materials.
Honda was the first international automaker capable of complete product creation in the U.S.
Honda entered North America in 1979 with its first facility in Marysville, Ohio, becoming the first Japanese automaker to establish operations there.
Honda operates a Power Equipment Headquarters, Auto Zone Finance Parts Center, and Honda Rider Education Center in Alpharetta, GA.

facts: During the Year, revenue amounted to HK$5,609 million; gross profit amounted to HK$1,375 million with a gross margin of 24.5%; net profit was adjusted to HK$49.5 million with a net profit margin of 0.9%.
Total assets reached HK$13.3 billion (a year-on-year increase of 11.2%), with net cash of HK$1.32 billion.
The Group operates three core business segments: die-casting, injection molding, and CNC machining centre.
Die-casting machinery revenue was HK$3,663 million (65.3% of total), while injection molding generated HK$1,798 million (32.1%) and CNC machining centre generated HK$148 million (2.6%).
R&D expenses reached HK$318 million during the Year, representing a year-on-year increase of 24.6%.
The Group commercialised the world's first 16,000T ultra-large intelligent die-casting unit and continues to secure and deliver multiple 9000T-class ultra-large die casting machines.

Its 6,000T–16,000T ultra-large intelligent die-casting cells integrate digital twin technology with the LK-NET cloud management system and were selected as one of the 'Outstanding Cases of New Quality Productive Forces in the Automotive Industry 2025'.
These solutions are fully compatible with mass production requirements for full vehicle body structural components.
As at the end of the Year, the Group ranked first globally in cumulative number of ultra-large die-casting machines delivered.

The Group's TPI semi-solid magnesium alloy forming technology supports modular transformation of traditional equipment, enabling a 50% reduction in energy consumption and a 20% increase in product toughness.

A self-developed 5,000-tonne TPI semi-solid forming module secured an order from a leading OEM for inner tailgate panels for new energy vehicles, integrating functions previously requiring nine parts to achieve a 45% weight reduction and 70% material utilisation.

Orders for TPI magnesium alloy equipment exceeded RMB100 million in shipment value, with related business revenue increasing by 291.1% in the second half of the financial year compared to the first half.
Self-developed zirconium-based amorphous alloy die casting equipment integrates vacuum melting and full-process automation for producing foldable screen hinges and medical implants.

Low-pressure die-casting machines feature inert gas pressurisation and waste heat recovery technologies, reducing energy consumption by 30% and are widely used in automotive wheels and motor end covers.
Horizontal extrusion casting machines with dual pressure and speed control systems are used for high-performance components like automotive steering knuckles, subframes, and battery end plates.

The Group developed an integrated intelligent machining system for post-processing large die-cast components with over 90% local data processing rate and over 95% accuracy in predicting machining abnormalities.
Digital twin technology was leveraged to develop intelligent die-casting cells capable of process parameter prediction, remote diagnostics, and digitalised management.
The Group proactively laid out new productivity tracks including energy storage, AI computing power, humanoid robots, and specialised casting to diversify its customer structure.
Revenue from Europe surged by 64.4% year-on-year, and the Group secured a high-end equipment order from a well-known European vehicle enterprise.
Revenue from other emerging markets and the Asia-Pacific region increased by 76.9% year-on-year, with a localised sales and service network covering Thailand, Vietnam, and Malaysia.
Revenue from the North American market declined year-on-year.
CEO Liu Zhuo Ming stated the Group will push into energy storage, photovoltaics, robotics, and computing power cooling while building a smart service system centred on 'AI + Equipment'.
L.K. Technology Holdings Limited is one of the world's largest die-casting machine manufacturers, operating 15 manufacturing bases and 60 sales and service centers worldwide.
Products are widely used in automotive bodies and accessories, household appliances, and electronic products.
The Group owns Idra, which developed the world's first Giga Press ultra-large die-casting equipment adopted by leading global new energy vehicle manufacturers.
Sales and service companies were established in the United States and India, and a casting factory operates in Fuxin, China for cast iron/steel components.
source_title: L.K. Technology Announces 2025/2026 Annual Results — UzDaily.uz
