Hyundai Motor Group newsroom Twitter handle

source_title: Georgia Officials: Hyundai’s $5.54 Billion EV Plant a Testament to Enduring Focus on South Korea  - Global Atlanta

facts: Daesol Ausys is a South Korean automotive supplier that specializes in designing and manufacturing motor and electric vehicle interior components and accessories.
Daesol Ausys announced an investment of $72 million to establish a new manufacturing facility in Harris County, Georgia.
The company is identified as a key supplier for Kia Georgia, Hyundai Motor Group, and General Motors by the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD).
The new facility will be located in West Point, Georgia, and will specifically produce luggage boards and covers.
The facility will create more than 140 new jobs in Harris County.
Daesol Ausys CEO Min Ho Kwon stated the company is committed to delivering world-class services that exceed its clients' requirements and expectations.
Min Ho Kwon expressed excitement about the project in the Northwest Harris Business Park and looking forward to continuing partnerships with the State of Georgia and Harris County.
New operations at the West Point facility are expected to begin in December 2024.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that West Point has become an automotive capital in its own right and is excited that Daesol Ausys will join other innovators in Harris County.

Governor Brian Kemp noted that Georgia's automotive industry employs over 50,000 hardworking Georgians and will continue to grow as e-mobility suppliers choose all corners of the state for future jobs.
South Korea was one of the top three international sources of job creation in Georgia during Fiscal Year 2023.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that economic development is a team sport driven by the local level and congratulated Harris County and community partners for helping bring Daesol Ausys to West Georgia.

Pat Wilson noted that the automotive industry is providing generational change in the region and across the state and expressed excitement for the opportunities Daesol Ausys will provide to Harris County residents.
source_title: Automotive Supplier Announces $72 Million Investment in Georgia | The Star News Network

facts: Hyundai Motor has missed out on the United States tax incentives for its new $7.6-billion manufacturing plant in Bryan County, Georgia.
The facility is named Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA).

According to industry sources, Hyundai Motor did not make the list of companies that will receive tax incentives in the second round allocations of the Qualifying Advanced Energy Project Credit (48C) program.
The 48C program is an allocated tax credit funded by President Joe Biden’s Investing in America agenda through the Inflation Reduction Act.
The Biden administration has assigned a total of $10 billion to the 48C program.
Selected companies under the 48C program could receive up to a 30 percent tax credit for their investments on American soil.
A local report estimated that Hyundai Motor could have received up to 480 billion won ($344 million) in tax incentives.
Construction of the Georgia plant has been smooth sailing despite missing the additional tax incentives.
Hyundai Motor already began early-stage production of electric vehicles at HMGMA in October.
Hyundai Motor is expected to start full operation of the new plant in the first quarter of next year.
A Hyundai Motor official stated that while they applied for the 48C program, they did not expect benefits because those tax incentives were made for parts makers not automakers.
The same official noted that the 48C program was not in consideration when investing in HMGMA.
The US Department of Energy announced a list of 35 projects across 20 states selected for a total of $1.93 billion in the first round allocations of the 48C program in April this year.

The first-round projects were categorized into four areas: clean energy and clean vehicle manufacturing, critical minerals and materials, grid components and modernization, and industrial decarbonization.
Mobis North America electrified Powertrain, a US subsidiary of Hyundai Motor Group’s auto parts maker Hyundai Mobis, was awarded $57.6 million in tax credits.
The awarded funds are for constructing three different EV component plants that will provide parts for the production of Hyundai and Kia electric vehicles.
source_title: Hyundai Motor plays down missed IRA incentives for Georgia plant - The Korea Herald

facts: Hyundai is one of two Korean brands competing in the U.S. market, with Kia as its corporate sibling.
Imports to the U.S. started in 1986 with one model and have expanded to over 15 models today, which include hybrids, plug-in hybrids, all-electric and fuel cell electric vehicles.
Today, over half of the Hyundais sold in the U.S. are American made at the Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama facility in Montgomery, Alabama.
The $1.8 billion facility can build just shy of 400,000 vehicles annually.
The facility currently produces the Hyundai Sonata and Elantra sedans and the Santa Fe SUV.
Kia offers a sportier lineup with its sedans compared to Hyundai.
Both brands have made great strides in offering premium materials and design at affordable prices compared to competitors.
Hyundai vehicles now have the build quality, safety, technology, and design expertise that rivals the best in the industry.
Consumer Reports ranks Hyundai in the Top 5 for auto manufacturer overall reliability.
Popular models include the Elantra and Sonata sedans, which are also offered in hybrid and plug-in hybrid variants.
Popular models also include the Tucson and Santa Fe SUVs.
An updated Santa Fe appears in the first quarter of 2021.
The Venue, Kona, and Palisade SUVs are the latest additions to the lineup.
Other models include the Veloster and Elantra GT, the Ioniq models (hybrid, plug-in hybrid, and EV), the Accent sedan, and the Nexo fuel cell electric SUV.
The 2021 Hyundai Venue starts at $18,750.
The 2021 Hyundai Kona starts at $20,400.
The 2021 Hyundai Tucson starts at $23,700.
The 2020 Hyundai Santa Fe starts at $26,275.
The 2021 Hyundai Palisade starts at $32,525.
The 2021 Hyundai Accent starts at $15,395.
The 2021 Hyundai Elantra starts at $19,650.
The 2021 Hyundai Veloster starts at $18,900.
The 2021 Hyundai Veloster N starts at $32,250.
The 2021 Hyundai Ioniq Hybrid starts at $23,200.
The 2021 Hyundai Ioniq Plug-in Hybrid starts at $28,000 (est).
The 2021 Hyundai Ioniq Electric starts at $34,500.
The 2021 Hyundai Kona Electric starts at $37,190.
The 2021 Hyundai Nexo Fuel Cell starts at $60,000 (est).

links.context: Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama facility website

source_title: Hyundai

facts: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America is located in Ellabell, Georgia, about 20 miles northwest of Savannah.
The plant is scheduled to produce 500,000 units by 2028.
The expansion involves a $2.7 billion investment.
The expansion will create 3,000 more jobs.
Hyundai CEO José Muñoz said the company wants to produce more than 80% of its cars sold in the U.S. domestically by 2030.
Several automakers are following similar steps after President Donald Trump announced steep auto tariffs to encourage domestic production.
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp congratulated Hyundai on the project in a statement.
Gov. Kemp wrote, “We look forward to working with them on their continued growth and success for years to come!”
On Sept. 4, ICE conducted a raid on the southeast Georgia manufacturing site, detaining about 475 people.
More than 300 South Korean workers were flown back to their home country in a charter plane last week.
The incident caused alarm in South Korea, whose citizens saw videos of workers shackled with chains around their hands, ankles and waists.

South Korean Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lee Jaewoong said in a televised statement from Seoul, “The business activities of our investors and the rights of our nationals must not be unjustly infringed in the process of U.S. law enforcement.”
Homeland Security Investigations officials said the raid resulted from a months-long investigation into accusations of illegal hiring at the site.
According to the workers’ lawyers, they were in the country to help install parts of the factory that American workers don’t have the training to do.
Gov. Kemp has since called for a rehaul of the U.S.’s visa system.
The automaker announced the plant expansion about two weeks after immigration authorities detained hundreds of South Korean nationals at the site.
source_title: Hyundai to expand Georgia plant hit by immigration raid with $2.7B investment

facts: Hyundai's highest-volume electric vehicle is the Ioniq 5.
The Ioniq 5 will be the first EV to roll out of Hyundai's new U.S. factory.
The factory is scheduled to open this fall.
The factory is located near Savannah, Ga.
Jose Muñoz provided details regarding the factory opening and the first vehicle model.
source_title: Hyundai Ioniq 5 to be first product of new U.S. Metaplant - Automotive News

facts: A Statesboro man was identified as the construction worker who died after a fall at the new Hyundai Metaplant.
An obituary by Lovett & Son's Funeral Home identifies the victim as 35-year-old Victor Javier Cajija Gamboa.
According to the Bryan County Coroner's Office, Gamboa was killed Saturday at the construction site located at I-16 and Highway 280 after a 60-foot fall.
The coroner's office received the call regarding the incident at 11:24 a.m.
The fall occurred in the area designated to become the paint shop of the electric vehicle plant.
Hyundai's new facility will be the largest economic development deal in state history.
The facility will be the first fully dedicated EV and battery manufacturing facility in Georgia.
Once complete, the facility will employ more than 8,000 workers.
Hyundai Motor Group stated the death occurred when Gamboa fell from a three-story structure.
Initial information indicates part of Gamboa's safety harness failed.
The Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) is investigating the workplace death.

Hyundai stated, “Together with the contractor and the relevant authorities, we are fully supporting the investigation to understand the full circumstances of the incident to ensure this type of accident does not reoccur.”
The Savannah Harbor Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority issued a statement on Tuesday.
The authority offered condolences to the family, friends, and coworkers of the subcontractor who died on April 29 at the construction site for Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
The deceased employee was employed by a subcontractor on the site.
Hyundai Motor Group, Hyundai Engineering America, and the subcontractor stated their primary concern is and will continue to be the safety and security of everyone on the construction site.
All parties are fully cooperating with the investigation.
Hyundai broke ground in October on its first U.S. plant devoted to manufacturing electric vehicles.
The automaker is investing $5.5 billion in the Georgia plant.
Georgia officials say it is the largest economic development project in the state's history.
Hyundai plans to open the factory in 2025.
The facility will produce up to 300,000 electric vehicles each year.

Hyundai expressed deep sadness that a subcontractor died on April 29, 2023, as a result of injuries sustained in a workplace accident at the construction site of its Metaplant America manufacturing facility in Bryan County, Georgia.
The company reiterated that safety and security is their highest priority and they have full commitment to following Occupational Industrial Safety and Health policies.
Hyundai confirmed the individual fell from a three-story paint shop structure.
Initial information indicates the individual’s safety harness lanyard failed.
Hyundai expressed deepest condolences to the individual’s family, friends, and coworkers.
source_title: Georgia construction worker identified as victim in deadly fall - WJCL

facts: A South Korean auto parts supplier will invest more than $240 million to build a transmission factory in western Georgia.
The company plans to hire 678 workers for the new facility.
Hyundai Transys announced plans on Tuesday for the new factory in West Point.
The new factory will adjoin an existing Hyundai Transys factory and a Kia Motors Corp factory.
Both facilities are part of Hyundai Motor Group, the Korean auto conglomerate that sells cars under the Kia and Hyundai brands.
The new plant will manufacture eight-speed transmissions.
The facility will be 620,000 square feet (58,000 square meters) in size.
The plant will be located on the Kia campus just off Interstate 85 in West Point near the state line with Alabama.
Hyundai Transys was created by a merger of two Hyundai affiliates last year.
The company also manufactures axles and vehicle seats.
The company has operations in South Korea, the United States, and seven other countries.
The investment will create an additional 165 jobs.
Once the new plant is complete, Hyundai Transys's total employees in Georgia will rise above 1,700.
West Point Mayor Steve Tramell stated he is thrilled about the long-term investment and the new jobs being created.
Many supplier plants are clustered near the Hyundai plant in Montgomery, Alabama, and the Kia plant in West Point.
Kia, its affiliates, and suppliers had more than 4,300 employees in Georgia’s Troup County in 2018.
This is the largest industrial announcement by investment value made so far in 2020 in Georgia.

Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Powertrain President Sangkil Jung cited years of trust built through capital investment, employment, training support, and unmatched opportunities as reasons Georgia was a natural choice.
Final incentives for Hyundai Transys have not been agreed upon yet, according to Marie Hodge Gordon, spokesperson for the Georgia Economic Development Department.
The company will qualify for a state income tax credit allowing it to deduct $3,500 per job from its Georgia income taxes for five years.

If Hyundai Transys does not incur $2.37 million in state income taxes each year, it can use the credit to receive cash payments redirected from money withheld from employee paychecks for state income taxes.
The plant's location is classified by the state as a less-developed area.
Workers must earn at least $28,000 annually for Hyundai Transys to qualify for the tax credits.
The state will pay to train Hyundai Transys workers.
The state could also contribute funding to pay for infrastructure.
Hyundai Transys will automatically qualify for tax breaks that will save it millions in sales and use taxes on machinery.
Local officials could also abate property taxes.
West Point’s financial statements show that in 2018, Kia, its affiliates, and suppliers were receiving $2.85 million in property tax breaks from the city alone.
Kia received subsidies and tax breaks worth more than $450 million to locate its plant in West Point, which opened in 2009.
The state aims to revitalize a region previously hit hard by the closure of textile and apparel plants.
Because the plant is only miles from the Alabama state line, a significant number of its workers live in Alabama.
source_title: Hyundai affiliate plans $240M transmission plant in Georgia | FOX 5 Atlanta

facts: Hyundai Transys Georgia Powertrain's revenues are gauged from an analysis of company filings.

The company's income statement tracks Revenue (Sales), Cost of Goods Sold, Gross Profit, Operating Expenses (Advertising, Salaries and wages, Other Operating Expenses), Total Operating Expenses, Operating Income, EBITDA, EBIT, and Net Profit.
A competitive analysis identifies companies in the same general field as Hyundai Transys Georgia Powertrain.
These competing companies are similar in business line and location to Hyundai Transys Georgia Powertrain.
Competitors in the same general field are rapidly expanding.
Companies may grow organically or through acquisition.
Some reported high growth rates for competitors may be caused by data that weren't available in previous years.
Some companies compete with neighboring businesses for customers.
Other companies may compete to attract skilled employees.
source_title: Annual Report on Hyundai Transys Georgia Powertrain's Revenue, Growth, SWOT Analysis & Competitor Intelligence - IncFact

facts: Hyundai Motor Group has entered a significant power purchase agreement (PPA) with Spanish renewable energy company Matrix Renewables to power its electric vehicle (EV) plant in Georgia.
The 147-megawatt deal represents the largest single PPA signed by a Korean company in the United States.
The agreement involves four Hyundai companies: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, Hyundai Mobis North America, Hyundai Steel Georgia, and Hyundai Transys Georgia Powertrain.
Together, they secured a joint purchase agreement for renewable energy, reducing costs compared to individual contracts.
The renewable energy will come from a new 210-megawatt solar power plant named Stillhouse Solar, currently under construction in Texas.
Hyundai Motor will obtain about 378 gigawatt-hours of power per year from this plant.
The obtained power is sufficient to supply electricity to approximately 100,000 households annually.
This renewable energy initiative is expected to lower carbon emissions by 140,000 tons annually.
The emission reduction is comparable to the emissions from 84,000 mid-sized sedans.
Hyundai Motor's EV-dedicated plant in Bryan County, Georgia, is set to begin operations in October.
The plant will begin operations three months ahead of schedule.

facts: Hyundai Motor Group inked a 147-megawatt power purchase agreement with Matrix Renewables, a Spanish renewable energy company, to power its electric vehicle plant currently under construction in Georgia.
The deal marks the largest single PPA signed by a Korean company in the US.

Four Hyundai Motor Group companies joined hands for the contract: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, Hyundai Mobis North America electric, Hyundai Steel Georgia, and Hyundai Transys Georgia Powertrain.
Through this deal, HMGMA has set up a renewable energy system for not only its auto plant but also its supply chains.
The deal signifies Hyundai Motor Group’s pan-conglomerate step to secure renewable energy.
The purchasing cost for the renewable energy was saved as the conglomerate’s overseas offices pushed for a joint purchase agreement instead of pursuing a deal individually.
The North American offices of Hyundai Mobis, Hyundai Steel and Hyundai Transys will utilize the secured solar power to supply automation parts, EV steel sheets and seats to HMGMA.
As this PPA delivers power from a new solar power plant instead of signing a contract with existing renewable energy plants, it will have better impacts on reducing global carbon emissions.
The solar power supply is expected to decrease carbon emissions by 140,000 tons per year.
This emission reduction is equal to absorbing the carbon emissions produced by about 84,000 mid-sized sedans annually.
Matrix Renewables is constructing a 210-megawatt solar power plant named Stillhouse Solar in Bell County, Texas with the goal to start operating in October 2025.
Hyundai Motor secured about 70 percent of the generation capacity for the project.
Hyundai Motor will obtain about 378 gigawatt-hours per year from the agreement.
The secured power is enough to power approximately 100,000 households for a year.
A Hyundai Motor Group official stated they will conduct investments and deals to turn Hyundai Motor Group’s US footholds into using renewable energy.
The official added that they will continue to push for cooperation between the conglomerate’s companies to secure renewable energy efficiently.
Hyundai Motor’s EV-dedicated plant in Georgia’s Bryan County is expected to begin operations in October this year.
The Georgia plant's start date is three months ahead of its initial planned timeline.
source_title: Hyundai Motor to power US EV plant with renewable energy - The Korea Herald

facts: A South Korean auto parts supplier will invest more than $240 million to build a transmission factory in western Georgia with plans to hire 678 workers.
Hyundai Transys announced plans for the new factory in West Point, adjoining an existing Hyundai Transys factory and a Kia Motors Corp factory.
Both facilities are parts of Hyundai Motor Group, the Korean auto conglomerate that sells cars under the Kia and Hyundai brands.
The new plant will build eight-speed transmissions.
The 620,000-square foot (58,000-square meter) plant will be part of the Kia campus just off Interstate 85 in West Point near the state line with Alabama.
Hyundai Transys was created by a merger of two Hyundai affiliates last year and also makes axles and vehicle seats.
The company has operations in South Korea, the United States and seven other countries.
The investment will create 165 other jobs and its total employees in Georgia will rise above 1,700 once the new plant is complete.
West Point Mayor Steve Tramell stated he is absolutely thrilled that Hyundai Transys will make this long-term investment in West Point for the new jobs being created.
Many supplier plants are clustered near the Hyundai plant in Montgomery, Alabama, and the Kia plant in West Point, with others scattered around and between the two plants.
Kia, affiliates and suppliers had more than 4,300 employees in Georgia’s Troup County in 2018, according to county financial documents.
It is the largest industrial announcement, by value of the investment, made so far this year in Georgia.

Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Powertrain President Sangkil Jung said the level of trust built through years of capital investment, employment, and training support along with new opportunities unmatched by any other states made Georgia a natural choice.
Final incentives for Hyundai Transys have not been agreed on yet, according to Marie Hodge Gordon, spokesperson for the Georgia Economic Development Department.
The company will qualify for a state income tax credit that allows it to deduct $3,500 per job from its Georgia income taxes for five years.

If Hyundai Transys does not incur $2.37 million in state income taxes each year, it can use the credit to get cash payments redirected out of money withheld from employee paychecks for state income taxes, given its location in a less-developed area.
Workers must make at least $28,000 a year for Hyundai Transys to qualify for tax credits.
The state will pay to train Hyundai Transys workers.
The state could chip in money to pay for infrastructure, and Hyundai will automatically qualify for tax breaks that will save it millions in sales and use taxes on machinery.
Local officials could also abate property taxes.
West Point’s financial statements show that in 2018, Kia, its affiliates and suppliers were receiving $2.85 million in property tax breaks from the city alone.

Kia got subsidies and tax breaks worth more than $450 million to locate its plant in West Point that opened in 2009, with the state aiming to revitalize a region hit hard by the closure of textile and apparel plants.
Because the plant is only miles from the Alabama state line, a significant number of its workers live in that state.
source_title: Hyundai affiliate plans $240M transmission plant in Georgia | AP News

facts: A South Korean worker at the huge car plant in Georgia was targeted in an immigration raid.
Hundreds of federal agents arrived at the plant, causing panic and confusion among workers.
Almost 500 workers were detained during the raid.

Some detained workers were led away in chains.
BBC Verify's Nick Beake has been piecing together what is known about the raid.

entity_name: Hyundai-LG Georgia EV and Battery Plant
canonical_name: HL-GA Battery Company / Hyundai Motor Group Georgia Facility
facts: ~475 people were detained during an immigration raid at a sprawling Georgia site where South Korean auto company Hyundai manufactures electric vehicles.
Steven Schrank, Special Agent in Charge of Homeland Security Investigations, said at a news briefing Friday that the majority of the detained people were from South Korea.
Schrank stated: "This operation underscores our commitment to jobs for Georgians and Americans."
Schrank noted this was the largest single site enforcement operation in the history of Homeland Security Investigations.
The investigation has been ongoing for several months, with authorities receiving leads from community members and former workers.
South Korean Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lee Jaewoong described the number of detained South Koreans as "large" though he did not provide an exact figure.
Lee stated the detained workers were part of a "network of subcontractors," and that the employees worked for a variety of different companies on the site.
The raid targeted one of Georgia's largest and most high-profile manufacturing sites, touted by Gov. Brian Kemp and other officials as the largest economic development project in the state's history.
Hyundai Motor Group, South Korea's biggest automaker, began manufacturing EVs a year ago at the $7.6 billion plant.
The plant employs about 1,200 people.
Hyundai has partnered with LG Energy Solution to build an adjacent battery plant, slated to open next year.
LG Energy Solution stated it was "closely monitoring the situation and gathering all relevant details" and couldn't immediately confirm how many of its employees or Hyundai workers had been detained.
LG added: "Our top priority is always ensuring the safety and well-being of our employees and partners. We will fully cooperate with the relevant authorities."
Hyundai's South Korean office did not immediately respond to requests for comment.
ICE spokesman Lindsay Williams confirmed federal authorities conducted an enforcement operation at the 3,000-acre (1,214-hectare) site west of Savannah, Georgia.
Agents were focused on the construction site for the battery plant.

Lee Jaewoong said the ministry is taking active measures to address the case, dispatching diplomats from its embassy in Washington and consulate in Atlanta to the site, and planning to form an on-site response team centered on the local mission.
Lee stated: "The business activities of our investors and the rights of our nationals must not be unjustly infringed in the process of U.S. law enforcement."

The Department of Homeland Security said in a statement that agents executed a search warrant "as part of an ongoing criminal investigation into allegations of unlawful employment practices and other serious federal crimes."

President Donald Trump's administration has undertaken sweeping ICE operations as part of a mass deportation agenda, with immigration officers raiding farms, construction sites, restaurants, and auto repair shops.

The Pew Research Center cited preliminary Census Bureau data saying the U.S. labor force lost more than 1.2 million immigrants from January through July, including people in the country illegally and legal residents.
Hyundai and LG's battery joint venture, HL-GA Battery Company, stated it is "cooperating fully with the appropriate authorities" and paused construction of the battery site to assist their work.
Operations at Hyundai's EV manufacturing plant were not interrupted, according to plant spokesperson Bianca Johnson.
source_title: Hundreds of South Koreans are among 475 detained in a Georgia immigration raid | Georgia Public Broadcasting

canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Group Savannah Manufacturing Facility
facts: Immigration authorities detained 475 people, most of them South Korean nationals, during a raid on the sprawling manufacturing site in Georgia where Hyundai makes electric vehicles.
Steven Schrank, lead Georgia agent of Homeland Security Investigations, said the raid resulted from a monthslong investigation into allegations of illegal hiring at the site.
Schrank described the operation as the “largest single site enforcement operation” in the agency’s two-decade history.
The Thursday raid targeted one of Georgia’s largest and most high-profile manufacturing sites, where Hyundai began manufacturing electric vehicles about a year ago at a $7.6 billion plant.
The site employs about 1,200 people and is located roughly 25 miles (40 kilometers) west of Savannah in Bryan County.
The facility sits on 3,000 acres (1,214 hectares) in a largely rural area, drawing workers from several surrounding counties and communities including Savannah.
Gov. Brian Kemp and other officials have touted the Hyundai site as Georgia’s largest economic development project.
Agents focused their operation on an adjacent plant still under construction where Hyundai has partnered with LG Energy Solution to produce batteries that power EVs.
The battery plant is operated by HL-GA Battery Co., a joint venture between Hyundai and LG Energy Solution, and is slated to open next year.
Court records filed this week indicated that prosecutors do not know who hired the “hundreds of illegal aliens” at the site.
The U.S. Attorney’s Office wrote in a court filing that the identity of the actual company or contractor hiring the illegal aliens is currently unknown.
The South Korean government expressed “concern and regret” over the operation targeting its citizens.
South Korean Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lee Jaewoong stated that the business activities of investors and the rights of nationals must not be unjustly infringed in U.S. law enforcement.
The South Korean Foreign Ministry is dispatching diplomats from its embassy in Washington and consulate in Atlanta to the site and planning to form an on-site response team.
Immigration attorney Charles Kuck said two detained clients arrived under a visa waiver program that allows stays of 90 days or less without a visa for tourism or business.
One client had been in the U.S. for a couple of weeks, while the other had been there for about 45 days.
Schrank said some detained workers illegally crossed the U.S. border, while others had entered legally but had expired visas or entered on a visa waiver that prohibited them from working.
Some of those detained worked for the battery manufacturer, while others were employed by contractors and subcontractors at the construction site.
Schrank did not know precisely how many of the 475 detained were Korean nationals but said they made up a majority.
No one has yet been charged with any crimes, and the investigation remains ongoing.
Most of the detainees were taken to an immigration detention center in Folkston, Georgia, near the Florida state line.
Hyundai began producing electric vehicles at the site last September.

A few months after production began, Hyundai Motor Group Executive Chairman Euisun Chung credited President Trump with the company’s decision to create more American jobs by building an EV factory in Georgia during a White House appearance.
Chung stated that the investment decision was initiated during his meeting with President Trump in Seoul in 2019 and would create more than 8,500 American jobs.

Agents sought employment records, personnel files, payroll information, bank account information, timecards, video and photos of workers, immigration documents, social security cards, visas, passports, birth certificates, and ownership/management records of multiple construction companies and contractors.
The search warrant materials included the names and photos of four people identified as “target persons” to be searched.
LG stated it was closely monitoring the situation and gathering all relevant details but could not immediately confirm how many of its employees or Hyundai workers had been detained.
Plant spokesperson Bianca Johnson said operations at Hyundai’s EV manufacturing plant were not interrupted by the raid.
Hyundai Motor Company stated it is working to understand the specific circumstances of the raid and understands that none of those detained are directly employed by Hyundai Motor Company.
HL-GA Battery Co. stated it is cooperating fully with the appropriate authorities.
Gov. Brian Kemp and other Georgia Republican officials issued statements saying all employers in the state were expected to follow the law following the raid.

The Democratic Party of Georgia condemned the raid, with chair Charlie Bailey calling it “politically-motivated fear tactics designed to terrorize people who work hard for a living, power our economy and contribute to the communities across Georgia that they have made their homes.”
source_title: 475 people were detained during immigration raid in Georgia: Homeland security official

facts: Kia Motors Corporation (KMC) announced plans to construct a $1.2 billion automotive assembly and manufacturing plant in West Point, Troup County, Georgia.

facts: Kia Georgia team members marked a historic day at its West Point facility on Feb. 24, 2026, with the start of production for the all-new 2027 Kia Telluride.
The 2027 Kia Telluride marked the first hybrid electric vehicle (HEV) assembled in the state of Georgia.
The 2027 Kia Telluride also represented the 5 millionth vehicle produced at Kia Georgia.
A ceremony for the milestone was attended by Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp, team members, and state/community leaders.
Kia Georgia is the only plant in the world that builds the Telluride.
Sean Yoon serves as President and CEO of Kia North America and Kia America.
The 2027 Kia Telluride is Kia's first US-assembled HEV.
Kia Georgia began mass production in 2009.
Since beginning production, Kia Georgia has grown into one of the Southeast region's largest employers.
The Telluride has been exclusively manufactured in Georgia since its introduction in 2019.
Stuart Countess serves as Kia Georgia's President and CEO.
The Kia Georgia plant produces gasoline-powered vehicles, EVs, and HEVs.
Kia Georgia, Inc. is Kia Corporation's first manufacturing site in North America.
Kia Georgia has an annual production capacity of 350,000 units.
Kia Georgia is located on a 2,200-acre site in West Point, Georgia.
Kia Georgia officially began mass production on Nov. 16, 2009.
Kia Georgia manufactures the Telluride, Sorento SUVs (excluding HEV and PHEV models), Sportage SUVs (excluding HEV and PHEV models), and the all-electric EV9 and EV6.
source_title: Kia Georgia celebrates trio of major milestones, including Start of Production for 2027 Kia Telluride

facts: Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia, Inc. (KMMG) is the first manufacturing site in North America for Kia Motors Corporation based in Seoul, Korea.
KMMG has an annual capacity of 360,000 units.
KMMG is located on 2,200 acres in West Point, Georgia.
KMMG began mass production on Nov. 16, 2009.
KMMG is home to the Sorento CUV and the Optima mid-size sedan, two of the brand’s top selling models in the U.S.
Stuart C. Countess serves as KMMG’s chief administrative officer.
Gov. Nathan Deal publicly acknowledged KMMG's corporate presence and community contributions in Georgia.
source_title: Team Members with Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia Support Hurricane Relief Efforts - Kia Georgia

facts: Kia Corp.'s Georgia subsidiary announced it reached cumulative production of 5 million vehicles as of Dec. 24 local time.
The 5 millionth vehicle was the all-new Telluride Hybrid, marking the first hybrid vehicle produced in the state of Georgia.
Kia has begun full-scale hybrid production at its West Point, Georgia plant with the launch of the 2027 model year "All-New Telluride."

Since starting production in 2009, Kia Georgia has established itself as a key manufacturing hub for the automotive industry in the southeastern United States and grown into a leading regional employer.
The Telluride is produced exclusively at the West Point plant and was developed based on North American consumer demands and U.S. road conditions as a local strategic model.
The first-generation Telluride swept major awards in 2020, including North American Car of the Year, World Car of the Year, and MotorTrend SUV of the Year.
Governor Brian Kemp joined local government officials and company employees at a commemorative ceremony to celebrate Kia Georgia's achievement.
Sean Yoon, president and CEO of Kia North America and head of the North America region, stated that Kia Georgia is the only plant in the world that produces the Telluride.
Reaching 5 million units and producing Georgia's first hybrid vehicle demonstrates Kia's technological advancement and future strategic direction, according to Sean Yoon.
Governor Kemp said the Telluride has become one of the most successful models in Kia's lineup since its 2019 launch due to a close partnership between the local community and state government.

Stuart Countess, chief administrative officer of Kia Georgia, stated that adding hybrid models to internal combustion and electric vehicle production proves the plant's flexibility and technical capabilities.
source_title: Kia Georgia Plant Hits 5 Million Vehicle Milestone, Launches Telluride Hybrid - Seoul Economic Daily

facts: Kia confirms it is developing a separate midsize pickup truck for the US market.
The new truck will target the midsize pickup segment.
There are rumors that the new truck could be an electric vehicle (EV).
More details about the new truck will be announced at Kia’s Investor Day on April 9.
Kia CEO Ho Sung Song confirmed the company is planning to enter the North American midsize pickup segment with a new model.
The new model will be entirely separate from the Tasman, which is slated for other global markets.
Kia CEO Ho Sung Song shared the update on Thursday during the ongoing Seoul Mobility Show in Korea.
Ho Sung Song stated: “We’re going to have more details next week about the pickup for the U.S. But in general terms … we are studying now.”
A formal announcement is expected during Kia’s Investor Day, scheduled for April 9.
Kia introduced a rugged “Weekender” version of the Tasman at the Seoul Mobility Show.
Ho Sung Song told Autonews: “We don’t develop that vehicle [the Tasman] for the American market.”
Ho Sung Song added: “But in the meantime, we are now seriously studying how to get into the pickup truck market in the United States through a different model that is not the Tasman.”
No details were offered on what kind of powertrain the future US-bound pickup might use.
Sources have previously suggested the future pickup could be an electric model.
Industry rumors point to a debut window for the new pickup sometime between 2026 and 2029.
The new pickup may potentially launch alongside a Hyundai-branded sibling.
Both Kia and Hyundai models are expected to be assembled in Georgia at Hyundai’s Metaplant America facility.
Building the trucks in Georgia is intended to sidestep ongoing trade tensions and avoid the 25 percent tariff on imported trucks known as the Chicken Tax.
Another option reportedly still in play is a deal with GM that would let Kia and Hyundai offer their own internal combustion versions of the Chevrolet Colorado and GMC Canyon.
Both strategies (building in Georgia vs. partnering with GM) could be on the table.
The current US midsize pickup scene is dominated by internal combustion vehicles like the Toyota Tacoma, Chevy Colorado, GMC Canyon, Nissan Frontier, Ford Ranger, and Jeep Gladiator.

Fully electric trucks like the Ford F-150 Lightning, Rivian R1T, and Tesla Cybertruck tend to sit in a larger category, leaving a potential opening for Kia to introduce a more compact, EV-friendly alternative.
The Tasman will launch in markets like South Korea, Australia, Africa, and the Middle East with traditional gas and diesel powertrains.
It is unclear whether Kia’s US-bound pickup will borrow design elements or utility features from the Tasman.
Kia is gearing up to expand its electric light commercial vehicle lineup, starting with the PV5 van.
The PV7 and PV9 are set to follow the PV5.
CEO Ho Sung Song framed Kia’s expansion into new segments, including LCVs and pickups, as “an opportunity” for the brand, “not a risk.”
source_title: Kia Confirms First Midsize Pickup For America | Carscoops

facts: Kia Georgia, Inc. is the first manufacturing site in North America for Kia Corporation based in Seoul, Korea.
The facility has an annual production capacity of 350,000 units.
Kia Georgia is located on 2,200 acres in West Point, Georgia.
The plant began mass production on November 16, 2009.
The facility produces the Telluride, Sorento, and Sportage SUVs.
The facility also produces the all-electric EV9 and EV6 SUVs.
Kia Georgia represents a total investment of $3.2 billion.
The plant employs 3,200 team members.
Manufacturing processes include high-tech stamping, weld shop advanced robotics, and highly automated assembly processes.
The facility produces one vehicle every 51 seconds.
Operations run on three full shifts, operating 24 hours a day, five days per week.
Tour registration is open until July 22, 2025, at 12:00 AM EDT.
The plant tour is available exclusively to Georgia Manufacturing Alliance (GMA) members.
Each company is limited to two tickets for the tour.
First-time GMA event attendees who are Georgia manufacturers can call for a complimentary ticket.
Visitors must be at least 13 years old to attend the tour.
Tobacco products, weapons (including firearms and knives), photography, and video recording are strictly prohibited on site.
Large purses, bags, and backpacks are not allowed inside the plant.
All tours are subject to cancellation with little or no notice depending on Kia Georgia's production schedule.
Visitors must wear long pants and enclosed-toe shoes to participate in the tour.
A portion of the tour will be outdoors, requiring weather-appropriate attire.
The facility address is 7777 Kia Parkway, West Point, Georgia 31833.
Navigation apps may occasionally direct visitors to the back of the property instead of the main entry gate.
Visitor parking is located on numbered front rows, and security passes are provided at Building 7797.

links.context: Call for complimentary tickets for first-time Georgia manufacturers

source_title: KIA Georgia Plant Tour - West Point  (Afternoon) 2025 -  Georgia Manufacturing Alliance

facts: The 2024 Kia EV9 lineup features EPA-estimated all-electric ranges of 230 miles for Light RWD, 304 miles for Light Long Range RWD, 280 miles for Wind AWD, 280 miles for Land AWD, and 270 miles for GT-Line AWD.
The EV9 utilizes a purpose-built EV platform with an available 99.8 kWh battery and dual motor e-AWD.
It supports charging from 10-80% in as little as 24 minutes using a 350kW DC fast charger.
Kia has teamed up with ChargePoint to offer drivers an available Level 2 at-home charging system for an additional cost, backed by a 3-year limited warranty.
The EV9 comes with 1,000 kWh of charging credits on the Electrify America network, which is equivalent to about 3,000 miles of driving range.
An optional portable Webasto Go charger is available for an additional cost, fitting in the front storage area with adaptors for both 120V and 240V outlets.
Starting MSRP for the Light RWD trim is $54,099, featuring a 76.1 kWh battery.
Starting MSRP for the Light Long Range RWD trim is $59,200, featuring a 99.8 kWh battery.
Starting MSRP for the Wind AWD trim is $63,900.
Starting MSRP for the Land AWD trim is $69,900.
Starting MSRP for the GT-Line AWD trim is $73,900.
The vehicle offers up to 5,000 lbs. of towing capacity with a self-leveling rear suspension and dedicated trailering mode.
It includes an onboard power generator delivering up to 1,920 watts for external power needs.
Vehicle-to-home (V2H) backup power capabilities are planned for the future, allowing the vehicle to reverse energy flow to properly equipped homes.
The EV9 features a heat pump to help preserve range in colder climates and a battery warmer to enhance charging performance.
Over-the-air updates can be performed automatically to enhance infotainment features and battery management systems.
source_title: The all-new, all-electric Kia EV9 | Kia Mall of Georgia

facts: BYD’s new Datang SUV recently racked up 150,000 pre-orders in a 53-day period in China, marking a pre-order record for any BYD model.
The Datang is booked without a single gram of nickel in its premium EV battery.
The industry's working assumption that killing range anxiety in long-range EVs required high-nickel ternary batteries has collapsed in China.
Datang’s global rollouts for Europe and Southeast Asia are scheduled to begin around the turn of the year.
BYD historically leaned on its now-discontinued F3 compact sedan, which retailed for about 50,000 yuan, or roughly US$6,900.
The Datang’s price band runs from 239,900 to 309,900 yuan, or roughly $36,000 to $46,500.
A single Datang generates the top-line revenue of five to six F3s.
Across its initial 150,000 pre-orders, the aggregate sales value is roughly in line with 900,000 entry-level cars.
The margin pivot is achieved entirely on a manganese-rich, nickel-free battery.
Every Datang runs on what BYD calls its second-generation Blade Battery.
Analysts believe the battery uses Lithium Manganese Iron Phosphate (LMFP) chemistry based on patent filings and its 3.8-volt platform.
BYD has not officially confirmed the cathode material, and a regulatory filing for an earlier Blade 2.0 vehicle described a standard LFP pack.
The formulation drops nickel and cobalt in favor of lithium, iron, phosphorus, and manganese.
BYD rates Datang’s top-tier pack at 950 kilometers of CLTC range.
The top-tier option features a 130.15-kWh pack that reportedly delivers real-world highway range well above 600 kilometers.
Indonesia pursued an OPEC-style mineral cartel to weaponize its vast nickel reserves and extract outsized profits from the global EV transition.

Chinese battery-maker CATL’s integrated $6 billion nickel mining, refining, and cell-manufacturing complex in Indonesia was designed as a showcase for downstream ambitions but has become a case study in sovereign risk.
Throughout 2026, Indonesia implemented abrupt mandates including sudden mining-quota cuts, arbitrary export levies, rigid foreign-exchange retention rules, and last-minute policy reversals.
Major Chinese nickel smelters are already throttling output, and planned ambitious capacity expansions are now on hold.
Appetite for a nickel-centric industrial model has cooled fast, hitting major capital commitments from Japan’s Sumitomo Metal Mining, LG Energy Solution, and Singaporean resource houses.
The Indonesian rupiah recently touched a record low of over 18,000 per dollar.
Fitch Ratings cut Indonesia’s sovereign credit outlook to negative, reflecting broader capital flight.
Indonesia’s policy instability has accelerated a technical substitution already underway in EV battery-making.
Advanced solid-state pipelines remain deeply committed to high-nickel cathodes, modifying only the electrolyte layer rather than cathode chemistry.

The real existential threat to Indonesia’s model is the rapid, mass-market scaling of manganese-rich platforms like LMFP, which can compete with or displace high-nickel ternary cells even in premium vehicles.
Automakers increasingly treat de-nickelization not just as cost-cutting, but as a geopolitical hedge against unpredictable resource-exporting governments.
Within Southeast Asia’s domestic auto markets, real growth is going to low-cost, zero-nickel systems split between sodium-ion platforms and manganese-rich chemistries.
CATL’s 15-gigawatt-hour high-nickel ternary facility in Indonesia depends almost entirely on premium export markets in Europe and North America.
That niche is shrinking as automaking giants including BMW, Volkswagen, and Volvo aggressively diversify their cell procurement to reduce exposure to Indonesian supply chains and ESG risks.
Strict US Foreign Entity of Concern rules and Inflation Reduction Act tax credits explicitly bar Chinese-majority joint ventures from subsidy eligibility.
CATL’s Indonesian project phase-one production is pushed to late 2026, with full capacity delayed until 2031.
The US IRA subsidy window closes in 2032, leaving at most six years of subsidized, high-margin operations for the facility.
Large battery plants typically need an eight-to-10-year runway to break even.
Battery-industry forecasts broadly agree that manganese-rich, nickel-free cathodes will dominate mainstream EV production by decade’s end.
Indonesia’s mining sector generates more than $32 billion a year in overseas revenue but remains tied to low-margin, primary downstream processing.

Optimistic backers point to the operational flexibility of local Rotary Kiln Electric Furnace (RKEF) facilities, which could theoretically be reverse-converted from battery-grade nickel matte back into raw Ferronickel or Nickel Pig Iron for stainless steel production.
Trading high-margin energy-transition feedstock for cyclical, low-margin bulk steelmaking undercuts the investment case for Indonesia’s nickel push.
Western and Chinese steel mills are absorbing regional nickel intermediates due to scrap shortages.
Wood Mackenzie’s Global Nickel Supply-Demand Outlook puts traditional stainless steel demand at roughly 72% of total global nickel supply.
BYD’s second-generation LMFP blade battery is the vanguard of a broader zero-nickel transition alongside sodium-ion and high-voltage manganese platforms scaling across major automotive platforms.
Long-term passenger vehicle nickel demand faces a steep decline.
The reported collapse of an Indonesia-Philippines nickel alliance over clashing commercial interests will further weaken Jakarta’s leverage over global nickel supply.
S&P Global commodity analysis found that Jakarta’s mining-quota cuts triggered faster nickel output growth in the Philippines, Madagascar, and parts of Africa.
Indonesia retains steady low-grade nickel demand from stainless steel production, but its ambition to monopolize high-value EV battery materials has collapsed.

The article is authored by Ju Liang, an independent policy analyst with over 20 years of experience in Southeast Asia, specializing in regional supply chain economics and commodity policy governance, based at Yunnan Agricultural University, China.
source_title: BYD battery breakthrough crashes Indonesia's nickel cartel dream - Asia Times

The 5 millionth vehicle produced was the all-new Telluride Hybrid, marking the first hybrid vehicle manufactured in Georgia.
Kia has begun full-scale hybrid production at its West Point, Georgia plant with the launch of the 2027 model year "All-New Telluride."

Since starting production in 2009, Kia Georgia has established itself as a key manufacturing hub for the automotive industry in the southeastern United States and grown into a leading regional employer.
The Telluride is produced exclusively at the West Point plant and was developed based on North American consumer demands and U.S. road conditions as a local strategic model.
The first-generation Telluride won major awards in 2020, including North American Car of the Year, World Car of the Year, and MotorTrend SUV of the Year.
Governor Brian Kemp joined local government officials and company employees at a commemorative ceremony to celebrate Kia Georgia's achievement.
Sean Yoon, president and CEO of Kia North America and head of the North America region, stated that Kia Georgia is the only plant in the world that produces the Telluride.
Reaching 5 million units and producing Georgia's first hybrid vehicle demonstrates Kia's technological advancement and future strategic direction, according to Sean Yoon.

Governor Brian Kemp noted that since its 2019 launch, the Telluride has become one of Kia's most successful models and credited the achievement to a close partnership between the local community and the state government.

Stuart Countess, chief administrative officer of Kia Georgia, stated that the 5 million production milestone reflects employee dedication and that adding hybrid models to internal combustion and electric vehicle production proves the plant's flexibility and technical capabilities.

facts: Randy Jackson, Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia, Inc.’s (KMMG) director of human resources and administration, addressed the Rotary Club of Carrollton at Sunset Hills Country Club in Carrollton, Ga. on June 10, 2008.
Jackson discussed the automotive industry's fast pace during the meeting.
Kia received a benchmark-setting 43,000+ applications during its online application phase earlier in 2008.
Many of the job applications came from the Carroll County area.
Jackson highlighted Kia’s relationship with Hyundai.
The plant site covers 2,245 acres.
Indirect jobs are linked to Kia through suppliers and other businesses locating in the area due to Kia’s decision to come to West Point, Ga.
Jackson stated, “The excitement level is overwhelming throughout this state.”
At full production, the KMMG plant will produce around 300,000 vehicles a year.
Kia’s policy requires potential employees to work 40 hours of pre-hire time before being offered a job to ensure they are a good fit for the company.
Jackson emphasized Kia’s goal to hire people who will stay long-term and provide extensive on-the-job training upfront to ensure mutual good decisions.
Jackson noted that local support through the purchase of Kia vehicles will help the plant grow.
Jackson, born in Macon, Ga., stated he never thought he would return to the state 25 years later to lead what is the largest project in the history of Georgia.
KMMG’s culture aims to blend Korean and American practices on a foundation of trust and open two-way communications to deliver the “Best of the Best” in North America.
Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia, Inc. (KMMG) is the first manufacturing site in North America for Seoul-based Kia Motors Corporation.
KMMG is located on 2,200 acres in West Point, Georgia.
The plant was scheduled to begin production in the last quarter of 2009.
At full capacity, the plant will employ approximately 2,500 team members.
source_title: Kia Director Addresses Rotary Club of Carrollton - Kia Georgia

canonical_name: Quick Start Program (Technical College System of Georgia)
facts: During fiscal year 2022, economic development projects in Georgia set an all-time record for job creation and investment.

The 358 located projects supported by the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team will result in more than 51,132 new jobs and $21.2 billion in investment for the state.
Gov. Brian Kemp has touted Georgia as the best place to do business, with Site Selection magazine ranking it as having the nation’s best business climate for most of the past decade.

Rick Douglas, director of people and culture for Kia Georgia Inc., stated that GDEcD made it easy for the Korean automaker to select West Point, Georgia, in March 2006 for its automotive assembly plant.
The Kia Georgia facility employs over 3,000 Georgians, covers 2,200 acres, and represents a total capital investment of $1.8 billion.

Before the ink was dry on Kia’s announcement to build in Georgia, the Technical College System of Georgia (TCSG) dispatched its Quick Start program to create a training program for the 3,000 automotive assembly jobs.

Jay Neely, Vice President of Law & Public Affairs at Gulfstream Aerospace, noted that Georgia’s significant investment in the Quick Start training center adds significantly to its capabilities, citing the Quick Start Advanced Manufacturing Center in Savannah as an example.

The U.S. labor force participation rate has never been lower, with millions of pandemic-era dropouts yet to return, and some states' community college education inadequately aligned to employer needs.
Georgia’s Quick Start program is ranked the No. 1 worker training program in the nation for 13 years in a row.

Early in its operation, Kia Georgia faced significant difficulty recruiting maintenance team members with automation and control equipment experience, which Quick Start and TCSG resolved by creating a Maintenance Certification Program based on Kia’s needs.

The customized training assessment for Kia was developed by Quick Start before moving to West Georgia Technical College (WGTC), where 85 Kia Georgia participants have enrolled and the program was added to WGTC’s public curriculum.
Steven Jahng, director of external affairs for SK Battery America Inc. in Jackson County, identified the Georgia Quick Start program as the key incentive for the Korean-owned EV battery producer.
SK Battery picked a site just off Interstate 85 in Commerce, Georgia, in November 2018 for a $1.67 billion plant, with its incentives agreement based on hiring 2,600 people.
According to Jahng, SK Battery is ahead of schedule toward its 2,600-employee target, and its hiring plans have accelerated.
Jahng stated that proximity to Atlanta (a 40-mile radius covering Gwinnett County and Athens) sold them on the Jackson County site due to population exposure.
Gov. Brian Kemp and GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson provided leadership and support that sealed the deal for SK Battery.
EV sector announcements in Georgia include Illinois-based Rivian building a $5 billion EV plant near Social Circle and Madison (about 45 miles east of Atlanta).
Korean-based Hyundai Motor Group is spending $5.5 billion to build battery and EV plants in Savannah.
Norwegian company FREYR Battery is investing $2.57 billion to construct an electric car battery plant in Newnan, Coweta County (just southwest of Atlanta).

GDEcD’s Wilson noted that Quick Start has been operating since 1968 and provides a track record of preparing Georgians to fill available jobs, with companies able to leverage the program repeatedly during ramp-up.
For Kia’s initial need to hire 1,000 people, there were 30,000 applications; Quick Start identified the best candidates and created a training program specific to Kia’s needs.

Rodger Brown, executive director of Quick Start (overseeing the process for 21 years), stated that for the SK Battery project, they visited facilities in Korea, documented equipment/tasks, and fabricated training stations that mirrored the plant's workstations rather than just simulating them.
Brown noted that Quick Start has worked with Kia for 15 years and will build, design, equip, and operate a training center for Hyundai in Bryan County.

Gulfstream Aerospace is investing $150 million to expand its manufacturing plant in Savannah, adding a 262,000-square-foot facility with G400 production and wing lines plus new material delivery and distribution areas.
As many as 200 new positions could be created by Gulfstream’s expansion, with Quick Start assisting in training.
Jay Neely highlighted Gulfstream’s long working relationship with Georgia Quick Start for decades, praising its flexibility and ability to tailor training to specific employer requirements.
Wilson added that transportation sector companies need a skilled workforce and can leverage Quick Start's integrated technical college system across rural communities.
source_title: Georgia Supercharges Growth Through Worker Training – Site Selection Magazine

facts: Kia Georgia celebrated 15 years of production on November 16, 2024.
Kia Georgia is Kia’s first North American manufacturing facility.
To date, Kia Georgia has helped create more than 14,000 local jobs.
To date, Kia Georgia has produced more than 4.5 million vehicles.
The Kia Georgia project was announced on March 13, 2006.
A groundbreaking ceremony for the facility took place on October 20, 2006.
Kia Georgia began production of its first vehicle, a Kia Sorento, on November 16, 2009.
The Kia Georgia facility sits on a 2,200-acre site.
The facility has a production capacity of 350,000 vehicles per year.
Kia Georgia is nearing completion of a $217 million expansion.
The $217 million expansion will allow the plant to produce EVs alongside its traditional products.
Kia Georgia currently produces the all-electric EV9 SUV.
Kia Georgia currently produces the Telluride, Sorento, and Sportage SUVs.
source_title: Kia - Autos Drive America

facts: Between July 1, 2023, and June 30, 2024, GDEcD’s Global Commerce team supported 429 facility expansions and new locations.
These expansions resulted in more than $20.3 billion in investment and the commitment of 26,900 new private sector jobs statewide.
83% of expansions and new locations are directed to communities outside the 10-county Atlanta region, representing more than 18,300 new jobs and $12.5 billion in investment.
Governor Brian Kemp emphasized prioritizing growth in rural Georgia and committing to continuing momentum by investing in education, workforce development, and infrastructure.

Lt. Governor Burt Jones highlighted the need for a sufficient workforce pipeline built by the Technical College System of Georgia, University System of Georgia, College and Career Academies, and private-public partnerships.
Speaker of the House Jon Burns credited cutting taxes, reducing burdensome regulations, and making historic investments in education, workforce development, and infrastructure for delivering results.
During FY2024, Governor and First Lady Kemp led economic development missions to Korea and Switzerland focusing on strengthening relationships with Hyundai Motor Group and its supplier network.
In the prior year, Governor and First Lady Kemp led similar missions to Israel, France, and Switzerland.
Georgia attracted more than 8,100 new jobs and $5.9 billion in investments from international companies expanding or locating in the U.S. market this past fiscal year.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson highlighted Georgia's strong partnerships, diverse industry landscape, and range of locations as major advantages for businesses.
The State of Georgia celebrated more than 50 years of continuous representation in Europe and Japan, which remain among the top sources of foreign direct investment.
The coming fiscal year will mark 40 years of continuous representation in Korea, a partnership that earned GDEcD the James A. Van Fleet Award from the Korea Society.
All markets where Georgia maintains business and trade representation accounted for 29% of total new jobs and investments in FY2024.
GDEcD Commissioner Kristi Brigman highlighted the state's transition from agribusiness and manufacturing to new heights in mobility and technology, celebrating project milestones across the state.
Manufacturing, including aerospace, automotive, and e-mobility sectors, accounted for over 50% of expansions or new locations in FY2024.
Kia Georgia added the EV9 to its lineup at its facility in Troup County.
Toyota Industries Corporation established a campus in Jackson County.
StandardAero operates facilities in Richmond County.
International life sciences company Gerresheimer completed a recent expansion in Fayette County.
Suppliers for Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America announced new locations across Bryan, Candler, Chatham, Coweta, Effingham, Harris, and Laurens Counties.
SOLARCYCLE, an innovator in solar panel recycling, announced plans for a new location in Polk County to support Qcells operations.
Investments in food processing and agribusiness totaled more than $1.3 billion in FY2024.
Japanese probiotic beverage company Yakult located in Bartow County.
Korean bakery CJ Foodville located in Hall County.
Walmart will source milk from local dairy farmers at a future dairy processing facility in Lowndes County.
Technology and corporate hubs are creating at least 4,400 new jobs as a result of FY2024 wins.
Cargill Corporation is establishing a new office hub in the City of Atlanta.
U.S. Soccer's future headquarters and national training center will be located in Fayette County.
Georgia earned the rank of No. 1 state for business from Area Development magazine for 10 consecutive years.
Georgia earned the No. 1 spot in Site Selection magazine’s annual Prosperity Cup rankings for successful investment recruitment in 2023.
Georgia was awarded a Gold Shovel by Area Development for achieving significant success in attracting new business investment and job creation in 2023.
Business Facilities magazine ranked Georgia as the No. 1 state for EV investments.
Business Facilities magazine ranked Georgia as a top 10 state for food processing, cybersecurity, aerospace and defense, and AI growth hubs.

links.context: View further details of state-announced economic development projects

source_title: Georgia Job Creation & Momentum Remains Strong - LaGrange CEO

canonical_name: Georgia Senate Bill on Income Tax and Corporate Incentives
facts: A Georgia state Senate bill would cut the state’s flat income-tax rate for individuals and corporations from 5.19% to 4.99% starting this year.
The legislation would exempt the first $50,000 of earnings for individual filers and the first $100,000 for married couples from income tax beginning in 2027.
If passed, Georgia could see a $5.1 billion decline in revenue in fiscal 2028, equivalent to about 13% of its budget.
The bill proposes ending dozens of incentives and exemptions for businesses including data centers, cigarette exporters, medical-equipment manufacturers, and banks.

Manufacturing giants Hyundai Motor Group, Rivian Automotive Inc., and Kia Corp. have accepted incentive packages with property-tax breaks, sales-tax exemptions, and job tax credits to open large warehouses and plants in Georgia.

A Rivian spokesperson stated the EV maker will continue to work closely with state and local partners in Georgia and remains committed to building a state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Stanton Springs.
Kia declined to comment on the bill, and Hyundai did not respond to requests for comment.
Amazon.com Inc., Meta Platforms Inc., and Microsoft Corp. operate data centers in Georgia that currently take advantage of sales and use tax exemptions on high-tech equipment.
The Senate bill would phase out the sales and use tax exemptions currently used by Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft for their Georgia data centers.
Republican Senator Blake Tillery, the measure’s sponsor, said the legislation targets corporations to avoid burdening residents with sales taxes typically used to offset income-tax cuts.
Senator Tillery stated, “All we’re trying to do is value people over corporate credits,” and added, “You shouldn’t be getting the credit just because you were wealthy enough to afford a lobbyist.”
Since the Inflation Reduction Act passed in 2022, state and federal incentives have positioned Georgia at the center of a surge in clean-energy manufacturing and AI infrastructure.
Don Bolia, a lobbyist representing businesses opposed to the bill, warned that companies could simply leave or reduce their business in the state if exemptions are cut.
Khara Boender, director of state policy at The Data Center Coalition, said the bill would discourage further investment and send a signal that Georgia is closed for business.
State projections indicate data centers could see their current tax burden more than double if the proposal becomes law.
A December report by Georgia’s Department of Audits and Accounts found data centers supported more than 5,000 jobs in 2025.

Senate Majority Leader Jason Anavitarte noted lawmakers leaned on a 2025 University of Georgia Carl Vinson Institute of Government report stating 70% of state data centers would exist without tax exemptions.

Anavitarte stated, “It’s our pro-business environment, historically low taxes, having a balanced budget, truly policies that allow the free market to thrive,” and believes companies will stay even if tax credits disappear.
Dave Williams, chief policy officer at Metro Atlanta Chamber, urged lawmakers to reduce income and property taxes while maintaining business incentives.

Critics charge Senator Tillery, who is running for lieutenant governor, and Republican legislators crafted the measure to claim credit for affordability measures heading into November’s midterm elections.
Democratic Senate Minority Leader Harold Jones II criticized the shift, noting Republicans previously credited these tax credits with making Georgia the number one state to do business.
The Georgia House has until April 2 to vote on the bill and can propose edits that could send it back to the Senate.
If passed by both chambers, the bill would go to Republican Governor Brian Kemp for his signature.
Governor Kemp’s spokesperson declined to comment while the legislation was being debated.
Other Republican-led states including Florida, Missouri, South Carolina, Mississippi, Oklahoma, and Kentucky have advanced or proposed income and property-tax cuts over the past year.

facts: Kia Corp plans to produce hybrid vehicles at Hyundai Motor's new U.S. factory starting in mid-2026.
Kia CEO Song Ho-sung announced the plan without disclosing specific models.
Hyundai owns a 35% stake in Kia Motors.
Hyundai broke ground for an all-electric car factory in Georgia in 2022 under former President Joe Biden's administration.
The Biden administration promoted EV-friendly policies at the time of the groundbreaking.
Hyundai is shifting strategy to add hybrid cars to the factory's production lines due to cooling demand for pure electric cars.
President Donald Trump's administration is threatening to end EV subsidies.
Kia vehicles will represent 40% of the Georgia facility's total production, according to CEO Song Ho-sung.
The announcement was made on the sidelines of an opening ceremony for the factory on Wednesday.
Hyundai plans to expand the factory's annual production capacity to 500,000 vehicles from an originally planned 300,000.
The expansion brings Hyundai and Kia's combined total U.S. production capacity to 1.2 million vehicles per year.
The upgraded plan is part of a $21 billion investment package announced by Trump and Hyundai chairman Euisun Chung at the White House earlier this week.
Trump announced 25% import tariffs on cars and auto parts on Wednesday.
The tariff move may hurt companies, especially Asian automakers, which are among the largest vehicle exporters to the U.S.
After Mexico, South Korea is the world's largest exporter of vehicles to the United States, followed by Japan, according to S&P data.
Kia Motors currently produces most of its hybrid cars, including its Sportage hybrid crossover, in South Korea.
The new U.S. factory would help achieve Kia's target of boosting U.S. sales to 1.25 million vehicles a year.
Kia's current U.S. sales volume is 850,000 vehicles.
source_title: Kia to produce hybrid cars at Hyundai's Georgia factory from next year - NewsBreak

facts: The Georgia Manufacturing Alliance conducted three tour groups at Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia.

The Georgia Manufacturing Alliance was founded in 2008 to support the state's manufacturing community through plant tours, educational sessions, and networking opportunities.

facts: Located at 1711 HWY 41 SOUTH, CALHOUN, GA 30703.
Operates under NAICS code 336360 for Motor Vehicle Seating and Interior Trim Manufacturing.
Had one OSHA inspection on record as of February 28, 2007, resulting in 6 violations and $5,000 in penalties.
Serious violations accounted for 50% (3 out of 6) of the total citations at this location.
No willful or repeat violations have been recorded at this establishment.
All inspections at this establishment have been closed.
The most recent OSHA inspection was opened on February 28, 2007, and has since been closed.

Violations cited include Control of Hazardous Energy (Lockout/Tagout), Medical Services and First Aid, Powered Industrial Trucks, General Requirements for All Machines (Machine Guarding), Electrical - General Requirements, and Electrical - Wiring Methods, Components, and Equipment.
Penalties for the six violations were $1,000, $750, $2,000, $950, $150, and $150 respectively.

The NAICS 336360 industry group comprises establishments primarily engaged in manufacturing motor vehicle gasoline engines and engine parts, motor vehicle electrical and electronic equipment, steering and suspension components, brakes, and transmission parts.

Common workplace hazards in this industry include machine guarding deficiencies on stamping and machining equipment, exposure to metalworking fluids and coolants, noise-induced hearing loss, caught-in hazards from automated production lines, amputation risks from presses, and chemical exposure.

Employers in motor vehicle seating and interior trim manufacturing reported 3.3 work-related injuries and illnesses per 100 full-time workers in 2024, according to the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics Survey of Occupational Injuries and Illnesses.

facts: Superior Essex, a global leader in the magnet wire and communication cables industry, is announcing the next phase in the rebranding of its magnet wire business.
As part of its recent buyout of Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire, the company will change its brand name to Essex Solutions.
Under the new name, Essex Solutions will encompass all current Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire locations worldwide.
Essex Energy in Italy will now be included in the organization, further reinforcing the company’s global reach and capabilities.
The related businesses Lacroix + Kress, IVA Insulations, and Hi-Wire will join the Essex Solutions organization while continuing to operate under their existing brand identities.
This consolidation will enable better coordination and collaboration among the brands, resulting in improved efficiency and effectiveness.

Daniel Choi, President and CEO of Superior Essex, stated that aligning the brands under one grouping allows the company to offer a complete suite of solutions for customers and take further advantage of vertical integration and global reach.
Choi noted the company's ability to proactively shape the xEV automotive industry, as well as power, electrification, and renewable energy markets experiencing rapid transformations.
During the transition period, customers and stakeholders may see materials bearing either the existing Essex Furukawa or the new Essex Solutions name.
All brand name changes are expected to be completed by April 30, 2025.
Essex Solutions will unveil a new logo in the near future to reflect its evolution and commitment to innovation and excellence.
Superior Essex is the parent brand of Essex Solutions, Superior Essex Communications, IVA, Lacroix + Kress, Hi-Wire, and Essex Brownell.
The company has over 3,000 employees across 11 countries on three continents.
Superior Essex is instrumental in creating disruptive technological advancements in the electrification of the automotive, energy, and industrial sectors.
The company is committed to pioneering smart building technology while setting the pace on sustainability.
Essex Solutions maintains an office at 5770 Powers Ferry Rd. NW Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30327.
Additional information about the company is available online.
source_title: Superior Essex Announces Essex Solutions, Relaunching Magnet Wire Brand | Essex Solutions

facts: Superior Essex is headquartered at 5770 Powers Ferry Rd. NW Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30327 – USA.

facts: Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire released its first annual sustainability report and announced CO2 reduction goals tied to its Vision 2030 initiative.
The sustainability report will be published annually going forward to track progress toward "Vision 2030: A sustainable future, our driving force."
The report covers results and targets for avoiding landfill waste at production facilities, reducing energy consumption, and achieving renewable energy goals.
Vision 2030 is built on three core values: breakthrough innovation, eco-friendly production, and agile digitalization.
CEO Daniel Choi stated that the report will help the company achieve its publicly set sustainability goals.
CEO Daniel Choi said: "The level of transparency in this report reflects the commitment we made when announcing Vision 2030 last April."
Austin Robinson, Sustainability Project Manager at Essex Furukawa, noted that consistent communication efforts will enable internal and external transparency.
Austin Robinson stated: "On the path to a sustainable future, we must consistently analyze our data, find additional solutions, and take a leading role in sharing results."
The Vision 2030 journey serves as a benchmark and course-setting for the future, with the first report acting only as an initial milestone.
Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire LLC is the leading global provider of magnet wire (Lackdraht).
The company's magnet wire is used by most major OEMs, Tier-1 suppliers, and industry leaders in automotive, energy, industrial, commercial, and residential construction markets.
The Atlanta-based company operates as a global joint venture founded in 2020 between Essex Magnet Wire and Furukawa Electric Co., Ltd.

facts: City council approved the replacement and rerouting of an original Camp Bowie water line that supplies the Superior Essex plant.
The water line along with its easement was given to the city by the federal government in 1948 and has breaks that have caused problems for the plant this past year.
A briefing sheet states that the water line has had four breaks in the past 12 months, causing plant production to cease for about eight hours each time.
The line goes through the middle of the equipment yard, making it difficult to access for repairs.
Heavy equipment and materials are stored on top of the line, mostly likely contributing to the weakness of the line.
A new easement will be created and a new 12” PVC water line replacing the old 16” cast iron line will be laid at the perimeter of the property with the approval now granted.
Materials are expected to take 6-8 weeks to be delivered, and installation should begin as soon as the materials arrive.
The city will pay for labor, equipment and engineering work which will cost $26,068 plus attorney and filing fees at contract rates.
Superior Essex will pay for the survey establishing the new easement at a cost of $1500.
The Brownwood Economic Development Corporation will pay for the materials totaling $54,525 as the city does not have funds budgeted for the materials.
source_title: City Approves Replacement and Rerouting of Water Line to Superior Essex | Brownwood News

facts: Superior Essex successfully completed the acquisition of the remaining minority interest in Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire from Furukawa Electric Co., Ltd.
The acquisition transaction was completed effective April 30, 2024.
The completion establishes Superior Essex as the sole owner of the brand, assets, and operations previously conducted by the global joint venture with Furukawa Electric.
Superior Essex plans to relaunch its magnet wire business under a new company name, which will be announced later in 2024.
The company will continue to operate all current facilities of the Essex Furukawa joint venture worldwide.
Current facilities include magnet wire facilities in Japan and Malaysia (Kuala Lumpur) that were contributed by Furukawa Electric in October 2020.
Daniel Choi serves as the CEO of Superior Essex.

CEO Daniel Choi stated, “Over the last several years we have been able to accomplish many great things working with the team from Furukawa Electric. I am proud of what was created and thankful for their contributions.”
The acquisition solidifies Superior Essex's control over global operations and reinforces its commitment to delivering products and services to customers worldwide.
Superior Essex plans to continuously respond to market demands in the xEV automotive industry, power, electrification, and renewable energy markets.
The company aims to strengthen its position by actively pursuing decarbonization and sustainability initiatives.

Superior Essex employs over 3,000 people across 11 countries on three continents.
