The company is instrumental in creating disruptive technological advancements in the electrification of the automotive, energy, and industrial sectors.
Superior Essex is committed to pioneering smart building technology while setting the pace on sustainability.
Furukawa Electric Group was established in 1884 and includes 127 group companies with approximately 50,000 employees worldwide.

Furukawa Electric provides technologies, products, and services for infrastructure fields (information and energy), automotive systems fields, and electronics fields using core technologies of metals, polymers, photonics, and high frequency.

facts: Superior Essex Inc. is based in Atlanta, Georgia.

Currently, it assembles the award-winning Kingquad line of ATVs for the North American and global markets.

Nobuyuki Yamane, president of Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corp., stated that the last couple of years have been very difficult due to the overall economic situation and its effect on the entire ATV industry.
The company is looking forward to a rebound in the ATV industry as it focuses its Management Plan for FY2011 under the theme '10 Years and Beyond'.
The plant has recently increased production capacity.
The plant recalled some team members affected by previous workforce reductions.
source_title: Suzuki ATV plant reaches milestone | Powersports Business

entity_name: Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corporation ATV factory
facts: The Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corporation ATV factory is located in Rome, Georgia.
An ATV's frame starts out as straight pieces of pipe that are bent into shapes by hydraulic presses and cut to length by robotic plasma cutters.
It takes about six hours for one of these frame pieces to make its way into a finished ATV.
Small frame pieces known around the factory as "kiddie parts" combine to make a "mother part."
Human welders perform some welding work where robot arms cannot fit into very small spaces, using special masks with light-sensitive windows that automatically darken during welding.
Once frames are fully assembled and welded, an automated system cleans, paints, and bakes them.
People assemble handlebars and other components before sending them to the assembly line.
The Rome facility manufactures red and black fenders using an injection-molding machine that uses 3,300 tons of pressure to mold melted resin granules.
Some of the black fenders receive a camouflage coating at another facility.
After fenders come off the conveyor, people trim them and apply plastic to protect them while they cure.
Stickers and logos are applied to the fenders at another station before they go to the assembly line.
Different fender shapes require different molds that are extremely heavy and require a 25-ton crane to move.
Employees use pneumatic and battery-powered tools on the assembly line to mount the engine, shock absorbers, fenders, and tires.
When the article was written, an ATV-in-process spent 102 seconds at each station on the assembly line.
As of July 2006, an ATV-in-process spends 89 seconds at each station.
At this speed, the Rome plant can make about 310 ATVs per day, which is equivalent to approximately 80,000 ATVs per year.
The last two stations on the assembly line are for inspection and packaging.
A seesaw-like device moves each ATV off of the assembly-line pedestal and onto its wheels.
Employees add gasoline to the tank and drive the ATV on a drum tester to ensure it runs properly at high speeds without actually moving.
The factory uses between 200 and 300 gallons of gasoline a week for testing purposes.
Each finished ATV is placed on a wooden pallet, boxed with heavy-duty staple guns, and carried by a forklift directly to a shipping truck.
The Rome plant does not include storage space for completed ATVs.
More than 250 people work in the Rome factory.
The entire manufacturing and assembly process, including the manufacturing of engines and other parts at other factories, takes more than 300 people.
If you include the people who design the ATVs, plan the production process and perform other support roles, it takes more than 350 people to build one ATV.

facts: Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corporation (SMAC) has become a 100% subsidiary of Suzuki Motor USA, LLC (SMO) effective March 1, 2024.
SMAC is based in Rome, Georgia and was founded in 2001.
SMAC serves as Suzuki’s All-Terrain Vehicle (ATV) manufacturing facility.

Suzuki ATVs are sold in the United States, Canada, and nearly 25 countries worldwide.
Prior to the change, SMAC stock was owned by Suzuki Motor Corporation (SMC) (20%) and Suzuki Motor of America, Inc. (SMAI) (80%).
SMAI is owned by SMC and acts as the holding company for Suzuki companies operating in the United States.
The organizational change consolidates ATV sales and production arms to strengthen the overall ATV business in the United States and globally.
A key objective of the move is strengthening the supply chain and purchasing policies for locally sourced parts.
SMO and SMAC leaders expect improved responsiveness to the ATV market by more accurately linking production to sales demand.
SMO plans to improve inventory management for both the company and its dealer body to protect sales margins, flooring costs, and profitability.
The restructuring will create better, more direct communication between SMO and SMAC, fostering a collaborative environment between Rome- and Brea-based teams.
Masami Haga, President of SMO, stated that expanding commitment to the growth of the entire powersports business is a primary goal of the change.
The move sets the stage for new Suzuki ATV offerings and enhancements to existing products in the future.
Suzuki’s United States distribution headquarters are located in Brea, California, where the company relocated in 1981 after first entering the U.S. market in 1963.
Suzuki is credited with introducing the first four-wheel ATV in 1982 with the LT125 model.
ATV industry sales are generally steady throughout the calendar year with demand typically increasing in the fall months.

The recall covers approximately 3,400 units of model year 2016 and 2017 LT-A750XP King Quad ATVs.
The recalled vehicles were sold nationwide at Suzuki ATV dealers from September 2015 through November 2016.
The recalled ATVs retailed for $8,600 to $9,600.
A battery failure in the KingQuad 750 can cause the engine to stop running while in use, potentially causing a crash.

entity_name: Ceramic Materials for Battery and Energy Storage Systems
canonical_name: Ceramic Materials for Battery and Energy Storage Systems
facts: Ceramic materials are a key component of modern energy storage technologies, used as solid electrolytes, protective coatings, or components in industrial high-temperature processes.
Ceramics enhance the safety, service life, and efficiency of batteries and storage systems for both electric vehicles and stationary storage.
Ceramic electrolytes are non-flammable and significantly increase the thermal stability of cells.
High ionic conductivity in ceramics enables greater energy density while reducing losses.
Ceramics are heat-resistant and durable, making them ideal for high-temperature processes in battery production and operation.
Ceramic materials withstand aggressive electrolytes and prevent degradation due to their chemical resistance.
The long service life and recyclability of ceramics contribute to resource conservation and sustainability.
In solid-state batteries, ceramic electrolytes replace conventional liquid electrolytes.
Materials used as solid electrolytes include lithium-lanthanum-zirconate (LLZO), lithium sulfide compounds, or NASICON-like structures.
These solid electrolytes combine high ionic conductivity with temperature and voltage stability.
Solid ceramic electrolytes are non-flammable, enable more compact cell architectures, and increase energy density.
Thin ceramic coatings on polymer films serve as safety separators in conventional lithium-ion batteries.
Ceramic-reinforced separators prevent short circuits, increase thermal stability, and help avoid dangerous thermal runaways.
Ceramic-reinforced separators are now standard in high-quality battery cells, particularly in vehicle batteries and industrial energy storage systems.

Refractory linings made of alumina, mullite, spinel, or silicon carbide protect furnaces, reactors, and calcination plants from chemical attack and extreme temperature fluctuations during battery production.
These refractory materials ensure a stable manufacturing process from cathode material synthesis to the sintering of ceramic electrolytes.
Ceramic sensors monitor battery cells in real time using piezoelectric temperature and gas detectors.
These sensors provide early warning of overheating, leaks, or material degradation, ensuring safety in large-scale stationary storage systems.
Hybrid designs combining ceramic, polymer, and metal components enable more flexible cell architectures.
Additive manufacturing allows for 3D-printed ceramics that enable new geometries for electrolytes, separators, and housings.
Porous ceramics can simultaneously serve as barriers, conductors, and protective layers through functional integration.
Built-in sensors made of piezoceramic materials enable automatic monitoring.
Recyclable ceramic components reduce the environmental footprint of storage technology.
Ceramic solid electrolytes reduce fire risks and increase energy density for safe storage solutions.
Refractory ceramics improve process reliability and material yield in cell manufacturing.
Sensor-active ceramics enable real-time monitoring and adaptive control for smart batteries.
Lithium-lanthanum-zirconia (LLZO), alumina (Al₂O₃), silicon carbide (SiC), mullite, or spinel are used as electrolytes, separators, or refractory linings depending on the application.
Research is continuously being conducted on new ceramic mixed-phase materials and composites to optimize conductivity, stability, and manufacturing costs.
Ceramic electrolytes enable operation at higher voltages and temperatures, significantly increasing battery cell performance and service life.
Ceramics protect equipment from chemical and thermal stress in furnaces, reactors, and process chambers, ensuring consistent product quality.
Additive manufacturing, hybrid structures, and functional integration are making ceramics a key driver of innovation for next-generation batteries.
Smart ceramic materials can simultaneously conduct electricity, insulate, and actively contribute to energy conversion.
ceramitec is a trade fair and platform for high-performance ceramics that brings together developers, suppliers, and users to discover innovations, share knowledge, and forge partnerships.

facts: Amara Industries has acquired Thomson Plastics, a recognized leader in large-tonnage injection molding.
The acquisition merges a newly-focused custom solutions provider with a veteran of high-tech manufacturing to create a formidable entity for high-performance molded products.
Amara Industries was recently launched as an independent entity from the customs division of Gracious Living Corporation and now acts as the parent company to the Gracious Living group.

The deal absorbs Thomson Plastics' two advanced facilities located in Georgia and North Carolina.
The acquisition expands Amara Industries' manufacturing footprint to five locations.

The acquisition signals an aggressive growth strategy aimed at the industrial, automotive, and commercial sectors.
Amara Industries provides end-to-end custom manufacturing solutions ranging from design and engineering to materials science.
Thomson Plastics specializes in large-tonnage injection molding, a specialized field where less than 4% of U.S. plastics manufacturers compete.

Thomson Plastics produces massive single-piece components essential for automotive body panels, industrial containers, and large consumer goods.

Thomson Plastics operates with 95% automation, with its machinery integrated with 3- to 6-axis robotics.
Thomson Plastics has developed in-house expertise for designing, customizing, and maintaining its robotic systems and critical end-of-arm tooling.
The deep integration of robotics minimizes cycle times, reduces waste, and ensures manufacturing consistency.
The acquired technical portfolio includes gas-assisted molding, which reduces the weight and cycle time of large, dense parts.
The acquired technical portfolio also includes over-molding, a process for creating components with multiple materials such as soft-grip handles on power tools or automotive steering wheels.
The acquisition establishes a major advanced manufacturing presence in Georgia and North Carolina.
Since 2020, the Southeastern region has seen an influx of over 650,000 workers into production and warehouse roles, with job growth outpacing the national average.
Placing these capabilities in the Southeast addresses supply chain fragility for automotive, appliance, and industrial sector customers concentrated in the region.
Local sourcing in the Southeast enables faster delivery, reduced logistics costs, and greater agility to respond to market shifts.
The acquisition strengthens regional supply chains, making them more resilient and less dependent on overseas production.
The North American injection molding industry is projected to reach a value of over $81 billion by 2033.
Market growth is fueled by relentless demand from the automotive sector for lightweight components to improve fuel efficiency, as well as innovation in consumer goods and packaging.
The combined entity plans to integrate advanced materials such as composites and bioplastics to meet rising demands for customization and sustainability.

facts: TI Fluid Systems plc is a British-American multinational company which develops, manufactures and supplies automotive fluid storage, carrying and delivery systems.
The company serves the automotive aftermarket through Bundy, Walbro and Marwal brands.
Headquarters are located in Auburn Hills, Michigan, United States.
The company was founded in 2000, tracing its history back to the Bundy Corporation which was founded in 1922 and supplied petroleum fuel lines to the Ford Model T.
It was acquired by TI Group plc in 1988.
Smiths Group acquired TI Group in 2000 and transferred its newly acquired automotive business into a corporate entity in 2001 thereby creating TI Automotive.
In 2007, the company was acquired by a consortium of private equity investors.
The company was forced to go through a debt-to-equity swap in 2009 following the global economic downturn.
William "Bill" L. Kozyra was named chairman, chief executive officer and president of TI Automotive in May 2008.
In January 2015, the company was acquired by Bain Capital for $2.4 billion.
In October 2017, the company floated on the London Stock Exchange under its new name of TI Fluid Systems.
The company floated 25 percent of its shares during the 2017 listing.
In September 2023, it was announced TI Fluid Systems has acquired the Halásztelek-headquartered plastic injection moulding company, Cascade Engineering Europe Kft. for $27.7 million.
In December 2024, the directors agreed a takeover offer from ABC Technologies of Canada, a company controlled by Apollo Global Management.
The £1.83bn deal includes plans to cut up to 2,700 jobs worldwide, around 10% of TI Fluid Systems’ workforce, with staff at its Oxford headquarters being reduced by a third.
The court approved the transaction on April 8, 2025, allowing it to be completed.
It was listed on the London Stock Exchange until it was acquired by Toronto-based ABC Technologies in April 2025.
Revenue was €3,360.3 million in 2024.
The company had 25,600 employees in 2023.
Parent company is Apollo Global Management.
Key people include Manfred Wennemer (Chairman) and Hans Dieltjens (CEO and President).
The company has two divisions: fuel tank systems and fluid carrying systems.
Fluid carrying systems include components such as brakes, battery cooling and climate control.
source_title: TI Fluid Systems - Wikipedia

facts: TI Fluid Systems (LSE:TIFS) introduced an innovative approach for coolant fluid management on electrified vehicles.
The new technology is an Integrated Thermal Manifold assembly (ITMa).
ITMa provides a one-piece, light-weight, blow-molded plastic manifold to optimize complex line design.
The ITMa replaces multiple line bundle assemblies of thermal loops for heating and cooling on next generation electric vehicles.
Current EVs typically use multiple complex rubber, aluminum or multi-layer plastic lines that are packaged tightly together under the hood or chassis mounted.
TI Fluid Systems evolved their Fuel Tank and Delivery Systems (FTDS) division fuel tank blow molding technology to produce a one piece integrated thermal manifold approach.
The single piece blow molded plastic manifold provides improved flow performance for efficiency gains.
The design offers a lower part count for sub and final vehicle assembly.
The component features lower weight and reduced investment tooling costs.
TI Fluid Systems' FTDS division demonstrated further integration potential for fluid storage, specifically coolant reservoir expansion tanks.
The division also demonstrated integration potential for fluid temperature control using heat exchangers and sensors.
Integration potential extends to fluid delivery components such as pumps.
These integrated thermal modular assemblies are very bespoke to each OEM vehicle or application.
The modules can be engineered for various system types of multiple thermal loops.
Hans Dieltjens, COO, stated that the company's innovations have captured the attention of OEMs as they move from 'build-to-print' components to 'innovative collaboration' of EV sub-systems.
TI Fluid Systems positions itself as a differentiated supplier to leverage fluid know-how on new thermal EV challenges.

The company is successfully demonstrating the ability to pivot its fluid competencies, capabilities, and assets to provide a family of new break-through EV products with various levels of integration.
TI Fluid Systems received their first ITMa global EV platform application release for model year 2023 introduction.
The application release is with a leading high-volume European OEM.
TI Fluid Systems is a leading global manufacturer of fluid storage, carrying and delivery systems primarily for the light duty automotive market.
The company has nearly 100 years of automotive fluid systems experience.
TI Fluid Systems operates manufacturing facilities in 107 locations across 28 countries serving all major global OEMs.
source_title: TI Fluid Systems introduces new integrated thermal manifold assemblies -  Press Releases - TASS

facts: Terry Campbell will become President & Chief Executive Officer of the combined ABC Technologies and TI Fluid Systems group.
Alexander De Bock will become Chief Financial Officer of the combined group.
Hans Dieltjens, current President & Chief Executive Officer of TI Fluid Systems, will step down following completion of the acquisition.
ABC Technologies' acquisition of TI Fluid Systems remains on track to complete during the first half of 2025.
Terry Campbell became President & CEO of ABC in 2022 and previously held senior leadership roles at Johnson Controls, Magna International, and The Woodbridge Group.
Alexander De Bock was appointed CFO of TIFS in 2023 after spending over 15 years in the industry with finance leadership roles at WABCO and ZF Group.
The acquisition will be implemented by way of a court-sanctioned scheme of arrangement under Part 26 of the Companies Act 2006.
TIFS shareholders approved the recommended all-cash offer from ABC Technologies Acquisitions Limited on February 5, 2025.
TI Fluid Systems will announce the anticipated completion timetable via a Regulatory Information Service and its corporate website.
The combination of ABC Technologies and TI Fluid Systems aims to create a larger, more diversified group better positioned to navigate the transition to electrification.
ABC Technologies is a leading global manufacturer and supplier of custom technical plastics and lightweight innovations to the North American light vehicle industry.
ABC Technologies serves more than 25 major original equipment manufacturer customers across 8 countries.
ABC Technologies operates with a skilled workforce of over 11,000 team members.
ABC Technologies is majority owned by affiliated funds of Apollo Global Management, Inc., with Oaktree Capital Management, L.P. owning a minority equity interest.

TI Fluid Systems is a global innovator of thermal and fluid systems solutions for current and developing vehicle architectures, operating across 26 countries with over 100 years of automotive supply experience.

facts: Neways develops and manufactures mission-critical electronics for semiconductor, defense, and mobility customers.
Neways is positioned to double its revenue within five years.
The MPS and S&OP infrastructure is in place across the group’s operating companies.

A new Supply Chain Excellence Manager role has been created to focus on continuous improvement, optimizing local processes, sharpening consolidation, and ensuring supply chain performance matches market demands.
Neways has over fifty years of experience and strong in-house engineering capability.
Neways serves as a technological partner for microchip production, advanced defense technologies, and electric vehicle charging applications.
The company employs over 2,500 specialists across multiple countries who design, prototype, and scale up highly complex electronics.
Products include advanced modules and assemblies, specialized cables, wiring, and cutting-edge microelectronics solutions.
Since 2021, Neways has made substantial investments in its footprint, capacity, and capabilities.
Semiconductor demand is accelerating globally, prompting ambitious growth targets for the years ahead.
The organization values long-term partnerships, technical depth, and accountability over hierarchy.
Each operating company runs its own MPS and S&OP process, which are already consolidated at the group level.
The new role aims to drive continuous improvement through sharper local planning cycles, optimized ERP tools, and a consolidated group-level MPS and S&OP.
The Supply Chain Excellence Manager will connect operating companies, implement best practices, and leverage systems and processes to advance the planning function group-wide.

One core workstream is programmatic: continuously improving local MPS and S&OP processes across all operating companies by strengthening planning, deepening ERP utilization, professionalizing consolidation tooling, and rolling out best practices.

The second workstream focuses on the consolidated group-level MPS and S&OP: challenging the order book monthly, flagging capacity constraints early, and providing management information to the Supply Chain Director.
The ideal candidate requires serious experience in supply chain planning, procurement, and inventory management, along with relevant manufacturing experience.
The role reports directly to the Supply Chain Director.
Toine Verbruggen is the Supply Chain Director at Neways.
A new ERP system has just been rolled out across the company.
The consolidated S&OP process is currently under active development.
Automotive and defense are identified as two major market segments that are growing simultaneously for Neways.
The successful candidate will have a direct hand in defining the S&OP function for the next three years, with monthly visibility at board level.

Supply Chain Director Toine Verbruggen stated: “A smooth MPS and S&OP is critical in realizing our ambitions, and that starts with someone who connects the processes across our operating companies. You bring the group perspective in, standardize processes, focus on improvements and maintain the rhythm month after month. Get this right, and you will see it in the numbers: faster decisions at the top and a supply chain that keeps pace with the market.”

facts: In 2007, General Motors (GM) launched a global public tender for mobile robots, and SIASUN secured a place among GM’s global suppliers.
SIASUN has provided industrial upgrading services to over 4,000 enterprises across more than 40 countries.
SIASUN is the preferred robot supplier for numerous Fortune Global 500 companies.
As a global strategic partner, General Motors has continually deployed SIASUN’s chassis assembly mobile robot systems in its factories across the US, Canada, and the rest of the world since 2007.

These mobile robots primarily serve the chassis assembly stage of the automotive final assembly line, enabling high-precision synchronized conveying and assembly of the car body, chassis, and critical components.
The solution offers stable pacing, accurate positioning, flexible adaptation, and cross-factory replicability.
It effectively supports the standardization of GM’s global manufacturing system.

At a Mexican factory of a globally renowned, ultra-large electronic information conglomerate, SIASUN industrial robots transport LCD panels and perform online panel position corrections via a vision system.
Paired with specialized fixtures, the robots autonomously adapt to vacuum gripping, film peeling, and assembly of panels of various sizes.
The new generation of bezel-less televisions produced here is widely exported to numerous European and American countries.
SIASUN successfully developed a collaborative robot palletizing system for the US factory of a leading global medical device manufacturer.
Integrated with an electric vertical lifting module, the collaborative robot performs high-density palletizing tasks—stacking 10 layers high and 12 boxes per layer—with stability and precision.
The system features online barcode scanning integrated before the palletizing station.
If an unreadable barcode or defective box is detected, the robot automatically diverts it to a designated No-Good (NG) rejection station.
This automated quality inspection and defect handling provides a highly efficient automation solution for the packaging phase of medical tubing manufacturing.

Boasting over 20 years of experience in automotive seat component welding automation, SIASUN has supplied over 10 laser welding machines and 20 arc welding machines to the Mexican factory of a prominent automotive seating system manufacturer over the past five years.

SIASUN’s seat component welding business covers the welding and manufacturing of critical structural parts, including backrest frames, seat pans, recliners, slide rails, lead screws, height adjusters, lock hooks, electric drives, and seat belts.
The standardized seat belt retractor assembly and inspection lines provided by SIASUN to a global automotive parts giant have earned multiple cross-border repeat orders.

At the client’s Mexican facility, SIASUN’s production line enables the flexible manufacturing of multiple product models, achieving a cycle time of up to 10 seconds and an Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) of over 90%.
The production line features automatic adjustment and correction functions at stations such as bending and press-fitting.
SIASUN has launched a standardized, modular smart warehousing solution that empowers supply chain upgrades in the North American Fast-Moving Consumer Goods (FMCG) sector.
The project is currently active in North America.

By leveraging a high-bay warehouse capacity of 5,000 pallets, 6 intelligent shuttle systems, Robot Control System (RCS) scheduling, automated guided forklifts, and a Warehouse Management System (WMS), it achieves fully unmanned operations.
This completely complies with the standards of leading global retailers, helping clients reduce costs and enhance efficiency.
SIASUN has established a global strategic partnership with a renowned US electric vehicle manufacturer.
Since 2023, SIASUN has continuously integrated its self-developed mobile robot systems into multiple factories across the US, with over 100 units deployed to date.
The project primarily handles the efficient transport and cycle synchronization of battery packs on the shop floor, supporting critical logistics within new energy vehicle manufacturing.
Delivering stable operations, flexible scheduling, precise docking, and multi-scenario scalability, the solution adapts to diverse line paces and station requirements.
It significantly boosts battery pack logistics efficiency and mitigates manual handling risks.
SIASUN has built a highly robust and comprehensive operational and service network across North America leveraging its subsidiaries in the United States and Mexico.
From June 22nd to 25th (local time), SIASUN will make an appearance at Automate, the premier showcase for manufacturing technology upgrades in North America.
source_title: From the “First Export” to “Two Decades of Dedication”:SIASUN and North American Partners Chart a New Blueprint for Smart Manufacturing Cooperation-SIASUN

facts: Many development authorities and other economic or industrial development authorities in the State of Georgia offer “Economic Development & Incentive” programs for private companies which commit to make new capital investments that promote trade and commerce and provide new employment or retain existing employment.
Under such programs, companies receive a reduction in the portion of the ad valorem taxes assessed on their real and personal property in connection with an authorized project.
This economic development tool is generally used by Authorities when a community is attempting to attract a specific industry or jobs creation or prevent the loss of jobs to another location.

All real and personal property is taxable in the State of Georgia unless some specific exemption applies, with each county authorized to assess an annual tax based on the fair market value of all real and personal property located within its geographical boundaries.

Under the Constitution of the State of Georgia, a county or other local government may not voluntarily, or by agreement, reduce or eliminate property taxes on real or personal property except in certain very limited circumstances.

If a project is included in an incentive program, companies receive an effective property tax reduction when title to real and personal property is transferred to an Authority and then subject to a lease-back to the company from the Authority.
Under Georgia law, these Authorities are not subject to ad valorem property taxation on property which is titled to them.
The leasehold interest held by the company is subject to assessment based on its “leasehold value,” which is generally less than the ownership.
Recent Georgia Supreme Court decisions have required that the jurisdiction’s Board of Assessors have a reasonable basis upon which to value the leasehold interest, supported by independent valuation.
The Authority’s acquisition and installation of the economic development project is accomplished through the issuance by the Authority of its Taxable Lease Purchase Bonds.

Examples of projects that have benefitted from Economic Development & Incentive programs include manufacturing and/or production facilities, mixed-use residential and commercial properties, office buildings and related real and personal property, and any project that the Authority determines advances the public purpose of developing trade, commerce, industry and employment.
Typically, the Authority will issue the bonds which will be purchased by the company.
Following the bonds issuance, the company will transfer title to the project to the Authority.
The Authority will lease the completed project to the company in exchange for rental payments in amounts sufficient to pay debt service on the bonds.
The rental payments to the Authority will be made back to the company, as the bondholder.
At the end of the lease term, title to the project will revert to the company.

If the project qualifies, the Authority typically adopts an inducement resolution, preliminarily approving the issuance of the bonds, and a financing team is established, usually including Issuer’s Counsel, Lessee’s Counsel, a Trustee and Bond Counsel.
The Authority contacts the County Assessor’s office to seek agreement on the calculation of the “leasehold value.”
The company, Authority and County Tax Assessor’s Office execute a Memorandum of Agreement, detailing how the “leasehold value” will be calculated throughout the term of the Memorandum of Agreement.

After the Authority approves the transaction and the County’s Tax Assessor’s office agrees to a methodology for calculating the “leasehold value,” the parties seek judicial approval of the transaction by way of a “judicial validation.”
The entire transaction can generally be documented and closed within 8-12 weeks.

For a project with a fair market value of $20,000,000 in a jurisdiction with a 30 mill tax rate, the first year’s saving could equal $120,000 (assuming a 40% Assessed Value and the first year’s assessment at 50% of the fee simple tax rate).
Typically, the amount of annual savings will be reduced over the term of the lease.
Actual savings will vary depending on the agreement reached with the County’s Tax Assessor’s office and on the county’s millage rate.

The term of the Incentive varies by jurisdiction, potentially as few as five years for equipment or as long as 20 or more years, with each jurisdiction having their own program and conditions related to the length of the lease.

The incentives typically begin on the January 1 following the year in which the Project received its Certificate of Occupancy or, if complete, the January 1 following the year in which title is transferred to the Authority.
The company has the benefits and burdens of ownership and is treated as the owner for federal tax purposes, carries the project on its balance sheet, and takes full depreciation expense.

The Authority typically subordinates its fee interest to the lender’s lien and generally grants leasehold mortgages and/or assignment of its rights, while the company may be asked to pledge the bond to the lender as collateral.
source_title: The Georgia Developer’s Toolkit: Economic Development and Employment Incentives

facts: Georgia has been ranked the No. 1 state for business for a decade, with success driven by focused, flexible, and community-driven leadership.

GDEcD Deputy Commissioner of Global Commerce Misti Martin shared in an interview with Site Selection magazine how the state’s partnerships, infrastructure investments, and business-first mindset continue to deliver results.

Martin stated: “We’ll accomplish our goals by delivering on our consistent and proven methods. Georgia’s collaborative approach and strong, solutions-based partnerships with our industries allow us to support their growth trajectories.”

Before joining GDEcD, Martin led economic development in Cherokee County, one of metro Atlanta’s fastest-growing areas, bringing local experience to a statewide strategy focused on job creation, smart expansion, and transformative investments.
The state is leveraging transformative investments from global companies including Hyundai Motor Group, Kia, Q CELLS, and Rivian.
Martin identifies three pillars giving Georgia its competitive edge: a skilled workforce, well-planned infrastructure, and a consistent business-friendly climate.
Georgia Quick Start is ranked as the No. 1 workforce training program in the nation.
The Technical College System of Georgia (TCSG) experienced a 10.6% enrollment boost in 2024.
Programs like GEORGIA MATCH and Be Pro Be Proud Georgia are helping students connect with in-state higher education and training to strengthen the talent pipeline for Georgia employers.

Beyond major electric mobility and clean energy headlines, the state is now focused on building out entire supply chains that include logistics providers, advanced manufacturers, recyclers, and R&D firms.
Billions have been allocated for highway, airport, and freight corridor improvements to help Georgia stay globally competitive for the next decade of growth.
GDEcD collaborates with local economic developers, utility partners, technical colleges, and employers to ensure each project aligns with regional goals.
Supporting Georgia-grown businesses to expand and innovate remains a top priority after initial company arrival.
Georgia’s logistics infrastructure includes two deepwater ports, the world’s busiest airport, and an integrated highway and rail system, making it one of the most connected states in the country.
Martin emphasizes that despite strong infrastructure, the state’s greatest competitive strength remains its people.
source_title: Why Georgia Stays on Top: GDEcD's Misti Martin on Growth, Talent, and What’s Next - Metro Atlanta CEO

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Toyota Industries Electric Systems North America, Inc. (TIESNA) will create more than 250 new jobs.
TIESNA is expanding the Toyota Industries Corporation campus in Pendergrass with a new manufacturing facility valued at $69 million in investment.
TIESNA broke ground on the new site on November 14, 2023.
Japanese Consul General Mio Maeda and local representatives attended the groundbreaking ceremony.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Georgia is a leader in the automotive industry and the "best state for business."
Governor Brian P. Kemp pledged continued collaboration with state and local partners to create opportunities across Georgia.
TIESNA is a subsidiary of Toyota Industries Corporation, which is headquartered in Japan.
Toyota Industries develops and manufactures automobile-related products, including car air-conditioning compressors and car electronics components for worldwide carmakers.
The Toyota Industries group has operated in Pendergrass since 2004.
The group's initial Pendergrass operation was TD Automotive Compressor Georgia, LLC (TACG).
Toyota Industries Compressor Parts America, Co. (TICA) was established in Pendergrass in 2012.
TACG and TICA currently support more than 400 jobs in Jackson County.
TIESNA’s new facility will be located at 1700 Valentine Industrial Parkway in Pendergrass.
The new facility will produce DC-to-DC converters that convert high voltage to low voltage to supply power to auxiliary equipment.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in 2025.
Jackson County Commission Chair Tom Crow welcomed TIESNA as the third Toyota Industries-related company in Jackson County.
Tom Crow praised TACG and TICA as outstanding corporate citizens and pledged county support for TIESNA's success.
Griff Lynch, President and CEO of the Georgia Ports Authority, welcomed TIESNA's expansion as part of Georgia's growing automaking sector.

The project was supported in partnership with the Jackson County Area Chamber of Commerce, Georgia Power, the Georgia Ports Authority, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that Toyota Industries has been part of the Jackson County community since 2004.
Pat Wilson highlighted that building long-term relationships gives Georgia a competitive advantage for company expansions.

In fiscal year 2023, job creation in Georgia's automotive industry increased by 324 percent compared to fiscal year 2021.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s OEMs resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.

Alex Case praised the company's $12 million investment in a new facility and new jobs as demonstrating confidence in the local economy and workforce.
Jeff Mullis serves as president and CEO of the Northwest Georgia Joint Development Agency.
Jeff Mullis described the investment as proof that Northwest Georgia is a premier place to develop and grow industry, especially in the automotive supply sector.
State Representative Mike Cameron praised Georgia's business-friendly climate and investment in workforce training.
Mike Cameron noted that the Southeast United States has become a magnet for automotive brands and suppliers over the last two decades with Georgia leading by example.
source_title: Trenton Pressing Unveils $12 Million Expansion, Adding 100,000 Square Feet To Manufacturing Operations - Chattanoogan.com

facts: Trenton Pressing, a tier one manufacturing supplier located in Dade County, broke ground on a new $10 million facility.
The new facility will be 105,000 square feet.
The additional space will allow for expanded manufacturing and assembly services and significantly increase the organization’s manufacturing capacity.
Hans Pfunder, chief operating officer for Trenton Pressing, stated the company is extremely grateful for Dade County’s support throughout the expansion process.

Over just over two years, Trenton Pressing purchased a struggling manufacturing company and completely turned it around through a reorganization of management, technology and equipment upgrades, and personnel training.
The expanded facility will allow the company to better service its growth and meet the manufacturing needs of clients which include BMW, Club Car, ZF, and more.
Trenton Pressing is currently one of the top five employers in Dade County with 220 employees.
The company plans to add 20 more jobs with the facility expansion.
Construction on the new facility is expected to be completed by December of 2023.
Trenton Pressing began operations in Dade County in 2021.
The company provides metal stamping processes, welding and assembly processes, warehousing, and shipping.

Dade County Executive Ted Rumley noted that Trenton Pressing has been a great community partner since beginning operations in the county in 2021, providing jobs and supporting schools and the environment.

Trenton mayor Alex Case stated that Trenton Pressing has been a great asset to the city since arriving just over two years ago and praised its commitment to improving the quality of life for citizens.

Jeff Mullis, president and CEO of the Northwest Georgia Joint Development Agency and retired Georgia state senator, commended Trenton Pressing on its expansion efforts and for adding jobs in the Northwest Georgia area.
source_title: Trenton Pressing Breaks Ground On New $10 Million Manufacturing Facility - Chattanoogan.com

facts: Trenton Pressing is a metal forming company located in Trenton, Ga.
The company operates as a longtime Tier 1 and Tier 2 supplier of stamped parts and complex assemblies for automotive companies and golf cart manufacturers.
During the pandemic, new owners purchased the business with the goal of diversifying its customer base beyond two consolidated industries.
The new ownership added metal fabricating capabilities to the company's existing stamping, robotic welding, and assembly services.
The strategic goal is to build full turnkey fabrications for customers rather than just supplying individual parts.
Brad Coffman works for Trenton Pressing and brings 30 years of experience in the metal fabricating industry to the new direction.
The company constructed a 100,000-sq.-ft. building on its campus specifically for warehousing and assembly activities.
Constructing the new facility freed up space in Plant 1 to accommodate a laser cutting machine and a press brake.
Plant 2 houses 15 robotic welding cells along with manual welding and assembly areas.
Trenton Pressing purchased a BesCutter 20-kW fiber laser cutting machine with a 5- by 10-ft. bed and automatic loading/unloading capability from Automated Solutions in late 2023.
The 20-kW laser cutting machine became operational a few months later in early 2024.
The high-powered laser can cut up to 2-in. steel plate and uses compressed high-pressure air for economical cuts on 3/8- to ½-in. material.
An automated load and unload gantry system was installed alongside the laser, utilizing vacuum suction cups for raw sheet metal and fork-style lifters for finished parts.
The automated loading system is capable of running unattended for eight hours without requiring material reloading.
The company purchased two cutting heads to allow quick switching during maintenance and prevent machine downtime.
Trenton Pressing installed a Haco EuroMaster 275-ton CNC press brake with a 10-ft. bed in early 2025.
The press brake features 6-axis backgauging, CNC crowning, automatic angle measurement during bending, hydraulic upper tool clamping, and offline programming capability.
Brad Coffman plans to train one of the company's engineers to operate the press brake, creating two forming experts to help train other staff members.
A box truck moves work-in-process between buildings at least three times per day using a kanban logistics system.
Management refinished approximately 60% of the plant floors with polyurethane paint after moving assembly operations to the newly constructed building.
The company's owners maintain high standards for the plants, viewing the clean facilities as a selling tool for potential customers.
source_title: Metal forming company jumps into the thick of metal fabricating

facts: Trenton Pressing is located in Trenton, Ga.
The company is a longtime Tier 1 and Tier 2 supplier of stamped parts and complex assemblies for automotive companies and golf cart manufacturers.
During the pandemic, new owners purchased the business with the goal of diversifying its customer base.
The company expanded its services to include metal fabricating alongside existing stamping, robotic welding, and assembly operations.
The ultimate goal of the new ownership is to build full turnkey fabrications for customers rather than just supplying individual parts.
A new 100,000-sq.-ft. building was constructed on the company campus specifically for warehousing and assembly activities.
Constructing the new building freed up additional space in Plant 1 for manufacturing equipment.
Plant 2 houses 15 robotic welding cells along with manual welding and assembly areas.
In late 2023, Trenton Pressing purchased a BesCutter Mach 3015 20-kW fiber laser cutting machine from Automated Solutions.
The laser cutting machine features a 5- by 10-ft. bed and automatic loading/unloading capability.
The new laser cutting machine became operational in early 2024.
The laser is capable of processing up to 2-in. carbon steel plate.
The machine utilizes compressed high-pressure air instead of oxygen or nitrogen for cutting, which reduces operating costs for 3/8- to ½-in. material.
An automated load and unload gantry system was installed with the laser, using vacuum suction cups for loading raw sheet metal and fork-style lifters for unloading finished parts.
The automated gantry system can run unattended for eight hours without requiring material reloading.
The company purchased two cutting heads to allow quick swaps during maintenance and prevent machine downtime.
In early 2025, the company installed a Haco EuroMaster 275-ton CNC press brake with a 10-ft. bed and a 21.25-in. daylight opening.
The press brake includes advanced features such as 6-axis backgauging, CNC crowning, automatic angle measurement during bending, hydraulic upper tool clamping, and offline programming capability.
Randall Hamilton serves as Trenton Pressing’s plant manager and operates the new press brake.
Brad Coffman, an employee with 30 years of metal fabricating experience, led the equipment selection process.
The company plans to train its own engineers to operate the new laser cutting machine and press brake to reduce reliance on specialized operators.
Work-in-process materials are moved between buildings using kanban trucks that make at least three stops per day at each facility.
Management refinished approximately 60% of the factory floors by grinding concrete and applying polyurethane paint to meet high operational standards.
source_title: Metal forming company jumps into the thick of metal fabricating | Guangdong Steel Pipe Co.,Ltd

facts: Valeo is the world leader in automotive electrification.
Valeo has adapted its 48V motors to bikes, resulting in a high-performance electric assistance system for bikes.
Jacques Aschenbroich serves as Valeo’s Chairman and Chief Executive Officer.

One of Valeo’s key strategic focuses is accelerating expansion in emerging markets for new “zero-emissions” mobility, including electric small city vehicles, electric motorcycles and scooters, last-mile autonomous delivery droids, and electric bikes.
Valeo is leveraging and adapting technological platforms developed for the automotive industry, specifically ADAS (advanced driver assistance systems) and low-voltage electrification (48V).
The Valeo Smart e-Bike System is the first solution in the world to integrate both an electric motor and an adaptive automatic transmission in the pedal assembly.
With this new electric assistance system, gears change automatically and algorithms instantly adapt to the amount of electric assistance needed from the first pedal stroke.
Valeo's technology was developed in partnership with Effigear.
The technology comprises a 48V electric motor and a seven-speed automatic, adaptive gearbox in a single unit located in the pedal assembly.
The motor’s torque is 130 Newton meters, multiplying the cyclist’s effort by eight.
Other systems on the market offer up to a fivefold boost compared to Valeo's system.
Used on a cargo bike, Valeo’s electric assistance allows a cyclist carrying a 150kg load to climb a 14% gradient without breaking a sweat.
Valeo’s electric motor is more efficient than the 24V or 36V motors installed on the vast majority of e-bikes available today.
The adaptive automatic transmission uses an algorithm to understand and instantly adjust to each person’s needs, taking into account riding style and journey.
Gears shift smoothly with no buttons or levers needing to be pressed.
The Valeo Smart e-Bike System includes an anti-theft function integrated directly into the pedal assembly that blocks bike use when activated.
The system boasts a pedestrian push-assist function useful for carrying heavy loads.
It includes a boost function to make it easier to overtake other cyclists and climb hills.
Valeo developed the system from scratch, eliminating the need for derailleurs, sprockets, handlebar shifters, cables, or a traditional bike chain.
The traditional chain is replaced by a cleaner belt system.
Electric bikes using this technology no longer need around 50 parts that are often fragile and require constant maintenance.
On average, a traditional model needs to be serviced at least once a month.
Valeo aims solely to equip bikes with its electric assistance solution rather than build bikes itself.
Three prototypes were developed to show adaptation to all types of models: a city bike, a mountain bike, and a cargo bike for transporting loads.
The cargo bike prototype features unique reversing and braking energy recovery functions.
The high-end solution meets the best and most demanding automotive standards in terms of quality, robustness, durability, and safety.
Valeo's major assets include its 48V technological platform, which enables meeting a broad range of needs from a single technology base.
Valeo has very high volume production capacity delivering significant economies of scale, producing around 30 million electric machines each year.
The electric bike market is booming, with a fifteen-fold expansion expected over the next ten years.
Global sales in the electric bike market are expected to bring 270 million euros in 2030 (McKinsey/Center For Future Mobility -2020).
The market for electric cargo bikes is set to grow substantially as regulations prohibiting access to city centers for polluting vehicles gradually come into force.
In 2019, 57% of Valeo’s Group sales were generated from technologies aimed at reducing CO2 emissions.
source_title: Valeo is revolutionising e-bike drivetrains - Auto Components India

facts: BYD might be halting its planned $1 billion investment in its passenger car plant in Turkey.
The deal for the factory to be located in the Manisa Organized Industrial Zone was signed in July 2024 by Turkish President Tayyip Erdoğan and BYD Chief Executive Officer Wang Chuanfu.
The Turkish plant is intended to have an annual production capacity of 150,000 vehicles.
BYD has been granted incentives by the Turkish government, including tax exemptions.
18 months after the deal was signed, BYD has yet to begin construction of the Turkish facility.
No timeline has been shared with the public regarding the Turkish plant despite recent media reports.
CHP lawmaker Sevda Erdan Kilic stated last week that no timeline has been shared.
Reuters reported in July that BYD was postponing mass production at its first European passenger-car plant in Hungary until 2026.
The delay to the Hungarian plant was said to reflect a shift in focus toward BYD’s planned Turkish factory, where production was expected to start sooner due to lower labor costs.
Construction equipment was reportedly spotted at the planned Turkish site in mid-January, suggesting work could finally be starting.
Local media outlet PA Turkey reported on Thursday that BYD has allegedly suspended the investment, citing sources within the industry.
Ali Suat Ertosun, a former member of Turkey’s Supreme Court of Appeals and current president of the Manisa Association for the Protection of Cultural and Natural Assets, is among the cited sources.
A formal announcement on the Turkish project's status is expected as early as this month or March.
Turkish Minister of Industry and Technology Mehmet Fatih Kacır was questioned in the Grand National Assembly last week about the project's status.

The opposition Republican People’s Party (CHP) submitted a 10-question parliamentary inquiry addressing how much of the $1 billion investment has been carried out, land allocation status, tax exemption details, and potential sanctions for non-completion.
Kacır stated the project remains under active supervision within Turkey’s investment incentive legislation, with oversight and compliance checks continuing in full.
Kacır added that if the investment is not completed as planned, sanctions set out in the incentive regulations would apply.
Kacır said: “These measures are valid for the BYD project as well. In addition, safeguard mechanisms ensure that our national interests are fully protected.”
Besides domestic production in China, BYD currently operates factories in Thailand, Uzbekistan and Brazil.
The Chinese new energy vehicle maker is also building new plants in Indonesia, Malaysia and Cambodia.
BYD has recently begun trial production at its European plant in Hungary following delays in completing construction of the passenger vehicle factory.
Manufacturing fully electric vehicles in Europe will allow the automaker to avoid the European Commission tariffs imposed in October 2024 on imported EVs.
Full production at the Hungarian plant is expected to begin in the second quarter and will ramp up to its planned capacity of 200,000 vehicles over several years.
Employment at the Hungarian plant will expand in parallel with production ramp-up.
Last year, BYD established its European headquarters in Hungary, saying the office was expected to create around 2,000 jobs.
A third European factory is under consideration according to several reports, though no location has been revealed yet.
The company aims to achieve 1.3 million sales outside China this year, representing a 24.3% increase year over year.
Last month, BYD’s sales more than doubled in Germany and the UK.
BYD continues to struggle to gain market share in Scandinavia.
BYD’s vehicle registrations declined overall in January, leading its stock down in both the Shenzhen and Hong Kong markets earlier this week.
source_title: BYD Quietly Halts $1B Plant Progress in Turkey, Formal Announcement Expected: Report | EV

facts: Valeo is a leading French patent applicant.
Valeo is committed to technological advancement and achieving carbon neutrality by 2050.
Valeo's core values include Smart Technology, Sustainability, Innovation, and Premium Experience.
Smart Technology involves leveraging cutting-edge technology to create smarter mobility solutions.
Sustainability involves committing to environmental responsibility and sustainable development.
Innovation involves continually pushing the boundaries of automotive technology to meet the evolving needs of the market.
Premium Experience involves ensuring a premium brand experience for all stakeholders, from automakers to end consumers.
Valeo is dedicated to making mobility smarter, safer, and more sustainable for everyone.
Valeo builds partnerships based on trust, innovation, and mutual success.
source_title: About Us Aftermarket Auto Parts | Valeo Service

facts: Hyundai wants to showcase the car factory of the future in Georgia with its new Metaplant America, bringing together electric cars, hybrids, AI, robotics, and local battery manufacturing.
Hyundai's US Metaplant complex covers almost 1,200 hectares of land in Ellabell, Georgia.

While many manufacturers discuss EV/hybrid/ICE production mixes, Hyundai has already acted in Georgia with the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA), one of the group's most ambitious industrial projects.
The HMGMA facility costs $7.6 billion and is intended as a blueprint for the next stage of vehicle production.
Hyundai describes HMGMA as a software-defined factory that integrates production, logistics, data, AI, and robotics more closely than classic assembly lines.
The facility bundles the most advanced manufacturing competencies of Hyundai Motor Group to show how flexible modern vehicle production can be when processes are digitally connected and automated.
Currently, the Hyundai Ioniq 5, Hyundai Ioniq 9, and Kia Sportage Hybrid are rolling off the line in Ellabell.
At full capacity, the plant will produce up to 500,000 electric and hybrid vehicles per year for Hyundai, Genesis, and Kia.
The facility is designed for a model mix of ten different vehicles.

At the official opening in March of last year, Euisun Chung, Executive Chair of Hyundai Motor Group, called the plant a showcase for advanced manufacturing capacities and the company's innovation claims.
Robotics and image processing systems are used at HMGMA to enable high-quality, flexible production and support human workers rather than replace them.
By 2031, 8,500 people will work in Ellabell.
Hyundai relies on a strongly integrated local production network in Georgia for EV competitiveness.
A battery factory is located directly on the HMGMA premises, operated by HLBMA, a joint venture of Hyundai and LG Energy Solution (formerly HL-GA Company).
The HLBMA battery manufacturing represents an investment of $4.3 billion in Georgia.
The HLBMA facility on the HMGMA complex has an annual cell capacity of 40 gigawatt-hours (GWh).
Currently, more than 500 people work at the HLBMA facility, mostly from the region.
According to HLBMA, the focus remains on safe and high-quality processes, including comprehensive training and own emergency capacities directly on site.
Hyundai is investing a further $6 billion to localize parts more strongly, strengthen logistics, and enter US steel production.
This $6 billion investment involves digital networking, connecting processes from order entry, procurement, and logistics to production to ensure robust supply chains in the USA.
The market for battery electric vehicles (BEVs) in the US has been unreliable recently, particularly since the removal of subsidy incentives under the Trump administration in September last year.
Hyundai was able to integrate hybrid models into production with comparatively few interruptions due to the flexibility of the new manufacturing.

Jerald Roach (HMGMA) states that the integration of robotics and image processing is very well suited for applications where an assembly process requires image data to position a component precisely on the vehicle.

Hyundai emphasizes that its long-term focus on electrification has not changed despite the removal of US tax incentives, though short-term production mixes will adapt to demand and political conditions while long-term investment logic remains.

HMGMA is Hyundai's industrial innovation flagship, with many technologies previously developed and validated at the Hyundai Motor Group Innovation Center Singapore now scaled for mass production in Georgia.

The plant combines AI, robotics, and data technologies into an automated production system where all processes from order receipt to assembly interlock to react faster, reduce friction losses, and adapt easily to new models.

Employees at HMGMA are called "Meta Pros", and automation is primarily used for repetitive, physically demanding, or potentially dangerous tasks to increase productivity, reduce injury risks, and improve the work environment.

Jerald Roach (Head of Department General Assembly at HMGMA) states that robotics and image processing can take over repetitive tasks beyond human physical limits, and HMGMA has integrated a high number of successful technologies into series production by selecting the right applications.
Hyundai does not disclose details about assembly equipment suppliers, but humanoid Atlas robots from Boston Dynamics will be deployed in HMGMA starting January 2028.

The Atlas robots are built at Boston Dynamics' headquarters in Boston and will initially be used for external scheduling and planning, specifically unloading and sorting incoming parts and packing boxes with large or heavy components.

Hyundai tested Atlas already in October last year when sorting roof racks for the assembly line, using an engineer wearing a VR headset to guide the robot's hands and arms, with resulting data used to train the AI models for autonomous operation.

For automotive production use, Atlas requires standardized parts and robust design, including an externally mounted battery that the robot can change itself, supported by fast-charging technology for entire robot fleets.
Atlas's range of motion matches factory safety logic for working alongside humans, utilizing camera technology and head mobility to capture its environment, body parts, and spatial position.

Hyundai Transys has built a high-capacity seat production facility with an annual output of 420,000 units at the site, using advanced automation including an auto-docking system and robots for applying release agents to molds.
Hyundai WIA supports HMGMA with autonomous robots to improve logistics automation, while Hyundai Rotem has supplied equipment for the press shop.

Battery cells for EVs come from the on-site HLBMA facility and are delivered to Mobis Battery System Assembly, which packs them onto skateboard chassis transported via conveyor technology to HMGMA's assembly area.
A second on-site facility, Mobis Modules, produces the frontend module for the vehicles.

Jerald Roach states that battery subassemblies are fed and mated via a highly automated process ensuring repeatability and precision for critical components like front/rear wheel suspensions, drive systems, and batteries, with all fasteners checked for presence and correct torque.
HMGMA maintains common positioning schemes for main components so automation can be easily adapted to new models regardless of drive type (ICE, BEV, or hybrid).

The plant was designed from the start to optimize BEV assembly, recognizing that connecting the large battery to the underbody must be considered during plant planning and vehicle design rather than integrated later.
Roach notes that plants designed for ICE vehicles would require significant structural changes or compromises to adapt to basic BEV differences.
Hyundai's modular vehicle design enables building different drive variants in a single plant, with common positioning schemes easing automation adaptation.

Hyundai uses automated material delivery systems in Georgia, including driverless transport systems and automated trailer unloading systems, reducing safety risks from tractors/forklifts and creating a safer, more efficient assembly plant.

Roach explains that an aggressive kitting strategy is used to distribute sequenced parts, increasing flexibility for multiple models, reducing decisions for Meta Pros, and improving visual management of assembly processes.
Vehicles move through the plant on driverless transport systems following programmed routes controlled via QR codes on the hall floor, rather than classic rigid conveyors.
Sustainability at HMGMA includes sourcing renewable energy, own solar power generation, energy-efficient building design, and lower emissions from producing electrified vehicles.
The plant features an advanced wastewater treatment and water management system, using treated water for landscaping and systems for collecting and controlled discharge of rainwater to save water.

In 2024, Hyundai Motor Group signed a 15-year power purchase agreement with energy developer Matrix Renewables, acquiring 147 megawatts of generation capacity from the Stillhouse Solar Project in Texas.
Hyundai expects to secure around 378 gigawatt-hours of electricity annually from this agreement and reduce CO2 emissions at its US locations by around 140,000 tons per year.

Despite political uncertainties, Hyundai remains optimistic about BEV production in the USA, with Roach stating the company will continue investing in local capacities and that electrification remains a long-term growth driver driven by localization, economies of scale, and customer choice.
source_title: Hyundai Metaplant America gestaltet die EV- und Hybridproduktion mit Smart-Factory-Automatisierung neu

facts: As the OE leader in electrification, Valeo equips one in three hybrid and electric cars worldwide.
Offers an extensive portfolio of over 6,000 essential references to support the maintenance and repair of electric and hybrid vehicles in the independent aftermarket.
Product portfolio includes filtration systems, wipers, brake pads, discs, calipers, and thermal solutions such as e-compressors, radiators, and condensers.
The large range of condensers currently covers more than 8 million electric and hybrid vehicles across Europe, with plans to increase this coverage to 10 million by the end of 2024.
Announced that its cutting-edge chiller for battery thermal management in electric and hybrid vehicles is available for the VW ID.3.
Plans to launch four new chillers by the second quarter of 2025 designed for Hyundai models like the Ioniq, Kona, and Tucson, as well as the Kia Niro.
Expanding its offer with five new electric driven compressors designed specifically to fit the latest "eTech" platform from Renault, including the Kadjar, Scenic, Captur, and Arkana models.
New electric driven compressors also fit the Tesla Model 3 and some applications from Kia and Hyundai.
Launched electric oil pumps in July for popular models such as the BMW X3 and Audi Q5.
The 48V DC-DC converter references 597202 and 597203, suitable for the Fiat 500, Peugeot 208, Citroen C5, and many other applications, will be launched during the first quarter of 2025.
Offers a wide range of 48V iBSG systems, noting that the car park is primarily evolving through mild hybrid vehicles.
New brake pads range includes 222 references with 92% electric and hybrid vehicle coverage, accounting for over 15 million European vehicles.
The e-performance brake pads use soft friction materials composed of carbons and ceramics that absorb noise and vibrations made during braking.
Offers a range of cables and mobile chargers for electric and hybrid vehicles, featuring 12 charging cable references and 15 mobile charger references in various lengths and charging speeds.
All charging cables and mobile chargers are fully compatible with all rechargeable vehicles.
Provides technical training through the Valeo Tech Academy program, which offers a comprehensive curriculum on Electric Vehicle and High Voltage systems.
The Tech Academy curriculum includes in-depth courses on HV safety protocols, component diagnostics, and repair procedures to ensure technicians can handle EV maintenance demands.
source_title: Leading the Way in Electrification | Valeo Service

facts: Fuel Cell Electric Vehicles (FCEVs) are powered by electricity produced from hydrogen.
A fuel cell uses the chemical energy of hydrogen or other fuels to cleanly and efficiently produce electricity.
If hydrogen is the fuel, the only products emitted are electricity, water and heat.

facts: Road transportation accounts for 18% of CO2 emissions.
Valeo is committed to playing a major role in the decarbonization of the automotive industry for new vehicles and current rolling vehicles.
Valeo Service operates under an environmental initiative called “I Care For The Planet” to limit its activity's impact on the planet.
Valeo aims to involve its entire ecosystem – plants, employees, suppliers, and partners – in the process of decarbonizing its activities.
CanopyTM was launched in June 2023 in Europe as the first Valeo Aftermarket innovation designed for the environment.
Canopy wipers produce -61% CO2 emissions compared to a wiper blade produced in Asia and packaged in plastic, verified by Bureau Veritas.
Valeo remanufactures around 1 million parts each year, with more than 8,000 part numbers (P/N).
Valeo addresses the rapid rise of light electric mobility (e-bikes, e-kick scooters, and e-mopeds) through industrial repair and remanufacturing for fleet operators, repairers, and manufacturers.
Valeo is a leader in Electrification for Powertrain and Battery Thermal Management.
More than 13 million electric or hybrid vehicles are estimated to be equipped with Valeo parts, which reduce CO2 emissions.
Valeo provides workshop training to help distributors and repairers understand new EV technologies.
Ineez cables and EV charging stations contribute to extending the charging station network, making EV charging more convenient and flexible for EV drivers.
Electric retrofitting consists of replacing a vehicle's internal combustion engine and fuel tank with an electric motor and a battery, offering an alternative to purchasing new electric models.
Valeo is expanding its product and technology portfolio into the EV retrofit market, adapting its solutions to the specific needs of industry professionals.
Since 2022, plastic packagings in Europe are replaced by eco-conceived cardboard packagings adapted to each sales channel and customer that are 100% recyclable.
More than 400 tons of plastic is saved each year due to the packaging change, which corresponds to the weight of more than 11 million plastic bottles based on 2020 sales volumes in Europe.
Valeo Service promotes direct delivery from factory to customers to save delivery time and reduce costs.
Since 2021, around 55,000 trees have been planted all over the world through a tree plantation program connected to direct delivery.

source_title: I Care for the Planet: what it is all about | Valeo Service

The newsletter reported on the volume of private activity bond issuances in the United States and specifically in the State of Georgia.
It highlighted efforts to spur manufacturing activities, including proposed federal legislation to relax rules regarding IDB financing and the National Network for Manufacturing.
It included innovation commentary on the financial status of the Highway Trust Fund, which supports national infrastructure.

facts: Governor Brian Kemp is calling for sweeping changes in education, workforce development, and infrastructure to ensure Georgia remains a national leader in business and opportunity.
Kemp laid out his agenda for the 2025 legislative session at the Georgia Chamber’s Eggs and Issues breakfast.
Kemp’s proposed “Top State for Talent Act” aims to address workforce challenges by linking education and job training to industry demands.

The initiative expands the Georgia MATCH program, which guarantees college admission for eligible high school students, by integrating it into students’ individual graduation plans starting in 9th grade.
The expanded program could provide families and students with greater access to high-paying jobs in growing industries.

Businesses could benefit from a pipeline of skilled workers tailored to their needs, helping Georgia maintain its competitive edge as workforce challenges like declining high school graduate rates loom nationwide.
Kemp’s Rural Workforce Housing Initiative is already providing grants to build housing for workers near new job hubs, with $42 million allocated to 21 communities.
The housing program ensures that as industries expand in rural Georgia, workers can live where they work.
Tort reform is Kemp’s top legislative priority for 2025, designed to address business leaders' concerns about Georgia’s legal climate being a barrier to economic growth and affordable healthcare.
Details of the tort reform package will be announced in the coming weeks.
Kemp called on local chambers of commerce to rally their members in support of the tort reform.
The proposed tort reform is expected to face pushback from trial lawyers and advocacy groups.
For small businesses and healthcare providers, the proposed changes could lower costs and reduce the risks of frivolous lawsuits.
Kemp announced a major push for infrastructure improvements, with $530 million earmarked for freight and logistics.
Kemp also allocated $250 million for water and wastewater projects.
These investments build on last year’s $1.5 billion infrastructure funding.
The infrastructure upgrades will likely mean smoother commutes, more efficient shipping for businesses, and better access to clean water and waste management for rural and urban communities alike.
By 2035, Kemp projects the state will save over $1 billion in reduced delays and crashes thanks to these investments.
source_title: Here Are Brian Kemp's Top Priorities for Georgia in 2025

facts: Mobileye and Valeo announced a new partnership to deliver software-defined, best-in-class imaging radars for next-generation driver assist and automated driving features.
The partnership was announced at IAA Mobility 2023 in Munich on September 4, 2023.
Imaging radar will serve as an enabling element for more advanced hands-off ADAS solutions and eyes-off automated driving features on highways and urban streets.
Nimrod Nehushtan, Mobileye’s Executive Vice President of Business Development and Strategy, stated the collaboration significantly advances a new phase in automotive safety and performance.

Marc Vrecko, President of Valeo’s Comfort and Driving Assistance Systems Business Group, said the partnership reinforces the strong relationship between Valeo and Mobileye and illustrates Valeo’s technological leadership in ADAS.
Mobileye’s imaging radars utilize advanced architecture including Massive MIMO antenna design, high-end radio frequency design developed in-house, and high-fidelity sampling.
The imaging radar delivers a detailed, four-dimensional image of surroundings up to 300 meters away and beyond.
The radar features a 140-degree field-of-view at medium range and a 170-degree field-of-view in close range.
Automakers have shown high market interest for Mobileye’s Imaging radar as they look to expand the operational design domains of their automated driving features.
Valeo, a world leader in Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS), has been developing and mass-producing radar technologies since 2006.

Valeo will lead the system design of the new imaging radar product by integrating Mobileye’s groundbreaking imaging radar technology and corresponding software and algorithms embedded in the Mobileye Radar chipset into Valeo’s automotive software and hardware radar solutions.
The complete Imaging radar solution will be produced by Valeo leveraging its expertise in producing automotive technologies at scale and its global industrial footprint.
Since 2015, Mobileye and Valeo have delivered more than 15 million Smart Front Cameras worldwide as part of their successful collaboration on front-facing cameras and other driver assist solutions.
source_title: Mobileye and Valeo Launch Partnership for World-Class Imaging Radars | Mobileye News

facts: French car parts maker Valeo expects to report lower core profit margin in 2022 amid geopolitical tensions and inflationary pressures, following a 2021 margin that missed the group's target.

RBC analyst Tom Narayan noted that Valeo's 2022 guidance was well below consensus expectations, consistent with cautious outlooks from Renault, Michelin, Faurecia, and Stellantis due to the global chip shortage.
Valeo shares fell 10% in midday trade, heading for its worst day since March 2020.
The company forecasts a 2022 annual core profit (EBITDA) margin between 11.8% and 12.3%, compared to 13.4% in 2021.
Valeo projects 2022 sales between 19.2 billion and 20.0 billion euros, representing 11-16% growth year-over-year.
The financial outlook excludes potential effects from the Ukraine-Russia crisis but includes a negative impact from the integration of Valeo Siemens eAutomotive.
CEO Christophe Perillat stated the direct impact of the Ukraine-Russia crisis should be minimal, as the company has no sales in Ukraine and less than 1% of total sales in Russia.
Valeo plans to increase original equipment sales by approximately 13% in the medium term, targeting 27.5 billion euros ($30.73 billion) in revenue by 2025 and 40 billion euros by 2030.
The company specializes in the design, production, and sale of components and services for the automotive sector.
source_title: Valeo gives cautious 2022 outlook amid global uncertainties By Reuters
