facts: Valeo is a leading French automotive technology provider.
Valeo expects ADAS adoption to accelerate in India’s passenger vehicle sector.
Valeo anticipates that by 2030, up to 50% of global vehicles will feature Level-2 ADAS.
Government regulations are expected to support widespread ADAS adoption in India.
Jayakumar G is Group President and Managing Director of Valeo India.
Jayakumar G noted that ADAS is likely to grow faster than expected in India, with every car requiring some form of ADAS functionality in the near future.
Jayakumar G highlighted the government’s role in promoting ADAS technology.
The front camera, a key safety component, is expected to become a regulatory requirement.
Valeo has developed an innovative approach to leverage front camera computing power for additional ADAS features.
This approach could pave the way for more affordable, software-defined vehicles.
Valeo plans to localize ADAS technology in India.
Jayakumar G emphasized that the company will ramp up its business plan once it sees higher volumes and customer orders.
Valeo successfully localized EV components such as the three-in-one unit and ultrasonic parking sensors.
Volume growth will lead to greater localization and lower costs, which is how Valeo will achieve cost parity for ADAS technology in India.
Valeo is a major supplier of ADAS components globally, including sensors, high-performance computing units, and software.
Valeo has worked with several car manufacturers, including Honda, to develop advanced ADAS functionalities.
The Honda Legend uses Valeo’s Scala 3D LiDARs and front cameras to achieve Level-3 ADAS functionality.
In India, Valeo supplies front cameras for the ‘Honda Sensing’ Level-2 ADAS suite available in models like the Honda Elevate, City, and Amaze.
Valeo is aware that India’s road infrastructure and driving behaviors present unique challenges for ADAS deployment.
Companies like Tata Motors and Mahindra & Mahindra have successfully implemented ADAS in models like the Curvv SUV-coupe and XUV700.
Valeo is currently fine-tuning its ADAS technology for the Indian market by collecting data through a demo vehicle tested across various cities.
This data collection exercise aims to tailor ADAS to local conditions and is expected to be completed in the coming months.
Valeo’s Indian technology centers are located in Chennai, Bengaluru, and Hyderabad.
These centers play a key role in global R&D activities, with over 50% of their work focused on ADAS development.
India contributes significantly to Valeo’s global software development efforts, with over 1,700 engineers dedicated to software, electronics, and cybersecurity.
This expertise is crucial as Valeo anticipates a shift toward Software-Defined Vehicles (SDVs) in India.
Valeo believes that as vehicle architectures evolve globally, the adoption of centralized computing platforms will reduce costs, making advanced features like ADAS more accessible in India.
Jayakumar G concluded that as India moves toward SDVs, the same advancements will be applied there, bringing benefits to both automakers and consumers.
Valeo is a global leader in the automotive industry, specializing in designing, manufacturing, and supplying cutting-edge technology solutions to enhance vehicle performance, comfort, and safety.
Valeo is headquartered in France.
Valeo operates across four key business areas: powertrain systems, thermal systems, visibility systems, and comfort and driving assistance systems.
The company is a pioneer in developing innovative solutions for electric vehicles, autonomous driving, and energy efficiency.
Valeo has a robust presence in over 30 countries.
Valeo collaborates with leading automotive manufacturers worldwide.
source_title: Valeo Prepares to Meet Growing ADAS Demand from Indian Automakers | Machine Maker - Latest Manufacturing News | Indian Manufacturing News - Latest Manufacturing News | Indian Manufacturing News - Machine Maker

facts: Vanguard National Trailer Corporation started production today at its new 356,000-square-foot dry van trailer manufacturing plant in Trenton, Georgia.
The facility is located 30 miles southwest of Chattanooga, Tennessee.
This location serves as Vanguard’s second dry van manufacturing plant, joining the primary facility in Monon, Indiana.
Planning for the facility began three years ago, with Trenton selected after considering locations in Tennessee, Alabama and other locations in Georgia.
The company broke ground on the 100-acre site in the Dade County Industrial Park on Sept. 3, 2015.
President Charlie Mudd stated the project came in on time and under the $36 million budget.
Vanguard noted there is not currently a dry van manufacturing plant in the Southeast United States, prompting the decision to locate one there.
The company designed the facility as a relatively small 10,000-trailer capacity plant to better service customers in the Southeast.
The state of Georgia, Dade County and the city of Trenton provided very strong support for the project.
At full capacity, the plant is anticipated to add 400 jobs to the area.
So far, 70 positions have been filled at the new facility.
The plant’s estimated 10,000-trailer capacity is expected to provide substantial relief from backlogs that are stretching out almost a year in some cases.
Vanguard’s written plan targets full capacity within three years, with an acknowledgment that start-up will be very slow.
The entire project was designed and planned by the Vanguard team.
The plant is equipped to operate at high efficiency while maintaining the capability to provide wide customization for customers.
Vanguard National & CIMC Intermodal ranked fifth on Trailer/Body Builders’ list of the top 25 manufacturers for 2015.
Vanguard National & CIMC Intermodal produced a total of 42,594 units in 2015, up from 28,374 in 2014.
The 2015 production volume included 10,835 dry freight vans, 2700 refrigerated units, 15,116 ISO container chassis, and 13,943 domestic chassis.
source_title: Vanguard Opens Plant in Trenton, Georgia | Trailer Body Builders

facts: Velocity Trailer Centers has expanded its Vanguard trailer distribution network across Tennessee, Alabama and Georgia.
The expansion took place in February and is aimed at improving equipment availability and support for fleets and owner-operators operating across the Southeast.
Customers will have access to a range of Vanguard dry van and refrigerated trailer configurations, including the latest 2026 and 2027 VXP dry van trailers and R8000B refrigerated trailers.
These units can be customized with options including Thermo King S-600 refrigeration units, stainless steel swing doors, Hendrickson suspension systems and various logistics configurations.

Robert Ramey, general manager of Velocity Trailer Centers, stated that combining Vanguard's proven trailer engineering with Velocity's growing service network helps customers access reliable equipment faster while supporting the continued growth of transportation and logistics across the Southeast.
The Southeast expansion will be supported by dedicated regional representatives who will assist customers with trailer specifications, inventory availability, and factory production orders.
source_title: Velocity expands Vanguard trailer availability in Southeastern US - Truck News

facts: Great Dane is opening a new factory in Statesboro, Ga., in the first quarter of 2012 to build refrigerated trailers.
Production at the new plant will start modestly and carefully.
The plant will employ 400 people when it becomes fully staffed.
Chris Hammond, vice president of dealer sales at Great Dane Trailers in Savannah, Ga., reported that quoting and ordering activity had picked up over a slow June, July, and August period.
Great Dane’s backlog for dry vans extends well into the middle of the first quarter of next year on some lines and into the second quarter on others.
source_title: Trailer Orders Jump by 31%; Backlog Stretches Into 2012 - TT

facts: The U.S. International Trade Commission is investigating truck trailer imports from Canada, China, and Mexico for unfair trade practices.
Foreign manufacturers are accused of selling trailers in the United States at less than fair value, known as dumping.
Those foreign trailers are allegedly receiving government subsidies from Canada, China, and Mexico.
The investigation stems from an antidumping/countervailing petition submitted by the American Trailer Manufacturers Coalition.
The coalition is an ad hoc group that includes Great Dane, Stoughton, and Wabash.
U.S. truck trailer manufacturers claim unfair practices have injured the domestic trailer industry and its workers.
The coalition states foreign manufacturers have gained a significant and increasing share of the U.S. market at the expense of the U.S. industry.
U.S. manufacturers have experienced significant drops in production, shipments, performance, and employment due to these imports.
Nearly 10,000 jobs are directly supported by the trailer industry.

Robert E. DeFrancesco III, counsel to the American Trailer Manufacturers Coalition, stated that trade relief is necessary to remedy injurious effects of unfairly traded van trailers from Mexico, China, and Canada.
He added that applying antidumping and countervailing duties will level the playing field and save America’s trailer manufacturing industry.
Hyundai Translead and Vanguard National Trailer Corporation are the primary focus of the probe according to the petition.
In 2016, Hyundai Translead added a fourth Mexican manufacturing facility.
From 2017 through 2024, truck trailer imports from Mexico averaged nearly 57,000 units per year, which was twice the rate from 2010 to 2015.
Vanguard’s parent company, China International Marine Containers, ramped up subassembly imports in China.
Following Section 301 tariffs on China in 2018, China International Marine Containers announced a Canadian facility in 2020.
Assembly operations at the Canadian facility began in 2021.
Canadian truck trailer imports rose 147% from 2021 to 2022 following the new facility's operations.
The coalition claims truck trailer purchases surpassed freight demand needs, with purchasers overstocking on low-priced imports in 2022 and 2023.
New trailer orders plummeted in 2024 due to this supply overhang.
Demand is expected to remain suppressed until the supply overhang is fully absorbed.
Subject imports continued using unfair pricing to win sales and capture remaining demand in 2024 and Hl 2025.
Dumping margins have been as high as nearly 300% from Canada, 1,400% from China, and more than 430% from Mexico.
Any eventual duties would apply to all van trailers imported from Canada, China, and Mexico, not just the focused companies.
A final decision on the investigation is not expected until next year, but relief can arrive sooner.
If dumping is found, the Department of Commerce can impose antidumping duties to offset the difference between fair value and sale price.
Countervailing duties will similarly offset foreign government subsidies.
The International Trade Commission's preliminary determination is due Jan. 5.
The Department of Commerce's preliminary determination is due in mid-2026.
A final determination is scheduled for the end of next year, though duties can be imposed at any time during the investigation.
The American Trailer Manufacturers Coalition was formed to protect the U.S. van trailer industry from unfair foreign trade practices.
The group's first public activity occurred earlier this year.
In May, the group asked the Department of Commerce to include truck trailers as steel and aluminum derivative products under Section 232 tariffs.
That request was granted in August.
A 25% tariff applies to the value of the steel and aluminum within the trailer, not the entire trailer.
Neither Hyundai Translead nor Vanguard could be reached for comment.
source_title: Feds investigate unfair foreign truck trailer pricing » Land Line Media

facts: U.S. trailer makers took in more orders for new equipment in 2012 than in any other year since 2006.
Trailer makers have a strong enough backlog to keep busy through the middle of 2013, even as the economy has cooled.
A Jan. 28 report from ACT Research Co. said net December orders came in at 30,594, or 21.7% above the final month of 2011.
The annual total rose to 239,219, up 1.1% from 236,589 in 2011.
The record for trailer orders was 286,500 units in 2006, the same year that truck sales peaked.
When the recession hit, trailer orders plunged to just 85,100 units in 2009.
Since then, annual orders have nearly tripled, according to ACT figures.
Shipments of finished trailers from factories rose by about 13% to more than 240,000 units last year from 213,000 in 2011.
Frank Maly, ACT’s director of analysis and research, stated that all 10 tracked types of trailers grew in shipments on a year-over-year basis.
December orders lay a strong foundation for 2013, with deals still rolling out in the pipeline.
Chris Hammond, vice president at Great Dane Trailers, noted the Savannah, Ga., company’s order backlog is pushing well into the second quarter of 2013.
Hammond cited a hangover effect from 2009 to 2010 as customers realign their trade-in cycles.
Glenn Harney, senior vice president at Hyundai Translead, said quote and order volumes have remained steady.
Production for vans at Hyundai Translead is fully booked through mid-May, and through mid-June for refrigerated trailers.
Utility Trailer Manufacturing Co. made more trailers last year than ever before, with 2012 sales volume exceeding 2011 by 13%.
Utility sold more than 18,000 refrigerated trailers in 2012, the second-highest amount in its history behind 2006.
Stoughton Trailers customers for dry vans are bumping up against an informal “10-year rule” where shippers won’t load/unload trailers older than 10 years due to safety concerns.
Sales observed by Stoughton Trailers are replacement purchases rather than growth in the nation’s total trailer fleet.
With another year of modest growth, orders will be comparable to the 2004-2006 high-volume days, when orders rose from 245,000 in 2004 up to the 2006 peak.
Fontaine Trailers management expects a solid year slightly off from 2012 levels.
Alan Briley, vice president of sales and marketing for Fontaine’s commercial platform division, noted flatbed carriers were replacing equipment for the first time in several years.
Fontaine’s backlog of unfilled orders dropped from nine to 12 months a year ago to three or four months currently.
MAC Trailer Manufacturing entered the pneumatic bulk tank business in 2009 and benefited from sales to companies hauling sand for hydraulic fracturing in oil and gas fields.
MAC Trailer Manufacturing had a record year for sales in 2012 after a record in 2011, with flatbed demand growing to compensate for dropped pneumatic bulk demand.
Jeffrey Kauffman, stock analyst for Sterne, Agee & Leach, believes fleet managers are shifting investment incrementally from trucks to trailers.
Orders for new trucks contracted year-over-year for most of 2012, and U.S. retail truck sales have shrunk since September, even though 2012 was better than 2011.

Rence Oliphant, vice president of sales and marketing for suspension maker Hendrickson International, noted a separation between trucks and trailers, with trailer purchases held on longer than tractor buys.
Fleet managers postponed trailer purchases longer than tractor buys, making the need to buy trailers more acute.
A new heavy-duty tractor can cost about $125,000, leading fleet managers to postpone such major purchases in favor of trailers as a better bargain for capital spending.
The dry van market is currently the hottest among trailer types.
Wabash National Corp. declined comment but announced it has purchased assets from Beall Corp.
source_title: 2012 Is U.S. Trailer Makers’ Best Year Since ’06 - TT

The company will participate in MD&M West 2025, taking place February 4-6, 2025, at the Anaheim Convention Center in Anaheim, California.

facts: Announced the initiation of a metallurgical program on material from the Upper Fir Tantalum & Niobium Deposit in British Columbia at SGS Burnaby under the direction of Michel Robert (M.Sc.).
The goal of the program is to process raw material from the Upper Fir Deposit through to the production of a tantalum-niobium (Ta-Nb) flotation concentrate.
Historic recovery rates for the flotation concentrate are +75%.
It is intended that this concentrate will then be used to test the extraction and production of separated final tantalum and niobium oxides for delivery to industry.
The Company intends to transport 2 tonnes of crushed rock from the 2,000-tonne Upper Fir Deposit bulk sample to SGS Canada Burnaby, from the ~120 tonnes in storage in Richmond.
It is expected that the pilot plant program will run until fall 2026.

The historic Preliminary Economic Assessment (PEA) completed by AMEC in 2011 for the Upper Fir Tantalum & Niobium Deposit was based upon the process design flow sheet producing a commercially marketable tantalum/ niobium concentrate.
In subsequent work, estimated overall recovery rates increased to 77.2% for tantalum and 75.1% for niobium, over the base case of 65-70% in the PEA.
Within the rougher and cleaner flotation circuit the recoveries achieved in open circuit exceeded 96% for tantalum and 92% for niobium.

The Upper Fir Tantalum & Niobium Deposit is one of the world’s largest defined resources of tantalum, with a current indicated resource of 48.4 M tonnes at 197 ppm tantalum and 1,610 ppm niobium, and an inferred resource of 5.4 M tonnes at 191 ppm tantalum and 1,760 niobium.
The indicated resource amounts to 21M pounds of tantalum and 171M pounds of niobium.
The inferred resource amounts to 2.2M pounds of tantalum and 21M pounds of niobium.
Capacitor Metals Corp. is a British Columbia Incorporated Company focused on the development of the Blue River Tantalum-Niobium Property located in British Columbia, Canada.
The Company is positioned to be one of the lowest cost tantalum-niobium producers globally, with a specific focus on being a long-term supplier of tantalum and niobium to the global market.

The Blue River Property is a fully serviced site in mining-friendly British Columbia, Canada with excellent infrastructure close at hand, including rail, paved road, water, and power adjacent to project site.
The Property contains three defined deposits of tantalum and niobium, the Verity, the Fir and the Upper Fir, and an additional 20+ mineralized known exploration targets.
The Upper Fir Tantalum & Niobium Deposit is advanced with significant work already completed, including CAD$34M+ spent to date.

Work completed to date includes +59,000 meters drilled in 271 drill holes, an historical Preliminary Economic Assessment (“PEA”), community consultations, and preliminary metallurgical and environmental work.

Within the Blue River Property, the Upper Fir deposit has a significant historical indicated resource of 9.6M kg contained tantalum and 77.8M kg contained niobium plus an historical inferred resource of 1.0M kg contained tantalum and 9.6M kg contained niobium.

facts: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) announced its second production vehicle: the 2026 IONIQ 9, an all-electric, three-row SUV.
The 2026 IONIQ 9 will be the first all-new Hyundai vehicle assembled at the state-of-the-art Metaplant.
Production of the IONIQ 9 is slated to begin in Q1 2025, with the SUV hitting the market in Q2.
The announcement follows news from June about the 2025 IONIQ 5, an updated version of Hyundai’s award-winning electric vehicle, which served as the first production vehicle at the Metaplant.
With the introduction of the IONIQ 9, HMGMA is doubling down on its electric lineup.
Oscar Kwon, CEO of HMGMA, stated that starting to build the all-new IONIQ 9 at the Metaplant brings an enhanced level of excitement for their 'Meta Pros.'
Kwon noted that seeing the expansion of the acclaimed IONIQ lineup being manufactured locally is particularly special.
HMGMA is the only facility to build IONIQ 9s in North America.
The IONIQ 9 was unveiled at AutoMobility LA 2024 in Los Angeles, alongside a simultaneous reveal by HMGMA at AutoNation Hyundai Savannah.
Meta Pros who contributed to the assembly of the preproduction vehicle were on-site to celebrate the launch.
Thanks to its U.S.-based assembly and battery sourcing, the 2026 IONIQ 9 is expected to qualify for the full $7,500 federal EV tax credit.
source_title: Hyundai's Metaplant Reveals 2026 IONIQ 9 as Newest Production Vehicle

facts: Plans to release its first electric vehicle with a solid-state battery in 2027-2028.
Promises increased range and faster charging for the new EV technology.
Collaborating with Sumitomo Metal Mining Co. for the mass production of cathode materials.
Aims to achieve the world's first practical application of all-solid-state batteries in electric vehicles.
New batteries use a cathode, anode, and solid electrolyte instead of existing liquid electrolyte solutions.
Claims the next-generation battery technology unlocks potential for reduced size, increased power output, and extended lifespan.
First electric car with a semi-solid-state battery went on sale on August 14, 2025.
Has been developing cathode materials for solid-state EV batteries with Sumitomo since 2021.
Using Sumitomo Metal Mining's proprietary powder synthesis technology to develop a high-strength cathode material.
New agreement was concluded after Toyota received METI certification for the production of new batteries in Japan last September.
Collaborating with several partners in Japan, including oil giant Idemitsu Kosan, to bring new EV batteries to market.
Idemitsu announced plans to build a large plant for the production of lithium sulfide, a raw material used in EV batteries.
Once operational, Idemitsu's plant will be able to produce 1,000 tons of lithium sulfide per year.
Idemitsu also plans to begin mass production of all-solid-state batteries in 2027.
New batteries are part of Japan's plans to establish a domestic supply chain and reduce dependence on China and South Korea.
Toyota is among several Japanese companies collectively investing $7 billion (1 trillion yen) in battery production domestically.
Other companies betting on the new technology include Mercedes-Benz, BMW, Volkswagen, and Honda.
Mercedes put the first car with a lithium-metal battery on the road in February.
Last month, Mercedes drove an EQS equipped with solid-state batteries almost 1205 km.
Mercedes technical director Markus Schäfer calls the new battery technology a turning point in the history of electric vehicles.
Mercedes plans to launch mass production of solid-state batteries by the end of the decade.
CATL and BYD, which already dominate the global battery market, plan to introduce new technology around 2027.
SAIC MG unveiled the new MG4 in August, calling it the world's first mass-produced electric vehicle with semi-solid-state batteries.
source_title: Toyota is set to release the "world's first" all-solid-state batteries for electric vehicles | УНН

facts: Gregory Beischer serves as CEO of Alaska Energy Metals.
Gregory Beischer contributed an article to EV World Magazine addressing nickel supply constraints for the electric vehicle market.
The rapid decarbonization of transportation is straining the global EV supply chain.
Nickel is an essential component in EV batteries and faces a risk of running short.
A potential nickel shortage could cause major disruption to the EV industry.
Batteries are central to electric vehicles and consist of lithium, cobalt, and 64 pounds of nickel.
Mining remains the only method to extract these essential battery materials.
The U.S. Department of Energy projects that nickel supply will be critically low by 2025.
Domestic mining operations must rapidly scale up extraction to meet essential metal demands.
Nickel miners face ongoing challenges including slow permit approvals and community pushback.
Limited resource supply combined with heightened demand threatens EV affordability and availability.
EVs are expected to maintain high price points due to current market conditions.
The current wait time for purchasing an electric vehicle ranges from two months to three years.
EV purchase wait times will likely increase further if existing supply chain issues remain unresolved.
Strained domestic nickel supply has forced a heavy reliance on Asian markets, particularly Indonesia.
Indonesia currently holds the position of the world's largest supplier of nickel.
Dependence on Indonesia for nickel creates future supply risks due to the country's political leverage over the resource.
Indonesian nickel production relies on coal-powered execution, which conflicts with global energy transition objectives.
Consumer demand for cleaner transportation is driven by concerns over climate change, air quality, high operating costs, and greenhouse gas emissions.
Securing the future energy supply requires refocusing and reprioritizing domestic nickel mining efforts.
source_title: EV World Magazine: Electric Vehicle Boom Portends Industry Breakdown | Alaska Energy Metals

Integrates customized logistics concepts seamlessly into customer manufacturing processes.

Delivers high-strength parts with optimum crash performance, body-in-white assemblies, and outer skin panels with excellent surface quality.
Operates as a business unit of the Metal Forming Division of voestalpine.

facts: Georgia Governor Nathan Deal announced that voestalpine Automotive Body Parts Inc. will expand its existing operation in Bartow County, Georgia.
The expansion involves a $50 million investment in the local community.
The investment will result in the creation of 150 new jobs.
The company currently employs approximately 100 Georgians at the Cartersville facility.
voestalpine Automotive Body Parts Inc. serves as the supply base for the German-based voestalpine Group.
The company produces ultra-high strength body-in-white components for premium car manufacturers.
The newly created jobs will range from advanced manufacturing to hot-forming and assembly positions.
The facility offers extensive training opportunities, including an apprenticeship program with students of the Bartow County College & Career Academy in Cartersville.

Gov. Nathan Deal stated: “voestalpine’s decision to expand operations in Bartow County strengthens Georgia’s growing automotive industry and highlights the state’s extensive manufacturing capabilities.”

Gov. Nathan Deal added: “When we met with voestalpine executives in Germany, we discussed the benefits that Georgia can continue to offer the growing company. The business environment in Bartow County and throughout the state will help voestalpine maintain its position as a leader in automotive supply, while bolstering Georgia’s reputation as a top state for business.”
Philipp Schulz is the managing director of voestalpine Automotive Body Parts Inc.
Philipp Schulz stated: “This investment is an important step in the growth strategy of our company in the U.S. and positions us well in the center of the southern automotive hub.”

Philipp Schulz added: “We would especially like to thank state and local officials as well as the Georgia Department of Economic Development for their assistance in this project. We are very excited about expanding operations in Cartersville, where our company has found great partners for our operations.”
Bartow County Commissioner Steve Taylor stated: “We are so proud and thankful for voestalpine’s investment in Bartow County.”

Steve Taylor added: “They were the first company to build in Highland 75 and their multiple additional phases, continued investment and creation of jobs shows that Bartow is a great location to do business.”
Steve Taylor noted the success of the Highland 75 location is a testament to collaboration between the county, the city of Cartersville, state officials, and corporate partners.
voestalpine Automotive Body Parts Inc. is a subsidiary company of the Metal Forming Division of the voestalpine Group.
The Metal Forming Division produces highly refined sections, tubes, precision strip steel products, and ready-to-install components made of pressed, stamped, and roll formed parts.

facts: As of 2022, Volvo Cars has production plants in Torslanda in Sweden, Ridgeville, South Carolina, in the United States, Ghent in Belgium, and Daqing in China.
Volvo Cars owns 50% of NOVO Energy (electric vehicle batteries).
Volvo Cars owns 18% of Polestar and 100% of Zenseact (AD and ADAS software) and HaleyTek (Android-based infotainment systems).
In June 2021, Volvo Cars and Swedish battery developer and manufacturer Northvolt announced its intention to establish a 50/50 joint venture consisting of a battery gigafactory and R&D center.
In December 2021, it was revealed the battery R&D center would be located in Gothenburg.
In February 2022, Gothenburg was also chosen as the location for the battery gigafactory.
In July 2017, the automaker announced that beginning in 2019, all of its new models will include an electric motor in their powertrain.
In early 2021 the company announced plans to completely stop selling fossil fuel-based cars by 2030, switching to electric-powered cars.
The plan includes phasing out fossil-fuel-only cars by 2025, manufacturing only electric cars or hybrids.
In 2024, Volvo Cars scrapped its goal of becoming fully electric by 2030, adjusting its strategy due to industry challenges.
In 2021, Volvo Cars set up a joint venture with Geely for its hybrid engine operations.

The new business, Aurobay will develop and market powertrain solutions, and as of 2022, includes Volvo Cars' engine plants in Skövde and Zhangjiakou, together with its powertrain research and development operations in Gothenburg.
Volvo's production output was 709,000 vehicles in 2023.
Geely Holding retains a 78.7% majority stake in Volvo Cars.
source_title: Volvo Cars - Wikipedia

facts: Volvo Cars has made it its mission to provide freedom to move in a personal, sustainable and safe way.
In 2024, Volvo Cars reported an operating income of 22.3bn SEK.
In 2024, Volvo Cars reported an EBIT margin of 5.6%.
In 2024, Volvo Cars recorded retail sales of 763.4k units.
In 2024, Volvo Cars reported revenue of 400.2bn SEK.
Since 2021, Volvo Cars has been publicly listed on the Nasdaq Stockholm stock exchange.
The Volvo Car Group structure includes Volvo Cars, software company Zenseact, and HaleyTek, a developer for Android-powered infotainment systems.
Volvo Car Group holds an 18% stake in its strategic affiliate, the electric performance brand Polestar.
Since 1927, Volvo Cars' global headquarters has remained in Gothenburg, Sweden.
Volvo Cars' Americas head office is located in Mahwah, New Jersey.
Volvo Cars' APAC head office is located in Shanghai.
Volvo Cars produces vehicles in Gothenburg (Sweden), Ghent (Belgium), Charleston (US), Chengdu (China), Daqing (China), and Taizhou (China).
Volvo Cars sells its vehicles in more than 100 countries.
Volvo Cars is committed to rethinking sustainability in its operations, cars, and society to help protect the shared world.

facts: Starting in late 2026, Swedish automaker Volvo will begin building the XC60 at its plant in Ridgeville, South Carolina.
The XC60 is Volvo's global best-seller and most popular model in the United States.
This production move strengthens Volvo’s manufacturing footprint in the U.S. to stay competitive against compact luxury SUVs dominated by BMW, Mercedes-Benz, and Lexus.
Currently, most XC60s are manufactured at Volvo's Torslanda plant in Sweden and Ghent facility in Belgium.
XC60 sales in the U.S. surged nearly 23 percent in the first half of 2025.
Volvo Car Americas reported 40,675 vehicle sales in the second quarter of 2025.
Electrified models made up 40.8 percent of that total.
The XC60 accounts for over a third of Volvo’s U.S. sales this year.
A quarter of those sales come from plug-in hybrids.
Globally, the XC60 just overtook the brand’s iconic 240 wagon to become Volvo’s best-selling model of all time.
Over 2.7 million cars are now on the road globally.
Håkan Samuelsson, CEO of Volvo Cars, stated that adding the XC60 to the Charleston production line will strengthen its market position and support/create American manufacturing jobs.
The South Carolina plant already builds Volvo’s fully electric flagship EX90 SUV.
The South Carolina plant also builds the Polestar 3.
More than $1.3 billion has been invested over the last decade since it commenced operations in 2015 with production of the S60 sedan.
The 2.3 million sq.ft facility is equipped with a revamped body and paint shop.
The facility includes a state-of-the-art battery pack line.
It has the capacity to handle multiple platforms and technologies.
Production of the XC60 will be in both mild hybrid and plug-in hybrid variants.
Luis Rezende, President of Volvo Cars Americas, stated that the XC60 is the right car for this market.
He added that it offers the best of Volvo in a versatile size with powertrain options to suit U.S. customers.
The U.S. raised tariffs on the import of cars and spare parts earlier this year.
Tariffs on Chinese-made electric vehicles went from 25 percent to 100 percent, forcing many automakers to rethink their supply chains.
Volvo, owned by China’s Geely but headquartered in Sweden, has largely sidestepped that impact by manufacturing most U.S.-bound vehicles in Europe.
Polestar, also under Geely, has localized production of the Polestar 3 at the South Carolina plant.
Volvo marks 70 years in the American market with over 5 million cars sold since 1955.
The Swedish giant is renowned for Scandinavian design and some of the safest cars on the road.
source_title:
Volvo Expands U.S. Operations With XC60 Production From 2026
– duPont REGISTRY News

facts: Starbucks is partnering with Volvo Cars and ChargePoint to install new DC fast chargers along the 1,350-mile (2,173 km) route between Seattle and Denver.
Driving an electric vehicle between Seattle and Denver should be a bit easier by the end of this year due to the new charger installations.
Anders Gustafsson serves as president and CEO of Volvo Car USA.

Anders Gustafsson stated: “Volvo wants to give people the freedom to move and lower their impact on the environment. Working with Starbucks, we can do that by giving them enjoyable places to relax while their cars recharge.”
Michael Kobori serves as chief sustainability officer at Starbucks.

Michael Kobori stated: “We are thrilled to partner with Volvo Cars to test how we can charge our customers’ electric vehicles at Starbucks stores. Imagine a future where Starbucks helps our customers to connect—more sustainably.”
The new DC fast chargers will be installed at Starbucks stores along the Seattle-Denver route.
Planning an EV drive between Denver and Seattle is currently relatively straightforward due to existing infrastructure.
Tesla currently operates 25 Superchargers along the Seattle-Denver route, spaced at convenient intervals through Washington, Oregon, Idaho, Utah, Wyoming, and Colorado.
Tesla’s infrastructure includes several fast chargers in southern Wyoming.
EVs that require CCS or CHAdeMO fast chargers currently must detour south to reach Denver via a longer route through Utah and Colorado to avoid running out of charge.
The US Department of Energy’s Alternative Fuels Data Center shows more than 140 DC fast chargers (both CCS and CHAdeMO) along the Seattle-Denver route.
source_title: Volvo, Chargepoint, Starbucks to expand Seattle-Denver EV charging - Ars Technica

facts: Volvo Cars powered up a sprawling U.S. assembly plant in Ridgeville, S.C., in the summer of 2018 with a $1.4 billion investment.
The 2.3 million-square-foot factory was designed to produce 150,000 vehicles annually.
According to Automotive News Research & Data Center estimates, Volvo built only 20,000 vehicles at the plant last year, representing 13 percent of its capacity.
Production of the S60 sedan ended a year ago, leaving production of just two electric crossovers: the Volvo EX90 and Polestar 3.
Analyst Jeff Schuster stated that auto assembly plants require a utilization rate of at least 50 percent to break even.
The factory faced challenges from the start due to difficulties finding skilled production talent in the largely rural area of South Carolina.
There has been significant churn in plant leadership.

Sustained losses at the factory, combined with a slump in Volvo's global sales and financial performance, have raised questions about whether Volvo has the financial capacity to continue its U.S. manufacturing effort.
Analyst Jeff Schuster noted that while Volvo can minimize damage by cutting shifts and slowing lines, doing so does not reduce fixed costs.
A Volvo spokeswoman stated the company is "fully committed" to the South Carolina plant, calling it a "key strategic advantage" in its plan to "build where we sell."
To boost factory utilization, Volvo is considering building a higher-volume model, potentially the midsize XC60 hybrid crossover, in the U.S.
Volvo Cars CEO Hakan Samuelsson said in mid-May that the company wants to bring in a midsize core vehicle selling in good numbers rather fast.
Producing the XC60 would require an additional investment of several hundred million dollars into the factory.
Analyst Jeff Schuster questioned whether investing more is the right plan to reach necessary volume or if Volvo should cut its losses now.
Volvo reported a 60 percent plunge in first-quarter operating income, partly due to a global sales slide taxing its balance sheet.
The automaker, which is majority-owned by China's Geely Holding, has lost more than two-thirds of its value since its 2021 initial public offering and has become a target for short sellers.
In May, Volvo announced a corporate restructuring to cut expenses by 1.5 billion Swedish crowns ($140 million), which includes jettisoning about 15 percent of its salaried workforce.
President Donald Trump's imposition of a 25 percent tariff on imports creates an existential crisis for Volvo, which currently ships 90 percent of the vehicles it sells in the U.S.
The tariffs also provide Volvo with a reason to keep the South Carolina plant operational.
CEO Hakan Samuelsson views Trump's tariffs as an opportunity to revive U.S. production.

During the automaker's annual general meeting on April 3, Samuelsson stated that globalization is being "dismantled" and that to avoid high import tariffs, Volvo needs to localize more, increase factory volumes, and lower costs.

Analyst Sam Fiorani suggested it would be prudent for Volvo to make additional investments and build the right products in South Carolina rather than abandoning its U.S. manufacturing investment early.
Fiorani, vice president at AutoForecast Solutions, noted that given the current tariff climate, Volvo could find it challenging to continue profitably in the U.S. without domestic production.
Production in South Carolina began with the third-generation S60 in a market shifting away from sedans.
Automotive News estimated the factory produced nearly 40,000 sedans at its peak in 2019.
Analyst Jeff Schuster criticized the S60 as "the wrong vehicle for the market" because it could not generate the volume required to reach capacity.
Volvo had originally planned to build the XC90 large crossover in Charleston in 2021, a vehicle more aligned with U.S. preferences.
The XC90 plan was derailed when Volvo pivoted to an all-electric strategy to capitalize on post-pandemic demand and government backing for zero-emission vehicles.
Last year, Volvo began building the battery-powered EX90 at the plant, followed by the Polestar 3.
This EV bet proved incorrect as early demand for electric vehicles fizzled.
Volvo eventually abandoned its EV-or-bust strategy in favor of leaning on plug-in hybrid models.
Analyst Sam Fiorani observed that Volvo established the plant to grow as the market transitioned to electric vehicles, but the EV market has not developed to the level the automaker planned.
source_title: Volvo’s little-used U.S. assembly plant is $1.4 billion migraine

VCUSA maintains parts distribution and training centers across the country.

Ambition to become a fully electric car maker.

facts: Volvo Cars has been granted a specific authorization from the Office of Information and Communications Technology and Services under the “Securing the Information and Communications Technology and Services Supply Chain: Connected Vehicles” rule in the United States.
Under the rule, Volvo Car USA was required to follow a process with the US Department of Commerce to obtain a specific authorization for the continued import and sale of connected cars in the US.

The process is carried out on a case-by-case basis and the issuance of a specific authorization follows constructive discussions with the US Department of Commerce and other US officials regarding Volvo Cars' governance, technology and data security.
With this specific authorization, Volvo Cars can continue its growth plans in the US.
The US is one of the largest markets for Volvo Cars and home to its state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Charleston, South Carolina.
Volvo Cars has invested over 1.3 billion USD to date at its Charleston, South Carolina manufacturing facility.
The Charleston, South Carolina manufacturing facility has created over 2,000 jobs.
At the end of September 2025, Volvo Cars announced additional investments in South Carolina.
The additional investments in South Carolina are intended to bring two additional vehicles into production before 2030.
The company’s US headquarters is based in New Jersey with around 400 people employed.
An additional 200 corporate employees are located across the country.
Last year, the company celebrated 70 years in the US.
Currently, Volvo Car USA has 281 dealers in 48 states.
The dealer network employs approximately 11,500 people.
This disclosure contains information that Volvo Car AB (publ) is obliged to make public pursuant to the EU Market Abuse Regulation (EU nr 596/2014).
The information was submitted for publication on 26-05-2026 at 19:53 CET.
source_title: Volvo Cars receives a specific authorization in the United States under the ICTS Connected Vehicles Rule | Volvo Cars Media Intl

facts: Volvo received authorization from the U.S. government to continue importing and selling connected vehicles in the United States.
The authorization was granted by the U.S. Office of Information and Communications Technology and Services on May 26th, 2026.
Volvo is majority-owned by Zhejiang Geely Holding Group, a Chinese automotive conglomerate that acquired the Swedish brand in 2010.
The approval allows Volvo to continue selling vehicles in the U.S. market despite new federal restrictions on connected vehicle technologies originating from China and Russia.
A Biden-era regulation took effect on March 17th, 2025, prohibiting the import and sale of connected vehicles using Chinese and Russian software starting with the 2027 model year.
Restrictions on imported hardware for connected vehicles are set to follow, beginning with the 2030 model year.
Volvo operates vehicle manufacturing facilities in Chengdu, Daqing, and Taizhou, as well as a design center in Shanghai.
The authorization represents only the first step for compliance, as manufacturers must still determine how to satisfy the broader requirements of the Commerce Department rule.
Volvo stated the authorization provides a pathway for the company to continue forward with its plans for growth in the U.S. auto market.

The new federal regulation targets hardware and software considered vulnerable to exploitation by foreign adversaries, potentially providing access to vehicle and driver data or allowing remote control of various vehicle functions.
The rule restricts manufacturers with certain connections to the Chinese government from selling connected vehicles in the United States, even if components or software are sourced elsewhere.
The decision arrives amid broader bipartisan efforts in Washington to limit the influence of Chinese automakers and technology providers in the U.S. automotive sector.
Legislation targeting Chinese vehicle imports has been proposed in both the House and Senate.
A letter signed by more than 70 lawmakers urges stricter oversight of Chinese vehicle imports.
GM confirmed it will build the Buick Envision at its Fairfax plant in Kansas City by 2028, after importing the Envision from China for roughly a decade.
source_title: Chinese-Owned Volvo Connected Car Tech Approved By U.S. Govt

facts: Seoul, Korea-based Hyundai Motor Group (HMG) will make a $5.54 billion investment to open its first fully dedicated electric vehicle (EV) and battery manufacturing facility in Georgia.
HMG’s new state-of-the-art U.S. smart factory will be located at the Bryan County Megasite.
Non-affiliated Hyundai Motor Group suppliers will invest approximately $1 billion in the project.
The project will create approximately 8,100 new jobs in Georgia’s coastal region.
Hyundai Motor Group Executive Chair Euisun Chung stated they are incredibly proud to share plans to open their first dedicated full EV and battery manufacturing facilities in the U.S.

Euisun Chung noted the U.S. has always held an important place in the Group’s global strategy and expressed excitement to partner with the State of Georgia for electrified mobility and sustainability goals.
Hyundai Motor Group expects to begin construction on the new facility in January 2023.
Full production is expected in the first half of 2025.
The facility will have an annual capacity of 300,000 units.
Hyundai Motor Company President and CEO Jaehoon (Jay) Chang stated they decided to build their first dedicated EV plant in the U.S. because America embraces change and drives innovation.
Jaehoon (Jay) Chang noted the new EV plant is the future of their business and will help meet growing demands of U.S. customers for leading edge design, safe, zero-emissions vehicles.
HMG cited speed-to-market, workforce, and the state’s ability to meet the company’s carbon neutrality standards as reasons for choosing Georgia.
Georgia is home to an existing network of Hyundai subsidiaries and suppliers.
The 2,923-acre Bryan County Megasite was purchased jointly by the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority (JDA) and the state of Georgia less than a year ago.
The megasite offers needed speed-to-market requirements that reduce barriers to speedy operations.
Industrial utilities are adjacent to the site, and extensive due diligence reports have been completed.
Governor Brian P. Kemp welcomed Hyundai Motor Group to Georgia and mentioned initial conversations on his economic development mission to Korea.
Governor Kemp noted Georgia’s investment in the Bryan County Megasite prepared them for this opportunity.
Governor Kemp stated this announcement is now the largest economic development project in the state’s history.
President and COO of Hyundai Motor Company José Muñoz expressed thrill to partner with Governor Kemp and the Savannah Joint Economic Development Authority.
José Muñoz described the facility as a flexible EV smart factory that will position Hyundai well to win in a competitive market with consumers demanding smart, sustainable mobility solutions.
The new highly connected, automated, and flexible EV smart factory will produce a diverse range of innovative full electric vehicles.
Through the battery manufacturing facility, HMG aims to establish a stable supply chain for EV battery and other EV components in the U.S. market.
Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor JDA Board of Directors Chairman Carter Infinger called the announcement transformative.
Carter Infinger stated the jobs and investment will reverberate for years and generations to come.
Carter Infinger noted this is a great day for Bryan, Bulloch, Chatham and Effingham counties as well as the entire region and the State of Georgia.
Bryan County is the fastest-growing county in the state.
The strategic purchase of the Bryan County Megasite in 2021 was the largest in state history.
The site is adjacent to Interstate 16 with immediate access from I-95 and I-16 to 250 major metro areas.
The site is less than 30 miles from the Port of Savannah, the single-largest and fastest-growing container terminal in the U.S.
Two Class I rail facilities on-site are provided by Georgia Central Railway.

Georgia Department of Transportation Commissioner Russell McMurry stated Hyundai’s facility announcement is evidence that Georgia’s collective focus on supporting the electric mobility future is working.
Russell McMurry highlighted the power of partnership and preparation, noting Georgia’s planning ensures logistics networks are safe, sustainable, innovative, and ready to support economic growth.
Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) Commissioner Pat Wilson stated they have been focused on putting Georgia at the forefront of electric mobility under Governor Kemp’s leadership.
Pat Wilson noted the announcement solidifies Georgia’s spot at the vanguard of the EV transition.
Pat Wilson welcomed the Hyundai Group to Georgia and celebrated the investment, stating the facility will create new possibilities for all Georgians and transform an entire region.
Since 2020, Georgia has announced more than 20 EV-related projects that will invest more than $13.54 billion and create nearly 18,000 jobs in the state.
source_title: Hyundai Will Build First Fully-Dedicated EV Plant In Georgia - Automotive - Business Facilities Magazine

facts: Volvo Cars of Macon is located at 4781 Riverside Dr., Macon, GA 31210-1115.
The dealership serves customers in Warner Robins GA, Milledgeville GA, Fort Valley GA, and Centerville GA.
Inventory includes new and pre-owned luxury vehicles such as the Volvo EX90, S90, XC40, XC6, XC90, and EX30.
The finance team provides lease and auto loan options with quality financing terms for vehicle purchases.
Service operations utilize factory-trained technicians and a selection of authentic Volvo parts.
Customers are provided with complimentary shuttle service and loaner vehicles during service appointments.

Warranty claims are handled by dealership staff who advocate for customers with Volvo corporate.
Sales department contact information is available via telephone.

The dealership offers online purchase capabilities and trade-in appraisals for its vehicle inventory.
