facts: Volvo will pull its smallest, most-affordable EV from the US market at the end of this model year (2026).
The Volvo EX30 is one of the most affordable and second-smallest EVs in America.
The EX30 is the quickest car Volvo has ever made.
A software update will add V2L capability to all EX30s via over-the-air update soon.
Volvo announced a new lower-priced trim for the EX30, though it did not specify if this trim would come to the US.
Volvo told dealers on Friday that the EX30 and EX30 Cross Country off-road variant will be pulled from the US market at the end of the 2026 model year.
Dealers have until the end of this week (Friday) to place orders for the EX30 and EX30 Cross Country.
Volvo Car USA stated it is ending sales of the EX30 and EX30 Cross Country in the US market after the 2026 model year.
The EX30 will remain available in other global markets, including Canada and Mexico.
Volvo cited "market conditions and financial factors" for the withdrawal decision.
Volvo confirmed that all current US owners will continue to receive support, service, parts, warranty, and software updates regardless of whether their model is still on sale.
Production of the EX30 was shifted from China to Volvo’s factory in Ghent, Belgium, in part to avoid tariffs.
The EX30 originally launched globally in the 2024 model year but arrived in the US in the 2025 model year due to US tariffs on EVs from China.
The US will only receive two total model years of the EX30.
Volvo will continue selling its other EVs in the US, including the EX40 SUV (formerly XC40), the EX90 three-row electric SUV, and the upcoming EX60.
The article attributes the withdrawal decision to Republicans inflating EV prices by $7,500, which disproportionately hit lower-end EVs.
The price inflation caused a pull-forward of demand before the hike, depressing subsequent EV sales.
Automakers are reacting quickly to depressed EV sales and market conditions.
The article notes that smaller, more efficient EVs like the EX30 could help address affordability crises and reduce oil dependency amid rising gas prices.
The EX30 is 166 inches long and offers a usable amount of space for a family despite its small size.
Competitors or alternatives mentioned include the Kia EV4, Rivian R3 (161-inch-long), and Chevy Bolt (169-inch-long).
source_title: Volvo is killing its smallest EV in the US, but the rest of the world can still get it

facts: Volvo Cars announced it will begin producing a new hybrid model in the United States by the end of this decade to adjust to President Donald Trump’s tariff policies.
The new hybrid model will be designed specifically for the U.S. market to help boost capacity utilization at Volvo's South Carolina plant.
Volvo Cars CEO Hakan Samuelsson stated that it will take time until America is fully electric.
CEO Hakan Samuelsson described the new larger hybrid model as primarily electric with a backup combustion engine that activates when the battery is depleted.
Automakers worldwide are rethinking production strategies to address President Trump's duties on foreign cars as part of a broader policy to boost U.S. manufacturing.

In April, Volvo Cars' CEO stated the company would increase U.S. vehicle production, and in July, the company announced plans to begin producing the XC60 mid-size SUV at its South Carolina plant starting in late 2026.
The South Carolina plant currently produces only the fully electric EX90 SUV and the Polestar 3, utilizing only a small fraction of its 150,000 vehicle annual capacity.
The announcement of the new hybrid model coincided with Volvo's 70th anniversary in the United States.
Volvo previously aimed to phase out all non-electric models by 2030 but reversed course last year to keep hybrids in its lineup.

Luis Rezende, president of Volvo Cars' Americas operation, stated that adding another model to local production will help realize the full potential of their U.S. manufacturing investments and workforce.
A July trade deal with the European Union reduced U.S. tariffs on European cars to 15%, though it has not taken effect and vehicles currently face a 27.5% levy.
CEO Hakan Samuelsson noted that eliminating 10% duties on auto imports from the U.S. to Europe makes selling the U.S.-produced EX90 abroad more attractive.
Samuelsson stated that the 10% tariff reduction provides an opportunity for growth in Volvo's European operations.
source_title: Volvo Cars to produce new US model amid tariff pressure – Wall Street Observer

facts: Volvo Cars set a new global sales record with full-year 2024 sales of 763,389 cars, an increase of 8 percent compared to 2023.
December 2024 global sales reached 73,804 cars, a decrease of 3 percent compared to December 2023.
Full-year 2024 fully electric car sales totaled 175,194 units, an increase of 54 percent compared to 2023.
Full-year 2024 plug-in hybrid car sales totaled 177,593 units, a 16 percent increase compared to 2023.
Electrified models accounted for 46 percent of all Volvo cars sold globally during 2024.
Fully electric cars accounted for 23 percent of global sales in 2024, up from 16 percent in 2023.
European sales reached 369,685 cars in 2024, a 25 percent increase compared to 2023.
Electrified models accounted for 65 percent of all cars sold in Europe during 2024.
US sales for 2024 amounted to 125,243 cars, down 3 percent compared to the full year of 2023.
Electrified model sales in the US increased by 20 percent and accounted for 34 percent of all cars sold in the US during 2024.
Sales in China decreased 8 percent compared to 2023, resulting in 156,370 sold cars during 2024.
Overall sales of electrified models in China grew 3 percent during the January to December 2024 period.
The Volvo XC60 was the top-selling model globally in 2024 with sales of 230,853 cars.
The XC40/EX40 was the second top-selling model with total sales of 173,890 cars.
The XC90 had sales of 108,621 cars in 2024.
Volvo Cars' production plants are located in Gothenburg, Ghent (Belgium), South Carolina (US), Chengdu, Daqing and Taizhou (China).
As of December 2023, Volvo Cars employed approximately 43,400 full-time employees.
Volvo Cars' head office, product development, marketing and administration functions are mainly located in Gothenburg, Sweden.
Volvo Cars has R&D and design centres in Gothenburg and Shanghai (China).
Volvo Cars is listed on the Nasdaq Stockholm exchange under the ticker “VOLCAR B”.
Volvo Cars aims to become a fully electric car maker and achieve net-zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2040.
source_title: Volvo Cars reports new global sales record in 2024 - Volvo Car AB (publ)

facts: Volvo Cars is funneling additional capital into its Ridgeville, South Carolina plant.
The investment builds on a $1.3 billion investment over the past decade.
The goal is to fully utilize the facility’s 150,000-vehicle annual capacity.
The Ridgeville plant has been operational since 2015.
The plant currently produces the electric EX90 SUV and Polestar 3 but runs below potential.
Volvo will introduce XC60 mid-size SUV production from late 2026.
The XC60 model sold over 27,000 units in the US in the first eight months of 2025.
XC60 sales are up nearly 20 percent year-on-year.
This production shift may counter rising tariffs on Chinese imports, which have disrupted global automotive trade since 2024.
Before 2030, a US-specific hybrid model, likely a next-generation XC90, will join the lineup.
The upcoming hybrid model will blend petrol and electric power to appeal to buyers hesitant about full EVs.
This reflects Volvo’s regionalization strategy, tailoring products and production to local markets to mitigate risks from geopolitical tensions and supply bottlenecks.
Håkan Samuelsson, chief executive, stated that the investment plans reinforce long-term commitment to the US market and manufacturing operations in South Carolina.
Volvo Cars is celebrating 70 years of presence in the United States this year.
Volvo has sold over 5 million cars in the US and plans to sell many more in years to come.
South Carolina’s status as a US automotive powerhouse leading in vehicle exports is bolstered by Volvo’s plans.
The state’s skilled workforce and port access have drawn over $2 billion in auto-related investments since 2020.
Volvo’s expansion is poised to add jobs and supplier activity, though exact figures remain undisclosed.
Nearby, Magna made a $200 million investment that created 200 roles.
The shift to electrification demands new skills in battery systems and software, areas where South Carolina lags behind tech hubs like California.
Henry McMaster, Governor of South Carolina, stated that Volvo's decision reinforces South Carolina's position as a national leader in automotive manufacturing.
Henry McMaster highlighted the strength of the state's workforce and the confidence top companies have in it, expressing pride in continuing the partnership with Volvo Cars.
The plant’s ability to handle multiple platforms and technologies offers agility in a turbulent market.
South Carolina’s reliance on foreign manufacturers like Volvo and BMW, whose exports exceed $10 billion annually, raises questions about economic sovereignty if trade policies harden.
Volvo’s US strategy aligns with its global network, including plants in Europe and China, to deliver region-specific products while pursuing net-zero emissions by 2040.
Volvo recently reported a $1 billion loss in Q2 2025.
Volvo now targets 50 to 60 percent electrified sales by year-end, down from earlier ambitions.
EV demand is waning amid sparse charging networks and reduced subsidies.
Hybrids are outpacing pure EVs in US sales, making Ridgeville’s planned hybrid a pragmatic bet.
Luis Rezende, Volvo Cars’ president of Americas, stated that the Charleston plant is foundational to the strategic growth plan for the US.

Luis Rezende added that adding another model into production will take a big step toward realizing the full potential of local manufacturing investments and workforce, accelerating progress toward commercial targets.
Volvo’s 2024 performance included a core operating profit of $2.87 billion.
Volvo’s 2024 revenue was $42.5 billion.
Global sales in 2024 were 763,389 vehicles.
Rising material costs and a softening premium market pose risks to Volvo's investment push.
Localizing production in Ridgeville may ease reliance on China’s EV supply chain, a strategy mirrored by rivals like BMW.
Success will depend on hybrids bridging the gap to full electrification, as consumer caution and infrastructure gaps slow the transition.
source_title: Volvo Cars boosts South Carolina plant with hybrid and EV production

facts: Volvo Trucks offers fuel-efficient designs and technology to increase fuel efficiency.
The company has introduced a new EPA 2027 powertrain solution.
Its trucks are engineered for long haul with aerodynamic design and advanced safety features.
The designs aim to boost productivity and profitability while delivering agility, efficiency, and comfort.
The Volvo VNR Electric is a zero-emission, high-performing truck that delivers up to 275 miles per charge.
The Volvo VNX is built for heavy loads and extreme demands, offering strength and control.
The Volvo VHD is purpose-built for vocational tasks and designed to handle tough jobs.
The low-profile Volvo VAH is optimized for auto transport, offering maneuverability and visibility.
The company frames its approach as an invitation to drive progress and sustainably shape the world.
The trucks are marketed as the safest for drivers and everyone else that shares the road.
Driving and living environments are optimized for maximum driver comfort.
On-board real-time diagnostics and service experts are available 24/7.
Powertrain options include battery electric, renewable diesel, and fuel-cell technology to minimize environmental footprint.
source_title: Volvo Trucks | Industry-Leading Commercial Semi Trucks

facts: The battery-electric version of Volvo Trucks North America’s VNR Class 8 regional hauler, dubbed the EVNR, is slated to go on sale at the end of 2020.
Peter Voorhoeve, VTNA President, announced the EVNR sales timeline at an Oct. 6 press conference at the American Trucking Associations’ annual Management Conference and Exhibition in San Diego.

The EVNR launch will follow ongoing testing of battery-electric trucks with two California-based fleets as part of the Volvo Low Impact Green Heavy Transport Solutions (Volvo Lights) development program.
The Volvo Lights development program is being conducted with California’s South Coast Air Quality Management District and over a dozen industry partners.
Most funding for the $90 million Volvo Lights project is coming from the California Air Resources Board, with about $4 million also coming from the SCAQMD.
Once on the market, the EVNR will initially target local and regional distribution fleets and short-haul drayage operations.
The EVNR features a conventional Volvo I-Shift automated transmission optimized for electric powertrains and uses large battery packs mounted on the frame rail ahead of the drive tires.
Sweden-based Volvo Group currently has a million connected assets operating globally, including 560,000 Volvo trucks, with 200,000 operating in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico.
Peter Voorhoeve stated that connectivity secures more uptime and drives sustainability alongside better vehicle maintenance.
North America is Volvo’s second-home market and its largest truck-selling market.
Voorhoeve estimated that 2019 will finish with 325,000 new Class 8 truck orders in North America.
A strong labor market and consumer spending are still driving U.S. GDP growth, though overall economic growth is slowing down and affecting confidence.
Volvo’s August year-to-date market share increased slightly from 13.5 to 13.9 in Canada.
Volvo’s August year-to-date market share increased significantly from 1.9 to 3.2 in Mexico.
Volvo’s August year-to-date market share decreased in the U.S. from 10.7 to 9.7, attributed to starting slow at the beginning of the year.
VTNA has made a decade of substantial investments in its dealer network, which now numbers 435 locations.
Dealers have also made significant investments, with one dealership recently opening a 100-bay facility compared to the typical high number of 30 bays.
Volvo is investing $400 million over six years to expand and upgrade its New River Valley, Virginia, assembly plant.
The Virginia plant expansion is partly intended to create capacity for building electric trucks and will lead to over 700 new jobs at the facility.
Volvo’s trucks are proudly assembled in the U.S.A., with their entire manufacturing process considered "made in the U.S.A."
VTNA continues to invest in its centralized Uptime Center, allowing staff to remotely monitor customer trucks and provide over-the-air reprogramming of connected components.
Over-the-air updates can change shift patterns of I-Shift automated manual transmissions to match new duty cycles.
VTNA partnered with telematics provider Geotab to offer an integrated electronic logging device (ELD) solution for all Volvo-powered trucks of model year 2015 and newer.

The ELD solution couples Volvo’s factory-fit telematics device with Geotab’s ELD application using a cloud-based system to ensure reliable data capture, FMCSA compliance, and full Android/iOS compatibility.
Fleet managers can access ELD data through the MyGeotab portal to track compliance, view driver logs, violation alerts, and running fleet status reports.
Geotab Drive for Volvo Trucks provides reliable ELD data-capture capabilities that keep HOS data safe and secure while avoiding pitfalls of hardwired systems, pairing requirements, or USB downloads.
Volvo Trucks began partnering with Geotab in 2016 to improve telematics capabilities.
VTNA’s sister company Mack Trucks separately announced it is rolling out Geotab Drive for Mack Trucks.

David Pardue, Mack Trucks Vice President of Connected Vehicle and Contract Services, stated the partnership uses existing cloud-based technology to report ELD information and help fleets maintain driver hours of service compliance.
Cloud-based data reporting simplifies the process for customers by eliminating the need for new ELD devices or reliance on hardware/wireless connections, making data collection more reliable.
The factory-installed telematics device in every Mack vehicle sends engine and positional data to the cloud to generate automatic duty status logs.
Drivers can manually enter duty status logs through the Geotab Drive app on a mobile device, which consolidates them into an accurate record of duty status.
source_title: Volvo to Sell Electric Class 8 Truck in the U.S. by End of 2020 | Heavy Duty Trucking

facts: Volvo Group posted a profit of $771 million in Q2 2025, less than half its $1.6 billion profit in the year-ago period.
Parent company revenue decreased 12% year over year to $12.59 billion from $14.36 billion a year earlier.
Truck sales revenue declined 7% to $8.37 billion in the three months ending June 30, down 14% from 9.74 billion krona in the year-ago period.
Operating margin was 8.1% in Q2 2025, compared with 14.5% in Q2 2024.
CEO Martin Lundstedt stated that net sales decreased by 5% adjusted for currency due to general stabilization in Europe and uncertainty among customers in North America.

The societal transformation to zero-emission vehicles is slower than anticipated, causing costs of $460 million related to compensation for lower battery volume commitments and impairment of battery-electric assets to negatively impact reported operating income.
Renegotiated battery delivery contracts required Volvo to pay suppliers $297 million in compensation, with $195 million taken against Q2 earnings.
The company took a $164 million charge against earnings on the value of its battery-electric assets.

CEO Martin Lundstedt warned that enabling conditions like charging infrastructure and demand stimulus have not yet been put in place, urging policymakers, state leaders, and stakeholders to take action.
Volvo sold 52,764 trucks globally in Q2, down 10% from 58,935 trucks in the same period a year earlier.
Volvo sold 101,597 trucks in the first six months of 2025, a decrease of 11% from 114,405 trucks a year earlier.
Weak demand in North America is due to uncertainty surrounding tariffs and EPA 2027 emissions regulations, prompting production capacity reductions to adapt to lower demand.
VTNA and Mack announced on April 23 that they would lay off 1,000 staff at manufacturing plants in the U.S.
Volvo peers Traton Group and Daimler Truck have also laid off or are about to lay off employees in North America due to soft demand for Class 8 on-highway vehicles.
Daimler Truck North America will temporarily lay off 2,000 production staff, with return contingent on an upturn in orders.
Parent company of Freightliner and Western Star sold 38,580 trucks and buses in Q2, down 20% from 48,246 a year earlier.
North America’s over-the-road freight market is in recession due to lower freight volume and rates, though the vocational market is relatively better.
Overall industrywide heavy-duty truck retail sales in North America through end of June were down 6% at 135,723, compared with 145,056 in the first half of 2024.
Volvo’s sales in North America decreased by 20% to 12,981 trucks from 16,234.
VTNA sales dived 43% year over year to 4,988 trucks from 8,779 in Q2 2024.
Mack sales rose 6% to 7,940 trucks from 7,475 in the year-ago period.
Order intake in North America across VTNA and Mack decreased by 16% to 8,243 trucks due to carriers' wait-and-see stance.
VTNA’s orders rose 29% to 4,131 trucks from 3,193 in the year-ago period, while Mack’s orders totaled 4,080 trucks, a 38% slump from 6,556 a year earlier.
VTNA’s North American heavy-duty truck market share was 7.6% in Q2, down from 9.5%, as it revamped its on-highway lineup.
Mack’s market share rose to 7.2% from 6.1% due to an improved supply chain and good vocational demand.
Volvo Group paid $40 million in October 2024 to bring all of Mack’s cab assembly in-house after buying Commercial Vehicle Group’s Kings Mountain, N.C., plant.
VTNA’s on-highway lineup saw a redesign of its flagship VNL tractor and the regional haul VNR tractor.
Mack launched a new flagship on-highway tractor called the Pioneer and repositioned its Anthem predecessor to the regional haul market.
At Volvo Group’s 2024 Capital Markets Day in Dublin, Va., executives outlined plans for 25% North American heavy-duty truck market share by 2030.

links.context: Top 100 for-hire carriers podcast and information

source_title: Volvo Q2 Profit Halves on US Truck, Battery-Electric Weakness - TT

facts: Volvo Construction Equipment (Volvo CE) is making a significant investment to expand production worldwide.
Approximately $261 million will be invested across three main sites for crawler excavator production: Shippensburg, Penn., USA; Changwon, South Korea; and a location in Sweden.
In Shippensburg, Volvo CE will add crawler excavator production and expand wheel loader production to include large wheel loaders.
The Shippensburg factory currently manufactures soil and asphalt compactors and mid-size wheel loaders.
Updates to the existing Shippensburg factory space will include installing assembly lines, integrating more automation technologies into the manufacturing process, and training employees.
The goal is to be production-ready in the first half of 2026.

Melker Jernberg, Head of Volvo CE, stated that strategically enhancing production capacity and flexibility will allow the OEM to meet current and future customer demands more effectively in a rapidly evolving market.
Expanding production capabilities in key markets will reduce Volvo CE's dependency on any single site and decrease reliance on long-distance logistics.
Supply chain risks will be mitigated by expanding domestic supplier bases, allowing the OEM to more nimbly manage economic or regulatory challenges.

Jernberg noted that fostering collaboration with local suppliers and customers will better position the company for sustained growth and innovation without compromising Volvo CE equipment's high standards.

Scott Young, Head of Region North America, stated that bringing excavator production to North America and growing the range of wheel loader models built there has always been part of their long-term industrial plan.

The increase in production capacity means that over 50% of Volvo CE's North American machine supply can be built in Shippensburg, resulting in shorter lead times while creating opportunities for supplier growth.
Volvo CE acquired the Shippensburg site in 2007 from Ingersoll Rand and relocated its regional headquarters there in 2012.
On top of recent investments, Volvo CE will invest approximately $40 million locally over the next five years.
source_title: Volvo Construction Equipment to Expand Production in North America

facts: The New River Valley assembly plant is located on 566 acres at 4881 Cougar Trail Road, P.O. Box 1126, Dublin, VA 24084 U.S.A.
The facility covers 2.3 million square feet and serves as the largest Volvo truck manufacturing facility in the world.
The plant is certified under ISO 9001 quality, ISO 14001 environmental, and ISO 50001 energy standards.
The New River Valley plant produces all Volvo trucks sold in North America.
Vehicle models manufactured at the site include the VNR, VNR Electric, VNL, VNX, VHD, and VAH.
Production of the all-new Volvo VNL model at the New River Valley plant is scheduled to start in mid-2024.
source_title: New River Valley Plant | Volvo Trucks USA

canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Group Meta Plant America
facts: Hyundai Motor Group Meta Plant America (HMGMA) is the largest single economic development project in Georgia history.
The facility is entering the final phases of commissioning as of November 2025.
The $7.6 billion EV and battery metaplant is located in Bryan County.
Genesis, Hyundai, and Kia electric vehicle production remains on track to begin in Q1 2025.
Battery pack assembly operations are starting even earlier than vehicle production.
The facility is Hyundai’s strategic centerpiece to secure full $7,500 U.S. federal EV tax credit eligibility under the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) by localizing both vehicle and battery production.

Unlike legacy automakers relying on retrofitted factories, Hyundai built a fully modernized “Metaplant” from the ground up with the E-GMP (Electric-Global Modular Platform) as the backbone of manufacturing.
A strategic goal is to qualify Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis EVs for the full $7,500 tax credit, which requires North American final assembly plus battery content.
A strategic goal is to reduce reliance on China-centered battery supply chains.
The facility aims to reach 300,000+ annual vehicle capacity by 2028.
The project will generate 8,500 direct jobs plus tens of thousands in supplier network employment.
The campus spans 2,900 acres.
The Vehicle Assembly Plant has a capacity of 300,000 units per year, expandable to 500,000.
Battery Pack Assembly (HMGMA + LG Energy Solution JV) has an initial capacity of 30 GWh per year, equivalent to approximately 300,000–350,000 packs.
The Stamping, Body, Paint, and Trim Shops utilize over 500 robots on fully automated lines.
Trials for the Stamping, Body, Paint, and Trim Shops were completed in Q4 2024.
An on-site Supplier Park hosts 12 Tier-1 suppliers including Glovis, Seohan, and DAS.

Confirmed launch models for 2025–2026 include the Hyundai IONIQ 7 (three-row E-GMP SUV), Kia EV9 GT (high-performance variant), Genesis GV90 (flagship luxury electric SUV), and next-gen Hyundai IONIQ 9 (expected sibling to IONIQ 7 for select markets).
All vehicles will feature 800V architecture.
All vehicles will feature ultra-fast charging capable of reaching 10–80% in approximately 18 minutes.
All vehicles will support Vehicle-to-Load (V2L) and Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G).
The battery plant is a 50/50 Hyundai–LG Energy Solution joint venture featuring NCMA high-nickel pouch cells with low cobalt content.
Battery cell capacity is expandable to over 50 GWh.
Cells are sourced from U.S. LG plants in Michigan and an upcoming Arizona plant.
Assembling packs in Georgia qualifies them for the full IRA battery credit.
This battery strategy mirrors successful North American partnerships used by Ford–SK On and GM–LG Ultium.
The State of Georgia provides $1.8 billion in cash and tax incentives conditioned on job creation and investment milestones.
Bryan County and local entities provide over $400 million in land and infrastructure support.
Federal IRA leverage provides indirect funding totaling hundreds of millions annually via consumer tax credits, conditioned on final assembly and battery content.
Total public support is approximately $2.2–2.5 billion.
The project cost grew to $7.6 billion due to expanded battery operations and higher construction costs.
More than 6,000 workers are on-site at peak construction.
Body and paint shop equipment installation is 95% complete.
First pilot vehicles were produced in September–October 2025.
Mass-production trial runs are set for December 2025.
A grand opening is expected in January 2025.
Hyundai reports progress is “on track or slightly ahead of schedule.”
Full tax credit eligibility will allow Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis EVs to compete directly with Tesla, Ford, GM, and Rivian on price.
Models like the IONIQ 7 could launch below $50,000 after incentives.
Georgia is becoming a Korean EV manufacturing hub combined with Kia’s West Point expansion and SK On–Hyundai battery plants in Bartow County.
Georgia is positioned as a major North American EV manufacturing corridor.
The Hyundai Metaplant represents a highly integrated EV ecosystem engineered specifically for IRA compliance, 800V E-GMP scaling, and next-generation manufacturing flexibility.
Once the first IONIQ 7 rolls off the line in early 2025, Hyundai’s evolution from rapid follower to genuine North American EV volume leader becomes official.
source_title: Hyundai Metaplant America: Georgia’s $7.6B EV Megafactory Nears Production – Everything You Need to Know

facts: The North America lithium-ion battery market size was valued at USD 22.1 Billion in 2025.
IMARC Group estimates the market to reach USD 52.9 Billion by 2034, exhibiting a CAGR of 9.86% from 2026-2034.
Market growth is driven by growing adoption of electric vehicles and expanding energy storage systems deployment.

Growth is further expanded by domestic lithium-ion battery manufacturing, increasing investment in battery recycling and second-life applications, and advancements in battery technology and alternative chemistries.
Base year for market analysis is 2025, with forecast years 2026-2034 and historical years 2020-2025.
Rising acceptance of electric vehicles and government emissions regulations prompting acquisition incentives are major causes supporting the North American lithium-ion battery market.
The Biden administration has provided funding for charging stations within domestic battery manufacturing boundaries to boost electric vehicle growth.
Leading automakers Tesla, General Motors, and Ford have ramped up production of electric vehicles.
In December 2024, Volkswagen and PowerCo joined to invest $48 million in Patriot Battery Metals to ensure a long-term North American lithium supply for EV batteries.
Battery technology improvements toward better energy density and faster charging are making electric cars competitive with internal combustion engine vehicles.
Growing deployment of energy storage systems supports the integration of variable renewable energy sources.
In November 2024, EDF Renewables secured a 20-year power purchase agreement with APS for the 250 MW Beehive BESS in Arizona.
Construction of the Beehive BESS facility is anticipated to initiate in 2025, focusing on storing and discharging renewable energy during peak-demand periods.
The Beehive BESS facility will encompass enclosures of lithium-ion batteries.
Lithium-ion batteries are selected for ESS applications due to elevated energy density, long life cycles above 5000 cycles, and decreasing costs.
Government programs supporting energy storage deployment and rising private sector investments aid market growth.
Requirements for residential, commercial, and bulk storage systems are anticipated to chart an upward growth trend.
North America is undergoing a significant ramp-up of lithium-ion battery manufacturing supported by government initiatives and private sector investments.
The US Inflation Reduction Act and Bipartisan Infrastructure Law provide incentives for domestic battery production and reduce foreign supply chain reliance.
Tesla, Panasonic, and LG Energy Solution are ramping up gigafactory building programs across the U.S. and Canada.
In November 2024, Asahi Kasei Battery Separator Corporation and Honda began constructing a lithium-ion battery separator plant in Ontario, Canada.
The Ontario separator plant is set to produce 700 million square meters per year by 2027.
Battery recycling and second-life uses are rapidly evolving into main sustainability strategies due to the magnifying need for lithium-ion batteries.
Companies invest in recycling technologies to recover valuable materials like nickel, lithium, and cobalt while reducing environmental disposal burdens.

In December 2024, American Battery Technology Company received a USD 144 million grant from the U.S. Department of Energy for a lithium-ion battery recycling plant in conjunction with Argonne National Laboratory.
Government policies favoring a circular economy and new business models for second-life battery applications support industry changes.
Redwood Materials and Li-Cycle are spearheading efforts to set up large-scale recycling infrastructure in North America.
Industry innovations emphasize energy density, charging speed, and safety advancements.
Next-generation battery chemistries like solid-state batteries and lithium-iron-phosphate (LFP) are trending due to improved thermal stability and cost-effectiveness.
LFP batteries are being adopted by automakers and energy storage companies for stationary storage and entry-level EVs, while high-performance applications see solid-state development.

In September 2024, BMW North America collaborated with Redwood Materials to recycle lithium-ion batteries, targeting a closed-loop system reintroducing 95-98% of critical minerals into the supply chain.
Lithium Cobalt Oxide (LCO) is the preferred cathode material for consumer electronics in North America due to high energy density and stable performance.
LCO adoption for EVs is complicated by high costs and cobalt supply chain risks, forcing manufacturers to search for alternative chemistries with little or no cobalt and higher nickel.

Lithium Iron Phosphate (LFP) is emerging as a new technology for North American EVs, energy storage systems, and industrial applications due to long cycle life, thermal stability, and lower cost compared to cobalt-based chemistries.
Tesla and Ford are increasingly implementing LFP in affordable electric vehicle models to meet sustainability goals and increase domestic battery production.
Lithium Nickel Manganese Cobalt (NMC) predominates in the North American EV scene due to high energy density and balanced performance.
General Motors, Tesla, and Rivian utilize NMC for electric vehicles requiring longer range.
Battery makers are optimizing high nickel levels in NMC variants to lessen cobalt dependency, improve cost efficiency, and enhance sustainability and energy output.
Lithium Manganese Oxide (LMO) is used for hybrid vehicles, power tools, and medical equipment across North America due to high thermal stability and safety.
Despite lower energy density, LMO's cost and safety make it preferred for short-range electric vehicles and backup power systems.
0 to 3000mAh lithium-ion batteries are used in North America for consumer electronics like cell phones, wearables, and wireless accessories.
Batteries ranging from 3000mAh to 10000mAh are used in North America for laptops, tablets, drones, medical gadgets, and power banks.
10,000-60,000mAh lithium-ion batteries support electric two-wheelers, power tools, industrial tools, and energy storage systems in North America.
Batteries greater than 60,000mAh are needed for EVs, grid storage, and heavy-duty industrial applications in North America.
Automakers Tesla, General Motors, and Ford are scaling up battery production using LFP and NMC chemistries to balance costs with sustainable practices.
Government incentives and ever-growing charging infrastructure provide impetus for accelerated EV market adoption in North America.
Lithium-ion battery storage systems in North America provide backup power, grid stabilization, and renewable energy integration for utilities and enterprises.
The United States dominates the North American lithium-ion battery market due to demand stimulated by EV manufacturing, energy storage, and consumer electronics.
Incentives from the Inflation Reduction Act support domestic battery manufacturing and supply chain development in the U.S.
Tesla, LG Energy Solution, and Panasonic have expanded their gigafactories in the U.S. to gain an advantage in the EV and ESS markets.
Canada plays a major role in the lithium-ion battery industry due to critical mineral supplies such as cobalt, lithium, and nickel.
Investments are being made in battery manufacturing and recycling in Canada to strengthen its supply chain for EVs and energy storage.
Lion Electric and BASF Canada are creating battery production hubs for sustainable mobility and energy storage solutions.
Mexico is rapidly becoming a major lithium-ion battery manufacturing center due to increased automotive production and the USMCA.
BMW and Ford are ramping up EV battery assembly in Mexico.
Mexico is exploring domestic lithium extraction from deposits in Sonora with the objective of integrating with the North American battery supply chain.
Companies are investing in gigafactories to localize production and mitigate supply chain risks.
In May 2024, Li-Cycle partnered with Daimler Truck North America to recycle lithium-ion batteries from electric vehicles to support a circular economy.

Key players in the North American lithium-ion battery market include A123 Systems, Duracell Inc., Tesla Inc., Saft America, Johnson Controls, CANBAT Technologies Inc., Corvus Energy, Prime Power, and Panasonic Corporation.
source_title: North America Lithium-ion Battery Market Size, Outlook 2034

facts: CARB announced a settlement with Volvo Group North America, LLC for nearly $197 million dollars for undisclosed auxiliary emission control devices (AECD) that violated the state’s emissions and certification requirements.
The state will receive $17.5 million in penalties and costs.
The state will receive $71 million to mitigate the air quality harms caused by the violations.
Volvo is committing $108 million toward emission reduction projects in California.
The case involves approximately 10,000 Volvo model year 2010 through 2016 diesel engines used in heavy-duty trucks throughout California.
During review of certification applications, CARB found inconsistencies in the data and uncovered alleged violations of California’s emissions regulations.
CARB alleged these engines used AECDs that were not adequately disclosed during the required emissions certification process.
These Volvo engines emitted more NOx than allowed by the emission standard.
Failure to report AECDs completely and accurately to CARB is a major violation of California’s heavy-duty engine regulations.
Volvo was cooperative throughout CARB’s investigation and agreed to offer a recall repair and extended warranty to address the AECDs.
Volvo will also carry out OBD testing.
The recall will apply to several of Volvo’s 2014 through 2016 model year 11, 13 and 16 L diesel engines.
The recall is expected to take place in 2027 and is not mandatory.
If Volvo fails to meet the recall target of 85% within 3 years, additional penalties will be applied.
Details of the AECD violation were not disclosed.
The extended warranty applies to NOx sensors (including engine out and system out), exhaust temperature sensors and components of the DEF dosing system.

The OBD testing requires evaluation of numerous monitors related to catalyst efficiency, DEF delivery, EGR system performance, boost pressure response, NOx sensor performance, fuel system injection quantity and pressure control and cold start exhaust temperature and EGR flow.
source_title: news: CARB reaches $197 million settlement with Volvo Group over emission violations

facts: Volvo Cars is restructuring its U.S. and Canadian operations.
The restructuring is part of an $88 million global effort to tighten spending, drive efficiencies, and update its workforce for an all-electric future.
source_title: Volvo to cut North American jobs to prepare for EV transition - Automotive News

facts: Volvo Trucks North America will showcase its newest truck lineup, expanded connected services, and award‑winning safety technologies at the Technology & Maintenance Council’s Annual Meeting & Transportation Technology Exhibition, taking place March 16–19 at the Music City Center in Nashville.

Volvo Trucks will host a press conference on Monday, March 16, 6:00–6:30 p.m. CT in booth #1301, where the company will preview new connected services designed to further improve fleet uptime and reveal early details of a new vocational truck engineered in North America to expand its presence in construction, waste and refuse, concrete, logging, agriculture, heavy-haul highway, and municipal.
Peter Voorhoeve serves as president of Volvo Trucks North America.
The new Volvo VNR 300 began production last month at Volvo’s New River Valley plant in Dublin, Virginia.

The new VNR is designed for high-density regional and urban operations, delivering enhanced maneuverability, advanced connectivity, and improved fuel efficiency to support demanding fleet applications.

The Volvo VNL 860 is Volvo’s flagship long-haul model that redefined the North American trucking standard when introduced in 2024 with a ground-up redesign that advanced aerodynamics, integrated new safety technologies, and elevated driver comfort to deliver measurable gains in fuel efficiency and uptime.
Both the VNL and VNR are built at Volvo’s New River Valley plant in Dublin, Virginia.

By engineering and manufacturing its trucks in the United States, Volvo Trucks continues to invest in domestic production while advancing vehicle and connectivity technologies tailored to North American fleets.
Load Finder aggregates data from more than 40 load boards into a single platform to reduce empty miles, improve route optimization, and increase revenue opportunities.

The Volvo Connect kiosk features live demonstrations of self-serve remote programming capabilities and proactive software updates that give fleets greater operational control and streamlined maintenance management.

Volvo Trucks’ integrated side curtain airbag will be recognized as a Heavy Duty Trucking 2026 Top 20 Product, an award honoring the most innovative new or significantly improved products introduced to the trucking industry in 2025.
The integrated side curtain airbag technology is now standard on every new Volvo VNL and Volvo VNR built for the North American market.
Volvo Trucks North America is headquartered in Greensboro, North Carolina.
Its Uptime Services commitment is delivered by a network of nearly 400 authorized dealers across North America and the 24/7 Volvo Trucks Uptime Center.
Every Volvo truck is assembled in the Volvo Trucks New River Valley manufacturing facility in Dublin, Virginia.

The Dublin, Virginia plant meets the internationally recognized ISO 9001 standard for quality, 14001 standard for environmental care and holds a dual ISO 50001/Superior Energy Performance certification at the platinum level.
Volvo Trucks North America provides complete transport solutions for its customers, offering a full range of diesel, alternative-fuel and all-electric vehicles.
Volvo Trucks supplies complete transport solutions with customer support provided via a global network of dealers with 2,200 service points in about 130 countries.
Volvo trucks are assembled in 12 countries across the globe.
In 2025 approximately 120,000 Volvo trucks were delivered worldwide.
Volvo Trucks is part of the Volvo Group, one of the world’s leading manufacturers of trucks, buses, construction equipment and marine and industrial engines.
source_title: Volvo Trucks sets the stage for enhanced connectivity and truck innovations at TMC 2026

facts: A U.S. auto safety agency imposed a $130 million civil penalty on Volvo Group North America for failing to recall vehicles in a timely fashion.
The penalty is one of the largest ever for violations under the recall law.
Volvo Group North America agreed to a three-year consent order that includes oversight by an independent third-party auditor.
The company will meet regularly with NHTSA to ensure it addresses any potential safety issues.
Acting NHTSA Administrator Ann Carlson stated that NHTSA will use the full scope of its authorities to protect the public from safety defects and manufacturers failing to comply with the law.
Volvo Group North America is the parent company to Mack Trucks, Nova Bus, Prevost Car (US), VNA Holding, and Volvo Group Canada.
The company stated it appreciated the opportunity to summarily resolve the matter and noted that wide-ranging improvements in its North American safety processes and systems are already underway.

NHTSA opened an investigation in October 2018 into the company’s compliance with recall requirements, including recall timeliness, manufacturer communications, and early warning reporting requirements.
Volvo Group failed to submit certain quarterly recall reports, manufacturer communications, report some death and injury incidents, and submit certain field reports.
Volvo Group must pay $65 million within 60 days, with an additional $45 million due if it fails to comply with the agreement.
Volvo is spending $20 million to develop a safety data analytics infrastructure to enhance its ability to detect and investigate potential safety defects.
The company will develop written procedures and training for its employees on compliance.
NHTSA may extend the consent agreement for up to two additional years if required.
The third-party auditor must evaluate all of Volvo's recalls since July 2013 and recommend any changes warranted.
Volvo Group is a separate entity from Volvo Cars.
source_title: U.S. hits Volvo Group North America with $130 mln penalty over delayed recalls | World Auto Forum

facts: Volvo Autonomous Solutions and Aurora have achieved a milestone in their partnership to jointly develop on-highway autonomous trucks in the U.S.
A prototype of Volvo Trucks’ flagship long-haul VNL model, integrated with Aurora Driver technology, was revealed on September 28, 2021.
The prototype represents an important step toward launching fully autonomous Class 8 trucks commercially in North America.
Volvo Autonomous Solutions is developing comprehensive Transport as a Service (TaaS) solutions for autonomous commercial trucks tailored to specific customer needs.
On-highway autonomous truck applications are being designed and engineered in the U.S.
Future production is planned at Volvo Trucks’ New River Valley Assembly Operations in Dublin, Virginia.
The autonomous truck’s core systems build on proven safety technologies already on the Volvo VNL, including Volvo Dynamic Steering (VDS) and automated transmission (I-Shift).
These existing technologies create a redundant safety-based solution combined with Aurora Driver technology to ensure safe operations during development.
Peter Voorhoeve, president of Volvo Trucks North America, stated the Volvo VNL model will serve as the foundation for developing North American autonomous trucks.
Bringing autonomous on-highway trucks to market aims to complement the transport system, meet increasing goods-moving demand, and minimize environmental impact.

Sasko Cuklev, head of on-road solutions for Volvo Autonomous Solutions, noted autonomous solutions will be implemented where there is a purpose and makes sense, emphasizing leadership in building the new ecosystem with customers and partners.
Volvo Autonomous Solutions and Aurora are working toward implementing their hub-to-hub transport vision in North America.
The partners are identifying specific regions and routes to serve as initial hubs for on-road highway testing.

Volvo Autonomous Solutions is working closely with customers to understand current and future needs, priorities, and demands in preparation for piloting the autonomous Volvo VNL in on-road, hub-to-hub transport scenarios.
The goal includes eventual commercial adoption of the autonomous technology.

Volvo Autonomous Solutions aims to increase development speed to support changing customer needs across segments and markets, ultimately bringing safer, more efficient, and sustainable commercial transport of goods across the U.S.

facts: Wabash National Corporation (NYSE: WNC) announced a significant expansion of its network in the Southeast.
The company is opening a new Parts & Service center in the greater Atlanta region to strengthen customer access and service.
The new Atlanta-area Parts and Services center doors opened on September 18, 2025.
Strategically located at the heart of the Southeast’s trucking corridor, the Atlanta site provides faster access to parts, service, and truck body upfitting.
The facility will offer Wabash’s Ready-to-Mount (RTM) truck bodies for dry freight and platform applications with rapid turnaround times for in-stock chassis.
The Atlanta site joins existing Wabash service centers in California, Florida, Ohio, Pennsylvania, and Texas, with additional locations planned.
Fleetco, a long-standing Wabash dealer, will expand its relationship to represent Wabash in the greater Atlanta market with trailer sales.
This dealer expansion broadens Wabash’s dealer footprint in the Southeast and gives customers access to new and used equipment through Fleetco.
Bill Tirrill, president at Fleetco Inc., noted over 14 years of experience working closely with Wabash and plans to expand the sales force across the region.
Wabash added three CS Truck and Trailer locations to its Preferred Partner Network in Georgia.
The Preferred Partner Network addition brings expert service, maintenance, and Wabash Genuine Parts to more customers through authorized third-party dealerships.
Dave Hill, vice president of Parts & Services at Wabash, stated the expansions reflect building an extensive partner ecosystem that puts parts, service, and support closer to customers.
Hill added that the Atlanta expansions mean faster turnaround, more coverage, and better reliability for fleets operating across Georgia, Alabama, South Carolina, and Tennessee.
Wabash combines physical and digital technologies to deliver end-to-end solutions optimizing supply chains across transportation, logistics, and infrastructure markets.
Headquartered in Lafayette, Indiana, Wabash designs, manufactures, and services dry and refrigerated trailers and truck bodies, platform trailers, tank trailers, and structural composites.
Customers gain access to a nationwide parts and service network, Trailers as a Service (TaaS)℠, and advanced tools through the Wabash Marketplace and Wabash Parts.

facts: Beckwood Press Company was awarded a contract by Wabash National Corporation to engineer and build a custom 3,500-ton hydraulic bulge forming press.
The press is scheduled for delivery in October 2019.
The machine will be used to form stainless steel and aluminum tank heads with diameters up to 156-in. for Wabash National’s tank and silo business.
The 3,500 ton, 285-in. x 216-in. press will weigh approximately 650,000 lbs when fully assembled.
The press will be installed in a 6-ft. deep pit.
The 160-in. by 160-in. bolsters will contain machined t-slots for use with the Quick Die Change system.
A fully adjustable 8-point square gib-guidance system will ensure bed-to-ram parallelism throughout the stroke.
The press will accommodate Wabash National’s existing tooling and be able to bulge form using either air (pneumatic) or water (hydro).
Beckwood will design a specialized closed-loop pneumatic control system for the bulge forming operation.
The control system allows Wabash National to form parts to specific dome heights while utilizing up to 750psi of air pressure.
An automated quick die change system with chain driven die clamps and a bed trolley will be included.
The trolley will be capable of transporting dies up to 30,000 lbs across a 30-ft track via a push-button controls station.
Polycarbonate guarding will be provided around the working area for added safety.
Hydraulically-actuated die safety blocks will secure the ram during die changeover.
A collision detection system will be installed on each end of the trolley.
Wabash National Lead Manufacturing Engineer Barb Yocum stated that Beckwood has been engaged and supportive, with detailed and careful work from quoting to design.
Beckwood president Jeffrey Debus stated the press is one of the largest and heaviest machines they have ever built.
Since moving into a new facility last year, Beckwood has seen an increase in requests for presses of this magnitude.
Extended ceiling heights and heavy crane capabilities allow Beckwood to assemble, program, and test these machines in-house, which greatly expedites delivery to customers.
Wabash National Corporation (NYSE: WNC) is a diversified industrial manufacturer and a leading producer of semi-trailers, truck bodies and liquid transportation systems.
The company was established in 1985 in Lafayette, Indiana.

Wabash National manufactures dry freight and refrigerated trailers, platform trailers, bulk tank trailers, dry and refrigerated truck bodies, structural composite panels, trailer aerodynamic solutions, and specialty food grade and pharmaceutical equipment.

Wabash National products are sold under the brand names: Wabash National®, Beall®, Benson®, Brenner® Tank, Bulk Tank International, DuraPlate®, Extract Technology®, Supreme®, Transcraft®, Walker Engineered Products, and Walker Transport.
source_title: 3,500-Ton Bulge Forming Press | Wabash National

facts: Trailer maker Wabash National Corp. announced that based on current demand levels, its need for additional hires continues to grow.
The company met its plans announced earlier this year to have increased hourly staffing by 190 associates by the end of the first quarter.
Wabash National far exceeded its quarterly hiring projection as order backlog has continued to grow.
The company expects that by the end of May, it will have added upward of 500 associates since the beginning of the year.

President & CEO Dick Giromini stated that the company is continuing to take a prudent route in filling near-term staffing needs with temporary-status associates because it is still in the early stages of an industry recovery.
VP & GM of the Transportation Products Group Brent Yeagy noted that the company will evaluate converting temporary associates to full-time status once the recovery is deemed sustainable.
Wabash National is based in Lafayette, IN.
The company manufactures dry freight vans, refrigerated vans, flatbed trailers, drop deck trailers, dump trailers, truck bodies, and intermodal equipment.
source_title: Wabash National reports hiring up | FleetOwner

facts: Management identified Q1 2026 as the cyclical low point, characterized by uneven order patterns and cautious customer capital decisions across the freight market.
Performance was primarily driven by lower-than-planned production volumes, which pressured operating efficiency and led to negative gross margins.
The company is executing a strategic pivot toward parts, services, and digital enablement to reduce cyclicality and build recurring revenue streams.
A 19% sequential increase in backlog to $837 million is cited as a historic high for a first quarter, signaling improving visibility into a broader market recovery.
Strategic capacity expansion at the Lafayette South plant provides the capability to produce 10,000 incremental trailers compared to previous upcycles without immediate large-scale hiring.
The truck body segment is experiencing a delayed downturn compared to dry vans, with recovery now expected to trail the trailer market by six to nine months.
Guidance for Q2 2026 assumes sequential revenue improvement to $380 million–$400 million, though adjusted EPS is expected to remain negative.
Management anticipates a constructive 2027 as spot rates, contract rates, and capacity contraction align to drive replacement demand and fleet expansion.
The company expects to achieve positive adjusted EBITDA for the full year 2026 as operational improvements and volume recovery progress through subsequent quarters.
Strategic targets include capturing over 25% of dry van market share during the first half of the upcoming growth cycle through digital differentiation and manufacturing efficiency.
New upfit site openings in Chicago, Atlanta, and Phoenix are projected to generate $10 million to $20 million in incremental revenue per site with gross margins approaching 20% at peak utilization.
Recognized $3 million in costs during Q1 related to the previously announced idling of facilities in Little Falls and Goshen to rationalize fixed costs.
Management expects meaningful relief and pricing stability in late 2026 and 2027 resulting from Section 232 tariffs and pending antidumping/countervailing duty rulings.
The company intends to address its existing ABL facility before September 2026 to maintain financial flexibility and liquidity ahead of the facility turning current.
Development of a repositioned refrigerated van product continues with low-level capital purchases for long lead-time items to support future cycle participation.
Management confirmed they have complete visibility on the backlog required to meet the Q2 revenue guidance range.
Recovery in truck bodies is heavily dependent on discretionary spending and the housing market, which drives demand for rental fleets and vocational units.
Management is specifically monitoring discretionary spending and the housing market for recovery, noting that current movement in manufacturing must continue and hold to support a 2027 rebound.
Current installed capacity and existing shifts are sufficient to meet initial demand increases without significant immediate hiring.
Additional hiring will be deferred until the latter half of 2027 when the company expects to add shifts to meet accelerating demand.
source_title: Wabash National Corporation Q1 2026 Earnings Call Summary

Fleetco, a long-standing Wabash dealer, will now represent Wabash in the greater Atlanta market with trailer sales.

Fleetco will improve the expertise and commitment expected from the Fleetco/Wabash team to serve clients and keep them on trucking.

facts: Wabash National plans to cut the cost of building new trailers with a new state-of-the-art production facility in Franklin, Ky., and changes to its plant in Lafayette, Ind.
These efforts are focused on optimizing existing production capabilities and do not involve the addition of new capacity.
The initiatives are expected to streamline production processes and improve manufacturing cost efficiencies in Lafayette.
The initiatives will establish a strategically located, cost-leading, state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Kentucky.
The initiatives will gain access to an expanded labor pool to meet future workforce growth needs.
The initiatives will provide manufacturing capabilities in closer proximity to new customers.
The new facility and changes to the existing plant will drive margin expansion by reducing the overall average production cost per trailer.
The initiatives will provide future opportunities to expand capacity at a lower cost point.
The initiatives will open manufacturing space in Lafayette for future growth initiatives.
Wabash National President and CEO Dick Giromini stated that while the trailer market faces current headwinds, the company is taking steps to improve its long-term cost position.
Giromini noted that even at cyclically low production levels, these initiatives are expected to favorably impact margins.

Giromini added that when demand for trailers improves over the next 12-24 months as industry experts expect, Wabash will be positioned to take full advantage of the benefits resulting from these initiatives.
Current plans are to begin construction of the $25 million Kentucky facility in late 2008 or early 2009, dependent on market conditions.
The 300,000-sq ft facility will produce both DuraPlate and FreightPro dry van trailers.
Wabash named Joseph M. Zachman to senior vice president and chief operating officer of its executive management team.

Zachman has over 24 years of manufacturing experience including serving as Vice President and General Manager for TTM Technologies, President of CDR Corporation, and Director of Operations of Sanmina Corporation.
Zachman began his career with Delco Electronics Corp.
source_title: Wabash to build new plant; appoints new COO - Truck News

facts: Wabash announced the launch of a new light-duty, home delivery refrigerated truck body with its patented molded structural composite (MSC) technology.
A large national grocery retailer placed an initial order over $10 million for delivery in the first half of 2022.
The new home delivery refrigerated truck body is designed to maximize both cargo capacity and delivery productivity on sub-10,000 GVWR chassis.
The purpose-built design facilitates a rack and tote system unique to the food distribution industry while creating easy access to separate temperature zones for fresh and frozen goods.
The design features walk-in side doors that provide cargo access for the driver.
Additional engineered solutions for the refrigerated home delivery market are currently in development.
Wabash anticipates an increasing need from customers to compete in a changing logistics and distribution environment fueled by ecommerce disruption.
Kevin Page serves as Wabash’s senior vice president – customer value creation.
Wabash developed a sustainable solution that helps grocery retailers meet home delivery goals while reducing their carbon footprints.
Wabash’s MSC technology improves thermal efficiency up to 30%, reduces weight, and provides a longer asset life.

The improvement in thermal efficiency compared to conventional refrigerated product designs allows operators to achieve better fuel efficiency as the refrigeration unit draws less energy to maintain temperature.
The lightweight properties of Wabash’s MSC technology can facilitate the adoption of an electric chassis in a refrigerated application.

MSC technology has undergone careful development, including extensive on-road testing to document the composite material’s ability to maintain temperature efficiently, reduce weight and lengthen asset life.
Wabash is moving quickly to scale production of this technology for the refrigerated transportation space.
Customers are realizing that sustainability initiatives help their bottom line.

Wabash designs, manufactures, and services a diverse range of products including dry freight and refrigerated trailers, flatbed trailers, tank trailers, dry and refrigerated truck bodies, structural composite panels and products, trailer aerodynamic solutions, and specialty food grade processing equipment.
Wabash is headquartered in Lafayette, Indiana.
source_title: Wabash Launches Next-Generation Grocery Delivery Vehicle, Secures Order with National Grocery Retailer

facts: Wabash National Corporation is a diversified industrial manufacturer and North America’s leading producer of semi-trailers and liquid transportation systems.
Wabash released details about its trailer that was part of Navistar’s SuperTruck combination vehicle.
The trailer includes numerous aerodynamic trailer devices, three of which were based on the company’s commercially available Ventix DRS® skirt, AeroFin® XL tail device and nose fairing.
Aerodynamic improvements reduced the trailer’s drag coefficient by more than 30 percent, according to Navistar.

The U.S. Department of Energy’s SuperTruck program is a five-year research and development initiative focused on improving freight efficiency (the amount of freight hauled per gallon of fuel used) by 50 percent compared to 2009 base model heavy-duty tractor-trailer vehicles.
Brian Bauman, vice president and general manager of Wabash Composites, stated that the trailer components were designed as concepts for next-generation aerodynamic devices from Wabash National.
Bauman noted that on the heels of the GHG2 rule being finalized, the industry will need to innovate to meet future fuel efficiency standards.
The SuperTruck initiative allowed Wabash to leverage fleet-proven aerodynamic technologies and years of aerodynamic research and development to innovate with customers in mind.
Wabash National reduced the weight of the trailer by more than 2,000 pounds compared to its 2009 dry van.
Components used to reduce weight, such as wide-base single tires and a variety of aluminum componentry, are commercially available.
Wabash National was part of Navistar’s SuperTruck team, one of four industry teams to participate in the SuperTruck program.
Navistar unveiled its SuperTruck on September 28, reporting a 104 percent improvement in freight efficiency.
The average tractor-trailer gets 6 miles per gallon; Navistar and Wabash National’s SuperTruck combination vehicle gets over 13 miles per gallon.
According to the DOE’s Office of Science, approximately 20 percent of the SuperTruck efficiency improvements come from advances in the truck’s internal combustion engines.
Further efficiency gains are attributed to improved aerodynamics, weight reduction, reduced rolling resistance with high-efficiency tires and specialty equipment that limits idle time.

Wabash designs, manufactures, and services a diverse range of products including dry freight and refrigerated trailers, flatbed trailers, tank trailers, dry and refrigerated truck bodies, structural composite panels and products, trailer aerodynamic solutions, and specialty food grade processing equipment.

canonical_name: National Truck Equipment Association's Work Truck Show
facts: The National Truck Equipment Association's Work Truck Show showcased vocational work truck packages and vehicles from major original equipment manufacturers.
Freightliner displayed its eM2 electric truck, which is set to go on sale in 2021.
The Class 6/7 Freightliner eM2 features a 325 kWh electric motor with an expected range of 230 miles and an 80 percent charge time of 60 minutes.
The eM2 is designed for last-mile logistics and pickup & delivery service applications.
Greg Treinen, advanced technology marketing manager for Daimler Trucks North America (DTNA), presented updates on the vehicle and DTNA's e-mobility platform.
Freightliner is developing the truck through a co-creation process working directly with customers to gather feedback.
The development process will lead to the Freightliner Truck Innovation Fleet, placing vehicles into customer hands for testing.
Some testing customers will form the Electric Vehicle Council to share experiences, funding and infrastructure concerns, and maintenance and safety tips.
The Electric Vehicle Council will serve as the basis for DTNA's e-consulting services designed for serial production customers.
Peterbilt announced its Model 348 will be available with the Bendix Wingman Fusion advanced driver assistance system.
The Bendix Wingman Fusion system integrates camera and radar technology to enable advanced collision mitigation features including object detection, lane departure warning, and overspeed alerts.
Peterbilt is adding SmartLINQ remote diagnostics to Models 348, 337, 330, and 325 when selecting a Paccar PX engine.
Customers selecting a natural gas engine will also have the option to add remote diagnostics.
Kim Lawton, Medium Duty Marketing Manager at Peterbilt Motors Company, stated that extending remote diagnostics to medium duty products gives customers real-time information to maximize uptime.
SmartLINQ provides real-time fleet health and generates notifications with severity, urgency, and recommended actions when a diagnostic code is generated.
A two-year subscription is included with the SmartLINQ option.
Isuzu introduced a new 12-foot Supreme service body for its NPR gas trucks as part of the Road-Ready Program.

The 12-foot Supreme Spartan Service body is available for 109-inch-wheelbase NPR and NPR-HD models and includes eight compartments with adjustable shelving, stand-up height interior, stainless-steel D-ring handles, LED dome lights, optional plexiglass sliders, E-track, curbside pipe door, translucent roof, and drop-down double ladder rack.
Isuzu announced three new Knapheide bodies for its N-Series trucks covering utility, dump, and landscaper applications.

The Knapheide KUVcc enclosed utility body is available in an 11-foot version for 109-inch wheelbase NPR and NPR-HD gasoline-powered trucks with eight exterior compartments, two fixed interior shelves, conduit/pipe doors, dome lights, glass window, Master locks, and Knapliner-coated step bumper.

The Knapheide 11-foot dump body is available on 109-inch wheelbase N-Series standard-cab trucks with GVWRs of 14,500 pounds and higher, featuring crossmemberless construction, fully-boxed long sills made of 7-gauge hot-rolled commercial-quality steel, an electric hoist powered by a 12-volt pump, and a versatile double acting tailgate.

The Knapheide Landscaper body is available on 109- and 132.5-inch wheelbase N-Series standard-cab models with GVWRs of 14,500 pounds and higher, as well as 150- and 176-inch wheelbase crew-cab models with 14,500-lb GVWRs and higher.
Ram Trucks announced updates to its chassis cab 3500, 4500, and 5500 models at the show.
With the updates, a Class 3 chassis cab now has a max gross vehicle weight rating of 14,000 pounds, Class 4 is up to 16,000 pounds, and Class 5 is up to 19,500 pounds.
Max payload on the updated Ram models is 12,510 pounds with a gross combined weight rating of 43,000 pounds, which is up 4,500 pounds from the 2018 model.
The updated Ram chassis cabs have a towing capacity of 35,220 pounds.
Ram introduced the Ram Chassis Cab Limited, described as a luxury version of its chassis cab models.
Dave Sowers, head of commercial truck marketing at Ram, stated it will be the most upfit friendly chassis cab with surprising luxury.
Ram also announced a Tradesman trim for its heavy duty models.
Wabash National and Supreme showcased new truck body technologies including Wabash's new National Cold Chain product.

Wabash's new technology uses molded structural composite material that delivers improved thermal efficiency, is lighter and stronger, and is two times more puncture resistant than truck bodies with aluminum side walls.
Supreme's Kold King multi-temp solution has removed wood, steel, and rivets from its construction.
Supreme showcased its Signature Van with DuraPlate Cell Core technology, which is up to 150 pounds lighter than traditional wall materials and up to five times more puncture resistant.
Supreme's Iner-City cutaway with a flat floor is now available on the Ford Transit and will be upfit with a Ranger Design shelving package.
Ranger Design announced its newest ladder rack, the Cargo +, designed for contractors using small commercial vans such as the Metris, Transit Connect, or Promaster City.
The Cargo + rack is made of aluminum with an anodized finish, is rust-free and lightweight, has a carrying capacity up to 300 pounds, and includes a rolling bar at the rear.
Nissan announced a new Rocky Ridge Titan model and Rocky Ridge upfit packages for several other models including the Frontier.
The new Rocky Ridge Titan model will come with a full 5-year, 100,000-mile warranty.
XL Hybrids is bringing a plug-in hybrid electric system (PHEV) to market for Ford F-250 Super Duty models.
The PHEV system offers a 50 percent improvement in miles per gallon and a 33 percent reduction in CO2 emissions.
The system features a 15 kWh lithium-ion battery pack that adds about 750 pounds in weight to the vehicle.
Eric Fullmer, director of marketing at XL Hybrids, stated the system is designed for commercial operators and municipalities setting their own emissions reductions goals.
The cost of the XL Hybrids PHEV system is likely to be around $25,000 when it launches later this year.
source_title: Wrapping Up The Work Truck Show With Some Of The New Products Displayed - Ford Motor (NYSE:F) - Benzinga

facts: Wheelabrator Group Inc is located in LaGrange, GA.
The company has over 100 years of experience establishing it as a reliable and innovative provider of sandblast equipment and related services.

Equipment is designed to work with metals, plastics, and composites, and is available in various sizes and configurations to suit specific customer needs.
Sandblast equipment is known for reliability, durability, and efficiency, making it an ideal choice for customers in aerospace, automotive, construction, energy, and many other industries.
Products are designed to help customers achieve production goals while maintaining high standards of quality and safety.
In addition to equipment, the company offers services to support customers throughout the entire surface preparation and finishing process.

A team of experts with extensive industry experience works closely with customers to develop customized solutions meeting specific needs and requirements.
The company offers aftermarket services including spare parts, upgrades, and retrofits to help customers maximize equipment lifespan and performance.
With a global network of sales and service locations, the company is committed to providing the highest level of support and service.
A team of trained professionals provides technical support, assistance, on-site training, and maintenance services to ensure customers maximize their equipment use.
In summary, Wheelabrator Group is a leading provider of sandblast equipment and related services, offering innovative and reliable solutions for surface preparation and finishing.
With a focus on quality, safety, and customer satisfaction, Wheelabrator is a trusted partner for customers in industries around the world.
