facts: WIKA USA teams from around the country convened in north Georgia for the 2024 national sales meeting.
Bill Edinger serves as vice-president of sales and marketing at WIKA USA.
Jeff Christian serves as senior sales director at WIKA USA.
About 80 WIKA sales and marketing employees gathered at Lanier Islands’ Legacy Lodge in Buford, Georgia, over four days in late January and early February.
The meeting agenda focused on getting back to the basics of supporting customers with exceptional service and products.
WIKA USA subsidiaries and sales reps had not convened together since 2022 because 2023’s gathering was devoted to training on a new management platform.
Todd Gardner serves as president of WIKA USA and executive vice-president of WIKA Group – Americas.
Todd Gardner discussed WIKA USA’s performance, vision, customer care, support, and plans for the next level of innovation.
Innovation initiatives include digital sales with a new product configurator and a focus on the industrial internet of things (IIoT).
Christian Payne serves as head of global product management – Gauges at WIKA Group.
Thomas Hasenoehrl serves as vice president of IIoT systems and solutions at WIKA Group.
Bill Edinger and Drew Firestone, vice-president of strategy and marketing, led sessions on go-to-market strategies for OEM, sales partners, and distribution.
The hydrogen economy and off-highway vehicles received extra attention as growth markets in the coming years.
Representatives from the WIKA USA branch in Houston, which produces diaphragm seals and electronic temperature measurement devices, attended the meeting.
Representatives from WIKA Sensor Technology, which focuses on force measurement in greater Columbus, Ohio, attended the meeting.
Representatives from WIKA Mobile Control, which manufactures safety devices of lifting machines in Chambersburg, Penn., attended the meeting.
Representatives from WIKA IIoT Solutions, which provides wireless sensing solutions in Boulder, Colo., attended the meeting.
Company leadership initiated a charity initiative to enhance team cohesion while supporting Rainbow House, an organization dedicated to safeguarding children in central Georgia.
WIKA USA contributed $2,500 toward Rainbow House through a creative fundraising endeavor and a notable donation from the executive team.
Jason Dean serves as sales development specialist at WIKA Mobile Control.
source_title: Recap of the 2024 WIKA USA National Sales Meeting - WIKA blog

facts: WIKA USA is a worldwide leader in industrial measurement technology.
The company was established when WIKA was founded in 1946, using core values as standards to measure itself and its employees.
WIKA USA operates its main campus in Lawrenceville, Georgia.
The company has run a monthly peer-nominated Core Value Award program for two decades to acknowledge employee dedication.
Award recipients receive a plaque, a monetary award, and a designated parking spot at the main WIKA USA campus.
The seven core values recognized by the awards are Integrity, Customer Satisfaction, Partnership, Safety, Continuous Improvement, Social Responsibility, and Excellence.
In March 2020, maintenance employees Peter Braun and Emmet Moore from the Pressure Gauge Division were recognized for Social Responsibility amid COVID-19 social distancing measures.
Inside sales employee Eden Hailemicael from the Pressure Gauge Division was recognized for Excellence after preventing a fraudulent purchase order that would have cost the company $35,000 in product.
source_title: WIKA USA’s Core Values Awards: March 2020 - WIKA blog

facts: Woodbridge is a global company with over 7,000 teammates focused on foam technology science and manufacturing next generation products for customers in multiple industries.
The company operates a facility in Lithonia, GA, US, 30058, with the nearest major market being Atlanta.
In 2024, Woodbridge was awarded Deloitte’s Best Managed designation for eight consecutive years.
The company maintains a corporate culture described as people first, innovation, lets get to it, and manufacturing focused, with a commitment to sustainability.
Woodbridge supports community giving through its Woodbridge Cares programs that serve communities around the world through volunteerism and philanthropy.
A Quality Manager job requisition (ID: 3319) was posted on Jul 5, 2026, at the Lithonia, GA location.
The Quality Manager role includes supervisory responsibilities over 5 subordinate employees.
Job qualifications require a Bachelor's degree from a four-year college or university, or one to two years of related experience and/or training.
Candidates are recommended to attain Certified Quality Manager (ASQ) and/or Certified Quality Auditor status.

The benefits package includes a competitive compensation and benefits plan, a defined contribution retirement savings plan with automatic company contributions to a top quartile plan, flexible working hours, an employee assistance program with free access to health and wellness coaches, and a continuous learning culture focused on upskilling and internal promotions.
Physical demands for the role include occasionally lifting and/or moving up to 10 pounds and requiring close, distance, color, and peripheral vision.
The work environment involves occasional exposure to moving mechanical parts with a usually moderate noise level.
source_title: Quality Manager Job Details | Woodbridge

facts: The Company provides material technologies for applications in a wide range of markets including automotive, commercial, recreational, packaging, healthcare and building products.
It is a global leader in the development and production of automotive polyurethane products.

For the automotive industry, the Company manufactures molded foam, expanded foam, chemicals, components, and engineered composites for applications including seating, structural support, occupant protection, headliner systems, exterior bumper assemblies, and NVH management.
The Company is privately owned.

Products include seatback panel assemblies, seat back bolsters, rear seat stacked interior components, flexible foam, climate-controlled seating foam, flexible foam lamination, low-emission flexible foam, and topper pads.

Products include molded foam, adaptive comfort and support foams, trimvisible foam products enabling visual transition between exposed seating components, textured surface foams, high-performance foams, and multi-zone foams.

Products include head restraints, head restraint assembly, energy management polyurethane foam, foam-in-place head restraint assembly, integral skin head restraint, foam-on-rod assemblies, high-resiliency foams, dual hardness foams, comfort safe head restraints, and head restraint mechanisms.

Products include an encapsulated StructureLite Module combining seating support with urethane seating pad in a single module, integral skin seat armrests, foam-in-place armrest assemblies, structurelite structural seat foam, and trimvisible BIO reduced GWP foam.

Interior products include cab back panel insulators, interior soft trim spacer foams, thin profile interior foam door armrests, thin profile interior foam console armrests, integral skin door armrests, integral skin console armrests, load floors, rear seat bolster foams, rear seat bolster assemblies, coverstock headliner foam, headliner composites, thin high-strength headliner composites, cargo management solutions, package trays, energy management foam, acoustical foam, and technical foams for filters or NVH applications.
Exterior products include structural exterior foam bumper assemblies, underhood insulators, underbody shields, tailgate liners, compressor shields, acoustical heat shields, and wheel liners.
The company began its business in 1978 as a privately owned Canadian supplier created by the purchase of the polyurethane foam division of Monsanto Corporation in Woodbridge, Ontario, Canada.
The company experienced growth primarily through acquisitions in the early 1980s.
In the late 1980s, the company expanded further by building new facilities strategically located near its customers.
In March 1999, Group and Poliuretanos S.W. de C.V. announced the expansion of their Joint Venture with the addition of a third manufacturing facility in Mexico.
The company opened a manufacturing facility in Sarnia, Ontario, Canada in April 1999.
In September 1999, the company signed contracts with the Belgian Group Recticel for a 51% stake in a manufacturing joint venture, while retaining a 49% stake.
In February 2000, the company acquired the Morval division of PFB Corporation of Calgary Alberta for CAD 17 million.
In March 2001, Woodbridge and its European partner Recticel together acquired 50% of Gestind.
In May 2001, the company signed a letter of intent to enter into a Joint Venture with Technicon Industries Inc. of North Carolina.
The company established a joint venture in China with Chinese company GSK in September 2005.

In May 2007, Johnson Controls joined with Proseat (a joint venture between Recticel and Woodbridge) to set up a new manufacturing joint venture for the production of molded foam seating cushions in Slovakia and Poland.
In November 2007, the Group and Inoac Corp. announced the establishment of four joint venture companies in North America.
In July 2008, Proseat signed an agreement for the takeover of all shares of Indepol, a Spanish moulded foam manufacturer.
In July 2011, the company and Wuxi Jixing Auto Parts Co., Ltd. agreed to form a joint venture named Wuxi Woodbridge Jixing Advanced Polymer Technology Co, Ltd. in China.
In August 2011, the company and Summa Group announced the expansion of their partnership with the establishment of a joint venture named DuraLite Formed Plastics in Leon, Mexico.
In January 2018, the company and Inoac set up a new equal joint venture named Woodbridge INOAC Technical Products.
In December 2018, the company announced it entered into an agreement to sell its stake in Proseat, its European joint venture, to partner Recticel S.A.
In November 2021, the company completed the acquisition of Hematite, a company specializing in NVH technology development.
In December 2023, the company formed a joint venture with TM Automotive Seating Systems called TMWB Foam Private Limited in India to produce seating systems for commercial vehicles.
In November 2024, the company formed a joint venture with Chengpeng called Woodbridge (Changzhou) Automotive Components Co., Ltd. in China for commercial vehicle and passenger seat foam.
source_title: Woodbridge Foam Corporation - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

facts: The Woodbridge AutoBond™ family of trim foams can be used in a variety of processes and applications.
The product line is designed to meet the most rigorous OEM durability specifications while still providing superior value to customers.
Woodbridge AutoBond flexible foam products are manufactured using a continuous-pour method.
AutoBond polyurethane flexible foams are available in flat and round blocks, and in polyether and polyester foam grades.
Applications for the foams include seating systems, interior systems, acoustical, and overhead systems.
Continuous pour flat-block is produced in widths up to 78” (1.98M).
The largest diameter round block in North America measures 50” (1.27M).
Polyether and polyester foam grades are available.
Products offer superior consistency for cell structure and visual appearance.
A wide range of firmness capability is available for superior craftsmanship.
Soft firmness options are designed for luxurious "soft" seat insert applications.
High firmness options are designed for challenging seat trim cover designs.
High density options are provided for tough bolster durability requirements.
Thin gauge capability is 0.065” or 1.65mm for low-cost designs.
Narrow width options are available.
Excellent acoustical solutions are offered.
Typical density ranges from 1.5-4.25 lb./ft3 or 27-68 kg/m3.
Firmness range is 15-175 (25% IFD lb./50 in2).
Thicknesses range from 1.65-25.5 mm (.065-1 inch).
Chemistry consists of polyether and polyester open-cell polyurethane.
Pour types include round block and flat block.
Fabrication methods include blocks, looping, peeling, sheeting, die cutting, reticulation, and roll compressing.
Lamination options include PE film, PU film, adhesive films and webs, textile scrims, and non-woven scrims.
Other key options include a variety of colors, FR/non-FR, acoustics, airflow, and low emission (VOCs and odor).
AutoBond flexible foams are designed to meet most OEM automotive interiors specifications and requirements.
Products meet Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standard FMVSS302.
Products meet General Motors specifications GMW15058, GM6291M, GM6083, and GM2101M.
Products meet Ford specifications WSS-99P48, WSS-M2D243, WSS-M2D491, and ESB-M4D113.
Products meet FCA specifications MSAY309 and CS-13398.
Products meet Toyota Motor Company specification TSM6712G.
Products meet Nissan specification NESM8047.
Products meet Tesla specification TM-4001.
Products meet Honda specification HES C209.
Products meet Hyundai Kia specification MS-300-34.
Products meet Daimler specifications DBL5450, DBL5454, and DBL5438.
Products meet Volkswagen specification VW50180.
The AutoBond Flexible Foam Family includes grades AB, AF, AFX, ABL, ABA, ABS, AL, GC, WLF, LF, and SF.
Grade AB is a standard Flat Block Ether, Flame-Bondable (FR), FMVSS302 compliant, and Pour-in-Place.
Grade AF is a High Flat Block Ester, Flame-Bondable (FR), and FMVSS302 compliant.
Grade AFX is the Highest Flat Block Ether, Flame-Bondable (FR), and FMVSS302 compliant.
Grade ABL is the Lowest Flat Block Ether.
Grade ABA is an Alternative Flat Block Ester.
Grade ABS is a Biocide Flat Block Ether, Flame-Bondable (FR), and FMVSS302 compliant.
Grade AL is a Flat Block Ether.
Grade GC is a Pour-in-Place Flat Block Ether, Flame-Bondable (FR), and FMVSS302 compliant.
Grade WLF is a Low Emission Flat Block Ether, Flame-Bondable (FR), and FMVSS302 compliant.
Grade LF is a Low Emission Flat Block Ester.
Grade SF is a Flat Block Ether and Flame-Bondable (FR).
source_title: Flexible Foam | Woodbridge

facts: Georgia offers a Job Tax Credit that rewards businesses for creating new jobs, with the potential to eliminate corporate income tax liability and reduce payroll withholding obligations.
The Job Tax Credit provides between $1,250 and $4,000 per year for five years for every new job created, depending on the business's county tier.

To qualify for the Job Tax Credit, businesses must be engaged in manufacturing, warehousing/distribution/logistics, software development, fintech, data centers, contact centers, telecommunications, or research and development facilities.

Qualified jobs under the Job Tax Credit must have a minimum 35-hour workweek, include health insurance benefits, and pay more than the average wage of the county with the lowest average wage in the state ($636 a week).
Businesses must meet and maintain the minimum number of new jobs created per year to qualify for the Job Tax Credit.
Georgia offers a Quality Jobs Tax Credit that incentivizes higher wages and robust benefits, starting at a minimum credit of $2,500 for jobs paying at least 110% of the county's average wage.
The Quality Jobs Tax Credit increases to $5,000 per new job for businesses creating jobs that pay at least 200% of the county's average wage.
Credits from the Quality Jobs Tax Credit can be applied to state corporate income tax and any excess to business payroll withholding.

Georgia's Investment Tax Credit rewards qualifying manufacturing and telecommunications businesses investing in growth projects, offering a credit of 1% to 8% of a $100,000 investment depending on county tier.
The Investment Tax Credit can be applied to 50% of tax liability, and excess credits for companies in designated rural Tier 1 and 2 counties can offset payroll withholding obligations.

Qualifying for the Investment Tax Credit requires operating a manufacturing or telecommunications business (or corporate facility/support office) in Georgia for at least three years and making a minimum $100,000 investment in a new or existing GA manufacturing/telecommunications company.

Eligible expenses for the Investment Tax Credit include land acquisition costs, building improvements, machinery and equipment, recycling and pollution control equipment, and defense plant manufacturing.

The Port Tax Credit Bonus is an add-on credit that offsets up to 50% of corporate income tax liability for businesses increasing imports or exports through Georgia deepwater ports by more than 10% from the previous year's base amount.
To qualify for the Port Tax Credit Bonus, companies must meet minimum Port Traffic requirements of 75 net tons, five containers, or 10 twenty-foot equivalent units.

Georgia's Mega Project Tax Credit rewards large companies with a $5,250 credit per job per year within a six-year period if they hire at least 1,800 net new full-time employees and invest a minimum of $450 million or have a payroll of $150 million.
Mega Project Tax Credit qualifications also require paying an average wage above specified minimums or demonstrating high growth potential.

The Georgia Child Care Tax Credit offers a credit equal to 100% of the cost if a business buys or builds a state-licensed child care facility for employees, or 75% of direct costs if sponsoring at a state-licensed facility.
Child Care Tax Credits can be applied to 50% of corporate income tax liability and are well above the federal credit which only offers 25%.

The Retraining Tax Credit offsets retraining costs for existing employees on new technology and equipment, providing 50% of direct costs (including materials and training wages) up to $500 per employee per approved system, with a maximum of $1,250 per employee per year.
Georgia's state Research and Development Tax Credit offers 10% of the cost of qualified R&D expenditures, applicable to 50% of net GA corporate income tax and excess payroll withholding.

Qualified R&D activities include new product development, developing/testing prototypes/dyes/molds, testing manufacturing assembly line or distribution processes, implementing plant equipment/ERP software, and improving legacy software or cloud storage.

The Life Sciences Manufacturing Tax Credit Bonus provides an additional $1,250 for creating jobs manufacturing pharmaceuticals, medicines, and medical equipment/supplies in Georgia, applicable to jobs created after July 1, 2021.

The PPE Tax Credit Bonus, available for tax years 2020 through 2024, offers $1,250 per new job creating personal protective equipment or hand sanitizer in Georgia, requiring qualification under the Job Tax Credit.
The Digital Entertainment/Film Tax Credit provides a 20% credit on qualified production expenditures in Georgia, with a minimum expenditure requirement of $500,000 within the state.

Companies can receive an additional 10% credit for using the Georgia logo on approved projects and linking to the Georgia Film website, subject to a gross income cap of less than $100 million for interactive entertainment companies.
Georgia caps the Digital Entertainment Tax Credit at $12.5 million per year on a first-come, first-served basis, with no single company receiving more than $1.5 million in credits.

The Premium Tax Credit offers insurance businesses between $750 and $3,500 for creating five to 25 net new jobs with at least a 35-hour workweek, reasonable health insurance, and wages above the lowest-paying county's average.
Georgia does not offer state tax credits for using solar energy or alternative energy, but businesses can apply for the federal Energy-Efficient Commercial Buildings Deduction.

Georgia provides sales and use tax exemptions for manufacturing companies on machinery/equipment, industrial repairs, raw materials/packaging, manufacturing energy, material handling equipment, pollution control equipment, clean room equipment, and inventory.
Data centers, high-tech companies, and distribution centers in Georgia can take sales and use tax exemptions on certain purchases, equipment, inventory, and primary material handling equipment.
Many cities and counties in Georgia offer local tax incentive programs that businesses can apply for by checking their respective county websites.
source_title: The Georgia Guide to State Tax Incentives | Gusto

facts: A South Korea-based auto parts maker will build its first U.S. manufacturing plant in Georgia.
Woory Industrial Co. will invest an estimated $18 million in the facility.
The facility will be located in Dublin, Georgia.
The project will create more than 130 jobs.
The company specializes in heating, ventilation, and air conditioning components for both electric and internal combustion vehicles.
Pioneering development by Woory has made the company a leader in EV heating technology.
Unlike internal combustion engines, EVs do not have a heating source, making cabin heaters necessary to maintain battery temperature.
The company is working to develop other new products for EVs as well as hydrogen-fueled vehicles.
Gov. Brian Kemp announced the project on Wednesday, June 7, 2023.
Gov. Brian Kemp stated that Woory Industrial is helping Georgia become the e-mobility capital of the nation.

Gov. Brian Kemp noted that the emerging industry continues to bring historic levels of new jobs and investment to communities across the state thanks to a highly skilled workforce and reliable infrastructure network.
Jungwoo Kim, Woory’s CEO, said the automobile industry is facing a revolution for the first time in 100 years.
Jungwoo Kim stated that the transition to electric vehicles is the future and the U.S. EV market is the key market among all automobile markets.

The Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Global Commerce team worked with the Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority, Georgia Power, and the Technical College System of Georgia’s Quick Start program to land the project.
Woory will be hiring for positions from all fields necessary for an automotive parts plant.
The new plant is expected to begin operations in November.
source_title: EV parts supplier to build plant in Georgia - The Augusta Press

facts: A South Korean company will open an $18 million plant in Georgia to build heaters for electric vehicles.
Woory Industrial Co., based in Yongin, South Korea, said Wednesday that it would open the plant in Dublin, Georgia, in November, hiring 130 people.
The company says it will make electric heaters and control units for electric and internal combustion automakers.
It’s Woory’s first U.S. manufacturing location.
The company has existing factories in South Korea, China, Thailand, Vietnam, India, Slovakia and Mexico.
Woory says it has been developing electric heaters over the last 10 years with hopes of becoming a leader in the market.
Heaters are needed in cold weather not only to keep passengers warm, but to keep electric batteries warm for better performance.
Woory is the ninth supplier to locate in the region after Hyundai said in 2022 that it would build a $5.5 billion plant to assemble electric vehicles and batteries in Ellabell, Georgia.
The site could grow to 8,100 employees and is slated to begin producing vehicles in 2025.
Suppliers have pledged to invest more than $2 billion and to hire nearly 4,700 people.
Woory could qualify for $2.6 million in state income tax credits, at $4,000 per job over five years, as long as workers make at least $31,300 a year.
The state will also pay to train workers.
The company could qualify for other incentives, including property tax breaks from Dublin and Laurens County.
source_title: South Korean firm to launch $18M electric vehicle heater plant in Georgia | Fox News

facts: Woory Industrial selected TouchNetix's aXiom AX54A chip for the heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) touch and force controls system in the Hyundai Genesis GV80.
The aXiom AX54A chip combines high-performance multi-touch sensing with safety-enhancing force sensing technology in one chip.
This integration helps avoid accidental user interaction by requiring additional intent from users to operate the HVAC controls.
The single-chip solution saves space on the Printed Circuit Board (PCB) and reduces overall costs for the customer.
Moon Soo Byeon, Director of Research Engineering at Woory Industrial, stated that TouchNetix’s dedicated engineering support was pivotal from design through to mass production.
Vegard Wollan, CEO at TouchNetix, noted that TouchNetix is thrilled to be selected by Woory Industrial and Hyundai for the project.
The aXiom AX54A chip supports 3D proximity and hover sensing for air gestures.
The chip offers multi-force sensing and low-latency haptic triggering.
It includes integrated support for passive and active Dial-on-Display.
The chip is compatible with curved shapes, thick lenses, and finger guiding overlays.
It is suitable for smart surfaces such as wood, plastic, foam, and leatherette.
The chip provides parallel multi-touch and multi-force capabilities for safety-enhancing systemic redundancy.
The aXiom AX54A chip is available and in stock.
The Genesis GV80 is currently available for sale.
source_title: Woory Industrial chooses aXiom for Hyundai Genesis GV80 HVAC solution | Electronic Specifier

facts: On Feb. 14, 2025, the company will exhibit high-pressure hydrogen containers at the H2 & FC EXPO – Int’l Hydrogen & Fuel Cell Expo.
Yachiyo Industry Co., Ltd., part of the Motherson Group, changed its name on Oct 1, 2024.
On Apr. 2, 2024, the company reached an Agreement on Letter of Intent for a Hydrogen High-Pressure Storage Tank with Choshu Industry Corporation of America, Inc.
Honda Motor Co., Ltd. conducted a tender offer for the company's shares, with results announced on Nov. 21, 2023.
The company published temporary reports and Q2 financial results for fiscal year 2024 in late 2023.
source_title: Motherson Yachiyo Automotive Systems Co., Ltd.

facts: Yachiyo Industry Co., Ltd. announced plans to set up a new plant in Georgia in the United States.
The new Georgia facility will take over the fuel tank business from Yachiyo Manufacturing of Alabama, LLC (YMA).
The Georgia plant will manufacture 400,000 plastic fuel tanks per year for Honda's facility in Alabama.
The Georgia plant will include a sunroof production line with an annual capacity of 200,000 units to meet growing North American demand.
Yachiyo announced it signed an agreement to sell the sheet metal components business of YMA to Unipres Corporation.
Unipres is a Nissan-affiliated structural components manufacturer seeking to expand business with Honda by strengthening its North American operations.
Yachiyo will expand its resin parts business, developing and marketing body panels and interior parts as a pillar of its midterm business plan starting in April 2014.
Automakers are increasingly using resin materials for outer panels to develop lighter and fuel-efficient vehicles.
Yachiyo will enter the hot stamped parts market by developing low-cost mass production technologies for high-tensile vehicle body frame parts with a tensile strength of 1,500MPa.
The company will use an electrical heating method to form steel for hot stamped parts to reduce production costs.
Yachiyo's decision to enter the hot stamped parts market was driven by Honda Motor Co., Ltd.'s introduction of these parts into mini vehicle production.
Yachiyo is developing plastic liners for high-pressure hydrogen tanks for fuel cell vehicles (FCVs) to replace aluminum liners and lower production costs.
The company plans to enter the FCV parts market with its plastic liner technology in anticipation of wider hydrogen tank adoption on vehicles.
Yachiyo developed a plastic fuel tank system with very low fuel-permeability that meets California's new Low-Emission Vehicle (LEV) Program regulations.
A new manufacturing method for the low-permeability fuel tanks, which installs valves and tubes before blow-molding, eliminates the need to create holes in the tank after molding.
The innovative production technique for the new fuel tanks resulted in a 12 percent reduction in hydrocarbon permeability.
Stricter LEV III emission guidelines are scheduled to be adopted in California in 2014.
Yachiyo will accelerate marketing efforts for its new fuel tank by highlighting its environmental advantages.
Planned capital investment for YMA (Alabama, USA) is 3,067 million yen from August 2013 to December 2014 to respond to new business and increase production capacity.

Planned capital investment for US Yachiyo Inc. (Ohio, USA) is 486 million yen from April 2013 to March 2015 to renew facilities, reduce energy consumption, rationalize operations, and respond to new business.
source_title: Yachiyo Industry Co., Ltd. Business Report FY ended 2014 - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

facts: Yachiyo Industry Co., Ltd. reported FY ended Mar. 31, 2018 revenue of 165,562 million JPY, a 1.6% decrease from FY 2017.
Operating profit for FY ended Mar. 31, 2018 was 9,369 million JPY, down 14.9% year-over-year.
Profit before tax for FY ended Mar. 31, 2018 was 9,223 million JPY.
Profit attributable to owners of the parent for FY ended Mar. 31, 2018 was 4,796 million JPY.
Revenue growth factors included increased product orders for auto parts outside Japan and favorable currency translation.

Operating profit changes were driven by higher revenue, optimized product selling prices, a single impairment loss, cost increases for new models in North America, and higher expenses for increasing production volumes.
The company announced it will transfer its vehicle assembly business to Honda Motor Co., Ltd. in April 2018 to consolidate managerial resources into parts businesses for sustainable growth.
A new headquarters will be established to supervise global production sites and strengthen automotive parts production, with current business management operations transferred there.
Yachiyo is developing lightweight, 70MPa high-pressure hydrogen tanks for zero-emission vehicles.
Yachiyo is developing 45kPa high-pressure, hermetically sealed resin fuel tanks for PHEVs.
The developed fuel tanks are compliant with LEV III Standards.
Commercial production of a next-generation panoramic sunroof with a 35% wider opening is planned for 2020.
Yachiyo is commercializing carbon fiber reinforced plastic products using pre-preg-compression-molding and resin-transfer-molding technology.
R&D activities include developing metal-honeycomb, high-precision fuel-cell carriers that comply with exhaust-gas regulations worldwide.
R&D activities cover plastic fuel tanks with new structural designs, new types of sunroofs, environmental technology for automotive parts, special-needs vehicles, and driver-assist systems.
Yachiyo of America Inc. is located in Ohio, U.S., established in July 2008, and handles designing and testing of sunroofs and fuel tanks manufactured in North America.
R&D expenditure for FY ended Mar. 31, 2018 was 4,305 million JPY.
Capital expenditure for FY ended Mar. 31, 2018 totaled 9,460 million JPY overall, with 2,402 million JPY allocated to the Americas.

Yachiyo Manufacturing of America, LLC is located in Georgia, USA, and has planned production facility investments of 314 million yen from Apr. 2018 to Feb. 2019 to renew facilities, reduce energy consumption, and streamline operations.
US Yachiyo Inc. in Ohio, USA has planned production facility investments of 2,501 million yen from Apr. 2018 to Mar. 2020 to renew facilities, reduce energy consumption, and streamline operations.

AY Manufacturing Ltd. in Ohio, USA has planned production facility investments of 383 million yen from Jan. 2018 to Mar. 2019 to renew facilities, reduce energy consumption, and streamline operations.
Sales forecast for FY ended Mar. 31, 2019 for automotive parts is 1,630 (in 100 million JPY), representing a 1.6% decrease from actuals.
Sunroof sales forecast for FY ended Mar. 31, 2019 is 540 (in 100 million JPY).
Fuel tank sales forecast for FY ended Mar. 31, 2019 is 397 (in 100 million JPY).
source_title: Yachiyo Industry Co., Ltd. Business Report FY ended Mar. 2018 - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

entity_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America
canonical_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America
facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America (YMMC) is immediately hiring over 300 new contract employees to assist in many assembly line functions.
The hiring aims to keep pace with growing demand for all Yamaha recreational products assembled at its Newnan facility.
YMMC is based in Coweta County, Georgia.
YMMC designs and assembles four key product lines including Golf Cars, All Terrain Vehicles (ATVs), Personal Watercraft, and Recreational Off-road Vehicles (ROVs/Side-by-Sides).
Yamaha's last hiring push was just over a month ago, adding over 100 employees to the team.
Bob Brown, Vice President of Finance and Operations Support, stated that demand for products has stayed incredibly strong because people are foregoing travel and choosing local recreation.
The company is gearing up for an incredible 2021, requiring more staff for a motivated, diverse, and talented team.
Richard Jones, Vice President of Operations, noted that Yamaha has produced almost 4 million power sports recreation vehicles at this location over 32 years.
The greatest hiring need is vehicle assembly.
Every Yamaha assembly worker starts through a contract partner at $13 an hour.
At 30 days, team members are eligible for full Yamaha hire, with pay jumping immediately to $15 and soon thereafter to $18 an hour.
Yamaha benefits include full healthcare, medical, dental, vision, profit sharing, 401k with company match, 10 paid vacation days, and 10 paid holidays per year.
Yamaha is a diverse and dynamic corporation with over 1500 employees.
Chris McGarrity, a Yamaha employee for eight years, stated he started as a material handler and moved to the front office as a new model planner, highlighting astronomical career opportunities.
Yamaha is offering over $1300 in bonuses for contract employees in their first six months.
Open positions include assemblers, material handlers, welders, certified forklift operators, and more.
YMMC was formed in 1986 and started manufacturing units in 1988.
YMMC manufactures Golf Cars, ATVs, Water Vehicles, and Recreational Off-Road Vehicles for global distribution.
YMMC celebrated 30 years of manufacturing at its Newnan location in June of 2018.
Yamaha products are marketed through Yamaha Motor Corporation, USA (YMUS), a recognized leader in the outdoor recreation industry.

YMUS product offerings include motorcycles, scooters, ATVs, side-by-side vehicles, snowmobiles, WaveRunner personal watercraft, boats, outboard motors, outdoor power equipment, power assist bicycles, golf cars, power assist wheelchair systems, surface mount technology (SMT), robotic machines, unmanned helicopters, accessories, apparel, and Yamalube products.
YMUS products are sold through a nationwide network of distributors and dealers in the United States.
YMUS has a corporate office in California, two corporate offices in Georgia, facilities in Wisconsin and Alabama, and factory operations in Tennessee and Georgia.

Additional U.S.-based subsidiaries include Yamaha Marine Systems Company (YMSC) with divisions Bennett Marine (Florida) and Kracor Systems (Wisconsin), Skeeter Boats (Texas) with division G3 Boats (Missouri), and Yamaha Precision Propeller (Indiana).
source_title: Yamaha hiring 300 more in Newnan – Winters Media

facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corp. plant in Newnan, Ga., is now home to nearly 100 percent of production for both side-by-side and ATV segments.
A second plant in Newnan, Ga., meant to house side-by-side production specifically, is fully operational.
The initial Newnan plant serves as the home for ATV production.
Yamaha Motor Corp., U.S.A. is moving its Cypress, Calif.-based ATV/side-by-side research and development and marketing personnel from California to the Yamaha corporate offices in Kennesaw, Ga.
The Atlanta-area Kennesaw facility also houses Yamaha’s WaterCraft and Marine divisions.
Engines for vehicles in both the side-by-side and ATV segments are still manufactured in Japan.
Yamaha plans to grow its side-by-side business almost tenfold over the next three years.

Mike Martinez, vice president of Recreation Vehicle Operations at Yamaha Motor Corporation, U.S.A., stated that dealers need to prepare for an expanding business for side-by-side vehicles that are two to three times larger than what they might currently sell.
Part of the staffing transfer to Georgia is working closer with the accessory group and the factory to support accessory growth.
Accessories are considered a huge part of the side-by-side puzzle, critical to Yamaha's future success and dealer profitability.
The Viking VI 3-seat side-by-side has an MSRP of $12,799 for the standard version and $13,799 for EPS models.
New accessories for the Viking VI include a molded sun top, soft sun top, second battery kit, camo polyester seat cover set, heated seat cover, chain saw mount, and bow case mount.
Mike Martinez revealed the growth plans during a Viking VI press event at Lost Pines Resort in Austin, Texas.
75 dealers who won a Viking sales contest attended the event to get an early look at the Viking VI.
Approximately 40 Yamaha staffers from Kennesaw, Cypress, and Japan attended the event alongside U.S. regional managers and regional marketing managers.
source_title: Yamaha’s SxS business expecting major growth | Powersports Business

facts: Yamaha Motor Co. will move its U.S. headquarters to Kennesaw after more than 50 years in California.

According to a press release from Gov. Brian Kemp’s office, Yamaha’s move comes after a meeting between the governor and Yamaha executives last October at the Southeast U.S./Japan (SEUS-Japan) annual gathering in Tokyo.
Yamaha Motor Co. is a manufacturer of ATVs, boat engines, personal watercraft, and other motorized products.
Yamaha Motor Co. already has a strong presence in Georgia, employing over 2,300 Georgians.
The relocation of Yamaha’s headquarters will begin this year and is expected to continue through 2028.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp stated: 'After many years of great partnership, we are honored and proud to welcome Yamaha’s American headquarters to the No. 1 state for business.'

Governor Kemp added: 'This is another loud and clear testament to what we offer job creators from around the world. To any other California-based companies looking for a better home, we’ll give you plenty of reasons to keep Georgia on your mind.'
The 1.3-million-square-foot Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America factory in Newnan opened in 1986.
The Newnan factory currently employs roughly 2,000 Georgians.
Yamaha relocated its marine division to Kennesaw in 1999.
The Kennesaw facility was expanded with a 75,000-square-foot Marine Innovation Center in 2023.
In 2019, the company moved its motorsports operations to the state to benefit from proximity to the existing facilities.
source_title: Yamaha moving U.S. headquarters to Kennesaw - NewsBreak

facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation (YMMC), based in Newnan, Georgia, broke ground on a new, advanced, and innovative “SMART” Warehouse.
The facility is the first-of-its-kind SMART Warehouse for Yamaha Motor Company worldwide.
The new warehouse will span 200,000 square feet.

The facility will use state-of-the-art automated storage and retrieval systems to reduce response time for fulfilling dealer orders for Yamaha WaveRunners, Golf Cars, ATVs, and Side-by-Sides by more than 50 percent.
SMART is an acronym for Sequenced Material and Reduced Transportation.
The medium parts automated storage system will retrieve and put away 240 containers per hour, compared to the current rate of 170 containers per hour.
The small parts automated system will handle 2400 containers per hour, compared to the current capability of 550 containers per day.
Automatic conveyors and tuggers will move parts through the SMART center to factories, enabling a 50 percent reduction in forklifts.

Mike Chrzanowski, President and CEO of Yamaha Motor Corporation, stated the warehouse will improve operational efficiency and material flow velocity to factory assembly lines to get product to dealers and customers faster.
Chrzanowski noted that demand for all outdoor recreational products produced at the factory continues to exceed supply.
The significant investment in the new facility underscores Yamaha's long-term commitment to YMMC employees, the City of Newnan, and Coweta County.
The warehouse will bring higher paid and more skilled jobs into Coweta County, including positions for robotics experts, software developers, and systems engineers.
The facility was designed by SSI SCHAEFER.
The SMART Warehouse will include an observation platform for groups such as local school students to learn about manufacturing and automated processes.
Yamaha expects to complete the SMART Warehouse in 2024.
source_title: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation Breaks Ground on Innovative SMART Warehouse in Newnan - Metro Atlanta CEO

facts: Yamaha Motor Co. announced it will relocate its U.S. headquarters to Kennesaw, Georgia.
The Japanese company already operates a marine sports campus in Georgia.
The relocation will occur in stages through 2028.
The move will vacate Yamaha’s longtime corporate home near Los Angeles.
After a half-century in California, the international motorsports company is moving its American home base to Georgia.
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp used the occasion to tout the Peach State’s business-friendly leadership and policies.
Kemp invited other companies to follow suit.
Kemp stated: “This is another loud and clear testament to what we offer job creators from around the world.”
Kemp added: “To any other California-based companies looking for a better home, we’ll give you plenty of reasons to keep Georgia on your mind.”
source_title: Yamaha Motor Co. to move U.S. HQ to Georgia

facts: Yamaha Motor Corporation, U.S.A. (YMUS) is hiring for a Digital Manufacturing Manager position in Kennesaw, GA.
The job posting was published on 2/10/2026 under requisition number 1792.
The position is full-time and reports to the Department Manager.
The role's mission is to modernize factory operations using data, digital tools, automation, and advanced technologies.
There is a strategic focus on future cloud-based ERP integration for the position.
The Digital Manufacturing Manager will partner with all seven manufacturing subsidiaries within the Marine Business Unit (MBU) of YMUS.
Responsibilities include leading the implementation of cost-effective, data-driven automation solutions to reduce time spent on data collection.
The role involves developing and deploying real-time production dashboards for Safety, Quality, Delivery, and Cost across all plants using low-cost, internally maintainable tools.
The position aims to avoid high-cost MES platforms when simpler solutions meet operational needs.

Responsibilities include digitizing and enhancing manual Manufacturing Operations Management Systems (MOMS) such as Leader Standard Work and Lean Daily Management with improved electronic alternatives.
The role focuses on maximizing the value of the existing Microsoft ecosystem, including Power Apps, Power BI, Power Automate, Dataverse, Forms, and Copilot/AI across all subsidiaries.
The position requires training and mentoring engineering teams to independently build fast, low-cost digital solutions like Power BI applications for Yard Management.
Responsibilities include leading QAD and SAP ERP system upgrades and improvements with the Information Systems Division (ISD) and external consultants.
The role involves expanding eMaint (CMMS) deployment for deeper and consistent implementation across the organization.
The position requires helping prepare a comprehensive ERP Upgrade ROI analysis by investigating options, building the business case, and delivering an investment proposal for future funding cycles.
Responsibilities include supporting and aligning with corporate ISD strategic initiatives such as APS, MES, ERP, and IoT.
The role involves revitalizing the North American Monozukuri (NAM), a Yamaha North American Continuous Improvement Consortium Automation Committee.
Responsibilities include managing IT projects like Building Automation Systems from concept to full integration while coordinating with local IT teams, developers, and outside resources.
The position requires identifying and propagating best practices across the Marine Business Unit.
Responsibilities include applying Lean/continuous improvement principles to technology deployment by prioritizing solutions that are affordable, maintainable, and scalable.
The role involves monitoring Industry 4.0 trends and Smart Factory technologies, recommending adoption only where an ROI is clear and implementation is practical.
Required qualifications include 5-7 years of experience in manufacturing IT, ERP systems, MOMS, MES, manufacturing digitalization, IoT solutions, and/or automation.
Candidates must have a Bachelor's degree in computer science, Information Technology, Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent professional experience.
Experience working in a manufacturing environment is required.
Strong knowledge of manufacturing/shop floor/MRP processes and package systems is needed.
Project management experience in a manufacturing setting with PMP or PPM certifications is required.
Proficiency with the Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps, Power BI, Power Automate, Dataverse) is required.
Working knowledge of ERP systems is required, with QAD experience strongly preferred and SAP or Business One also valued.
Working knowledge of both on-premises and cloud applications, platforms, and infrastructure is needed.
Strong oral and written communication and presentation skills are required.
Preferred qualifications include experience with CMMS systems like eMaint or equivalent.
Advanced Planning & Scheduling (APS) and Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES) experience is preferred.
Experience building dashboards and data visualization solutions is preferred.
Lean Six Sigma, Kaizen, or continuous improvement certification or demonstrated experience is preferred.
Database experience with SQL, Progress, or general database administration is preferred.
Familiarity with AI/LLM tools applied to manufacturing or business process automation, such as Microsoft Copilot or Azure AI, is preferred.
Experience with low-code/no-code workflow automation platforms like Power Automate or n8n is preferred.
Understanding of cloud data infrastructure concepts including data lakes, ETL pipelines, and API integrations is preferred.
Benefits include 401(k) and Profit Sharing.
The company offers Fertility Benefits and a 37.5-hour work-week.
Health benefits include Medical, Dental, Vision, Life and AD&D Insurance, Wellness Program, Short-Term Disability Coverage for hourly roles, and Long-Term Disability.
Additional benefits include Student Debt Repayment Benefits and the ability to borrow Yamaha products.

Yamaha Motor Corporation, USA is an equal opportunity employer that considers all applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran or disability status.
source_title: Digital Manufacturing Manager | Kennesaw, GA | Yamaha Motor Corporation, U.S.A.

facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Company (YMMC) celebrated International Women’s Day on Mar/08/2025 under the theme “Accelerate Action.”
The celebration emphasized taking swift and decisive steps to achieve gender equality and addressing systemic barriers and biases women face in personal and professional spheres.
Yamaha is recognizing female employees and championing STEM education for aspiring young women.
Kristin Webb serves as Manager of Communications and Corporate Social Responsibility at Yamaha.
Kristin Webb stated, “We believe in fostering the next generation of female leaders in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics, ensuring they have the tools and support to succeed.”
Yamaha partnered with the Coweta Community Foundation for the 3rd annual Coweta Grand Prix.
Yamaha hosts the Downhill Dreams Academy program where students collaborate with Yamaha engineers to design, build, and race their own soapbox cars.
The Coweta Grand Prix event took place on April 19, 2025.
The event featured young innovators, including scholarship participants from the Howard Warner Boys & Girls Club where 56% are girls.
The Downhill Dreams Academy program develops hands-on STEM skills while embracing teamwork, creativity, and perseverance.
Kristin Webb stated, “In addition to supporting future generations, we are celebrating the incredible women at Yamaha today.”
48% of hourly employees at Yamaha's Newnan facility are women.
Female employees' contributions drive innovation, create meaningful connections across the global workforce, and shape a company culture built on inclusion, empowerment, and excellence.
Yamaha presented gifts to all female employees as a token of appreciation for their dedication and hard work.
Recognizing and celebrating female achievements is considered essential for fostering an inclusive and supportive workplace at Yamaha.

Kristin Webb stated, “As we celebrate International Women’s Day, we reaffirm our commitment to creating opportunities for women to thrive—within Yamaha, in our communities, and in the industries we serve. We are proud to support a future where all women and girls can pursue their passions, break barriers, and lead with confidence.”
Yamaha is hiring for those interested in joining a company that values innovation, diversity, and excellence.
Kristin Webb concluded, “Together, we celebrate, empower, and inspire.”
YMMC employs more than 2,100 metro Atlanta residents to design and build recreational vehicles at its Newnan manufacturing facilities.
All of the world’s Yamaha golf cars and Side-by-Sides are manufactured at the plant in Coweta County, Georgia.
Most of Yamaha's WaveRunners and ATVs are manufactured at the plant in Coweta County, Georgia.
source_title: Celebrating Women's Day at Yamaha. | Yamaha

facts: Progressive American Flat Track, sanctioned by AMA Pro Racing, named Yamaha Motor Corp., USA an OEM Partner for the 2025 season.
Yamaha continues as title sponsor for the Yamaha Senoia Short Track event on Saturday, March 29 at Senoia Raceway in Senoia, Georgia.
This marks Yamaha’s third consecutive season holding the title sponsor role in Senoia.
It is the sixth time the manufacturer has sponsored Progressive AFT rounds in the greater Atlanta area since opening its Marietta, Georgia, offices in 2018.
In the 2024 Progressive AFT campaign, riders Dallas Daniels and JD Beach finished on the podium in all 16 races aboard Estenson Racing Yamaha MT-07 motorcycles.
The Daniels/Beach duo won five races with an average finishing position of better than second place (1.875).
Tom Drane led the AFT Singles competition with seven victories and 13 podiums in 16 attempts aboard the Estenson Racing Yamaha YZ450F.
Daniels and Drane will return on Yamahas in 2025 as leading contenders for their respective championships.
Yamaha will bring approximately 250 employees to the Senoia event, featuring a branded presence in the Fan Zone and dedicated parking area.

Bob Brown, President of Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America, stated that YMUS and YMMC are thrilled to sponsor the Senoia Short Track for the third consecutive year, emphasizing passion for team member activities and community involvement while showcasing innovation.
Yamaha Motor Corp., USA (YMUS) maintains two corporate offices in Georgia.
YMUS operates factory facilities in Tennessee and Georgia, along with additional facilities in Wisconsin and Alabama.

YMUS product portfolio includes motorcycles, scooters, ATVs, side-by-side vehicles, snowmobiles, outboard motors, WaveRunners, boats, outdoor power equipment, power assist bicycles, golf cars, wheelchair systems, SMT machines, unmanned helicopters, accessories, and apparel.
YMUS products are distributed nationwide through a network of distributors and dealers.
YMUS corporate headquarters is located in Cypress, California.
Other U.S.-based Yamaha companies include Skeeter Boats (Texas), G3 Boats (Missouri), Bennet Marine (Florida), Yamaha Precision Propeller (Indiana), and Kracor, Inc. (Wisconsin).

links.context: News, schedule details, and ticket information

source_title: Yamaha Continues as an OEM Partner of Progressive AFT and Title Sponsor of Senoia Short Track - American Flat Track

facts: Yamaha has shifted from being a brand known for pianos and motorcycles to becoming a pioneer in the manufacture of engines for electric vehicles.
The brand has been developing motors for e-bikes and working on zero-emission technologies for over 30 years.
Recent advances have led to the creation of a compact and powerful electric motor with capabilities ranging from 35 kW to 200 kW.
Yamaha formed an alliance with Caterham to develop an electric motor for a revolutionary sports car project.
The collaboration with Caterham also involves VT Holdings and the designer Italdesign.
The Yamaha motor used in Caterham's Project V incorporates IPMSM motor technology.
The motor can reach 272 HP.
The development includes an impressive thermal management system.
Yamaha continues to work on innovations such as outboard motors for boats.
Yamaha has long-range visions including the development of hydrogen engines.
Past collaborations with Toyota and other industrial giants reinforce its ability to lead this transformation.
Yamaha's electric motor is adaptable to a variety of vehicles, from e-bikes to cars.
Since 1887, Yamaha has been synonymous with quality and innovation in the production of musical instruments.
Yamaha's foray into the world of motorcycles began in 1955.
The continued investment in electric technology reflects a commitment to the environment and to the evolving market towards electric mobility.
source_title: Yamaha: The Transformation of Pianos and Motorcycles to Innovation in Electric Motors

facts: Yamaha Marine U.S. Business Unit opened a new Marine Innovation Center in Kennesaw, Georgia.

The new facility is the fourth major Yamaha facility in Georgia, joining the company's Marine headquarters in Kennesaw, the Motorsports and Intelligent Machinery Group headquarters in Marietta, and a manufacturing facility in Newnan.
Yamaha currently employs approximately 2,300 team members in Georgia.
The company plans to add more employees by partnering with local education institutions to hire engineers for the new Kennesaw facility.
Products under development at the center include DockPoint™, a feature of Helm Master® EX Full Maneuverability that automatically docks properly equipped boats.
The facility is also developing Siren Marine® products, which allow customers to monitor and control boat functionality from mobile devices.
New Yamaha WaterCraft vehicles and accessories are currently under development at the facility.
Career opportunities within the Marine Connected Division include business planning, user experience, IoT/Cloud Infrastructure, product planning, data analysis, sales, and marketing positions.

Positions within the U.S. Marine Development, Product Management and Marine Technical Marketing Division include control engineers, perception engineers, product development engineers, project managers, hydrodynamics engineers, and various software engineers.
The Yamaha Marine Innovation Center is currently accepting applications for employment.
The Yamaha U.S. Marine Business Unit markets and sells marine outboard motors ranging from 2.5 to 450 horsepower.
The unit also markets and sells fiberglass, jet-drive sport boats ranging from 19 to 27 feet, as well as personal watercraft.
The business unit includes manufacturing subsidiaries and divisions of Yamaha Marine Systems Co., Inc.
Kracor of Milwaukee handles rotational molding for the unit.
Bennett Marine of Deerfield Beach, Fla. manufactures trim tabs.
Siren Marine, Inc. of Newport, RI develops Connected Boat technology.
Yamaha Marine Precision Propellers of Indianapolis produces stainless steel propellers.
Yamaha Marine Group operates as a division of Yamaha Motor Corporation, U.S.A., based in Cypress, California.
Governor Kemp stated that Georgia has been the number one state in the country for business for nine consecutive years.
According to the Outdoor Industry Association, recreational businesses bring over $27 billion to Georgia's economy annually and employ around 238,000 people.

links.context: Careers page for the new facility

source_title: New Marine Innovation Center in Kennesaw - Yamaha Outboards

facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation (YMMC) and DHL Supply Chain are progressing with studies, permitting, and filings that could lead to the construction of a new, state-of-the-art distribution facility on YMMC’s Newnan, Georgia campus.
The proposed distribution facility would be 784,700 square feet in size.
The project represents an estimated investment of $60–90 million.
The facility would be solely dedicated to YMMC operations.
Currently, freight traffic flows between YMMC’s main plant and off-site distribution centers, contributing to congestion along Highway 34 and surrounding intersections.
Consolidating logistics operations on the YMMC campus could reduce truck traffic on local roads.
The project could ease congestion and improve safety with no added cost to taxpayers.
Bob Brown, President of YMMC, stated that the opportunity is about strengthening their supply chain while being a good neighbor.
Key potential benefits include a dedicated, world-class logistics hub to support Yamaha’s manufacturing operations.
The project will invest in roadway improvements such as new turn lanes and upgraded intersections to improve traffic flow and safety.
Sustainable features will include stormwater management systems, tree buffers, and pervious surfaces to reduce environmental impact.
Pending successful completion of studies and required approvals, construction could begin as early as 2026.
Operations at the new facility could potentially start in the first quarter of 2028.

Dave Rumler, President of Automotive Engineering and Manufacturing at DHL Supply Chain North America, stated that DHL is committed to delivering integrated, end-to-end solutions and is proud to collaborate with Yamaha on a project that enhances supply chain performance and supports infrastructure improvements for the Newnan community.

links.context: Information about Yamaha Motor Manufacturing and its role in the Georgia community

source_title: DHL Supply Chain to Build New Distribution Facility on Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Campus  | The Citizen

facts: Foreign-owned affiliates are heavy investors in Georgia and are increasingly relying on communication between shop floor devices and mainline computer systems to prevent problems, allocate resources, and react to customer preferences.
Alcon/Novartis manufactures contact lenses with layers thinner than a human hair.
Elesys produces sensors that help cars avoid collisions.
Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corp. of America manufactures golf cars, Waverunners, and four-wheelers.
The Sept. 22 Next Generation Manufacturing annual conference focused on foreign ownership and a commitment to efficient manufacturing through the use of technology.
A panel discussion at the conference was part of a broader program focused on getting Georgia manufacturers to share best practices on strategic innovations.
Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corp. of America operates a factory in Newnan, Georgia.
A decade ago, Yamaha's Newnan factory required technicians to periodically take laptops to production lines to download machine information; now operations happen in real time.
Yamaha Vice President Phil Trickey stated the company cut its downtime in half by getting ahead of problems through machines communicating with them.
Phil Trickey added that connected systems can help forecast supply-chain gaps and material shortages.
Alcon Inc., now part of Swiss drug maker Novartis AG, collects data on multiple points along its complex production line for an FDA-regulated medical device product.
Alcon's head of engineering, Ireland native Shay Foley, stated the 'Internet of Things' provides information but the challenge is making it actionable to add value.
Shay Foley noted that customer feedback channels exist in each country to keep the company updated on consumer complaints and preferences.
Alcon's massive plant is located in Johns Creek, Georgia, and can react quickly to data generated from new contact lens colors in Asia or patient discomfort sources.
Alcon took over the former CIBA Vision facility after it merged with Novartis.
Mike Price of Elesys, a subsidiary of Japan's Nidec, stated connectivity enables firms to centralize processes and trim costs across global operations.
Elesys supplies automotive sensors to manufacturers like Honda.
Mike Price noted that collecting data instantaneously from plants allows one core group to analyze it and send directions, avoiding duplication of effort at each plant.
Nearly 5 million units per year roll off Elesys's assembly lines in Suwanee, Georgia.
Suwanee is one of four factory locations in Elesys's international system.
Connectivity helps keep manufacturing in high-cost places like the U.S. versus low-cost locales by allowing companies to hold less inventory.
Yamaha foresees a shift from a 'push' business model selling through dealer networks to a 'pull' business where consumers demand customized colors and styles due to online sales.
Some companies see cybersecurity and difficulty finding an appropriate workforce as hindrances to introducing more connectivity to their plants.
Other companies see their parent companies' legacy systems holding them back from further connectivity adoption.
Workforce development was a key theme of the sixth annual Next Generation Manufacturing conference held on Sept. 22.
Gulfstream Aerospace, the state's largest manufacturer, highlighted apprenticeships with 60 high schoolers a year and a co-op program through which nearly all technicians are hired.
A tribute was held for Randy Jackson, the late chief administrative officer for Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia, who died in May.
Randy Jackson was esteemed for his long-term view of workforce challenges facing Troup County and led the company's investment in a college and career academy.
Randy Jackson prodded county officials to improve early childhood education to ensure Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia's access to a 'world-class' labor supply.
Former Troup county commission Chairman Ricky Wolfe stated in a video that Jackson was always happy with reports but never satisfied.
The Pendleton Group is the presenting sponsor of Global Atlanta's Economic Development Channel.
source_title: Foreign-Owned Factories Turn to Connectivity to Improve Efficiency - Global Atlanta

facts: Yamaha Motor's U.S. subsidiary plans to relocate its headquarters from Southern California to suburban Atlanta.
The relocation will begin at the end of this year and be completed by the end of 2028.
Yamaha Motor U.S. intends to sell all fixed assets owned by the company in Cypress, California, including land, offices and warehouses.
The Japanese company expects the move to help combat tariffs by improving efficiency and profitability in the U.S. market.
Yamaha has maintained a corporate headquarters in California for nearly 50 years; it acquired the land in 1978 and opened its offices one year later.
Twenty years later, Yamaha moved its marine business to Kennesaw, Georgia.
Its motorsports division relocated to the state in 2019.
It currently employs thousands of people in Georgia, including a 1.3-million-square-foot plant in Newnan with about 2,000 employees.
All that remains in Cypress are corporate functions and financial services sitting on a more than 25-acre site.
The company plans to use a sale-and-leaseback arrangement for a certain period in order to ensure business continuity and to facilitate a smooth relocation to Kennesaw.
Details, including the sale price, purchaser, and timing of the transaction, are currently under review.

Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp said Tuesday that Yamaha's decision to move came after a meeting between the governor and Yamaha executives in October of last year at the Southeast U.S./Japan annual gathering in Tokyo.
Kemp also took the opportunity to pitch other companies — particularly those in California — on relocating to the Peach State.
Yamaha is the second-largest motorcycle manufacturer in the world.
Yamaha is the fourth-largest motorcycle manufacturer by sales in the U.S.
source_title: Yamaha Ditches California for Georgia

facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation (YMMC) broke ground on a new SMART Warehouse in Newnan, Georgia, on June 12, 2022.
The facility is the first-of-its-kind SMART Warehouse for Yamaha Motor Company worldwide.
The warehouse spans 200,000 square feet and utilizes state-of-the-art automated storage and retrieval systems.
The new facility will fulfill dealer orders for Yamaha WaveRunners, Golf Cars, ATVs, and Side-by-Sides.
The warehouse is expected to reduce response time for fulfilling dealer orders by more than 50 percent.
SMART is an acronym for Sequenced Material and Reduced Transportation.
The medium parts automated storage system will retrieve and put away 240 containers per hour, compared to the current rate of 170 containers per hour.
The small parts automated system will handle 2400 containers per hour, compared to the current capability of 550 containers per day.
New automated systems will enable Yamaha to reduce forklift usage by 50 percent through automatic conveyors and tuggers moving parts to factories.
Mike Chrzanowski, President and CEO of Yamaha Motor Corporation, stated the warehouse will improve operational efficiency and material flow velocity to factory assembly lines.
Chrzanowski noted that demand for all outdoor recreational products produced at the Newnan factory continues to exceed supply.
The significant investment underscores Yamaha's long-term commitment to YMMC employees, the City of Newnan, and Coweta County.
The facility will bring higher paid and more skilled jobs into Coweta County, including roles for robotics experts, software developers, and systems engineers.
The warehouse was designed by SSI SCHAEFER.
The facility will include an observation platform for local school students to learn about manufacturing and automated processes.
Yamaha expects to complete the SMART Warehouse in 2024.
source_title: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation Breaks Ground on Innovative SMART Warehouse in Newnan - Middle Georgia CEO

facts: Japan-based window and door manufacturer YKK AP America recommits to Macon and the United States by moving employees and operations into a new facility in the city.
The company is investing $112 million in the new state-of-the-art facility.
Future plans include hiring more local employees.
source_title: Best in Atlanta Real Estate Awards: YKK AP America's new Macon plant - Atlanta Business Chronicle

facts: YKK AP America Inc. announced an expansion investment of $125 million.
The expansion includes hiring 100 additional people.
Equipment installation is planned at a newly constructed plant in Macon.
YKK AP is a subsidiary of YKK AP Inc. in Tokyo.
YKK AP is a sister company of YKK, the Japanese zipper giant that first entered Georgia in the early 1970s, starting in Macon.
YKK AP America operates its largest plant in Dublin, Ga., which opened in 1991 and focuses on commercial fenestration systems for facades.
The Macon location has housed the residential division since 2007, primarily making vinyl windows and doors for builders and homeowners.
The new plant is scheduled to open by the first quarter of 2024.
The goal is to make the new plant a showpiece for future facilities across the U.S. and Canada.
The facility will feature vertical integration consolidating vinyl extrusion, glass, and window production in one location.
The current Macon workforce of about 250 people will move to the new plant upon completion.

YKK AP America Inc. President Oliver Stepe stated the investment builds toward a long-term vision to lead the residential window and door market and expand business in and beyond the Southeast region.
Across North America, YKK AP America employs 900 people in Georgia.
YKK AP America also employs another 300 people at a Canadian subsidiary in Ontario.
The new facility will be located at Macon-Bibb I-75 Industrial Park.
JE Dunn will manage construction of the new facility.
SSOE Group will serve as architects and engineers for the project.
source_title: Japan’s YKK AP Investing Another $125M in Georgia  - Global Atlanta

facts: YKK AP and JE Dunn have partnered to build a new manufacturing facility in Macon, Georgia.
The facility will span 400,000 square feet.
The facility is designated for the manufacturing of residential windows and doors.
The project is expected to create 100 new jobs for residents in Macon and Middle Georgia.
The expansion will increase YKK's total workforce in Macon to 350 employees.
Construction on the facility is currently underway.
Officials project the facility will be complete by October.
JE Dunn Construction Superintendent Josh Oesterreicher stated that working conditions will be humidity and heat controlled, making them highly favorable for workers.
Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority Board Chairman Robby Fountain noted the development is significant for retaining existing jobs and creating a few hundred more in Macon-Bibb.
source_title: New YKK AP manufacturing facility promises job growth in Middle Georgia - 41NBC News | WMGT-DT

facts: YKK AP America Inc. held a ribbon cutting ceremony last week, marking the official opening of its first greenfield residential manufacturing facility in Macon-Bibb County, Georgia.

The event was held in Macon with commissioner Pat Wilson of the Georgia Department of Economic Development, Mayor Lester Miller of Macon-Bibb County, chairperson Robby Fountain of the Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority, and other local leaders in attendance.

Macon-Bibb County Mayor Lester Miller stated that YKK AP’s expansion into a new, leading-edge facility is a testament to its strong connection with the community and marked the beginning of a new chapter offering more opportunities to enrich the economy, improve the lives of families, and strengthen the area's position as a hub of innovation and industry.
The facility completed construction in December 2023 and began operations in January 2024.
The facility currently employs more than 250 people in the Macon community.

YKK AP America Inc. president and CEO Oliver Stepe stated that the event marked more than a celebration of the new residential manufacturing facility, symbolizing growth and confidence in the business and employees moving forward, and aligned with the company's purpose to build a better society through architectural products.
Oliver Stepe expressed gratitude to the state of Georgia, the Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority, and all partners for helping celebrate the milestone.
source_title: YKK AP Holds Ribbon Cutting Ceremony for New Residential Manufacturing Facility

facts: YKK USA Inc is a privately owned company founded in 1934.
The company operates as a branch location in Macon, GA.
The company's industry is listed as Fasteners, Buttons, Needles, and Pins.
YKK USA Inc reports an annual sales range of $5,000,000,000 to $9,999,999,999.
The company employs between 25,000 and 50,000 people.
source_title: YKK USA INC Macon GA, 31217 - Company Profile

facts: The YKK zipper plant in Macon celebrated its grand opening on October 18, 1974, with a $15 million investment, though it actually began operating in the spring of that year.
The Macon fastener plant currently employs about 600 workers and is located on Ocmulgee East Industrial Park in Bibb County.
During its peak employment period of 2000-2001, YKK employed approximately 1,100 workers at the Macon facility, making it the largest zipper manufacturer in the world at that time.
The company originally required 11 acres for a small plant but was persuaded by Bibb County to purchase a site of more than 50 acres and commit to employing 500 to 700 workers.
Bibb County provided incentives including deeding land to the Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority for an industrial park and increasing water and sewerage capacity to 1 million gallons a day.
The Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority and the state of Georgia have invested in YKK through grants and tax credits to encourage its growth and stability.
Five million zipper sliders are manufactured in Macon every day, with 2.5 million exported globally and 2.5 million consumed in North and Central America.
The Macon facility is one of only two YKK brass wire plants in the world, alongside a plant in Japan, and ships to 12 different countries and 14 different factories.
YKK manufactures primarily zippers, as well as snaps, buttons, hook-and-loop fasteners, and plastic hardware for various industries including automotive, healthcare, military, and aerospace.
The company developed silent zippers for soldiers, fire-retardant zippers for firefighters, and air-tight/water-tight zippers used in applications ranging from outer space to the bottom of the ocean.
YKK manufactures its own machinery for zipper production and sells continuous chains to customers so they do not need to inventory different sizes.
The company adopted 3-D printing technology to produce slider tab samples in minutes for 50 cents, replacing a process that previously took weeks and cost hundreds of dollars.
YKK AP America began producing architectural products like aluminum doors and windows at a $45 million plant on 202 acres in Dublin, Georgia, which opened in 1992.
In 2007, YKK began production of residential architectural products on its Macon campus, utilizing space no longer needed by the zipper plant.
In July 2014, YKK AP announced plans to double its production capacity at the Macon plant by January 2015, employing about 100 people.
The Macon facility currently has 2.5 million square feet of manufacturing space, with approximately 25 percent expected to be empty after the YKK AP expansion is complete.

YKK operates more than 108 companies in over 570 locations across 71 countries, employing more than 40,700 workers worldwide under the leadership of founder Tadao Yoshida's son, Tadahiro "Tad" Yoshida.
The company's mission is based on the founder's philosophy of the "cycle of goodness," which dictates that no one prospers without rendering benefit to others.
YKK remains committed to Macon and Georgia despite industry downturns, with leadership publicly stating that Macon is their home and they will always be a friend of the community.

The company has been involved in numerous community activities including the Cherry Blossom Festival, Sister Cities program, education, civic activities, and the Greater Macon Chamber of Commerce, employing thousands of Georgians and paying millions in wages and taxes.

YKK's restructuring and downsizing were attributed to retailers buying less expensive garments globally, leading to the disappearance of many U.S. brand-name jean manufacturers that were previously its biggest customers.
To remain competitive against lower labor costs abroad, YKK relies on innovation, standardization of products worldwide, and developing new products in Macon for production in other countries.
Interstate 16 was being built from Savannah’s port to Macon at the time of the plant's selection, providing critical logistics infrastructure.
YKK El Salvador performs the same manufacturing functions as YKK Macon, making Macon a regional hub that serves much larger areas than originally intended.

Middle Georgia State College economics professor Greg George noted that economic activity in the country has become increasingly concentrated over the past 30 years with a slow but steady decline in manufacturing.
source_title: Four decades of zippers, and much more, from Macon’s YKK  | Macon Telegraph

facts: YKK Corporation expanded the jurisdiction of its North and Central America Group headquarters, located in Marietta, Georgia, to include YKK’s South America Group.
The newly formed regional group is called “YKK Americas”.
YKK Americas is led by Jim Reed, President of YKK Corporation of America and YKK Corporation Group Officer.
John Smith, President of YKK (U.S.A.) Inc., has expanded his duties to become the YKK Americas Fastening Business Leader.
The organizational change is part of a global reorganization at YKK Corporation aimed at expediting communication and time to market while ensuring proper governance.
The leader of each regional group reports directly to YKK’s global president in Tokyo.
Previously, the YKK North and Central America Group covered eleven operating companies in Canada, the United States, Mexico, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Nicaragua, and Colombia.
The new YKK Americas Group covers five additional companies in Brazil, Argentina, and Chile.
The expanded YKK Americas Group consists of a total of approximately 2,400 employees.

Jim Reed stated that the group is committed to solving complex fastening and attaching challenges for customers and will leverage regional engineering expertise, integrated production capabilities, and regulatory compliance to solve problems quickly and sustainably.
John Smith stated his focus is building a united Americas team to meet individual market needs, leveraging regional knowledge to support customer growth and adapt to evolving business needs.
source_title: YKK’s Georgia Regional Headquarters Expands to Include South America - YKK Americas

facts: YKK AP America Inc. started operations at its new manufacturing facility in Macon, Georgia.
The 400,000-square-foot purpose-built facility is located at 100 YKK AP Way in Macon’s industrial district.
The facility manufactures residential windows and doors for homebuilders and homeowners throughout the Southeast region.
The opening of the facility follows more than 18 months of design and planning.
The new facility doubles production capacity to enhance customer satisfaction amid a growing residential market.
The facility sets the stage for producing upcoming new and upgraded StyleView Classic series products on new manufacturing lines.
All employees have been relocated from the previous manufacturing facility to the new site.
Oliver Stepe serves as president and CEO of YKK AP America.
The facility integrates vinyl extrusion, fabrication, and glazing processes under one roof with a strategic floor layout.
The company plans to add automated goods-to-person technology to reduce the manual movement of heavy windows across the plant.
The facility is 100% climate controlled.

facts: ZF Gainesville, LLC is located in Gainesville, GA and is part of ZF's commercial vehicle division.
