The Manufacturing Engineering team consists of six Manufacturing Engineers and two Controls Engineers.
The open Manufacturing Engineer role focuses on machining operations and collaborating with Controls Engineers using Siemens controls and FANUC robots to ensure efficient and precise processes.
Responsibilities include developing innovative solutions for manufacturing production problems through identification and implementation of advanced manufacturing processes.
The role requires delivering projects on schedule by researching, specifying, purchasing, and implementing equipment and tooling to customer specifications.
Engineers must utilize engineering processes such as DOE, PFMEA, APQP, and Six Sigma for data-driven solutions to manufacturing problems and quality improvements.
The position involves providing day-to-day engineering services by troubleshooting equipment and processes to determine downtime causes, quality improvements, and cycle time reductions.
Candidates must hold a BS in Mechanical Engineering, Industrial Engineering, or a related engineering discipline.
Applicants need 2+ years of experience working in a production environment.
Required technical skills include CNC programming for lathes and machining centers.

Familiarity with engineering database maintenance and DNC systems is required.
A working knowledge of SAP is preferred.
Experience with Siemens controls and FANUC robots is preferred.

facts: The Greater Hall Chamber Economic Development Council reports 2025 year-end results of ten new and expanding developments which will add 691 new jobs and $186.5 million in new capital investment to Gainesville-Hall County.
Since January 1, 2020, a total of 76 new capital investments have been announced with more than 4,712 new jobs and $2.0 billion in new capital investment.
The fundamental economic drivers indicate that 2026 will feature better than average economic performance for Gainesville-Hall County when compared to other metro areas in Georgia and the nation.

Georgia has once again earned the #1 ranking for its business environment for an astonishing twelfth consecutive year, with Gainesville-Hall County being the Top Job producing Metropolitan Area in the State.
In 2025, the Milken Institute named the Gainesville-Hall County Metro Area as the 2025 Top Small Metro Area in Georgia and #1 in the Nation for Best Performing Small Metros.
Forbes magazine has rated Gainesville-Hall County as one of the “Best Small Places for Business & Careers” for the last ten years.
Business Facilities named Gainesville-Hall County the #1 Small Metro in the Nation for Best Business Climate.
Since 2015, the Chamber’s economic development program has assisted 181 new and expanding businesses, which have announced 10,005 jobs and $3.18 billion in new capital investment.
Many of the jobs and capital investment announced during 2025 are a direct result of the Chamber’s cornerstone effort to support existing business and industry.
Existing industry expansions announced during 2025 include expansions at King’s Hawaiian, Bend Bioscience, and Lawson Air Conditioning & Plumbing.
Investments in new automation technologies and localizing the supply chain continue to be a trend for existing industry investments.
Kubota Manufacturing of America marked a major milestone with the grand opening of its new $190 million Front End Loader facility in the Gateway Industrial Centre in Gainesville-Hall County.
This new 700,000 SF facility will employ an additional 500 workers in Gainesville-Hall County, bringing the company’s total employment in Northeast Georgia to more than 3,500.

New corporate facilities announced in 2025 included Koon Chun Sauce Factory (China), ESD Work America (Korea), R+L Carriers (US), Roquet North America (Spain), Therapak Inc. dba Resonant Clinical Solutions (US), SILENT-US, Inc. (Czech), and SOPO US, Inc. (Czech).
The new and expanded facility locations represent 862,609 square feet of new advanced manufacturing and US headquarters locations.
Construction of the new locations began in 2025 and will continue into 2026.

Expansions announced in 2024 that were under construction in 2025 include Caldwell Electrical Contractors, Geveko Markings, Ranger Manufacturing, VDL Industries, Mimaki USA, ETI Solid State Lighting, and Auto Metal Direct.
All of these projects have either recently started operations or are on a timeline to complete construction and begin operations in the year ahead.
In early 2024, the Georgia Ports Authority issued a notice to proceed to its General Contractor, Carroll Daniel Construction and Simpson Trucking & Grading, to construct the new Blue Ridge Connector.

The new inland port terminal, located north of Gainesville on Highway 365, will diversify the supply chain for container shipments between Northeast Georgia and global markets via a new rail connection to the Port of Savannah.
Local contractors and subcontractors played a leading role in the Blue Ridge Connector’s $127 million construction throughout 2025.
The Blue Ridge Connector adds about 20 direct jobs at the inland port and is estimated to indirectly create 63,000 jobs in the Northeast Georgia region, with 9,682 of those jobs in Hall County.

Surrounding road and infrastructure improvements, including the realignments of White Sulphur Road and Cagle Road, have been completed, with other road improvement projects in the pipeline to alleviate traffic and increase road safety.
The Georgia Ports Authority confirmed that the construction of the Blue Ridge Connector is on schedule and will begin operations in Spring 2026.

International investments were heavily featured in Gainesville-Hall County’s recent expansions, including Koon Chun Sauce Factory (China), ESD Work America (Korea), Roquet North America (Spain), SILENT-US, Inc. (Czech), and SOPO US, Inc. (Czech).
Hall County is home to more than sixty-five internationally owned advanced manufacturing locations, many of which serve as a US or North American headquarters for the business.
Japan and Germany are the top investor nations for employment and number of facilities in the county.

Georgia’s largest Japanese owned manufacturing headquarters is Kubota Manufacturing of America with 3,500 employed in the company’s manufacturing, research and development roles across six Hall County locations.

Major commercial developments that opened in 2025 include the new five-story office Bradford Street building developed by Capstone Property Group, LLC, Interactive Neighborhood for Kids development in Oakwood, The Crest at Oakwood, Prose Gainesville Apartments, Livano Limestone Springs Apartments, and additional downtown developments.
Branch Properties announced a plan to redevelop Gainesville’s 49-acre Lakeshore Mall site into a dynamic mixed-use destination, expected to break ground in late 2026.

The region will begin the new year with approximately 2 million square feet of new industrial speculative building developments in Gainesville, Oakwood, Flowery Branch and Buford available for occupancy in 2025.

The region’s largest industrial developers are actively building and leasing new space in Hall County, including Pattillo Industrial Real Estate, Logistics Property Company, Crow Holdings, Adams Properties, Hughes Commercial Real Estate, and Presco Properties.
New industrial investments underway in Hall County will total more than $200 million in real property improvements.

Tim McDonald, President of Lanier Technical College and 2025-26 Chair of the Chamber, stated that the Gainesville-Hall County Metro Area’s success reflects a commitment from business and elected leaders to work together on infrastructure, planning and investment to support business growth and economic development.
Infrastructure development in 2025 focused on Highway 365, the Athens Highway corridor and the new 1,300-acre Gainesville 85 Business Park.
Phase III of the road in the business park opened, creating a new intersection of James Simpson Blvd and Athens Hwy/US 129 for easier ingress and egress.

Planned infrastructure investments in 2025 include major investments in roads, water, sewer, gas, electric distribution, expanding broadband access and investments in Hall County and Gainesville City Schools.
The Georgia Department of Labor reports the Gainesville-Hall Metropolitan Area is finishing the year with an unemployment rate at 2.7%, compared to the GA rate of 3.4% and the U.S. rate at 4.3%.
The Gainesville-Hall County labor force sits at 109,367 as of September 2025.
More than 130 employers have job openings posted on the Greater Hall Chamber’s website seeking full-time and part-time employees.

Tim Evans, President & CEO of the Greater Hall Chamber of Commerce, stated that Gainesville-Hall County enters 2026 with real momentum supported by world-class advanced manufacturing, healthcare, education, and a deep bench of small businesses driving job creation, investment, and long-term economic stability.
The Greater Hall Chamber hosted two job fairs in 2025 and will host a Spring Job Fair & Career Expo at the Gainesville Civic Center on April 2, 2026.
Both the 2025 Spring and Fall Job Fairs each had nearly 1,000 job seekers in attendance.

With growing demand for talent and low unemployment, the Greater Hall Chamber, business leaders and educators remain focused on Work Based Learning and Apprenticeships for high-skilled career paths in demand by area businesses.
During 2025, Lanier Technical College reached a record enrollment of more than 7,366 students and maintains a 100% job placement rate for its graduates.

The Chamber’s Workforce Development Council is a partnership with Lanier Technical College, Hall County Schools, Gainesville City Schools, Brenau University, the University of North Georgia, and private businesses to ensure businesses can acquire talent and skills for sustainable growth and replace retiring workers.
Approximately 480 regional healthcare providers located in Gainesville-Hall County employ 16,120 people.
A recent economic impact study confirmed the Northeast Georgia Health System has an annual economic impact of $7.8 billion on the region.
Northeast Georgia Health System completed a major $600 million campus expansion for the Northeast Georgia Medical Center Gainesville Green Tower.
An expansion to the NGMC Braselton campus, estimated at $565 million, is expected to be fully complete in 2026.
More than 1,500 providers are part of the medical staff at NGMC, and Northeast Georgia Health System currently employs more than 12,000 people throughout the region.

NGMC’s Graduate Medical Education Program has earned accreditation in Family Medicine, General Surgery, Internal Medicine, OB/GYN, Emergency Medicine, Psychiatry, Cardiovascular Disease and Hospice and Palliative Medicine.
Brenau University recently added a Master of Science in Physician Assistant Studies.
Some 99% percent of the 5,957 business establishments in Hall County are small businesses using the U.S. Small Business Administration’s definition of fewer than five hundred employees.
Approximately 94% of all businesses employ fewer than 50 people.
In November, voters passed the SPLOST IX 1% sales tax renewal of the current 7% total sales tax rate.
Since 1985, Hall County has had an active SPLOST program that has funded major infrastructure, roads, public safety facilities and vehicles, parks, trails, community centers, and libraries.
The SPLOST IX program is estimated to generate approximately $440 million over the next 6 years to fund projects including park enhancements, trail extensions, and road resurfacing.
Notable upcoming Chamber events include the 2026 Economic and Political Forecast on January 27th at Lanier Technical College, featuring a panel discussion with local business and political leaders.

facts: Gainesville-Hall County, Georgia maintains a stable business tax environment and offers attractive incentives to new and existing businesses to encourage the creation of new jobs and investment.

Businesses located in a Georgia Opportunity Zone may qualify for the state’s maximum job tax credit of $3,500 per job when creating two or more jobs; credits may be applied against state corporate income or withholding tax.
The West Gainesville/Atlanta Highway area in the Gainesville-Hall County MSA is designated as a Georgia Opportunity Zone.
Gainesville-Hall County has three census tracts designated as Federal Opportunity Zones.

The Federal Opportunity Zone distinction was created by the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act to spur economic growth in low-income areas by allowing investors to temporarily defer and, ultimately, exempt federal capital gains on investments made in the Federal Opportunity Zone.

Qualified businesses in Gainesville-Hall County may qualify for the Job Tax Credit Program if they create at least 25 new jobs in a calendar year; Job Tax Credits are $1,250 each year for five years for each new full time job created and maintained.
Creating 25 new jobs over five years yields $156,250 in tax credits under the Job Tax Credit Program example calculation.
A Port Job Tax Credit Bonus of $1,250 per job is available for businesses that already qualify for the job tax credits and have a 10% increase in Georgia Port traffic over the previous year.
Businesses qualify for Quality Job Tax Credits if they create a minimum of 50 new jobs that pay at least 110% of the county average (est. $61,032 annually).
Quality Job Tax Credits are awarded per job, per year, for up to 5 years based on an increasing scale system.

Direct investment for employee retraining for quality and productivity enhancements may qualify for credits of 50% of the employer’s direct cost of up to $1,250 per employee under Retraining Tax Credits & WIOA Incumbent Worker programs.
Additional tax credits are available for equipment, capital investments, retraining employees, childcare, and R&D.

Job, Investment, and Retraining Credits earned in Hall County may be used to offset up to 50% of a company’s Georgia corporate income tax liability in any taxable year following the year in which the credits are earned.
Credits not used may be carried forward and applied for up to 10 years.
Georgia’s Corporate Income Tax rate is 4.99%.

The “Single Factor Gross Receipts” apportionment formula results in a substantial and permanent reduction of state income taxes for companies that manufacture products within the state of Georgia and sell those products or services to customers in other states.
This formula is designed to encourage manufacturing, distribution and service companies to invest or expand in Georgia.
Gainesville-Hall County and State of Georgia Sales Tax total 7%.

Georgia Law provides a 100% sales and use tax exemption for qualified purchases including Manufacturing Machinery & Equipment Purchase & Repair, Raw Materials & Packaging, Energy Used in Manufacturing, Primary Material Handling Equipment, Pollution Control Equipment, and Consumables.
Effective property tax rate for the City of Gainesville is 1.137% of Fair Market Value (FMV).
Effective property tax rate for Hall County (Unincorporated Areas) is 0.952% of FMV.
Effective property tax rate for the City of Oakwood is 1.131% of FMV.
Effective property tax rate for the City of Flowery Branch is 1.095% of FMV.
Effective property tax rate for the City of Buford is 0.825% of FMV.
Effective property tax rate for the City of Lula is 0.964% of FMV.
Effective property tax rate for the Town of Braselton is 1.164% of FMV.
Depreciation percentage for personal property in GROUP 1 during the first year of business is 84%, with a typical economic life of 5-7 years.
Depreciation percentage for personal property in GROUP 2 during the first year of business is 91%, with a typical economic life of 8-12 years.
Depreciation percentage for personal property in GROUP 3 during the first year of business is 95%, with a typical economic life of 13 years or more.
Depreciation percentage for personal property in GROUP 4 during the first year of business is 76%, with a typical economic life of 1-4 years.
Companies operating 85% of total available time over a 12-month period may be eligible for accelerated depreciation on equipment under the Multi-Shift program.

A 100% Freeport Tax Exemption (ad valorem) with annual filing is available for raw materials and goods in process of manufacture, finished goods held by the manufacturer, and finished goods destined for shipment out-of-state.

Tax exempt bond financing is available for companies seeking to finance the purchase of land, buildings and equipment for eligible manufacturing projects through Industrial Revenue Bond (IRB) Financing.
Rates for tax-exempt bonds are market-based and established on the credit and strength of the individual company and projects.
Tax-exempt IRB financing can be used for projects with capital investment between $1 million and $20 million.
Tim Evans is the designated contact person for questions regarding Georgia business tax specifics.

facts: In the second quarter of fiscal 2026, Adient plc reported sales of US$3,865 million and net income of US$27 million.
The Q2 fiscal 2026 results reversed a net loss from a year earlier.
Adient modestly raised its full-year guidance for fiscal 2026.
Adient confirmed a profitable first half for fiscal 2026.
Adient expanded its manufacturing footprint by acquiring a foam production plant in Romulus, Michigan.
The Romulus, Michigan acquisition reinforces Adient's vertically integrated seating supply chain.
The acquired foam plant increases capacity for key automaker programs.
Adient's investment narrative centers on turning modest revenue growth into durable, higher quality earnings while managing intense pricing and platform pressure from global automakers.
Near-term earnings stabilization is identified as a key catalyst for Adient's stock.
Persistent restructuring needs and thin margins, especially in Europe, remain the biggest risks to Adient's execution.
The Romulus foam plant acquisition deepens vertical integration in a core component to potentially support margins and supply reliability if volumes hold up.
Adient's narrative projects $15.5 billion in revenue and $292.1 million in earnings by 2029.
Meeting the 2029 projections requires 1.8% yearly revenue growth and approximately a $595 million earnings increase from -$303.0 million today.
Some lower-ranked analysts assume only about 1.3% annual revenue growth and earnings of roughly US$265.4 million by 2029.
Analyst fair value estimates for Adient include a $30.62 target, representing a 40% upside to the current price.

facts: Adient is a leading seating supplier to the industry with about a midteens share of the global market including unconsolidated joint venture business.
Its share in China is around 20%, down from about 45%, following the sale of its main joint venture there at the end of fiscal 2021.
Unconsolidated revenue from joint ventures was about $3.5 billion in fiscal 2025 and consolidated China revenue was $1.3 billion.
The company is headquartered in Ireland but has corporate offices in the Detroit area.
Fiscal 2025 (Sept. 30 year-end) consolidated revenue, which excludes joint venture sales, was $14.5 billion.
Adient began trading Oct. 31, 2016, when Johnson Controls spun off its automotive experience segment.
Over the 3M period, Adient showcased positive performance, achieving a revenue growth rate of 3.54% as of 30 September, 2025.
With a net margin of 0.49%, the company demonstrates strong profitability and effective cost management.
With an ROE of 1.01%, the company may face hurdles in generating optimal returns for shareholders.
With an ROA of 0.2%, the company may encounter challenges in delivering satisfactory returns from its assets.
Adient's debt-to-equity ratio surpasses industry norms, standing at 1.36.
The company's market capitalization falls below industry averages, signaling a relatively smaller size compared to peers.
source_title: Demystifying Adient: Insights From 8 Analyst Reviews - Adient (NYSE:ADNT) - Benzinga

facts: Adient is a global leader in automotive seating systems, generating revenue by designing, manufacturing, and supplying complete seats and components to major automakers worldwide.
The company's business is structured around three geographic segments: Americas, EMEA (Europe, Middle East, Africa), and Asia, with substantial joint venture operations in China.
Q1 2026 consolidated sales increased 4% year-over-year to $3.6 billion, driven by FX tailwinds and robust China growth offsetting North American production headwinds.
Adjusted EBITDA rose 6% year-over-year to $207 million in Q1 2026, with margin improving to 5.7%.
Adient's booked and pending U.S. onshoring and conquest wins now total $500 million in incremental revenue opportunity.
Management expects $300 million of the $500 million onshoring pipeline to impact fiscal year 2027, with the remaining portion impacting fiscal year 2028.
The onshoring pipeline includes both direct onshoring (production moving into the U.S.) and indirect business, as well as a major conquest win.
Adient leverages its established just-in-time supply network and strong OEM relationships to position itself as a net beneficiary of the North American manufacturing reshoring trend.
Modutech, Adient's modular seat architecture, enables higher automation, significant labor and freight cost reductions, and up to 20% total value chain savings.
The modular seat architecture supports long-distance just-in-time delivery and can reduce floor space by 15%, enhancing supply chain resilience.
Americas operations are focused on executing key launches including Kia Telluride and Rivian R2, and expanding automation.
EMEA operations underperformed due to customer mix and deliberate portfolio actions, with new business wins and restructuring expected to gradually restore margins by fiscal year 2027.
Asia's growth is led by China, where Adient is deepening ties with domestic OEMs and launching innovative programs, though new business mix in the region is margin-dilutive in the near term.
Restructuring spend remains elevated in Europe for fiscal year 2026 but is expected to decline in fiscal year 2027 as higher-margin programs ramp.
Adient achieved a 42% reduction in Scope 1 and 2 emissions since 2019 and sources 30% of electricity from renewables.
The company has assessed 80% of its suppliers for sustainability, aligning with OEM responsible sourcing priorities.
Q1 2026 free cash flow was $15 million, exceeding internal expectations due to working capital management and commercial settlement timing.
Full-year 2026 sales guidance was raised to $14.6 billion from a prior estimate of $14.4 billion.
Full-year 2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance was raised to $880 million from a prior estimate of $845 million.
Full-year 2026 free cash flow guidance was raised to $125 million from a prior estimate of $90 million.
Q2 2026 EBITDA is expected to be similar to Q1, with earnings and cash flow ramping in the second half of the year.
Production schedules are normalizing, especially in North America, supporting volume-driven upside for the full year.
Adient maintains a robust liquidity position of $1.7 billion and net leverage at 1.7x.
The company executed $25 million in share repurchases during Q1 2026.
Jerome Adams serves as President & Chief Executive Officer of Adient.
Mark serves as Chief Financial Officer of Adient.
Key risks include execution of complex launches, particularly in EMEA, and the pace of margin recovery in China as new business ramps with lower initial profitability.
Prolonged European volume weakness and continued Chinese import penetration could delay restructuring benefits.
Timing mismatches in commercial recoveries and working capital swings add cash flow volatility.
Macroeconomic or trade policy shifts could disrupt onshoring momentum and customer production outlooks.
source_title: Adient (ADNT) Q1 2026: Onshoring Pipeline Expands to $500M, Amplifying Margin and Growth Visibility | EarningsIQ

facts: Adient is an auto-seat supplier.
On Nov. 7, Adient reported its adjusted operating income for the quarter ended Sept. 30 fell 7% from a year ago to $138 million due to weaker sales in the U.S. and China.
Adient's adjusted earnings per share of $0.63 beat Wall Street's average estimate of $0.26 per share as reported by Thomson Reuters.
Adient's revenue of $3.92 billion was roughly in line with analysts' average estimate of $4.0 billion.
The financial result was better than investors had expected, largely due to cost improvements.
Adient uses a fiscal year that begins on Oct. 1, making the quarter ended Sept. 30, 2019, its fourth quarter of fiscal 2019.
Q4 2019 revenue was $3.92 billion, representing a 5% year-over-year decline.
Q4 2019 EBIT was $102 million, which was $1.146 billion higher than the prior year period.
Q4 2019 adjusted EBITDA was $215 million, a 14% year-over-year decline.
Q4 2019 net income was a loss of $4 million, marking a decline of $2.645 million.
Q4 2019 adjusted net income was $59 million, down 52%.

Beginning in the second quarter of fiscal 2019, Adient changed its business segment reporting from global results for seating, seat structures and mechanisms, and interiors to regional results for Americas; Europe, Middle East, and Africa (EMEA); and Asia.

Americas adjusted EBITDA was $64 million in Q4 2019, down from $70 million a year ago, due to lower sales volumes partly driven by a 40-day strike that idled General Motors' U.S. factories, partially offset by cost reductions.
Americas adjusted EBITDA margin was 3.3%, down 0.2 percentage points from a year ago.
EMEA adjusted EBITDA was $47 million in Q4 2019, down from $55 million a year ago, due to higher manufacturing costs somewhat offset by more favorable commodity prices.
EMEA adjusted EBITDA margin was 3.1%, down 0.4 percentage points from the same period last year.

Asia adjusted EBITDA was $126 million in Q4 2019, down from $146 million a year ago, as China's auto market slumped and Adient's direct sales fell alongside equity income from its Chinese joint ventures.
Excluding equity income, Asia's adjusted EBITDA margin rose to 10.2% in Q4 2019 from 9.2% in the year-ago quarter, helped by lower operating costs.
As of Sept. 30, Adient had $924 million in cash and cash equivalents, down from $1.03 billion as of June 30.
Adient had $3.738 billion in total debt as of Sept. 30, compared to $3.777 billion as of June 30, resulting in net debt of $2.814 billion.
For the fiscal year ending Sept. 30, 2020, Adient expects revenue between $15.6 billion and $15.8 billion (fiscal 2019 result was $16.53 billion).
For the fiscal year ending Sept. 30, 2020, Adient expects capital expenditures between $465 million and $485 million (fiscal 2019 was $468 million).
For the fiscal year ending Sept. 30, 2020, Adient expects adjusted EBITDA between $820 million and $860 million (fiscal 2019 was $787 million).
source_title: Adient's Profit Dropped 7%, but It Could Have Been Worse | The Motley Fool

facts: Interior demolition work for Adient Ltd.'s future downtown Detroit headquarters has begun.
Adient is the nation's largest automotive seat supplier.
The company plans an open work space inside the historic Marquette Building.
The planned workspace will leave no room for individual offices.
The relocation to the new headquarters is pegged for mid-2019.
source_title: Adient pegs downtown Detroit move for mid-2019 - Crain's Detroit Business

facts: An auto manufacturing plant in Tillsonburg, Ontario, suddenly closed its doors on Tuesday, leaving nearly 200 workers without a job.
Adient Seating Canada, formally known in the town as Johnson Controls Ltd., had been making polyurethane seating foam for the auto industry for at least 50 years.
The 192 workers, including management and salaried employees, were informed at 11 a.m. on Tuesday that Monday’s shift was their last and that the facility would be shutting down.
Jobs will be moved to Adient facilities in Tennessee and Kentucky, both right-to-work states.
A spokesperson for Adient stated that after evaluating their foam plant footprint, the company decided to close operations in Tillsonburg.
Adient continually re-evaluates its facilities footprint to ensure operations are most efficient and meet customer needs.
The Tillsonburg plant opened in 1969 and manufactured foam for automotive seats, with foam production moving to several different plants in the U.S.
According to Adient’s website, the company employs more than 150,000 workers in 33 countries across the world.
In 2019, the Tillsonburg plant hired more than 100 employees after winning a contract to manufacture foam seats for the Ford F-150.
Many of the Tillsonburg employees had at least 30 years at the plant, with probably half having 20-plus years of service.
Talks between the union and Adient are ongoing with meetings scheduled with local management on Friday and with corporate representatives on Dec. 5.
The union aims to bargain for extension of benefits, severances, and pensions if jobs cannot be brought back.
Unifor national president Lana Payne condemned the closure, stating Adient managers have no decency or respect for workers who made them a wealthy and successful multinational corporation.
London and area region director for Unifor Luis Domingues stated the plant had extraordinarily good safety records and productivity numbers with no inkling that the closure was going to happen.
source_title: Nearly 200 jobs lost after Adient Tillsonburg, Ont. plant suddenly closes Tuesday - London | Globalnews.ca

facts: The Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant was built on bare fields in Ellabell, Georgia, less than three years ago.
The facility spans 697,000 square meters (7.5 million square feet) across 11 buildings and is set to become America’s largest dedicated plant for EVs and hybrids.
The Metaplant has a production capacity of 500,000 Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis models per year.
The U.S. $7.6 billion investment in the Metaplant produced its inaugural 2025 Ioniq5 vehicle in October.

Ioniq models assembled at the plant are eligible for $7,500 consumer tax credits for U.S.-built EVs with North American batteries, although the credits face potential elimination under the Trump administration.
The factory provides Hyundai with protection against potential tariffs and positions the automaker ahead of many rivals.
Hyundai executives describe the Metaplant as North America’s most heavily automated factory, serving as a showcase for AI and robotic technology.
Workers, referred to as “Meta Pros,” can park in nearly 1,900 spaces beneath solar roofs that provide up to five percent of the plant’s electricity.
Hyundai targets obtaining 100 percent of its energy from renewable sources.

The plant utilizes hydrogen trucks from Hyundai-owned Xcient, the world’s first commercialized hydrogen fuel-cell semis, with a fleet of 21 trucks hauling parts from area suppliers using 400-kilometer ranges and zero tailpipe emissions.
Finished vehicles are primarily shipped by rail to reduce fossil-fuel emissions and the company's carbon footprint.
Approximately 850 robots at the plant unload parts from hydrogen trucks at the docks.

About 300 automated guided vehicles (AGVs) navigate the factory without tracks, avoiding human workers and automatically ferrying parts to workstations for just-in-time delivery via an AI-based procurement system.
Jerry Roach, senior manager of general assembly, states that the AGV system eliminates reliance on human decision-making for part delivery timing and locations.
A fleet of sled-like AGVs autonomously slide under rolling electric Hyundais to grab wheels and ferry finished vehicles to parking areas without human drivers.
The welding shop houses 475 industrial robots that assemble “bodies in white” chassis components with high speed and precision.
Robotic quadrupeds designed by Hyundai-owned Boston Dynamics, called Spots, use 360-degree vision to inspect car welds for potential defects.
Hyundai plans to deploy Atlas humanoid robots from Boston Dynamics across its global factories to sense environments, avoid collisions, and manipulate parts in complex sequences.
Hyundai executives state that while automation will inevitably replace some jobs, it is primarily used to free humans from hazardous and repetitive tasks rather than replace autoworkers entirely.
Collaborative robots are used to install bulky doors on the assembly line without physical safety separation, ensuring perfect placement and preventing paint damage.

The plant features an onsite steel stamping facility where robots stamp roofs, fenders, and hoods in a quiet environment requiring no ear protection, with parts automatically stored in overhead racks.
The Metaplant was designed from the ground up as an electrified car plant, avoiding the need to retrofit existing internal-combustion factories like those used by Tesla and Rivian.
The facility currently employs 1,340 “Meta Pros” with an annual average pay of $58,100, which is 25 percent higher than the Bryan County, Georgia average.
Hyundai’s annual local payroll at the plant has already reached $497 million.
The company projects a total of 8,500 direct jobs on site and 7,000 indirect jobs for local suppliers and businesses in the future.
Hyundai currently sources battery cells from Georgia and SK On, with some Ioniq5 batteries imported from Hungary.

The Metaplant campus includes HL-GA, a $4 billion joint venture with LG Energy Solutions that plans to produce nickel-cobalt-magnesium cells starting next year, with packs assembled on site by Hyundai’s Mobis subsidiary.
Hyundai is on track to open a second $5 billion battery plant in Georgia as a joint operation with SK On.
These projects are part of Hyundai’s planned $21 billion investment in the U.S. between now and 2028, exceeding its previous $20 billion investment since entering the market in 1986.
source_title: Hyundai’s Metaplant in Search of Diligent Robots – GretAi News

facts: In 2023, Georgia surpassed $49.7 billion in exports, eclipsing the record-setting $47 billion set in 2022.
The state currently ranks seventh in the U.S. for dollar value of trade and twelfth in the country for dollar value of exports.
Georgia facilitated more than $186 billion in total trade to 222 unique countries and territories.
Governor Brian P. Kemp joined the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) to announce the export record-breaking milestone.
An estimated 87 percent of Georgia's exports come from small businesses.
International trade touches every county in the state, supporting jobs in logistics, manufacturing, agriculture, and other sectors.
Top exports for 2023 included civilian aircraft products, motor vehicles, turbojets and turbines, poultry, and chemical woodpulp products.
Canada, Mexico, China, Germany, and Singapore were the top destinations for Georgia exports.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that international representatives in overseas markets help find homes for Georgia-made goods and play a significant role in supporting small businesses.

The state's top five bilateral trading partners in 2023 were Mexico, South Korea, Canada, China, and Germany, with notable year-over-year growth in total trade between the State of Georgia and South Korea.
Markets where Georgia maintains full-time international trade representation accounted for 67 percent of exports and 71 percent of bilateral trade in 2023.
GDEcD Deputy Commissioner of International Trade Lizann Grupalo noted that an estimated one in nine jobs in Georgia is tied to the logistics industry.
Georgia is home to Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport, described as the busiest and most efficient airport in the world.
The Port of Savannah is the fastest growing and third-busiest container gateway in the U.S.
The Port of Brunswick is one of the nation's busiest gateways for Roll-on/Roll-off cargo.
Georgia offers 5,000 miles of rail and 20,000 miles of federal and state highways, providing robust rail and highway infrastructure.
Support from Georgia’s International Trade office is a complimentary benefit available to all Georgia-based businesses.

facts: Adient announced that Jerome Dorlack will be appointed executive vice president and chief financial officer, effective Dec. 1, 2022.
Jeff Stafeil will resign from his role as Adient’s executive vice president and chief financial officer, effective Nov. 30, 2022.
Jim Conklin will become the executive vice president, Americas, also effective Dec. 1, 2022.

Doug Del Grosso, Adient’s president and chief executive officer, stated that Jerome has been a key member of the leadership team and they are fortunate to have someone of his caliber step into the CFO role.
Doug Del Grosso noted that Jim’s long tenure and deep knowledge make him ideally positioned to lead the Americas region.
Doug Del Grosso expressed confidence that Adient is strongly positioned to continue driving value for stakeholders due to its leading market position, leadership depth, and talented employees.
Doug Del Grosso thanked Jeff Stafeil for his commitment and contributions since joining the company in 2016.
Jeff Stafeil stated it has been an honor to work with a dedicated team and expressed confidence that Adient is well-positioned for the future.
Jim Conklin has been with Adient in various roles of increasing responsibility since October 2000, most recently serving as vice president, North Americas operations.
Jerome Dorlack has served as Adient’s executive vice president, Americas, since 2019.
Jerome Dorlack previously served as vice president and chief purchasing officer of Adient from 2018 to 2019.
Prior to joining Adient, Jerome Dorlack served as senior vice president and president, electrical distribution system and president, South America of Aptiv plc from 2017 to 2018.
Prior to that, Jerome Dorlack served as vice president, powertrain systems and general manager, global powertrain products of Delphi Automotive plc from 2016 to 2017.
Jerome Dorlack previously served as executive vice president global procurement of ZF Friedrichshafen from 2015 to 2016.
Jerome Dorlack previously served as vice president, global purchasing, supplier development and logistics of ZF Friedrichshafen from 2013 to 2015.
Adient operates approximately 75,000 employees in 31 countries.
Adient operates more than 200 manufacturing/assembly plants worldwide.
Adient produces and delivers automotive seating for all major OEMs.
Adient's products are integrated into more than 20 million vehicles every year.

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. collaborated with Palantir Technologies Inc. to build the AI foundation for the future of advanced aviation technologies.

The companies plan to leverage Palantir Foundry and artificial intelligence platform to accelerate the scaling of Archer’s aircraft manufacturing capabilities at its Georgia and Silicon Valley facilities.
The partnership will advance the development of software solutions and drive innovation across the entire value chain.
Archer and Palantir will develop advanced AI software to improve various aviation systems, including air traffic control, movement control, and route planning.
The AI software initiative aims to boost industry efficiency, safety, and affordability.
Archer and Palantir will formalize this partnership later Thursday during a signing ceremony at Palantir’s AIPCon.
The ceremony will be hosted by Alex Karp, co-founder and CEO of Palantir, and Adam Goldstein, founder and CEO of Archer.
Archer Aviation stock surged 46% in the last 12 months.
The company reported a fourth-quarter loss of $1.42 per share, missing analyst estimates of $1.24 per share.
Archer’s quarterly spending stayed within the guided range and remained close to flat for the second straight quarter.
The company ended the period with $834.5 million in cash and equivalents.
JPMorgan analyst Bill Peterson noted that the first piloted flight in the U.S. could draw investors toward Archer’s technology.
Peterson projected a first-quarter loss of $0.21 per share for Archer.
Palantir Technologies stock surged 235%, backed by the AI frenzy, with analysts associating its rise to artificial intelligence capabilities and Karp’s support for government efficiency initiatives.
Archer Aviation stock was down 1.21% at $7.38 at last check early Thursday.
Palantir Technologies stock was down 4.38% to $79.96 at last check early Thursday.

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. is developing electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft for commercial air taxi services under its Urban Air Mobility (UAM) sector focus.
Archer Aviation targets a 2026 U.S. commercial launch for its Midnight eVTOL aircraft.
The company secured a Part 135 Air Carrier and Operator Certificate from the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) in June 2024, enabling commercial operations for system refinement.
Archer Aviation obtained a Part 141 certification from the FAA in February 2025 specifically for pilot training.
As of August 2025, Archer had completed 15% of its FAA certification compliance documents for the Midnight eVTOL, progressing toward Type Inspection Authorization (TIA).
United Airlines is a strategic investor in Archer Aviation and holds a provisional order for up to 200 Midnight aircraft.
Archer Aviation and United Airlines have jointly announced planned routes, including a corridor between Manhattan and Newark Liberty International Airport.
Global automaker Stellantis partners with Archer Aviation, providing equity capital and critical manufacturing expertise.
Archer Aviation operates a high-volume manufacturing facility located in Covington, Georgia.
The Covington, Georgia facility aims to produce up to 650 eVTOL aircraft per year.
Production of eVTOLs at the Georgia facility commenced in early 2025.
As of October 2025, six Midnight aircraft are in production at the facility, with three units in final assembly.
Archer Aviation maintains a liquidity position of approximately $1.7 billion as of Q2 2025.
The anticipated commercial launch of air taxis is expected to accelerate investment and development for companies involved in vertiport construction and charging infrastructure.

Archer's progress validates the eVTOL market and attracts investment into ancillary industries including battery technology, advanced materials, air traffic management software, and urban planning for vertiport integration.
The company's immediate operational focus includes accelerating the FAA Type Certification process for the Midnight aircraft to meet its targeted 2026 U.S. commercial launch.
Ramping up production at the Covington, Georgia facility is a key indicator of Archer's ability to scale operations and meet future demand.
Archer Aviation was selected as the Official Air Taxi Provider for the LA28 Olympic and Paralympic Games.
Archer delivered its first piloted Midnight aircraft to the UAE in August 2025 for flight tests.
In June 2025, Archer formed a partnership with Jetex in the UAE.
On October 8, 2025, Archer reached an agreement with Cleveland Clinic Abu Dhabi to establish the UAE's first hospital-based vertiport.
Archer has commercial deals in Asia with Japan Airlines and Sumitomo Corporation's joint venture, Soracle, for services in Osaka.
source_title: FinancialContent - Archer Aviation Soars: Midnight's Ascent Fuels 6.43% Rally as Urban Air Mobility Takes Flight

facts: Archer designs and develops electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft for urban transport.
Its eVTOLs use electric propulsion to take off and land vertically, enabling compact, city-friendly air mobility that can serve urban commuters and feed into air taxi networks.
The company sells its aircraft directly and may in the future generate revenue from air taxi services or related operations, blending aircraft sales with service fees.

Archer differentiates itself by focusing on the urban air mobility market and pursuing a direct-sales approach paired with potential operation services, aiming to partner with city planners and transportation networks to integrate eco-friendly, on-demand urban transport.
The goal is to advance sustainable, city-centered air mobility and help cities reduce congestion and pollution by offering practical, electric aerial transit.
Company size is 1,001-5,000 employees.
Company stage is IPO.
Headquarters is in San Jose, California.
Founded in 2020.
Archer Aviation has partnered with Hopscotch Air, an FAA-certificated air taxi operator, to test concepts for future advanced air mobility operations.
The collaboration provides Archer with real-world operational insights to refine its aircraft, software and operating models for commercial air taxi services.
The partnership supports Archer's preparation for early operations around the White House eVTOL Integration Pilot Program, where the company is working with partners in Texas, Florida and New York.
Hopscotch brings day-to-day operator feedback to help Archer develop repeatable, scalable services.
Archer's narrative projects $533.9 million revenue by 2029, requiring 1,111.8% yearly growth.
The company continues to face significant challenges, including regulatory hurdles, prolonged certification timelines and substantial cash burn whilst pursuing commercialisation milestones.

Archer Aviation shares are facing pressure despite federal support, with the company's momentum score dropping from 14.54 to 7.50 week-on-week, placing it in the bottom 10% of the market for price strength.
The stock has declined roughly 29% over the past year.
The downturn follows a fourth-quarter earnings report showing an adjusted EBITDA loss of $138 million.

JPMorgan analysts warned that Archer may need to raise capital "potentially several times" before achieving positive free cash flow, whilst Needham analysts noted widening losses create "additional capital needs".
Archer recently secured a position in the Trump administration's eVTOL Integration Pilot Programme, aiming to launch electric air taxis in major hubs by 2026.
The stock closed at $6.01 on Thursday.

Archer Aviation Inc. is advancing towards FAA certification for its electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft, positioning itself in a market Morgan Stanley projects could reach $1.5-2.9 trillion by 2040.
The company recently acquired Hawthorne Airport for $126 million to serve as its Los Angeles air taxi network hub, partnering with Palantir Technologies for AI-powered operations.
Archer's Midnight aircraft has demonstrated 55-mile flights at 126 miles per hour, carrying four passengers with a 12-propeller safety system.
The company has secured over $1 billion in orders, though delivery timelines remain uncertain.
Whilst not yet generating revenue, Archer reported a Q3 net loss of $129.9 million on operating expenses of $174.8 million.
However, it maintains strong liquidity with approximately $2.3 billion in cash and short-term investments, supporting its high-risk commercialisation efforts.

Archer Aviation is advancing its position in electric air mobility through development of its Midnight eVTOL aircraft, designed for short-distance urban transportation with lower noise and emissions than conventional helicopters.
The aircraft is intended to carry multiple passengers on high-frequency routes within urban air mobility networks.
The company is expanding manufacturing capabilities and working with aviation authorities on aircraft certification.
Archer is also building partnerships with operators and infrastructure providers to support commercial air taxi services.
The Zacks Consensus Estimate suggests earnings per share will decline 63.49% in 2026, then grow 7.77% in 2027.
The stock trades at a trailing 12-month price-to-book ratio of 1.9X, below the industry average of 7.02X.
Competitors include Joby Aviation and Vertical Aerospace, both developing similar eVTOL aircraft for urban transportation.
Archer Aviation, an electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft developer, has underperformed since going public via SPAC merger in September 2021, with shares falling from $9.90 to around $6.
The company has only manufactured two eVTOLs, far below its target of 250 units for 2025, and generated just $0.3 million in revenue against a $618.2 million net loss in 2025.
Despite this, Archer holds an indicative backlog of $6 billion with orders for approximately 1,200 aircraft from customers including United Airlines, Japan Airlines, and Ethiopian Airlines.
Its Midnight eVTOL carries four passengers plus a pilot and travels up to 100 miles per charge.
Analysts forecast revenue of $512.4 million by 2028.
Commercial operations face delays pending FAA approval and regional conflicts affecting planned Abu Dhabi flights.

facts: Partnering with global leaders in ground transportation to seamlessly integrate air mobility into the future of door-to-door travel.
Forming partnerships with leading global airlines to integrate its air taxi service into existing aviation networks.
Working with key infrastructure partners to build the physical backbone needed for convenient everyday flight.
Collaborating with pioneers in manufacturing and innovation to create a vertically integrated, world-class production ecosystem.
Leveraging aviation technology partnerships to power systems supporting navigation, autonomy, pilot training, and operational excellence.
Collaborating with forward-thinking government agencies to shape policy, accelerate innovation, and enable the introduction of advanced air mobility.

facts: Grizzly Research issued a bearish report on Archer Aviation, calling the company “the Nikola of the skies.”
The short seller argued Archer built its reputation in the eVTOL space through misleading projections and press releases.
After conducting analysis with eVTOL engineers, Grizzly believes Archer’s Midnight aircraft is “fundamentally flawed and likely uncertifiable.”
Archer claimed earlier this month that it had six Midnight aircraft in production, including three in final assembly.
The company recently delivered its first Midnight aircraft to the UAE and commenced its flight test program in Abu Dhabi.
Archer expects initial commercial payments later this year, but Grizzly is skeptical of the company’s progress.
Site visits to Archer's ‘state-of-the-art’ Covington, Georgia manufacturing facility in June, July and August 2025 showed little to no production activity.

This lack of activity comes despite claims of current production along with scaling to a production rate of 50 aircraft per year and a final goal of 650 aircraft per year with support from Stellantis.
Grizzly alleged that Archer’s $6 billion order book is being inflated by fraudulent commitments, including Air Chateau’s MoU for 100 aircraft which lacks the scale or funding for such a fleet.
Grizzly highlighted a deal with Future Flight Global that is tied to a shell company with no operations.
KakaoMobility’s commitment did not meet its deadline but is reportedly still included in the company’s backlog.
Grizzly alleged Archer’s “Launch Edition” aircraft was “nothing more than a staged hover.”
The company recently pivoted to serve the defense industry, which Grizzly described as a “desperate attempt” to stay relevant in the eVTOL space.
Competitor Joby Aviation has made impressive progress on all fronts.
Joby's methodical, engineering-first approach has positioned it to deliver a high-quality, mission-ready product well ahead of Archer.
source_title: Short Seller Calls Archer Aviation The 'Nikola Of The Skies,' Says Joby Is Well Ahead In eVTOL Race - Arc - Benzinga

facts: Archer Aviation is an electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft manufacturer.
Stellantis serves as a major backer of Archer Aviation.
Stellantis reported preliminary first-half 2025 earnings showing a $2.68 billion loss, raising fears the automaker might scale back its support for Archer to cut costs.
A Delaware Chancery Court ruling allows a shareholder lawsuit related to Archer’s 2021 SPAC merger to proceed, alleging misrepresentation of share value and development progress.
Archer secured its Part 135 Air Carrier Certificate in June 2024, marking a critical step toward commercial operations.
Archer's Midnight eVTOL is a four-seat, zero-emission aircraft designed for short urban trips.
Archer is on track to complete FAA Type Certification later this year (2025).
Completion of FAA Type Certification would enable revenue-generating flights in the United Arab Emirates by late 2025 and in the U.S. by early 2026.
Archer's Georgia manufacturing facility, backed by Stellantis, aims to produce two aircraft per month by the end of 2025.
Archer's Georgia manufacturing facility aims to scale production to 650 aircraft annually by 2030.
United Airlines placed a $1 billion order for 200 Midnight eVTOL aircraft.
Archer plans to develop air taxi networks in cities including New York and Los Angeles.
Archer conducted recent test flights in Abu Dhabi.
Archer plans to participate in the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles.
Archer raised $850 million in capital in June, bringing its total liquidity to approximately $2 billion.
Archer's financial position supports a $6 billion order backlog and funds production scaling.
Archer reported a first-quarter loss of $0.17 per share, beating analyst expectations of $0.28 per share.
Archer outsources its manufacturing to Stellantis, contrasting with competitor Joby Aviation's vertically integrated model.
The eVTOL market is projected to grow from $760 million in 2024 to $170.6 billion by 2034.
Morgan Stanley projects the U.S. air taxi industry could be worth $1 trillion by 2040 and $9 trillion by 2050.

facts: Archer Aviation Inc announced it has completed a capital raise of $850 million.
The capital raise comes amid the White House’s recent executive order to establish an eVTOL Integration Pilot Program.
This initiative aims to accelerate the deployment of electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft within the United States.
Archer plans to work closely with the Department of Transportation and the Federal Aviation Administration to integrate this program with its own expansion plans.
The company is the Official Air Taxi Provider for the LA 28 Olympic Games and Team USA.
CEO Adam Goldstein hailed the executive order as a “seminal moment,” emphasizing that Archer now possesses the strongest balance sheet in the sector.
The company’s pro forma liquidity now stands at approximately $2 billion.

The new capital, raised through the sale of 85 million shares at $10.00 each, will fund general corporate purposes, the build-out of commercial infrastructure and the development of an AI-based aviation software platform.
Next week, Archer says the company will showcase its “Midnight” aircraft at the Paris Air Show.

facts: Archer Aviation announced it is developing an air taxi network in New York City in partnership with United Airlines.
The service will utilize the piloted electric air taxi named Midnight.
Midnight aircraft are built at the company’s manufacturing facilities in San Jose, California, and Covington, Georgia.
The Midnight aircraft is designed to carry four passengers.
Passengers will eventually be able to book these flights as an add-on to regular airline tickets or through Archer’s app.
Initially, the service will target business and first-class travelers flying from nearby airports and will be more expensive.
The long-term goal is to make the service affordable enough for everyday commuters to use it regularly, potentially replacing car rides to and from the airport.
Archer Aviation was founded in 2018 and went public three years later.
The company has a market valuation of $3.94 billion.

Archer has signed agreements with major airports in the region including John F. Kennedy International Airport, LaGuardia Airport, Newark Airport, Westchester County Airport, Teterboro Airport, and Republic Airport, as well as three city helipads.
The company previously announced plans to establish air taxi networks in Los Angeles and San Francisco.
Archer is currently in the final stages of securing approval from the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA).
Founder and CEO Adam Goldstein stated Archer aims to launch commercial operations as early as this year in Abu Dhabi.
A New York City launch for the service is targeted for 2026, pending regulatory approval which comes with high standards.
Goldstein said his motivation behind launching the aircraft was to help modernize urban transportation due to significant population growth and outdated transit solutions.
Goldstein noted that traditional helicopters typically have 200 to 300 single points of failure.
Archer's all-electric propulsion system features fewer moving parts, no combustion engine, and no gearbox, allowing for added redundancy compared to piston or combustion engines.
Instead of a single main rotor, the aircraft has 12 separate propellers, enabling it to continue flying even if an engine or propeller fails.
A digital flight control system helps prevent any command or maneuver that could damage the aircraft.
Fixed wings allow the aircraft to glide up to 20 miles, providing an added layer of safety not typically found in rotorcraft.
The FAA has set higher aircraft certification safety standards for Archer's aircraft than for conventional light helicopters and light airplanes.
Goldstein stated that highly redundant systems enable the aircraft to exceed these higher safety standards.
Joby Aviation is pursuing a similar business model while simultaneously working in partnership with Delta Air Lines and Uber to develop electric flying taxis.
Joby plans to launch its operations in Dubai as early as this year, with U.S. operations expected in the next few years pending completion of the FAA certification process.
Unlike Joby's six-rotor configuration with tilting propellers, Archer employs a 12-rotor design with fixed wings.
While Joby develops many components in-house, Archer uses traditional aerospace suppliers.
Archer Aviation trades under the ticker ACHR with a last price of $7.23 and a change of +0.18 (+2.55%).
Joby Aviation trades under the ticker JOBY with a last price of $5.89 and a change of +0.10 (+1.73%).
source_title: Archer Aviation teams up with United Airlines to make air taxis a reality | National Guns

facts: Archer Aviation received an additional $55 million investment from Stellantis under the companies’ strategic funding agreement.
The additional investment follows Archer's achievement of its transition flight test milestone last month (June 2024).
This latest investment builds on a previously announced series of open market purchases by Stellantis of 8.3 million shares of Archer’s stock in March 2024.
During 2023, Stellantis invested $110 million into Archer through a combination of open market stock purchases and investments under the strategic funding agreement.
Archer remains on track to complete construction of its high-volume manufacturing facility in Georgia later this year (2024).
The first phase of the facility build-out is a ~350,000 square foot facility located on an ~100 acre site.
The facility is designed to support production of up to 650 aircraft annually.
This capacity would make it one of the largest manufacturing facilities by volume in the aircraft industry.
Archer aims to establish a factory that supports its planned commercial ramp by leveraging Stellantis as its contract manufacturer.
Stellantis has served as a strategic partner to Archer since 2020 through various collaboration initiatives.
Stellantis has been an investor in Archer since 2021.
Archer leveraged Stellantis’ deep manufacturing, supply chain, and design expertise to design, develop, and commercialize its eVTOL aircraft.

Carlos Tavares, Stellantis CEO, stated: “Few things rival the excitement of seeing a dream take flight. I applaud the innovation, expertise and hard work of the engineering and manufacturing teams from Stellantis and Archer.”
Carlos Tavares added: “With this additional investment in Archer, we remain on course for a future where freedom of mobility extends beyond today’s roads.”

Adam Goldstein, Archer CEO, stated: “The commitment by Stellantis to Archer has been unrivaled, from its foresight to provide the manufacturing expertise and capital needed to accelerate Archer’s business objectives, to the strategic vision and steadfast support from Stellantis CEO Carlos Tavares.”

Adam Goldstein added: “Together, we’re working to redefine urban transportation, opening new worlds of opportunity for citizens across the globe by providing more efficient access to people, places, and events across the regions they live in.”

Archer’s goal is to transform urban travel by replacing 60–90-minute commutes by car with estimated 10–20-minute electric air taxi flights that are safe, sustainable, low noise, and cost-competitive with ground transportation.
Archer’s Midnight aircraft is a piloted, four-passenger aircraft designed to perform rapid back-to-back flights with minimal charge time between flights.
source_title: HeliHub.com : Stellantis Invests $55M more into Archer - HeliHub.com

facts: Archer Aviation has completed construction of its high-volume manufacturing facility, ARC, in Covington, Georgia.
The 400,000-square-foot facility is located next to Covington Municipal Airport and serves as a key milestone for scaling production of Archer's Midnight eVTOL aircraft.
Initial manufacturing at the facility is set to begin in early 2025.
The decision to establish ARC in Georgia followed an extensive nationwide site selection evaluation.

Key considerations for selecting Georgia included local labor talent availability, utility availability, ability to conduct seamless flight test operations, construction costs, and logistics, according to Glen Burks, Archer’s Vice President of Operations.
Archer received strong support from state and local officials during the process, including Governor Brian Kemp and Newton County leaders.
The ARC facility is expected to create over 1,000 jobs long-term, cementing Georgia’s role as a hub for advanced manufacturing.

Georgia is home to more than 800 aerospace companies and maintains a robust aerospace education pipeline with a dozen high schools offering field training, five technical colleges with aviation programs, and several universities offering aerospace engineering degrees.
Archer has been working closely with local educational institutions to attract and prepare local talent by creating opportunities for Georgia residents.
Archer is leveraging its partnership with Stellantis, which has embedded dozens of personnel in the project to apply automotive manufacturing expertise to eVTOL production.
Stellantis provided Archer with expertise throughout the site selection process and continues to contribute across several key teams related to manufacturing and beyond.
With ARC’s completion, Archer is transitioning from research and development into full-scale commercialization, aiming to ramp production to two aircraft per month by the end of 2025.
source_title: Archer Completes 400,000 square foot eVTOL manufacturing facility

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. (NYSE:ACHR) shares rose in Tuesday’s premarket session, extending Monday’s gains after the stock closed up 4.15%.

The company announced Monday that its partners in Texas, Florida, and New York were selected by the U.S. Department of Transportation and the Federal Aviation Administration to participate in the White House’s eVTOL Integration Pilot Program.

The initiative aims to create a regulatory pathway for launching electric air taxis, supporting the introduction of the first new aircraft category in nearly 80 years, and reinforcing U.S. leadership in next-generation aviation.

Archer will work with partners, including the Texas Department of Transportation, the Florida Department of Transportation, and the Port Authority of New York and New Jersey, to prepare for early operations of its Midnight aircraft, potentially starting in the second half of 2026.
CEO Adam Goldstein stated: “This is the clearest sign yet from the White House, the FAA, and the DOT that bringing air taxis to market in the United States is a real priority.”
Midnight is a piloted electric air taxi designed to carry up to four passengers.
Archer aims to replace 60- to 90-minute car trips with quieter, all-electric flights to reduce travel time and urban congestion.
Archer Aviation reported a fourth-quarter loss of 26 cents per share, wider than analysts’ estimates for a 24-cent loss.
The company generated revenue of $300,000, falling short of expectations of about $666,000.
Despite the earnings miss, Archer said it plans to continue expanding its piloted Midnight aircraft fleet through 2026.
Several aircraft are currently in different stages of production.
The company is targeting its first passenger-carrying flights in 2026 as it moves toward commercial air taxi operations.
Archer Aviation is expected to provide its next financial update on May 11, 2026.
Analyst EPS estimate for the next update is a loss of 25 cents (down from a loss of 17 cents).
Analyst revenue estimate for the next update is $1.65 million (up from $0.00 million).
The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $11.45.

Needham issued a Buy rating and maintained its target at $10.00 on January 14.
Goldman Sachs initiated coverage with a Neutral rating and an $11.00 target on December 1, 2025.

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. and United Airlines have announced their first airport to city center route between United’s hub at Newark Liberty International Airport (EWR) and New York City.

The Downtown Manhattan Heliport, located just above Battery Park on Pier 6 has been confirmed as the first planned takeoff and landing site for Archer’s broader plan to launch an Urban Air Mobility (UAM) network across the New York Metropolitan Area.
The goal of Archer’s electric air taxi network is to provide passengers with safe, quick, quiet and cost-effective transportation to and from EWR.
Archer’s proposed Manhattan to EWR route marks the first specific route that has been announced in the eVTOL industry.
United’s hub at EWR is ideally located to serve Manhattan and its surrounding boroughs, as well as communities in New Jersey.

Archer estimates the journey from the Downtown Manhattan Heliport to EWR will take less than 10 minutes via its all-electric Midnight aircraft, a low noise, sustainable and safe alternative to helicopters.
A similar journey by car can take upwards of an hour or more during rush hour traffic.

Adam Goldstein, Archer’s Founder and CEO, stated they are excited to confirm New York as the first of many routes they will announce alongside United as they work to build out their national UAM network.

Goldstein expressed looking forward to continuing collaboration with United and working closely with state and local government leaders in the New York and New Jersey area to bring this new form of transportation to life.

Michael Leskinen, President of United Airlines Ventures, said electric air taxis will fundamentally change how United customers experience comfort, convenience, and efficiency during commutes to the airport.

Leskinen noted that Archer’s New York to Newark route marks another important benchmark towards providing a carbon-neutral and safe commute that drastically reduces travel time and produces a fraction of the sound emitted by helicopters.
Archer’s early launch routes will provide airport to city center transportation service, which it refers to as “trunk” routes.
Once primary trunk routes are established, Archer will begin building out “branch” routes to connect to surrounding communities.
The first phase of this UAM network is targeted for launch in 2025.
This press release was prepared and distributed by Archer Aviation.
source_title: Archer and United Airlines announce first commercial electric air taxi route in the U.S. - Vertical Mag

facts: Archer Aviation was named the official air taxi provider of the 2028 Los Angeles Olympic and Paralympic Games.
Archer Aviation will also serve as the air taxi provider for Team USA at the LA28 Games.
The Midnight aircraft, which is partially being manufactured at the Covington “ARC” facility in Covington, Georgia, will be the primary aircraft used for the partnership.
Adam Goldstein, CEO and founder of Archer Aviation, expressed excitement about the partnership in a released statement.
Goldstein stated that Archer wants to transform transportation in Los Angeles and leave a legacy shaping the future of transportation in America during the LA28 Games.
The Midnight aircraft will fly passengers over Los Angeles emblazoned with the Team USA logo and the Olympic Rings and Paralympic Agitos.
Archer labels the Midnight aircraft as an affordable alternative for medium-distance drives.
Chief People and Partnerships Officer Dr. Tosha Perkins previously called the Midnight “the uber of the skies” in an interview with The Covington News.
Perkins explained that users will be able to book routes via the Archer app, reducing travel times from 60-90 minutes by car to 10-15 minutes in the aircraft.
The Midnight aircraft can host up to four passengers along with carry-on luggage and is operated by a licensed pilot.
Travel speeds for the Midnight are expected to pace up to 150 mph with minimal charge times between back-to-back flights.
Flight prices for the Midnight are expected to be in the range of “20% higher than an Uber black.”
The Midnight is currently in its preliminary production phase following the completion of the ARC facility in December 2024.
Archer expects a full grand scale rollout of the Midnight by 2026.
source_title: Archer Aviation named as official air taxi provider of 2028 Olympic Games - The Covington News

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. (NYSE:ACHR) inked a memorandum of understanding with InterGlobe Enterprises to launch and operate an all-electric air taxi service in India in 2026.

Under the deal, the company aims to provide a revolutionary transportation solution for the country, improving urban mobility with a safe, sustainable, and low-noise electric air taxi service that is cost-competitive with ground transportation.
The partnership also plans to finance the purchase of up to 200 of Archer's Midnight aircraft for the India operations.
Archer's CEO and Founder, Adam Goldstein, commented that the all-electric Midnight aircraft is designed to provide a revolutionary transportation solution that can help address congestion issues.
The parties plan to collaborate with chosen business partners within the country to manage the operations of Archer's aircraft.
The parties plan to fund and construct vertiport infrastructure for the India operations.
The parties plan to provide training for pilots and other essential personnel for these activities.

In addition to urban air taxi services, the parties plan to pursue various other use cases for electric aircraft in India, including cargo, logistics, medical and emergency services, private companies, and charter services.
ACHR shares are trading higher by 1.73% to $5.30 premarket on the last check Thursday.

facts: Trial flights of Archer Aviation’s first fully electric air taxi, the vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft “Midnight,” are scheduled to begin before the end of 2025.
Dr. Talib Alhinai serves as the general manager of Archer Aviation.
Archer recently launched a manufacturing facility in Georgia, United States.
The Georgia manufacturing facility features an initial annual production capacity of 650 aircraft.
Archer has plans to expand its Georgia facility's production capacity to 2,400 aircraft annually.
Archer has signed a framework agreement with the Abu Dhabi Investment Office to establish a research center and explore local production in the UAE.
The “Midnight” eVTOL is designed for sustainable urban transport and can accommodate four passengers and a pilot.
The “Midnight” operates entirely on electric power and takes off and lands vertically like a helicopter.
Initial test flights will occur in unpopulated areas, gradually expanding to urban zones as part of a phased approach to full deployment.
A formal date for commercial service has yet to be established and will be announced pending the completion of all necessary technical and operational requirements.
The “Midnight” is equipped with a fixed wing and 12 electric motors—six at the front and six at the rear.
The front motors can rotate between 0 and 90 degrees, allowing for a smooth transition from vertical ascent to horizontal flight in just 45 seconds.

facts: Archer Aviation has closed agreements with financing and development partners for the construction of its high-volume manufacturing facility in Covington, Georgia.
The California-based start-up plans to mass produce its Midnight eVTOL aircraft at this facility.
Evans General Contractors, an international construction firm with headquarters in Georgia, has agreed to design and build Archer’s aircraft factory.
Synovus Bank has agreed to provide the company with a $65 million credit line to fund the development.
That $65 million represents a substantial majority of the cost of construction for the first phase of Archer’s eVTOL manufacturing facility.
Archer has not disclosed the estimated full cost of the facility.
For the first phase of the development, Archer intends to build a 350,000-sq-ft factory.
The company has said the first-phase factory will be capable of producing up to 650 Midnight aircraft per year.
Nikhil Goel, Archer’s chief commercial officer, told AIN that the phase one facility is expected to be complete by Q2 of 2024.
The company’s four-passenger Midnight aircraft is expected to enter service in 2025.
In the first year of production, Archer will be able to produce in the single-digit hundreds of aircraft.
Production capacity will then ramp up to over 2,000 aircraft per year.
To support increasing production rates, Archer aims to eventually expand the Covington facility to 900,000 sq ft.
The state of Georgia is providing Archer with government incentives that will support the scaling of the facility in a pretty cost-efficient way.
Car-making giant Stellantis, Archer’s production partner, has agreed to provide Archer with up to $150 million in equity capital for the facility’s development.
In seeking development and financing partners for the Covington facility, Archer focused on companies in the southeastern U.S. that have expertise in high-volume manufacturing facilities.
Archer founder and CEO Adam Goldstein stated: “We are committed long-term partners to the community in Covington, Georgia.”
Goldstein added: “Consistent with our capital efficient strategy of getting to commercialization, we’ve achieved attractive terms on the project.”

Goldstein said: “These agreements, along with our already strong partnership with Stellantis, give us the flexibility to ramp up our capacity and become the leading scale manufacturer of eVTOL aircraft in the world.”

facts: Archer Aviation, a California company based in Santa Clara, will invest $118 million to construct a plant near Atlanta, Georgia.
The company expects to receive a roughly $40 million incentive package from Georgia and local governments for the project.
The incentive package includes the donation of 96 acres (39 hectares) of land at Covington Municipal Airport.
The plant will eventually hire up to 1,000 people.
Archer's plan involves a battery-powered vertical takeoff and landing craft with six propellers that hold four passengers and a pilot.
The aircraft's propellers pivot to allow it to take off and land like a helicopter and fly like a plane.
United Airlines put down a $10 million deposit to buy 100 aircraft from Archer for a total of $1 billion.
United Airlines plans to fly the craft from downtown Manhattan to Newark Liberty International Airport in New Jersey, cutting travel time to 10 minutes.
After building a prototype weighing 3,330 pounds (1,510 kilograms), Archer plans to unveil a production model dubbed "Midnight" on Wednesday.
The company aims for the aircraft to enter service in 2025.
The U.S. Air Force is evaluating Archer's aircraft for possible use.
Archer is seeking Federal Aviation Administration certification and says it could receive it in 2024.
Archer wants to produce 650 aircraft per year at the Georgia site, possibly scaling up to 2,300 per year.
Automotive firm Stellantis, owner of Dodge, Chrysler, Jeep, and Ram, is providing manufacturing and engineering help.
The incentive package includes a cash grant from the state and other tax incentives, including a property tax break.
A job tax credit could be worth up to $15 million to Archer over five years, contingent on employees making at least $31,300.
The company chose the Georgia location for locally available workers, the ability to conduct test flights, affordable construction costs, and good highway, rail, and air connections.

Archer CEO Adam Goldstein stated that their eVTOL technology can transform how urban and rural communities live and commute and that the factory can create pathways to highly skilled manufacturing jobs and other ladders of social and economic opportunity.
Archer expects to borrow money from Georgia-based Synovus Financial Corp for the plant's construction.
Construction is expected to begin and conclude in 2024.
The company went public last year, raising about $850 million.
Archer lost $91 million in the quarter that ended Sept. 30, reporting $600 million in cash on hand.
Georgia economic development officials have been focusing on recruiting electric car companies, landing major plants from Rivian Automotive and Hyundai Motor Group last year.
Norwegian battery startup Freyr announced a $2.6 billion plant in Georgia on the same day as Archer's announcement.
A constructed electric battery plant owned by South Korean firm SK Innovation already exists in Georgia.

Georgia Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson said economic recruiters focused on aerospace had worked with Archer, noting that technology and innovation continue to drive change across strategic industries.

FAA Deputy Administrator Chris Rocheleau expects "valuable operational experience" from the program, with an eye on safe advanced air mobility.
Archer remains grouped with Joby and BETA under regulators, despite differences in their aircraft and business strategy.
Shares of the publicly traded eVTOL firms moved higher after the pilot program announcement.
Archer wrapped up 2025 holding roughly $1.96 billion in cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments.

facts: The automotive industry was the second-largest source of new jobs and investment, after advanced manufacturing.

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. announced that it received the certificate of occupancy for its high-volume manufacturing facility, ARC, earlier this week.

Adam Goldstein, Archer’s founder and CEO, said the completion is a testament to the industry shifting from R&D into commercialization.

Arnaud Deboeuf, Stellantis Chief Manufacturing and Supply Chain Officer, commented that supporting Archer’s electric aircraft growth with deep manufacturing expertise is a great opportunity for both companies.
ARC will produce Archer’s all-electric vertical take-off and landing aircraft, Midnight.
