facts: The Georgia Environmental Protection Division Air Protection Branch issued a public advisory for air quality permit applications for the week of July 21, 2025.
Johns Manville Corp. Winder Facility in Barrow County applied for Air Quality Permit No. 923756 to construct a new non-bonded insulation manufacturing line at 713 Bankhead Hwy, Winder, 30680.
Sewon America, Inc. in Effingham County applied for Air Quality Permit No. 29785 to construct and operate an E-coating process line at 1000 Sewon EV Rd, Rincon, 31326.
LX Hausys America, Inc. in Gordon County applied for Air Quality Permit No. 932921 to change emissions control paths for Process Line L001 - HiMACS at 310 LX Dr SE, Adairsville, 30103.

Lunawood, LLC in White County applied for Air Quality Permit No. 29794 to construct and operate a wood treatment facility with two thermo vacuum kiln systems at 176 Appalachian Trail Drive, Cleveland, 30528.
Hanwha Q Cells USA, Inc. in Whitfield County applied for Air Quality Permit No. 29791 to reclassify its status from synthetic minor to Title V major at 300 Nexus Dr, Dalton, 30721.

Chemical Products Corporation in Bartow County was proposed a renewal of its Title V Operating Permit (No. 616365) for its inorganic chemical manufacturing plant that processes barite ore and celestite ore at 102 Old Mill Rd, Cartersville, 301201692.
Milliken & Co Valway Plant in Troup County was proposed a renewal of its Title V Operating Permit (No. 690941) for its fabric finishing and coating facility at 1300 Fourth Ave, LaGrange, 30240.
Milliken & Co Valway Plant in Troup County was also proposed a Title V Significant Modification (No. 877082) to construct and operate two boilers rated at 21 MMBtu/hr each.

The Milliken & Co Valway Plant modification includes emission increases of +17.9 tpy NOx, +15.1 tpy CO, +1.0 tpy VOC, +1.36 tpy PM/PM10/PM2.5, +0.1 tpy SO2, +0.3 tpy single HAP (Hexane), and +0.3 tpy combined HAP.
The comment deadline for SIP public advisories is August 22, 2025.
The comment period/deadline for Milliken & Co Valway Plant permit renewal expires on August 25, 2025.
The comment period/deadline for Milliken & Co Valway Plant Title V significant modification expires on August 25, 2025.
Written comments can be submitted via email or by mail to the Air Permit Manager at 4244 International Parkway, Suite 120, Atlanta, Georgia 30354.
Draft permits and supporting materials are available for review at the EPD Air Protection Branch office in Atlanta by appointment only.
Final issued permits are available for public viewing online.
Further information can be obtained via email or phone contact with the agency.
Copies of draft permits and applications may also be requested by contacting Steve Allison.

entity_name: Alabama Electric Vehicle Charger and Medium- and Heavy-Duty Diesel Vehicle Replacement Rebates Program
canonical_name: Alabama Department of Economic and Community Affairs (ADECA) EV Charger and Diesel Replacement Rebate Program
facts: The Alabama Department of Economic and Community Affairs (ADECA) offers grants for light-duty EV chargers and the replacement of qualified medium- and heavy-duty diesel vehicles with new diesel or alternative fuel vehicles.

Grants are available for EV chargers; medium- and heavy-duty trucks; school, shuttle, and transit buses; freight switchers; airport ground support equipment; and forklifts and port cargo handling equipment.
Funding amounts for the rebates are based on vehicle type, fuel type, and applicant type.
The rebate program is funded by Alabama’s portion of the Volkswagen Environmental Mitigation Trust.
The state administers an excise tax on compressed natural gas (CNG) and liquefied natural gas (LNG) used as vehicle fuel.
CNG taxes are set at $0.13 per GGE from October 1, 2023, until September 30, 2028, and $0.18 per GGE from October 1, 2028 and beyond.
LNG taxes are set at $0.13 per DGE from October 1, 2023, until September 30, 2028, and $0.18 per DGE from October 1, 2028 and beyond.
A GGE is defined as equal to 5.66 pounds or 126.67 cubic feet of natural gas for CNG, and a DGE is equal to 6.06 lbs. for LNG.
The Alabama Legislature established a Green Fleets Review Committee and Green Fleets Policy outlining procurement procedures for state vehicles based on fuel economy and life cycle costing.

State fleet managers must submit annual plans reflecting a 4% annual increase in average fleet fuel economy for light-duty vehicles, a 3% annual increase for medium-duty vehicles, and a 2% annual increase for heavy-duty vehicles per fiscal year.
Government entities must manage fleets to be energy efficient, minimize emissions, and reduce petroleum dependency using proven technology identified by the Committee.
Any motor vehicle equipped with an auxiliary power unit or idle reduction technology may exceed gross, axle, tandem, or bridge formula weight limits by up to 400 pounds.

A vehicle primarily powered by natural gas may exceed state gross vehicle weight limits by a weight equal to the difference between its weight and a comparable diesel vehicle's weight, with a maximum gross weight of 82,000 lbs.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Natural Gas Laws and Incentives in Alabama

entity_name: Mine of the Future – Proving Ground Initiative
canonical_name: Mine of the Future – Proving Ground Initiative
facts: NOFO number is DE-FOA-0003390.
Funding Amount is $80,000,000.

On November 14, 2025, the U.S. Department of Energy’s Office of Fossil Energy announced up to $80 million in federal funding to establish Mine of the Future proving grounds for real-world testing of next-generation mining technologies.
The Department announced in August its intent to invest $1 billion to advance and scale mining, processing, and manufacturing technologies.
These actions will deliver on President Trump’s Executive Orders, Unleashing American Energy and Immediate Measures to Increase American Mineral Production.
The initiative aims to secure America’s critical material supply chain, increase domestic mineral production, reduce reliance on foreign sources, and strengthen U.S. energy independence.
Field sites are essential for minerals and mining stakeholders to test innovative technologies under real-world conditions.

Awards made under this NOFO will develop mining proving grounds and support research and development projects that accelerate technologies past laboratory and/or bench scale to testing and demonstration in a field-scale setting.
This field-scale testing is an essential step to de-risk commercial adoption of new mining technologies.

Projects selected under this initiative will create opportunities for hands-on workforce training to prepare the next generation of American miners and engineers to operate advanced mining systems safely and efficiently.
Eligible applicants include institutions of higher education, for- and non-profit entities, state and local governments, and Indian Tribes.
The funding opportunity covers one topic area: Development of Mining Technology Proving Grounds & One or Two Accelerated Mine Technology R&D Projects.
DOE expects to award up to $80 million in federal funding with a minimum of 20% cost-sharing from the awardees.
NOI Issue Date is 09/26/2025.
Funding Opportunity Issue Date is 11/14/2025.
Submission Deadline for Full Applications is 1/30/2026 at 5:00 PM ET.
Expected Date for Selection Notifications is February 2026.
Programs will advance innovative mining processes and technologies to help revitalize the domestic mining landscape and enhance U.S. mineral security.

The U.S. Department of Energy announced $355 million for two notices of funding opportunities to expand domestic production of critical materials essential for advancing U.S. energy production, manufacturing, transportation and national defense.
source_title: Funding Notice: Mine of the Future – Proving Ground Initiative | Department of Energy

facts: Novelis Inc. announced it will invest $365 million to build a highly advanced recycling center for automotive in North America.
The new recycling facility will have an annual casting capacity of 240 kt of sheet ingot.
The facility is expected to reduce the company's carbon emissions by more than one million tons each year.
The new recycling facility will be built adjacent to Novelis' existing automotive finishing plant in Guthrie, Kentucky.
The center will enable Novelis to grow its closed-loop-recycling programs with more automotive customers in North America.
Through closed-loop recycling, the company takes back aluminum remaining after automotive parts are stamped from sheets and remakes it into the same product for new vehicle production.
The center will also have the capability to process aluminum from vehicles at the end of their lifecycle.
Using recycled aluminum as input material requires only 5 percent of the energy used to make primary aluminum, thus avoiding 95 percent of the carbon emissions associated with production.

Steve Fisher, President and CEO of Novelis, Inc., stated that Novelis aims to be the world's leading provider of low-carbon, sustainable aluminum solutions that advance business, industry and society toward the benefits of a circular economy.
Through this investment, Novelis will continue to increase the amount of recycled content in its products, reducing CO2 emissions and moving closer to carbon neutrality.
The new recycling center will be equipped with industry-leading processes including advanced shredding and sorting technology, as well as energy-efficient innovations.
Novelis has a sustainability goal to reduce energy intensity by 10 percent by 2026.
Groundbreaking for the new recycling center is scheduled for early 2022.
Commissioning of the facility is expected in 2024.
When fully operational, the expansion will add approximately 140 new jobs in Guthrie.
Novelis held the grand opening of its automotive finishing plant in Guthrie in October, which currently employs 150 people and will grow to 190 over the next two years.

Tom Boney, Executive Vice President and President of Novelis North America, stated that Kentucky has been a great business partner with a strong, highly skilled workforce to meet the growing need for high-strength, low-carbon aluminum.
Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear stated that companies are committed to creating quality job opportunities for Kentucky families as Western Kentucky builds back.

Gov. Beshear noted he joined Novelis for the grand opening of its automotive aluminum finishing plant just over two months ago and praised the leadership's decision to quickly move forward with expansion plans.
Gov. Beshear stated that Novelis is proving to be a great fit for Todd County and the surrounding area.
Novelis has a 37-year history of creating jobs in Kentucky and investing in local communities, employing 1,600 people in the state.
In addition to the Guthrie automotive finishing facility, Novelis operates an aluminum beverage can recycling plant in Berea and the Logan Aluminum joint venture in Russellville.
Novelis is a global leader in the production of innovative aluminum products and solutions and the world's largest recycler of aluminum.

Novelis aims to be the leading provider of low-carbon, sustainable aluminum solutions and achieve a fully circular economy by partnering with suppliers and customers in aerospace, automotive, beverage can, and specialties industries across North America, Europe, Asia, and South America.
Novelis had net sales of $12.3 billion in fiscal year 2021.

Novelis is a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Limited, an industry leader in aluminum and copper, and the metals flagship company of the Aditya Birla Group, a multinational conglomerate based in Mumbai.
source_title: Novelis to Build $365 Million Recycling Center to Support North American Automotive Customers and Reduce Carbon Emissions by More Than One Million Tons a Year

facts: On September 21, 2023, Governor Brian P. Kemp accepted the James A. Van Fleet Award at The Korea Society’s 2023 Annual Dinner in New York, NY on behalf of the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD).

The General James A. Van Fleet Award was established in 1992 and is awarded annually to recognize outstanding contributions to U.S.-Korea relations, being one of the most prestigious awards in the field.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that his first economic development mission as governor was to Korea.
Georgia is the first state to receive this honor alongside former U.S. presidents, diplomats, and business icons who have been presented with the award in previous years.
Alongside GDEcD, the Korea International Trade Association (KITA) also received a 2023 Van Fleet Award.

Previous awardees include former U.S. Presidents George W. Bush, George H.W. Bush, and Jimmy Carter; former South Korean President Kim Dae-Jung; former Secretaries of State Colin Powell and Henry Kissinger; General Raymond G. Davis; organizations including The Korea Chamber of Commerce and Industry, LG, and General Motors; and corporate leaders like Hyundai Chairman Mong-Koo Chung, Samsung Chairman Kun-Hee Lee, SK Chairman Tae-won Chey, and CJ Group Chairman Kyung-Shik Sohn.
The Korea Society is a nonprofit organization that has promoted greater awareness, understanding, and cooperation between the U.S. and Korea for over 65 years.

Thomas Byrne, President and CEO of The Korea Society, stated that both awardees are serving to enhance trade and investment between the two countries, creating high-paying jobs in American communities and securing supply chains in critical technologies.
To advance statewide business development opportunities, GDEcD opened an office in Korea in 1985.
At least 144 facilities with Korean parent companies operate in the state of Georgia.
For the last three years prior to the announcement, Korea has committed to creating more jobs in Georgia than any other country through foreign direct investment.
Over the last decade, Korean investment has exceeded $23.6 billion through economic development projects in Georgia.
Total trade between Georgia and Korea reached $13 billion in 2022.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Georgia’s long-term commitment to diplomatic, cultural, and business engagement in Korea has supported transformative growth over decades, providing economic benefits for both countries.

Governor Kemp and the State of Georgia were introduced at the event by José Muñoz, President and COO of Hyundai Motor Company and President and CEO of Hyundai and Genesis Motor North America, as well as Stuart Countess, Kia Georgia President and CEO.
Billions of additional investment dollars and tens of thousands of Georgia jobs in the mobility industry are a result of investments from Hyundai and Kia.
Firms from Korea that operate in Georgia have helped to create one of the most advanced statewide manufacturing hubs in the U.S.
Korean companies operating in Georgia span diverse industries, including SK Group, CJ Foodville, Kumho Tire, LG, POSCO, and Hanwha Qcells.
Korea has maintained a Consular presence in the State of Georgia since 1976.
Three Georgia communities have sister city agreements with South Korea: Atlanta with Daegu; Cobb County with Seongdong-Gu; and Gwinnett County with Gangnam-Gu.
Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport offers 12 weekly direct flights from Atlanta to Seoul, South Korea through Delta Air Lines and Korean Air.
There are six international cargo flights per week between Incheon, South Korea, and Atlanta.
The Georgia Ports Authority (GPA) in Savannah has an established relationship with ports of Korea and offers seven regular shipping services to the Port of Busan.
GPA’s Brunswick Port offers shipping services to Incheon, Masan, Pyongtaek, and Kunsan.
The Van Fleet Award is named for General James A. Van Fleet, commander of the U.S. Eighth Army at the height of the Korean War in 1951.
Beginning in 1957, General Van Fleet served as the first president of The Korea Society.

GDEcD serves as the state’s sales and marketing arm and the lead agency for attracting new business investment, encouraging expansion of existing industry, locating new markets, attracting tourists, promoting arts/film/digital entertainment, and planning/mobilizing state resources for economic development.
source_title: Governor Kemp Accepts James A. Van Fleet Award from The Korea Society | Georgia

facts: Oneda Corporation is a third-generation family-owned contract precision metal stamping manufacturer based in Columbus, Georgia.
The company was founded in 1989.
Oneda Corporation is a subsidiary of Japan-based Oneda Electric Corp.
The Columbus location has 67 employees.
The company primarily serves the automotive and heating and air conditioning industries.
Leaders at Oneda Corporation recognized the need to increase the company’s online presence and reputation to continue expanding the business.
The company worked with the Georgia Manufacturing Extension Partnership (GaMEP) at Georgia Tech, part of the MEP National Network™, on other projects.
GaMEP now offers marketing services to its manufacturing clients.
Michael Brooks serves as Oneda’s manager of marketing, customer service, and logistics.
Katie Takacs is the group manager for marketing at GaMEP.
Takacs worked with Oneda to develop and implement a strategic marketing plan, including goals, tactics to meet the goals, and a system to track progress on the plan.
GaMEP added services including implementation, strategizing about email and social media, writing content for those platforms, and redesigning the website.
The project entailed writing a tip sheet to generate leads, writing customer success stories, and training Brooks on the development of content so that he could manage it.

Michael Brooks stated, 'If you're not on social media, and you're not keeping up with that, you will lose your audience,' noting that younger audiences use websites and social postings to evaluate business relationships before contacting them.
Following the launch of the website and revamped LinkedIn page, GaMEP identified 100 companies and purchasing managers in eight states as potential new customers for the company.
GaMEP created a three-step lead generation campaign to reach those potential customers.
Oneda engaged the GaMEP team on an energy assessment analysis that led to a decrease in energy consumption by the company.
The website was so well received internally that parent company Oneda Electric Corp. is now working to upgrade its own site.
source_title: Manufacturing New Market Share | NIST

entity_name: Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America
canonical_name: Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America
facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America is a tier-one automotive supplier.
On Nov. 28, 2023, at AWS re:Invent, Panasonic Automotive announced the availability of Virtual SkipGen™ (vSkipGen™) on AWS Marketplace.
vSkipGen is a virtual replica of the physical, 3rd generation Digital Cockpit solution known as SkipGen.
The platform enables automakers to 'shift left' the automotive development lifecycle, allowing engineers and developers to begin work earlier without requiring physical hardware.
vSkipGen decouples software development from the hardware development lifecycle to reduce time to market and help improve software quality.
The solution leverages industry standard VirtIO-based device virtualization technology.
It offers fully optimized support for Android Automotive for infotainment and Automotive Grade Linux for cluster vehicle domains.
The platform supports needed automotive peripherals and sensor simulation for the development of modern cockpit systems.
The virtual platform is cloud-native and utilizes cloud servers' computing capability to provide hardware acceleration for multimedia, graphic, audio, and display rendering.
Panasonic and Amazon maintain an ongoing collaboration to evolve the eCockpit.
Andrew Poliak serves as chief technology officer at Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America.

Poliak stated that the partnership with AWS to run cockpit domains as virtual machines in the cloud is critical to ensure development teams can seamlessly work in parallel, enable 3rd party and app development, and conduct more predictive integration and testing earlier in the process.
Panasonic has invested in advancing the software defined vehicle to support market growth and its GREEN IMPACT mission.
The solution reflects Panasonic's environmental goals by eliminating the need for a development or prototype hardware platform, thereby saving resources and components.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America is headquartered in Peachtree City, Georgia.
The company maintains sales, marketing, and engineering operations in Farmington Hills, Michigan.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America operates as a division company of Panasonic Corporation of North America.
Panasonic Corporation of North America is based in Newark, New Jersey.
Panasonic Corporation of North America serves as the principal North American subsidiary of Osaka, Japan-based Panasonic Corporation.
source_title: Panasonic Automotive Introduces Virtual SkipGen, Now Available on AWS Marketplace

facts: Pirelli North America launched its first closed-loop circular recycling initiative to increase the use of recycled and bio-based materials in its products.
The project is executed in collaboration with Bolder Industries.
The initiative focuses on recovering scrap tires generated during the manufacturing process in North America.
Scrap tire materials are collected and processed by Bolder Industries using pyrolysis technology.
The processing produces ISCC PLUS-certified BolderBlack® recovered carbon black (rCB).
Pirelli reuses the recovered carbon black in the North America production of new tires.

The project is part of a broader Pirelli plan that will involve additional facilities to create industrial ecosystems capable of valorizing waste into recovered materials for reintroduction across the Group's production network.
BolderBlack can partially replace virgin carbon black in tire production, contributing to more efficient resource use.
The initiative was recognized by the Tire Recycling Foundation with a Circle of Change Award in the Value Chain Collaboration category.
Claudio Zanardo, CEO of Pirelli North America, stated that the Rome plant is one of the most technologically advanced manufacturing facilities in Pirelli.
Zanardo noted the initiative reflects an approach focused on increasing recovered materials within existing production processes while maintaining consistency in performance and quality.

Tony Wibbeler, CEO of Bolder Industries, stated that the collaboration demonstrates a traceable, mass-balance approach to tire-to-tire circularity that is achievable and ready to scale inside a premium manufacturing environment.
The initiative aligns with Pirelli's broader target to reach over 80% bio-based and recycled materials in its best products available on the market by 2030.
Pirelli also targets reaching 40% bio-based and recycled materials in its total production by 2030.
The project centers on Pirelli's manufacturing facility located in Rome, Ga.
source_title: PIRELLI NORTH AMERICA LAUNCHES THE GROUP'S FIRST CLOSED-LOOP RECYCLING INITIATIVE, TURNING SCRAP TIRES INTO NEW TIRES

facts: SK Battery America is actively seeking dedicated individuals with strong work ethics and commitment to excellence for its manufacturing team.
The company offers career opportunities ranging from various production levels to administrative positions.
SK Battery America values a strategic mindset, strong leadership capabilities, and a passion for excellence in its employees.
Employees at SK Battery America have the opportunity to take on challenging projects, make critical decisions, and shape the direction of the organization.
The company's Electrode team creates anode and cathode components for batteries starting from raw materials.
The Assembly team combines electrode components into pretested battery cells.
The Formation team finalizes battery cell production by subjecting each cell to rigorous aging and inspection processes.
The Module team assembles individual battery cells into larger battery modules and prepares those modules for shipment to clients.
source_title: Career Opportunities < Careers < SK battery America

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy announced $8,055,000 to support seven projects that will advance efficient, scalable, high-throughput, and high-quality processes for manufacturing fuel cell and electrolyzer materials through the Roll-to-Roll (R2R) Consortium.
The R2R Consortium focuses on developing efficient processes to reduce the cost of manufacturing electrolyzers and fuel cells.
Funding for the seven projects is provided through the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act.
Nel Hydrogen received $1.5 million in federal funding to develop a roll-to-roll process for alkaline exchange membrane water electrolyzers.
Solvay Specialty Polymers received $1.5 million in federal funding to accelerate the scale up of hydrocarbon ionomer manufacturing.
Power to Hydrogen received $1 million in federal funding to develop an ink formulation and roll-to-roll coating process for manufacturing of cathodes.
FOM Technologies received $755,000 in federal funding to develop slot-die and drying systems optimized for roll-to-roll coating of fuel cell membrane electrode assemblies.
Ionomr Innovations received $1 million in federal funding to improve and scale up ionomer process chemistry and membrane production methods.
Pajarito Powder received $1 million in federal funding to develop and validate a simplified one-pot, solvent-free synthesis process for manufacturing engineered carbon supports.
Kraton Corporation received $1.3 million in federal funding to enable commercial-scale production of low-cost, high performance alkaline and proton exchange membranes.
The funding supports DOE's Hydrogen Shot—part of DOE's Energy Earthshots Initiative—which aims to reduce the cost of producing hydrogen to $1 per kilogram.

These efforts will complement other DOE-funded manufacturing and recycling efforts, helping to fast-track the growth of the hydrogen economy as laid out in the U.S. National Hydrogen Strategy and Roadmap.
Projects will be led by private sector entities partnering with at least two national laboratories through the R2R Consortium.
Projects will span approximately two years in duration, with industry partners providing at least 50% cost sharing.

The R2R Consortium comprises the National Renewable Energy Laboratory, Argonne National Laboratory, Oak Ridge National Laboratory, Sandia National Laboratories, and Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory.
source_title: U.S. Department of Energy Announces $8 Million for Projects to Advance Electrolyzer and Fuel Cell Manufacturing RD&D through National Lab Consortium | Department of Energy

facts: Stryten Energy LLC is a U.S.-based energy storage solutions provider headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia.
The company offers advanced vanadium redox flow battery (VRFB) technology which provides medium and long-duration energy storage (LDES).
VRFB technology helps ensure grid stability and facilitates increased utilization of solar and wind for businesses and consumers across the U.S.

The Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) includes vital Investment Tax Credits (ITC) for domestically sourced and manufactured standalone clean energy storage intended to lower costs, increase energy efficiency and strengthen energy security for America.

Tim Vargo, Chief Executive Officer of Stryten Energy, welcomed the legislation’s long-term, standalone energy storage investment tax credits and its ten-year runway, noting it will help customers incorporate medium and long-duration storage such as VRFB batteries into their operations more economically.

Vargo stated that leveraging domestic VFRB technology and other long-term energy storage solutions will enable reliable access to clean power and help the U.S. achieve energy security as it transitions to a clean energy economy.

Scott Childers, Vice President of Stryten Energy’s Essential Power Division, stated that Stryten’s patented flow battery technology is uniquely positioned to provide the safe, long-term, reliable on-demand energy needed to build a new economy powered by clean energy.

Childers added that the Inflation Reduction Act is expected to accelerate the demand for domestically sourced energy storage, especially for commercial applications such as charging stations, manufacturing plants and businesses with 24/7 energy requirements.
Stryten’s VRFB technology uses a domestically sourced vanadium electrolyte that is easily recycled.
The VRFB technology provides increased power density and reduced battery downtime.
The technology offers the potential of limitless cycle life with proper maintenance.
The batteries feature high-temperature ratings and are thermally safe.
The VRFB system is scalable to meet varying energy storage capacity needs.
Stryten’s VRFB technology is ideal for applications that require more than four hours of storage capacity to deploy clean energy on demand from renewable sources such as solar and wind energy.
Target applications for the technology include utility-scale storage, microgrids and military bases.
Stryten Energy helps solve energy challenges with a broad range of solutions across the Essential Power, Motive Power, Transportation, Military and Government sectors.
The company partners with some of the world’s most recognized companies to meet the growing demand for reliable and sustainable energy storage capacity.
Stryten powers applications ranging from submarines to subcompacts, microgrids, warehouses, distribution centers, cars, trains and trucks.
The company’s stored energy technologies include advanced lead, lithium and vanadium redox flow batteries, intelligent chargers and energy performance management software.

facts: Bonnell Aluminum is a subsidiary of Tredegar Corporation (NYSE: TG).
Bonnell Aluminum commends the Biden administration’s proclamation to strengthen the rules to qualify for trade benefits between the U.S. and Mexico.
Under the proclamation, aluminum must meet a 'smelted and cast' requirement to qualify for relief from the Section 232 Tariff.
The proclamation prohibits preferential treatment for imports where the primary country of smelt, secondary country of smelt, or country of most recent cast was Belarus, China, Russia, or Iran.
This negotiated requirement recognizes unfair trade practices where U.S. trading partners process subsidized metal from countries like China and Russia.

Georgia Power’s Economic Development team started 2026 with strong momentum, helping companies relocate or expand in Georgia and deliver more than 3,200 jobs and $3 billion in capital investment despite a cooling national economy.

Project activity remains above average, reflecting Georgia’s competitiveness for large-scale, energy intensive manufacturing.

HMGMA’s American-made electric vehicles will land at U.S. dealerships before the end of the year.

entity_name: Joint Office of Energy and Transportation
canonical_name: Joint Office of Energy and Transportation
facts: The Joint Office has electric transportation experts available to help answer questions on alternative fuel vehicles and fueling infrastructure, including EV charging.

The Joint Office is tasked with helping to ensure that individual efforts in the National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure (NEVI) Formula Program, Charging and Fueling Infrastructure (CFI) Discretionary Grant Program, and other related grant programs contribute toward a convenient, reliable, and affordable national EV charging network.
EV charging projects developed under the NEVI Formula Program and CFI Discretionary Grant Program must comply with minimum standards for federally funded EV charging infrastructure projects.
EV charging projects developed under the NEVI Formula Program and CFI Discretionary Grant Program must comply with the Build America, Buy America implementation plan for EV charging equipment.
EV charging projects developed under the NEVI Formula Program and CFI Discretionary Grant Program must comply with the NEVI Formula Program Interim Final Guidance, updated 8/11/2025.
The Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (IIJA) established the NEVI Formula Program.

The NEVI Formula Program provides funding to states to deploy electric vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure and establish an interconnected network to facilitate data collection, convenience, and reliability.

Under the NEVI program, each state is required to submit an annual EV Infrastructure Deployment Plan that describes how the state intends to use its apportioned NEVI Formula Program funds in accordance with federal guidance.

The Charging and Fueling Infrastructure (CFI) Discretionary Grant Program from the Federal Highway Administration provides funding to strategically deploy public EV charging and hydrogen, propane, and natural gas refueling infrastructure.

Created by IIJA, the CFI Program provides two funding categories of grants: Community Charging and Alternative Fueling Grants (Community Program) and Charging and Alternative Fuel Corridor Grants (Corridor Program).
The Alternative Fuel Life-Cycle Environmental and Economic Transportation (AFLEET) CFI Emissions Tool is a resource to guide the buildout of alternative fueling infrastructure.
The AFLEET CFI Emissions Tool can assess estimated emissions reductions from EV charging infrastructure and hydrogen, propane, and natural gas fueling infrastructure along designated AFCs.

As part of technical assistance resources, the Joint Office connects states with critical data and tools to understand where EV charging and alternative fueling infrastructure is installed and where existing AFCs are designated.
The Alternative Fueling Station Locator can be used to find existing EV charging and alternative fueling infrastructure.
The Alternative Fuel Corridors website can be used to learn about AFCs and see existing corridors.
The Station Data for Alternative Fuel Corridors tool allows users to download data and analyze stations that meet the criteria for corridors and view potential EV charging and AFCs.
Contact information for technical assistance on EV chargers and alternative fueling infrastructure is available through the Joint Office.

The Alternative Fuels Data Center provides resources and connects users with seasoned experts to answer technical questions about alternative fuels, advanced vehicles, and alternative fueling infrastructure.
The Public EV Charging Infrastructure Playbook provides interactive resources to help communities and other stakeholders plan and build infrastructure to support a modern transportation system.
EV Charging Community Readiness provides a summary of resources, tools, and publications designed to help communities prepare and plan for the expansion of EVs and charging infrastructure.

Planning and Zoning Guidance for Electric Vehicle Charger Deployment is a guidance document published by Rocky Mountain Institute, along with the Interstate Renewable Energy Council and the Sustainable Energy Action Committee to support local code officials with planning and zoning for EV charger deployment.
The EV States Clearinghouse serves as a repository for state EV program documents, current state-level EV roadmaps, and other resources to support EV infrastructure program development.
source_title: Technical Assistance and Resources for States · Joint Office of Energy and Transportation

entity_name: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution Battery Cell Manufacturing Joint Venture
canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution Joint Venture
facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution (LGES) will invest an additional $2 billion in their battery cell manufacturing joint venture at the Metaplant in Bryan County, raising the JV’s total investment value to more than $4.3 billion.
This expansion will create another 400 new jobs.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that in a single year, they broke ground on the largest project in state history, landed multiple suppliers across the state for Hyundai’s Metaplant, and welcomed LGES to Bryan County.
Governor Kemp aims to make Georgia the e-mobility capital of the nation through these investments.

Governor Kemp noted that major investments ultimately go to hardworking Georgians in the form of paychecks, improved schools and infrastructure, and thanked Hyundai Motor Group and LGES for recognizing Georgia as the No. 1 state for business.

José Muñoz, president and global COO of Hyundai Motor Company and president and CEO of Hyundai and Genesis Motor North America, stated the incremental investment reflects a continued commitment to create a more sustainable future powered by American workers.

José Muñoz expressed pride in partnering with LGES and gratitude for the support of Governor Kemp and Georgia communities in driving operations as a global leader in the electrified mobility industry.

In May 2023, Hyundai Motor Group and LGES signed a memorandum of understanding establishing LGES as the partner for Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America’s onsite battery cell manufacturing joint venture.
With this announcement, the EV manufacturing facility and the battery joint venture represent an estimated $7.59 billion in investment.
The combined projects will create 8,500 new jobs for the region over the next eight years.

Dong-Myung Kim, president and head of the Advanced Automotive Battery Division of LG Energy Solution, stated the investment underscores a dedication to driving America’s EV transition while bolstering the local economy through quality jobs.
Dong-Myung Kim thanked the State of Georgia for its support and expressed excitement to bring top-quality products and operational experience to grow with local communities.
The 30 GWh facility will be able to support the production of 300,000 units of EVs annually at full operations.
Hyundai Mobis will assemble battery packs using cells from the plant and supply them to Hyundai Motor Group’s U.S. manufacturing facilities for production of Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis EV models.
Carter Infinger, Chairman of the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority, stated the announcement brings 400 additional well-paying jobs for those in the Savannah region.
Carter Infinger noted that continued investment by Hyundai Motor Group and announced suppliers like LGES will be transformative for the region.

Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Global Commerce team on this competitive project in partnership with the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority, Georgia Power, Georgia Ports Authority, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated Governor Kemp set a goal of Georgia becoming the top location for EV manufacturing in the United States.
Pat Wilson highlighted Georgia’s focus on building an ecosystem that supports the entire EV supply chain to bring jobs home to Georgians statewide.

Pat Wilson credited state and community partners like the Savannah-area JDA, utility providers, Georgia Ports Authority, and Georgia Quick Start for creating a collaborative, business-friendly environment.
Since 2018, Georgia has pursued job creation along the entire EV supply chain, resulting in more than $25 billion in investments and the creation of over 30,000 jobs.
Battery related projects have accounted for at least $11 billion in investment in Georgia.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent when compared to FY21.
source_title: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution to Invest Additional $2B in Bryan County – Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority

facts: Georgia Tech was named one of sixty regional coalition finalists for the Build Back Better Regional Challenge administered by the U.S. Economic Development Administration.
The project is called the Georgia AI Manufacturing Pilot Facility and Technology Corridor, abbreviated as “GA-AIM.”
GA-AIM will be used for technical assistance to create the Georgia Artificial Intelligence Manufacturing Corridor.
The project is led by Thomas Kurfess and Aaron Stebner in the George W. Woodruff School of Mechanical Engineering at Georgia Tech.
GA-AIM aims to fill existing technology gaps and build a technological opportunity framework that includes underrepresented communities and rural Georgia counties.
The initiative seeks to better secure Georgia’s manufacturing infrastructure.

Georgia Tech’s partners in the effort include the Russell Innovation Center for Entrepreneurs, Spelman College, the Technical College System of Georgia, and the Georgia Department of Economic Development.
Each of the sixty regional coalition finalists will compete for up to $100 million in American Rescue Plan Funding.
The finalists have proposed projects that will develop or scale industry sectors, develop and train the workforce of today, and build resilient economies.
Phase 2 of the Challenge will award 20-30 regional coalitions up to $100 million.
The GA-AIM project was selected from a pool of 529 applicants.
GA-AIM will receive an initial grant of approximately $500,000 to develop the proposed project further.
These initial grants will help finalists take projects to the next level and position non-awardees to find new partners and funding sources.
The total GA-AIM project is valued at $30 million.
Phase II matching funds for the $30 million project include a $2M contribution from Georgia state entities GDEcD and TCSG.

Georgia Tech will contribute investments valued at $5M, including Career Grants, Technology Incubation Grants, leadership and staff salaries and benefits toward the program, facility investments, capital planning, and management for the AMPF renovation.
An industry cost share of $8M will complete the application matching fund requirement.

Alejandra Y. Castillo, Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Economic Development, stated that the EDA is helping communities work together in coalitions to craft ambitious plans to rebuild communities and tackle challenges related to climate change, manufacturing, and supply chains.
source_title: Georgia Tech Named a Finalist in the $1B Build Back Better Regional Challenge

Garrick Ward started his career at JTEKT North America in 2000 as an Industrial Engineer at JTEKT’s Koyo bearings facility in Sylvania, Georgia.

Louisiana Araujo took business strategy and Human Resources courses at Clemson University.

Shannon Garrett began her career as a Principal Financial Analyst in 2012 and was promoted to Compliance Manager in 2017.
JTEKT has employees that have been with the company for more than 40 years, reflecting its value of loyalty.
Shannon Garrett noted JTEKT is becoming active in the Greenville community through various committees and activities.

facts: JTEKT North American Corporation issued a WARN Notice on Dec. 4.
The company intends to permanently close its manufacturing plant located in Telford, Tennessee.
The closure will affect 136 Tennessee employees.
Plant closure is scheduled to begin “on or around April 30, 2026” continuing through August 31, 2026.
The Northeast Workforce Development Board’s rapid response team, employed by the First Tennessee Development District, was notified to coordinate services with the employer and affected employees.
JTEKT North American Corporation describes itself as a “global leader in engineering and manufacturing automotive systems.”
The company also works in bearings and machine tools.
JTEKT employs 5,000 people across 23 facilities globally.
Two of JTEKT's facilities are located in Morristown and Vonore, Tennessee.
source_title: East Tennessee manufacturing plant closing, affecting more than 100 workers

canonical_name: Element Six Synthetic Diamond Grit Plant
facts: Federal officials announced a Japanese-financed synthetic industrial diamond manufacturing facility will be built in Georgia under the first round of projects tied to a sweeping $550 billion investment pledge between the two countries.
The investment package, totaling roughly $36 billion, also includes a massive natural gas power plant in Ohio and a crude oil export terminal in Texas.
Georgia's win comes in the form of a new advanced manufacturing plant aimed at producing synthetic diamond grit, a critical material for the semiconductor, automotive, and energy industries.
According to the U.S. Department of Commerce, the Georgia facility is expected to cost about $600 million.
The facility will be operated by Element Six, a unit of the global diamond company De Beers.

The plant is designed to meet 100 percent of U.S. demand for high-pressure, high-temperature synthetic diamond grit used in industrial cutting, semiconductor fabrication, and other advanced manufacturing applications.
Officials say the project will bolster domestic supply chains that have long relied on overseas sources, particularly China, for critical materials.
Economic development leaders in Georgia are already touting the project as a potential job creator and long-term economic asset.
Specific job estimates for the plant have not been released.

Officials say the plant could bring high-skilled manufacturing and industrial technology jobs to the state, complementing Georgia's existing strengths in logistics, automotive supply chains, and advanced manufacturing.

The investment announcements follow a landmark deal between the United States and Japan under which Tokyo agreed to invest up to $550 billion in U.S. projects as part of a broader trade and economic framework.
The arrangement also included a move to reduce U.S. tariffs on most Japanese imports to 15%, a key concession that helped unlock the investment commitments.

Secretary of Commerce Howard Lutnick said in published statements that "we will no longer rely on foreign supply for this essential material" and that the projects will create "thousands of high-quality American jobs."
Trump highlighted the project on Truth Social, stating, "The scale of these projects are so large, and could not be done without one very special word, TARIFFS."
source_title: Georgia to get new Japan-backed synthetic diamond grit plant in first major investment under U.S.–Japan trade deal - CBS Atlanta

facts: Textron Inc. announced its intent to separate its Industrial segment from its core aerospace and defense businesses to enhance strategic focus and drive long-term stakeholder value.
The Industrial segment is composed of Kautex and Textron Specialized Vehicles.
Textron intends to explore multiple paths for the separation, including a sale of the Industrial businesses or a tax-free separation into a standalone, publicly traded company.
Following the separation, New Textron will become a pure-play aerospace and defense company aligned with its core franchises: Textron Aviation, Bell, and Textron Systems.
Lisa M. Atherton serves as Chief Executive Officer of Textron.
Scott C. Donnelly serves as Textron's Executive Chairman.
New Textron expects over $12 billion in expected 2026 revenues and $19 billion in backlog post-separation.

New Textron will be anchored by general aviation under the Cessna and Beechcraft brands, military and commercial rotorcraft under the Bell brand, and Textron Systems' aerospace and defense products and services.
The Industrial segment expects over $3 billion in expected 2026 revenues post-separation.
Kautex is a leader in plastic fuel systems, battery enclosures, and clear-vision systems for the automotive industry.
Textron Specialized Vehicles manufactures specialized vehicles and equipment with globally recognized brands including E-Z-GO, PACE Technologies, Jacobsen, and TUG Technologies.
The company is targeting completion of the separation within 12 to 18 months, subject to customary conditions including regulatory approvals and final board approval.
Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC is serving as financial advisor to Textron for the transaction.
Latham & Watkins LLP is serving as legal advisor to Textron for the transaction.

facts: The feasibility of plastic enclosures for high-voltage batteries in electric vehicles has been proven.
A technology demonstrator made from polyamide 6 passes all important mechanical and thermal tests.
Prototype testing is being conducted in an electric test vehicle.
Development projects for series production with automotive manufacturers are being launched.
LANXESS was responsible for the material development of the jointly developed technology demonstrator.
Kautex Textron was responsible for the engineering, design, and manufacturing process of the demonstrator.
Dr. Christopher Hoefs is the Project Manager e-Powertrain at LANXESS.
The near-series demonstrator passes all mechanical and thermal tests that are relevant for such enclosures.
Solutions for the thermal management and leak tightness of the enclosure have been developed.
An enclosure prototype is being road tested in a test vehicle to verify its suitability for daily use.
LANXESS and Kautex Textron are currently jointly tackling the first series-production development projects with automotive manufacturers.
Felix Haas is the Director Product Development at Kautex Textron.
Calculations revealed that the carbon footprint of the plastic enclosure is over 40 percent smaller compared to an aluminum design.
The lower energy use in the production of polyamide 6 compared with metal helps minimize the carbon footprint.
The omission of time-consuming cathodic dip painting to prevent corrosion where steel is used helps minimize the carbon footprint.
The thermoplastic component design makes recycling the enclosure easier compared with thermoset materials such as sheet molding compounds (SMC).

Tests on the technology demonstrator were carried out in accordance with internationally recognized standards for battery-powered electric vehicles such as ECE R100 from the Economic Commission for Europe.
Tests also followed the Chinese standard GB 38031.
The large-format all-plastic enclosure measures around 1,400 millimeters in both length and width.
The enclosure meets the requirements of the mechanical shock test, which is used to examine the component’s behavior in the event of severe shocks.
The enclosure meets the requirements of the crush test, which examines the resistance of the battery enclosure in the event of slow deformation.
Results of the drop and vibration tests were positive.
Results of the bottom impact test, which examines the stability of the batteries in the event of ground contact or impacts from sizeable stones, were positive.
All test results corroborate the previous simulations and calculations.
A critical failure of the plastic enclosure would not have occurred in any of the load cases.
The demonstrator proved its resistance to external sources of fire underneath the vehicle in accordance with ECE R100 (external fire).
The demonstrator was developed based on the aluminum battery housing of a mid-size electric vehicle and designed for mass production.
It is manufactured in a single-stage compression molding process with a molding compound based on the polyamide 6 compound Durethan B24CMH2.0 from LANXESS.
The manufacturing process does not require any further rework.
Crash-relevant areas are specially reinforced with locally placed blanks made from the continuous-fiber-reinforced, polyamide 6-based composite Tepex dynalite 102-RGUD600.
Compared with an aluminum design, there is a weight saving of around 10 percent.
The weight saving is advantageous for the range and therefore the carbon footprint of the vehicle.
Integration of functions such as fasteners, reinforcing ribs, and components for thermal management reduces the number of individual components significantly compared with the metal design.
Reducing the number of individual components simplifies assembly and logistical effort and reduces manufacturing costs.
LANXESS is a leading specialty chemicals company.
LANXESS had sales of EUR 6.8 billion in 2019.
LANXESS currently has about 14,400 employees in 33 countries.
The core business of LANXESS is the development, manufacturing, and marketing of chemical intermediates, additives, specialty chemicals, and plastics.
LANXESS is listed in the leading sustainability indices Dow Jones Sustainability Index (DJSI World and Europe) and FTSE4Good.
source_title: Kautex Textron and LANXESS Introduce Large Battery Enclosures Made from Plastic Series-ready - Manufacturing / Robotics - LANXESS | NewswireToday

facts: Kautex Textron GmbH & Co. KG (Kautex), a Textron Inc. company, announced it has received an order from a leading automotive OEM for a full-BEV thermoplastic composite lower battery housing unit.
The lower housing is part of the company's Pentatonic battery enclosure portfolio, facilitating battery electric and hybrid electric vehicle production.
The Kautex Pentatonic product line offers lightweight, customizable EV solutions made from thermoplastic composite or composite metal hybrids.

These solutions include products which not only enhance the thermal management of the battery pack but also contribute to the overall lightweight design, boosting vehicle performance and extending driving range.

The lower housing seals and protects the vehicle battery while meeting extensive structural and stringent thermal requirements, such as crush and bottom impact testing, and external and internal fire tests.
It will be produced for a major BEV platform, spanning across multiple vehicle brands.

This Kautex Pentatonic solution offers advantages over its conventional metal counterparts, with lower cost through a reduction in components due to its high functional integration and thus a leaner bill of material.
The thermoplastic composite material helps to lower the carbon footprint of the part, while the lightweight housing contributes to reducing carbon emissions while driving.
This multi-material approach provides corrosion resistance with no compromise in performance throughout the lifespan of the vehicle.

Joerg Rautenstrauch, President & CEO of Kautex, stated that the order marks a significant milestone for the company and reflects their dedication to providing cutting-edge solutions that meet the highest standards of safety, performance, and sustainability.
This order marks Kautex's second Pentatonic business win, following its initial success in January 2023 with a full-BEV underbody protection skid plate that entered serial production in 2024.
Pentatonic has garnered numerous awards, including the SPE Grand Innovation Award in Europe, as well as the Lingxuan and Gasgoo Innovation Awards in China.
Kautex has deployed three on-road test vehicles in the US, Japan and Europe, validating the performance of full-BEV and hybrid Pentatonic enclosures across various terrains and weather conditions.
Collectively, these vehicles have been road-tested for over 100,000 miles.

As a Tier One automotive supplier with 30 plants in 13 countries, Kautex is a pioneer in the design and manufacture of traditional and hybrid fuel systems, engine camshafts and plastic industrial packaging solutions.

For the era of new mobility, Kautex is also expanding its portfolio to offer thermoplastic composite and composite-metal hybrid battery systems as well as assisted and autonomous drive vehicle cleaning systems.
Textron Inc. is a multi-industry company that leverages its global network of aircraft, defense, industrial and finance businesses to provide customers with innovative solutions and services.
Textron is known around the world for its powerful brands such as Bell, Cessna, Beechcraft, Pipistrel, Jacobsen, Kautex, Lycoming, E-Z-GO and Textron Systems.
source_title: Kautex Textron Wins Significant BEV Battery Housing Contract with Leading Automotive OEM

facts: Lund International operates in the truck and automotive accessories sector.
The company was founded in 1965 by Allan W. Lund, who previously worked as a teacher and insurance salesman.
Before entering the truck accessory market, Lund designed fiberglass prototypes for recreational vehicles.
In 1974, Lund began producing the Lund SunVisor from a chicken barn in Minnesota.
The Lund SunVisor attaches to the cab of a truck or van.
As light truck sales grew in the late 1970s, Lund expanded its offerings to include a variety of appearance accessories.
In 1997, investment firm Harvest Partners bought out Allan Lund and prompted the acquisition of Deflecta-Shield Corporation.
The merger with Deflecta-Shield positioned the combined entity as a major player in the truck accessories market.
Lund purchased Ventshade Company in 1998.
Lund acquired Smittybilt, Incorporated in 1999.
Over the years, Lund made numerous acquisitions, including AMP Research in 2013, which significantly broadened its product lines.
The company underwent several ownership changes, including a period under Highlander Partners.
In May 2019, Truck Hero, a major player in the truck and Jeep accessory market, announced its strategic acquisition of Lund International.
The merger with Truck Hero created one of the largest automotive accessory providers in the United States.

facts: First Solar is the biggest U.S.-based manufacturer of solar panels.
First Solar entered into two deals to sell up to $700 million worth of tax credits.
The deals were announced a week after the U.S. Treasury and IRS officially defined the scope of incentives available to domestic renewable energy equipment manufacturers.
This transaction is the “first significant” tax-credit transfer in the solar manufacturing business, according to First Solar.
Fully integrated domestic solar manufacturers can take advantage of tax credits of up to about 16 cents per watt for a solar module manufactured in a U.S. factory.
This incentive levels the playing field with Chinese producers, whose modules flirt with prices of 10 cents per watt.
The Inflation Reduction Act enables companies to sell earned tax credits on an open market to any company looking to reduce its tax bill.
First Solar sold the credits to Fiserv, a global fintech and payments company, at a price of $0.96 per $1 of tax credits.

Mark Widmar, First Solar’s CEO, stated: “This is the [Inflation Reduction Act] delivering on its intent, which is to incentivize high value domestic manufacturing by providing manufacturers with the liquidity they need to reinvest in growth and innovation.”
In 2022, the U.S. produced a mere 5 gigawatts of solar modules, according to the National Renewable Energy Laboratory.
In 2022, the U.S. imported 29 gigawatts of solar modules from Asian countries.
China has invested tens of billions of dollars to command more than 80 percent of global solar manufacturing capacity, now exceeding 800 gigawatts.
U.S. module production capacity is growing rapidly but currently stands at just about 11 gigawatts, which is far from enough to compete with China on the world market.
First Solar has scaled a manufacturing business around an exotic cadmium telluride chemistry that no one else in the world has been able to tame.
First Solar uses semiconductor deposition methods that can turn a sheet of glass into a solar panel in about four hours.
First Solar has weathered global market ups and downs since 1999.
First Solar is likely to be one of the leading beneficiaries of the 45X production tax credit.
First Solar is investing more than $2 billion to build new manufacturing capacity in Alabama, Louisiana and Ohio.
First Solar expects to have 14 gigawatts of vertically integrated U.S. solar manufacturing capacity by 2026.
Vertically integrated solar manufacturer Qcells is another potential winner of the incentives.
The South Korean firm Qcells is investing more than $2.5 billion to build and expand its solar-cell and module production line in Dalton, Georgia.
Qcells' total production capacity will be 8.4 gigawatts when completed in 2024.
Many other low-carbon technologies, like wind turbine equipment and battery production, are eligible for the 45X production tax credit and its transferability mechanism.
The new process allows project developers and manufacturers to monetize the IRA’s generous tax credits without relying on traditional tax-equity markets.
First Solar could have elected to receive a direct cash payment from the U.S. Department of Treasury over a five-year period as a refund from its annual tax return.
First Solar chose the third-party transfer route, citing stronger than anticipated demand for credits and the opportunity to receive cash 12 to 18 months earlier than anticipated.
First Solar plans to use the capital from the transfer to support its manufacturing and R&D investments.

Jigar Shah, director of the DOE Loan Programs Office, wrote on X that the news is a “[g]reat data point showing that the Inflation Reduction Act is working. A well functioning market will take time, but [it’s a] great start.”
source_title: First Solar cashes in with sale of IRA tax credits… | Canary Media

facts: Jonathan Randall, president of Mack Trucks North America, announced that the order book for the all-new Anthem officially opened this week.

The new Anthem features a 113.5-inch bumper-to-back-of-cab length, making it ideal for navigating tight spaces and urban environments.
The redesigned hood dramatically improves forward visibility, allowing drivers to see approximately 12 % closer to the front bumper.
Fernando Couceiro, vice president of highway trucks at Mack Trucks, stated that customers can finally experience these innovations firsthand.
The Anthem excels in maneuverability.
Fleet operators can save up to $5,000 annually in fuel costs per truck with the Anthem.
The Anthem is available in day cab, 44-inch sleeper, and 64-inch sleeper configurations.
The Anthem features premium interior cab space and a cab that's nine inches wider than the previous model.
Advanced safety features include Mack Protect, Mack’s new standard collision mitigation system.
Advanced safety features include a front airbag.
An optional Side Integrated Curtain Airbag (ICA) system is available on the Anthem.
An optional digital mirror system is available on the Anthem.
The truck incorporates Mack Connect telematics platform and GuardDog Connect integration.
GuardDog Connect has demonstrated a 37% reduction in overall downtime through predictive maintenance capabilities.
GuardDog Connect has demonstrated a 20% reduction in unplanned stops through predictive maintenance capabilities.
Target industries for the new Anthem include bulk haulers, dry bulk operations, food and beverage distribution, general freight operations and less-than-truckload (LTL) services.
Serial production on the all-new Anthem is scheduled to begin in January 2026.
Mack Trucks has provided purpose-built transportation solutions for 125 years.
Mack is one of North America’s largest producers of heavy-duty trucks.
Mack trucks are sold and serviced through an extensive distribution network in more than 45 countries.
Mack trucks, diesel engines and transmissions sold in North America are assembled in the United States.
Mack manufacturing locations are certified to the internationally recognized ISO 9001 standard for quality.
Mack manufacturing locations are certified to the ISO 14001 standard for environmental management systems.
Mack manufacturing locations are certified to the ISO 45001 standard for health and safety management systems.
Mack is a proud sponsor of Share the Road, an American Trucking Associations public information campaign aimed at enhancing the safety of our nation's roadways.
Mack Trucks is part of the Volvo Group.
The Volvo Group offers trucks, buses, construction equipment, power solutions for marine and industrial applications, financing and services that increase customer uptime and productivity.
The Volvo Group was founded in 1927 and is committed to shaping the future landscape of sustainable transport and infrastructure solutions.
The Volvo Group is headquartered in Gothenburg, Sweden.
The Volvo Group employs about 104,000 people.
The Volvo Group serves customers in almost 190 markets.
In 2024, net sales amounted to about $50 billion.

facts: HL Mando Corporation is a public company and the largest global Tier 1 Korean Original Equipment Manufacturer.

The company supplies brake, steering, suspension systems, chassis, automotive components, and automotive safety and ADAS products to General Motors, Cadillac, Ford, Chrysler, Chevrolet, Nissan, Kia Motors Company, Fiat, Volkswagen, BMW, Suzuki, Hyundai Motor Company, and many other global automobile distributors.
HL Mando has an annual profit topping over US$6 billion every year.
The company is currently owned and run by the original founding parent company, Halla Group (also referred to as HL Group).
In 2014, Mando was split into two companies, with the legal successor changing its name to Halla Holdings and a new entity named Mando being created to establish a holding company structure.
Mando has three main business areas: steering parts, brake parts, and suspension parts.
Mando recently opened a joint-venture company with Hella to produce ABS sensor software algorithm.

The company is part of the Halla Group, which comprises automobile, logistics, investment banking, ground transportation, education, construction, and sports and entertainment companies with over 10,000+ employees around the world under one CEO.
Mando was created by founder Chung In-Yung on October 1, 1962, initially named Hyundai International Inc., and changed its name to Mando Machinery Corporation in February 1980.
In 1988, Mando opened the Brake System Plant in Pyeongtaek, and two years later opened the Munmak Steering System Plant (currently renamed Wonju operations).
By 1995, Mando Machinery had a suspension system plant in Iksan as well as a dedicated R&D center.
Mando America Corporation (MAC) was founded in 1996.
In 1999, during the financial crisis, Mando was sold to Sunsage B.V., a daughter company of JP Morgan, and promptly renamed as Mando Corporation before Halla Group repurchased it.
As of 2013, Mando Corporation has six affiliated locations within Michigan, Alabama, and Georgia in the United States.
Its affiliates have a 1,300-employee presence in North and South America.
It is projected that Mando's affiliates will employ roughly 2,400 employees in the United States alone by 2017.
Worldwide, the manufacturer employs over 10,000+ workers and has an annual revenue of more than $6 billion.

Mando products include brake systems (drum brake, master cylinder & booster, ABS, ESC, AHB, EPB, MoC), steering systems (steering gear system, manual/hydraulic power steering gear, intermediate shaft, steering column, electric power steering variants), suspension systems (damper spring module, shock absorber, suspension strut, self-levelizer, semi-active suspension), driver assistance systems (smart cruise control, lane keeping assist system, smart parking assist system, blind spot detection, autonomous emergency braking), and electronics-iron casting components (power pack, yaw & g sensor, ultrasonic/torque/torque angle sensors, 77GHz/24GHz radar, front camera module).
In 2016, Mando was served with two separate patent infringement lawsuits from Bosch GmbH and Jaguar Land Rover Limited for multiple products.
This was in addition to Nexteer Automotive Corporation v. Mando America Corporation alleging trade secret theft and other numerous claims.
Mando has faced past lawsuits regarding various discrimination claims.
source_title: HL Mando - Wikipedia

facts: HL Mando was awarded the "2022 Supplier of the Year" by General Motors Company (GM).
This marks the third consecutive year HL Mando has received a Supplier of the Year award from GM, beginning with the "Launch Excellence Award" in 2020.
The company has won the SOY Award nine times in total since 2003.
HL Mando overcame the COVID-19 pandemic and a semiconductor supply shortage in 2020 to secure three straight years of awards.
In 2022, HL Mando received top marks for Business Performance, including defect-free product launching, product supply, and quality maintenance.
HL Mando also earned top marks for Cultural Priorities, specifically safety, communication, and transparency in 2022.
The 31st ceremony of the 2022 SOY was held on March 23, 2023, in San Antonio, Texas.
The award is given to outstanding partner companies within the top 1% of GM's suppliers, selected annually from over 20,000 suppliers worldwide.
One hundred seven suppliers from ten countries attended the ceremony, including HL Mando America.
Senior President and CEO Steve (Seong-hyeon) Cho stated it is a great honor to receive the award for three consecutive years.
Vice President Yun Lee, Executive Director Jason (Jae-hyuk) Kim, and Director Jeff Pontius also attended the ceremony representing HL Mando Americas.

GM vice president of Global Purchasing and Supply Chain Jeff Morrison recognized the suppliers for overcoming obstacles and demonstrating resilience, resourcefulness, determination, and commitment to sustainable innovation.
CEO Steve Cho expressed gratitude to labor union members and branches/subsidiaries across the world during the ceremony.
HL Mando Corporation is a leading global automotive parts supplier specializing in xEV and autonomous driving solutions.
The company was founded in 1962 and is headquartered in the Republic of Korea, trading under stock code KS 204320.
HL Mando holds an A grade in the MSCI (2021) ESG ratings.

The company offers state-of-the-art driving systems including Brake, Steering, Suspension, Propulsion, and Power solutions, along with software solutions for vehicle manufacturers and mobility service providers.
HL Mando's technology focuses on three domains: vehicle integrated safety, autonomous driving, and electric mobility.
The company serves over sixty (60) customers from over 50 locations worldwide, encompassing local and global automakers in every major region.
source_title: HL Mando Awarded "2022 Supplier of the Year" by General Motors Company (GM)

facts: Georgia Power and Mitsubishi Power announced the delivery of the first of three new state-of-the-art simple cycle gas combustion turbines at Plant Yates in Coweta County, Georgia.
Plant Yates is located about an hour southwest of Atlanta.
The turbine was assembled at Mitsubishi Power's Savannah Machinery Works facility.
The turbine was transported to the site using both rail and truck.
The turbine weighs nearly 350 tons and measures 50 feet long by 18 feet wide.
The new natural gas units will feature Mitsubishi Power M501JAC combustion turbines (CTs).
These are the first new natural gas turbines added to Georgia Power's fleet in a decade.
When all three units are in service, they will provide 1,300 megawatts (MW) of generation capacity for customers.
Plant Yates began commercial operation in 1950 and was the first Georgia Power plant built to support the post-World War II economic boom.
In 2014, five of the seven coal-fired units at Plant Yates were decommissioned, with the remaining two converted to natural gas generation.
The expansion will provide approximately 600 jobs during the construction process.
The project will add 15 permanent new jobs once completed, expanding the plant workforce to 75 full-time jobs.
The new units were approved by the Georgia Public Service Commission (PSC) in the 2023 Integrated Resource Plan (IRP) Update.
The units are expected to be online by the end of 2027 to meet new energy demand in the state.

Rick Anderson, senior vice president and senior production officer for Georgia Power, stated that the new natural gas units will use existing property and infrastructure to deliver value for customers and provide investment in Coweta County.
The three advanced class gas turbines provide higher output and greater efficiency than previous generations of simple cycle CT designs.
The air-cooled Mitsubishi Power M501JAC Series design eliminates the need for steam cooling, offers a shorter start-up time of approximately 30 minutes, and has a lower turn down rate.
As a contingency, the units have built-in flexibility to run on oil, with on-site oil storage capability.
With minor future modifications, the M501JAC is capable of using a hydrogen mix as a fuel.
Georgia Power recently partnered with Mitsubishi Power for a 50% hydrogen-blending project at Plant McDonough-Atkinson.

Cheryl Boddiford, senior vice president of North American Manufacturing and Supply Chain Management at Mitsubishi Power, noted the delivery reflects a long-standing partnership and commitment to U.S.-based manufacturing and skilled workforce development.
Georgia Power has been approved for combined cycle and simple cycle upgrades on all combustion turbines at Plant McIntosh near Savannah in the 2025 IRP.
The Plant McIntosh enhancements will add an additional 268-megawatts of capacity.
A recent all-source RFP certification filing with the PSC includes a request to certify five new combined cycle (CC) units totaling 3,692 MW.
The proposed CC units are planned to be placed at Plants Bowen, McIntosh, and Wansley to help ensure grid stability and reliability.
source_title: First new natural gas turbine delivered to Georgia Power's Plant Yates - Aug 15, 2025

facts: Georgia announced Mercedes-Benz's expansion and new Research & Development hub.
Mercedes-Benz will establish Atlanta as its headquarters in North America by centralizing and uniting key corporate functions.
The company will move up to 500 jobs to the existing Mercedes-Benz facility, known as “1MB,” in Fulton County.
The company will make a multi-million dollar investment in a future state-of-the-art Research & Development (R&D) facility to also be located nearby.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Mercedes-Benz will establish Atlanta as its North American headquarters by centralizing and uniting key corporate functions.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia continues to lead the way in the future of mobility and technical innovation, attracting world-class companies like Mercedes-Benz that are driving the automotive industry forward.
Governor Brian Kemp expressed excitement that a job creator with close ties to Georgia is doubling down on its choice and growing its presence in the best state for business and opportunity.
Mercedes-Benz opened its “1MB” facility in 2018 in Sandy Springs.
The “1MB” facility currently supports approximately 800 jobs in Georgia.

Jason Hoff, CEO of Mercedes-Benz North America, thanked the State of Georgia for its support in deepening Mercedes-Benz’s roots in the Atlanta area as they bring more talented team members to the city.
Jason Hoff stated that the expansion strengthens their position for continued growth and reinforces their established commitment to the U.S. market.
Jason Hoff noted that bringing teams closer together will enable agility, increase speed to market, and ensure the best customer experience.
The “1MB” facility located in Sandy Springs will house existing sales teams, financial services teams, and corporate functions.
The new state-of-the-art Research & Development hub will be located near Sandy Springs.
Mercedes-Benz anticipates that the move to metro Atlanta will be completed by August 2026.
Mayor Rusty Paul of the City of Sandy Springs expressed excitement about Mercedes-Benz expanding in Sandy Springs.
Mayor Rusty Paul stated that since establishing their headquarters in 2018, Mercedes-Benz has been an outstanding corporate partner.
Mayor Rusty Paul noted that the decision to grow in Sandy Springs highlights the success of the city’s recent infrastructure and capital investments which are now clearly paying dividends.
Mayor Rusty Paul described the expansion as a wonderful opportunity and a significant milestone for continued development.

Robb Pitts, Chairman of the Fulton County Board of Commissioners, stated that having a globally recognized brand like Mercedes-Benz reaffirm its commitment is a testament to the strength and vitality of the community.

Robb Pitts noted that this proves Fulton County continues to be a destination for corporate solutions, providing major companies an accessible, vibrant, and growing community for their business to thrive in.

Katie Kirkpatrick, President & CEO of the Metro Atlanta Chamber, stated that the expansion is a testament to both Mercedes-Benz’s commitment to excellence and metro Atlanta’s strength as a hub for innovation and talent.
Katie Kirkpatrick noted that when the 1MB facility opened in 2018, it quickly became an integral part of the business landscape, driving economic growth and elevating the region’s global presence.
Katie Kirkpatrick expressed pride in seeing the partnership deepen as Mercedes-Benz continues to invest in future shared success.
Elizabeth McLean, Assistant Director of Statewide Projects, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was executed in partnership with the City of Sandy Springs, Select Fulton, Metro Atlanta Chamber, and Georgia Power.
Pat Wilson, GDEcD Commissioner, stated that since the strategic decision to relocate Mercedes-Benz USA to Sandy Springs in 2018, they have become an integral part of the business community.
Pat Wilson noted that their continued growth and community involvement are a prime example of why Georgia recruits industry leaders such as Mercedes-Benz.

Pat Wilson stated that this expansion and commitment to R&D in the metro Atlanta area will further strengthen the company’s long-term success and highlights the talent and collaborative partnerships fostered by the University System of Georgia.
source_title: Mercedes-Benz Establishing North American Headquarters, new Research & Development Hub in Metro Atlanta - Metro Atlanta Chamber
