facts: Mercedes-Benz North America has signed a lease on a space that will become its new research and development center in West Midtown.
The tech hub is part of an ongoing consolidation of the automaker's operations into metro Atlanta.
The German automaker moved from its U.S. headquarters from New Jersey to Sandy Springs in 2018.

The success of the move prompted an announcement in May that the company would relocate another 500 employees here, mainly corporate and finance roles from Michigan, to create a base for the entire North American region.
At the time, it teased a separate innovation center that it would locate near the Sandy Springs mothership, noting how important universities like Georgia Tech have been to the company’s growth.
Building relationships with emerging mobility startups would also be a key objective.
The company confirmed that it had signed a lease on a 60,000-square-foot space at Northyards, originally a Southern Railway train maintenance facility built in 1925.
The historic six-building complex, which includes a 1911 roundhouse, was converted into creative office space in the early 2000s.
Mercedes-Benz says it will spend $34 million on a buildout to be managed by North Avenue Partners and designed by Goree + Abel architects.
The space is set to open by summer 2026.

Jason Hoff, CEO at Mercedes-Benz North America, stated that finding the home for the new Mercedes-Benz Atlanta Technology Center in midtown Atlanta is a major milestone in establishing their unified North American headquarters.
The $34 million investment is described as part of establishing this unified North American headquarters in the region.

The state of Georgia welcomed the news, noting how woven into the fabric of the state’s business community Mercedes-Benz has become, especially given its naming rights of the stadium serving the Atlanta Falcons and Atlanta United.

Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson said companies that bring their U.S. operations to Georgia gain access to world-class talent and innovative ecosystems while keeping teams close to fuel collaboration.
Mercedes-Benz will bring 160 people to the new technical center, a mix of local hires and relocated team members.
These jobs are part of the original 500 announced in May.

Partners Real Estate announced the transaction on Oct. 22, with leaders from the firm saying it showed how corporate leaders are demanding space with “both unique character and cutting-edge technology.”
source_title: Mercedes-Benz to Invest $34M in Midtown Atlanta Tech Hub - Global Atlanta

facts: Michelin has deployed TreadEye, a technology designed to enhance the retread process and deliver measurable value to fleets.
After a period of phased implementation, TreadEye has launched across the Michelin Retread Technologies franchise network in the USA.
TreadEye is a tread depth assessment tool that helps fleets maximize tire investment.
The system collects 1,200 measurement points, providing precise insights into tread wear and casing condition.

Nate Kirian, vice president of sales and distribution, commercial business lines for Michelin North America, stated: “TreadEye is a game-changer for our retread operations and for the fleets we serve.”
Nate Kirian added: “It’s a powerful example of how Michelin continues to innovate in ways that directly benefit our customers.”
Key benefits of TreadEye include identifying pull points by fleet location, maximizing rubber use by avoiding early tire removals, and preventing casing damage.
The system also helps reduce DOT violations and downtime caused by late pulls, while improving the overall efficiency of the tire removal process.
source_title: Michelin rolls out TreadEye across US retread network | Tire Technology International

facts: Michelin Retread Technologies (MRT) is increasing its production capacity, adding a new press at its Covington GA operation that can produce one million new treads per year.

The new MRT press has been designed to produce new high-technology treads and to take advantage of Michelin's advanced co-extrusion technique, a process where two different rubber compounds are combined to ensure longer life and cooler casing temperatures, maximizing the life of the casing.
Michelin is currently the only manufacturer in the world offering co-extruded treads to its retread customers.
The new press also will allow the company to expand its offering of wider treads for Michelin's X One wide-single tire.
Expansion of the Covington tread pressing plant is the first major expansion since the facility opened in 1999.
The new retreading press along with other future additions is expected to double the volume of the facility.
The facility currently produces a variety of Pre-Mold treads for MRT franchises and retread plants throughout North America.
source_title: Michelin installs new retread press at plant | Bulk Transporter

facts: Michelin North America announced that its BFGoodrich Tires plant in Tuscaloosa, Alabama will be wound down by the end of 2028.
Production from the Tuscaloosa facility will be consolidated into a more capable plant in Fort Wayne, Indiana.
The closure requires a one-time provision of around €220 million to cover severance and decommissioning costs.
Phased layoffs in Tuscaloosa will result in the loss of 1,200 jobs over a two-year drawdown period ending in 2028.
Michelin has committed to providing severance packages and support for displaced workers, adhering to its stated value of “Respect for People.”
The Tuscaloosa plant began manufacturing tires in 1946 and was acquired by Michelin in 1990.
Over its operational lifespan, the Tuscaloosa facility produced nearly half a billion tires.
The plant historically contributed over $100,000 annually to local charities in Tuscaloosa.
Michelin cited that its U.S. BFGoodrich sites were running “well below their designed capacities,” creating structural inefficiencies that cannot be sustained.
Intensifying competition in the recreation and off-road tire market is driving Michelin's need for agility, cost-efficiency, and modern production lines.
The Fort Wayne, Indiana facility possesses superior size, footprint, and infrastructure to become the BFGoodrich brand's North American hub.
Michelin has been investing in the Fort Wayne facility for years, including a $50 million upgrade completed over a decade ago.
This consolidation follows a nearly identical playbook to Michelin's 2023 announcement to close its Ardmore, Oklahoma plant by 2025, which impacted 1,400 workers.
The Ardmore closure was driven by the facility's inability to pivot cost-effectively toward manufacturing tires for electric vehicles (EVs) and incorporating sustainable materials.
Michelin is strategically shedding legacy assets that are too expensive or inflexible to retool for the next generation of automotive technology.
The rise of EVs is fundamentally changing tire demand, requiring different compounds, tread patterns, and load-bearing capabilities.
Modernizing a World War II-era plant to meet modern EV tire specifications is often less economical than consolidating production in newer facilities.
Michelin is simultaneously pouring over $200 million into its Canadian facilities in Nova Scotia to produce advanced EV tires.
The tire industry is being squeezed by a combination of electrification, automation, and sustainability demands.
Industry 4.0 technologies, including AI, robotics, and digital twins, are becoming competitive necessities for faster operation, higher precision, waste reduction, and supply chain resilience.
Industrial investment is shifting toward locations with superior infrastructure, skilled workforces, and modernization potential rather than solely the lowest costs.
Tuscaloosa's mayor pledged to collaborate with state and local partners to support displaced families and court new investment during the transition.
A former auto executive noted that while severance packages may be fair, they cannot replace the economic stability and community identity provided by a long-standing industrial anchor.
The €220 million provision covers direct closure costs but does not account for ripple effects on local suppliers, small businesses, or the city's tax base.
source_title: Behind the Numbers: Michelin's Tuscaloosa Closure is a Sobering Bellwether - BriefGlance.com

facts: Michelin® Retread Technologies offer a sustainable and cost-effective alternative to new tires, providing significant environmental benefits and cost savings.
Advanced technology and rigorous manufacturing processes ensure Michelin retreads meet the highest standards of safety and performance.
Retreads extend tire life and improve fuel efficiency to maximize total cost of ownership while boosting the bottom line and reducing waste.
Retreads reduce tire expenses significantly, decrease waste and carbon emissions, meet high safety standards, and offer a lifespan comparable to new tires.
The trucking industry saves billions each year by retreading, with smart owners investing in high-quality tires instead of new ones.
Michelin's innovative retread process utilizes new-tire design technology, proven tread designs, and new-tire manufacturing standards to deliver reliable retreads designed for specific applications.
The MICHELIN® Retread Technologies (MRT) process delivers low rolling resistance, SmartWay® verified retreads for maximum fuel efficiency.
Michelin proprietary technology yields multiple retreads from a single casing.
The manufacturing process is monitored and audited, using a 9-step procedure that delivers uniform results.
Bib Tread mobile on-site digital app provides increased accountability of tire assets.
A consistent, nationwide retread warranty is backed by Michelin.
Step 1 (Initial Inspection) inspects every casing and detail to enable trained operators to detect subtle irregularations indicating internal damage such as zippers.
Step 2 (Buffing) is computer automated, guided by a casing-specific database and sensors to provide an under tread of optimal profile, depth, and texture.
Step 3 (Casing Integrity) uses SHEAROGRAPHY, a two-step laser photography performed on every tire to reveal belt separations or blisters within the belts or other not visible areas.
Step 4 (X-Ray) is available and used when needed to allow visual inspection of the steel belts within the casing.
Step 5 (Repair) utilizes best practice repairs where thermal curing along with mechanical pressure exceeds industry standards.
Step 6 (Tread Building) uses MICHELIN® tire tread, combining applying hot cushion gum and pre-molded tread into one operation.
Step 7 (Double-Enveloping) uniquely applies an inner envelope to pressurize both sides of the repair, ensuring every retread surface undergoes uniform pressure and temperature.
Step 8 (Curing) is computer controlled for minimum temperature variations to achieve optimal uniformity and enhance full integration of all repairs.
Step 9 (Final Inspection) thoroughly inspects the crown, sidewall, bead, and interior to ensure the best retread every time.
Michelin® retreads help reduce waste by extending the life of original tires and decrease carbon emissions through improved fuel efficiency.
Retreads are significantly less expensive than new tires, reducing overall tire expenses and cost per mile.
Michelin® retreads meet rigorous safety and performance standards, making them suitable for various vehicle types including commercial trucks.
Michelin® retreads are designed to provide nearly the same lifespan as new tires when maintained properly.
Not all tires are suitable for retreading; tires must pass a thorough inspection to ensure they meet the criteria for retreading.
source_title: Explore MICHELIN® Retread Technologies: Sustainable & Cost-Effective Tire Solutions

facts: Michelin is rolling out its industry-leading TreadEye tread depth assessment technology across its entire US retread network.
TreadEye accurately and consistently collects 1,200 measurement points to provide precise insights into tread wear and casing condition.
The system helps fleets maximize their tire investment by ensuring every 32nd of an inch counts.

Key benefits of TreadEye include identifying pull points by fleet location, maximizing rubber on the road by avoiding early pulls, preventing casing damage, reducing DOT violations and downtime from late pulls, and improving efficiency in the tire removal process.

Nate Kirian, Vice President of Sales and Distribution for Commercial Business Lines at Michelin North America, Inc., stated that TreadEye is a game-changer for retread operations and fleets served, highlighting it as a powerful example of Michelin's customer-benefiting innovation.
source_title: Michelin Announces Rollout Of TreadEye Technology Across US Retread Network | Retreading Business

facts: Michelin North America announced the launch of TreadVision by Michelin Retread Technologies, a new approach to the retread process using artificial intelligence and advanced data insights to enhance retread quality.
The announcement was made at a press conference during the Technology & Maintenance Council’s annual meeting in Nashville, Tenn.
Retread Brand Manager Emily Ledbetter said TreadVision is designed not to replace humans, but instead to reduce manual and repetitive tasks.
A key feature of TreadVision is the TreadEye depth assessment tool, which consistently collects 1,200 measurement points, providing precise insights into tread wear and casing conditions.
The TreadEye tool helps fleets identify pull points, protect casing integrity, and reduce downtime.
TreadVision incorporates proprietary automated inspection powered by AI to help identify imperfections and anomalies that may not be detected by traditional methods.
TreadVision integrates automated tire handling to streamline retread plant operations and help increase turnaround times.

Janet Foster-Whitley, Michelin’s senior director of enterprise dealer and North America retreading, stated that retreading plays a vital role in helping fleets extend asset life and control operating costs.
The evolved TreadVision process aims to deliver greater consistency, improved quality, and faster turnaround times.
Also at the TMC meeting, Michelin Connected Fleet announced the launch of Smart Predictive Tire for trailers hauled by heavy-duty fleets.
The predictive trailer tire monitoring solution helps fleets proactively manage tire health and improve uptime through real-time pressure and predictive maintenance insights.
Pilot programs conducted in Europe showed that fleets using Smart Predictive Tire saw up to 80% fewer emergency roadside tire events.
Fleets using the solution saw up to 9% longer tire life where chronic under-inflation was corrected.
Fleets saw up to 4% lower fuel consumption when recommended pressures were maintained.

Damon Newquist, Michelin’s vice president of sales, stated that emergency roadside service is a major pain point for fleets, especially with trailers, and improper inflation is the overwhelming root cause of tire-related events.
The Smart Predictive Tire solution empowers fleet operators with tools and alerts to address inflation issues before they become critical.
source_title: Michelin unveils new retread, trailer tire monitoring technologies - Truck News

facts: The project was a total site preparation package for construction of two new AP1000 Model, 1100Mw Nuclear Power Generating Units in Waynesboro, GA.
Site preparation covered over 1,000 acres of land disturbance.
Scope included laydown areas, craft and management parking areas, access roads, cooling towers, on-site batch plant area, and future switchyard.
Work entailed the clearing, grubbing, and grinding of 670 acres, as well as miscellaneous concrete and RR demolition.

Project involved the design, construction, and maintenance of a 50-acre multi-staged surface and subsurface dewatering system for 90-foot-deep Nuclear Island excavations to the geologically stable layer of Blue Bluff Marl.
Over 20 million cubic yards were excavated for the project.
Morgan Corp. installed 4 million cubic yards of safety-related backfill for the nuclear reactors.
Morgan Corp. installed over 400,000 cubic yards of select material screening.
Morgan Corp. installed 40 miles of silt fencing.
Morgan Corp. installed 24,000 linear feet of storm drain piping and associated structures.
Morgan Corp. installed over 60 acres of cement-treated soils and RCC paving.
110,000 tons of stone base placement was installed.
500 acres of grassing was installed.
Over 1 million square yards of erosion-control matting was installed.
50,000 square feet of MSE wall panels up to 40 feet in height were installed.
All work completed within the footprint of the Unit 3 and 4 Nuclear Islands met all NRC Nuclear Safety Related quality assurance standards per Nuclear Quality Assurance Level 1 (NQA1) guidelines.
At its peak, there were 550 Morgan Corp. employees at the site.
Employees worked around the clock Monday through Thursday, as well as 13-hour shifts Friday through Sunday to complete the job.
source_title: Plant Vogtle Units 3 & 4 - Morgan Corp.

facts: Pipeline operator Kinder Morgan Inc. (KMI) is moving ahead with a major natural gas pipeline expansion to serve growing demand from the electric power sector in the Southeast.
KMI completed an open season for the proposed $3 billion South System 4 Expansion on the Southern Natural Gas Co. LLC pipeline system during the quarter.
The open season secured 20-year take or pay contracts with creditworthy shippers.
The South System 4 expansion project is targeted for in-service in late 2028.
The South System 4 expansion would increase capacity by 1.2 Bcf/d.
CEO Kim Dang stated that upon completion, the project will help to meet the growing power demand and local distribution company demand in the southeastern markets.
The South System 4 expansion makes up the lion’s share of a $1.9 billion increase to KMI’s backlog at the end of the second quarter.
KMI’s backlog rose to $5.2 billion from $3.3 billion in the first quarter.
KMI is in commercial discussions for more than 5 Bcf/d of incremental midstream capacity related to power demand.
Of that incremental capacity, 1.6 Bcf/d is targeted for data centers.

CEO Kim Dang noted that while certainly not all of these projects will come to fruition, the activity level supports a belief that growth in natural gas between now and 2030 will be well in excess of 20 Bcf/d.

Executive Chairman Richard Kinder stated that consensus on the expected electric demand growth for artificial intelligence (AI) and data centers from power consumers, utilities, and regulators has been “jaw dropping.”
Annual electricity demand growth over the last 20 years has averaged around 0.5%.
Industry experts have predicted annual electricity demand growth from now until 2030 at a range of 2.6%.
One projection estimates annual electricity demand growth from now until 2030 at an amazing 4.7%.
KMI transports about 40% of the natural gas consumed in the United States.
KMI expects natural gas to play an important role along with renewables and dedicated nuclear power to help the industry meet the needed 24/7 reliability.
Executive Chairman Richard Kinder noted that ERCOT has estimated Texas would need 152 GW of power generation by 2030.
This Texas estimate is up from last year’s estimate of 111 GW.
The 152 GW estimate represents a 78% increase from 2023 peak power demand of about 85 GW in Texas.
The Texas Energy Fund, a program that would extend low-cost loans for new natural gas-fired generating facilities, was massively oversubscribed.
Requests to the Texas Energy Fund could finance 55,908 MW of proposed dispatchable power generation projects for the state’s electric grid.
Kinder stated that this oversubscription is clear evidence that generators are projecting increased demand for natural gas-fired facilities.

KMI believes the additional fuel needed to supply the surge in power generation will be significant and makes the future even more robust for natural gas demand overall and for its midstream industry.
Natural gas gathering volumes were up 10% year/year in the quarter, according to President Tom Martin.
The volume increase was driven by Haynesville shale volumes, which were up 21%.
The volume increase was also driven by Eagle Ford shale volumes, which were up 8%.
KMI is lowering its guidance for gathering volumes for the remainder of the year as a result of low gas prices.
Given the current gas price environment, KMI expects gathering volumes to average about 6% below its 2024 plan.
Despite the pullback, KMI expects gathering volumes to still be 8% over 2023.
NGI’s Henry Hub bidweek price averaged $1.890/MMBtu in 2Q2024.
NGI’s Henry Hub bidweek price was $2.096 in 2Q2023.
KMI views the slight pullback in gathering volumes as temporary.
Higher production volumes will be necessary to meet demand growth from LNG expected in 2025.
KMI reported net income of $575 million (26 cents/share) in the second quarter.
This net income is roughly flat with profits of $586 million (26 cents) in 2Q2023.
Revenues totaled $3.57 billion in the second quarter.
Revenues were up $71 million from the second quarter of last year.
source_title: Kinder Morgan Expanding Southeast Natural Gas Pipeline, Citing ‘Jaw Dropping’ Expectations for Power Demand

facts: A Morgan County judge on Thursday shot down a central component of the $1.5 billion incentive package offered to electric vehicle startup Rivian.
The ruling threatens the future of one of Georgia’s biggest economic development projects.
If the ruling survives a likely appeal, it would disrupt the Rivian project.
The ruling could provide a pathway for residents in other parts of the state to challenge controversial local property tax breaks to corporations and developers.
The ruling sent shockwaves through Georgia economic development circles.
Georgia economic development circles use incentives known as tax abatements to woo industry and new development.
Rivian and state leaders have anticipated a ceremonial groundbreaking in the coming weeks.
It is unclear whether this ruling could delay construction of the planned $5 billion factory along I-20 east of Atlanta.
Georgia and local leaders near Savannah struck a similar incentives agreement with Hyundai for another massive EV factory in Bryan County.
Officials said Friday they did not think the Rivian ruling would impact that project.
source_title: Georgia judge’s incentives ruling threatens Rivian EV plant

facts: Electric vehicle startup Rivian Automotive Inc. plans to hold two ceremonial events to christen the forthcoming construction of its long-delayed $5 billion factory an hour east of Atlanta.
The company will play host to a community event Sept. 14 and a formal groundbreaking ceremony Sept. 16 with stakeholders, media and government officials, including Gov. Brian Kemp.
The events are at the roughly 2,000-acre project site along I-20.
The events bolster promises by Rivian leaders to begin vertical construction of the factory next year.
The factory project has followed multiple delays and setbacks.
source_title: Despite setbacks, EV maker Rivian presses ahead with Georgia plant

Candidates must demonstrate intermediate or advanced Microsoft Excel proficiency and strong analytical skills for Murata’s supply chain data.

Murata offers competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits for this position.

facts: Novelis celebrated the grand opening of its Global Research & Technology Center in Kennesaw on June 21, 2012.
The 160,000-square-foot facility serves as the global hub of the company’s extensive research and development activities.
The Center will create 150 new jobs and $20 million in investment and new technologies.

Representatives from the Cobb Chamber, Cobb County Government, the City of Kennesaw, the Georgia Department of Economic Development and the Technology Association of Georgia attended the grand opening to welcome Novelis to Cobb’s thriving business community.

Brooks Mathis, vice president of Economic Development at the Cobb Chamber, said Novelis is a prime example of the companies they are working to bring to Cobb County and that the center will be the home of its greatest talents producing innovative, sustainable aluminum products for the global market.
The Cobb Chamber is proud to welcome Novelis to Cobb’s family of Fortune 500 companies and corporate headquarters.

The state-of-the-art center will be the critical focal point for all research and development within the company, focused on world class solutions for the company’s can, automotive and specialty customers.
It is also the central point for all of Novelis’ technical activities worldwide, including engineering, manufacturing excellence and metallurgy.

Stefan Erdmann, Novelis’ vice president of Global R&D, said the new center will bring together some of world’s leading metallurgists, materials scientists, engineers and technologists all working for the benefit of Novelis’ customers across the globe.
The center will tackle challenges ranging from increasing fuel efficiency in automobiles to making consumer packaging and electronics products more sustainable.

Located on 1950 Vaughn Road, just north of the Novelis world headquarters in Atlanta, the facility includes a variety of materials development and testing laboratories, a pilot beverage can production line and meeting space.
Future plans include a small scale aluminum rolling mill, and heat treatment capabilities for finishing automotive aluminum sheet.

Novelis is partnering with Southern Polytechnic State University (SPSU) and the Georgia Institute of Technology on research opportunities, recruitment and internships for the future generation of engineers.
In addition, Novelis approached SPSU to design a statue of a transformer for the new center.

Dr. Simin Nasseri of the university’s Mechanical Engineering Technology Department and three of her students—Chris Cutter, James Smith and Timothy Del Rocco—designed a 10-foot-tall statue incorporating Novelis’ aluminum sheeting.
The design represents Novelis’ mission of transforming raw aluminum into useful products and also has some hexagonal elements representing SPSU.
Dr. Nasseri said this collaboration enhances the success potential of SPSU-Novelis projects, the quality of the research work produced and the quality of young, future engineers.
The new facility will become the hub of Novelis’ global network of R&D activities, which includes seven other facilities located in the U.S., Canada, Germany, Luxembourg, Switzerland and Korea.
In 2010, the company created 153 new jobs for the Atlanta community in its global headquarters office which currently employs around 250 people.
Novelis Inc. is the global leader in aluminum rolled products and aluminum can recycling.
The company operates in 11 countries, has more than 11,000 employees and reported revenue of $11.1 billion in fiscal year 2012.

Novelis supplies premium aluminum sheet and foil products to automotive, transportation, packaging, construction, industrial, electronics and printing markets throughout North America, Europe, Asia and South America.
Novelis is a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Limited (BSE: HINDALCO), one of Asia’s largest integrated producers of aluminum and a leading copper producer.
Hindalco is a flagship company of the Aditya Birla Group, a multinational conglomerate based in Mumbai, India.
source_title: Novelis Opens Global Research & Technology Center - SelectCobb

facts: Novelis will make a $2.5 billion investment as the anchor tenant to the South Alabama Mega Site.
The company will need 1,000 employees to operate its state-of-the-art plant.
President of North American Operations for Novelis Tom Boney said the search for key roles has already begun.
Key roles and responsibilities are expected to be announced hopefully before the end of May 2022.
Groundbreaking is scheduled to occur in approximately two and a half months from the announcement, with employee hiring starting very early in the project.
Groundbreaking is expected in early summer and the plant should be in operation in 2025.
The company’s initial footprint on the Mega Site property will be 1,000 acres.
It will be the first fully integrated aluminum mill built in the United States in 40 years.
The facility will use recycled water and operate as a zero-waste facility.
Novelis produces products from recycled aluminum, including soda and beer cans, pickup truck beds, and aircraft components.
President of the Baldwin County Economic Development Alliance Lee Lawson played a critical role in landing Novelis at the Mega Site.
Lee Lawson stated that spending in the billions will ripple through the community and benefit local and regional suppliers.
Local resident Larry Vaughn noted the project will help school systems and create future job opportunities for children to raise families locally.
Novelis North American headquarters is located in Atlanta, and the company operates on four continents.
The company plans to reduce its carbon footprint by 30 percent by 2026 and achieve carbon neutrality by 2050.
source_title: Novelis, Inc. announces $2.5 billion investment in South Alabama Mega Site

facts: Novelis Inc announced that its indirect wholly-owned subsidiary Novelis Corporation has priced an offering of USD 750 million aggregate principal amount of 6.875 per cent senior notes due in January 2030.
The bond offering represents an increase of USD 250 million from the offering size previously announced.
Novelis Inc operates as a sustainable aluminum solutions provider that deals in aluminium rolled products.
The company reported an 18 per cent decline in net income to USD 128 million for the September quarter of fiscal year 2024-25.
In the same period of the preceding financial year, Novelis reported a net income of USD 157 million.
Net sales for the second quarter rose by 4.5 per cent to USD 4,295 million compared to USD 4,107 million in the year-ago period.
The increase in net sales was mainly driven by higher average aluminium prices and a one per cent rise in total flat rolled product shipments reaching 945 kilo tonnes.
Novelis achieved record beverage packaging shipments during the quarter.
The company's global footprint allowed it to mitigate customer impact from a flooding-related outage at Sierre.
Steve Fisher serves as President and CEO of Novelis Inc.
Devinder Ahuja holds the position of executive vice president and CFO at Novelis Inc.

facts: On September 16, 2025, Novelis Inc. entered into an amendment to its Credit Agreement dated March 11, 2025 with Novelis Holdings Inc., lenders, and Citibank, N.A. as administrative agent.
The Amendment reduces the Applicable Margin for loans under the Term Loan Credit Agreement effective September 16, 2025.
Prior to September 16, 2025, borrowings accrued interest at either a base rate/federal funds rate/Term SOFR plus 1.00% margin or Term SOFR plus 2.00% margin.
On or after September 16, 2025, the Term Loan accrues interest at either a base rate/federal funds rate/Term SOFR plus 0.75% margin or Term SOFR plus 1.75% margin.
The Amendment extends the period during which a prepayment premium may apply from September 11, 2025 to March 16, 2026.

On or after September 16, 2025, the Term Loan may be prepaid without penalty unless connected to a repricing amendment or refinancing prior to March 16, 2026, which subjects it to a 1.00% prepayment premium.
On September 18, 2025, Novelis Corporation completed a financing transaction via a Loan Agreement dated September 1, 2025 with The Industrial Development Authority of Baldwin County.
The Industrial Development Authority of Baldwin County loaned $100 million in proceeds from Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds (Novelis Corporation Project), Series 2025B, to Novelis Corporation.
The bond proceeds will finance a portion of the costs of constructing Novelis Corporation's solid waste disposal facilities located in Baldwin County, Alabama.
The Bonds were issued pursuant to an Indenture of Trust dated September 1, 2025 with Regions Bank as trustee.
The Bonds were sold at 100% of their principal amount and bear interest at 4.625% per annum.
A mandatory tender for purchase of the Bonds occurs on June 1, 2032.

After the initial term interest rate period on June 1, 2032, the Bonds may convert to a variable interest rate period or remain in a term interest rate period, with rates resetting at the end of each period.
The Bonds mature on June 1, 2055.
Novelis Corporation will make semiannual interest payments on the outstanding principal on January 15 and July 15 of each year, with the first payment due January 15, 2026.
On the Mandatory Tender Date and other specified dates, bondholders must tender bonds for purchase by BofA Securities, Inc., acting as remarketing agent.
The Bonds are guaranteed jointly and severally by Novelis Inc. and certain of its subsidiaries via a Guaranty Agreement dated September 18, 2025.
If the Bonds are declared taxable or the Loan Agreement is invalid, they are subject to mandatory redemption within 60 days at 100% of principal plus accrued interest.
The current report was signed by Christopher Courts, Executive Vice President and Chief Legal Officer of Novelis Inc., on September 18, 2025.
source_title: September 18, 2025 - 8-K: Current report | Novelis Inc. ()

facts: Atlanta-based Novelis Inc. and Georgia Tech have announced a new collaboration to establish the Novelis Innovation Hub at Georgia Tech.
Novelis has committed $2.5 million to initiate research, faculty, student, and educational program support.
The collaboration will promote basic and translational research, innovative business models, and related educational endeavors at Georgia Tech.
The hub will serve as a cross-functional hub connecting Novelis’ technical and business innovators with Georgia Tech’s students and faculty.
Steve Fisher, president and CEO of Novelis, stated that Georgia Tech is one of the world’s premier research institutions in their hometown.
Fisher noted the opportunity to collaborate with Georgia Tech to help train a new generation of students poised to advance engineering and research frontiers.
With Novelis funding, Georgia Tech intends to recruit a senior interdisciplinary faculty member to serve as the director of the Novelis Innovation Hub.
The hub director is expected to be identified and in place by the 2020-21 academic year.

Chaouki Abdallah, Georgia Tech’s executive vice president for research, stated the collaboration demonstrates a focus on providing students and faculty with unmatched academic and practical experiences through innovative corporate relationships.
Abdallah highlighted Georgia Tech’s expertise in materials science, advanced manufacturing, and business and AI systems as among the best in global higher education.
Under the agreement terms, Novelis will annually identify sponsored research and provide a list of innovation needs for Georgia Tech faculty to propose solutions.
An advisory board comprised of representatives from Georgia Tech and Novelis will evaluate proposals utilizing Novelis funding and external funding opportunities.
The advisory board will also foster future technology collaborations.
Todd Summe, chief research and development officer at Novelis, stated establishing closer ties with Georgia Tech provides tremendous benefits for students and the company.
Summe noted combined efforts will advance Novelis’s mission to lead the aluminum industry as the partner of choice for innovative solutions.
The partnership aims to fill the industry’s talent pipeline with outstanding innovators.
Summe described this as the next step in a partnership that can serve as a model for transformative innovation across Novelis, the Aditya Birla Group, and the industry at large.
Novelis is building upon longstanding ties with Georgia Tech, having historically provided internships and full-time employment as well as mentorship to more than 50 graduate students over the years.
Novelis has consistently funded sponsored research as well as research equipment at Georgia Tech.
Most recently, Novelis co-invested in additive manufacturing in the Advanced Manufacturing Pilot Facility.
The company provides funds for graduate student research with renowned faculty and for Georgia Tech’s InVenture Prize, a student competition fostering creativity, invention, and entrepreneurship.
Novelis Inc. is a global leader in innovative products and services and the world's largest recycler of aluminum.
The company is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia.
Novelis operates 23 facilities in nine countries.
Novelis has approximately 11,000 employees.
Novelis recorded $12.3 billion in revenue for its 2019 fiscal year.
Novelis is a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Limited, an industry leader in aluminum and copper.
Hindalco Industries Limited is the metals flagship company of the Aditya Birla Group, a multinational conglomerate based in Mumbai, India.
source_title: Novelis and Georgia Tech Establish Novelis Innovation Hub | Supply Chain and Logistics Institute

facts: Novelis, Inc. is a leader in producing sustainable aluminum products, aluminum rolling and aluminum recycling.
The Company focuses on providing solutions for the beverage can, automotive, aerospace and high-end specialty markets.
The Company is a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Limited, the metals flagship company of the Indian multinational company Aditya Birla Group.
The Company is organized into four operating segments based on geographical area as follows: North America, Europe, Asia, and South America
The Company supplies rolled aluminum components for automotive bodies including body-in-whites, body panels and side walls, doors, hoods and trunks, wings, spoilers and fins, fenders, and liftgates.

The Company supplies structural components including engine supports, cross members, crash-resistant passenger compartments, platforms to mount drive units and axle modules, peripheral energy-conversion areas, stabilizer bars, frame rails, bracket frames, gussets, and bumpers.
The Company supplies car trim and sealings, heat exchangers (aluminum fin stocks, air conditioners, brazing sheets, radiators), electric vehicle battery enclosures, and transportation components.

Transportation components supplied include flexible tubes, heat protection tubes, air ducts, exhaust pipes, thermal insulation hoses, energy absorption elements, tubes for hot and cold air distribution, heat protection mats and sleeves, hoses, silencers, tanks and rigid containers.
The Company was created in 2005 as a spin-off from aluminum producer Alcan Inc.
In May 2007, the company was acquired by Hindalco Industries Limited.
In May 2013, the company signed a cooperation agreement with ThyssenKrupp Tailored Blanks for the development of tailored aluminum blanks.
In May 2013, the company Europe qualified its rolling and finishing facility in Gottingen, Germany to produce automotive aluminum sheet.
In July 2013, the company announced the opening of its expanded aluminum rolling operations in Pindamonhangaba, Sao Paulo, Brazil.
In October 2013, the company completed a two-year, USD 400 million expansion program in South Korea.
In October 2013, the company commissioned a USD 200 million expansion of its rolling operations in Oswego, New York, U.S.
In December 2013, the company announced an investment of an additional USD 205 million to further expand global manufacturing operations serving the automotive market in U.S. and Germany.
In April 2014, the company held a groundbreaking ceremony of a new aluminum automotive heat treatment line in Nachterstedt, Germany.

In April 2014, the company signed an agreement with Changzhou National Hi-Tech District to build China's first aluminum automotive sheet manufacturing facility with an annual capacity of 120,000 metric tons.
In January 2018, the company announced an investment of USD 305 million to locate a new processing plant in Guthrie, Kentucky to produce automotive-grade aluminum sheet.

In February 2018, the company signed a binding agreement to acquire operating facilities and manufacturing assets at its Sierre plant from Constellium for EUR 200 million, expected to close in the first quarter of fiscal 2019.
In April 2020, the company completed an acquisition of Aleris Corporation, a global supplier of rolled aluminum products, for approximately USD 2.8 billion.

In January 2021, the company and Constellium launched Alumobility, a non-profit organization focused on providing innovative implementation-ready solutions to advance the adoption of aluminum automotive body sheet.
In October 2021, the company established a new operation USD 300 million automotive aluminum finishing in Guthrie, Kentucky which employs over 150 people.
In May 2022, the company broke ground on a new USD 365 million aluminum recycling center in Guthrie, Kentucky which will serve the automotive market with an annual capacity of 240,000 tons.
source_title: Novelis, Inc. - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

facts: Novelis Inc. is a leading sustainable aluminum solutions provider and a world leader in aluminum rolling and recycling.
Novelis' Latchford plant in the UK was awarded £4.6 million to establish hydrogen burning trials.
The award is part of the UK Government’s £55m Industrial Fuel Switching Competition.
This competition is part of the £1bn Net Zero Innovation Portfolio (NZIP).
The project is also part of the wider regional HyNet project.
Novelis joined HyNet in 2017 and has been supporting the development of the regional infrastructure project.
Novelis has been conducting its own technical feasibility studies on using hydrogen as a direct replacement for natural gas.

Emilio Braghi, Executive Vice President of Novelis Inc. and President of Novelis Europe, stated that switching to renewable energy sources is a key initiative to advance toward carbon-neutral production.
Braghi noted the collaboration drives industrial decarbonization of the whole North-West region in the UK.
With the grant from the Department for Energy Security & Net Zero, Novelis’ Latchford plant will test hydrogen on one recycling furnace in a demonstration phase in 2024.

Allan Sweeney, Plant Manager of Novelis Latchford, said they are pioneers using hydrogen within the aluminum industry and the trials will advance research on integrating hydrogen power in recycling operations globally.
The trial is set up in collaboration with Progressive Energy, an independent UK energy company.
The project requires installing new burners and regenerators capable of operating with hydrogen or a blended hydrogen/gas input.
The furnace lining material will be replaced with one suitable for hydrogen.
Depending on the final configuration, replacing natural gas with hydrogen to feed the remelting furnace could reduce CO2eq emissions by up to 90% compared to using the same amount of natural gas.

Suzanne Lindsay-Walker, Vice President of Sustainability at Novelis Inc., stated decarbonizing melting processes is critical to achieving sustainability goals of reducing carbon footprint by 30% by 2026 and being carbon neutral by 2050 or sooner.
Novelis’ research & development teams worldwide are investigating the ability to use plasma, electricity, and biomass to power manufacturing operations.
Novelis Inc. is a global leader in the production of innovative aluminum products and solutions and the world’s largest recycler of aluminum.
Novelis aims to be the leading provider of low-carbon, sustainable aluminum solutions and achieve a fully circular economy.
Novelis partners with suppliers and customers in aerospace, automotive, beverage can, and specialties industries across North America, Europe, Asia, and South America.
Novelis had net sales of $18.5 billion in fiscal year 2023.
Novelis is a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Limited, an industry leader in aluminum and copper.
Hindalco Industries Limited is the metals flagship company of the Aditya Birla Group, a multinational conglomerate based in Mumbai.
Novelis Latchford in Warrington, UK, is one of Europe’s largest aluminium-used beverage cans recycling plants.
It is also Europe’s largest closed-loop recycling operation for automotive aluminum rolled products with an annual recycling capacity of up to 195,000 tonnes.
The plant is an essential part of Novelis’ European production, which has enough capacity to recycle every aluminum beverage can sold in the UK.

Novelis collaborated with Jaguar Land Rover (JLR) to create the first closed-loop system in Europe with a dedicated railway service to efficiently deliver aluminum between Novelis and JLR’s production site in the UK.
Due to high economic integration and geographical proximity between the EU, UK, and Switzerland, Novelis operates across borders in well-established and efficient supply chains.
This interconnectedness creates important economies of scale that benefit value chains across Europe.
It enables Novelis to maximize the use of recycled inputs in line with its ambition to provide low-carbon, sustainable aluminum solutions to customers.
source_title: Novelis to Start Hydrogen Burning Trials in UK Recycling Plant - H2 IQ

facts: Novelis Inc. is set to invest $175 million to upgrade its already massive plant between Rio de Janeiro and Sao Paulo, Brazil.
The facility collects and recycles scrap aluminum into ingots that are flattened into rolled products.
The project at the remote Pindamonhangaba plant is to create more than 50 new jobs.
Novelis is adding 17 percent more aluminum sheet capacity and 13.3 percent more recycling capacity, up to 680 kilotonnes and 450 kilotonnes, respectively.
The investment includes the purchase of 90 acres of land for expansion.
The project also includes a new water sourcing system.
Novelis officials stated in a Nov. 13 news release that these initiatives show their commitment to sustainability.
The company won the support of Investe SP, the state investment promotion agency for the state of Sao Paulo.

Investe SP director Sergio Costa stated: “It’s very important for the state of Sao Paulo to contribute to this high value-added project. Sao Paulo needs initiatives like this that promote the economic, social and environmental development of the state.”
No incentives were disclosed in the news release regarding the project.
The plant is a major driver of economic growth in the state.
The facility was established in 1977.
The plant currently employs more than 1,100 people.
Atlanta-based Novelis is a subsidiary of India’s Aditya Birla Group.
Novelis posted revenues of about $11.5 billion in 2017.
Novelis's Atlanta headquarters is located just across the driveway from the Brazilian consulate in Buckhead’s Alliance Center development.
source_title: Novelis to Invest $175M in Brazil Aluminum Recycling and Production Plant  - Global Atlanta

facts: Novelis is a leading sustainable aluminum solutions provider and the world leader in aluminum rolling and recycling.
Three of Novelis' North American plants that produce material for the beverage can market have been awarded ASI Performance Standard Certifications.
The certified North American plants are located in Greensboro, GA; Berea, KY; and Warren, OH.
Novelis has been delivering ASI-certified material since August 2021 in Europe, South America, and Asia.
Tom Boney serves as Executive Vice President and President of Novelis North America.
The ASI Performance Standard addresses environmental, social, and governance principles for the production of aluminum.
Novelis aims to achieve ASI Certification in all its plants worldwide.
Fiona Solomon is the Chief Executive Officer at the Aluminium Stewardship Initiative (ASI).
One of Novelis' certified North American plants recycles approximately 20% of the region's used beverage cans (UBCs).
Novelis' plant in Pindamonhangaba, Brazil, has also earned the ASI Performance Standard Certification.
Novelis has achieved both Performance Standard and Chain of Custody Certifications by ASI at all of its 11 European manufacturing sites.
All rolled aluminum products of Novelis Korea are now sourced and produced in accordance with the ASI Performance Standard and Chain of Custody Standard.
Novelis aims to be a carbon-neutral company by 2050 or sooner.
Novelis aims to reduce its carbon footprint by 30% by 2026.
Novelis has targets to reduce waste to landfills by 20%, energy intensity by 10%, and water intensity by 10% by 2026.
Increasing the amount of recycled aluminum in its products is at the core of Novelis' carbon reduction strategy.
Novelis serves customers in the aerospace, automotive, beverage can, and specialties industries throughout North America, Europe, Asia, and South America.

Tom Boney stated that the certification is the latest step in their sustainability journey and demonstrates an unwavering commitment to providing sustainable aluminum solutions to valued customers.

Suzanne Lindsay-Walker stated that Novelis aims to achieve ASI Certification in all its plants worldwide as part of an ongoing commitment to being a responsible partner to customers and working with them to achieve sustainability goals.

Fiona Solomon stated that the certified plants are significant nodes in the North American beverage can market and expressed delight at Novelis' ongoing ASI Certification journey extending through global operations.

facts: The global aluminium market size reached 72.05 Million Tons in 2024.
IMARC Group expects the global aluminium market to reach 107.07 Million Tons by 2033, exhibiting a growth rate (CAGR) of 4.10% during 2025-2033.
AI-driven optimization technologies enable aluminium producers to enhance smelting operations, reducing energy consumption by up to 10% through predictive analytics and real-time monitoring systems.

Machine learning algorithms streamline quality control processes in aluminium manufacturing, with automated defect detection systems improving product consistency and reducing waste by 15% across production facilities.

Novelis partnered with Sortera to develop advanced AI-powered scrap sorting technologies that enable rapid separation of mixed aluminium alloys, increasing recycling efficiency and supporting circular economy initiatives.
Automakers increasingly utilize lightweight aluminium components to enhance electric vehicle efficiency and range.
The automotive sector's shift toward electrification drives demand for aluminium body panels, chassis components, and battery housings.
Global aluminium recycling rates exceed 90% in automotive and construction sectors.
Over 60% of aluminium production in Europe comes from recycled sources.
Companies implement closed-loop recycling systems and blockchain technology for supply chain transparency.
Aluminium consumption in solar panel mounting systems, wind turbine components, and energy transmission infrastructure increases substantially.
The material's corrosion resistance, lightweight properties, and electrical conductivity make it essential for renewable energy installations.
Consumer preference shifts toward sustainable packaging solutions boost aluminium can and foil production.
The aerospace industry's reliance on aluminium for aircraft manufacturing continues expanding, with commercial aviation recovery and defense spending driving demand.
Aluminium's superior strength-to-weight ratio remains critical for fuel-efficient aircraft design.

Series 3 aluminium accounts for the majority of market shares due to an exceptional balance of strength, corrosion resistance, and cost-effectiveness.
Extrusions dominate the processing method market due to versatility in creating complex profiles and widespread use across automotive, construction, and aerospace sectors.
Building and construction enjoys the leading position with 32.0% market share, driven by aluminium's strength, durability, and sustainability in modern infrastructure.
Asia-Pacific dominates the regional market with 60.0% market share in 2024, driven by rapid industrialization, urbanization, and strong manufacturing base across the region.

In July 2025, the Union Minister of India officially launched the Aluminium Vision Document during the International Conference on Sustainable and Responsible Mining, presenting strategies to double recycling rates and increase aluminium manufacturing sixfold by 2047.

In June 2025, VELUX Group and Novelis Inc. signed a multi-year commercial agreement for supply of aluminium with over 70% recycled content, advancing sustainability goals in window manufacturing and reducing carbon emissions.

In April 2025, AMG Metals & Materials and Rio Tinto signed a Memorandum of Understanding to evaluate establishing a renewable energy-powered integrated low-carbon aluminum production plant in India, with capacity for up to 1 million tonnes per year of primary aluminum and 2 million tonnes per year of alumina.

facts: Novelis Inc. established the Fiscal 2026 Annual Incentive Plan (AIP) for eligible employees in certain job bands, corporate offices, and the Bay Minette Project.
The plan covers a performance period from April 1, 2025, to March 31, 2026.
Payouts under the plan are based on company and individual performance measures, including safety, adjusted operating EBITDA, and cash flow.
Eligibility includes employees in job bands 7 and higher, all corporate and regional headquarters office employees, and all employees assigned to the Bay Minette Project Plan.

Performance weightings for Bay Minette project participants vary by role, including Steering Committee, Operational Readiness Committee, Construction Project Team, and Operational Readiness Project Team.
The Bay Minette Project Milestones metric tracks Earned Construction, Employment Level Adherence, Core Process Readiness, and Supporting Functions Schedule Plan.
An 'active employee' for the Bay Minette project is defined as a candidate who has accepted an offer and has been actively employed for 30 days by February 28, 2026.
Core Process Readiness is measured by the completion of scheduled deliverables via a gate review process, with the Steering Committee authorized to modify the number of deliverables.
The Supporting Functions Schedule Plan tracks achievement for IT, Finance, Procurement, Metal Procurement, and Supply Chain.
The overall AIP plan threshold for payout on any metric requires at least 75% EBITDA.
Novelis Inc. administers the plan through its Human Resources department and Compensation Committee.
source_title: Novelis Inc. Fiscal 2026 Annual Incentive Plan | Novelis Inc. | Business Contracts | Justia

The company offers interior systems, such as isolation dash pads, tunnel pads, floor modules, car mats, carpet skins, and sound absorption pads.
The company offers exterior systems, including under covers, insulator tunnels, and wheel guards.

facts: NVH Korea announced plans to invest $72 million in a new manufacturing plant located in Locust Grove, south of Atlanta.
The company plans to hire 140 workers for the new facility.
The plant will manufacture sensors, connections, and protectors specifically for electric vehicle batteries.
Operations at the new Georgia plant are scheduled to begin in 2024.
Chairman and CEO Ja Kyum Koo stated that producing electric battery components marks NVH Korea's first venture into this sector.
NVH Korea is headquartered in Ulsan, South Korea.

The Locust Grove facility will be the 10th supplier to locate in the region following Hyundai's 2022 announcement of a $5.5 billion electric vehicle and battery assembly plant in Ellabell, Georgia, near Savannah.
Hyundai's Ellabell site is projected to eventually grow to 8,100 employees.
Hyundai's Ellabell plant is slated to begin producing vehicles in 2025.
Suppliers for the regional automotive ecosystem have collectively pledged to invest nearly $2.1 billion.
Regional suppliers have pledged to hire nearly 4,900 people.
The Locust Grove project marks NVH Korea's second plant in Georgia.
NVH Korea's subsidiary, AFS America, is located in Columbus and manufactures floor mats and cargo trays for Hyundai's Montgomery, Alabama plant, Kia's West Point, Georgia plant, and General Motors.
NVH Korea operates a total of 19 plants worldwide across South Korea, North America, China, India, Europe, and Russia.
The company's broader product portfolio includes sound-dampening parts and materials, floor mats, headliners, insulators, and components for managing engine and cooling system temperatures.
The state of Georgia will fund the training of NVH Korea's new workforce.
NVH Korea may qualify for up to $1.2 million in Georgia state income tax credits, calculated at $1,750 per job over a five-year period.
Eligibility for the state income tax credits requires employees to earn a minimum annual wage of $31,300.
The company may also be eligible for additional incentives, including property tax breaks from the city of Locust Grove and Henry County.
source_title: S Korean firm plans $72M Georgia plant to make battery parts, hiring 140 | World News - Business Standard

facts: Automotive parts manufacturer NVH Korea will establish a new facility in Locust Grove.
The project will create over 160 new jobs.
The project will bring $72 million in investment to Henry County.
This is the second economic development project Governor Kemp has announced this week, alone.
Governor Kemp is continuing to promote Georgia to international job creators through a packed agenda while abroad.
NVH Korea chose the State of Georgia as its company’s first EV battery component manufacturing site.
Cooperation between state agencies and NVH Korea staff helped guide them throughout this project.
Ja Kyum Koo is Chairman and CEO of NVH Korea.
This is the first step of NVH Korea’s effort to contribute to the electrification of the mobility industry.
NVH Korea is an automotive parts manufacturer based in the City of Ulsan.
NVH Korea was established in 1984.
The company specializes in automotive noise, vibration, and heat-control parts.
NVH Korea currently produces these parts in Korea, Europe, and India.
The company operates a manufacturing facility that produces floor mats, cargo mats, and cargo trays in Georgia.
The new facility will supply battery parts for electric vehicles (EVs) in the United States.
The new facility will supply parts to manufacturers such as Hyundai Mobis, Kia Georgia, and Hyundai Motor Group.
NVH Korea’s new facility will be located at Gardner Logistics Park on Colvin Drive in Locust Grove.
The facility will produce EV battery system components that protect, connect, and sense the performance of battery cells.
Operations are expected to begin in the second quarter of 2024.
Mayor Robert Price is the Mayor of the City of Locust Grove.
Carlotta Harrell is Chair of the Henry County Board of Commissioners.
Project Manager Jacob Lee represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was in partnership with the Henry County Development Authority, Georgia EMC, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that an economic transformation is happening across Georgia as manufacturers create jobs of the future in line with cutting-edge technologies and industry trends.
GDEcD welcomes innovators establishing their first U.S. or e-mobility manufacturing presence, like NVH Korea.

For over a century, Georgia has fostered healthy industry practices, encouraged collaboration and innovation, and positioned the state as a leader in developing and harnessing emerging technologies for the evolving automotive and mobility industry.
From July 1 through December 31, 2022, the automotive industry was the second-largest source of new jobs and investment.
Georgia has pursued job creation along the entire EV supply chain as the market continues to grow.
Since 2020, Georgia's pursuit of EV supply chain job creation has resulted in more than $22.7 billion in investments and over 28,400 jobs created.

Oneda Corporation is located in Columbus, Georgia.

Oneda specializes in innovative robotic transfer and progressive tooling capabilities with press sizes ranging from 25 to 500 tons.
Oneda Corporation is a subsidiary of Oneda Electric Corporation in Japan.
The company serves the Automotive, Medical, and Consumer Electronics industries with competitive performance and cost-effective metal stampings.
Services range from prototyping of initial design to full mass production.
Oneda is a customer driven and process centered organization striving to ensure the highest standards of quality, customer satisfaction, and timelines of all projects.

The company works in partnership with customers using combined extensive experience in flexible manufacturing processes to significantly reduce tooling expenses, improve overall unit costs, and increase production capacity.
Oneda uses advanced comprehensive engineering and design experience to achieve unique manufacturing techniques in factory automation.
Oneda has developed tooling technology and manufacturing capabilities to produce several different part numbers all at once within the same process.
This methodology provides significant cost saving advantages.

facts: AT&T and Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America formed a working agreement to explore and test connectivity solutions for consumers and commuters.
The project provides a framework for creating customized products for global automotive manufacturers in North America.
Initial concept testing for interfaces between in-car infotainment systems and emerging mobile devices will begin in late 2011.
Testing will take place in the Peachtree City testing community.
AT&T will provide network services for the project.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Company will supply hardware and integration services.
Financial terms of the agreement are not being disclosed.

Steve Deguchi, President of Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America, stated that drivers spend increasing amounts of time on the road seeking vehicles that complement their hyper-mobile lifestyles.
Deguchi noted automakers successfully delivering smart, personalized services to drivers have a tremendous opportunity to stand out from competition.
Deguchi added the most appealing vehicles will serve as another node in a driver’s existing network of devices.
The project aims to create Peachtree City as a connected model city fully open to automotive manufacturers, technology providers, and others in the ecosystem.
Both companies will continue pursuing independent initiatives but plan to work together on select programs combining their strengths for automakers and consumers.
Glenn Lurie, President of emerging devices, resale and partnerships at AT&T, stated there are incredible wireless opportunities in the automotive space that have only been scratched.
Lurie noted AT&T continues exploring ways to develop a safer connected ecosystem for emerging devices.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America is based in Peachtree City, Georgia.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America maintains Detroit area operations located in Southfield, MI.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America is a division company of Secaucus, New Jersey-based Panasonic Corporation of North America.
Panasonic Corporation of North America is the principal North American subsidiary of Panasonic Corporation (NYSE: PC) of Osaka, Japan.
Panasonic Corporation is one of the world’s leading developers and producers of innovative digital and other electronic products for consumer, business, and industrial use.

Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America acts as the North American operating company of Panasonic’s Automotive Systems Company, which coordinates global automotive industry systems and components operations.

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems of America introduced its new Augmented Reality (AR) Head-Up Display at CES 2021 on January 11, 2021.

The AR HUD combines advances in optics, volume optimization, and imaging technology with AI from the SPYDR cockpit domain controller to render near-field and far-field content for vehicle data, object/pedestrian detection, and route guidance.
Scott Kirchner serves as president of Panasonic Automotive and executive director of Panasonic Smart Mobility.

Traditional HUDs only cover a small section of the road, whereas Panasonic's AR HUD solutions cover more of the roadway with traditional cluster content in the near field and 3D overlays in the far field.
The AR HUD projects 3D, AI-driven key information into the driver's line of sight to help reduce driver distraction and potentially increase safety on the road.
Panasonic's AR HUD development utilizes a PRIZM process to address all aspects of users' needs today, tomorrow, and in the future.
Panasonic collaborates with Envisics to provide striking, dual-plane, high-resolution laser holography for the AR HUD.
Envisics develops a patent-protected, dynamic holographic platform that enables true holography across multiple mobility applications.
Panasonic collaborates with Phiar to provide 3D localization technology and AI navigation and situation awareness analytics for the system.
Phiar develops a patent-protected spatial-AI, AR navigation platform.
The AR HUD could provide an important added level of comfort and assurance for autonomous vehicle passengers in the future.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America is headquartered in Peachtree City, Georgia.
The company maintains sales, marketing, and engineering operations in Farmington Hills, Michigan.
Panasonic Automotive operates as a division company of Panasonic Corporation of North America.
Panasonic Automotive is a leading global supplier of automotive infotainment and connectivity system solutions.
Panasonic Corporation of North America is headquartered in Newark, New Jersey.
Panasonic Corporation of North America is the principal North American subsidiary of Osaka, Japan-based Panasonic Corporation.
Panasonic was named one of Interbrand's Top 100 Best Global Brands of 2020.

facts: Japan's Panasonic Holdings will sell its entire stake in Panasonic Automotive Systems (PAS) to funds managed by U.S. private equity firm Apollo Global Management.
The transaction has a total enterprise value of 311 billion yen ($2.06 billion), subject to adjustments at closing.
The sale is expected to close by the end of the first quarter of 2025.
Panasonic will acquire a 20% stake in Star Japan Holdings, which will own the new parent company of PAS.
The automotive business will maintain its strategic relationship with Panasonic Group following the transaction.
Panasonic has previously stated that the PAS business could potentially be listed in the future.
Panasonic's unit Blue Yonder will acquire U.S. digital supply chain network supplier One Network Enterprises for $839 million.
The Blue Yonder acquisition is expected to be completed in the July-September quarter of 2024.

facts: PASI is a subsidiary of Panasonic Automotive Systems Co. Ltd, a global leader in advanced automotive solutions that make driving more enjoyable and fulfilling, providing systems that support safe driving, and offer optimal in-vehicle systems and solutions for environmentally friendly vehicles.

PASI focuses on the development, manufacturing, and sale of automotive components and parts like infotainment solutions (audio, video and navigation), sensors, relays, cameras, parking assistance systems, telematics control units, smart entry systems, LED solutions for headlamps and batteries.

PASI offers the latest technologies such as Head-up Displays (HUD), Telematic Control Units (TCU), Temperature Oil Sensor, and in-car infotainment to advance India’s automotive industry across global markets.
Bhupesh Kanyal serves as the Director of Panasonic Automotive Systems India (PASI).
The Indian motor vehicle industry is the fourth largest in the world and continues to grow rapidly.
India is the largest manufacturer in the two-wheeler segment.
Strong economic growth and rising income of Indian households drive significant growth potential in the automotive industry.
The advent of new-age technologies and newer forms of mobility such as Electric Vehicles are evolving consumer aspirations.
Indian consumers today demand premium commuting experiences, passenger safety, and environmental safety, resulting in demand for an improved passenger vehicle experience.

Panasonic is transforming the in-car experience by harnessing new technologies such as Connected Devices, Head-Up Displays, Wireless Chargers, and 360° Camera Solutions that are simple to use, safe, durable, and cost-effective.
PASI currently caters to the top OEMs of the automotive industry in India and aims to expand its product gamut.
PASI’s range of solutions is categorized under three themes: Beyond Comfort, Integrated Safety, and Electrification.
The Beyond Comfort theme caters to automobile interiors featuring cockpits, navigation systems, and HUDs.

The Integrated Safety theme aids in safe commuting and reducing traffic accidents, featuring products like back & corner sensors, interior rearview mirrors, vehicle camera modules, LED modules for headlamps, and steering angle sensors.
In the area of electrification, Panasonic caters to Electric Vehicles (EVs) in the form of Li-ion batteries for EVs, Heater Devices, and on-board charging systems.
PASI was established in April 2010.
As of the article's date, PASI has a presence across all automotive clusters in India working with top OEMs.
In India, PASI maintains a vast delivery and service network with highly trained employees and fully equipped facilities to support OEMs and end users.

Head-Up Displays (HUD) present data to drivers while keeping their attention on the road, with Panasonic offering in-house-designed, cost-effective HUD technologies meeting specific OEM needs, including windshield HUDs and combiner HUD systems.

In-Vehicle Infotainment (IVI) solutions provide audio/video entertainment and information content including radio, media player, TV and video, telephony, Navigation, speech control, Apps, and Connectivity features to help OEMs provide a holistic electronic cockpit concept.

Telematics Control Units (TCU) assist in locating the vehicle's position, making emergency calls during accidents or breakdowns, tracking stolen vehicles, recording diagnostic codes for service stations, and providing holistic information about vehicle health, riding/driving mode, tyre pressure, and fuel level/battery charge status via a mobile application.
Panasonic provides Back/Lane Watch cameras integrated in Outer Rear View mirrors (ORVM) and rear view cameras for parking assistance to cover blindspots and avoid driver distraction.
Vehicle sensors are provided for 2W and 4W applications for Engine applications, Speed monitoring, Ambient Temperature, and Crank Angle Detection.
Panasonic provides high quality and reliable LED modules for headlamps, winkers, and tail lights.

Panasonic's wireless chargers use moving coil technology with a proprietary algorithm to automatically detect the device's coil and align it with the charger's coil, improving alignment accuracy and enabling highly efficient charging.
source_title: Panasonic Automotive Systems India to offer futuristic technological solutions

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems (PAS) operated a booth at Tokyo Auto Salon 2026 from January 9 to January 11, 2026, at Makuhari Messe in Chiba Prefecture.
The booth's theme focused on how AI is changing mobility experiences.
PAS demonstrated signage that uses cameras and AI to read a visitor's hand skeletal structure in real time without X-ray scanning.
The system reproduced hand gestures as a 3D wireframe model with almost no lag using only camera video images and AI analysis.
PAS exhibited a steering wheel with embedded sensors that analyzes a driver's state of mind by detecting heart rate and minute perspiration on palms to infer mental state while driving.
The steering wheel system can detect when drivers are tired or impatient, even if the drivers haven't noticed themselves.
PAS displayed signage using cameras to watch people and determine actions like yawning, making phone calls, or drinking beverages.
Sensors and AI were demonstrated to assess people's states with a high degree of accuracy for mobility applications.
PAS showcased the "WELL Cabin Craie2," a vehicle concept using internal and external sensing functions and AI to provide "Joy in Motion."
The WELL Cabin Craie2 proposes driving routes based on driver preferences, avoiding congested roads, narrow streets, or areas causing discomfort.
While driving, the car uses sensors to detect surrounding conditions and can relax drivers by complimenting them.
The system includes sensors for blind spots like rear left corners to issue alerts.
It detects if a driver yawns and suggests taking a break.
Back-seat passengers can connect to the car via smartphone to share route information and suggest stops, changing the route directly without distracting the driver.
Future possibilities for the WELL Cabin Craie2 include using information from occupants' smartphones to recommend places to eat or music to listen to.
The system uses AI to analyze what is seen from the car, such as identifying fireworks by gesture and explaining their names and origins.
If a passenger points at a building, the AI can identify it.
The car can detect when a passenger looks at the camera and smiles, automatically taking a photo and compositing it with the view in front of the car to send to the passenger's phone.
PAS coordinated with the MAHA Group, which provides hospitality services to inbound tourists, to provide premium mobility experiences through the "WELL Cabin Luxe" based on the Toyota Alphard.
An actual floor model of the upcoming next-generation "WELL Cabin GranLuxe" was exhibited at the Tokyo Auto Salon.

The WELL Cabin GranLuxe focuses on providing a heightened sightseeing experience and features an exterior designed in collaboration with Italian design studio Italdesign, featuring a traditional Japanese motif of blue ocean waves.
The GranLuxe cabin includes comfortable seats, elegant indirect lighting, acoustic speakers, and a large 55-inch transmissive screen.
Occupants can watch informational tourism videos or ambient videos like campfire scenes on the display, with lighting linked to the video content.
Presentation techniques are used to prevent car sickness, including a transparent front-facing view that blends real-world scenery with video content.
The GranLuxe features "WELL Attendant," an AI concierge that conveys information about sightseeing destinations like history or culture through natural conversation with an AI avatar.
WELL Attendant answered questions instantly and naturally, taking a slight pause after the user finished speaking to avoid talking over them.
The AI concierge supports numerous languages including English, Chinese, French, Mandarin, Cantonese, and various dialects from across Japan.
The goals of the AI concierge are to provide greater "Joy in Motion" and help enterprise operators address issues like a lack of sightseeing guides or difficulty providing multilingual support.
Future plans for the sightseeing info include optimizing it based on passenger states such as facial expressions.
PAS's vision is to become a "Joy in Motion" design company, advancing intensely human technologies to turn mobility into a partner that provides wonderful experiences.
source_title: We’ve entered an era in which cars watch over and accommodate people.In this report, we look at the Tokyo Auto Salon’s “Joy in Motion” design booth. | Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd.
