facts: Tesla battery cell supplier Panasonic broke ground on a new lithium-ion battery manufacturing facility in De Soto, Kansas.
The facility contributes approximately 30 GWh of annual capacity in North America.
The facility will focus on rapidly ramping up the manufacture of 2170 cylindrical cells to match surging domestic demand within the United States.
Panasonic is planning to mass produce the battery cells by March 2025.
Upon completion, the facility will add 30 GWh of capacity of battery supply to EV companies in the United States.
Kazuo Tadanobu serves as President and CEO of Panasonic Energy Co., Ltd.
Tadanobu stated that the groundbreaking is an important step towards expanding production capacity in North America to meet rapidly growing demand for lithium-ion batteries.
The construction of the additional battery production plant in Kansas reflects Panasonic’s intention to dominate as an EV battery supplier for numerous automotive companies in the United States.
Panasonic already supplies batteries for Tesla through Gigafactory Nevada.
The Kansas production facility is expected to funnel cells to Gigafactory Texas, where Tesla utilizes 2170 and 4680 cells for recent Model Y builds.
Panasonic selected Kansas as the plant location in July after potentially considering land plots in nearby Oklahoma.
Kansas offered advantages including a diverse talent pool expected to fulfill 4,000 jobs at the factory.
Panasonic is investing $4 billion into the plant.
Tadanobu said the facility will help strengthen production capacity in an increasing demand market.
While only explicitly mentioning 2170 cells, the company is expected to eventually transition to produce 4680 cells at the plant.
Tesla and Panasonic have been working together to develop the 4680 cell, which Tesla unveiled at its Battery Day event in late 2020.
Panasonic reportedly sent 4680 samples to Tesla in June.
Large-scale prototype production of the 4680 cells is expected to launch in Japan in May 2023, according to Tadanobu.
Tesla issued an open challenge called JUNI x Tesla Battery Cell Giga Challenge targeting startups to bring battery technology solutions directly to Gigafactory Berlin.
The startup challenge opened applications with a deadline of July 24, 2026.
The program targets startups with solutions that can make battery cell manufacturing faster, cheaper, safer, and more scalable at an industrial level.
On May 12, 2026, Giga Berlin plant manager André Thierig announced a $250 million investment to scale the factory’s annual 4680 cell production capacity from 8 GWh to 18 GWh.
The expansion is expected to create more than 1,500 new jobs.
Combined with a previously announced battery investment at the Grunheide site, Tesla's total approaches $1.2 billion.
The challenge looks specifically for startups with proven solutions across five categories: materials, equipment, operations, automation, and artificial intelligence.
Applications are screened directly by Tesla’s cell manufacturing team in Grunheide, moving through technical discussions, a pitch day, and potentially a paid pilot project.
Tesla requires applicants to demonstrate working prototypes, test data, or prior pilots before being considered.
Elon Musk first announced plans for what he called the world’s largest battery cell production facility alongside Giga Berlin in 2020, targeting up to 250 GWh of annual capacity.
Those initial plans were shelved in 2022 when Tesla shifted its battery investment focus to the United States to take advantage of Inflation Reduction Act incentives.
The revival of cell production at Giga Berlin is backed by over $1 billion in committed capital.
The 4680 format is central to Tesla’s long-term cost reduction strategy across vehicles, energy storage, the Tesla Semi, and Cybercab.
Tesla demonstrated its Cybercab robotaxi at the National Federation of the Blind Annual Convention in Austin, Texas on July 3.
The Cybercab demonstration highlighted accessibility features including Braille lettering on physical controls, space for service animals, and wheelchair-height seating.

entity_name: The Ray Highway Connected Vehicle Demonstration
canonical_name: Georgia Department of Transportation, The Ray, Kia Georgia, and Panasonic Partnership
facts: On May 5, 2022, the Georgia Department of Transportation (GDOT), The Ray, Kia Georgia, and Panasonic demonstrated a fully operational connected vehicle environment along 18 miles of Interstate 85 known as "The Ray Highway."

The demonstration utilized the Cirrus by Panasonic® connected vehicle data management platform that detects real-time roadway events and communicates safety-critical messages to travelers about driving conditions.

The partnership between the four organizations is focused on raising awareness of the life-saving potential of connected vehicle technology as well as deploying real-world use cases with measurable benefits.

GDOT and Kia Georgia vehicles equipped with vehicle-to-everything (V2X) technology were showcased to deliver real-time situational awareness to drivers along this key logistics and manufacturing corridor in West Georgia.
GDOT, The Ray, and Panasonic are providing "ride-along" experiences for stakeholders where drivers will be warned of hazardous conditions ahead on the roadway before they come into view.

These ride-along experiences showcase how connected vehicle technology can detect and warn drivers about weather events, work zones, hard braking, and vehicle crashes in a real-world highway environment.

The experience includes an in-vehicle visualization of traveler warnings, V2X technology capabilities installed on and along roadways, digital communication enabling connected roadway intelligence, and the Cirrus cloud platform managing data for GDOT to detect events and provide real-time alerts.

Russell McMurry, Commissioner of the Georgia Department of Transportation, stated that Georgia DOT leads in deploying transportation technology to benefit drivers, move freight across the region more efficiently and safely, and advance Georgia as the technology epicenter of the east coast.

McMurry emphasized that connected vehicle technology will save lives and expressed determination to see it implemented in Georgia as soon as possible, highlighting the power of partnerships with Kia, The Ray, and Panasonic.

Stuart Countess, President and CEO of Kia Georgia, stated that customer safety is the number one priority for Kia Georgia team members and that the V2X program is a forward-thinking initiative putting Kia at the heart of solutions where vehicles and infrastructure communicate seamlessly to enhance highway safety and efficiency.
Countess noted Kia Georgia's pride in partnering with these organizations while showcasing products built by the Kia team in West Point, Georgia.

Allie Kelly, Executive Director of The Ray, stated that connected vehicle technology creates an opportunity to increase travel efficiency and driver safety simultaneously, requiring investments in physical and digital infrastructure for connected and autonomous vehicles.

Kelly noted that Georgia DOT and The Ray have made investments to improve and modernize transportation infrastructure and offer real-time warnings about crashes, construction work zones, hard braking, and weather impacts like black ice, flooding, and hydroplaning.

Chris Armstrong, Vice President of Cirrus at Panasonic's Smart Mobility Office, stated that the project solves transportation challenges by bringing perspectives from public, private, and philanthropic organizations united in improving safety and championing wide-scale adoption of life-saving technology.
Panasonic Corporation of North America is a Newark, NJ-based company and the principal North American subsidiary of Osaka, Japan-based Panasonic Holdings Corporation.

Panasonic is a leading provider of Consumer Lifestyle technologies, Smart Mobility, Sustainable Energy, Immersive Experiences, and Integrated Supply Chain solutions, and was named one of Interbrand's Top 100 Best Global Brands of 2021.
Panasonic serves as a technology partner and integrator to businesses, government agencies, and consumers across the region.

links.context: Learn more about Panasonic's ideas and innovations

source_title: Georgia Department of Transportation Demonstrates Safer, Connected Roadways with Kia Georgia and Panasonic Smart Mobility Technology on "The Ray Highway"

facts: Established in 1914, Peterson Spring is the largest privately held spring manufacturer in North America.
The company designs, manufactures, and supplies springs, snap-rings, wire forms, clips, clamps, stampings, and a variety of sub-assemblies.
Peterson Spring has expanded to 12 worldwide locations.
The company address is 21200 Telegraph Road, Southfield, Michigan, United States ZIP: 48033.
The mission of Peterson American Corporation is to work with customers to provide the best products available to the world market.
The company aims to improve quality and reduce costs through practical, innovative thinking while maintaining equitable relationships with customers, employees, shareholders, and communities.
Peterson Performance Culture emphasizes discipline, hard work, integrity, operational and business excellence, and innovation by all.
The company's history begins in the 1880s with the immigration of Norwegian blacksmith August Christian Peterson to America.
August studied blacksmithing and gradually rose through spring manufacturing ranks in the early 1900s.
In 1914, August moved his family to Detroit to form a company meeting demand for springs in the railroad industry and the budding automobile industry.
August worked with his eldest son Alfred, who left school after eighth grade to work in steel mills' spring plants.
Other sons Sigurd, Conrad, and Harold joined the business later; Sigurd made the greatest contribution as a mechanic who developed innovative machines.
Precision Spring prospered alongside the automobile industry and post-WWII manufacturing explosion.
The 1960s and 1970s saw overseas expansion through purchases of foreign firms and joint ventures.
In 1978, the company split into two halves with one renamed Peterson American Corporation.
The entire Peterson family of companies was brought back under the PAC umbrella in 1991 when they purchased the assets of the departed half.
Peterson Spring is a founding member of the Spring Makers Institute (SMI) since its inception in 1933.
Former employees Bud and Pete Peterson, grandson and great-grandson of founder August Christian Peterson, have both served two-year terms as president of SMI.
In 1989, Bud received the SMI’s Golden Coil Award for contributions to the spring industry.
Numerous Peterson Spring employees have served on SMI committees.
Peterson American Corporation operates 13 facilities worldwide with a geographic footprint allowing customer focus and competitiveness.
All facilities are TS/ISO/QS certified and hold numerous customer achievement and recognition awards.
Decades of innovating thinking in the high-performance automotive market have given Peterson Spring knowledge to thrive in fields implementing springs.
Peterson’s metallurgists, engineers, and designers create springs used daily in products ranging from lawnmowers to military applications.
source_title: Peterson Spring. United States,Michigan,Southfield, Steel/Iron Company | ScrapMonster

facts: Peterson Spring, also known as Peterson American Corporation (PAC), has launched a new master distribution program with Signature Engineered Solutions (SES).

The partnership aims to service and support industrial customers while allowing Peterson to remain focused on leading research and technologies to deliver world-class metal engineered components for the industrial, automotive, and motorsports industries.

Steven Crain, CEO of Peterson American Corporation, stated that as a 105-year-old company, they chose SES as their master stocking distributor to simplify and improve service and support for industrial customers.

Peterson’s research and development utilizes advanced engineering expertise, racing technology, and validation laboratories to develop new products for global customers that reduce assembly time, cost, and weight.

Steven Crain noted that the company's experience and technical know-how have earned credibility in the industry, with customers and suppliers relying on them to solve problems and identify opportunities for improvement.
Peterson Spring is owned by MiddleGround Capital and is one of the largest privately owned spring making groups in North America.
The company specializes in designing, prototyping, testing, and manufacturing springs, rings, spring steel stampings, and wire forms for various markets.
PAC currently operates 12 manufacturing and distribution facilities across four countries: the United States, Canada, Mexico, and the United Kingdom.
Peterson supplies over 450 customers in 48 different countries.

Signature Engineered Solutions provides lean product assembly solutions for original equipment manufacturers and related supply bases for all spring and fastener requirements from concept to production.
source_title:
Peterson Spring Teams Up With Distribution to Create Game-Changing Experiences With Industrial Customers |

facts: Piolax Corporation is a U.S. subsidiary of Piolax Inc. that produces and sells automotive parts such as precision springs, plastic and metal fasteners, and fuel system components.
Piolax established a new logistics base for automotive parts at its main plant in Georgia (GA).
The company introduced an automated pallet warehouse and a high-speed rail-guided vehicle system called “STV” to handle increased goods volume due to growing demand.
Prior to the new facility, Piolax conducted its logistics operations at an external warehouse adjacent to the main plant.
Finished auto parts were previously stored on pallet racking with a capacity of about 3,100 pallets, and forklifts were used to move them in and out of the warehouse.
A significant increase in the volume of goods over the past five years made the previous storage capacity and processing capacity for warehousing, shipping, and picking insufficient.
Piolax built a 92,000m2 logistics center on the plant site in October 2016 to reduce warehouse rent, improve operation efficiency, and increase safety levels.
The new automated warehouse features five stacker cranes with a storage capacity of 5,632 pallets.
The storage capacity increased by approximately 80% compared to the previous system to accommodate future increases in goods volume.
Seven STVs operate in a loop system, transporting goods at high speed between the automated warehouse and the receiving, shipping, and picking stations.
Operating the STV system increased processing capacity and reduced worker waiting time.
The new system drastically reduced the time required for logistics operations.
Only two forklifts are needed instead of the six to eight that were previously required.
Inventory management and First-In First-Out operations have been thoroughly carried out, enabling prompt and accurate delivery of goods according to the drawback.
Piolax reported that the new system achieved cost reduction and safety assurance goals, improved the work environment, and reduced overtime hours.
The company stated it will consider installing automated warehouses at its other locations in the future.
source_title: Piolax Corporation | Case Studies | Solutions | DAIFUKU

Cherokee County has a strong advanced manufacturing sector that includes automotive, aerospace, and medical industries.
The county takes advantage of its proximity to global markets, a skilled workforce, and transportation opportunities to expand manufacturer reach and productivity.
Cherokee County is an ideal destination for advanced manufacturing companies, offering a combination of a skilled workforce, superior infrastructure, and strategic market access.
Businesses can draw from a labor force of nearly 2.5 million from within Cherokee’s labor market area.
46.4% of residents in Cherokee County hold a bachelor’s degree or higher.

facts: Pirelli Motorcycle Tire North America, LLC reorganized its roadrace distribution and service partnerships effective immediately as of December 23, 2011.
Distributor CT Racing will sell and service Pirelli road racing DOT, slick and racing rain tires in the western U.S.
Orion Motorsports will continue to sell product and service customers in the Canadian and eastern US markets.
Beginning in 2012, Pirelli will offer 125 GP/Moto 3 Slick and racing rain tires along with DOT tires (Diablo Rosso II).
New tire sizes will be available for the popular Kawasaki EX250 and Honda CBR250 model sports bikes.
Pirelli Tire North America, LLC designs, develops, manufactures and markets tires for passenger vehicles in both the original equipment and replacement markets.
The company also markets and distributes tires for motorcycles and motorsports.
Pirelli’s manufacturing facility is located in Rome, Georgia.
Pirelli’s Modular Integrated Robotized System (MIRS) employs state-of-the-art technology to manufacture tires for both export and domestic markets.

entity_name: Pirelli Tire North America (PTNA) Rome Facility
facts: PTNA rolled out four new tires in a two-week worldwide event attended by dozens of enthusiast, tire and financial journalists and well over 100 North American dealers.
The event included a ride-and-drive introduction to the new products at Road Atlanta raceway.
The event included a tour of PTNA’s new headquarters and tire plant in Rome, Ga.
Introduced was the Scorpion STR, a T-, H- and V-rated light truck/SUV radial.
Introduced was the Scorpion Zero Asimmetrico, a W-rated high performance light truck/SUV line.
Introduced was the P Zero Nero M+S, an all-weather extension of the W-rated ultra-high performance line.
Introduced was the P Zero Corsa, a Y-rated DOT tire designed for road and racetrack use.
The Georgia plant features Pirelli’s Modular Integrated Robotized System (MIRS), its fully automated production process.
The Georgia plant has three complete MIRS modules running, producing some 350 tires per module daily but not at full speed as yet.
The plant plans to add two more MIRS modules by early 2004.
The Rome facility has some 460,000 square feet of production space under roof.
The facility's space is enough for 20 MIRS modules.
Officials say there is enough land to build two more like-sized buildings at the Rome facility.
Each MIRS module uses only 3,767 square feet of floor space.
Each module produces and assembles all tire components, including beads, steel belts, casing plies, and cap plies.
Each module can simultaneously produce six different sizes of the same tire.
Gaetano Mannino is PTNA’s president of CEO.
MIRS is being used to produce motorcycle tires in Europe.
MIRS is being tested for production of medium truck tires.
There are no short- or mid-term plans to produce motorcycle or medium truck tires for North America according to Mannino.
Pirelli will open a new tire plant in Bahia, Brazil, in early 2004.
The Brazil plant is a traditional manufacturing facility that will exclusively service the North American market.
PTNA’s strategy is to build replacement business through its dealers and by winning key OE fitments.
To date, PTNA has OE contracts with Mercedes-Benz, BMW, Volvo, Jaguar, Saab, Audi and Porsche.
PTNA has earned new business on top end Lincoln and Ford models.
The new Scorpion Zero Asimmetrico is OE on Dodge’s 500 hp Ram SRT 10.
Pirelli has a position on an undetermined Ford Focus model.
The company is working with Ford, GM and others on new fitments for 2004.
PTNA has hired Value Partners to conduct customer satisfaction surveys of Pirelli dealers to determine where it needs to improve.
PTNA launched a Web site component that allows dealers to order tires and track tires online through delivery.
PTNA’s new incentive program will reward dealers with their choice of new vehicles based on order levels from $2 million on down.
Dealers can choose a new Maserati Coupe, Porsche Cayenne, Ford Excursion, Dodge Ram SRT10, Volvo XC70 or XC90, Ford Focus 2X3 or BMW Mini Cooper based on order levels.
Orders of $150,000 earn Panoz racing school packages.
Scorpion STR is M+S rated and offers sizing covering nearly 85% of light duty vehicles in North America.
PTNA has 29 sizes of the T-, H- and V-rated tire, covering 15- to 18-inch wheels.
Three extra load sizes are also available for the Scorpion STR.
The Scorpion Zero Asimmetrico was created for the growing LT performance tuner segment.
The Scorpion Zero Asimmetrico line features 10 sizes, all extra load, covering 21- to 24-inch wheels.
The P Zero Nero M+S was developed for the sport tuner market and provides excellent wet and dry handling and grip.
The P Zero Nero line is available in 14 sizes covering 16- to 19-inch wheels.
The P Zero Corsa has 13-sizes, is Y-rated, created for "extreme driving," and is available for 17 and 18-inch wheel diameters.

links.context: Dealer tire ordering and tracking website

source_title: When in Rome - Tire Review Magazine

facts: Pirelli will start production of Cyber Tyre at its Rome, Georgia plant.
Investment and output expansion details for the project will be communicated in the coming months following the finalization of development plans.
The Georgia plant is already dedicated to producing technologically advanced products for the U.S. market, including the High-Value segment and Motorsport.
The production start highlights the strategic importance of the U.S. market for Pirelli's global growth roadmap.
The announcement coincides with Pirelli's participation in the SelectUSA Investment Summit, a flagship event promoted by the U.S. Department of Commerce.
Cyber Tyre is being showcased at the summit as a core innovation shaping the future of smart mobility.
Cyber Tyre is the world's first hardware-and-software system capable of collecting data and information from sensors embedded in tires.
The system processes sensor data through Pirelli's proprietary software and algorithms.
It communicates in real time with a vehicle's electronics to enable new functionalities integrated with driving and control systems.
These functionalities enhance the driving experience, increase safety levels, and support connected infrastructure.
At the SelectUSA summit, Cyber Tyre was showcased at the Georgia State booth.
Pirelli has been established in Georgia for more than 20 years.

Claudio Zanardo, CEO of Pirelli North America, stated that the start of Cyber Tyre production in Rome, Georgia is a significant milestone reflecting the company's commitment to bringing advanced technologies closer to the market and strengthening its industrial footprint and innovation capabilities in the United States.
Pirelli is finalizing the introduction of the latest version of the MIRS (Modular Integrated Robotized System) production process at the Rome plant.
The MIRS process will be the most advanced manufacturing process for high-end, premium production within the entire Pirelli Group and is exclusive to the Georgia factory.
MIRS enhances robotized production capabilities, increasing productivity, flexibility, and quality.
The system is highly digitalized and enables a direct link between product design and its industrial application.
This development lays the groundwork for growth in Pirelli's production capacity in Rome as part of the Group's industrial development plans.
The Rome, Georgia plant specializes in high-value tire production for the North American market.
The facility hosts a dedicated R&D center to strengthen its role in technological development and product innovation.
The facility is recognized for responsible sourcing practices, including the use of FSC®-certified natural rubber.
source_title: PIRELLI WILL START CYBE TYRE PRODUCTION IN GEORGIA UNDERLINING THE STRATEGIC IMPORTANCE OF THE U.S. MARKET

facts: Pirelli is to start producing its Cyber Tyre connected tyre technology at its Rome, Georgia plant in the US.
Investment and output details will follow once development plans are finalised.
The announcement marks the technology’s first US production location.
The Italian firm is pitching the move as a deepening of its industrial commitment to a strategic market.
Cyber Tyre is a hardware-and-software system using sensors embedded in the tyre to collect operating data.
Operating data is processed through Pirelli’s proprietary algorithms and communicated in real time to the vehicle’s electronics.
The technology supports new driving functionalities, safety features and connected infrastructure use cases.
Claudio Zanardo, Chief Executive of Pirelli North America, stated that the start of Cyber Tyre production in Rome, Georgia is a significant milestone for Pirelli in this country.
Zanardo noted the move reflects a commitment to bringing advanced technologies like Cyber Tyre closer to the market.
The Rome plant is being upgraded with the latest version of Pirelli’s Modular Integrated Robotized System (MIRS) process.
The MIRS upgrade will be the most advanced across the Pirelli Group and exclusive to Georgia.
The site has operated for more than 20 years and houses a dedicated R&D centre.
Pirelli is showcasing Cyber Tyre at the SelectUSA Investment Summit at the Georgia State booth.
The showcase is timed to coincide with the US Department of Commerce-promoted event.
source_title: Cyber Tyre output starts at Pirelli's Georgia factory | Automotive World

facts: Pirelli will begin production of its Cyber Tyre at its Georgia facility, with further details expected in the coming months.
The company’s management is set to provide an update on this development during its first quarter 2026 results on Thursday.
Pirelli’s only production facility in Georgia, located in Rome, is relatively small and currently satisfies approximately 5% of the company’s US exposure.

While Cyber Tyre represents a small volume product, the commencement of production indicates that the US Department of Commerce is now more comfortable with Pirelli undertaking capital expenditure in the US and producing this technology-rich product.

Citi analysts view this development as long-term positive for Pirelli, suggesting it may indicate improved ability for the company to undertake US factory expansion following recent governance changes.
The analysts believe any such capacity expansion can be funded with existing capital expenditure budgets, enabling a more local manufacturing footprint over time.
source_title: Pirelli starting Cyber Tyre production in Georgia facility is a positive for stock: analyst By Investing.com

entity_name: Prometeon Tyre Group Commercial Solutions L.L.C.
canonical_name: Prometeon Tyre Group Commercial Solutions L.L.C.
facts: Prometeon Tyre Group Commercial Solutions L.L.C. has launched a Pirelli-brand regional-application truck tire series designed specifically for North American conditions.
The company plans to add a complementary Tier 2 brand before year-end.
The operations are located in Rome, Ga.
source_title: Prometeon launches Pirelli-brand regional truck tire line for North America - Tire Business

facts: Pirelli Tire North America (PTNA) services NAFTA (North American Free Trade Agreement) member countries through its Modular Integrated Robotized System (MIRS) facility and research and development center at its Rome, Georgia headquarters.
PTNA operates a state-of-the-art manufacturing plant in Silao, Mexico.
The company manufactures, distributes and markets original equipment and replacement tires for export and domestic car/motorcycle applications (US, CA, MX) and Agro/Heavy Truck platforms (MX).
Pirelli is the fifth largest tire manufacturer in the world in terms of sales.
Pirelli is present in over 160 countries.
Pirelli has 22 manufacturing sites globally.
Pirelli employs approximately 36,000 people around the world.
Pirelli is a leading producer of premium tires due to its commitment to R&D.
Pirelli has successfully competed in motorsport since 1907.
Pirelli is the exclusive supplier of the Superbike world championship and many single marque championships around the world.
Pirelli was the sole supplier for the Formula 1 championship for the 3-year period 2011-13.
The Angel GT tire features a new tread pattern and bi-compound technology, offering a 30 percent increase in mileage over the ST model.
The Angel GT reduces braking distance by approximately three feet at speeds of 47 mph and boasts improved grip and handling in wet conditions.
Pirelli partners with AIMExpo for promotional events, including VIP tours during the inaugural show in Orlando from October 16–20.
source_title: Pirelli Announces "Fly Into Summer" Angel GT Promotion - Roadracing World Magazine | Motorcycle Riding, Racing & Tech News

facts: Pirelli SpA formally opened its new $300 million passenger and light truck/SUV tire plant in Silao, Mexico.
The Silao plant is Pirelli’s first facility in Mexico and 22nd globally.
The facility is part of a planned $400 million investment in tire production in Mexico.
The plant will produce higher-end performance and light truck/SUV tires for the U.S., Canada, and Mexico at an initial rate of 400,000 annually by the end of 2012.
Targeted capacity expansions will increase production to 3.5 million tires per year in 2015 after the first phase of development.
The facility will reach full operational capacity of 5.5 million units annually in 2017.

Mexico President Felipe Calderón Hinojosa attended the May 31 opening ceremony, arriving via helicopter to meet Pirelli executives and unveil a ceremonial plaque alongside Chairman and CEO Marco Tronchetti Provera.
The plant supports Pirelli’s premium strategy by providing a shortened supply chain and increased capacity for North American customers.
Prior to this facility, the NAFTA market was served mostly from Brazil, with partial supply from Europe and China.
Marco Tronchetti Provera stated the new plant allows Pirelli to deliver tires anywhere in the U.S. within four days, compared to 45 days from Brazil.
The new facility will enable Pirelli to service customers that were previously unable to be supplied.
The initial phase consists of a 135,000-square-meter building and represents a $300 million initial investment.
An additional $100 million will be spent on the project by 2017.
The facility will employ approximately 1,000 workers through 2013.
Additional hiring is planned to bring total employment to 1,800 in 2017.
Pirelli stated the new plant will augment its existing factory in Rome, Ga., which specializes in production using MIRS technology.
With the new plant, Pirelli’s North American production will reach 3.9 million tires in 2015.
North American production is projected to rise to 5.9 million pieces in 2017.
The new facility increases Pirelli’s local NAFTA production share from 6% in 2011 to 11% in 2012, and finally to 53% in 2015.
Paolo Ferrari, recently appointed chairman and CEO of Pirelli Tire North America (PTNA), outlined near-term market share goals during the ceremony.
Ferrari stated that premium market share is currently around 6% in the U.S. and 15% in Canada.
As part of a three-year plan, PTNA aims to grow premium volume at least twice as fast as the overall market, potentially gaining two to three points of market share.
Ferrari attributed this growth strategy to improved service and supply plans, upcoming launches of significant new products, and an aggressive marketing plan.

Tom Gravalos, PTNA vice president of marketing, motorsports and original equipment, stated the company wants to set a new standard in product launches and maximize the hundreds of millions invested in the Silao plant.
Pirelli officials planned to announce new products coinciding with Formula One's return to the U.S. at November’s F1 race in Austin, Texas.
source_title: $300 Million Mexico Plant is Cornerstone of Pirelli's N.A. Growth Targets - Tire Review Magazine

facts: On August 10, 2023, Pirelli North America announced a new partnership with The Ray, a non-profit organization based in Atlanta.
The Ray has helped develop a net-zero highway testbed on an 18-mile stretch of Interstate 85 in West Georgia, located near Pirelli's factory in Rome.

The partnership aims to advance research and solutions in road safety technology, the circular economy, EV charging infrastructure, renewable energy, and the environmental and social impacts of tires and roads.
As part of the non-profit’s Advisory Board, Pirelli will contribute to strategic thinking around future roadways.
Pirelli is investigating future projects with The Ray to research the impact of rubber-modified asphalt.
Pirelli is also investigating the use of tire-derived aggregate for stormwater galleries.
The brand is joining a multi-tiered partnership focused on tires and the circular economy to drive education, innovation, solutions development, and sharing of best practices.

Maureen Kline, Vice President of Public Affairs and Sustainability at Pirelli North America, stated that being part of sustainability solutions extending beyond their product is important to their climate action commitment.
Kline noted that tires are the only thing between the vehicle and the road, and working with The Ray allows them to identify challenges, develop solutions, and drive real change.
Pirelli believes tire design will play a key role in shaping the future of mobility and demonstrated its commitment to pursuing increasingly challenging sustainability goals through this partnership.
The company has set goals to increase the renewable and recycled content of tires and reduce environmental impacts.
Pirelli is focusing on mitigation strategies for broader product impacts, including end-of-life tires and tire and road wear particles.
Allie Kelly, Executive Director of The Ray, stated that Pirelli leads from Georgia with an advanced manufacturing facility that was the first FSC-certified tire plant in the world.

Kelly added that the alignment between the organizations, focused on safety, circularity, and innovation, will power the partnership to produce groundbreaking projects and research in the coming years, benefiting communities across the country.
Pirelli's Rome, Georgia headquarters includes a Modular Integrated Robotized System (MIRS) facility and a research and development center.
The Rome location is described as a state-of-the-art manufacturing plant.
Pirelli Tire North America also operates a manufacturing plant in Silao, Mexico, and sales and marketing offices in New York City, Los Angeles, Detroit, Montreal, and Mexico City.
The company manufactures, distributes, and markets original equipment and replacement tires for export and domestic car and motorcycle applications.
Pirelli was founded in Milan in 1872 and produces innovative tires for cars, motorcycles, and bicycles tailored to specific consumer needs through partnerships with prestigious manufacturers.
The Ray is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit charity and net-zero highway testbed located on 18 miles of Interstate 85 between Lagrange, Georgia, and the Georgia-Alabama state line.
The highway stretch is named in memory of Ray C. Anderson (1934-2011), a Georgia native and green business leader who challenged Interface, Inc. to pursue a zero environmental footprint.

The Ray's mission is to reimagine community connections in a way that is safer, more climate-responsive, regenerative to the environment, and capable of creating economic opportunity through innovative transportation infrastructure ideas and technologies.
The Ray Highway aims to pave the way for a zero carbon, zero waste, zero deaths highway system.

Current The Ray projects include using highway rights-of-way for solar arrays, cloud-based data management for connected vehicle safety and efficiency, drones used as first responders, pollinator gardens for endangered species, and further deployment of bioswales.
The Ray defines net-zero transportation as “Zero Deaths, Zero Waste, Zero Carbon and Zero Impact.”

The organization facilitates building and scaling clean transportation and energy projects with state Departments of Transportation, tollway and turnpike agencies, regional collaboratives, and local governments.
Kelly stated that the partnership with Pirelli will bring The Ray one step closer to achieving its net-zero transportation goals.

source_title: PIRELLI ANNOUNCES PARTNERSHIP WITH THE RAY, A GEORGIA BASED NON PROFIT ORGANIZATION, WITH A MISSION TO DRIVE  MORE SUSTAINABLE SOLUTIONS  FOR FUTURE MOBILITY  - Truck Industry Today - EIN Presswire

facts: Pirelli announced that it is working with Fisker Inc. to create advanced low rolling resistance tires for the new Fisker EMotion luxury electric vehicle, set to launch in late 2019.
Pirelli will develop summer P Zero tires in the following sizes for the Fisker EMotion: 285/35R24 (F) and 305/35R24 (R).
The focus of this tire development will be on low rolling resistance, spirited handling and extra comfort due to tall sidewalls.

Pierluigi Dinelli, CEO and chairman of Pirelli Tire North America, stated the partnership underscores Pirelli’s commitment to innovation from the track to the street and aligns with a common goal of pushing the boundaries of premium driving performance.
The Fisker EMotion is an AWD sedan featuring a range of more than 400 miles.
The EMotion includes hardware to support fully autonomous driving.
The EMotion features fast-charging capability that adds 125 miles in 9 minutes.
The EMotion has internet connectivity enabling automatic updates to be streamed directly to the vehicle.

Henrik Fisker, chairman and CEO of Fisker Inc., is the prominent automotive designer behind the EMotion, renowned for past work on iconic creations like the BMW Z8, Aston Martin DB9, VLF Force 1, Karma and more.
The EMotion features never-before-seen design elements that reflect the brand’s supercar DNA.

Henrik Fisker stated that incorporating Pirelli’s low-rolling resistance technology into the EMotion really completes the equation where groundbreaking supercar design elements and technology meet the very best in performance.
The tires will be produced in Rome, Georgia, strengthening Pirelli’s ‘local for local’ approach in North America.
Fisker is also exploring utilizing the Pirelli Noise Cancelling System and connected tire technology features.
The Fisker EMotion will start at $129,900.
The Fisker EMotion is slated for production in the United States.
source_title: Pirelli Partners With Fisker On New 'EMotion' Vehicle Launch

facts: Pirelli Tire North America is categorized under Tire Manufacturers.
The company was established in 1997.
It was incorporated in Georgia.
Current estimates show this company has an annual revenue of 652168.
The company employs a staff of approximately 200.
The location type is unknown.

Kevin E Riddett serves as President.

facts: Pirelli Tire North America (New Haven, Conn.) has started construction on a tire manufacturing plant in Rome, Georgia.
The company awarded H&M Company Incorporated (Jackson, TN) a design-build contract for a 400,000 square foot building that will include manufacturing, offices and research facilities.
Pirelli is providing engineering services for the approximately $140 million project.
Site work started this past March and the plant is expected to be operational by mid 2002.
The plant will manufacture tires for new vehicles and for the replacement tire markets utilizing Pirelli's recently developed Modular Integrated Robotized System (MIRS).
The MIRS process allows completion of the entire production cycle from compounding to finished product without interruption or human intervention.
Initially, the plant will be equipped with four MIRS modules, each capable of producing approximately one tire every three minutes.
Pirelli plans to add a total of fifteen modules.
The Rome site will serve as the company's North American headquarters.
The Rome site will replace a plant in Hanford, California that was closed earlier this year.
In total, 55 corporate employees and 250 production workers will be employed after three years.
Pirelli S.p.A controls all of Pirelli's operations worldwide.
Pirelli S.p.A has holdings in 24 countries and 5 continents.
source_title: Pirelli Builds New $140 Million Tire Plant in Rome, Georgia

entity_name: Toyo Tire North America Manufacturing Inc.
canonical_name: Toyo Tire North America Manufacturing Inc.
facts: Gov. Nathan Deal announced on November 20, 2018 that Toyo Tire North America Manufacturing Inc. will create more than 150 jobs and invest over $138 million in Cartersville.
New jobs created by the investment will include positions in production, support, and administration.
Gov. Nathan Deal stated Georgia is the No. 1 state for business and offers strategic resources necessary to retain dynamic companies and support industry leaders in long-term growth.

Gov. Nathan Deal noted Toyo Tires is a valued member of Georgia’s family of innovative automotive manufacturers and suppliers, and its leadership recognizes the benefits of operating in Georgia's competitive economic environment.

Gov. Nathan Deal said by choosing Cartersville over international competition, Toyo Tires will continue to hire from Georgia’s talented workforce and utilize highly connected transportation infrastructure to reach its growing customer base.
Gov. Nathan Deal expressed appreciation for Toyo Tires’ commitment to creating more quality jobs in Georgia and looked forward to further strengthening their partnership.
Through a two-phase expansion, Toyo Tires will construct a new 254,000-square-foot facility.
The new facility will have an annual production capacity of 2.4 million passenger vehicle tires.
Upon completion of the expansion, Toyo Tires will employ approximately 2,100 Georgia residents.
Don Bunn, president of Toyo Tire North America Manufacturing Inc., said the level of cooperation from the State of Georgia and Bartow County over the years continues to exceed expectations.

Don Bunn stated Toyo Tires is proud to be a member of the Georgia business community and strives to show appreciation by being a great place to work and supporting community organizations throughout the state.
In 2016, Toyo Tire North America Manufacturing Inc. became the Toyo Tires Group’s largest manufacturing operation with an annual production capacity of 11.5 million tires.
Bartow County Sole Commissioner Steve Taylor said Bartow County was fortunate to be selected as Toyo Tires’ home years ago and is honored each time they choose to invest again.
Steve Taylor stated the county strives for long-term partnerships with industries, noting this partnership has been incredibly successful.
Steve Taylor said Toyo Tires has had a significant impact on Georgia’s automotive sector and Bartow’s economy, with many accolades to show for it.
Steve Taylor appreciated Toyo Tires' corporate citizenship, citing donations and volunteer hours to schools, environmental causes, and citizens in need.
Steve Taylor highlighted the company’s annual commitment to provide food for the community during the holiday season as a significant example of corporate citizenship.
Steve Taylor called Toyo Tires great neighbors and expressed pleasure in supporting the company’s growth again.
The state-of-the-art Toyo Tires manufacturing facility began operations in 2006.
The facility features a proprietary Advanced Tire Operation Module production system.
The facility location was chosen to enhance distribution and inventory availability to meet increasing demands for Toyo and Nitto tires sold through Toyo Tires’ expanding network of retailers.

Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) Director of Existing Industry & Regional Recruitment Lindsay Martin represented the Global Commerce Division in partnership with the Cartersville-Bartow County Department of Economic Development and Georgia Power.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson said Toyo Tires is an outstanding example of an international manufacturer that has found rapid success in Georgia.
Pat Wilson stated Toyo Tires and Bartow County have proven to be a winning team and looked forward to their continued success in Georgia.
Toyo Tire North America Manufacturing Inc. is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Toyo Tire Holdings of Americas Inc.
The company is responsible for the production of both the Toyo Tires and Nitto tire brands.

Toyo Tires maintains a commitment to innovation, quality, performance, and excellent customer service, with significant and continuous investments made in its facility in White, Georgia, to produce high-quality tires.

The company’s tire portfolio covers a broad range of applications, including high-performance racing-inspired tires, rugged off-road tires for light trucks and SUVs, luxury touring tires, and tires for truck and bus applications.
Toyo Tires was named the 2016 Georgia Automotive Supplier of the Year by GDEcD.
source_title: Toyo Tire North America to create more than 150 jobs in Bartow County | WBHF

facts: Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) and Nobu Hotel Atlanta teamed up to create an immersive, first-of-its-kind Porsche-themed experience for travelers from across the globe.
The Nobu Hotel Atlanta is located just north of Porsche’s U.S. headquarters.
The hotel unveiled the Porsche Icon Suite and Porsche Classic Room as part of the collaboration.
This marks the first Nobu property worldwide to offer a Porsche-themed experience.
Ayesha Coker, Vice President of Marketing of PCNA, stated that from sports cars to experiences, the goal is to provide an elevated experience for everyone who engages with their brand.
The collaboration brings together two luxury brands to offer Atlantans and travelers a unique and immersive stay while visiting Atlanta.
Nobu Hotel Atlanta designed rooms and suites that integrate sleek Porsche-inspired art and decor alongside exclusive memorabilia celebrating iconic Porsche moments.
Guests have access to a Porsche-dedicated executive conference room onsite for hosting business meetings.
The property provides all-electric Porsche Taycan house cars for off-site excursions within a three-mile radius, expertly navigated by dedicated personal drivers.
Package offerings include a two-night stay in the Porsche Icon Suite with breakfast for two and Nobu’s Signature Omakase dining experience.
Guests will receive a stylized Nobu Champagne bottle in-room and a personalized Porsche bathrobe to take home.
The Porsche Icon Suite and Porsche Classic Room are available to book now at Nobu Hotel Atlanta.
source_title: Porsche and Nobu Hotel Atlanta debut Porsche-themed experience | horecatrends.com

facts: Porsche has opened the nation’s first freestanding service center on the campus of its North American headquarters near Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport.
The facility is named the Porsche Service Center South Atlanta.
The service center is a 22,000-square foot facility.
12,000 square feet of the facility is dedicated to servicing vehicles.
This project marks the first phase of a multimillion-dollar effort at Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA).
The PCNA initiative also includes a significant expansion of the existing Porsche Experience Center track.
The service center features 13 lift bays.
The interior design utilizes the “Destination Porsche” corporate architecture, which was first piloted in the U.S. at Porsche Palm Springs in 2019.
Kjell Gruner, President and CEO of PCNA, stated that opening the service center kicks off expansion plans at their Atlanta headquarters and highlights a shared commitment to customer experience.
Gruner noted that bringing two dealerships together to create a first-class service experience is a significant milestone.
PCNA expressed appreciation for the dealerships' investment and expects customers will appreciate the new facility as well.
source_title: Porsche opens nation’s first freestanding service center on its Atlanta campus

facts: According to state and local economic development sources, Porsche Cars North America plans to invest $535,000.00 to build out 33,009 square feet of new space in Atlanta, Georgia.
The company plans to occupy the new space at 1 Porsche Dr SW in Atlanta on or about March 1, 2022.
According to the company website, Porsche Cars North America thrives on a foundation of precision engineering fueled by the American entrepreneurial spirit.
The company carries on the vision of founder Ferry Porsche by delivering impassioned experiences as legendary as the cars it builds.

Porsche is fueling 'Porsche Passion' as an unprecedented brand built on the idea of being better together as a diverse and gifted workforce united in the desire to be the world's most aspirational brand and most empowering employer.
As an extension of the Porsche family, each team member brings a unique perspective and master craft to the workforce.
Porsche boasts engineers, brand builders, marketing minds, business development leaders, analytical aficionados, and corporate professionals in the automotive business.
Porsche leads its industry in performance and customer satisfaction.
Klaus Zellmer serves as the Chief Executive Officer of Porsche Cars North America.
source_title: Porsche Cars North America to spend $535,000.00 to occupy 33,009 square feet of space in Atlanta Georgia. - intelligence360

facts: Porsche Financial Services, Inc. (PFS) is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia.
PFS issued an auto Asset Backed Securities (ABS) offering in the USA with a principal amount of $870.4 million dollars.
This transaction marked the second bond issuance for PFS in 2025.
PFS is an indirect, wholly owned subsidiary of German luxury car maker Dr. Ing. h.c. F. Porsche AG ("Porsche AG").
The securities issued in the Rule 144A transaction maintained a 'AAA' rating from the rating agencies and achieved competitive pricing.
The transaction was divided into five tranches, including a floating rate tranche, and was backed by a pool of auto contracts financing Porsche vehicles.
RBC Capital Markets led the transaction, with BofA Securities, Scotiabank, Société Générale, and Truist Securities serving as book runners.
The deal achieved an upsize from a growing investor base comprised of investment funds, asset managers of financial institutions, trusts, banks, and corporates.
Tobias Hausladen serves as Treasurer & Chief Financial Officer for Porsche Financial Services, Inc.

PFS stated that despite launching during one of the busiest weeks in the 2025 ABS primary market, PFAST 2025-1 received solid investor support and reflected confidence in their credit platform and performance consistency.
PFS is the dedicated provider of leasing and financing products for Porsche in the United States.
PFS was founded in 1991 and provides custom financial solutions and products to Porsche customers and dealers in the United States.
In 2012, PFS expanded its North America operations to become the captive finance provider for the exclusive brands of the Volkswagen Group which include Bentley, Lamborghini, and Bugatti.
source_title: Porsche Financial Services, Inc. Issues Prime Auto Transaction in the U.S. ABS Market

facts: A delegation of six Austrian suppliers wrapped up a mid-April visit to the Southeast U.S. with a focus on opportunities in Georgia’s automotive sector.
The visit was hosted by Advantage Austria, represented by Angelika Heitzmann.
The group consisted of six Austrian firms considering expansion into the U.S. market.
The delegation began its itinerary in Greer, S.C., touring a BMW factory described as the automaker’s top export plant globally.
The group also toured an inland port facility in Greer, S.C.
Upon arrival in Atlanta, the delegation was welcomed by Honorary Consul and industrial real estate executive Paul Seefried.
On April 17, the group attended the German-American Chamber of Commerce South’s annual SME Summit in Duluth.
The trip’s theme focused on uncovering the “Best of Automotive & Logistics in the Southeast USA.”
Key discussion topics for the Austrian companies included workforce development, manufacturing innovation, and logistics solutions for market entry or expansion.
The delegation met with Porsche Cars North America CEO Timo Resch at the Porsche Experience Center near the Atlanta airport.
Timo Resch shared insights on Porsche’s growth and its strategic positioning of Atlanta as a nationwide logistics hub.
Rick Walker of the Georgia Automotive Manufacturers Association briefed the group on recent investment announcements by Hyundai, Kia, and Rivian in the region.
The delegation met with Knapp North America, an existing Austrian investor in Georgia that provides warehouse automation technologies.
Knapp North America recently expanded its Kennesaw facility to accommodate a growing workforce.
Knapp invested $7 million in its Kennesaw facility in 2020 to create space for 400 employees, including 150 on the warehouse floor and 250 in the office.
Stefan Harrigan of the Metro Atlanta Chamber pitched Atlanta to the delegation after visiting Austria in the months prior to the mission.
A reception at Arnall Golden Gregory LLP closed out the April 15-18 trip, where attorney Tycho Stahl shared insights on market entry.
The participating Austrian companies were MSG Mechatronic Systems, DEWETRON, Hödlmayr International, CNT Management Consulting, AMAG Austria Metall, and VEGU Precision Turning Parts.
Advantage Austria serves as the Austrian government’s trade promotion arm, with its Atlanta office part of a network spanning New York, Washington D.C., Los Angeles, San Francisco, and Chicago.
source_title: Six Austrian Automotive, Logistics Firms Wrap Up Southeast U.S. Visit in Atlanta - Global Atlanta

facts: Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) announced a new multi-million dollar investment to further develop its North American headquarters and U.S. footprint in Georgia.
The new 33-acre development is located at the Northeast corner of Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport in Atlanta, Georgia, adding to PCNA's existing 27-acre headquarters.
Phase one of the development begins construction in September 2020 with a standalone Porsche Service Center South Atlanta.
The service center will be independently developed, owned, and operated jointly by Hennessy Porsche North Atlanta and Porsche Atlanta Perimeter.
PCNA will invest in a new Porsche Classic Factory Restoration facility and a track extension for the Porsche Experience Center Atlanta over the next three years.

Kjell Gruner, newly appointed President and CEO of PCNA, stated that Porsche is committed to the United States and Atlanta, and sees a prime opportunity to enhance the customer experience by expanding the campus for faster service next to the world's busiest airport.
The Porsche Experience Center Atlanta has already welcomed over 270,000 visitors since opening in 2015.
Governor Brian Kemp noted that Georgia's automotive industry is a $3 billion economic driver in the state and praised Porsche's continued commitment to Georgia.
Mayor Alan Hallman of Hapeville, Georgia, expressed excitement over the expansion, stating it symbolizes tremendous growth and opportunities for the city.

Atlanta Mayor Keisha Lance Bottoms thanked Porsche for investing in Atlanta and stated her administration looks forward to strengthening the local economy through highly skilled and middle-wage opportunities.
The Porsche Service Center South Atlanta will be a 20,815 square foot facility on 2.5 acres of land.
The new service facility will include 13 lift bays for customer vehicles, a Porsche Driver's Selection boutique, and a reception area with a waiting lounge.
It is one of two standalone service centers for Porsche in the U.S., offering greater service capacity and convenience for Atlanta and surrounding cities.
The expected completion date for the service center is the third quarter of 2021.
Phase two of development is expected to begin in 2021 with construction of a parking structure, followed by the Classic Factory Restoration facility and Experience Center track extension.
This second phase of development is expected to be completed in mid-2023.
Established in 1984, PCNA is the exclusive U.S. importer of the Porsche 911, 718 Boxster and Cayman, Macan and Cayenne, Panamera, and Taycan.
PCNA has been headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, since 1998.
The company operates the first Porsche Experience Center in North America featuring a module-based 1.6 mile driver development track, business center, and Restaurant 356.
PCNA also operates a second Porsche Experience Center near Los Angeles, which is a 53-acre complex featuring a 4.1 mile driver development track, business center, and Restaurant 917.
PCNA supports 192 independently owned and operated Porsche dealerships in the U.S., supplying parts, service, marketing, and training.
PCNA is an indirect wholly-owned subsidiary of Porsche AG, which is headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany.
source_title: Porsche Commences New MultiMillion Dollar Development In Georgia Adjacent To Its North American Head

Assessments from the training center are used to formulate unique training plans that maximize efficiency.
The facility includes Restaurant 356, which is named after the first automobile to carry the Porsche family name.

facts: Porsche Cars North America officially opened its new $100 million Porsche Experience Center (PEC) and headquarters in Atlanta, GA.
The 27-acre complex is located at the Northeast corner of Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport.
This facility represents the largest investment ever outside Germany for the sports car manufacturer.
The industry-first facility includes a driver development track, classic car gallery, restoration center, human performance center, driving simulator lab, and a fine dining restaurant.
A state-of-the-art business center features 13,000 square feet of conference and event space.
The experience center is specifically designed as a destination for the public, automotive enthusiasts, and Porsche customers.
An estimated 30,000 guests are expected to visit the Porsche Experience Center each year.
Visitors and corporate groups can hone driving skills, reserve meeting space, view classic Porsche automobiles, or dine at Restaurant 356.
The 1.6-mile Driver Development Track runs through the facility's courtyard and includes six instructor-led driving modules.

The track features a Handling Circuit, Off-Road Course, Dynamics Area, Low Friction Handling Circuit, Low Friction Circle, and the only Kick Plate in North America to demonstrate Porsche engineering and technological capabilities.
Georgia Governor Nathan Deal stated that Porsche's investment represents confidence in the future success of the state, economy, and workforce.
The facility is located at One Porsche Drive, with windows overlooking I-75.
More than 70 alternative locations across multiple states were considered during site selection.
Atlanta was chosen due to its transportation infrastructure, skilled workforce, and strong support from the city and state.
Proximity to the world's busiest airport allows approximately 80 percent of the U.S. population to reach the center within two and a half hours of flying time.

Atlanta Mayor Kasim Reed cited Atlanta's third-highest concentration of Fortune 500 companies, low cost of doing business, high quality of life, and access to efficient passenger airport and fastest-growing container port as key factors.
Porsche Cars North America (PCNA) has been based in the Atlanta area since 1998.
PCNA is now joined by Porsche Financial Services, Porsche Business Services, Porsche Consulting, and Mieschke Hofmann & Partners, with all five entities housed under the same roof.
Ultimately, 450 employees will be based at the new headquarters, over 100 of whom fill positions that are new to Georgia.
PCNA employs approximately 300 people who provide parts, service, marketing, and training for 189 dealers.
PCNA is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Porsche AG, which is headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany.
Established in 1984, PCNA is the exclusive U.S. importer of Porsche 918 Spyder, 911, Boxster and Cayman sports cars, the Macan and Cayenne SUVs, and Panamera sports sedans.

Timo Resch serves as President and CEO of PCNA.

Established in 1984, PCNA is the exclusive U.S. importer of the Porsche 911, 718 Boxster, 718 Cayman, Macan, Cayenne, Panamera and Taycan.
PCNA has been headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia since 1998.
The Atlanta campus is home to the first Porsche Experience Center in North America, which features two module-based 1.6 mile driver development tracks, a business center and Restaurant 356.
The Atlanta campus is also home to the U.S. headquarters of Porsche Classic.

PCNA operates a second Porsche Experience Center near Los Angeles featuring a driver development track with eight educational modules totaling 4.1 miles, a business center, Restaurant 917 and the headquarters of Porsche Motorsport North America.
PCNA supports 198 independently owned and operated Porsche centers, two Porsche studios and five satellite stores in the U.S., including supplying parts, service, marketing, and training.

facts: RealTruck is a global aftermarket product and accessory brand.
RealTruck partnered with survivalist Bear Grylls to design and build two extra-tough concept vehicles.
The concepts were built from a 2021 Ford F-150 and a 2024 Toyota Tacoma.
Both concept trucks are emblazoned with the word “Bear” in honor of Grylls.
Bear Grylls is known for his TV show “Adventure Garage with Bear Grylls,” which airs next year.
The two concepts feature extreme vehicle transformations fit for rugged parts of the world.
The vehicles were built by the Superlift Suspension team in West Monroe, Louisiana.
The concepts are currently on display at the RealTruck booth at this year’s SEMA (Specialty Equipment Market Association) Show.
Companies like RealTruck and eBay Motors have seen a huge surge in accessories sales due to growing interest in overlanding, camping, and off-roading.

The Ford F-150 concept includes a Husky Liners Rocker Guard, Low Profile Vent Visors and LightShield Pro, Bushwacker Pocket Style Fender Flares, Go Rhino Element Front Bumper, Sport Bar 2.0, Dominator Xtreme DT Side Step, and an XVenture Gear Hard Case.

The Toyota Tacoma concept features GoRhino XVenture Gear Tool Rolls and Xplor Blackout Series Maxline Hi/Low LED Round Lights, RealTruck Sport Bar, Husky Liners Weatherbeaters, Universal Mud Guards and Low Profile Vent Visors.
A collaboration with NBA superstar Dwayne Wade features a custom 2017 Ford F-250 Super Duty on display at the SEMA show.

The Dwayne Wade truck modifications include a RealTruck Superlift 6-inch lift kit with King coilovers and rear shocks, RealTruck RetraxPRO MX tonneau cover, RealTruck AMP Research BedStep and Powerstep XL, and Husky Liners Weatherbeater floor liners.
The Dwayne Wade truck will be auctioned off at Barrett-Jackson’s spring event in April, with all proceeds benefiting Wade’s non-profit organization Social Change Fund United (SCFU).
SCFU is co-founded by Dwayne Wade to harness influence and resources to help underrepresented communities of color.
Legendary artist and designer Chip Foose joined forces with RealTruck for another vehicle auctioned for charity.

The Chip Foose vehicle is a 2021 Ford F-150 featuring a traction recovery winch, a Rugged Ridge traction recovery kit, Go Rhino rear and front bumpers, and Husky Liners RVL fender flares, all from RealTruck.

Proceeds from the Chip Foose vehicle will be donated to the Progeria Research Foundation, which helps with treatments and works toward finding a cure for Progeria, a rare genetic premature-aging disease.
source_title: RealTruck Releases Two Bear Grylls Adventure Vehicle Concepts

facts: RealTruck Inc.’s financial performance has been strained by weaker organic sales volumes, higher restructuring charges, and elevated capital expenditure (capex).
The company experienced greater-than-expected negative free operating cash flow (FOCF) in 2024 and tightening liquidity.
S&P Global Ratings revised RealTruck’s outlook to negative from stable and affirmed the 'B-' issuer credit rating.

The negative outlook reflects the possibility of a downgrade over the next 12 months if EBITDA margins do not recover, combined with continued investment in working capital and elevated capex causing sustained negative FOCF.
RealTruck’s top-line growth and margins are expected to remain weaker this year, leading to further cash flow deficits, reduced liquidity, and higher leverage.

EBITDA margins have been weaker over the past several quarters due to lower organic sales volumes and increased restructuring related to the integration of recent acquisitions and expansion of manufacturing in its Mexico plants.
Relocation of some manufacturing to Mexico from the U.S. resulted in better gross margins during 2024, but marketing costs remain elevated.
Ongoing restructuring activity is expected to continue weighing on RealTruck's EBITDA margins.
The company is confronting tariffs and potentially lower consumer demand.
Direct tariff impacts are considered manageable assuming United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA) parts remain exempt, given the company’s large North American manufacturing footprint.
Tariffs are expected to strain U.S. consumer spending, particularly for some of RealTruck's most discretionary products.
Capex has remained elevated as RealTruck ramped production at its facilities in Mexico.
This contributed to four consecutive quarters of cash burn in 2024.
S&P Global Ratings notes a high degree of unpredictability around U.S. administration policy implementation regarding tariffs and potential effects on economies, supply chains, and credit conditions.
For 2025, organic revenues are assumed to decline 2%-5% due to weaker consumer demand.
EBITDA margins are expected to improve to 16.3% from 15.3% in 2024 due to the margin accretive acquisition of Vehicle Accessories Group (VAI) and somewhat lower restructuring.
These projected margins remain lower than historical margins and previous forecasts given assumed weaker sales and continued elevated restructuring costs.
Further investment in working capital and high capital spending are expected as the company invests in its plants and likely stockpiles inventory ahead of tariffs.
Debt to EBITDA is forecast to exceed 10x, with negative cash flow in 2025 of over $90 million.
RealTruck has sufficient liquidity sources to service projected cash flow deficits over the next 12 months, though its cushion will contract.
The company had balance sheet cash of $26.2 million as of year-end 2024 and $247.8 million of net asset-based loan (ABL) availability, for total liquidity of $274 million.
RealTruck has no near-term debt maturities until its first-lien term loans expire in January 2028, followed by senior unsecured notes in January 2029.
Demand for highly discretionary auto parts is expected to decline in a more protracted recession.
A longer U.S. recession would reduce discretionary consumer spending and demand for RealTruck's products more than base-case assumptions.

Ratings could be lowered if margins weaken further, working capital intensifies, capex remains elevated, weaker discretionary consumer spending leads to lower sales, or the company cannot pass through inflationary or tariff pressures to end customers.
Outlook could be revised to stable if FOCF stabilizes to at least break-even on a sustained basis, contingent on sales volumes and EBITDA margins stabilizing.
source_title: RealTruck Inc. Outlook Revised To Negative On Tig | S&P Global Ratings

facts: Rivian broke ground on a new Georgia plant that could eventually build 400,000 vehicles a year and create 7,500 jobs by 2030.
The Social Circle plant will have an eventual capacity of 400,000 units, coming in two phases of 200,000 units each.
Building is expected to start in 2026 and production is scheduled for 2028.
Rivian stated the plant will produce next-gen vehicles, including the midsize R2 SUV and R3 crossover.
The investment is described as multi-billion-dollar.

An analysis commissioned by the company estimated more than 15,000 direct and indirect jobs tied to the project, including about 2,000 construction jobs, with over $1 billion in annual labor income once fully operational.
Rivian said that it will close its factory in Normal, Illinois, for three weeks starting in September, as it ramps up for the launch of the R2 SUV in 2026.
The midsize version is expected to launch in the first half of 2026 and is projected to have a price tag of $45,000.
Rivian plans to manufacture validation builds of the vehicle later this year at the Illinois facility, which can produce up to 215,000 units annually.
The company said the Georgia plant will provide additional capacity for the program.
source_title: Rivian Stock Soars On Georgia Plant Kickoff With 400,000 EV Capacity — Retail Hails ‘One Of The Best Technical Setups’

facts: Robert Bosch GmbH is a German company founded in 1886 by Robert Bosch to repair electrical equipment.
The company is Europe’s largest auto-parts brand manufacturer and one of the world’s leading makers of auto ignition, fuel injection, and antilock braking systems.
Bosch also produces industrial hydraulic and pneumatic equipment, telecommunications equipment and systems, power tools, household appliances, radios, television sets, and audio-visual equipment.
The company's headquarters are located in Stuttgart.
Subsidiaries of the company operate throughout Europe, North and South America, Asia, and Australia.
Bosch’s magneto and his partner’s spark plug became the basis for the company’s line of auto parts.
The company began selling auto parts to the American automotive market around 1900.
By 1914, two-thirds of the company’s sales were in the United States.
The majority of Bosch’s American businesses were expropriated by the U.S. government in 1918 during World War I.
Bosch’s American operations were taken over again during World War II.
By the 1950s, the company had resumed sales to American and other foreign automotive makers.
In the 1950s, Bosch began diversifying into household appliances, power tools, and hydraulic equipment.
Advanced fuel-injection systems developed by Bosch engineers from 1949 were widely adopted by automakers in the 1970s and ’80s for reducing fuel consumption and minimizing exhaust pollution.
Antilock braking systems pioneered by Bosch came into wide use on European and American cars during the 1980s and ’90s.
In the early 1990s, the company experienced a decline in revenues accompanied by large layoffs.
The company subsequently regained its financial health after the early 1990s revenue decline.
Bosch’s stock was publicly held between 1917 and 1937.
The company is now primarily owned by the Robert Bosch Foundation.
source_title: Bosch GmbH | automotive parts, engineering services, home appliances | Britannica Money

facts: Representatives from the Robert Bosch Charleston plant received the 2011 Palmetto Workforce Partnership Award from the SC Department of Employment and Workforce on March 13, 2012.
The award was presented during a luncheon on February 22 in Columbia, SC.
Robert Bosch Charleston won this year’s Palmetto Workforce Partnership Award in the large business category.
The winner was selected based on 5 categories: job creation, innovative partnerships, support and development of the workforce, quality of work environment, and environmental awareness.
Through Bosch Charleston’s holistic business excellence approach using the EFQM Model, there was a great story to tell in each category that landed the company the award.

Larry Lautenschlager, Senior Vice President Manufacturing of Robert Bosch, LLC, said the recognition shows they are on the right path of balancing efforts between a successful business and being a good corporate citizen and employer in South Carolina, and accepted it for associates' dedication and community involvement.
Each year, the SC Department of Employment and Workforce joins forces with workforce partners to recognize the state’s top employers for jobs provided and community contributions.

Michael L. "Mike" Thurmond, former Commissioner of Labor for the state of Georgia, served as keynote speaker and presented Bosch with the award after addressing community leaders and workforce professionals.
Robert Bosch LLC in Charleston, SC is an automotive component manufacturing plant producing Clean Diesel products, ESP and ABS active safety systems and gasoline systems products.
Bosch Charleston currently employs about 1700 associates.

In the U.S., Canada and Mexico, the Bosch Group manufactures and markets automotive original equipment and aftermarket products, industrial drives and control technology, power tools, security and communication systems, packaging technology, thermotechnology, household appliances, solar energy, healthcare telemedicine and software innovations.
Having established a regional presence in 1906, Bosch employs over 22,500 associates in more than 100 locations with sales of $9.8 billion in fiscal year 2011 per preliminary figures.
The Bosch Group comprises Robert Bosch GmbH and its more than 350 subsidiaries and regional companies in some 60 countries, or roughly 150 countries if sales and service partners are included.

More than 300,000 associates generated sales of 51.4 billion euros ($71.5 billion) in automotive and industrial technology, consumer goods, and building technology for the Bosch Group in fiscal year 2011.
The Bosch Group spent more than 4 billion euros ($5.5 billion) for research and development in 2011 and applied for over 4,100 patents worldwide.

source_title: Robert Bosch LLC Wins Palmetto Workforce Partnership Award - Dorchester County Economic Development

facts: Over the next five years, Bosch will invest approximately $125 million in its manufacturing facility in Dorchester County.
The investment will create approximately 300 new jobs.
Job creation based on this investment is expected to begin in the second half of 2011.
Bosch will add personnel and equipment to support production of its latest passenger car diesel and gasoline fuel injectors for the North American automotive market.
Bosch will add personnel and equipment to support production of next generation anti-lock brake and electronic stability control systems for the North American automotive market.
Dorchester County plans to provide reclaimed water for the company’s manufacturing processes as part of a green initiative.
The reclaimed water initiative will conserve 80,000 gallons of wastewater per day.

The sustainable water alternative will be developed with assistance from a grant from the U.S. Department of Commerce, Economic Development Administration (EDA) and matching funds from Dorchester County.
The EDA grant for the reclaimed water initiative is currently pending final approval.
The facility is located at 8101 Dorchester Road in Dorchester County, South Carolina.
The facility currently employs more than 1,500 people.
The facility produces a wide range of diesel, gasoline, and chassis products.
The Dorchester County facility has been in operation since 1974 and was the company’s first operation in South Carolina.

Gene Oswalt, vice president and commercial plant manager at Robert Bosch LLC, stated the investment is designed to strengthen the business at the plant and support Bosch’s global focus on environmental sustainability.
South Carolina Governor Mark Sanford noted that the state has added 4,500 manufacturing jobs over the past seven months and highlighted Bosch’s confidence in South Carolina’s workforce.
Joe Taylor, Secretary of Commerce for South Carolina, stated that the company’s decision to expand reiterates Bosch’s confidence in the state’s low taxes and regulatory environment.
Jamie Feltner, Dorchester County Council Chairman, noted that Bosch has called Dorchester County home since 1974.
Sean Bennett, chairman of the Charleston Regional Development Alliance, stated that the expansion proves the region's competitiveness for world-class manufacturers.
The Bosch Group is a leading global supplier of technology and services with approximately 275,000 associates and $53 billion in sales for fiscal 2009.
The Bosch Group spends more than $5 billion annually on research and development and applies for approximately 3,800 patents worldwide each year.
In the U.S., Canada, and Mexico, the Bosch Group employs over 20,000 associates in more than 70 locations with reported sales of $7.3 billion in fiscal 2009.
The announcement was made by Gov. Mark Sanford, the South Carolina Department of Commerce, Dorchester County, the Charleston Regional Development Alliance, and Robert Bosch LLC on December 3, 2010.
source_title: Bosch Announces Expansion and Green Initiative in Dorchester County - Dorchester County Economic Development

facts: Businesses choose to invest in Right to Work Georgia due to the great economic environment that its Right to Work laws allow to shine through.
Companies currently investing in Right to Work Georgia include Sam’s Club, Idea Nuova, and Sewon America.
Each company is adding a new location somewhere within the state and creating new job opportunities.
The three companies will create a total of 1,420 new jobs altogether.
Sam’s Club is investing $142 million to add a new fulfillment center in Douglas County, Georgia.
Sam’s Club will create 600 new jobs at its new location.
Sam’s Club stated it is on a journey to transform its entire supply chain through strategic expansion and cutting-edge automation, with this location being a great step in that direction.
Sam’s Club noted the investment is critical to deliver to members with speed and provide an overall great experience.

Local officials expressed excitement that Sam’s Club has chosen Douglas County to expand its supply chain capabilities and thanked economic development partners for supporting the state’s continued growth.
Idea Nuova is investing $19 million to add a new facility in Sylvania, Georgia.
Idea Nuova will create 80 new jobs at its new facility.
Officials noted that Idea Nuova and its partners in Screven County and the City of Sylvania are creating jobs and opportunities to support a healthy community.
Advanced manufacturing and logistics companies are thriving in Georgia due to affordable costs and easy access to the logistics hub of Atlanta and port cities of Savannah and Brunswick.
Idea Nuova will join a strong and growing industry base and workforce in Screven County.

The company plans to create 80 jobs, invest heavily in facilities and equipment, and pay lucrative wages to provide opportunities for residents of the City of Sylvania, Screven County, and the region.
Sewon America is investing $300 million to add a new location in Rincon, Georgia.
Sewon America will create 740 new jobs at its new location.
Sewon America was founded 15 years ago in west Georgia and is building another facility in east Georgia, viewing the two locations as one family.
The company's growth is supported by the Sewon team, Governor Kemp, the Georgia Department of Economic Development, and Effingham County.
Sewon America will continue as an excellent corporate and community partner with continued success expected in Effingham County.
Sewon America supports Hyundai Motor Group’s operations from Effingham County, which offers a skilled workforce, infrastructure, and collaborative spirit to support the company’s growth.
Sewon America selected Grande View industrial park as the best location for its significant investment and future growth.
Sewon America is recognized as one of the largest automobile parts manufacturers in North America.
