facts: Anovion Technologies launched on March 25, 2022.
Anovion was formed through the combination of key assets from affiliates of Amsted Graphite Materials and the acquisition of Pyrotek’s battery materials division.

The combination creates a leader in North America’s rapidly growing battery materials supply chain and the largest commercially operational production capacity of synthetic graphite anode material in the United States.
Anovion has over 140 years of combined history in synthetic graphite production.
Synthetic graphite is critical to the USA’s migration to an electrified future powered by rechargeable batteries, according to CEO Eric Stopka.
Foreign producers, overwhelmingly from China, have controlled the global supply of anode material.
Launching Anovion is intended to secure the supply chain for the electrified future in the United States and throughout North America.

Synthetic graphite is the key advanced material in the anode of lithium-ion batteries used in electric vehicles, consumer electronics, critical defense applications, and stationary storage for electric grids.
Long-life batteries require high purity, highly consistent anode material, driving a strong preference for synthetic graphite, according to Chief Growth Officer Ian McCallum.
Anovion's integrated supply chain is 100% domestic.
The supply chain includes USA-sourced raw materials, multiple manufacturing operations, research and development hubs, and other office locations throughout the country.
Amsted Graphite Materials is located in Clarksburg, West Virginia.
Pyrotek’s battery materials division operates at locations outside of Niagara Falls, New York, and in Spokane, Washington.
Anovion is headquartered in Chicago, Illinois.
Anovion uses proven, reliable, scalable graphitization technology to ensure products with high crystallinity and low impurities.
Anovion currently has the largest anode powder graphitization capacity in North America at 5,000 tonnes per annum (tpa).
A first expansion plant is planned to launch by 2025, which will boost capacity up to 50,000 tpa of finished product.
The company has a longer-term roadmap targeting 150,000 tpa in capacity or more.
Dr. Jeremy Schrooten serves as Vice President of Technology at Anovion and is formerly Pyrotek’s Technical Director.
Joining Amsted Graphite Materials to launch Anovion aims to create supply chain security and efficiencies in the rapidly growing market, according to Dr. Schrooten.
Anovion follows environmentally responsible manufacturing practices that yield high purity and exceptional quality without compromising throughput.
Anovion’s pilot operations include a proprietary thermal treatment process that brings its direct emissions profile to near zero.
The company uses clean, cost-effective hydroelectric power to achieve its sustainability targets.
The investor group supporting Anovion includes affiliates of Amsted Industries and Monomyth Group.
Both Amsted Industries and Monomyth Group are headquartered in Chicago, Illinois.
Anovion operates North America’s first commercially operational capacity dedicated to synthetic graphite anode material.
Anovion has domestic commercial production and ships to North American customers.
Anovion has continually expanded its portfolio of high-performing anode material for a wide range of lithium-ion battery applications.
Anovion products are already qualified and scaling production with customers in commercial electric vehicles, aerospace, and defense markets.
Anovion is undergoing qualification testing with leading automotive electric vehicle OEMs and suppliers.
source_title: Anovion Technologies Launches as a Leader in North American Battery Materials Supply Chain

facts: Anovion Technologies held a groundbreaking ceremony for its new Bainbridge facility off of Newton Road on a Tuesday.
The Chicago-headquartered company specializes in the production of synthetic graphite, a key component in lithium-ion batteries.
According to Anovion CEO Eric Stopka at an initial announcement, this facility will be the first large-scale factory of its kind in North America.
Development Authority Vice Chairman Andy Bell spoke at the podium, recognizing all state and local officials present.
County Commission Chair Pete Stephens delivered the invocation at the event.
Mayor Edward Reynolds spoke at the event and introduced Governor Kemp.

Mayor Edward Reynolds stated that throughout Governor Kemp’s first term, Georgia has consistently broken economic records with jobs and investment coming to every corner of the state, including Bainbridge and Decatur County.
Mayor Edward Reynolds touted the mayor’s record during his introduction, including tax cuts, teacher pay raises, and a crackdown on human trafficking.

Governor Kemp thanked officials present and stated the project represents many great victories for the state as a culmination of hard work by local communities and sustained talks at the state level through the Department of Economic Development and support from the general assembly.
Governor Kemp praised Anovion’s business and its stated commitment to build up the American supply chain for lithium-ion batteries, noting much of it is currently based in Asia.
Governor Kemp reiterated that the facility will create 400 new jobs and represents an $800 million investment.

Governor Kemp stated that with this project’s groundbreaking, all four corners of Georgia have seen major investment and significant job creation as a result of leading the next Industrial Revolution in the state.
Anovion Executive Chairman Chip Dunn spoke at the event, recounting how he met Governor Kemp and how Anovion was persuaded to come to Georgia.
According to Executive Chairman Chip Dunn, factors contributing to choosing Bainbridge include its inland port and access to waterway and land transport.
Executive Chairman Chip Dunn stated that Anovion is the only commercial sale battery-grade synthetic graphite company in North America.
Executive Chairman Chip Dunn discussed which components in lithium-ion batteries use synthetic graphite and emphasized the company’s commitment to being climate-focused.

Executive Chairman Chip Dunn focused on the company’s commitment to reduce emissions associated with producing synthetic graphite, aiming to shift the narrative of the environmentally unfriendly process in China.

According to Executive Chairman Chip Dunn, Anovion hopes to reduce its production’s carbon footprint to zero over time and aims to be the first major manufacturing business in the world to be carbon-free.

Congressman Sanford Bishop spoke at the podium discussing how state and national government were involved in bringing Anovion to Georgia and how the state has benefited from federal manufacturing incentives.

Congressman Sanford Bishop stated that federal resources combined with resources from the governor and general assembly are making historic improvements in high-speed internet connecting families, schools, and businesses.
Congressman Sanford Bishop noted the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act is improving roads, ports, railways, bridges, and airports as arteries for commerce.
SRTC President Jim Glass delivered closing remarks at the ceremony.
SRTC President Jim Glass revealed that Anovion will be working to offer scholarships in the future, with updates to come later.
The facility is projected to be operational by 2025.
The ceremony concluded with officials present breaking ground.
Story provided by Ethan Reddish of The Post-Searchlight.
source_title: Anovion Technologies holds groundbreaking ceremony - Development Authority of Bainbridge and Decatur County

facts: Mannington Mills will invest $42 million in Calhoun by 2021.
Mannington Mills will create more than 200 jobs at the new Calhoun site.
Gov. Nathan Deal announced the investment and job creation for the expansion.
Mannington Mills purchased a 78-acre site to expand upon its existing presence in Georgia.
New jobs at the Calhoun site will include positions in production, management, engineering, marketing, supervisory, and administrative roles.
The new site is located less than one mile from a current Mannington Commercial facility that employs more than 700 people.
The existing Mannington Commercial facility occupies 800,000 square feet of manufacturing, warehouse, and office space.
Gov. Nathan Deal stated that Georgia’s economic environment and skilled workforce provide businesses like Mannington Mills with opportunities for growth and innovation.
Russell Grizzle, President and CEO of Mannington, stated the company is experiencing strong growth in Georgia and the site acquisition will help accommodate that growth.
Russell Grizzle described the purchase as another installment in Mannington’s ongoing long-term growth investment plan for Calhoun.
Kathy Johnson, president and CEO of the Gordon County Chamber of Commerce, stated the community was involved in the beginning of the modern flooring industry in the United States.
Stephanie Scearce, GDEcD Regional Project Manager, represented the Global Commerce Division in collaboration with the Gordon County Chamber of Commerce and North Georgia EMC.
North Georgia EMC partnered with the Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) for the project.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated the expansion speaks to the extensive resources northwest Georgia provides to the flooring industry.
Mannington Mills, Inc. is based in Salem, New Jersey.
The company manufactures residential and commercial sheet vinyl, luxury vinyl, laminate, resilient, and hardwood floors.
The company also manufactures commercial carpet and rubber under the Mannington Residential, Mannington Commercial, Amtico, and Burke brands.
source_title: GA: Mannington Mills to Invest $42M, Create 200+ Jobs in Gordon County | Trade and Industry Development

entity_name: Ascend Elements Kentucky Battery Materials Plant
facts: A design-build team of Turner Construction Co., Kokosing Industrial and SSOE Group are building a sustainable battery materials manufacturing plant for Ascend Elements, announced Dec. 6.
Westborough, Mass.-based Ascend Elements will spend $310 million on the facility’s first phase.
Ascend Elements may invest as much as $1 billion over additional phases.
The plan calls for construction of a 500,000-sq-ft plant dubbed “Apex 1” on a 140-acre site in Hopkinsville, Ky.
Matthew Bierschbach, business leader at Toledo, Ohio-based SSOE and project manager, said the Turner-Kokosing team would begin grading the site this month.
SSOE is continuing to work with Ascend Elements on the design of the facility.
Apex 1 would be a first-of-its-kind plant according to Ascend Elements.
The facility would use the company’s “hydro-to-cathode” closed-loop process to manufacture battery materials from recycled batteries.
The closed-loop process reduces the need for mining and generates less waste and fewer carbon emissions than traditional cathode manufacturing processes.
The plant would produce enough materials to equip 250,000 electric vehicles per year.
In October, the U.S. Dept. of Energy announced it would support construction of two plants at the site with a $480-million grant to Ascend Elements.
Ascend Elements’ Covington, Ga., battery recycling facility would be a key source of feedstock for the Kentucky plant.
The Hopkinsville location was selected in part because of its proximity to electric vehicle and battery plants.
Several EV and battery projects are currently underway in Kentucky and surrounding states.
Up to 400 full-time jobs are expected at the plant.
Tom Muraski, president of Westerville, Ohio-based Kokosing Industrial, stated that the design-build team would work with the local building trades and contracting community.
Tom Manahan, senior vice president of New York City-based Turner, noted that Hopkinsville was hit by a tornado at the start of the year.
Deadly tornados also struck other parts of western Kentucky in December 2021.
Tom Manahan stated that construction of this plant represents the single largest investment in western Kentucky history.
Tom Manahan added that the result of their efforts will support economic growth in the region.
source_title: Turner-Kokosing-SSOE Win Kentucky Recycled Battery Plant | 2022-12-06 | Engineering News-Record

facts: Ascend Elements announced it has raised $162 million in new equity investments.
Major investors in the new round include Just Climate, Clearvision Ventures, and IRONGREY.
The new equity financing brings the company’s 12-month funding total to $704 million.
This total includes a $542 million funding round led by Decarbonization Partners, Temasek, and Qatar Investment Authority in September 2023.
CEO Mike O’Kronley stated that the investment underscores market confidence in North America’s critical EV battery infrastructure.
Ascend Elements recycles lithium-ion batteries to produce new, engineered battery materials with lower carbon emissions.
Managing Director at Just Climate Aruna Ramsamy noted the company is backing technology that transforms spent lithium-ion batteries into critical materials used in the creation of new batteries.
The new funding will advance construction of Ascend Elements’ Apex 1 facility in Hopkinsville, Kentucky.
Apex 1 will be North America’s first sustainable cathode precursor (pCAM) manufacturing facility, scheduled to open in early 2025.
When complete, the 1-million-square-foot facility will produce sustainable pCAM and CAM (cathode active materials) for up to 750,000 electric vehicles per year.
The project is supported in part by the U.S. Department of Energy and the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law (BIL).
pCAM and CAM are engineered materials made to precise microstructure specifications for use in electric vehicle batteries.
Most of the world’s pCAM and CAM are currently manufactured in China from primary (mined) metals.
Ascend Elements is commercializing an ultra-efficient method to make sustainable pCAM and CAM from black mass, which is the traditional output of lithium-ion battery recycling facilities.
The company's patented Hydro-to-Cathode direct precursor synthesis process eliminates several intermediary steps in the traditional cathode manufacturing process.
This process provides significant economic and carbon-reduction benefits.
Peer-reviewed studies have shown that Ascend Elements’ recycled battery materials perform as well as similar materials made from primary (mined) sources.
Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC acted as the sole placement agent for the Series D funding round.
source_title: North American Clean Energy - Ascend Elements Raises Additional $162 Million to Build Sustainable Lithium-Ion Battery Materials in United States

facts: Electric vehicle battery supplier Ascend Elements has paused construction on its factory in Christian County.
After breaking ground in 2022, construction is now on hold for the Apex 1 project.
Several major customers asked to push back the start of precursor cathode active material (pCAM) deliveries by 12 to 18 months due to changing market conditions.
The delay provides extra time to re-evaluate vendor relationships and contracts without delaying shipments to customers, according to Thomas Frey, Senior Director of Marketing & Communications.
Turner-Kokosing, the construction firm hired for the project, recently filed a lawsuit against Ascend seeking $138 million in unpaid bills.
Ascend said it is prepared to pay for all "authorized and legitimate work performed" but is also committed to being responsible stewards of both public and private funds.
The company added it is considering other contractors for the project.
Ascend plans to restart construction in the third quarter of this year with operations starting in late 2026.
Last month, Ascend reached an agreement with the U.S. Department of Energy to cancel a $164 million federal grant for the Hopkinsville plant.
Citing changing market conditions, the company said it would no longer produce one of the main components of lithium-ion batteries.
source_title: EV battery supplier Ascend Elements pauses construction on Hopkinsville plant

facts: The Ministry of Economic Development and Technology announced a substantial cash subsidy for Ascend Elements valued at up to 1.22 billion PLN.
The grant is equivalent to approximately USD $320 million.
This represents one of the largest financial supports ever provided by the Polish government.
Ascend Elements is set to construct a new manufacturing facility dedicated to sustainable precursor cathode active material (pCAM) production.
The advanced pCAM material is critical for the creation of lithium-ion batteries utilized in electric vehicles and various industrial applications.
The initiative aligns with the European Union's Temporary Crisis and Transition Framework, supporting Europe's shift towards a net-zero economy.
CEO Linh Austin referred to the grant as a significant step in their European growth strategy.
He emphasized the company's commitment to establishing a sustainable supply chain for battery materials on both sides of the Atlantic.
The project underscores how essential battery sector initiatives are to PAIH's investment portfolio.

Pawel Pudlowski, Vice President of PAIH, remarked that this investment would enhance Poland's footprint in the battery supply chain, fostering regional development and promoting collaboration with local educational institutions.
The facility is indicative of Ascend Elements' commitment to technological advancement and environmental responsibility.
At the new facility, Ascend Elements plans to utilize its proprietary Hydro-to-Cathode® technology to produce sustainable nickel, manganese, and cobalt (NMC) pCAM from recycled lithium-ion batteries.
This method demonstrates significant efficiency improvements over traditional recycling techniques, contributing to a reduced environmental footprint.
The innovative process has yielded impressive results in life cycle assessments, showing a dramatic reduction in carbon emissions.
By 2030, Ascend Elements aims for an 86% reduction in CO2 emissions and a remarkable 94% decrease in particulate matter emissions through further decarbonization strategies.
In 2024, Ascend Elements formed a joint venture with Elemental Strategic Metals, launching the AE Elemental JV battery recycling facility in Zawiercie, Poland.
The new pCAM manufacturing plant will be entirely owned and operated by Ascend Elements.
The company already operates one of North America's largest battery recycling facilities in Covington, Georgia, leveraging this knowledge to enhance production capabilities.
Ascend Elements is also constructing a Hydro-to-Cathode® pCAM facility in Hopkinsville, Kentucky.
The Kentucky facility is expected to manufacture materials for up to 750,000 electric vehicle batteries per year.
This Kentucky facility will be recognized as North America's largest NMC pCAM manufacturer.
source_title: Ascend Elements to Construct Major Lithium Battery Facility - Investors Hangout

facts: Ascend Elements aims to power the clean energy transition by being the world's leading producer of recycled battery materials.
The company utilizes a patented Hydro-to-Cathode® direct synthesis process that yields high-purity, low-carbon cathode active material (CAM).
Its proprietary technology produces 99.9% pure CAM from recycled feedstock.
Ascend Elements has secured over $1 billion in private funds and Department of Energy (DoE) grants to build gigafactories.
The company received a $480 million DoE grant award.
It is perfectly positioned for IRA 45X tax credits, which boost its margins.
Ascend Elements has secured multi-billion dollar offtake deals with partners including Honda and SK.
A $1 billion+ offtake agreement was signed with a major OEM.
The company's Apex 1 facility in Kentucky is currently ramping up production but has not yet reached full capacity.
Flawless operational execution at the Kentucky gigafactory is critical to meet partner commitments.
Delays or cost overruns at the Kentucky plant could be catastrophic for the company.
Ascend Elements relies on a hub-and-spoke supply chain model where collection centers feed its gigafactories.
The company is dependent on partners for consistent, high-quality black mass feedstock.
Securing diverse, long-term feedstock sources beyond existing partners is a key priority.
Feedstock from manufacturing scrap is concentrated among a few OEMs, while end-of-life battery supply remains fragmented.
Future competition for end-of-life batteries is expected to intensify.
The company is adapting its process to produce LFP (lithium iron phosphate) cathodes to tap into a fast-growing market.
Ascend Elements manufactures custom-engineered battery cathode materials from recycled lithium-ion batteries.
Its customer base includes global automotive OEMs, lithium-ion battery cell manufacturers, and stationary energy storage providers.
The company's revenue streams include sales of CAM and pCAM, recycling services (tipping fees), and sales of recovered commodity materials.
ASCEND ELEMENTS faces intense competitive rivalry from well-funded players like Redwood Materials and Li-Cycle, who are scaling rapidly with major deals.
Commodity price volatility for nickel and cobalt directly impacts the company's margins.
The development of sodium-ion or solid-state batteries could potentially disrupt its lithium-ion focus in the future.
Ascend Elements was founded in 2015 and is headquartered in Westborough, Massachusetts.
The company employs approximately 500 people.
It operates under SIC Code 3341 and NAICS Code 335910 (Battery Manufacturing).

Strategic priorities include dominating with its proprietary direct synthesis, rapidly building plants in key global auto hubs, securing long-term feedstock and offtake, and focusing expansion on IRA and Critical Minerals Act (CRMA) subsidized regions.
source_title: Ascend Elements SWOT Analysis & Strategic Plan 2025-Q4

facts: Ascend Elements received $125 million in federal funding awarded by the U.S. Department of Energy.
The award is part of a federal effort to boost battery production and recycling for electric vehicles.
Ascend Elements will put the federal award toward a new battery recycling process at facilities in Hopkinsville, Ky.
The project is being executed in partnership with Orbia.
The companies will extract graphite from the recycled batteries.
The extracted graphite will be processed and enhanced at an Orbia facility in Louisiana.
The companies plan to sell the new battery-grade graphite.

White House National Climate Advisor Ali Zaidi stated the funding is helping support the technologies that we need in the market today, the components that we will need in the near future, and the innovative technologies we need to advance our vision for a circular domestic battery supply chain.
The initiative positions the United States to continue leading the global effort on clean energy.
source_title: Another $125 million in funding has been awarded by the U.S. Department of Energy to Ascend Elements which is part of a federal effort to boost battery production and recycling for electric vehicles.

facts: Ascend Elements recently shipped decarbonized cathode materials to Freudenberg e-Power Systems, a supplier of emissions-neutral energy solutions for heavy-duty applications.
Cathode materials made from recycled battery metals can help EV battery manufacturers qualify for U.S. tax credits under the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA).
Eric Gratz, Ph.D., CTO and co-founder of Ascend Elements stated that battery manufacturers in the United States are starting to use engineered battery materials made in America from recycled metals.

Engineered cathode material is normally made in Asia using material from mined sources, but Ascend Elements is now making it domestically from recycled EV batteries and production scrap while generating just half the carbon footprint.
Ascend Elements uses a patented process known as hydro-to-cathode direct precursor synthesis to manufacture NMC pCAM and CAM from used lithium-ion batteries and battery manufacturing scrap.
The closed-loop process eliminates up to 15 intermediary steps in the traditional cathode manufacturing process.

A recent independent third-party life cycle assessment found Ascend Elements’ hydro-to-cathode process produces EV battery cathode material at a 49% reduction in carbon emissions compared to traditional cathode manufacturing processes.
By 2030, Ascend Elements aims to achieve a 90% reduction in the carbon footprint for its decarbonized cathode products.

Cathode material is the single largest contributor to a lithium-ion battery cell’s carbon footprint, so Ascend Elements’ low-carbon cathode material will impact Freudenberg e-Power Systems’ batteries.
The shipment of pCAM material was engineered and manufactured at the Ascend Elements pilot facility in Westborough, MA.
The materials were sintered and finished as CAM at the Ascend Elements location in Novi, MI before being shipped to Freudenberg e-Power Systems at the XALT Energy plant in Midland, MI.

Eric Gratz noted that this is a relatively small shipment relative to the intended full-scale commercial program but represents an important milestone for Ascend Elements and the U.S. battery materials industry.

Ascend Elements engineers worked closely with Freudenberg e-Power Systems for over a year to engineer and manufacture the low-carbon cathode material to meet particularly high-performance requirements for lifetime, charge time, and safety.
Ascend Elements is building a $1 billion advanced manufacturing campus in Hopkinsville, KY.
The 1-million-square-foot Apex 1 facility at the campus is scheduled to begin operations in early 2025.
The Apex 1 facility will eventually produce enough pCAM and CAM for 750,000 EVs per year.
In June 2023, Ascend Elements landed a $1 billion contract to manufacture sustainable pCAM for a major U.S.-based company.
Commercial-scale shipments of material to the undisclosed customer under the June 2023 contract will begin in Q1 2025.
source_title: Ascend Elements ships domestically decarbonized cathode materials

facts: The US battery-materials company Ascend Elements has entered into a development partnership with Freudenberg e-Power Systems.
Recycled battery materials could be used in heavy commercial vehicles and ships in the future as part of the agreement.
Ascend Elements will develop an NMC cathode material that meets Freudenberg’s exact specifications.
Freudenberg manufactures cells, packs and system solutions for heavy-duty vehicles and ships at the battery plant of its subsidiary Xalt Energy in Midland in the US state of Michigan.

Max Kley, CEO of Freudenberg e-Power Systems, stated that integrating Ascend Elements’ recycled cathode material into their high-performance cell design reduces environmental impact while ensuring the highest product quality and exceptional durability.
Max Kley also noted that Ascend Elements’ domestic manufacturing contributes to the stability of their supply chain.
Freudenberg e-Power Systems tested the recycled materials in advance and found them to be good.

Mike O’Kronley, CEO of Ascend Elements, stated that the contract is evidence that recycled battery metals can perform as well as virgin metals, noting that metals are essentially the same whether from a mine or a used lithium-ion battery depending on how the cathode material is engineered.
To win the contract, Freudenberg tested Ascend Elements’ customized cathode product extensively and found it to have truly exceptional cycle-life results while achieving best-in-class safety.
Ascend Elements raised $542 million in capital from investors a few days prior to the article's publication, equivalent to about €506 million.
The fresh capital is designated for the completion of Ascend Elements' factory in Hopkinsville in the US state of Kentucky.
The NMC cathode material for Freudenberg will be developed at Ascend’s research and development centre in Westborough, Massachusetts.
In March 2023, Ascend Elements commissioned its first commercial-scale lithium-ion battery recycling plant in the US state of Georgia.
The Georgia facility, referred to as ‘Base 1’, is North America’s largest recycling plant for electric vehicle batteries.
Up to 30,000 tonnes of batteries can be processed at the Georgia facility annually, which is equivalent to around 70,000 e-vehicle batteries.
source_title: Ascend Elements develops cathode material for Freudenberg - electrive.com

facts: Serra Hall, executive director of the Newton County Industrial Development Authority (NCIDA), states that Newton County has become recognized as a 'boom town' with $6.9 billion announced and over 9,000 quality jobs committed in the last two years.

According to NCIDA, the median income in Newton County has increased 35% in the last 10 years, and the county’s tax digest has more than doubled in the last eight years, from $2 billion to $4.2 billion.

Debbie Harper, president of the Newton County Chamber of Commerce, identifies advanced manufacturing, biopharma, film/gaming/music, and commercial as the four main sectors that keep the industrial base virtually recession-resistant.
Piedmont Healthcare recently opened a new 30,000-square-foot medical office complex anchoring the redeveloped Eastside Crossing Shopping Center with a $10 million investment.
The Piedmont Healthcare offices are located about a mile from Piedmont Newton Hospital and will eventually house cardiology, neurology, endocrinology, and rheumatology practices.
Approximately 5,000 new residential units are in the pipeline to account for projected population growth of 8.75% by 2026, according to the Covington mayor’s office.

Mayor Steve Horton states that due to industrial, commercial, and residential growth, the City of Covington is investing significant resources into rehabilitation and improvements to water and sewer infrastructure.
The Newton County Water and Sewerage Authority, the Newton County Board of Commissioners, and the City of Covington were awarded a $212.8 million drinking water grant in January.

Mike Hopkins, executive director of the Newton County Water and Sewerage Authority, notes this is the most funding his organization has received in 53 years, far exceeding the previous record of $900,000 in 2009.
The $212.8 million grant was funded through the American Rescue Plan Act via the State of Georgia’s $400 million Drinking Water Projects to Support Increased Population (DWPSIP) grant program.
Tres Thomas, Covington’s city manager, states the joint governmental application was one of only three to be awarded funds and highlights the success as an example of intragovernmental collaboration.
A majority of the Newton County Water and Sewerage Authority’s $91 million portion of the grant will go to the county water reuse facility.
The county water reuse facility will return water to users, taking pressure off the county’s potable water supply while allowing for additional growth without additional strain on water resources.

Marcello Banes, chair of the Newton County Board of Commissioners, states that Takeda, Facebook (Meta), and Rivian found Newton County through a long-standing vision to work collaboratively on its multicounty industrial park.
Stanton Springs industrial park supports sustainable development and hosts major businesses including Meta data centers, Takeda Pharmaceuticals, and Rivian.

Covington is investing $52 million to upgrade the Eastside Sewer Basin, improve the Eastside Lift Station, and improve the Water Reclamation Facility using a combination of grant dollars and a loan from the Georgia Environmental Finance Authority.
Covington uses a Georgia Environmental Protection Division-sponsored Land Application System (LAS) that is jointly owned by the city and the Newton County Water and Sewerage Authority.
The LAS covers 2,000 acres of vegetated land that naturally filters wastewater while growing trees for timber and soybeans for livestock feed.
Covington’s Green Fuels Facility is one of the few public compressed natural gas filling stations in Georgia with plans to incorporate electric vehicle chargers in the near future.
Mayor Horton states Covington has leveraged sustainable resources and partnered with a local nonprofit, Sustainable Newton, to integrate environmentally sustainable initiatives and educate citizens.
Ascend Elements opened the largest lithium-ion battery recycling facility in North America in Covington in March with a $43 million investment.

CEO Mike O’Kronley states the Ascend Elements facility is the first commercial-scale EV battery recycling operation and delivers annual capacity to process 30,000 metric tons of lithium-ion batteries or the equivalent of about 70,000 EV batteries.
Ascend Elements’ facility occupies a converted 154,000-square-foot industrial building.
As of April, Ascend Elements employed approximately 100 people in Newton County with plans to hire 85 more team members in the next year.

O’Kronley states proximity to electric vehicle battery manufacturing facilities, accessibility to railroads, highways, and renewable power sources were key locational advantages for choosing Newton County.
The Newton County Industrial Development Authority and the Georgia Department of Economic Development played important roles in Ascend Elements' site selection.
Archer Aviation broke ground on a new electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft (eVTOL) manufacturing facility next to the Covington Municipal Airport in March.
Glen Burks, vice president of manufacturing at Archer, states the Silicon Valley-based aerospace company will advance $118 million in investment over the next decade.
Burks projects that as production output increases, the facility will create upwards of 1,000 new jobs long-term for the local community.
The two-building Archer Aviation facility will be able to produce up to 2,300 eVTOL aircraft per year after completion in the first half of 2024.
The Archer Aviation site is more than 96 acres located less than 1.5 miles from Interstate 20.

Burks states Newton County stood out during site selection due to proactive planning, affordable housing solutions, and improved infrastructure including roads, school systems, and utilities built ahead of growth.

Mayor Horton notes Covington’s involvement in the film industry dates back more than 70 years, with iconic productions including The Dukes of Hazzard, Cannonball Run, In the Heat of the Night, Remember the Titans, The Vampire Diaries, and Sweet Magnolias.
Three Ring Studios operates just outside city limits as a sign that Covington’s reputation as a regional filming destination will continue to thrive.
A quarter-million tourists are expected to visit the area each year.
A record 96,383 people from 57 countries checked in at the Covington Welcome Center in 2022, according to the city’s community development office.
Mayor Horton reports that surging hotel-motel tax revenue translated into $200 in property tax savings on average per resident in 2022.

Debbie Harper states the Newton County Chamber of Commerce is partnering with the county on a new comprehensive plan nearing completion to offer strategic vision for rural estate living, multiuse development, and walkable communities.

Sara Vinson, Sustainable Newton co-founder, states the organization is educating people about the Inflation Reduction Act (2022 law) that will lower energy costs for consumers and reduce U.S. carbon pollution by up to 40% from 2005 levels by 2030.
Sustainable Newton wants to ensure Newton County households are aware of IRA tax credits and rebates to go solar, electrify appliances and vehicles, and make energy efficiency improvements.
A grant from Meta is funding the installation of a solar-energy system for a local early childhood education nonprofit.

David Eady, mayor of Oxford and senior manager at the Ray C. Anderson Center for Sustainable Business at Georgia Tech’s Scheller College of Business, connected Sustainable Newton with students to gather data on converting the City of Covington fleet to electric vehicles and reinstituting glass recycling.

General Mills, one of Newton County’s largest manufacturers, sponsored the annual 5K Cheerios Challenge race on Earth Day to showcase electric vehicles, bicycles, and yard tools while raising awareness of IRA benefits through an interactive Rewiring America IRA calculator exhibit.

Maurice Carter, Sustainable Newton co-founder, notes Yale University reports 53% of Americans are alarmed or concerned about global warming, but only 8% of citizens are actively engaged in climate action.
source_title: Newton County: People, Partnership, Progress - Georgia Trend Magazine

facts: Aspen Aerogels took a $296 million impairment charge when it pulled the plug on its Georgia plant.
Executives are unveiling a turnaround strategy that targets data centers and European automotive markets instead of domestic electric vehicle production.
source_title: Aspen Aerogels lists Georgia factory for sale - Providence Business First

facts: Aspen Aerogels announced it is halting construction of its planned second aerogel manufacturing facility in Statesboro.
CEO Donald R. Young made the announcement during an investor call on Thursday morning, February 13, 2025.
The company will explore options to leverage its Statesboro assets, including potentially relocating some equipment to its existing plant for upgrades and expansion.
To meet future aerogel demand, Aspen will focus on maximizing capacity at its East Providence facility and implementing a flexible supply strategy.
The announcement comes nearly three years after Aspen unveiled plans for a $325 million, 500,000 square foot facility in the Bruce Yawn Industrial Park, promising 250 jobs.
Aspen's decision to build in Statesboro came three months before Hyundai announced the construction of the Meta Plant in Bryan County, though the two projects were not connected.
The project has faced numerous challenges, with the company previously announcing a “right-timing” of construction in 2023.

CFO and Treasurer Ricardo C. Rodriguez stated that in early 2023, pre-empting a reset in EV demand expectations, the company decided to right-time the construction and subsequently ramped up external manufacturing capacity for its Energy Industrial business.

Hopes for the Statesboro project were briefly rekindled late last year when Aspen announced a conditional commitment from the U.S. Department of Energy for a proposed loan of up to $670.6 million for a planned facility in Register, Georgia.
The latest announcement dashes hopes for the Statesboro project.
Benjy Thompson, CEO of the Development Authority of Bulloch County, stated that businesses often reevaluate their strategies and expressed disappointment over the cessation of construction.
Thompson noted that taxpayer protection is a priority and clawback provisions are in place to recoup local investment should the project not move forward.

The Development Authority, along with local and state partners, will continue to pursue long-term projects that bring good jobs, smart investment, and economic opportunities to Bulloch County citizens.
source_title: Aspen Aerogels halts construction of Statesboro plant  - Grice Connect

facts: Aspen Aerogels, Inc. (NYSE: ASPN) plans to construct an advanced manufacturing facility at Bulloch County's Southern Gateway Commerce Park.
Aspen intends to invest a minimum of $325 million at the new site near Statesboro.
The company plans to create more than 250 advanced manufacturing jobs at the new facility.
Production at the new Georgia site is expected to begin in late 2023.
Combined with Aspen's original East Providence, Rhode Island plant, the planned facility will triple Aspen's nameplate aerogel capacity.
The project aims to capitalize on increased demand for Aspen's Aerogel Technology Platform™ as EV OEMs adopt its PyroThin® thermal barrier solutions.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Aspen Aerogels has put its confidence in Georgia and the growing electric mobility sector, adding an American company to the state's rapidly expanding EV portfolio.
Governor Kemp noted that the state is utilizing its assets to bring jobs to Georgians and highlighted this as the latest project to come to Bulloch County and southeast Georgia.
Don Young, President and CEO of Aspen, expressed pleasure at being in Georgia for the siting, development, and construction of the company's second aerogel manufacturing plant.

Young cited the City of Statesboro and Bulloch County region offering superior operating features, including access to efficient ports, a strong available workforce, and secure low-cost utilities and critical raw materials.

Aspen is increasingly being asked to provide broader solutions for improved battery performance and safety centered on PyroThin materials that provide thermal management, mechanical stability, and fire protection properties to EV battery systems.
Aspen plans to build a 500,000-square-foot advanced manufacturing facility situated on a 90-acre parcel in southeast Georgia.
The company plans to begin hiring personnel in the second half of 2022 across multiple disciplines.
Hiring will span roles including skilled manufacturing operators, advanced process engineers, quality and maintenance technicians, and professional support staff.
Billy Allen, Vice Chair of the Development Authority of Bulloch County, welcomed Aspen Aerogels to the Statesboro-Bulloch County community.
Allen highlighted the region's site and logistics advantages as a testament to its commitment to innovation in the electric mobility ecosystem.
He expressed confidence that local education resources, including Georgia Southern University and Ogeechee Technical College, will provide critical support to help Aspen flourish.
Assistant Director Alyce Thornhill represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development's (GDEcD) Global Commerce division on the project.
The GDEcD partnered with the Development Authority of Bulloch County, Statesboro, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia EMC on this project.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson welcomed Aspen Aerogels' innovative, sustainable solutions for the electric vehicle and energy storage markets to Georgia.

Wilson looked forward to Georgia employees collaborating with Aspen's network of world-class scientists, engineers, and additional skilled workforce to solve challenges in the changing mobility landscape.
Southern Gateway Commerce Park is a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certified park.
To earn GRAD certification, a property must undergo due diligence and complete specific certification requirements prior to a visit by a prospective business.
Aspen's PyroThin® thermal barrier products enable solutions to thermal runaway challenges within the electric vehicle and energy storage markets.
The company's carbon aerogel program seeks to increase the performance of lithium-ion battery cells to enable EV manufacturers to extend driving range and reduce the cost of electric vehicles.
Aspen is headquartered in Northborough, MA, and manufactures its products at its East Providence, R.I. facilities.
source_title: Aspen Aerogels, Inc. - Aspen Aerogels to Open Advanced Manufacturing Facility in Bulloch County

facts: Aspen Aerogels, Inc. sells aerogel materials used in energy industrial applications, sustainable insulation, and electric vehicle (EV) markets.
The company announced that it had halted construction of its second manufacturing facility in Statesboro, Georgia.
Instead of building the new facility, Aspen Aerogels opted to maximize capacity at its existing East Providence plant and rely on external manufacturing.
Aspen Aerogels (NYSE:ASPN) projected revenue between $75 million and $95 million for Q1 2025, significantly below analyst expectations of $104.2 million.
The company reported strong Q4 2024 results but issued weak guidance for Q1 2025.
source_title: Why Aspen Aerogels (ASPN) Is Plunging in 2025?

facts: Aspen Aerogels, Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, and sells high-performance aerogel insulation materials primarily serving the energy industrial and electric vehicle (EV) thermal barrier markets.
The company operates through two reportable segments: Energy Industrial and Thermal Barrier.
Proprietary products include PyroThin thermal barriers specifically designed for EV battery packs.

Other product lines include Pyrogel and Cryogel blankets for refineries, petrochemical plants, LNG facilities, and power generation assets, as well as Spaceloft products for building and subsea applications.
Manufacturing operations are concentrated in the United States, including a facility in East Providence, Rhode Island.
The East Providence facility experienced an explosion in April and began a staged restart of manufacturing operations following mechanical and safety reviews.
Aspen Aerogels serves a global customer base spanning North America, Europe, Asia, Latin America, and beyond.
Top-tier partners include General Motors, SK, Evonik, and ExxonMobil.
General Motors named Aspen Aerogels its 2025 Supplier of the Year for Innovation in Electric Vehicle Solutions.
The company employs approximately 554 full-time staff.
Q1 2026 earnings call highlighted expectations of approximately 20% energy industrial growth for 2026, suggesting a meaningful demand recovery in the EV segment.
Investors are watching the company's EV battery thermal barrier expansion as a longer-term growth catalyst.
Aspen Aerogels reported a 66% Q4 revenue decline and launched a strategic review with Piper Sandler as financial advisor in February.
The company reported a steep 51.88% year-over-year revenue decline and a 92.14% drop in net profit in its latest quarterly results.
Q1 2026 EPS of -$0.88 missed estimates of -$0.29 alongside a steep 51.9% year-over-year revenue decline.
Guidance for Q2 revenue is $40M–$48M alongside an expected net loss.
Aspen Aerogels reported a year-end cash balance of $158.6 Million and initiated a strategic review amidst a $389.6 Million net loss for 2025.
A Norwegian firm, Beerenberg, prevailed in a Korean Patent Court ruling that invalidated three of Aspen's patents related to improved hydrophobic aerogel technology.
Analysts maintain a Buy consensus with an average price target of $6.50, though Barclays issued a Sell rating and Canaccord cut its price target to $5 citing liquidity concerns.
source_title: Aspen Aerogels, Inc. Stock Price: Quote, Forecast, Splits & News (ASPN)

facts: Aspen Aerogels is planning a $325 million factory investment.
The facility will be located in Bulloch County’s Southern Gateway Commerce Park.
The factory has been designed to be expandable for future growth.
Phase 1 of the project will create at least 250 advanced manufacturing jobs.
Top company executives stated that the total number of jobs could nearly double over time.
Aspen Aerogels anticipates rapid growth in the electric vehicle market.
The company cites a growing need for batteries and insulation as key drivers for its expansion.
Company executives announced these details on Thursday.
source_title: Aspen Aerogels execs: 250 jobs, $325M investment just Phase 1 for Bulloch plant - Statesboro Herald

facts: Aspen Aerogels reported Q3 2024 revenues of $117.3 million, marking a 93% increase year-over-year.
The company raised its full-year 2024 revenue outlook to $450 million and adjusted EBITDA to $90 million.
Gross profit margin improved to 42%, with adjusted EBITDA of $25.4 million for the quarter.
The company reported a net loss of $13 million for Q3 2024, including a $27.5 million loss on debt extinguishment.
Aspen Aerogels announced a new OEM supply agreement with Mercedes-Benz for PyroThin Thermal Barriers, with production expected to begin in 2027.
The Mercedes-Benz award is through ACC, a battery cell joint venture between Stellantis, Saft, TotalEnergies, and Mercedes-Benz.
Earlier awards from ACC are for Stellantis' STLA medium platforms, expected to ramp in late 2025.
The company aims to double its Energy Industrial business size within five years.
EV Thermal Barrier revenue reached $90.6 million in Q3, up 176% year-over-year.
Aspen Aerogels has over $113 million in cash and a conditional commitment for a $670 million loan from the U.S. Department of Energy's Loan Programs Office.
The company is preparing for future EV demand, noting that Plant 2 requires an additional $200 million of demand to be viable.
Project costs for Plant 2 in Georgia have risen to an estimated $800 million to $960 million.
Initial production expectations for the Audi platform have been delayed to the second half of next year.

The U.S. Department of Energy awarded Aspen Aerogels a $7.3 million R&D grant dedicated to advancing aerogel technology for battery materials, specifically fast-charging high-power LFP cathode materials.
Aspen is partnering with Oak Ridge National Laboratory under the DOE grant to validate and scale the fast-charging LFP technology from lab to pilot.
The Statesboro aerogel manufacturing plant (Plant 2) is expected to have a revenue capacity between $1.2 billion and $1.6 billion.

Plant 2 is designed to be accretive to margins and generate positive cash flow at moderate capacity utilization levels, requiring only $200 million of revenues within the EV Thermal Barriers segment to break even.
The company spent $316.3 million in cumulative capital expenses on Plant 2 in Georgia to date.
Aspen Aerogels spent $36 million year-to-date advancing construction of Plant 2 in Statesboro, Georgia.
The company is targeting Q1 of next year (2025) to close the DOE loan and restart construction on the Statesboro plant.
On October 21, Aspen Aerogels closed an underwritten public offering with net proceeds of $93.2 million.
As of October 31, the company's cash balance was $203 million.
The company expects its EV Thermal Barrier segment to deliver $315 million of revenues in 2024, a 31% increase over the prior outlook.
The Energy Industrial segment is expected to supply $135 million of product in 2024, with at least $42 million in Q4.
General Motors confirmed plans to produce 200,000 EVs for its own brands in 2024, with Aspen leading Ultium-based production expectations unchanged at 225,000 vehicles.
The company's diluted EPS outlook improved by $0.02 per share to $0.11 per share on a post-offering share count of 84 million shares.
CAPEX excluding Plant 2 is expected to be reduced by $5 million to $40 million for the year.
The company's market capitalization stands at $1.29 billion.
Don Young serves as President and CEO of Aspen Aerogels.
Ricardo C. Rodriguez serves as Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer.
Neal Baranosky is the Senior Director, Head of Investor Relations and Corporate Strategy.
source_title: Earnings call: Aspen Aerogels raises outlook after strong Q3 performance By Investing.com

facts: Aspen Aerogels develops and manufactures high-performance aerogel insulation materials and custom engineered solutions.

The company combines proprietary aerogel formulations with advanced manufacturing processes to deliver products known for their low thermal conductivity, lightweight construction and robust mechanical properties.
The company’s product portfolio spans blanket insulation, boards, and custom shapes built around several proprietary brands, including Pyrogel, Cryogel and Spaceloft.
Pyrogel and Cryogel insulation blankets are widely used in oil & gas and petrochemical applications, where minimizing heat loss and improving operational efficiency are critical.
Spaceloft aerogel products target oilfield tubulars, subsea pipelines and lightweight structural insulation.
Business segments in power generation, chemical processing and building materials benefit from Aspen’s thermal management offerings.
The company leverages its aerogel expertise to develop advanced materials for battery thermal management, aerospace and specialty markets.

Aspen Aerogels serves customers across North and South America, Europe, the Middle East, Africa and Asia-Pacific, with manufacturing facilities in the United States and the Netherlands and a network of distribution partners worldwide.

Under the leadership of President and Chief Executive Officer Suzanne H. Watson, the company continues to invest in research and development, expanding production capacity and exploring new applications for aerogel technology.

By aligning product innovation with the needs of energy-intensive industries and clean-energy transitions, Aspen Aerogels aims to enable end users to reduce emissions, improve process efficiency and lower lifetime operational costs.
source_title: ASPN Q1 2026 Earnings Report on 5/7/2026

facts: Aspen Aerogels Inc. is a leading provider of high-performance aerogel insulation for energy, industrial, and electric vehicle (EV) markets.
The company released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2023.
Total revenue was $329.6 million, representing an increase of 113% from $154.5 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2023.
Revenue growth was driven by accelerating demand in the EV market and continued market share gains in the sustainable insulation materials market.
Gross profit was $135.8 million, a significant increase from $27.3 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2023.
Gross profit improvement reflected improved manufacturing and fabrication yields and a favorable mix of products sold.
Income from operations was $39.8 million, compared to a loss of $50.6 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2023.
Net income was $2.0 million, compared to a net loss of $45.3 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2023.
Net income per share was $0.03, compared to a net loss per share of $0.65 for the nine months ended September 30, 2023.
The company operates in two main segments: Energy Industrial and Thermal Barrier.
The Energy Industrial segment focuses on high-performance aerogel insulation for energy and industrial markets.
The Thermal Barrier segment targets the electric vehicle market with products like PyroThin® aerogel thermal barriers designed for battery packs in EVs.
Revenue from the Thermal Barrier segment has shown significant growth driven by demand in the EV market.
Major contracts for Thermal Barrier products are held with OEMs like General Motors, Toyota, and Audi.
The Energy Industrial segment serves oil producers and operators of refineries and petrochemical plants with products like Pyrogel® and Cryogel® aerogel blankets.
In the U.S., revenue was strong driven by demand for thermal barriers in the EV market.
Internationally, significant revenue contributions came from Latin America, Europe, Canada, and Asia.
Latin America showed a notable increase in revenue due to higher sales of thermal barriers.
The company is actively developing new aerogel products for the EV market, including PyroThin® thermal barriers designed to enhance battery safety in electric vehicles.
These products are expected to drive future growth as they gain traction with major automotive OEMs.
The company is expanding its manufacturing capacity with a new plant in Bulloch County, Georgia, to meet growing demand for its aerogel products.
This Georgia facility will support increased production volumes for both the Energy Industrial and Thermal Barrier segments.
The company anticipates continued strong revenue growth driven by demand in the EV market.
It plans to enhance manufacturing capabilities, improve product formulations, and leverage external manufacturing facilities to meet this demand.
The company expects to benefit from a conditional commitment from the U.S. Department of Energy for a loan to finance its new manufacturing facility.
source_title: Aspen Aerogels Inc. Reports Strong Q3 Performance with Significant Revenue Growth — TradingView News

facts: Aspen Aerogels shares surged more than 13% Wednesday following the announcement of a major funding development and strong preliminary Q3 2024 results.
The company secured a conditional commitment from the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) for a loan of up to $670.6 million.
The funding is part of the Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing loan program.
The funding will finance the construction of Aspen's second aerogel manufacturing facility in Register, Georgia.
The new plant will provide a revenue capacity between $1.2 billion and $1.6 billion.
Revenue will be driven by the production of PyroThin thermal barriers designed to enhance battery safety for electric vehicles (EVs).
Aspen CEO Donald Young stated the proposed loan would fully fund the required CAPEX for the second aerogel manufacturing facility.
The facility is expected to play a key role in scaling PyroThin supply for Aspen's rapidly growing Thermal Barrier business.
Aspen's PyroThin product has attracted six major OEM contracts in the U.S. and Europe.
PyroThin contributed to a 277% year-over-year growth in thermal barrier revenues.
Aspen reported preliminary Q3 financial results of $117 million in revenue and $25 million in adjusted EBITDA.
The company posted a net loss of $13 million, impacted by a one-time $27.5 million charge from the extinguishment of a convertible note.
Chief Financial Officer Ricardo Rodriguez stated the proposed loan and its terms will provide an opportunity to optimize capital structure if finalized.
The Register Plant project is expected to create 255 permanent jobs.
The project aligns with the DOE's goal of supporting domestic manufacturing for zero-emissions vehicles.

facts: About 1,400 workers at Blue Bird Corp.’s Fort Valley school bus manufacturing plant voted to join the United Steelworkers Union (USW).
The unionization vote contradicts the South’s non-union history.
United Steelworkers International President Tom Conway stated pride in the vote and readiness to help bargain a fair contract that accounts for workers' contributions to the company's success.

The Blue Bird union organizing effort is one of the most significant in the South, a region marked by state right-to-work laws that neither require workers to join a union nor pay union dues as a condition of employment.
President Joe Biden issued a statement congratulating the Blue Bird workers for voting to unionize.
Biden stated that unions built the middle class and that union workers will be a big part of America's future.
Blue Bird is an active participant in America’s move toward clean energy, manufacturing both low-emission and zero-emission school buses.
Blue Bird has been approved to receive significant funding through the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency’s Clean School Bus program.
USW District 9 Director Dan Flippo stated that the vote gives workers a seat at the bargaining table.

Flippo noted that corporations historically viewed the South as a place to suppress wages and working conditions by preventing unionization, but the USW has a history of fighting for Southern workers.

Political and business leaders in Georgia and other Southern states, along with overseas corporate executives, have long cited low union membership in the region as a key factor in corporate decisions to locate job-creating operations there.
source_title: Blue Bird workers in Fort Valley unionize

facts: Blue Bird Corporation, together with its subsidiaries, designs, engineers, manufactures, and sells school buses in the United States, Canada, and internationally.
The company operates through two segments: Bus and Parts.

It offers Type C, Type D, and specialty buses; and alternative power options through its propane powered, gasoline powered, compressed natural gas powered, and electric powered school buses, as well as diesel engines.
Blue Bird also sells replacement bus parts and provides extended warranties related to its products.
The company sells its products through drop ship and a network of dealers, as well as directly to fleet operators, the United States government, and state governments.
Blue Bird works with independent service centers and maintains a parts distribution center.
Blue Bird Corporation was founded in 1927 and is headquartered in Macon, Georgia.
The company's strategic shift to electric school buses positions it as a frontrunner in the growing ESB market, with strong funding visibility and secure projections.

facts: Ten of Blue Bird Corp.'s 2014 All American buses were delivered on Wednesday to the Georgia Department of Corrections.
The delivered buses will be placed into service immediately, replacing existing fleet buses that have each accumulated over 300,000 miles.
The redesigned buses can seat 50 passengers, increasing seating capacity by four compared to previous models.
The buses feature pass-through luggage compartments and interior storage bins to fully maximize storage.
The buses include a custom onboard lavatory.
The delivery event took place in Forsyth, Ga., with officials from Blue Bird Corp. and dealer Yancey Bus Sales and Service.
The Georgia Department of Corrections’ buses are the first of Blue Bird’s redesigned 2014 All American buses to roll off the production line in Fort Valley, Ga.
The buses feature Blue Bird’s exclusive purpose-built chassis.
The buses feature an ergonomic driver’s cockpit for increased driver comfort.
The buses feature a larger windshield that provides improved driver sightlines.
The buses feature an excellent turning radius.
The buses feature a larger entrance door.
The buses feature enhanced serviceability.
The buses feature excellent fuel economy.
The buses feature greater parts commonality with other Blue Bird buses.

Phil Horlock, president and CEO of Blue Bird Corp., stated that as a Georgia-based manufacturer for over 86 years and the second largest manufacturer in the automotive category in Georgia, Blue Bird is pleased to provide buses crafted by and for Georgians.
Blue Bird’s manufacturing facilities are all based in Fort Valley, Ga.
Blue Bird employs 1,500 Georgians from over 20 Georgia counties.
Over 20% of the materials used to build Blue Bird buses are sourced from Georgia-based companies.
A 2009 economic multiplier study found that for every $1 Georgia school districts and other customers spend on Blue Bird buses, that dollar is multiplied four times within the state.
Every Blue Bird bus sold in Georgia provides a positive impact to the state gross domestic product by approximately $86,000.

Commissioner Brian Owens of the Georgia Department of Corrections stated that their partnership with Blue Bird Corp. is a new beginning and expressed pleasure in owning buses designed and built by a Georgia-based company.

Eric Arnold, vice president of power systems for Yancey Brothers Co. (parent company of Yancey Bus Sales and Service), noted the dealership is honored to provide buses to the Georgia Department of Corrections and looks forward to future opportunities for safe transportation throughout Georgia.

Officials in attendance at the delivery event included state of Georgia Chief Operating Officer Bart Gobeil, Chris Cummiskey (commissioner of the Georgia Department of Economic Development), local elected officials, and representatives from the Monroe County Chamber of Commerce.
source_title: First Blue Bird redesigned All Americans delivered in Georgia | School Bus Fleet

facts: The Zacks Domestic Auto industry is set for renewed momentum as affordable EVs and proposed tax incentives encourage buyers despite economic uncertainty and rising tariffs.
More budget-friendly EVs are expected to expand adoption.
Tariffs may limit foreign rivals, supporting domestic manufacturers.
The Trump administration's proposed tax deduction of up to $10,000 on interest for U.S.-assembled vehicles could further boost demand.
2025 auto sales are projected to reach 16.1-16.2 million units.
Domestic automakers like General Motors Company, Polaris Inc., and Blue Bird Corporation are likely to gain from acquired momentum.

The Zacks Domestic Auto industry includes companies engaged in designing, manufacturing, and retailing vehicles across the globe, including passenger cars, crossover vehicles, sport utility vehicles, trucks, vans, motorcycles, and electric vehicles.
The industry is highly consumer cyclic and provides employment to a large number of people.
Widespread use of technology and rapid digitization are resulting in a fundamental restructuring of the automotive market.
Several companies in the industry have engine and transmission plants, and conduct research and development, and testing of electric and autonomous vehicles.
Even with higher tariffs and economic uncertainty, the growing availability of lower-priced electric vehicles is expected to draw in more buyers.
Until now, most EVs sold in the United States have been positioned at premium price points, putting them beyond the reach of many shoppers.
Carmakers are shifting attention to more economical models, with multiple EVs priced at $35,000 or below slated to launch in the next few years.
Stricter tariffs are likely to limit foreign automakers, especially dominant Chinese players, from entering the domestic market, potentially boosting the share of American manufacturers.

Per Forbes, the Trump administration's proposed "One Big Beautiful Bill Act" would allow eligible buyers of newly purchased, U.S.-assembled vehicles to deduct up to $10,000 in interest paid on auto loans.
The tax benefit excludes leases and commercial vehicles and begins phasing out for individuals earning more than $100,000 or couples making above $200,000.
The average new car is priced at $48,000 with loan rates near 8.64%.
Someone paying $2,000 in annual interest could save roughly $400 each year or about $2,000 across a five-year loan under the proposed deduction.
These savings could help counteract price increases linked to Trump's import tariffs.
The measure is expected to stimulate demand for new vehicles.
Per S&P Global, U.S. auto sales in 2025 will climb to 16.1-16.2 million units, up from 15.98 million in 2024.
Chinese automakers boosted their collective share of Europe's total vehicle sales to around 5.1% in the first half of 2025, nearly double their share from a year earlier.
In China, domestic EV companies have tightened their hold on the market, with their share jumping from 65% in the first half of 2020 to nearly 90% in the first half of 2025.
American automakers are finding it increasingly difficult to defend their global market positions, constraining their sales growth outside the domestic market.
The global share loss limits U.S. automakers' scale, margins, and investment capacity, ultimately influencing pricing and competitiveness in their home market as well.
The Zacks Automotive – Domestic industry currently carries a Zacks Industry Rank #65, placing it in the top 27% of 250 Zacks industries.
The group's Zacks Industry Rank indicates bright near-term prospects based on positive earnings outlook for constituent companies.
Analysts are optimistic about this group's earnings growth potential based on aggregate earnings estimate revisions.
The Domestic Auto industry has underperformed the auto sector and the Zacks S&P 500 composite over the past year.
The industry has returned 6.3% compared with the sector and S&P 500's growth of 7.1% and 14.5%, respectively.
On the basis of trailing 12-month enterprise value to EBITDA, the industry is currently trading at 53.06X compared with the S&P 500's 18.63X and the sector's 25.8X.
Over the past five years, the industry has traded as high as 69.52X, as low as 10.25X and at a median of 29.25X.
General Motors is one of the world's largest automakers and held the largest share of the U.S. auto market at 16.5% in 2024.
Strong demand for GM's leading brands continues to fuel sales growth, driven by popular pickups and SUVs.
GM expects ICE volumes to stay robust while strengthening its production base with onshoring initiatives.
Upcoming launches include the next-generation Cadillac CT5, redesigned XT5, and the Orion Assembly plant's relaunch in early 2027, set to produce the Cadillac Escalade and new full-size pickups.
GM is gaining strong momentum in its software and services business, which is becoming a key growth driver.
GM currently sports a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy).
The Zacks Consensus Estimate for GM's 2025 and 2026 EPS has moved north by 6 cents and 41 cents, respectively, over the past seven days.
GM has surpassed earnings estimates in each of the trailing four quarters, with an average earnings surprise being 8.95%.
Polaris develops, engineers, and produces both off-road and on-road vehicles.
Polaris posted a solid third quarter with $1.8 billion in revenues.
Along with its On Road and Marine segments outperforming their industries, the company secured notable Off Road market share gains during the quarter.
Polaris introduced its updated 2025 full-year outlook and expects adjusted sales in the range of $6.9 billion to $7.1 billion.
The company reported sales of $7.17 billion in 2024.
PII currently sports a Zacks Rank #1.
The Zacks Consensus Estimate for PII's 2025 loss per share has narrowed by $1.15 over the past 60 days.
The Zacks Consensus Estimate for PII's 2026 EPS has moved up 40 cents over the past 60 days.
PII has surpassed earnings estimates in each of the trailing four quarters, with an average earnings surprise being 179.12%.
Blue Bird is engaged in designing, engineering, manufacturing and sale of school buses and related parts.
It also offers alternative fuel applications with its propane-powered and compressed natural gas-powered school buses.
Blue Bird reiterated its fiscal 2026 financial outlook based on solid performance in 2025.
It expects fiscal 2026 revenues of $1.5 billion and adjusted EBITDA of $220 million.

The company expects 2026 to mirror the record results delivered in 2025 and anticipates continued profitable growth in the years ahead as it works toward reaching approximately $2 billion in revenues and an adjusted EBITDA margin exceeding 16%.
BLBD currently carries a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy).

The consensus mark for BLBD's 2026 EPS has moved up 6 cents over the past 30 days.

facts: The aluminum market size is valued to increase by USD 45.99 billion, at a CAGR of 4.1% from 2025 to 2030.
Proliferation of electric vehicle adoption and automotive lightweighting will drive the aluminum market.
The demand for lightweight, high-strength materials in the automotive sector, particularly for electric vehicles, is a primary catalyst for growth.
The transportation segment was valued at USD 57.77 billion in 2024 and showed a gradual increase during the forecast period.
The shift to electric vehicles necessitates high-strength aluminum for structural frame components and vehicle battery enclosures to offset battery weight and improve range.
Advanced manufacturing techniques in the automotive sector, including high-pressure die casting, have increased aluminum use per vehicle.
Sustainable aluminum solutions and the decarbonization of industry further reinforce the material's strategic importance in modern vehicle engineering.
Automotive lightweighting with high-strength aluminum is critical for improving efficiency and battery range.
Advancements in rolled aluminum products and architectural aluminum systems are being developed for vehicle battery enclosures.
Secondary aluminum recycling consumes approximately 95% less energy than primary production, making it a strategic priority for supply chain sustainability.
The industry is implementing low-carbon aluminum production methods to meet environmental mandates and supply chain requirements for electric vehicles.
Companies are focusing on advanced scrap sorting infrastructure to improve circular material flow and produce high-grade secondary aluminum for automotive applications.
The global aluminum market faces challenges from escalating energy costs and stringent environmental compliance mandates, impacting supply chain stability.
Geopolitical fragmentation and protectionist trade policies disrupt established supply chains for bauxite and alumina, forcing companies to diversify sourcing strategies.
source_title: Aluminum Market Growth Analysis - Size and Forecast 2026-2030 | Technavio

facts: Bonnell Aluminum is a subsidiary of Tredegar Corporation (NYSE:TG).
Bonnell Aluminum announced it has broken ground on a future on-site health center for employees and family members at its Carthage, Tennessee manufacturing site.
The on-site health facility is scheduled to open in the middle of 2019.
The facility will include a medical clinic and fitness amenities.
The health center will significantly benefit over 500 employees and their families.
Brook Hamilton, President of Bonnell Aluminum, commented that the company first became interested in this concept after acquiring Futura Industries, which has a similar facility in Utah.
Hamilton stated that the concept is expected to produce higher employee satisfaction and lower healthcare costs.
Onsite Care Clinics will serve as the medical care provider at the new facility.
Bonnell Aluminum plans to work with Onsite Care Clinics to develop innovative healthcare solutions at its additional sites outside of Utah.
Bonnell Aluminum's operating divisions include AACOA and Futura Industries.

The company is a leading manufacturer of customer-fabricated and finished aluminum extrusions serving the building and construction, automotive and transportation, consumer durables, machinery and equipment, and electrical and distribution markets.
Bonnell Aluminum has manufacturing facilities located in Newnan, Georgia; Carthage, Tennessee; Niles, Michigan; Elkhart, Indiana; and Clearfield, Utah.

facts: Bonnell Aluminum is a subsidiary of Tredegar Corporation.
Bonnell Aluminum announced it will be investing approximately $18 million over the next 12 months to fund an expansion project.
The investment will fund the purchase of a new state-of-the-art aluminum extrusion line at its AACOA facility in Niles, Michigan.
The new line will be comprised of a state-of-the-art 3,600-ton extrusion press, housing a 9-inch container, handling systems, and ancillary equipment.

The project also includes additional floor space to accommodate future value-added fabrication capacity.
Planned startup of the new extrusion line is the second quarter 2017.
AACOA primarily serves the consumer durables and machinery and equipment markets.
The Niles facility serves the automotive market due to its regional location.

Ira Endres is vice president Sales and Marketing for Bonnell Aluminum.
Bonnell Aluminum operates a facility in Newnan, Georgia.
