facts: Goodyear Tire & Rubber Co. plans to move truck tire production out of its Danville, Va., plant.
Goodyear currently has the most TBR tire production capacity in North America.
The Danville factory dates back to 1966 and will continue to manufacture tires for aviation applications.
The Danville factory can build up to 11,000 TBR tire units a day according to the latest MTD research.
Goodyear’s other TBR tire plant is located in Topeka, Kan., with a daily production capacity of 5,500 medium truck tire units.
The Topeka, Kan. plant also builds OTR tires.
Bridgestone Americas Inc. has the second-largest TBR tire production capacity among all North American tire companies, with a total estimated ceiling of 15,200 units per day.
Bridgestone's TBR production is split between two plants in LaVergne, Tenn., and Warren County, Tenn.
The LaVergne, Tenn. plant can make up to 5,800 tires daily.
MTD estimates the Warren County, Tenn. plant has a total daily capacity of 9,400 units.
A $550 million investment at the Warren County plant is still on track with its original schedule to facilitate new technology development capabilities according to Bridgestone officials.
The capital infusion is expected to expand the Warren County facility by 850,000 square feet.
The expansion will give Bridgestone the ability to manufacture TBR tires equipped with radio frequency identification tags.
Michelin North America Inc. has a total TBR tire production capacity of 13,400 units per day according to MTD research.
Michelin’s plant in Spartanburg, S.C., can build 6,400 units per day at full throttle.
The company’s Waterville, Nova Scotia, plant enjoys a capacity of 7,000 tires a day.
Continental Tire the America LLC’s plant in Clinton, Miss., began producing TBR tires in 2020 and has a 2,800-unit-a-day capacity.
Continental’s Mount Vernon, Ill., plant can build up to 10,300 TBR tires on a daily basis when running at full bore according to MTD data.
Yokohama Tire Corp.’s TBR tire plant in West Point, Miss., can produce up to 3,000 units per day according to MTD research.
Grupo Carso/Euzkadi (Continental AG) operates a plant in San Luis Potosi, Mexico, with a daily capacity of 3,800 medium truck tires.
Corporacion de Occidente SA de CV’s Guadalajara plant can build around 2,800 TBR tire units daily.
JK Tyre & Industries, formerly CIA Hulera Tornel, enjoys a daily TBR production capacity of 1,500 units at its Mexico City plant according to MTD data.
Hankook Tire America Corp. is currently about half-way through the construction process at its plant in Clarksville, Tenn., with the project remaining on track.

In August 2021, Hankook announced it would invest $1.6 billion at the Clarksville plant to double its passenger and light truck tire production to 11 million units while adding TBR tire production capabilities.
Rob Williams, Hankook Tire America Corp. president, stated that the addition of TBR tire production at the facility is a vital step to their growth in the medium truck tire market.
Two months ago, Sumitomo Rubber North America Inc. (SRNA) shuttered its consumer, TBR and motorcycle tire production facility in Buffalo, N.Y.
The Buffalo plant was one of the oldest still-operating tire factories in the U.S., dating back to 1923, after SRNA took it over from Goodyear in 2015.
The shuttered Buffalo plant had the capacity to build around 2,300 TBR tires per day according to MTD research.
source_title: MTD Looks at TBR Tire Production Capacity  | Modern Tire Dealer

facts: Goodyear Tire & Rubber Co. exceeded its Q4 2024 expectations with a net income of $76 million, a huge jump compared with last year’s net loss of $291 million, due to actions under its corporate transformation plan.

The tire manufacturer’s Q4 net sales were down 3.3% year over year at $4.9 billion and the full year’s net sales were down nearly 6% at $18.9 billion due to decreased tire units sold, unfavorable pricing and reduced inventory in its Americas and Asia Pacific segments.
Net income for the full year skyrocketed to $70 million compared to 2023’s $689 million net loss, driven by a slight bump in segment operating income amid the company’s corporate strategy overhaul.
The rubber tire maker’s corporate transformation plan was initially announced in 2023 and includes streamlining its portfolio and production to reduce costs.
Other goals under the plan include generating $2 billion from divesting and seeking alternatives for its businesses, such as its chemical division.
So far, the company has closed on the $905 million sale of its off-the-road tire business to The Yokohama Rubber Co. on Feb. 3.
Last month, Goodyear announced an agreement to sell the Dunlop brand to Sumitomo Rubber Industries for approximately $701 million, set to close in mid-2025.
The company also set aside $1 billion as part of the restructuring plan, according to EVP and CFO Christina Zamarro on a Feb. 14 earnings call.
Other priorities under the plan include cutting $1.5 billion in costs by the end of the year through optimizing manufacturing plants, supply chain and research and development efforts.
Last month, the company announced plans to refocus its Danville, Virginia, facility on producing rubber mixing and aviation tires.
The shift at the Danville facility will include reductions in production capacity and the layoff of approximately 850 workers.
Goodyear expects to complete the Danville facility transformation plan by year’s end and spend between $130 million to $140 million implementing the job cuts and facility transformation.

While scaling down one plant, the company plans on increasing capabilities at its Lawton, Oklahoma, facility through a modernization project that will add about 10 million units of new capacity for premium tires in 2025 and 2026.

President and CEO Mark Stewart stated the company will accelerate the introduction of new products to more effectively compete in the premium tiers and capitalize on blank space opportunities in the U.S.
Goodyear will introduce five new product lines in the U.S. this year, each with significant improvements in the large rim SKU coverage than the predecessor lines.
Last month, Goodyear named Don Metzelaar SVP of global manufacturing and supply chain, and Alain Kohnen VP of manufacturing Americas and global plant optimization to help with its revamp goals.
The rubber tire maker seeks to generate $750 million in 2025, but could lose momentum as a result of the Trump administration’s tariffs and ensuing higher raw material costs.

Looming tariffs on Mexico and Canada could reduce the company’s flexibility to use its global manufacturing facilities to meet tire demand, as Goodyear and other Tier 1 tire makers source their volumes from factories located in those countries.
Goodyear is in talks with government officials about the tariff issue and looks forward to continuing collaborations with leaders in Washington to address critical issues impacting its business.
The company is working across its operations to mitigate any potential near-term impacts of tariffs related to its Canadian and Mexican supply, remaining agile as the situation may be fluid.
The company is also anticipating the European Commission’s decision on whether or not to place reciprocal tariffs on the U.S.
Goodyear expects raw material costs to increase by about $350 million in the first half of 2025, according to CFO Christina Zamarro.
source_title: Tariffs threaten Goodyear’s continued sales rebound | Manufacturing Dive

facts: Great Dane company leaders will receive a special proclamation from the Georgia General Assembly this week in honor of the company's 125th anniversary.
Great Dane was founded in 1900 in Savannah, Ga.
The company is recognized as an industry leader in the transportation business.
The recognition ceremony will take place at 10:40 a.m. Thursday in Georgia's Capitol House Chamber.
The proclamation highlights the company's ongoing resilience, pioneering leadership in the transportation industry, and long-standing commitment to quality and innovation.
Great Dane is one of only 0.5% of U.S. companies that have remained in business for more than 100 years.
Great Dane joins a prestigious group of iconic American companies that have also reached this milestone, including Leopold's, Coca-Cola, and Harley-Davidson.
source_title: Georgia recognizing Great Dane on 125 years | Trucks, Parts, Service

facts: Great Dane is a premier trailer manufacturer with a history dating back to 1900.

Great Dane's product offerings include refrigerated trailers (Everest, Alpine, and Johnson models), dry trailers (Champion Sheet & Post, Champion Composite, and Sahara models), and open deck solutions.
Great Dane engineers durable and high-quality trailers and truck bodies.
Great Dane operates nine manufacturing plants across the United States.
Great Dane operates 20 sales branches across the United States.
The Savannah facility is located at 400 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31401.
The Savannah location offers new equipment, used equipment, parts, service, accessories, and financing.
source_title: Great Dane Trailers - Savannah - Lathrop Ave | Equipment Radar | Equipment Radar

facts: Great Dane closed its manufacturing plant in Savannah, Georgia.
The last trailer rolled off the Savannah facility's assembly line three days before January 12.
The final trailer produced was a reefer headed for Sysco.
Great Dane was founded in 1900 in Savannah and built its first trailer there in 1930.
Great Dane announced the Savannah facility closure in November, citing the extended economic downturn on the transportation industry and the need for more efficient production.
Corporate offices will be retained at the Savannah location, housing the research and development lab, sales and marketing, accounting, customer service, and engineering.
On January 8, Great Dane gathered its 270 employees who would lose their jobs, fed them breakfast, and took group pictures.
Chris Hammond IV is 41 years old and serves as vice president of dealer sales.
Kit Hammond is 69 years old and is a retired Great Dane president.
Chris Hammond II is 96 years old and is a retired chairman of the board.
Chris Hammond II was present when the first refrigerated trailer rolled off the assembly line in the 1940s.
Bernie Griner is a quality-control supervisor who worked at the plant for 35 years; his father worked there for 45 years.
Connie Barker is a plant superintendent and a 42-year veteran of the company.
Walter Ross is a specialist at the plant.

Dewey Cribbs was a former plant manager who started working at age 15, remained for 53 years, requested his tombstone feature a Great Dane outline, and was buried on May 6, 2002, wearing his 50-year service pin.

The Savannah plant historically manufactured virtually every conceivable trailer type except dump trailers, including truck bodies, marine and domestic containers, refrigerated trailers, van trailers, flat trailers, lowboy trailers, gas/petroleum tank trailers, asphalt, grain, bulk fruit, and open top trailers.
In 1900, The Savannah Blowpipe Company was established in Savannah with a total capitalization of $3000.
By 1912, the company began fabricating light structural steel and steel plate products.
In 1919, the company moved to a new location on Lathrop Avenue with 10,000 square feet and a 10-ton traveling crane.
In 1931, The Savannah Blowpipe Company was incorporated as The Steel Products Company, with total assets of $100,000.
In 1938, The Steel Products Company responded to market pressures with lightweight tank trailers and van trailers made of high-tensile steel.
In the early 1940s, The Steel Production Company pioneered the refrigerated trailer field by building the first produce van with a wet ice bunker and a gas engine and blower system.
In 1947, they made the first mechanically refrigerated trailers.
By 1949, the company produced a van trailer made of aluminum with a tandem axle.
In 1953, refrigerated aluminum trailers became the standard for the eastern United States due to demand in the Florida perishables market.
Sales outlets were established in 31 cities and 18 states throughout the eastern half of the United States by 1953.
In 1958, The Steel Production Company changed its name to Great Dane Trailers, Inc.
In 1959, Great Dane brought out a redesigned line of van trailers and a new tank trailer line, both largely of lightweight aluminum.
Great Dane entered the piggyback and container market, completing its first piggyback trailer order in 1961 and its first maritime container order in 1963.
Sales outlets were established in 24 states blanketing the eastern part of the country by 1963.
In 1966, Great Dane announced a $2 million expansion program, which increased trailer production by 100%.
In 1972, Great Dane acquired Arrow Trailers in Memphis, Tennessee.
Since 1972, production at the Memphis plant has increased by almost 200%.
In 1974, a decision was made to build another manufacturing facility for van trailers in Brazil, Indiana.
The Brazil facility was expanded by adding a refrigerated trailer production line, completed during the first quarter of 1978.
In 1988, Great Dane acquired the SuperSeal reefer product line and the Wayne, Nebraska, plant from Timpte.
In 1996, Great Dane opened a dry van plant in Terre Haute, Indiana, as well as a parts distribution center adjacent to it.
In 1997, there was a merger of Great Dane and Pines Trailer, creating Great Dane Limited Partnership, the world's largest trailer company.
In 1999, Great Dane expanded the Little Rock, Arkansas, sales office to a full-service branch.
In 2000, Great Dane celebrated its 100th anniversary and completed its plant's 250,000th trailer in Savannah.

Between 2001 and 2002, Great Dane made two major acquisitions: Strick's Eastern Van Manufacturing facilities in Danville, Pennsylvania, and Abbeville, South Carolina, and two of Trailmobile's manufacturing facilities in Jonesboro, Arkansas, and Charleston, Illinois.
In 2001, Great Dane introduced the "Next Generation from Great Dane" campaign, including redesigns of all refrigerated and platform trailers, plus two new freight van trailers.
In 2002, the SSL dry freight van was added to the product line, featuring steel-lined interior walls.
In July 2003, a new branch opened in Charlotte, North Carolina.
Between 2004 and 2005, Great Dane announced five new products: three new trailers and two advancements in liner technology.
The FREEDOM line of flatbeds combined aluminum and steel.
The Super LT refrigerated trailer was designed for the eastern US and Canadian truckload market.
The i-Van was introduced as a semi-insulated freight van for temperature-sensitive loads like candy and chemicals, produced in Brazil, Indiana.
In early 2005, the company announced a $5 million re-tooling of the Brazil plant for special foam presses and modular assembly needed for the i-Van.
Great Dane introduced PunctureGuard liner for reefers and vans, a thermoplastic innovation.
The company introduced ThermoGuard, a glass-reinforced thermoplastic with a composite layer to seal reefer insulation.
In fall 2004, Great Dane Limited Partnership announced the acquisition of a production facility in Huntsville, Tennessee, previously owned by Wabash National Corporation.
By the third quarter of 2005, two full production lines and two finishing lines were added to the Huntsville plant.
Kit Hammond officially retired from day-to-day responsibilities at the end of 2005 after more than 40 years.
source_title: Great Dane closes Savannah plant, takes a look back at rich history of trailer production | Trailer Body Builders

facts: Great Dane Trailers officially broke ground on the site of its new refrigerated trailer manufacturing plant in Statesboro, Ga.
The groundbreaking ceremony included local business representatives, state and local political leaders, and company executives.

Georgia Gov. Sonny Perdue, Statesboro Mayor Joe Brannen, and Bulloch County Commission Chairman Garrett Nevil hailed the cooperation among the state, local economic developers, and Great Dane as key reasons for the success of the site selection process.
The 118-acre Statesboro site was chosen because it is located just northwest of Savannah and remains close to where Great Dane was founded in 1900.

Bill Crown, president and CEO of CC Industries and CEO of Great Dane Limited Partnership, stated that continuing Great Dane’s legacy by keeping it close to the company’s deep roots in Savannah was just as important in the site selection.
The plant will encompass 450,000 sq. ft., including office and show room space.
The facility will have the capacity to produce more than 5,000 trailers annually.
The plant will employ more than 400 people.
Phill Pines, president and COO of Great Dane Trailers, stated that the refrigerated trailers built there will be complex and detailed products.
The new reefer plant will feature the latest technology, making it the most modern, efficient plant in the world.
Construction is expected to get underway later this year to bring the plant into production in 2012.
source_title: Great Dane Trailers breaks ground on new Southeast plant

facts: Great Dane announced that Chief Executive Officer Bill Crown intends to step away from his role as CEO.
Effective January 1, 2023, Dean Engelage, Great Dane’s President, will succeed Crown as the company’s Chief Executive Officer.
Mr. Engelage’s appointment is part of a managed executive transition coordinated by Bill Crown, the Great Dane executive leadership team and Great Dane’s ownership.
Bill Crown served as Great Dane's CEO over the past 25 years.
Dean Engelage has served as Great Dane’s President for the past nine years.
Under Engelage and his team, Great Dane became the safest company in the trailer industry for its associates.
Under Engelage and his team, Great Dane elevated quality to the highest levels in the company’s history in areas including products, services, and people.
Bill Crown and Dean Engelage named Rick Mullininx to succeed Engelage as President and Chief Operating Officer of Great Dane, also effective January 1, 2023.
Rick Mullininx has been with Great Dane for 40 years, serving in various engineering and manufacturing roles within the company.
Most recently, Rick Mullininx served as Executive Vice President of Engineering before his new role.
In his previous role, Mullininx was responsible for product design, production engineering, manufacturing engineering, quality assurance, and customer service.
Rick Mullininx first joined Great Dane in 1980 as a summer engineering intern in the Research and Development department in Savannah.
Rick Mullininx graduated from the Georgia Institute of Technology with a bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering.
Rick Mullininx earned a Master of Science in Mechanical Engineering from the University of South Carolina.
Dean Engelage joined Great Dane in 2011 as Executive Vice President of Sales and Strategic Planning.
Dean Engelage was appointed President of Great Dane in 2013.
Prior to Great Dane, Engelage worked for American Airlines and General Dynamics in marketing, purchasing and finance.
Engelage spent more than 16 years with CC Industries in various management positions.
Engelage began his career with CC Industries in 1996 in the marketing department of Woodard, becoming President of Woodard in 2004.
After presiding over the sale of Woodard in 2008, Engelage worked on the Johnson Truck Bodies and Trail King acquisitions and managed CC Industries’ Asia operations.

Great Dane is a leading manufacturer of high-performance commercial transportation equipment, including dry and refrigerated trailers and truck bodies, as well as steel, aluminum, and combo platform trailers.
Great Dane has 11 manufacturing plants strategically located throughout the United States.
Great Dane is headquartered in Chicago, Illinois, with additional corporate offices in Savannah, Georgia.

Great Dane operates a network of corporate-owned service centers and full-line independent dealers offering distribution points for new and pre-owned trailers as well as aftermarket services across North and South America.
source_title: Dean Engelage Named Great Dane CEO - Trailer Technician

facts: Albany’s economy has a lower economic risk profile than most cities, making it a good place to grow a business and raise a family.
Albany’s economy is likely to perform on par with the U.S. economy.
Economic growth may slow in 2025 but it is unlikely to stall, mostly because of success in landing private-sector economic development projects.
This is due in part to Albany’s local economic developers, who are expanding the area’s economic base while simultaneously diversifying economic activity.
Last year, the Albany-Dougherty Economic Development Commission announced that automotive supplier GRUDEM will build an auto parts manufacturing facility that will create 65 jobs.
And Diamond Door Products announced that it would open a metal door manufacturing facility in Albany, creating 25 jobs.
In 2022, Thrush Aircraft announced it will expand production with a new product line and create 125 jobs to meet demand for its agricultural aircraft.
Pratt Industries’ $18 million capital expansion at its corrugated box manufacturing facility created about 30 new jobs.
And Woodgrain’s $10 million investment to expand its millwork operations in neighboring Lee County created about 150 new jobs.

Outdoor Network, a dealer, distributor and manufacturer of marine and powersports equipment, put $28.1 million into an expansion of its advanced manufacturing facility and distribution headquarters in Albany.
The company exceeded its initial announcement of new 92 jobs, as the demand for outdoor recreational vehicles is stronger now than pre-pandemic.
Albany’s diversified economy moderates the ups and downs of the business cycle.
Compared to the state and the nation, Albany’s economy is more dependent on government, education, healthcare, retail and agriculture.
These are not very cyclical industries.
Within manufacturing, Albany is focused on the production of nondurable goods, sales of which are less cyclical than sales of durable goods.
Albany’s manufacturing base produces basic consumer staples that households buy, whether the economy grows or shrinks.
Procter & Gamble, Molson Coors, Mars Wrigley Confectionary and Georgia-Pacific Lumber are good examples.
Although I expect the national economy to slow, Albany’s economy has a lower economic risk profile than most cities, making it a good place to grow a business and raise a family.

Albany is a strong regional transportation hub with quality four-lane highways and Southwest Georgia Regional Airport, which provides direct access to Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport.
The Albany airport’s new 48,000-square-foot hangar, refinished runways and a new general aviation terminal reinforce the city’s role as a logistics hub for air cargo.
That plus an 85-acre tract at the airport that is certified for accelerated development means Albany is well positioned for airport-based economic development.
Despite significant strengths, Albany MSA’s population has been declining.
Outmigration thus remains a challenge and the result of too few high-paying, high-tech jobs, which then pushes some of the most educated residents to look elsewhere for jobs.
Albany is slowly moving away from traditional manufacturing and government towards private-sector, service-providing industries.
A good example is Albany’s booming healthcare industry.
To prosper, Albany will have to pull many more patients from surrounding areas.
Albany’s workforce development initiatives signal future economic growth.
Efforts by the Albany Area Chamber in concert with educational institutions and strategic partners will help employers build the pipeline they need.
Albany’s agriculture economy should do well because global demand for ag products will increase faster than supplies.
Further development of the city’s food processing industry will help add ag production in the area.
Increases in defense spending will benefit the U.S. Marine Corps Logistics Base Albany.
I expect military pay raises to outpace inflation.
Over time, the base’s high-tech mission should spur more high-tech development in the private sector of Albany’s economy.
There will be more high-tech employment opportunities locally, which should reduce outmigration.
Albany’s housing market is a plus.
In early 2024, Albany’s home prices were 55% above their pre-pandemic peak.
The year-over-year percentage change was 13%.
Despite these gains, Albany’s homes are not overvalued and are unlikely to decline in price.
That will sustain growth in consumer spending, especially for home-related goods and services.
Put it all together and Albany’s prospects are good.
Jeffrey Humphrey is Director of the Selig Center for Economic Growth at the University of Georgia’s Terry College of Business.
source_title: Albany Focus - Georgia Trend Magazine

facts: Hanon Systems is a Hankook Company.
Hanon Systems is committed to addressing the effects of climate change.

Hanon Systems supports the need to protect the planet for future generations, and joins other companies in the worldwide effort to mitigate the effects of carbon dioxide (CO₂) emissions on the earth’s climate, resources and its people.
Hanon Systems has declared a goal to be carbon neutral by 2040 and net-zero by 2050.
source_title: Hanon Systems | Automotive Thermal Solutions

facts: A leading automotive air conditioning system supplier in Korea.
The company mainly supplies products to Hyundai, Kia, and Ford.
The company manufactures air conditioning, powertrain cooling, compressors, fluid transport, and hydraulic control systems.
Established in 1986 as a joint venture between Ford and Mando Machinery (now Mando) of Korea.
In 1999, Ford transferred its interest to Visteon (USA) and the company became a subsidiary of Visteon.
Acquired thermal exhaust business from Cooper-Standard in 2014.
In 2015, Visteon sold its interest to affiliates of Hahn & Company and Hankook Tire, and the company changed its name to Hanon System Corp.
Acquired Magna International's hydraulic controls business (Fluid Pressure and Controls) in 2019.
Acquired condenser business of Keihin Corporation (now Astemo) in 2021.
Established a new engineering center in Portugal in 2024.
The company had the largest market share for automotive air conditioning systems in Korea in the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024.
Korean automotive air conditioning market share was 48% in FY ended Dec 31, 2024, 46% in FY ended Dec 31, 2023, and 46% in FY ended Dec 31, 2022.
Main customers for the Korean market share data are Hyundai Kia.
As of Jan 3, 2025, major shareholders were Hankook Tire & Technology Co., Ltd. (54.77%) and Hahn & Co. Auto Holdings Co., Ltd. (21.63%).
HVAC products include Ultra-Flat HVAC, Super slim HVAC system, Straight Airflow Path HVAC, Ultraviolet (UV) LED photocatalyst, CO2 sensor, fragrance system, and ionizers.
Compressor products include Fixed Swash Plate Compressor, Rotary Suction Compressor, Variable Swash Plate Compressor, and Electric Compressor.
Offers fluid transport controllers, cooling modules, condensers, radiators, charge air coolers (CAC), EGR systems, pumps & valves, electronic throttle bodies, and electronic wastegate actuators.
EV/HEV thermal management products include battery thermal management systems, heat pump systems, and high voltage cooling fan motors.
FCEV products include centrifugal air compressors, high voltage cooling fan motors, and e-compressors.
In July 2009, the company acquired the remaining shares in Halla Climate Control Alabama Inc. (HCSA), making it a wholly owned subsidiary.

Visteon sold its approximately 70 percent ownership interest to an affiliate of Hahn & Company and Hankook Tire Co., Ltd. for approximately USD 3.6 billion in Dec 2014, with completion expected in the first half of 2015.
Established Beijing BHAP Hanon Systems Co., Ltd. in Dec 2017 as a joint venture with Shandon Binzhou Bohai Piston Co., Ltd. (subsidiary of BAIC) to manufacture HVAC control systems.
Established Chongqing Hanon Jianshe Automotive Thermal Systems Co., Ltd. in Jan 2018.
Established Hanon EFP Corporation, FAWER Hanon Automotive Components (Changchun) Company Ltd., and Hanon systems EFP Deutschland GmbH in Aug 2018.
Established EFP Operations Mexicana, S.A.de C.V and Climate Systems Services Mexicana EFP, S.A. de C.V. in Nov 2018.
Completed acquisition of Gibbs-Hungary Die Casting Ipari Termelo Kft in Nov 2018.
Completed acquisition of Magna International’s Fluid Pressure and Controls Division in Mar 2019.
Merged Hanon Systems Southern Germany GmbH into Hanon Systems Deutschland GmbH in Nov 2019.
Merged Hanon Systems EFP Bad Homburg GmbH and Hanon Systems Bad Homburg GmbH into Hanon Systems EFP Deutschland GmbH in Nov 2019.
Divested Jinan Han Cai Automotive Parts Company in Dec 2019.
Sold Hanon Bangalore Operations Private Limited in Feb 2021.
Acquired Hanon Thermal Technology Czech, s.r.o. in Mar 2021.
Acquired Powertrain Cooling de Mexico, S.A. de C.V. in May 2021.
Acquired Keihin Corporation’s North American and European condenser operations in May 2021.
Established Hanon Systems Hubei Co., Ltd. in Dec 2021.
Liquidated Hanon Systems Rus LLC in Oct 2024.
source_title: Hanon Systems (Formerly Halla Visteon Climate Control) - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

facts: Hanon Systems announced on the 8th that it had developed the world’s first 4th-generation heat pump system for electric vehicles, which has been commercialized since July.
Hanon Systems’ 4th-generation heat pump system is the first to use a parallel heat source recovery method that utilizes external air heat and waste heat from motors and batteries.
The recovered heat is used for heating, cooling, and battery temperature management, contributing to improving electric vehicle driving range.
This modular heat pump system incorporates several new patents, securing a technological advantage.

It features an integrated control system that manages the refrigerant control module (RCM) and the coolant control module (CCM), enabling comprehensive management of the entire thermal management system.

Nagasubramony Ramachandran, co-CEO of Hanon Systems, stated, “Hanon Systems is the first in the world to develop a 4th-generation heat pump system, contributing to improvements in driving range. As the global second-largest company in thermal management, we continue to aim for ‘lightweight’ and ‘high-efficiency’ parts and tirelessly develop new technologies.”
Hanon Systems first mass-produced a waste heat recovery type heat pump system in 2015.
Its second generation, mass-produced in 2018, added battery thermal management features and contributed to increased electric vehicle range as the first integrated thermal management system.
The third generation, developed in 2021, was the first to be applied to an 800V electric vehicle platform.
The fourth-generation heat pump system was first applied to Kia’s EV3.
Production will start in Korea, and the company plans to expand its manufacturing bases to the US and Europe.

Wang Yun-ho, Vice President of the AP Innovation Center at Hanon Systems Research Institute, said, “We were able to develop the world’s first new technology proactively through close collaboration with our clients, and I am pleased that it contributes to enhancing the driving experience of electric vehicle users.”
Moreover, Hanon Systems has also applied the world’s first Thin HVAC technology.
Thin HVAC means a ‘reduced’ heating, ventilation, and air conditioning system.

Reducing the overall size of the HVAC system by about 30% minimizes the exposure of the HVAC system area under the cockpit (dashboard module), providing more legroom for passengers and a comfortable and quiet driving experience with low power consumption and low noise design.
source_title: Hanon Systems Develops the World’s First 4th-Generation Heat Pump System for Electric Vehicles, “Improving Driving Range with Parallel Heat Source Recovery Method” < Tech & Bio < Article - AVING NEWS

facts: Hanon Systems has unveiled the development of the first fourth-generation heat pump system designed for electric vehicles.
The thermal management system has been commercially available since July.
The new pump system could be the first to employ a simultaneous heat recovery method.
The system allows both waste heat from the engine and battery, as well as external heat from the air, to be reused for multiple applications.
Recovered heat is redirected for vehicle heating and cooling, and simultaneously for battery thermal management.
Constant improvement in the company’s heat pump system design has enabled optimization of energy efficiency and reduction of consumption, thereby extending the range of electric vehicles.
Hanon Systems is the world’s second largest thermal management supplier.
The company remains committed to developing lightweight, highly efficient components through continuous innovation.
Subu Nagasubramony, co-CEO of Hanon Systems, stated that constant improvement in design has enabled optimization of energy efficiency and reduction of consumption.
The heat pump system features a modular design incorporating several patent-protected or patent-pending features.
The system integrates the refrigerant control module (RCM) and refrigerant circuit control module (CCM) into a unified control unit for comprehensive thermal system management.
Hanon Systems’ track record in heat pump production dates back to 2015, when it began mass production of systems with waste heat recovery.
In 2018, the company introduced its second-generation system, which added battery thermal management functions and became the first integrated system to improve electric vehicle range.

In 2021, Hanon Systems developed a third-generation system implemented on a platform dedicated to electric vehicles, featuring an 800 V electric compressor to reduce charging times and energy consumption.
The fourth-generation heat pump system has initially been implemented in the Kia EV3 in South Korea.
Hanon Systems plans to expand production of the fourth-generation system to the United States and Europe.
Yoon Ho Wang serves as vice president of the AP Innovation Center at Hanon Systems Research Institute.
The company has launched Thin HVAC (Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning) technology, marking a world first.
Thin HVAC is a reduced HVAC system that decreases the overall size of the system by 30%, providing more legroom for passengers.
source_title: Hanon Systems introduces new heat pump system for electric vehicles

facts: Invest Ontario is supporting a $155 million investment by Hanon Systems for the construction of a new facility in Woodbridge, Ontario to manufacture cutting-edge e-compressors for electric vehicles (EVs).
The new facility will be North America’s first high displacement e-compressor plant.

The facility will allow world-class automakers in both Canada and the United States to meet their growing demand for critical high quality EV components from Ontario’s rapidly expanding EV supply chain.
Hanon Systems is headquartered in South Korea and is one of the world’s largest automotive thermal and energy management solutions companies.
This investment is part of a firm-wide strategy to expand the company’s global e-compressor manufacturing capabilities.
E-compressors are specially designed for EVs due to their lack of belt-driven engines.
These components are vital to vehicle performance and safety as they form a critical part of the thermal management system.
Hanon Systems’ heat pump system, equipped with these e-compressors, enhances thermal management in EVs, improving energy efficiency and driving range.
Over the last four years, Ontario has attracted $45 billion in auto and EV-related investments from global companies with the help of Invest Ontario.
Ontario boasts an end-to-end auto supply chain, a skilled workforce, a wealth of critical minerals, and a thriving tech ecosystem needed to build the cars of the future.
source_title: HANON SYSTEMS INVESTS $155 MILLION TO BUILD ONTARIO’S FIRS

facts: The US is urging manufacturers to invest through a series of initiatives, including the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA).
South Korea-based EV and parts suppliers are accelerating their moves to invest in the US.
source_title: Korea-based Hanon Systems to make in Georgia as IRA triggers investment wave in US

facts: Hella Automotive India (HAI), the Indian arm of German major Hella, has secured a niche in the four-wheeler electronics segment manufacturing auto sensors, actuators and horns.
HAI is planning to foray into the two-wheeler segment.
Naveen Gautam, managing director of HAI, said the two-wheeler segment is a big market in India that cannot be ignored anymore.
Gautam noted that bikes today are linked to lifestyle change and users now aspire for car-like comforts.
The company plans to capitalize on this changing trend.
Electronic throttle is one feature HAI could see employed in motorcycles.
Keys, which are currently mechanical, will see remote start coming up.
Despite potential in the aftermarket, the company will focus more towards OEM based solutions.
Hella’s current portfolio includes accelerator pedal sensor, climate sensor, rain and light sensors, head lamp levelling actuator, body actuator, switches and horns.
HAI counts on companies like Tata Motors, Maruti Suzuki, Mahindra Reva, Ashok Leyland, Hyundai, Honda and many more as clients.
HAI will soon make Body Control Modules (BCM) and remote control keys, which are likely to be launched in a couple of months for an Indian OEM.
BCM is not intended to reduce manufacturing cost but to improve energy management and make the car more efficient.
The intelligence of the BCM system will help reduce the complexity of wiring, thus reducing weight of the car and improving heat dissipation.
Manufacturing BCM may not be the biggest project in India but is surely the most complex one, defining a new era for Hella and its India plant.
As far as exports are concerned, HAI will firmly keep its focus on horns.
India is the only place where Hella Automotive manufactures horns for the two-wheeler segment, making them the most exported products from the country.
The company is planning to introduce electronic horns and multi-tone horns.
At present, over 30 percent of HAI’s revenue comes from exports.
Overall, HAI constitutes less than five percent of Hella’s global revenue with plans to reach 10 percent by 2020.
Hella spends about 12 percent of global sales, roughly Euro 5 billion, on R & D.
In India, Hella spends little more than 12 percent because it is in the development phase and leads responsibility for some products while offering support for others.
Apart from a manufacturing plant in Dhankot and corporate office in Gurgaon, Hella has an R&D centre in Pune for electronics and one in Chennai for lighting.
source_title: Hella Automotive eyes two-wheeler segment - Auto Components India

facts: HELLA GmbH & Co. KGaA ("FORVIA HELLA") sponsors the 2025 Time Attack 1 Division Award at the 2025 Pikes Peak International Hill Climb.
HELLA will provide the HELLA Hype & HELLA Heritage Awards plus cash rewards for the event.
Pikes Peak veteran Robb Holland will drive a HELLA-sponsored 2022 Porsche 911 GT3 RS Cup car prepared by Rotek Racing.
Veteran Clint Vahsholtz will drive a HELLA-sponsored 2023 Porsche 718 Cayman GT4 RS Clubsport.
HELLA is continuing a program to further increase its brand awareness in categories including brake systems, leveraging its Original Equipment competency and history with high-performance vehicles.
Robb Holland's 911 features minimal upgrades for the climb except JRZ active dampers and a Starlink datalink to livestream his attempt at the 103rd running of the PPIHC on June 22.
The race climbs to a 14,115ft summit over a public street course.
Holland is expected to be the first driver to livestream his Pikes Peak attempt for fans.
Robb Holland's racing career dates back to 2011 and he set a front-wheel drive record at his first Pikes Peak attempt in 2016.
Holland is using extensive experience and simulator preparation to aim for the HELLA Time Attack 1 class winner's trophy.
Clint Vahsholtz's Cayman GT4 RS won both the 2025 Pirelli GT America and GT4 America road racing championships.
Vahsholtz aims to claim the Pikes Peak GT4 Trophy by Yokohama driving the car with a HELLA livery.
Clint Vahsholtz has competed at Pikes Peak since 1992, initially rode motorcycles before switching to cars in 1996, and holds 24 class wins predominantly in Super Stock and Open Wheel.

Robert Tinson, Vice President at HELLA, stated the company celebrates more than 125 years of engineering excellence and shares the spirit of innovation, performance, and endurance exemplified by the race.

The Time Attack 1 Division includes close-bodied modified and specialized production-based two- and four-wheel drive vehicles with eligibility restricted to original configuration for air/fuel delivery, number of cylinders, and number of drive wheels.
The division is dominated by Porsche models but also includes a Subaru Impreza WRX STI, BMW M2 CS Racing, Mitsubishi Lancer Evo, and others.
The HELLA Hype Award allows fans to nominate drivers via online voting at Fan Fest on Friday June 20, with the winner receiving a $2000 reward presented by Vice President Robert Tinson.
The HELLA Heritage Award plus $2000 cash reward will be presented to the driver with the most finishes during their competitive career in the historic race.
All trophies and awards will be presented during the PPIHC trophy ceremony.

FORVIA HELLA is an international automotive supplier specializing in high-performance lighting technology, vehicle electronics, and Lifecycle Solutions for spare parts, workshop business, and special vehicle manufacturers.
FORVIA HELLA has approximately 36,500 employees across over 125 locations worldwide and generated €8.1 billion in adjusted sales in the 2024 financial year.

Parent company FORVIA is the seventh largest supplier of automotive technology globally, combining strengths of Faurecia and HELLA with approximately 260 industrial sites, 78 R&D centers, over 150,000 employees (including more than 15,000 R&D engineers) in over 40 countries.
FORVIA holds a portfolio of over 13,000 patents and aims to be the preferred innovation and integration partner for automotive manufacturers worldwide.
Contact details for HELLA Automotive Sales, Inc are located in Peachtree City, GA.

facts: Hitachi Construction Machinery Americas held a ceremony to open its new state-of-the-art headquarters in Newnan, Georgia, for the development and production of new machinery and to elevate customer support.
The company entered the North American market more than 40 years ago, opening an office in Marietta, Georgia, in 1981.
Since then, it has continued to expand sites within the state.
Spanning 19 acres, the Newman site was originally established as a wheel loader assembly and manufacturing plant in 1987 and has served as company headquarters since 2018.
Al Quinn serves as CEO of Hitachi Construction Machinery Americas.
Changes at the facility represent and reinforce the company’s commitment to people, excellence, and the environment, according to Quinn.

The new regional headquarters will help the company focus on key growth areas including the compact and construction business network in North, Central, and South America and expansion in sales of mining excavators for construction and quarry and mining sites.
By combining the resources of the excavator, loader, and mining businesses, the company has experienced a tenfold demand from previous years, according to Quinn.
Since taking the lead for the brand’s construction and mining equipment in the Americas, the company has more than tripled its employee count.
The headquarters encompasses 88,128 square feet of office space and 27,686 square feet dedicated to conference, ConSite command center, and training space.
The investment in a regional Americas facility helps strengthen that direct connection to the customer, according to Quinn.
Hitachi Construction Machinery Americas continues to support an extensive network of independent local dealers and rental companies.
In addition to the new headquarters facility, Hitachi Construction Machinery Americas is creating operating bases for parts remanufacturing and expansion of the rental business.
Development of the new facility comes only a couple of years after the dissolution of a 33-year marketing and manufacturing agreement with John Deere.
The companies jointly produced excavators for North and South America at plants in North Carolina, Canada, and Brazil.
In February 2022, the agreement was officially dissolved and Deere purchased the plants to produce its brand of excavators.
Hitachi and Deere have a new supply agreement for Deere excavators.
Deere also announced a new agreement for compact excavators with Wacker Neuson.
In May 2022, at the Newman facility, Hitachi rolled out its first set of independently produced excavators for North America, which were made in Japan and imported here.
The design of the Hitachi Construction Machinery Americas headquarters is meant to reflect the core values of the company.
Designed with energy efficiency in mind, the facility features Low-E glass that blocks 62% of solar energy while allowing 70% of visible light to pass through.
Skylights and roll-up doors contribute to natural lighting, integrating the indoors with the outdoors.
The company says an advanced energy management system monitors and optimizes energy consumption and temperature changes.
In March 2022, Hitachi set out on a path to establish a more direct connection with customers, according to Quinn.
The company's culture has been very much involved with listening, understanding, and implementing customer feedback.
Embracing sustainable practices, Hitachi Construction Machinery Americas opted for adaptive reuse principles in the development of the existing property.
Establishing a direct connection with customers means listening and aligning with their needs and values, according to Quinn.
The industry has actively embraced a move toward sustainable practices, and this thoughtful consideration is reflected in the design of the new headquarters, according to Quinn.
More than 75,347 square feet of greenspace was reclaimed from the site by reconfiguring the employee parking areas and eliminating buildings and concrete.
Sustainable practices utilized in the construction of the headquarters included the recycling of concrete, steel, and wood already on the site.
Reclaimed materials included 5,101 cubic yards of recycled concrete that was repurposed for roadway, fill, and redistribution.
Reclaimed materials also included 40,580 square feet of recycled exterior metal wall panels, and 125 tons of reused steel frame structure.
The company said 69,965 square feet of existing concrete slabs were reused, 4,920 square feet of roof purlins, and 48,976 square feet of old metal roof panels became decking for the new roof.
Reclaimed pine wood totaling approximately 1,545 linear feet was utilized on stairways and stair seating areas, and recycled plastics were used to manufacture all carpet products.
source_title: Hitachi advances expansion in U.S. with new headquarters | Equipment World

facts: Hwashin Georgia Corporation was awarded a $1.2 million Economic Development Growth and Expansion (EDGE) grant.
The grant award was celebrated in partnership with the Georgia Department of Economic Development and the Department of Community Affairs.
Hwashin Georgia is the first tenant at the new Industrial Park on Highway 257.
Hwashin Georgia is investing $176 million into its facility.
The project will bring 475 new jobs to Laurens County.
The EDGE grant specifically funded the purchase of 56 advanced robots for Hwashin's manufacturing process.
Hwashin Georgia operates as a tier-one supplier to the Hyundai Motor Group Meta Plant.
Hwashin Georgia manufactures components for Kia, Ford, and Rivian.
Hwashin Georgia's operations are located in Dublin, Georgia.
The Heart of Georgia Altamaha Regional Commission (HOGARC) partnered in securing the grant and other critical grants for the industrial park.
Sung-Kee Kim serves as the CEO of Hwashin Georgia.
Jae Oh Hwang serves as the CFO of Hwashin Georgia.

facts: The Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority has received a nearly $3 million federal grant for infrastructure improvements to the Ga. 257 Rail Served Site.
The grant was announced Tuesday and awarded by the U.S. Commerce Department’s Economic Development Administration.
The funding will support the upcoming Hwashin auto part manufacturing facility as well as further prepare the site to attract potential investors.

Roger Folsom, who chairs the development authority’s governing board, said the funding awarded today from the Economic Development Administration and the U.S. Department of Commerce will help fulfill the commitment for infrastructure for Hwashin Georgia Corporation.
This is the largest grant award ever received by the Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority.
Construction continues of the Hwashin plant site, a $176 million facility that is expected to create 460 jobs.
The funding will go toward building roads, a sewer network and storm water runoff system on the 331-acre site at the intersection of U.S. 441 and Ga. 257.

Development authority president Ryan Waldrep said the infrastructure that will be constructed as a result of this project will support Hwashin Georgia Corporation and provide vital capacity building infrastructure essential for continued development for future industrial growth at the Site.
The grant must be matched by $1.1 million in local funding, which Waldrep said the authority is seeking additional grant funding to provide.
A $600,000 grant from the Southern Crescent Regional Commission has already been obtained and other funding source applications are pending.
The authority intends to reduce the local cash expense as much as possible.
source_title: Development Authority receives $2.9 million grant for infrastructure improvements – Courier Herald Today

entity_name: Hyundai Motor and LG Energy Solution Joint Venture Plant
canonical_name: Hyundai Motor and LG Energy Solution Joint Venture Plant in Ellabell, Georgia
facts: Governor Brian Kemp is expected to arrive in Korea soon.
Georgia was not well-known to Korea until events on Sept. 4.

U.S. immigration officials searched, shackled, and detained more than 300 Korean nationals during a raid on a plant under construction in a joint venture by Hyundai Motor and LG Energy Solution (LGES) in Ellabell, Georgia.
The plant is part of a massive $7.6 billion investment.
LGES was in the process of constructing a battery factory at the site when the immigration raid took place.
Detained Korean nationals included individuals directly employed by LG and its suppliers.
Immigration officials stated the detained workers lacked proper visas or had overstayed them.
Most detained workers were on a B-1 (temporary business visitor) visa or through the visa-free Electronic System for Travel Authorization (ESTA).
The U.S. suddenly and unexpectedly deemed the visa-free ESTA improper for anyone seeking to work in the United States.
Korea and the U.S. worked together to seek the release of the detained workers and find better visa solutions.

Seoul and Washington officials agreed that Korean workers investing in the U.S. could stay under B-1 visas or ESTA for installing, servicing, and repairing foreign-purchased equipment at their U.S. plants.
Gov. Kemp called for visa reform in the aftermath of the raid.
LGES has resumed sending workers to the Georgia plant.
Department of Homeland Security officials promoted the Georgia plant raid as 'the largest single-site enforcement operation in U.S. history'.
Koreans with family members among the approximately 300 detainees spent days waiting for news of their release.
The Korean public was shocked by the raid, and anti-U.S. sentiment percolated.
Kemp is familiar with Seoul's economic investments and plans in Georgia from his third trip to Korea.
The U.S. has yet to allot an appropriate H-1B visa quota for Korean nationals comparable to countries like Singapore.
The two sides have not yet agreed on setting up a dedicated visa for highly skilled Korean workers.
Trump issued an order requiring anyone newly applying for an H-1B visa to pay a $100,000 fee to encourage businesses to hire Americans.
Kemp should continue pressing for improved visa policies for Korean workers staffing plants and creating jobs in Georgia.
Kemp should apologize for the September raid to assuage Korean concerns and ensure Georgia's business environment remains vibrant.
The governor's visit was planned well before the raid.
source_title: [ED] Gov. Kemp of Georgia should address Korea's collective sense of betrayal - The Korea Times

canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution Joint Venture
facts: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution (LGES) announced the formation of a new joint venture to build a U.S. EV battery plant in Bryan County, Georgia.
The plant will be capable of supporting the production of 300,000 vehicles per year.
The anticipated investment for the plant is more than $4.3 billion.
The new battery plant will be located adjacent to Hyundai’s Metaplant America.
Hyundai’s Metaplant America is its first full-electric vehicle (EV) facility in the U.S.
Hyundai’s Metaplant America was announced last year and is currently under construction.
Hyundai stated the new facility will help respond fast to the soaring EV demand in the U.S. market.
The new facility aims to create a stable supply of batteries in the region.
Battery packs assembled using cells from the plant will be supplied for the production of Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis EV models.
Earlier this year, Hyundai announced a goal to become one of the world’s top 3 EV manufacturers by 2030.
Jaehoon Chang, President and CEO of Hyundai Motor Company, stated that Hyundai Motor Group is focusing on electrification efforts to secure a leadership position in the global auto industry.
Jaehoon Chang stated they will create a strong foundation to lead the global EV transition through establishing a new EV battery cell plant with LG Energy Solution.
The launch follows an executive order by President Joe Biden in 2021 mandating that zero-emissions vehicles make up half of new vehicle sales in the U.S. by 2030.
The launch follows the allocation of $7 billion from the U.S. Bipartisan Infrastructure Law aimed at strengthening the U.S.’ domestic battery supply chain.
Automotive leaders GM, Ford, BMW, Toyota, Honda and Stellantis have each announced significant investment plans in EV and battery capacity over the past several months.
LGES was spun out of chemical giant LG Chem in 2020.
LGES is now concentrating most of its resources in the U.S. to expand battery production capacity.
The new JV brings LGES's number of battery plants currently operating or being constructed in the U.S. to seven.
This includes a $4.4 billion JV launched earlier this year with Honda.
Youngsoo Kwon, CEO of LG Energy Solution, stated that two strong leaders in the auto and battery industries have joined hands to drive the EV transition in America.

Youngsoo Kwon stated LG Energy Solution will commit its best efforts to offering ultimate sustainable energy solutions to customers by advancing product competitiveness and global operational expertise.
Construction on the new JV’s battery plant is expected to begin later this year.
Battery production at the plant is anticipated to begin as soon as late 2025.
source_title: Hyundai, LG Energy Launching $4.3 Billion EV Battery Plant to Meet “Soaring” U.S. Demand – ESG Investing

facts: A joint venture between Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution will result in a new lithium-ion battery plant in Georgia to supply EVs in North America.
Korea-based Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution will build a $4.3 billion facility in Bryan County, near Savannah, Georgia.
The new facility is located near Hyundai Motor Group's Metaplant America site, which is currently under construction.
Battery production at the joint venture plant is expected to begin at the end of 2025.
The facility will have an annual production capacity of 30 gigawatt-hours (GWh).
The plant's output will support the production of 300,000 electric vehicles annually.
Korea-based LG Energy Solution is the second largest supplier of lithium-ion batteries to the auto industry.
LG Energy Solution has partnerships and joint ventures with customers including Ford, General Motors (GM), and Stellantis to supply EV battery needs.

LG Energy Solution is building three battery plants in partnership with GM: one in Warren, Ohio (already opened), one in Spring Hill, Tennessee (opening in 2024), and a third in Lansing, Michigan (scheduled to open in early 2025).
Including the new Hyundai joint venture, LG Energy Solution has seven battery plants currently operating or under construction in the U.S.
In the Georgia joint venture, Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution will each hold a 50 percent stake in the cell production facility.
Hyundai will assemble the lithium-ion cells from the new plant into battery packs and supply them to its manufacturing facilities for Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis EV models.
LG Energy Solution currently supplies EV batteries for Hyundai Group models, including the Elantra Hybrid, Kona Electric, and IONIQ 6.
Hyundai is building a new $5.5 billion EV manufacturing plant in Georgia to produce new Hyundai and Genesis models.
Following the 2022 Inflation Reduction Act, plans for more than 20 new EV battery production facilities have been announced.
The Inflation Reduction Act ties EV purchase incentives to a manufacturer's North American production of vehicles and battery packs.
To be eligible for a $7,500 federal tax incentive, EVs must be manufactured in the U.S., Canada, or Mexico.
To qualify for the incentive, SUV EVs must be priced under $80,000, while sedans, coupes, hatchbacks, and wagons must be under $55,000.
In 2020, the total U.S. market share for battery EVs was under 2 percent.
By 2022, the U.S. battery EV market share rose to around 6 percent.
U.S. battery electric vehicle sales are projected to reach 25 to 30 percent by 2030, and 45 to 50 percent by 2035.
Companies like GM have announced plans to phase out gasoline models by 2035.
The transition to electric transportation presents continuing challenges regarding domestic sourcing of raw materials for battery cells.
Automakers including Ford, GM, and Tesla are investing in domestic lithium sources through established mining companies and new startups exploring novel extraction methods.
source_title: Hyundai, LG Energy Solution to Open US Battery Plant - Tech Insights

canonical_name: LG Energy Solution and Hyundai Motor Group Joint Venture
facts: LG Energy Solution (LGES) and Hyundai are teaming up to build a battery cell manufacturing plant in Savannah, GA.
Hyundai Motor Group and LGES signed a memorandum of understanding on May 26 in Seoul, Korea.
Each company will hold a 50% stake in the joint venture for the U.S.-based battery plant.
The companies plan to invest more than $4.3 billion in the battery facility.
The parties agreed to produce EV batteries so Hyundai could accelerate its electrification efforts in North America.
The battery plant would allow Hyundai EVs to qualify for the Inflation Reduction Act’s $7,500 tax credit.
Jaehoon Chang, president and CEO of Hyundai Motor Company, stated that Hyundai Motor Group is focusing on its electrification efforts to secure a leadership position in the global auto industry.

Jaehoon Chang stated that Hyundai will create a strong foundation to lead the global EV transition through establishing a new EV battery cell plant with LG Energy Solution, a leading global battery producer, and long-time partner.
Construction on the new battery facility is expected to start in the second half of 2023.
Cell production at the facility is slated to begin by the end of 2025.
Hyundai and LGES aim for an annual production capacity of 30 GWh at the battery facility in Georgia.
The 30 GWh capacity is enough to support the production of 300,000 EVs annually.
Hyundai Mobis will assemble battery packs using the cells from the plant in Georgia.
After assembly, the battery packs will go to U.S. manufacturing facilities producing Hyundai, Kia and Genesis EV models.
Hyundai aims to become one of the top three global automakers by 2030.
Hyundai is preparing to achieve its goal by investing $18.1 billion in electric vehicle production.
Hyundai plans to increase its annual production run rate for EVs to 3.64 million globally by 2030.
Hyundai will launch 31 EV models from its brands, including Kia and Genesis, to reach its goal.
Most of Hyundai’s EV models will be available in all major markets.
source_title: LG Energy Solution, Hyundai to Build EV Battery Plant in Georgia - Autobody News

entity_name: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution Joint Venture
canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution Battery Manufacturing Joint Venture
facts: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution will invest an additional $2 billion in battery cell manufacturing in Bryan County, Georgia.
The additional investment raises the joint venture's total investment value to more than $4.3 billion.
This expansion will create another 400 new jobs.

Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that in a single year, they broke ground on the largest project in state history, landed multiple suppliers across the state for Hyundai’s Metaplant, and welcomed LGES to Bryan County.
Governor Brian Kemp stated the goal is to make Georgia the e-mobility capital of the nation.
Governor Kemp noted that major investments provide paychecks, improved schools, and infrastructure, and thanked Hyundai Motor Group and LGES for recognizing Georgia as the No. 1 state for business.

José Muñoz, president and global COO of Hyundai Motor Company and president and CEO of Hyundai and Genesis Motor North America, stated the incremental investment reflects a continued commitment to create a more sustainable future powered by American workers.
Muñoz stated Hyundai is proud to partner with LGES and is grateful for the support of Governor Kemp and Georgia communities while working to be a global leader in the electrified mobility industry.

In May 2023, Hyundai Motor Group and LGES signed a memorandum of understanding establishing LGES as the partner for Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America’s (HMGMA) onsite battery cell manufacturing joint venture.
The EV manufacturing facility and the battery JV represent an estimated $7.59 billion in total investment and will create 8,500 new jobs for the region over the next eight years.

Dong-Myung Kim, president and head of LG Energy Solution's Advanced Automotive Battery Division, stated the investment underscores a dedication to driving America’s EV transition while bolstering the local economy through quality job creation.
Kim thanked the State of Georgia for its support and expressed excitement to bring top-quality products and operational experience to grow with local communities.
The 30 GWh facility will be able to support the production of 300,000 units of EVs annually at full operations.
Hyundai Mobis will assemble battery packs using cells from the plant and supply them to Hyundai Motor Group’s U.S. manufacturing facilities for production of Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis EV models.
Carter Infinger, Chairman of the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority, stated the announcement will bring 400 additional well-paying jobs to the Savannah region.
Infinger noted that continued investment by Hyundai Motor Group and announced suppliers like LGES will be transformative for the region.

Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project in partnership with the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority (JDA), Georgia Power, Georgia Ports Authority, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated Governor Kemp set a goal of Georgia becoming the top location for EV manufacturing in the United States.
Wilson stated that through building an ecosystem supporting the entire EV supply chain, Georgia continues to add to industry-leading success and bring future jobs to Georgians statewide.

Wilson credited state and community partners like the Savannah-area JDA, utility providers, Georgia Ports Authority, and Georgia Quick Start for creating a collaborative, business-friendly environment in Georgia.
Since 2018, Georgia has attracted more than $25 billion in investments and created over 30,000 jobs along the entire EV supply chain.
Battery-related projects have accounted for at least $11 billion of Georgia's total investment.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in Georgia's automotive industry increased by 324 percent compared to FY21.
Hyundai Motor Group has about 250,000 employees worldwide and its mobility brands include Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis.
The announcement was made in Atlanta on August 31, 2023.
source_title: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution to Invest Additional $2B in Battery Manufacturing in Georgia Bryan County - Batteries News

entity_name: Hyundai-LG Energy Solution EV Battery Plant Joint Venture
canonical_name: Hyundai-LG Energy Solution EV Battery Plant Joint Venture
facts: South Korea’s Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution (LGES) announced they will build a $4.3 billion EV battery plant in Georgia.
The two companies signed a memorandum of understanding during a signing ceremony at LGES’s headquarters in Seoul.
The new EV battery plant will be a 50/50 joint venture located in Bryan County, south of Savannah, adjacent to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America is currently under construction.
The new battery plant will have an annual production capacity of 30 GWh.
The plant will be able to support the production of 300,000 EVs annually.
The Biden administration’s Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) offers a $7,500 tax credit for EVs that meet domestic manufacturing requirements, including their batteries.
The IRA also provides incentives for companies to build manufacturing hubs for EVs, batteries, and clean energy.
Hyundai’s EVs, along with its Kia and Genesis EV models, currently do not qualify for the IRA tax credit because they do not meet domestic manufacturing requirements.
Hyundai Motor Group and LGES decided to make their latest major joint industrial investment in the US.
Senator Jon Ossoff (D-GA) led an April trade mission to South Korea where he met with senior executives from both firms.
Senator Jon Ossoff stated that securing the LG-Hyundai battery investment to Georgia was a key goal of his trade delegation trip.
Senator Jon Ossoff noted that the IRA’s manufacturing incentives continue to bring jobs and investment to Georgia.
According to the Financial Times, Georgia is second in the US, behind South Carolina, in new investments resulting from the IRA’s manufacturing incentives.
Construction of the Bryan County EV battery plant will start in the second half of 2023.
Battery production at the new plant is expected to start at the end of 2025 at the earliest.
The investment is expected to create 3,000 jobs.
LGES now has seven battery plants currently operating or being constructed in the US following this announcement.
LGES is concentrating most of its resources on expanding production capacity across its US battery plants.
source_title: Hyundai and LG are going to build a $4.3B EV battery plant in the US

facts: Prior to March 2011, the Company operated as a single entity, Kikuchi Co., Ltd.
From the 2012 fiscal year, figures reflect the Company's performance after its merger with Takao Kinzoku Kogyo., Co. Ltd.
Sales for the FY ended Mar. 31, 2012 were 135,376 million JPY.
Operating income for the FY ended Mar. 31, 2012 was 9,779 million JPY.
Ordinary profit for the FY ended Mar. 31, 2012 was 8,864 million JPY.
Net income for the FY ended Mar. 31, 2012 was 13,727 million JPY.
Production volumes significantly dropped after the Great East Japan Earthquake and flooding in Thailand but recovered as customer production increased.
Revenue for operations in Japan was 47,517 million yen with a profit of 3,613 million yen.
Four companies were added as consolidated subsidiaries in North America after the merger, generating revenue of 43,649 million yen and profit of 1,196 million yen.
One company was added as a consolidated subsidiary in Europe, generating revenue of 9,545 million yen and profit of 1,158 million yen.
Four companies were added as consolidated subsidiaries in Asia, generating revenue of 15,684 million yen and profit of 2,475 million yen.
Operations of Global Auto-Parts Alliance India Private Ltd. were transferred to G-TEKT India Private Ltd. due to a reorganization of Indian operations, resulting in an extraordinary loss.
One company was added as a consolidated subsidiary in China, generating revenue of 24,719 million yen and profit of 1,122 million yen.
Sales of automotive body parts in South America decreased due to the Great East Japan Earthquake, resulting in revenue of 4,316 million yen and a loss of 38 million yen.
The Company announced it will form a new manufacturing subsidiary in Karawang, West Java, Indonesia named PT. G-TEKT Indonesia Manufacturing.
The Indonesian subsidiary will produce automotive body structural parts and thick precision parts.
G-TEKT plans to invest 4 billion yen in the new Indonesian company.
The land area of the Indonesian facility is 73,078 square meters and the building will cover 11,209 square meters.
The Indonesian company is scheduled to be operational in January 2013 with estimated annual sales of 3.2 billion yen in 2015.
The Company established a joint venture, G-One auto Parts De Mexico, S.A. De C.V., with H-One in March 2012.
Operations at the Mexican joint venture are scheduled to start from January 2014.
The Mexican JV will be established in Celaya, Guanajuato, Mexico, with an operating officer of G-TEKT serving as its representative.
Total investment for the Mexican JV is approximately US$46 million (approx. 3,528 million yen).
Planned sales for the Mexican JV are approximately US$66 million (approx. 5.06 billion yen) in 2015.
The Mexican JV will utilize large-size transfer press and welding facilities to produce automotive parts for 200,000 vehicles per year.
The JV is a fifty-fifty partnership expected to be operational in April 2014.
R&D expenditure for the FY ended Mar. 31, 2012 was 199 million JPY.
G-TEKT will launch development of a hot-stamping method for ultra-high-tensile steel sheets to further increase material strength levels.
By the end of 2012, dedicated production equipment will be installed at its Hamura Plant in Tokyo to develop new mass production technology.
Honda Motor Co., Ltd., a major customer, has adopted structural components made from hot stamped ultra-high-tensile steel sheets in its new mini passenger vehicle.
G-TEKT aims to engineer a highly competitive hot-stamping method to help customers introduce hot-stamped, ultra-high-tensile steel sheets to a wider variety of vehicle models globally.
R&D activities involving new technologies and products are conducted at the Company's main Technology Development Department.
Fifteen engineers work at the Product Development Section as of March 2012.

Major FY R&D achievements include developing hot-press technology, forming technology for high tensile strength steel sheets, non-destructive inspection technology for spot-welded points, precision stamping technology for heavy sheet-metal, forming simulation technology, and vehicle weight reduction technology.
Capital expenditure for the FY ended Mar. 31, 2012 was 12,698 million JPY.
Main capital investments included acquiring production facilities at the Saitama Plant, expanding the Tochigi Plant, enhancing Chinese operations, and increasing die production capacity.

Planned capital investment for Jefferson Southern Corporation in Georgia, USA includes a new facility (353 million yen from Jan. 2013 to Feb. 2013) and equipment for manufacturing auto parts including stamping/welding equipment (1,157 million yen from Mar. 2012 to Mar. 2014) and dies/jigs/tools (197 million yen).
The purpose of investments at Jefferson Southern Corporation in Georgia is increasing production capacity and dealing with model changes.

Planned capital investment for Jefferson Industries Corporation in Ohio, USA includes a new facility (429 million yen), stamping/welding equipment (1,052 million yen), and dies/jigs/tools (747 million yen) from Apr. 2012 to Mar. 2014.

Planned capital investment for Austin Tri-Hawk Automotive Inc. in Indiana, USA includes a new facility (35 million yen), stamping/welding equipment (1,151 million yen), and dies/jigs/tools (1,405 million yen) from Aug. 2011 to Jul. 2012.

Jefferson Industries Corporation, Jefferson Southern Corporation, Austin Tri-Hawk Automotive Inc., Jefferson Elora Corporation, G-KT do Brasil Ltda., Auto Parts Alliance (China) Ltd., Wuhan Auto Parts Alliance Co., Ltd., G-TEKT Europe Manufacturing Ltd., G-TEKT (Thailand) Co., Ltd., G-TEKT Eastern Co., Ltd., and G-TEKT India Private Ltd. hold nonexclusive licenses from G-TEKT for production, use, and sales rights regarding automotive parts, stamping dies, and jigs expertise.
Technology contract with Jefferson Industries Corporation runs from 2003.10.01 with automatic annual extension.
Technology contract with Auto Parts Alliance (China) Ltd. runs from 2011.07.01 to 2016.06.30.
Technology contract with G-TEKT India Private Ltd. runs from 2008.06.26 to 2013.06.25 with automatic annual extension.

facts: Joon Georgia will invest $317 million to produce parts in Bulloch County.
The supplier plans to hire 630 workers at a new factory in southeast Georgia.
The new factory will supply Hyundai Motor Group's first US electric vehicle plant that is under construction nearby.
The supplier will open shop roughly 30 miles (50 kilometers) west of the southeast Georgia site where Hyundai executives broke ground on the new EV plant two weeks ago.
Joon Georgia is "the first of many" expected to come to Georgia to supply the $5.5 billion Hyundai plant in Bryan County.
Hyundai plans to open its Georgia plant in 2025, producing up to 300,000 electric vehicles per year.
Joon Georgia is a subsidiary of Ajin USA, which supplies parts to other Hyundai plants.

Joon Georgia already operates a facility in Cusseta, Alabama, near the Georgia line that makes parts for Hyundai's plant in Montgomery, Alabama, as well as for Kia's auto plant in West Point, Georgia.
The Joon Georgia factory is expected to open near Statesboro in mid-2024.
Gov. Brian Kemp's office announced the investment in a news release.
Pat Wilson, commissioner of the Georgia Department of Economic Development, called the announcement "a landmark moment as we drive Georgia's automotive industry into the future."
State and local officials in Georgia lured Hyundai with tax breaks and incentives worth $1.8 billion, making it the state's largest economic development deal.
In addition to Hyundai hiring 8,100 workers, suppliers are expected to create thousands of additional jobs in the state.

revalyu Resources broke ground on its first advanced PET (polyethylene terephthalate) recycling facility in the United States in Statesboro, GA, on October 5, 2023.
