facts: Kumho Tire Co., Inc. is a major South Korean tire manufacturer founded in 1960 and headquartered in Seoul.
Kumho operates manufacturing plants in South Korea, China, Vietnam, and the United States (Macon, Georgia).
The Macon, Georgia facility opened in 2016 after a $200+ million investment.
The Macon, Georgia plant employs hundreds of American workers.
The Macon, Georgia plant primarily produces passenger car and light truck tires for the North American market.
Kumho is an independent, publicly traded company that is NOT a subsidiary of any other tire company.
In 2018, Chinese tire manufacturer Doublestar acquired a controlling stake (45%) in Kumho Tire.
Kumho operates dedicated R&D centers in South Korea and maintains its own manufacturing standards and engineering teams.
Kumho holds ISO 9001, ISO 14001, and IATF 16949 quality certifications.
Kumho has earned OEM fitments from BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Hyundai/Kia, Ford, and Chrysler/Dodge.

Kumho's popular tire lines include the Solus TA71 (Grand Touring All-Season), Crugen HP71 (SUV/Crossover Touring), Ecsta PA51 (Ultra-High Performance All-Season), and Road Venture AT52 (All-Terrain for Trucks/SUVs).
The Solus TA71 comes with a 70,000-mile treadwear warranty.
The Crugen HP71 carries a 65,000-mile warranty.
Kumho tires sold in the US market fully comply with Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards (FMVSS), specifically FMVSS 139 for new pneumatic radial tires.
Consumers can verify the manufacturing origin of a specific tire by looking at the DOT code on the sidewall.
Kumho is the second-largest tire maker in South Korea and ranks among the top 15 tire manufacturers globally.
source_title: Who Makes Kumho Tires and Are They Worth Buying in 2026 - Tire Advise

facts: Kumho Tire traces its origins to 1960 in Gwangju, South Korea, founded by Incheon Park of the Kumho Asiana Group.
Initially operated at a capacity of 20 tires per day under the original name Samyang Tire.
First tires were produced in 1961 to meet nascent domestic demand for vehicle components.
Exported to Thailand in 1965 totaling USD 7,700.
Secured the DOT mark from the U.S. Department of Transportation and KS quality standard in 1966.
Operational Songjeong plant began in 1971, with the Gwangju plant completed in 1974.
Developed Korea's first radial and aircraft tires in 1975.
Annual production surpassed 1 million units by 1976.
Received the grand prize at the 5th National Quality Management Award in 1979.
Achieved a USD 100 million export milestone trophy in 1980.
Received approval from General Motors for supplying tires in the 1980s.
Established the Gwangju Central Research Center in 1981 to strengthen domestic R&D capabilities.
Merged with Kumho Industry Co., Ltd. in 1984, rebranding as Kumho Co., Ltd.
Completed the Gokseong Plant proving ground in 1989.
Established the Kumho America Technical Center (KATC) in Akron, Ohio in 1990 as its first overseas R&D facility.
Ranked among the world's top 10 tire manufacturers by 1992 with cumulative production exceeding 100 million tires.
Completed the Gokseong Plant and Proving Ground in 1990 to enhance testing capabilities.
Initiated research into racing tires in 1990, positioning itself as a motorsport leader by the late 1990s.
Developed the world's fourth run-flat tire technology in 1999.
Received ISO 9001 certification in 1994 and ISO 14001 in 1996.
Renamed to Kumho Tire Co., Ltd. in 1996.
Completed the Tianjin Plant in China in 2006 to serve Asian demand.
Completed the Changchun Plant in China in 2007.
Started operations at Nanjing's truck and bus radial (TBR) facility.
Opened a factory in Vietnam in 2008 to diversify production bases.
Supplied tires for the Korea Super Prix F3 in 2000 and became the official supplier for the F3 Euro series in 2003.
Captured 40% of the global F3 tire market by the end of the decade through specialized racing compounds.
Pioneered the world's first 32-inch ultra-high-performance (UHP) tires in 2006 for luxury vehicles.
Provided original equipment supply to Ford in 2008, integrating advanced materials for handling and durability.
Entered a debt workout program in late 2009 amid financial distress of parent Kumho Asiana Group.
By 2010, creditors including the Korea Development Bank converted portions of debt to equity, securing a combined 42% stake.
Creditors injected approximately 3.9 trillion won in additional support over subsequent years to prevent insolvency.
Balance sheet deteriorated by 2017 due to weak sales and high debt servicing.
September 2017: Creditor consortium ousted Kumho Asiana's influence and assumed direct management.
December 29, 2017: Formal separation from the Kumho Asiana Group occurred as part of creditor-directed reforms.
March 2018: Qingdao Doublestar Co. acquired a controlling stake in Kumho Tire for $597 million.
Under Doublestar's ownership via Xingwei Korea (holding about 45% as of mid-2025), the company focused on cost reductions and supply chain integration with the parent.
Majority-owned by Xingwei Korea Company Limited, a South Korean subsidiary of China's Qingdao Doublestar Group, holding approximately 45% of shares as of recent filings.
Other significant shareholders include IBK Asset Management Co., Ltd. (7.43%), The Korea Development Bank (7.43%), Woori Financial Group (3.95%), and the Government of South Korea (1.12%).
Governance is overseen by a Board of Directors comprising seven members, including four independent directors.
ESG Management Committee established in February 2023 as an independent board subcommittee chaired by CEO Jeong Il-taek.
Operates eight manufacturing facilities across four countries, primarily producing passenger car (PC) tires.
South Korean facilities include the Gwangju Plant (PC, T&B, combat plane tires), Goksung Plant (PC, T&B), and Pyungtaek Plant (PC).
Chinese facilities include the Nanjing Plant (PC, T&B), Tianjin Plant (PC), and Changchun Plant (PC).
Vietnam facility in Binh Duong Province produces PC tires.
United States facility is the Georgia Plant in Macon, Georgia, producing PC tires and operational since 2016.
Gwangju plant suffered a major fire on May 17, 2025, suspending operations due to damage to rubber compounding and blending lines.
Production at Gwangju partially resumed in November 2025, with capacity restored to approximately 10,000 tires daily by March 2026.
Global production capacity reached over 62 million tire units annually following expansions at Vietnam and Georgia facilities completed in 2023.
This marked an increase from 55-57 million units prior to those projects.
Gwangju plant has an annual capacity of 12 million tires, accounting for approximately 20% of total output.
Vietnam plant was expanded to 12 million tires per year by mid-2025.
Macon, Georgia plant is rated at 4 million tires annually.
November 2024: Announced a further $300 million investment to expand the Vietnam facility by 36%, adding 4.5 million units of passenger car and light truck tire capacity.
Modifications at the China plant aim to allocate 1.4 million units for new-energy vehicle tires by 2025.
Global workforce totaled 10,240 employees as of the 2024 sustainability report.
Georgia plant employs over 380 workers focused on tire production.
North American headquarters supports an additional 50 staff for regional operations and sales.
Office workers' unions in South Korea affiliated in April 2024 amid efforts to align compensation with welfare benefits.
Core product lines include the Ecsta brand (high-performance/UHP), Solus series (grand touring/all-season), Road Venture range (SUV/light truck), and Marshal brand (value-oriented).
Commercial offerings include the KRS03 all-position rib tire and SmartWay certified KLD11e drive axle tire for medium-duty trucks.
Maintains a global R&D network including its Central Research and Development Center in Yongin, South Korea, plus facilities in China, the United States, and Germany.
EV tire development is a core priority, with the 'EnnoV' brand launched in March 2024 for electric mobility innovations.
In 2022, prioritized EV tire development among 257 projects, completing 171 focused on reducing rolling resistance and improving durability for heavier EV loads.
Aims for 100% sustainable materials by 2045, incorporating R&D into raw material substitutions and end-of-life tire management.
European R&D center in Frankfurt develops high-performance tires for diverse driving conditions and original equipment applications.
Introduced a digital twin tire development system in November 2024 to accelerate design and lower costs through virtual simulations.
2023: Introduced the Solus TA51a grand touring all-season tire engineered for coupes, sedans, crossovers, and SUVs.
2024: Launched Ecsta Sport All-Season PA71 and Ecsta Sport Summer PS72 tires at the SEMA show in November.
August 2025: Announced the Road Venture RT rugged-terrain light truck tire, available from September in 27 sizes for 17- to 22-inch rims.
"Volume" and "Origami" conceptual tire designs earned recognition at the 2025 U.S. International Design Excellence Awards for speculative approaches to structure and sustainability.
2010 revenue was 2.702 trillion KRW, reflecting a 42.6% year-over-year increase after returning to operating profitability.
Revenue grew to 4.04 trillion KRW with net income of 157.8 billion KRW in 2023.
Preliminary 2024 revenue reached 4.53 trillion KRW with net income of 324.9 billion KRW.
source_title: Kumho Tire — Grokipedia

facts: Kumho Tire USA Inc. is constructing a new tire manufacturing facility in Macon-Bibb County, Georgia.
The first phase of the new Macon-Bibb County plant requires a $410 million investment.
The first phase will produce four million passenger and light truck tires annually.
The Macon-Bibb County plant is expected to open in January 2016.
Initially, the Georgia plant will produce original equipment (OE) tires, with replacement units to follow later.
Production at the new facility will help resolve fill rate issues caused by increased OE demand and pending U.S. duties on China-produced tires.
Kumho moved its tire molds from China to South Korea in preparation for operations at the Georgia plant.
Depending on marketplace conditions, the Macon-Bibb County plant could undergo a second stage of expansion.
Harry Choi serves as president of Kumho Tire USA Inc. and outlined the company's five-year business plan targeting 2018.
John Hagan joined Kumho as national sales vice president in late 2013 to strengthen the sales organization.
Kumho aims to sell 10 million tire units locally in the U.S. and gain a 3.5% market share by 2018.
The company announced six new tires set for rollout by mid-2016, including the Ecsta V720, Ecsta 4X II, Solus TA71, Solus TA11, Road Venture AT51, and Crugen Premium KL33.
Kumho established a partnership with the NBA as its "Official Tire Sponsor" to raise brand awareness and support sales.
The company introduced an Apple iOS app for dealers and consumers to browse products, view details, and access training materials.
Kumho is working to eliminate spot deals and establish a stable, consistent pricing policy for its dealer network.
The tiremaker refreshed its consumer tire naming system to a four-character format identifying performance/season and grade.

facts: The US Tire Manufacturers Association (USTMA) represents 13 tire manufacturers in the US with annual sales of more than $27 million.
The USTMA operates 57 locations across 17 states.
Yong-Sik Shin of Kumho Tire USA Inc. was elected to the USTMA board of directors.
Pietro Berardi of Pirelli Tire North America Inc. was elected to the USTMA board of directors.
The USTMA extended Board Chair Steve McClellan's term through October 2021.
Steve McClellan serves as president of Americas for Goodyear.
USTMA CEO Anne Forristall Luke stated the board's decision to ask McClellan to continue as chair speaks to confidence in his strong leadership during unpredictable times.

Anne Forristall Luke stated that the elections of Berardi and Shin augment an exceptionally talented and strategic board to help steer the industry through a robust economic recovery and resilient future.
Shin has been CEO of the American Business Division for South Korea's Kumho Tire Co. Inc. since September 2019.
Shin previously served as the company's head of global sales.
Shin leads four Kumho subsidiaries: Kumho Tire USA Inc., Kumho Tire Canada, Kumho Tire Latin America, and the Kumho Tire plant in Georgia from a sales and overall operations perspective.
Berardi joined Pirelli in March following his work as vice president at the Alliance of Renault, Nissan and Mitsubishi.
At the Alliance of Renault, Nissan and Mitsubishi, Berardi managed global commercial operations and strategy for the parts and services business.
McClellan stated he is honored to continue his role as chair of USTMA's board, particularly now.
The tire industry is a $148 billion (€125 billion) industry that helps power the US manufacturing economy.
It is critical for the industry to maintain a strong and unified voice on reopening the American economy and on policy issues affecting nearly 100,000 women and men who work for member companies.
source_title: USTMA adds board members, asks chair to remain in place | European Rubber Journal

facts: The United States tire market size reached 363.1 Million Units in 2025.
IMARC Group expects the US tire market to reach 408.8 Million Units by 2034, exhibiting a growth rate (CAGR) of 1.32% during 2026-2034.
An expansion in the automotive industry and the rising demand for electric vehicles (EVs) are primarily driving tire market growth in the United States.

Rising environmental consciousness coupled with extended support by the U.S. Federal Government to promote eco-friendly and sustainable manufacturing techniques in tire production processes is contributing to market growth.
Replacement tire demand is experiencing tremendous growth, especially in the passenger vehicle segment, due to high car ownership levels and cars being held for longer periods of time.

In March 2024, Bridgestone created a high-performance lunar rover tire through the use of Air Free technology, with applications for greater durability and sustainability in global and U.S. tire marketplaces.

In January 2025, Goodyear revealed its SightLine smart tire with integrated sensors to sense rain and ice, allowing for earlier automatic emergency braking to enhance vehicle safety on low-friction roads.
Smart tires improve vehicle performance, safety, and fuel efficiency, particularly in commercial fleets and electric vehicles.

Manufacturers are creating tires with reduced rolling resistance, recycled content, and longer life due to increasing pressure towards environmental sustainability and federal/state regulatory objectives for energy efficiency and emissions reduction.
E-commerce and online retail platforms are revolutionizing tire purchasing through convenience, extensive choice, competitive prices, and access to customer feedback.
Radial tires are the market leaders in the United States due to their better structure design, performance, longevity, traction, fuel economy, and heat dissipation.

Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) are a dominant segment of the tire industry, with automakers seeking customized tire solutions compatible with particular model specifications to enhance vehicle performance and fuel efficiency.
The growth in electric vehicles (EVs) is impacting OEM demand, with manufacturers specifically seeking tires that feature low rolling resistance and quiet running.
Passenger cars hold the largest proportion in the United States tire market due to high vehicle ownership ratios and continuous urbanization.
Growing interest in electric and hybrid cars is driving the creation of dedicated passenger car tires designed to ensure quiet running and efficiency.
Offline distribution maintains dominance through physical dealerships, auto service workshops, and specialty shops that provide expert installation, alignment, and servicing.
All-season tires are the most popular seasonal option in America due to their versatility, effective performance in summer and winter, and low maintenance requirements.

The South region has the highest regional percentage of the United States tire market, fueled by high vehicle density, large road networks, and a favorable climate that lessens the need for seasonal tire changes.
Texas, Florida, and Georgia are among the contributing states to overall volume due to their size, extensive metropolitan areas, and prevalent culture of vehicle ownership.
Due to relatively stable weather conditions, South customers value durability, heat tolerance, and fuel efficiency more than winter features.
The expanding logistics and transportation industry in the South underpins demand for commercial and fleet tire usage.

Key players in the competitive landscape include Michelin North America, Inc., Bridgestone Americas, Inc., The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company, Continental AG, Sumitomo Rubber Industries, Ltd., Kumho Tire USA, Inc., Hankook Tire Co., Ltd., Toyo Tire U.S.A. Corp., Pirelli Tire S.p.A., and Yokohama Tire Corporation.
The sudden outbreak of the COVID-19 pandemic led to stringent lockdown regulations across the nation, resulting in the temporary closure of numerous tire manufacturing units.
source_title: US Tire Market Size, Share, Trends & Forecast to 2034

facts: Kyungshin America Corp. has selected the 20 million-square-foot Savannah Gateway Industrial Hub for the location of its new distribution center in Rincon, Ga.
The South Korea-based automotive part supplier will open a 136,000-square-foot facility in February as part of its production expansion.
The Broe Group, together with its transportation affiliate OmniTRAX and Effingham County Industrial Development Authority, is the owner of the 2,600-acre multimodal logistics park.
Kyungshin’s new facility will import and distribute automotive components to Hyundai Motor Group’s $7.5 billion electric vehicle plant in Savannah.
The company is a certified Hyundai supplier that produces wire harnesses for automotive companies.
The move will create approximately 70 new jobs.
The move will generate more than $22 million investments in Effingham County, according to the Effingham Herald.
Savannah Gateway Industrial Hub offers various site configurations for lease or purchase and build-to-suit options.
The industrial campus is part of the Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development Program.
The industrial campus provides easy access to the Port of Savannah, CSXT and Norfolk Southern Railroads systems, and major transportation corridors including interstates 95, 16, and U.S. Highway 21.
The master-planned industrial park currently has 33 buildings in planning stages or under construction.
Colliers Principals Cliff Dales and David Sink are in charge of leasing at the hub.
Tenants at Savannah Gateway Industrial Hub include DHL, Quantix, Bissell Homecare, Inc., and Lion Brand Yarns.
Savannah Gateway Industrial Hub has been in development since 2016, when Effingham County Industrial Development Authority selected OmniTrax as its development partner for the megaproject.
In 2019, a commitment from A&R Logistics (now rebranded as Quantix) sparked the groundbreaking on the industrial park’s first building, totaling 1 million square feet.
At the time of the 2019 groundbreaking, Quantix’s build-to-suit lease amounted to 610,000 square feet over 12 years.
In 2021, the Broe Group sold the 998,472-square-foot building at 1004 Gateway Parkway to Walton Street Capital.
In late 2022, TerraCap Management LLC acquired Buildings 1H and 1F in a $116 million deal.

facts: Lear Corp. held its Q4 earnings call highlighting operational strength and strategic progress amid softer auto production headwinds.
Management highlighted record net performance savings, strong cash generation, a rich pipeline of new business in seating and thermal comfort, and accelerating digital and automation initiatives.
Executives expressed confidence in margin improvement and structural earnings power heading into 2026.
Lear closed the year with full-year revenue of $23.3 billion and core operating earnings of $1.1 billion, representing a 4.6% margin on net sales.
Adjusted earnings per share came in at $12.80, a 1% increase versus 2024 and the fifth consecutive year of EPS growth.
The company continued to expand earnings through operational discipline and efficiency measures rather than relying solely on volume growth.
Fourth-quarter sales were up 5% year over year.
Adjusted EPS rose to $3.41 from $2.94 a year earlier, an increase of roughly 16%.
Management credited the Q4 improvement largely to share repurchases and a lower tax rate.
Lear delivered about $1.1 billion of operating cash flow and $527 million of free cash flow for 2025.
The company repurchased $325 million of stock, surpassing its $250 million target, and returned nearly $500 million to shareholders when dividends are included.
Management is targeting free cash flow conversion above 80% and plans more than $300 million of share repurchases in 2026.
Lear delivered a record $195 million in net performance savings, 56% above its original $125 million target.
These efficiencies added roughly 60 basis points to Seating margins and about 110 basis points to E-Systems margins.
The company’s “Idea by Lear” initiatives alone generated $70 million in savings, while restructuring efforts contributed another $85 million.
Lear secured the largest seating conquest in the company’s history: complete seats for General Motors’ full-size SUVs and pickup trucks at Orion, with launch targeted for 2027.
On the E-Systems side, the company booked more than $1.4 billion in awards during 2025, marking its strongest performance in over a decade.
These new business wins deepen Lear’s relationships with key OEMs and enhance visibility into medium-term revenue.
Thermal comfort emerged as a strategic bright spot with 33 thermal comfort awards expected to generate about $170 million in average annual revenue at peak.
Nine thermal comfort programs are already in production, with 14 additional launches slated for 2026.
The company emphasized that its acquisitions of Kongsberg and IGB have created a vertically integrated, full-solution offering in thermal comfort systems.
Lear’s digital transformation continues to scale, with the Palantir Foundry platform growing to more than 17,000 users and over 300 custom applications across the business.
A cycle-time deviation tool is delivering 3%–5% efficiency gains, translating into roughly $10 million in savings in 2025 and an expected $15 million in 2026.
Additionally, a tariff-tracking solution helped the company recover nearly all tariff-related costs.
Lear’s backlog provides a buffer against a sluggish production backdrop, with a two-year sales backlog of $1.325 billion.
The backlog includes $725 million of expected net new business in 2027—about $465 million in Seating and $260 million in E-Systems.
For 2026, Lear expects roughly $60 million in net new business.

Coupled with 2026 revenue guidance of $23.2–$24.0 billion (about 2% growth at the midpoint) and a projected mid-single-digit increase in core operating earnings, the backlog supports a cautiously constructive outlook.
E-Systems results reflected tangible volume and mix pressures, with full-year segment sales declining 2% to $6.0 billion and down about 5% excluding FX and other items.
Adjusted earnings for E-Systems fell to $293 million, or 4.9% of sales, from $310 million and a 5.1% margin in 2024.
Management cited lower volumes on GM EV platforms, the wind-down of Ford Escape/Corsair programs and discontinued product lines as key headwinds for E-Systems.
E-Systems also faces a roughly $140 million headwind in 2026 from specific roll-offs.
The core Seating business delivered essentially flat full-year sales of $17.3 billion, up just 0.4% year over year, while adjusted earnings declined 1%.
The Seating drag came primarily from weaker volumes and unfavorable mix on several programs, notably with Jaguar Land Rover and Mercedes.
Net performance savings helped cushion the impact on Seating, but results highlight that market-leading supply positions are not immune to model shifts and regional demand softness.
Across the enterprise, Lear absorbed roughly an $800 million drag from unfavorable volume and mix during the year.
This drag was driven by reductions in GM EV volumes, program changeovers and lower volumes on certain European and legacy platforms.
These factors constrain top-line momentum and explain why management is leaning heavily on productivity, restructuring and automation to deliver earnings growth in a flattish volume environment.
Lear’s 2026 guidance builds in conservative industry assumptions, expecting global auto production, on a sales-weighted basis, to be down about 1%.
Fourth-quarter production trends were already soft, with North America flat, Europe down 2% and China up 3%.
This backdrop suggests limited tailwind from volumes in the near term, pushing Lear to focus on content growth, new business wins and cost actions to support earnings.
While management reaffirmed its ambition to reach $1 billion in thermal comfort revenue and a 10% EBIT margin, it acknowledged that the original January 2027 timing has slipped.
The delay reflects slower-than-expected ramp-up and shifting program timelines rather than a change in the underlying opportunity.
Despite strong full-year cash metrics, Lear’s quarterly numbers showed working capital-driven volatility.
Fourth-quarter operating cash flow was $476 million, down from $681 million in the same period last year, primarily due to timing effects in working capital.
Management framed this as normal variability rather than a structural issue, but it is a reminder for investors that quarterly cash flows can be lumpy even when the annual picture is solid.
Lear is stepping up restructuring to align its footprint with demand and to fund automation.
The 2026 outlook includes about $175 million in restructuring costs tied to footprint rationalization and automation investments, which will weigh on near-term earnings.
However, these actions are expected to support longer-term efficiency and margin improvement, consistent with the strong net performance record the company has already demonstrated.
The company continues to streamline its workforce but is also dealing with the impact of joint venture consolidation and shifting production schedules.
In 2025, Lear reduced hourly headcount by 7,000 and by 22,000 over the last two years.
Overall hourly staffing remained slightly higher than projected due to the consolidation of two Chinese joint ventures and schedule changes.
This dynamic underscores the operational complexity of managing global capacity in a changing production landscape.
For 2026, Lear guided revenue to a range of $23.2–$24.0 billion, implying about 2% growth at the midpoint.
Core operating earnings are projected between $1.03 billion and $1.20 billion, with the midpoint suggesting around 5% year-over-year growth.
Company-adjusted margins are expected to improve by about 10 basis points, with Seating at roughly 6.5% and E-Systems near 5.0%.
Net performance is forecast to add about 40 basis points to Seating margins and 80 basis points to E-Systems margins.
Lear plans about $660 million in capital spending, $1.2–$1.3 billion of operating cash flow and around $600 million of free cash flow at the midpoint for 2026.
The company intends to repurchase at least $300 million of shares while maintaining roughly $3 billion of liquidity and a cost of debt under 4%.
Its two-year backlog of $1.325 billion, including modest net new business in 2026 and a stronger $725 million contribution in 2027, supports the medium-term growth narrative.
In summary, Lear’s earnings call combined a cautiously optimistic view of the near term with a confident stance on long-term value creation.

While industry volumes and program-specific roll-offs present clear challenges, the company’s record operational savings, robust cash generation, major new business awards and expanding digital and thermal comfort capabilities provide a solid foundation.
For investors, the story is less about explosive top-line growth and more about disciplined execution, margin resilience and consistent capital returns in a choppy auto cycle.
source_title: Lear Corp. Earnings Call Highlights Margin-Focused Strategy - TipRanks.com

facts: Unionized workers walked off the job at Lear Corp’s seat plant.
Unifor, a worker’s union representing workers at GM’s Oshawa, Canada plant, staged the latest measure in its campaign to keep operations running through September 2020.
200 workers at Lear Corp, a seat plant in Whitby, Ontario, just walked off the job in response to GM’s decision to close Oshawa Assembly.
According to a Reuters report, the workers left the site and would not return for their 6:30 AM to 2:30 PM shift.
Lear Corp is just-in-time seat supplier for the GM plant.
The GM plant manufactures the Chevrolet Impala and Cadillac XTS, as well as the last-generation Chevrolet Silverado and GMC Sierra trucks.
Under a just-in-time strategy, GM works with local companies like Lear to supply parts for their vehicles as they need them, rather than keeping a significant stockpile on site.
The idea is to work them into the production schedule to keep things running smoothly.
At the moment, Lear employs 350 unionized workers at its plant.
Unifor also represents 2,600 assembly line workers at the GM Oshawa plant, as well as 1,800 workers employed with GM suppliers.

Unifor president Jerry Dias said of General Motors’ decision to close Oshawa by the end of 2019, “We demand that [the Canadian] government act to protect these workers who stand to lose everything, and work with Unifor and GM to find a solution for the Oshawa assembly plant.”
It’s unclear what effect this will ultimately have on GM’s vehicle production, or whether the walkout would continue past Friday.
Unifor Local 222 president Colin James said this was about more than just the Oshawa plant.
“The message that we’re sending is that it’s not just about General Motors’ workers. All the suppliers, including Lear Whitby, are impacted by a potential closure.”
In addition to its Canadian plant closure, GM also plans to close four more plants in the United States.
source_title: 200 Workers At GM Oshawa Supplier Lear Corp Walk Off The Job - TFLcar

International trade negotiations are creating significant uncertainty that is affecting production volumes and product mix.

Sales in both the Seating and E-Systems segments declined by 7% compared to the previous year.

facts: Lehigh Technologies is a specialty materials company that manufactures micronized rubber powders (MRP).
Based in Atlanta, Georgia, Lehigh Technologies operates a MRP manufacturing plant at its headquarters.
The Atlanta facility has an annual production capacity of more than 100 million pounds.
Lehigh also operates a MRP research and development facility, its Application & Development Center (ADC).
Lehigh has been manufacturing MRP at its commercial plant since 2007.
In 2010, Lehigh was selected as a Technology Pioneer by the World Economic Forum (WEF).
In June 2011, Lehigh announced that more than 100 million tires on the road had been manufactured using the company's MRP.
In 2014, the company announced that more than 250 million tires produced to date had incorporated MRP.
In October 2017, Lehigh Technologies was acquired by Michelin as part of Michelin's "4R" circular economy strategy.

In November 2010, Lehigh expanded its in-house capabilities with the launch of the Application & Development Center (ADC), a dedicated research and development facility staffed by a team of industry professionals.

The stated mission of the ADC is to develop new applications for MRP and accelerate adoption of existing MRP applications by improving understanding of MRP chemistry, providing expert advice for new material formulations that incorporate MRP, and developing new materials based on MRP.
In January 2014, Lehigh announced an expansion of its ADC that includes the hiring of new technical staff and investment in new mixing and analytical equipment.
According to Lehigh, this expansion improves the ADC's ability to develop functional materials based on MRP.
In addition to its production facility in Atlanta, Lehigh has developed a network of partners and distributors.
Lehigh partnered with Connell Brothers Corporation (CBC), the largest distributor of specialty chemicals and ingredients in Asia-Pacific, to market its MRP in Japan.

In 2014, Lehigh announced an expanded partnership with CBC, aimed at broadening industry outreach, supply chain efficiency and technical support in Japan, as well as introducing MRP to South Korea and Southeast Asia.

In September 2014, Lehigh announced the closing of an $8 million fundraise with JSR Corporation, a $4 billion specialty chemicals company based in Japan, to support the company's geographic expansion and technology roadmap for MRP in the region.
In October 2011, Lehigh expanded into Brazil with the appointment of a country manager.
In December 2012, Lehigh announced a partnership with Andes Chemical Corporation to market MRP in Central America.
In September 2012, Lehigh partnered with HERA Holding, a Spanish waste-to-resource company, to market MRP to the tire, consumer, and industrial plastics and coatings industries in Europe.
In 2018, Lehigh and Hera announced the completion and startup of a manufacturing facility in Spain to supply EMEA markets.
Since 2008, Lehigh has raised more than $50 million from investors that include Kleiner Perkins Caufield & Byers, Index Ventures, Leaf Clean Energy and NGP Energy Technology Partners.
Lehigh has commercialized a proprietary cryogenic turbo mill technology that transforms end-of-life tires and post-industrial rubber materials into micronized rubber powders.
Lehigh produces MRP from vulcanized elastomeric material, most often end-of-life tire material.
MRP can also be produced from post-industrial nitrile rubber, ethylene propylene diene monomer (EPDM), butyl, and natural rubber compounds.
Lehigh's MRPs are available in two product lines, PolyDyne and MicroDyne.
Industries incorporating MRP into their products include tire, automotive, construction, industrial components and consumer products.
It is also used as an additive in tires, plastics, asphalt, coatings, and sealants.
MRP can be incorporated into prime or recycled grade polypropylene (PP), high-density polyethylene (HDPE) and nylons.
The incorporation of MRPs in thermoplastic elastomers (TPE) and thermoplastic vulcanizates (TPV) makes it a feasible ingredient for automotive and building and construction applications.
The particle size distributions of Lehigh's MRP typically range from 830μm (20 mesh) to 50μm (300 mesh).
Its MRP is free of foreign particulates, such as metal and fiber.
Lehigh claims that its cryogenic turbo mill technology represents an evolution over previous post-manufactured rubber technologies.
The most common rubber processing technology converts end-of-life tire and post-industrial rubber material into rubber chips that are typically one inch or larger.
A second-generation processing technology converts end-of-life tire and rubber material into crumb rubber, also known as ground tire rubber (GTR).
GTR typically comprises chips between one inch and 30 mesh in size, with the associated fiber and steel mostly removed.
Lehigh markets its micrometer-scale MRP as a third-generation rubber processing technology.
Lehigh's MRP is used as a compound extender to replace natural rubber and synthetic polymers, as well as to act as a process aid in material production.
Lehigh claims that because it replaces commodity-priced rubber- and oil-based feedstocks, MRP can reduce formulation costs.
The company has asserted that MRP can offer up to 50 percent cost savings over virgin raw materials in some cases.
Because it is derived from end-of-life materials, MRP can also improve the sustainability, and in some cases the performance, of the compounds in which it is used.
Lehigh has stated that the smaller particle sizes of MRP increase the impact strength of certain plastic compositions.
source_title: Lehigh Technologies - Wikipedia

facts: Lehigh Technologies Inc. is a Georgia, USA-based producer of Micronised Rubber Powders (MRP) from end-of-life tyres and post-industrial rubber.

facts: Michelin acquired Lehigh Technologies, a specialty materials company based in Tucker, Ga., near Atlanta.
The acquisition was completed on Oct. 13.
Lehigh Technologies uses patented cryogenic turbo mill technology to transform rubber from end-of-life tires and industrial goods into materials for new tires and other products.
This process reduces the amount of raw materials initially needed, such as elastomers and fillers from oil- and rubber-based sources.
Lehigh Technologies employs about 100 people.
The firm produces highly engineered, versatile raw materials called micronized rubber powder (MRP).
MRP is a low-cost, high-performance, sustainable material that substitutes for other oil- and rubber-based materials used in manufacturing tires, plastics, asphalt and construction materials.
Its customers include some of the largest tire companies in the world, as well as companies in construction materials, asphalt modification and other markets.

Pete Selleck, chairman and president of Michelin North America, stated that the acquisition demonstrates a strategic intent to achieve safer and more sustainable mobility using high-technology recycled materials without compromising safety or performance.

Christophe Rahier, senior vice president of strategic planning materials for the Michelin Group, noted that the acquisition extends Michelin's materials expertise beyond tires to foster advanced rubber recycled materials in tire and non-tire industries.

Alan Barton, CEO of Lehigh Technologies, said the deal provides an opportunity to continue growth, expand capabilities, execute a global growth strategy, and pursue new market opportunities with full backing from Michelin.
Michelin North America is headquartered in Greenville, S.C., employs about 22,700 people, and operates 19 major manufacturing plants.
Michelin designs, manufactures and sells tires for every type of vehicle, including airplanes, automobiles, bicycles, earthmovers, farm equipment, heavy-duty trucks and motorcycles.
source_title: Michelin Acquires Lehigh Technologies, a Specialty Materials Company | www.inknowvation.com

facts: Lehigh Technologies owns the world’s largest MRP manufacturing plant.
The plant is located just outside of Atlanta in Tucker, Georgia.
The Tucker, Georgia facility has an annual production capacity of 140 million pounds.
Lehigh Technologies operates an Application and Development Center (ADC).
The ADC routinely tests MRP from different end-of-life rubber materials in model formulations to ensure product performance meets market and customer expectations.
Manufacturing lines at the facility are managed using a state-of-the-art process control system with a “human machine interface” (HMI).
The HMI system allows production engineers to control dozens of important process parameters simultaneously.
Lehigh Technologies is ISO9001 and ISO14001 certified.

Continuous investment in equipment and systems, on-going safety training, and meeting environmental, health and safety (EHS) common standards are core elements of the company's production management system.

facts: LINDE + WIEMANN drives innovations in structural assemblies for the automotive industry by combining profiling, stamping, joining and gluing technology.
The German site in Hagenbach produces ready-to-install assemblies of high-strength steel for global vehicle manufacturers and automotive suppliers.
Automated 3D measurement technology solutions from ZEISS support the tier-1 supplier throughout the entire manufacturing process.
Three integrated ScanBox systems scan measuring data and submit it directly to upstream production using ZEISS PiWeb.
Deviations are detected quickly and appropriate corrective measures are initiated.
LINDE + WIEMANN regularly shares its measuring data with customers.
Peter Kluge is the Head of Quality Management at LINDE + WIEMANN.
Michael Kray is the Quality Manager at LINDE + WIEMANN.
ATOS sensors digitize the entire surface of structural components via fringe projection within seconds.
ZEISS INSPECT software displays deviations between actual 3D coordinates and CAD data through a color comparison.
The low set-up time of the ScanBox system enables inspection of other structurally relevant components within just a few minutes.

facts: The Canadian automotive company Magna announced an investment of more than 150 million dollars and the generation of more than 3,000 jobs in Coahuila.
Coahuila has consolidated as the main production bastion for Magna, having almost half of its national operations in the entity.
The announcement resulted from a meeting between Governor Miguel Riquelme Solis and his delegation with Magna executives during an economic promotion tour in Canada.
Executives stated that the place for the expansion is still being analyzed, with the Laguna Region being the tentative location due to its recent industrial growth.
The new investment will exceed previous job expectations for all of Magna's plants in Coahuila, increasing employment from more than 14,000 currently to around 17,400 with the new positions.
Governor Miguel Riquelme reiterated his government's support for new projects by Magna and other companies to grow employment and competitiveness for thousands of families in Coahuila.

The governor highlighted that economic development in Coahuila is possible due to skilled labor, technical and higher-level schools, high schooling and worker specialization, as well as road, railway, and airport infrastructure.

Jaime Guerra Perez, head of the Ministry of Economy and Tourism of Coahuila (SEDECT), stated that there are 28 Canadian companies with 47 plants in the automotive, mining, chemical industry, and metal-mechanic manufacturing sectors mainly in the state.
Jaime Guerra Perez noted that Magna is the main Canadian investor in Coahuila, where it has 14 plants out of a total of 32 across all of Mexico.
The Secretary of Economy added that in addition to the announced 150 million dollars, the company is fulfilling more studies to bring more investment to the state, showing confidence in the entity.
Magna is analyzing the possibility of creating an engineering center, which could be located in Coahuila.
Headquartered in Aurora, Ontario, Magna is the largest manufacturer of components for automobiles in Canada and owns Magna Steyr, a manufacturer of automobiles located in Steyr, Austria.

Globally, Magna operates as an automotive supplier with more than 335 manufacturing operations and 102 product development, engineering, and sales centers in 29 countries, employing more than 170,000 people.
In North America, the auto-parts company operates 137 manufacturing plants: 32 in Mexico, 50 in Canada, and 55 in the United States.
Magna has 25 engineering, product development, or sales centers in North America: 13 in the United States, 11 in Canada, and one in Mexico.
Magna was established in Ramos Arizpe in 1999 and currently employs more than 14,000 people across its 14 factories that produce a wide range of automotive components for the world.
The company offers complete vehicle engineering services, with product capabilities including body, chassis, exterior, seats, power train, active driver assistance, vision, locking systems, and roof.
Magna also has electronic and software capabilities in many of these areas.
Although it supplies parts to major OEMs, the majority of its sales are to six customers: General Motors, Ford, Fiat Chrysler Automobiles, Daimler, BMW, and Volkswagen.
The company's origins trace back to Frank Stronach, an Austrian who migrated to Canada in 1954 with 200 dollars.
Frank Stronach turned a common workshop of paint and parts manufacturing in Toronto into one of the largest suppliers of automotive equipment in the world.
Stronach was born on September 6th, 1932, in Weiz, southern Austria, left school at age 14, and migrated to Canada with only apprenticeship training in a tool-making workshop.
After changing his name and doing different jobs, he launched his own company, Multimatic, in 1957, initially as the only employee.
In 1967, he merged Multimatic with another company to register Magna Electronics Corp on the stock exchange, which became Magna International in 1973.
By the end of the 1970s, sales of control panels and brake systems exceeded 150 million dollars.

A week-long economic promotion tour in Canada aimed to attract investments to Coahuila, generate well-paid jobs, diversify the industry, increase competitiveness, and provide development opportunities.

The promotional meetings highlighted Coahuila's competitive advantages, including its strategic position, security, educational level, qualified workforce, leader status in the automotive sector, and 68 industrial parks.

The state's potential in manufacturing and clean energy, infrastructure development, connectivity, and natural resources in mining, agriculture, livestock, milk, brewing, and wine were also exposed to investors.
During the tour, visits and interviews with Canadian companies in Toronto and Montreal led to agreements for an immediate visit to Coahuila's soil by businessmen.

The Coahuila delegation included Governor Miguel Angel Riquelme, mayors of Saltillo (Manolo Jimenez), Monclova (Alfredo Paredes Lopez), Frontera (Florencio Siller Linaje), and CANACINTRA Centro Region president Rolando Rivero.
Achievements from the tour included investment announcements, expansion projects, commitments, trade agreements, and educational/training partnerships.

The delegation met with universities and businessmen from the Auto-parts Manufacturers Association, Mold Manufacturers Association of Canada, Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan, Samuel Associated Tube Group, Centennial College, Linamar, Conestoga College, The Woodbridge Group, Canimex Group, RDV Soucy International, Espacio por la vida, Montreal Metropolitan Chamber of Commerce, Commission for Environmental Cooperation, and Trans-Pro Logistics.

Work meetings were held with Aero Montreal, an aerospace sector cluster, to reach joint projects, highlighting Coahuila's existing aerospace companies in engineering service, parts machining, security systems, heat treatment of metals, harnesses, and connectors.
source_title: Magna to invest 150 million dollars and strength relations between Coahuila and Canada

facts: German automotive supplier MöllerTech announced it will invest $46.3 million to build a manufacturing facility in Bibb County.
The project will create 222 jobs in Alabama’s auto sector.
Governor Robert Bentley stated that attracting investment and new jobs to rural counties is a key priority in Alabama's economic development strategy.
Governor Robert Bentley noted that MöllerTech’s Bibb County facility represents the type of project the state has been targeting.
Governor Robert Bentley mentioned new tools are available to help rural counties overcome economic development obstacles.
MöllerTech plans to construct a 150,000-square-foot facility in the Scott G. Davis Industrial Park.
The site is located near Interstate 20/59 and close to the Woodstock community in northern Bibb County.
The facility will manufacture interior parts for Mercedes-Benz’s next-generation SUVs.
These SUVs will be produced at Mercedes-Benz’s Alabama assembly plant following a $1.3 billion expansion.
MöllerTech North American President and CEO Steve Jordan said the Bibb County facility will serve as the company’s flagship U.S. manufacturing facility.
The facility will incorporate new ideas and best practices from other operations in the company’s global manufacturing footprint.
After visiting various states and counties, the company chose to settle in Bibb County due to a good fit with MöllerTech's culture centered on people and local communities.
Financial and personal support from the state and county helped influence the decision to locate in Bibb County.
The Birmingham region competed for the project, which is codenamed “Helios,” against other sites across the Southeast.

The Bibb County Commission, Alabama Department of Commerce, Economic Development Partnership of Alabama, Alabama Power Co., and Birmingham Business Alliance collaborated to bring MöllerTech to the region.
Greg Canfield, secretary of the Alabama Department of Commerce, joined company leaders and local officials at an announcement ceremony at the Bibb County Board of Education.
Bibb County provided 20 acres of land in the Scott G. Davis Industrial Park for the facility.
Bibb County provided an abatement of non-educational sales and use taxes and property taxes.
The state provided jobs and investment credits plus AIDT workforce training, all valued at $12.3 million over a 15-year period.
Bibb County Commissioner Ricky Hubbard stated the project will be a great boost for the county and its workforce.
MöllerTech will become the second largest manufacturing employer in Bibb County and the third largest overall employer.
MöllerTech originated as a family copper trading and processing business in 1730.
It represents the 18th German corporate investment in the seven-county Birmingham metro area, according to the Birmingham Business Alliance.
These 18 companies will employ approximately 1,700 people once MöllerTech is operational.
Brian Hilson, president and CEO of the BBA, expressed excitement about the project's impact on the local workforce and families.
MöllerTech is the automotive arm of Germany-based MöllerGroup, which remains family-owned with 2,300 employees worldwide.
Besides Mercedes-Benz, MöllerTech develops and produces interior parts for automakers including Audi, BMW, General Motors, Honda, and Toyota.
The company has existing U.S. facilities in Elberton, Georgia, and Shelby Township, Michigan.
The Alabama facility is located just six miles from the Mercedes plant in Vance.
The company hopes to reach full production at the Alabama facility by the end of 2019.
source_title: MöllerTech plans $46M Alabama auto parts plant with 222 jobs - Made in Alabama

The design team was challenged to produce budget-friendly and easily constructible details with typical materials but express the owner’s intent for a modern, premium-designed aesthetic.
The building features floor-to-ceiling glazing, canted recesses, highlighted material usage, and specifically placed accent colors.

facts: Nidec Corporation is a Japanese manufacturer and distributor of electric motors.
Its products are found in automobiles, hard-disk drives, electric appliances, commercial equipment, and manufacturing equipment.
Automotive products account for 22% of the company's sales.
Hard-disk drive motors account for 16% of sales.
The company is the global market leader for spindle motors that power hard-disk drives.
As of 2017, Nidec operates 296 subsidiary companies located across Japan, Asia, Europe, and the Americas.
Consolidated headcount reached 107,062 employees as of March 31, 2017.
In 2006, Nidec established Nidec Motors & Actuators by acquiring the motor and actuator business of French company Valeo S.A.
On January 31, 2017, Nidec acquired Leroy-Somer from Emerson Electric, forming Nidec Leroy-Somer Holding.
On July 31, 2017, Nidec acquired Secop GmbH, a German hermetic compressor manufacturer, from Aurelius Equity Group.
In April 2018, Nidec entered an agreement with Whirlpool Corporation to acquire the refrigeration compressor business of Embraco.
In November 2018, Nidec became a shareholder of Taiwan-based cooling company Nidec Chaun-Choung Technology Corporation, increasing its stake to 67% by December 2020.
Due to antitrust concerns, Nidec sold its Secop business in 2019.
The company changed its name from Nippon Densan Corporation to Nidec Kabushiki gaisha in April 2023.
Shigenobu Nagamori serves as chairman of the board, president, and CEO.
FY 2017 revenue was US$11.15 billion (JPY 1199.3 billion).
Nidec is listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange under ticker TYO: 6594 and is a constituent of the TOPIX 100 index.
source_title: Nidec - Wikipedia

facts: Nidec Elesys Europe, a Japanese company, has recently signed a lease contract for 700 sqm of Class-A office space in Sofia Airport Center, which is part of Revetas Capital portfolio of assets in Bulgaria.
The company was founded in 1973 and is now entering the Bulgarian market for the first time.
Since July 1973, Nidec group with $12.1B turnover in 2021 is designing, manufacturing, and sales motors for the industrial, home appliance and automotive markets.
The company operates in more than 40 countries with over 107,000 employees.
Nidec strategy focuses on capturing four big industrial waves: electrification, robotics, energy saving appliances and drones.
Nidec Elesys Europe will develop inverters for hybrid and full electric vehicles (e-Traction) in the premises rented at the Sofia Airport Center.
The number of experts employed by the company in Bulgaria is expected to reach 100 in 2023.
Nidec Bulgaria is hiring engineers in new major technologies to shape the new e-mobility world.
Eric Assimakopoulos, Founding Partner at Revetas Capital, states that the contract signing indicates a shared vision towards high corporate responsibility and a greener future.
Sofia Airport Center’s LEED Gold certification assures tenants that the complex follows successful eco-friendly practices.
The Sofia Airport Center business complex comprises 47,000 sqm of offices and logistics buildings.

facts: Governor Nathan Deal’s office announced that NIFCO KTW, a German-based automotive supplier of high-quality injection molded components, will locate its U.S. operations in Stephens County.
The company approved a project agreement for “Project Hummingbird” to establish its first North American headquarters.
The facility will be a 130,000 square foot building on 32 acres in the Hayestone-Brady Business Park, located on Highway 17 between Toccoa and Martin.
Over the next five years, the company plans to invest $27 million and create 200 jobs.
Officials stated that more than half of the 200 jobs will be created in the first year of operations.
NIFCO KTW supplies automotive manufacturers around the world, and the Toccoa plant will supply parts to BMW and Mercedes Benz USA.
Stephens County Commission Chair Dennis Bell welcomed the company, stating it is great news for the community and exactly what citizens needed.
Toccoa Mayor Terry Carter stated the addition of the company will invigorate the economy and put Stephens County on the map for future manufacturing prospects.

Mayor Carter credited the state’s strong support and incentives for ensuring the area remained a leading competitor during site selection, and noted local leadership stepped outside customary parameters to secure the project.
The city committed to spending about $300,000 to run water, wastewater, and gas to the company’s building at no cost to NIFCO KTW.
The county provided 25 years of full property tax relief, though the county is not currently collecting tax on the property because it is owned by the Development Authority.
The company is qualifying for state job creation tax credits.
Stephens County Development Authority Chairman Brian Akin called the project a huge win for the local job market and praised local leadership for working through the recruitment process.
Toccoa-Stephens County Chamber Board Chair Greg Roach pledged to work with the company in any capacity possible to help them acclimate to the local area.
Governor Deal expressed that he looks forward to the high-quality jobs NIFCO KTW will bring to the region.
The project agreement required the company to begin constructing its building later in 2015 with completion expected in 2016.
Stephens County Development Authority Executive Director Tim Martin stated the company could come online in the second half of 2016.
The company has plans to expand its Toccoa site in the future, and land is available at Hayestone-Brady for that expansion.
source_title: Hummingbird A Go; German Company Locating in Toccoa - WNEG

facts: The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) announced that Germany-based GEIGER Automotive will open a U.S. manufacturing facility in Gwinnett County.
The new facility will create as many as 120 jobs over the next several years.
To better reach its growing customer base in the Southeast, the company will move into a 48,000-foot facility in Suwanee.
The new facility will manufacture plastic automotive components for engine compartment powertrain applications.
GEIGER produces highly engineered products for cooling and brake systems in relation to air and fluid management, as well as many other functional products under the hood.
The new jobs will be in injection molding and assembly manufacturing, as well as customer support positions.
GDEcD Commissioner Chris Carr stated that GEIGER Automotive’s decision underscores Georgia’s booming auto hub and the growth of German companies in the state.
Georgia’s strategic location to large OEMs, including the Volkswagen Chattanooga Assembly Plant, coupled with a highly skilled workforce, creates an ideal location for GEIGER Automotive.

Ralph Ralston, president of the NAFTA region at GEIGER Automotive, USA, Inc., said the location was strategically selected to optimize supply to current automotive customers in the southeast U.S. and noted increasing interest from automotive OEMs in the region with high growth potential in Gwinnett County.

GEIGER has grown to become one of the leading partners of the automotive industry, with other state-of-the-art production locations in Germany, Mexico, and China manufacturing precision components and subassemblies.
Since June 2014, German automotive companies have invested more than $160 million and created more than 1,400 jobs in Georgia.
Nico Wijnberg, GDEcD international project manager, supported the company on behalf of Georgia, along with Lonnie Smallwood, project manager at Electric Cities of Georgia and Partnership Gwinnett.
Gwinnett Board of Commissioners Chairman Charlotte Nash welcomed GEIGER to the growing international business community and noted Gwinnett’s commitment to supporting international business expansion.
Suwanee Mayor Jimmy Burnette called the facility a great win for the City of Suwanee and expressed confidence that GEIGER will find necessary support and resources in the community.
GEIGER Automotive is headquartered in Murnau, Germany, and supplies primarily German automotive manufacturers with highly engineered plastic components for powertrain applications.
The company operates three production facilities in Germany, one in the USA, and is opening new facilities in Mexico and China in 2015.
GEIGER Automotive employs a workforce of more than 700 people globally.
GEIGER Automotive is wholly owned by Sanoh Industrial Co., Ltd.
source_title: GA: GEIGER Automotive to Create 120 Jobs at New U.S. Mfg Facility | Trade and Industry Development

facts: Gov. Nathan Deal announced that Nisshinbo Automotive Manufacturing, Inc. will create 100 jobs and invest $72 million in an expansion of its Covington facility.
New jobs at the facility will include positions in production and administration.
NAMI is a leading manufacturer and supplier of environmentally friendly brake pads.
NAMI has maintained a presence in Newton County for more than two decades.
Along with NAMI’s business development center in Sterling Heights, Michigan, the Newton County facility produces high-quality friction materials for automotive manufacturers.
The Newton County facility develops environmentally friendly, copper-free brake pads.
NAMI CEO Shigeru Matsuki announced the deal together with the Governor’s Office and the Newton County Industrial Development Authority.
Select Newton Vice President of Economic Development David Bernd noted that more than $70 million in Foreign Direct Investment is being secured for 100 well-paying advanced manufacturing careers.
Georgia Department of Economic Development Project Manager for International Investment Joseph Huntemann represented the Global Commerce Division in partnership with Select Newton.
Japanese-affiliated companies currently operate more than 500 facilities in Georgia that employ approximately 30,000 Georgians.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that NAMI’s success in Georgia is a combination of strong international relationships and a pro-business environment.
Gov. Nathan Deal stated that Georgia provides the business climate, skilled workforce and thriving automotive sector necessary for manufacturers like NAMI to enjoy long-term growth.
NAMI CEO Shigeru Matsuki expressed hope to grow in Covington for the next 20 years and beyond.

facts: Nivel Parts & Manufacturing, North America’s largest independent provider of golf car aftermarket parts and accessories, will create more than 50 jobs in Cairo, Georgia.
The company will invest heavily in its existing facility in Cairo, increasing manufacturing production at the site.
Building improvements and machinery relocation at the Cairo facility began in February 2019.
Nivel has already started hiring new employees for the expansion and will continue hiring through July 2020.

Governor Brian P. Kemp announced the expansion, stating that Nivel is a fantastic partner with the local community in Grady County, offering internships through the local high school and creating job opportunities for students entering the workforce.

Rob Darrow, Director of Change Management at Nivel, stated that Georgia was chosen as a center of excellence for research, design, and manufacturing to fit the company's values of providing high-quality products at competitive prices.
Grady County Commissioner Chairwoman LaFaye Copeland expressed pleasure over Nivel choosing Grady County, noting the county has dedicated citizens eager to work and learn.

The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) Regional Project Manager Tina Herring represented the Global Commerce Division in partnership with the Grady County Joint Development Authority and Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson congratulated those involved, noting that Nivel’s expansion highlights economic development assets in rural communities and golf industry assets, as all three of Nivel’s manufacturing customers are located in Georgia.
Nivel has been involved with the Cairo High School’s College and Career Academy and will continue to support Grady County with internship opportunities.
The company was established in 1968 and is headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida.

Nivel offers a broad product line of over 10,000 items for all golf car models from major OEMs including Club Car, E-Z-GO, and Yamaha, as well as Columbia/Harley Davidson, Cushman, Hyundai, Jacobson, Legend, Melex, Noland, Nordskog, Pargo, Taylor Dunn, and vintage or discontinued golf cars.
Nivel specializes in providing exclusive, hard-to-find, and discontinued parts for the golf car industry.

Nivel Manufacturing produces brake kits, brush guards, bumpers, club protectors, enclosures, lift kits, shifters, tops/assemblies, valances, and windshields for PTVs and UTVs.

Nivel offers tailored solutions on a commercial scale leveraging its unique, U.S. based manufacturing capabilities.

Founded as a small dealership business, Nivel has evolved into one of the largest suppliers in the niche vehicle aftermarket industry.
Its transformation has been driven by organic growth, product innovation, and an acquisition-led expansion strategy that integrated numerous complementary businesses and brands.

facts: OTR Wheel Engineering, Inc. is headquartered in Rome, Georgia, located 1-1/2 hours northwest of Atlanta.
The company operates a global footprint comprising 32 facilities across the U.S., Canada, Mexico, England, Germany, the Czech Republic, China, Thailand, and Sri Lanka.
OTR provides manufacturing, painting, assembly, tire mounting, sequencing, warehousing, subassembly, and foam- and tire-fill services to support OEM localization initiatives.
The company maintains 16 U.S. locations totaling nearly 2 million square feet to support OEMs and distributors with coast-to-coast aftermarket coverage.
OTR manufactures and paints wheels, rims, and components in four U.S. locations: Rome, Georgia; Omaha, Nebraska; Jacksonville, Florida; and Quincy, Illinois.

Additional U.S. facility locations include Chambersburg, Pennsylvania; Timmonsville and Clinton, South Carolina; two locations in Jacksonville, Florida; Louisville, Mississippi; Mansfield, Texas; Hesston and McCook, Kansas; Phoenix, Arizona; Denver, Colorado; and Oconomowoc, Wisconsin.
Several U.S. locations provide subassembly services as a leveraged resource for OEMs to achieve cost savings.
OTR was recognized with Excel Industries Gold Supplier Awards in 2017, 2018, and 2019.
The company received the Honda Challenging Spirit Award for Support and Performance in 2019.
OTR won the Kubota Kaizen Community Award in 2013 and 2014.
OTR Canada is headquartered in Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario, with additional locations in Guelph and Sudbury, Ontario; Saskatoon, Saskatchewan; and Edmonton, Alberta.
All five Canadian facilities produce multi-piece rims and components for earthmoving, surface and underground mining, construction, and specialty vehicle applications.
The Sudbury facility recently installed a state-of-the-art bake oven system with temperature and humidity control and a new priming process to optimize adhesion and durability.
OTR Europe operates facilities in Derby and Suffolk, England; Sande, Germany; and the Czech Republic, providing tire mounting, sequencing, just-in-time supply, and warehousing.
The Suffolk facility manufactures and repairs wheels primarily for agricultural vehicles and provides foam-filling for OEM customers.
OTR opened its Czech Republic facility in 2013 to service Europe, Russia, and eastern European countries.

OTR's China facility is located in Wuxi, two hours northwest of Shanghai, and manufactures multi-piece steel rims, hubs, and components for surface and underground mining, material handling, heavy-duty, and specialty vehicles.
The China facility produces wheels ranging from 10 inches to 63 inches using HSLA grade steel, seamless forgings, sub-arc welding, and precision CNC machining.

OTR's China location operates four paint lines with advanced pretreatment and primer systems, wet and powder coating, and conducts in-process ultrasonic and magnetic particle testing for quality assurance.
The China facility is ISO 9001:2015 certified.
OTR's Thailand facility in Chon Buri manufactures steel wheels primarily for agricultural OEMs using automated construction for high-volume production.
The Thailand location provides value-added services including tire mounting and sequencing, with a video demonstration available online.
OTR's Sri Lanka facility in Ekala manufactures steel rims, wheels, accessories for off-the-road applications ranging from 22 to 49 inches, as well as steel bands.
Manufacturing processes at the Sri Lanka facility feature multiple paint lines using advanced 5-stage wash, wet, and powder coat systems compliant with OEM standards.
Rigorous testing at all facilities measures tensile strength, dimensional accuracy, and compliance with ISO 9001:2015 standards and OEM specifications.
source_title: OTR Wheel Engineering, Inc. - Locations

facts: OTR Wheel Engineering Inc is located at 195 Chatillon Road in Rome, Georgia.
On November 19, 2025, the company welcomed CPR Training School to its facility for on-site emergency response training.
Fifteen dedicated employees earned CPR, First Aid, AED, and Bloodborne Pathogen certifications during the session.
The company specializes in off-the-road (OTR) wheel engineering and services.
OTR Wheel Engineering supports industries including mining, construction, agriculture, and heavy equipment operations.

The training curriculum covered recognizing cardiac emergencies, operating automated external defibrillators, managing bleeding and shock, protecting against bloodborne pathogen exposure, and performing adult, child, and infant CPR.
Leadership's decision to certify 15 employees reflects a commitment to building a workplace safety culture and emergency preparedness.
The on-site training model allowed teams to train together in their actual work environment while accommodating operational scheduling needs.
OSHA guidelines recommend that every workplace include employees trained in first aid and CPR with emergency response capabilities available within 3-4 minutes.
OTR Wheel Engineering serves a vital role in Rome’s industrial landscape.

facts: OTR construction equipment wheels range in size from 12”-28”, in steel and aluminum (for cranes), and are engineered for specific vehicle and machine fitments.

facts: PAI Industries is a manufacturer and distributor of high-quality parts to the heavy-duty truck industry.
PAI Industries has partnered with ECM to bring heat treatment in-house using an ECM NANO vacuum furnace system.
The company operates a 112,000-square-foot manufacturing facility in Suwanee, Georgia.
The facility already contains state-of-the-art machinery and previously relied on outsourced heat treating.
The new in-house system expands PAI’s capabilities to expedite production and increase capacity.
Transitioning from primarily outsourced heat treatment to in-house vacuum technology enables low-pressure carburizing and through-hardening 24/7.
The ECM NANO system features modularity, an external loader, a three-position temper oven, and queuing positions.
Vacuum technology benefits include no IGO, zero CO2 furnace emissions, no fire hazards or open flames, increased repeatability, and improved metallurgical results.
Prior to project acceptance, testing was conducted at the ECM NANO located in the ECM USA Synergy Center in Pleasant Prairie, Wisconsin.

facts: Joint venture NSP Panels and more LLC was founded in the USA.
The joint venture is headquartered in Commerce, Georgia, USA.

Founding partners include Franz Neuhofer (owner and managing director of Austrian family company FN Neuhofer), Marco Seitner (owner and board member of Selit North America Inc.), and David Pritchard (owner and president of Pak-Lite Inc.).
The name NSP Panels and more is derived from the names Neuhofer, Selit North America Inc., and Pak-Lite Inc.
The joint venture was established to introduce functional design in the form of acoustic panels, wall elements, and accessories for floors, walls, and ceilings to the American market.

Franz Neuhofer stated that he is pleased to announce the founding, highlighting a strong partnership among the founders, combined expertise in product and production know-how, and knowledge of the American market.
NSP Panels and more successfully appeared as a company at the Surfaces (TISE) and IBS 2024 trade shows in Las Vegas, USA.
Interest in the acoustic panels at the trade shows was outstanding, with promising conversations held.

facts: Pak-Lite, Inc. was founded in Atlanta, Georgia by Frank Pritchard in 1972 as a manufacturer of protective packaging products.

Pak-Lite employs over 300 dedicated professionals.
Manufacturing capabilities include extrusion lamination to foams and rubber substrates.
The company combines materials to composite form and supplies die-cut parts, components, roll stock, or sheeting.
Finished parts often include pressure-sensitive adhesives and release liners.
Additional capabilities include material selection, prototyping, skiving/slitting, rotary/flatbed die cutting, kiss cutting, spooling, precision parts manufacturing, and assembly.
Pak-Lite is ISO 9001:2015 and IATF 16949 certified.
The company converts foams, rubbers, and nonwoven fabrics for OEMs and Tier 1 automotive industry customers.
Pak-Lite assists customers in designing components to meet demanding specifications.

facts: Pak-Lite, a supplier of underlayment, announced the creation of a new joint venture called NSP Panels and More.

The initiative is a partnership between David Pritchard, owner and president of Pak-Lite Inc. based in Commerce, Ga., Marco Seitner, owner and director of the Board of Selit North America Inc., and Franz Neuhofer, owner and managing director of FN Neuhofer.
NSP Panels and More stands for premium acoustic panels and more.
The company name is a combination of the names Neuhofer, Selit North America Inc., and Pak-Lite Inc.
The joint venture was founded with the aim of establishing functional design in the form of acoustic panels, wall elements, and accessories for floors, walls, and ceilings on the American market.
David Pritchard expressed excitement over the formation of NSP Panels and More LLC, citing a strong partnership combining manufacturing, marketing, and long-standing industry relationships.
Pritchard stated certainty that the new innovative family of products will be a tremendous success and become another American-made success story.
NSP acoustic panels combine a sound absorbing base panel made of 60% recycled PET fibers with real oak veneered MDF slats.
The base panel is said to effectively reduce sound by up to 45% and minimizes noise reverberation.
With a substructure of 2 inches, the sound insulation can increase to 70%, meeting highest quality standards and ensuring durability.
The panels are designed to complement various residential spaces including bedrooms, hallways, waiting rooms, kitchens, study rooms, offices, children’s rooms, living rooms, and playrooms.

facts: PPG recently unveiled its latest innovation for automotive refinish operations, the PPG MIX‘N’SHAKE automated stirring technology.
The MIX‘N’SHAKE system automates one of the final manual steps in the paint preparation process, stirring and delivering consistent, ready-to-spray paint mix in under 90 seconds.
PPG is integrating this technology with PPG’s LINQ digital refinish ecosystem to digitalize and automate every stage of the refinishing process.
The system forms part of a broader push to help repair centers operate faster, in a cleaner way and more profitably.
The technology utilizes a patent-pending notched mixing cup that connects directly to the spray gun, streamlining workflow while minimizing waste and contamination.
MIX‘N’SHAKE replaces the repetitive, manual stirring process with an automated closed system that ensures color consistency, accuracy and superior finish quality.
Its pre-programmed settings are optimized for primers, basecoats and clearcoats, making it compatible with both waterborne and solvent-borne paints.
This flexibility allows body shops to use the system across a wide range of applications without needing multiple setups.
The closed design prevents splashes, spills and cross-contamination, maintaining a safer and more organized workspace.
Users simply insert the cup, select the paint type, and the machine delivers a homogenous mix that’s immediately ready for application.
According to PPG’s internal data, shops can increase productive labor hours by up to 6% by automating paint stirring.
The system can reduce annual manual labor time by as much as 16 working days per year, based on a facility handling around six repair jobs daily.
The technology is expected to reduce consumable waste by up to 10%, cutting costs on items like stirring sticks, strainers and disposable cups.
Improved color uniformity and consistent paint quality reduce rework, saving further time and material.
PPG maintained its full-year 2025 adjusted earnings per share guidance of $7.75 to $8.05.

This outlook factors in continued share gains and internal improvement initiatives, while accounting for current global economic conditions, currency exchange rates and varied demand trends across its regions and business segments.
source_title: PPG Unveils MIX N SHAKE to Streamline Automotive Refinish

facts: Rivian Automotive has applied for a federal loan to enable construction of its electric vehicle factory in Georgia, according to a U.S. Department of Energy filing.
The U.S. Department of Energy has not made a final decision on Rivian's loan application.
The loan filing did not disclose the requested amount or terms.
The Georgia facility is scheduled to begin partial operations in the third quarter of 2027.
The first production capacity block at the Georgia facility is scheduled to reach full operation in 2028.
Rivian did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
Rivian had paused construction of its planned USD 5 billion factory in Georgia.
The construction pause was implemented to prioritize speeding up production of vehicles on the R2 midsize platform and conserving cash.
Rivian aims to save USD 2.25 billion and speed up production by shifting manufacturing of its R2 midsize SUV to its Illinois factory.
The R2 midsize SUV is set to compete with Tesla's Model Y.
Production of the R2 at the Illinois factory is slated to begin in 2026.
Rivian had received USD 827 million in an incentive package from the State of Illinois to expand operations at its Normal facility.
The Illinois Normal facility also manufactures electric delivery vans for Amazon.com.
In 2022, Rivian secured a USD 1.5 billion incentive package from the State of Georgia and local authorities for the Georgia facility.
Rivian lowered its annual production forecast earlier in the day.
Rivian missed third-quarter delivery expectations due to a parts shortage and a slowdown in EV demand.
At the end of June, Rivian had cash and cash equivalents of USD 5.76 billion.
At the end of June, Rivian had long-term debt of USD 5.53 billion.
In June, Volkswagen said it would invest USD 5 billion in Rivian as part of a new joint venture to share EV architecture and software.
source_title: Rivian applies for federal loan to restart Georgia EV factory construction, ETAuto

entity_name: Electric Vehicle (EV) Market and Supply Chain
