facts: Electric vehicles are one of the biggest economic vulnerabilities that Republicans have in the battleground states such as Georgia, Michigan and Arizona.
EV sales in the U.S. reached 325,000 in 2020, accounting for 2.4% of all passenger vehicle sales.
EV sales are projected to rise in 2024 to almost 2.5 million vehicles, or 17.0% of all sales, according to BloombergNEF’s Electric Vehicle Outlook report.
The projected 2024 EV sales volume would exceed the 1.8 million vehicles Toyota Motor Corp. sold in the U.S. in 2022.
Between August 2022 and May 2023, $70 billion of investments were announced in the EV supply chain.
Battleground states Georgia, Michigan, Arizona, Nevada and North Carolina each received billions of dollars in projects during that period.
In Georgia, Rivian Automotive Inc. is building an EV production facility east of Atlanta.
In Georgia, Hyundai Motor Co. is building an EV production facility outside of Savannah.
Each of the Georgia EV production facilities will be capable of producing hundreds of thousands of vehicles annually.
President Biden is expected to conduct hard-hat tours and ribbon-cuttings at these facilities over the next 16 months, touting them as outcomes made possible by his signed legislation.
Donald Trump stated in Georgia that he would scrap support for EVs on his “first day in office” should he win the election.

Florida Governor Ron DeSantis awarded money for electric transit buses and discussed efforts to bolster growing electric vehicle usage, but Florida has not attracted high-profile EV supply chain investments comparable to other states.
Automakers are committing tens of billions of dollars to shift production to EVs and will not change their minds based on the outcome of a presidential election.
China surpassed Japan to become the world’s largest exporter of automobiles in the first quarter.
China’s BYD Co. is competing with Tesla Inc. to become the most important EV company.
China already dominates the EV battery supply chain.
source_title: Opinion: Booming EV market is complicating Republicans message

facts: The US Department of Energy announced a direct loan of up to $6.57 billion ($5.975 billion principal and $592 million capitalized interest) to finance Rivian’s EV factory in Stanton Springs North, near Social Circle, Georgia.
The Georgia plant project was shelved in early March due to an urgent need to reduce costs.
A cash crunch forced Rivian to pause construction of the Georgia plant in March.
Rivian has burned through $19 billion since going public in 2021 and has been unable to meet production and sales targets.
The new Georgia facility is intended to help Rivian boost production capacity for more affordable EV models, as its current R1 vehicle costs $70,000 or more.
Rivian plans to construct the Georgia facility in two phases, each yielding 200,000 units of annual production capacity for a total of 400,000 units annually.
Phase 1 of the Georgia plant is expected to begin production in 2028.
Rivian expects to create approximately 7,500 operations jobs at the Georgia facility through 2030.
The project will also include approximately 2,000 full-time construction jobs utilizing regional talent.
These Georgia positions complement thousands of existing and planned jobs at Rivian's current manufacturing plant in Normal, Illinois.
In June, German automaker Volkswagen provided Rivian with a $5 billion investment via a joint venture to help stem cash losses.
The DOE loan announcement comes ahead of President-elect Trump's expected elimination of the EV tax credit, currently valued at up to $7,500 for new EVs and $4,000 for used ones.
Tesla CEO Elon Musk has publicly supported rolling back EV tax credits, claiming it would bankrupt Rivian's competitors.
Following the announcement, Rivian shares rose 8% in premarket trading to approximately $12.56.
As of the previous Monday's close, Rivian stock was down 50% year-to-date, with roughly 18% of its float shorted (about 135 million shares).
The incoming Trump administration may claw back the DOE loan funds intended for struggling companies like Rivian.
source_title: Biden Throws Struggling Rivian $6 Billion Lifeline For EV Factory  – iftttwall

facts: Rivian will raise nearly $15 billion in debt for an EV plant in Georgia.
Rivian Automotive intends to raise around $15 billion in debt to assist in the construction of an electric car manufacturing facility in Georgia.

According to a November 9 agreement, taxable bonds for the project will be issued by the Georgia Department of Economic Development and the Joint Development Authority of Jasper, Morgan, Newton, and Walton Counties.
Rivian has committed to buying bonds as they are issued.
The company has committed to paying property taxes totaling at least $300 million until 2047.
Property tax payments will go up if the automaker makes more money than its $5 billion investment.
In 2021, Rivian announced its intentions to establish the Georgia factory and stated that it would be operational by 2024.
The new facility will eventually produce 400,000 cars annually and employ over 7,500 people.

facts: Rivian Automotive, Inc. is set to significantly scale its electric vehicle (EV) production capabilities in Georgia.
The strategic move is part of the company’s broader initiative to increase output of more affordable EV models targeting a growing consumer segment.
Rivian is receiving financial backing from the U.S. Department of Energy to support this expansion.
The combined financial backing is expected to accelerate the development of new production lines and infrastructure at its Georgia facility.
The funding will be directed toward research and development efforts focused on reducing vehicle costs, a key factor in making EVs more accessible to a broader audience.
Rivian is leveraging federal and state support to position itself to scale efficiently while maintaining financial stability.
The expanded capacity is intended to meet rising consumer demand and strengthen the company’s competitive position in the EV market.

The Georgia expansion represents a pivotal shift in Rivian’s production strategy from historically focusing on premium EVs such as the R1T and R1S toward a broader product lineup with more attainable price tags.
This transition is expected to drive higher volume sales and diversify the customer base.
Infrastructure development is a central component of the expansion plan, including investments in new assembly lines and battery production capabilities.
Rivian will invest in supply chain enhancements to ensure a steady flow of components and materials.
The company is investing in workforce training and development as part of the expansion.
As part of the expansion, Rivian will increase hiring and provide specialized training to meet the technical demands of EV manufacturing.
The focus on skilled labor underscores the company’s long-term commitment to building a sustainable and scalable production model.
The financial support provided by the Department of Energy and state incentives is a significant enabler for Rivian’s growth ambitions.
These funds reduce the financial burden of capital-intensive expansion and allow the company to allocate more resources toward innovation and market development.
The loan and incentives are structured to support long-term economic growth and job creation in Georgia.
As the expansion progresses, Rivian aims to reinforce its market presence by increasing vehicle availability and improving customer access.
The company’s ability to scale production while maintaining cost efficiency will be key to achieving its strategic objectives.
With federal and state backing, Rivian is well positioned to accelerate its growth trajectory and continue shaping the future of electric mobility.
source_title: Rivian to Expand Georgia Production Capacity by 200% With Federal Support

entity_name: Rivian Electric Car Plant in Walton County
facts: The proposed Rivian electric car plant in Walton County, Georgia, is touted as the state’s largest economic development project ever.
Community members are concerned about the project, with some feeling robbed of their rights as property owners.
Chas Moore, a resident of Rutledge and part of the group Our Communities Oppose Rivian Assembly Plant, stated that locals have not been spoken to by Rivian despite potential partnership benefits.
Only one meeting for homeowners with officials has occurred since the project's December announcement.
The proposed plant will span 20 million square feet, an area large enough to fit almost 10 Mercedes Benz stadiums inside the site.
Locals are concerned the plant site sits on land that refills their groundwater water supply.
Chas Moore noted that residents outside the city of Madison rely entirely on private wells and lack infrastructure to connect to city water in case of emergencies.
The Jasper, Morgan, Newton & Walton Development Authority, a collaborative group formed by members from those four counties, is listed as the developer of the site.

The development authority states groundwater will be protected through storm water runoff management, use of water and sewer service from the Newton County Water & Sewerage Authority, and strict adherence to state and federal soil erosion guidelines.
Rivian would receive the biggest tax break ever offered by the state for this project.
The Georgia government claims the plant will bring in 7,500 jobs.
Chas Moore observed that there is already a substantial amount of unfilled industrial jobs available in the surrounding area.
Rivian's vice president of public policy expressed hope to start construction on the plant this summer.
source_title: Community concerned about proposed Rivian electric car plant in Walton County | KRDO

facts: On December 16, 2021, Gov. Brian Kemp announced that Rivian Automotive would invest $5 billion in a 20 million square foot carbon-conscious campus in Georgia to produce electric adventure vehicles.
Financing for the project is set to come from a bond-funded stimulus package of at least $1.5 billion and up to $15 billion.
The nearly 2,000-acre location of the Rivian complex is known as the East Atlanta Megasite.
The site is represented by the Joint Development Authority (JDA) of Jasper, Morgan, Newton, and Walton counties.
The JDA was formed in 1999 and bought the land, which includes residential areas, forests, and historic farmland.
Non-disclosure and non-disparagement clauses prevented critical landowners in Morgan County from sharing alleged intimidation tactics used to encourage them to sign contracts.
Rivian, the JDA, and other government officials had been in secret talks for months crafting the deal before the December 2021 announcement.

A study by the Northeast Georgia Regional Commission (NEGRC) concluded the Rivian development is generally inconsistent with Morgan County's 2017-2036 Comprehensive Plan prioritizing rural living, conservation, and gradual development for its less than 20,000 residents.
Morgan County's comprehensive plan lists desired land uses to include undeveloped land left in its natural state, agriculture, and low-density, large-lot residential homes.
Once up and running, Rivian's plant is scheduled to produce 400,000 electric vehicles annually.
The facility will generate at least 6,700 tons of toxic, hazardous wastes annually that will need to go to a landfill.
The proposed site contains at least 29 acres of wetlands and an additional 232 acres of wetlands are within a mile of the site.
92 percent of the proposed Rivian site lies within a Conservation area.

On September 29, 2022, Morgan County Superior Court Judge Brenda Trammell denied the $15 billion in bonds, describing the bond structure as a mechanism to obtain tax abatement for a private company for which there is no ability legislatively or constitutionally.

Instead of paying property taxes, the arrangement had Rivian set to make at least $300 million in PILOT payments to the JDA for 25 years by asserting that the company's rental agreement would qualify as a usufruct.
Under the terms of Kemp's agreement, the local JDA would issue bonds that Rivian would purchase, with rent paid by Rivian used to repay the principal and interest due on the bonds.
Judge Trammell ruled that the terms of Rivian's rental agreement did not qualify as a usufruct, leaving the company responsible for standard property taxes.
A December 27, 2022 report by the Center for Economic Accountability (CEA) declared the deal Georgia's Rivian Plant Subsidy Recognized as 2022's Worst Economic Development Deal of the Year.
CEA President John C. Mozena proclaimed that Georgia made a massive, speculative investment of taxpayer money in an early-stage company without doing basic due diligence.
In February 2022, Rivian investor George Soros lost nearly $1 billion after a prolonged plunge in the company's share price.
Amazon invested $1.3 billion in Rivian in 2019 as part of a plan to go green and took a $7.6 billion loss on its shares in April 2022.
In late October 2022, the GDEcD and JDA submitted a notice of appeal to the Georgia Court of Appeals challenging Judge Trammell's ruling, with a calendar date of February 2023.
On December 28, 2022, the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers issued a federal Clean Water Act Section 404 permit allowing the Rivian assembly plant project to move forward on the entire 2,000-acre site.
JoEllen Artz, president of No2Rivian, called the 404 permit devastating and urged Gov. Kemp to pause the Project until the legal system resolves the bond issuance case.

Rivian stated in a statement to the Atlanta Journal Constitution that it remains excited to work with Georgia to bring good paying manufacturing jobs to the state while respecting the natural environment.

Felton Jenkins III, an anti-Rivian member, claimed his family was threatened with land condemnation to sell off property for the project and called the industrialization a violation of Morgan County zoning regulations.
Rivian was founded in 2009 by Robert J. Scaringe as a spinoff of Mainstream Engineering, a Florida-based developer that received significant funding from partnerships with the U.S. government.
In 2017, Rivian purchased a former $100 million Mitsubishi factory in Illinois.

Rivian received $1.5 million from the state-funded Space Florida organization and retained a $2 million grant from the Florida Office of Energy to build a lightweight modular prototype vehicle delivered to NASA in 2012.
In September 2019, Amazon agreed to purchase 100,000 electric vehicles from Rivian, marking the largest order of EV delivery vehicles ever at the time.
In July 2020, Rivian raised $2.5 billion in funding led by existing investors T. Rowe Price, Amazon, and Ford Motor.
Cox Enterprises holds a 4.7 percent stake in Rivian and supplies services to the company.
Sandy Schwartz, a Cox executive who oversees the AJC, is on Rivian's board of directors and holds stock personally.

On December 8, 2022, Gov. Kemp announced that Hyundai Motor Group and SK On selected a site in Bartow County for a new electric vehicle battery manufacturing facility to supply Hyundai plants, wooed by an incentive package valued at more than $1.8 billion.
Gov. Kemp announced that Korean solar panel maker QCells would invest over $2.5 billion in new solar panel plants in Georgia, described as the largest solar investment in U.S. history.
Since 2020, more than 30 EV-related projects have announced more than $21 billion in investment and announced more than 26,300 jobs in Georgia.
source_title: Anti-Rivian Residents Stand Strong as Gov. Kemp Touts GA as Nation’s EV Hub | UncoverDC | uncoverdc.com

facts: An electric vehicle company planning to hit huge production milestone figures could face a roadblock depending on the 2022 Georgia gubernatorial election results.
A planned production plant for Rivian Automotive Inc (NASDAQ:RIVN) in Georgia has become a focal point in the primary battle between two Republican candidates for the state’s gubernatorial race.
Former U.S. Sen. David Perdue is running against incumbent Gov. Brian Kemp for the Republican nomination in Georgia.
Perdue claimed Kemp pledged tax dollars to lure a California company funded by George Soros to Georgia, calling it ‘economic development’.
Perdue stated the factory announcement was done by Kemp to promote himself in an election year.
Perdue asserted that real economic development should be straightforward and organic, with local community and leaders involved in the process.
Perdue claimed many Georgia residents are against Rivian’s announced electric vehicle production factory in the state.
Perdue accused Kemp of acting like a career politician who is ignoring locals’ concerns and cutting them out of the process.
Perdue said he will prioritize growing Georgia jobs without the George Soros connection if elected governor.
Soros Fund Management is an investor in Rivian and owns around 2% of the company’s shares, making it around the 10th-largest shareholder.
Amazon.com Inc (NASDAQ:AMZN) and Ford Motor Company (NYSE:F) are also investors in Rivian, with larger stakes than Soros.
A Kemp campaign spokesperson said the governor planned to make comments Tuesday regarding Perdue’s accusations.
Rivian announced plans for its second plant in Georgia, consisting of nearly 20 million square feet of space on a 1,978-acre piece of land.
The Georgia plant will include production, onsite vehicle delivery, an event center, training facility, and a charging station with 144 stalls.
Rivian was making around 50 units a week in December.
Rivian has plans to increase its annual capacity to 200,000 vehicles.
The Georgia plant would add an annual capacity of 400,000 vehicles.
Rivian plans to eventually have four assembly plants worldwide.
Rivian aims to produce 1 million cars annually by 2030.
Rivian produced 1,015 electric vehicles in 2021 and reported 920 deliveries, falling short of original company estimates.
The winner of the GOP gubernatorial primary in Georgia is expected to face off against Democratic candidate Stacey Abrams.
In the 2018 election, Kemp narrowly defeated Abrams with 50.2% of the popular vote versus 48.8%.
Abrams has previously voiced support for the Rivian plant.
Rivian could soon find itself rooting for a matchup of Kemp vs. Abrams for Georgia governor.
Rivian shares are down 8.98% to $61.49 Tuesday afternoon.
Rivian shares have traded between $50 and $179.46 since going public at an IPO price of $78 in November 2021.
source_title: How Georgia's Gubernatorial Race Could Impact Rivian, Its Factory Plans - Rivian Automotive (NASDAQ:RIVN) - Benzinga

facts: Alexander Perry has resumed coverage of major North American automakers at BofA Securities as Head of North American Autos Research.
Perry replaces Federico Merendi, who returned to Wolfe Research after three and a half years with Bank of America’s equity research team.
The analyst has taken a generally more bullish stance on auto stocks than predecessors Federico Merendi and John Murphy.
In a new research note published on Wednesday, the analyst set a $17 price target on Ford Motor Co. stock with a Buy rating.
The target implies an upside potential of 33.9% on Ford’s shares based on Tuesday’s closing price of $12.70.
Ford’s stock has jumped 31.6% in the last 12 months despite a 12.0% decline recorded in the past seven days.
BofA’s valuation of Ford is based on 3.8 times its adjusted EBITDA estimates for 2027.

The analyst stated that Ford is positioned well to capitalize on the significant shift in the regulatory backdrop under the current administration, which should enable it to shift focus to its most margin accretive trucks/SUVs.
Ford stopped production of the fully electric Ford F-150 Lightning late last year due to low interest for high-end EVs.
CEO Jim Farley cited low interest for high-end EVs as the reason for stopping F-150 Lightning production.
The company is shifting focus towards internal combustion engine and hybrid models while preparing to launch a more affordable electric pickup truck under its Universal EV Platform.
In December, Ford reported a loss of $19.5 billion as it restructured its EV business.
Ford expects its ‘Model e’ business to become profitable by 2029.
The company announced that its EV unit lost $4.8 billion in 2025 and was expected to lose $4 billion to $4.5 billion in 2026.
UBS analyst Joseph Spak called Ford’s $20 billion write-down a “bold action.”
BofA’s Alexander Perry sees near-term benefits as Model E losses abate and the company’s high-margin commercial business stabilizes.
The analyst expects Ford to make progress toward its 8% EBIT margin guide, including a large step-up in 2027.
Alexander Perry assumed coverage of Tesla with a Buy rating and $460 price target on the stock.
In a research note published on October 29, 2025, Federico Merendi had reiterated a Neutral rating on Tesla’s stock.
Tesla closed 2.7% down at $392.43 on Tuesday’s trading session, implying a 17.2% upside potential based on BofA’s updated target.
Tesla’s stock climbed to an all-time high of $498.83 in late December before dropping below $400 in February.
The analyst emphasized the value BofA assigns to Tesla’s humanoid robotics and energy businesses.
Perry valued Tesla’s Optimus humanoid segment at over $30 billion, representing 2% of the company’s valuation.
Optimus will likely first be used in manufacturing and possibly replace a portion of the 13 million US manufacturing jobs, with future adoption in households.
Elon Musk said it’s “probably true” that people will forget Tesla ever built cars after launching the Optimus V3, scheduled for later this year.
The humanoid robot will be built at GigaTexas on manufacturing lines formerly dedicated to Model S and Model X production.
Bank of America values Tesla’s energy business at $90 billion, representing 6% of the company’s valuation.
BofA expects Tesla to have leading share with its residential Powerwall batteries and Megapacks for utilities and data centers.
Alexander Perry resumed coverage on US EV makers Lucid Motors and Rivian, both headquartered in California.
The analyst reiterated an Underperform rating on Lucid Motors with a $10 price target, matching the pre-reverse split basis set in August last year.
Lucid’s stock has crashed by nearly 99% from its peak five years ago and hit a new record low of $8.90 immediately after earnings release, equivalent to $0.89 pre-reverse split.
Based on Tuesday’s closing price of $10.11, BofA’s price target implies a 1% decline on Lucid’s stock.
Federico Merendi maintained a $10 price target on Rivian with an Underperform rating last October.
Rivian’s stock climbed to a 2025 high of $22.45 on December 22 after Autonomy Day before falling by nearly $10 during the first two months of 2026.
Following better-than-expected fourth-quarter results reported on February 12, Rivian’s shares surged more than 15% but later retreated to pre-earnings levels.
The Irvine-based EV maker’s stock closed at $15.10 on Tuesday.
Investors anticipate the launch of the upcoming R2 mid-size SUV, which is expected to increase demand for Rivian’s vehicles.
source_title: BofA Resumes Coverage of Tesla, Rivian, Lucid and Ford With More Upbeat Outlook | EV

facts: Saft has been operating in America for half of its 100-year history.
The company operates five key locations across the US, comprising four manufacturing plants and a distribution center.
Saft produces a range of battery chemistries for customers including the military and energy companies.
In 1954-1957, Saft entered the US market with the ambition of bringing nickel cadmium technology to the US airline industry.
In 1951, Saft began talks with the Bureau of Aeronautics through Hazard Reeves for a license to produce Saft batteries in the US.
After submitting Alcabloc and Voltabloc batteries to the Bureau of Standards for approval, the Bureau of Aeronautics signed a $500,000 contract.
In May 1957, Saft Corporation of America was formed in Lodi, New Jersey as a joint enterprise with Reeves Soundcraft Corporation.
The new company struggled to win against American lead-acid battery competitors and closed in October 1957.
By 1974, Saft determined it needed its own local production site to develop rapidly in the US.
In May 1973, Saft increased its holding in Gulton Industries' aircraft-battery manufacturing division to become the largest stakeholder.
The company was renamed Saft America, with Gulton manufacturing nickel-based prismatic cells for aircraft from a New Jersey facility.
In 1975, Saft America relocated operations to a brand-new plant in Valdosta, Georgia, producing portable cells for the commercial market and prismatic cells for aviation.
The Valdosta facility remains operational today, with some original staff members still working there more than 40 years later.
In 1978, Saft purchased SCORE, a small company in Cockeysville, Maryland that made thermal batteries for the space and defense market.
The SCORE acquisition provided Saft with lithium production facilities that became increasingly important in later years.
Within two years of the acquisition, Saft made the previously loss-making business profitable.
In 1983, Saft purchased Gould National Batteries, which operated facilities in St Paul, Minnesota and Tijuana, Mexico.
A couple of years after the Gould deal, Saft relocated the Minnesota facility to Valdosta while retaining the Tijuana site for assembly and warehousing.
The Tijuana site closed in June 2001, with remaining production moved to France.
In the late 1980s, Saft purchased Duracell’s business-to-business operations in Valdese, North Carolina.
The Duracell deal expanded Saft's lithium sulfur dioxide (Li-SO2) capabilities and added a new US site.
During the 1980s, the US Army was the world’s largest consumer of lithium-based batteries, mostly for radio applications.
Saft decided to manufacture Li-SO2 in the US because the market was about 10 times larger than in Europe.
After the Duracell deal, Valdese became the center for both Li-SO2 and Li-SOCI2 production, with Cockeysville's capability relocated there.
At around the same time, Saft America purchased Catalyst Research, which brought lithium-anode technology to its Cockeysville portfolio.
In 1991, Operation Desert Storm created massive demand for batteries for GPS and night-vision applications, leading to a $120m order, the largest in Saft’s history.
To meet the Desert Storm order, the Valdese plant quadrupled in size and grew again a dozen years later with the launch of the second Gulf War.
In 1995, Saft signed a contract to provide the battery for the Lockheed Martin F-22 Raptor (Advanced Tactical Fighter), which had its first flight in 1997 and final delivery in 2012.
The F-22 was the first application for Saft’s Aircraft Maintenance-Free Battery (AMFB).
In 1993, Cockeysville ceased production after Saft stopped making unprofitable thermal cells, becoming an R&D-only facility for the next 10 years.
During the 1990s, Saft developed in-house lithium-ion technology and industrial processes, securing major discoveries and patents.
The Cockeysville R&D team built lithium-ion prototypes quickly, especially for defense applications, making lithium-ion one of Saft’s core technologies.
In 1996, Saft began developing its own electronic battery-control systems because lithium-ion cells could not be used safely without them.

Over the next decade, the Cockeysville team developed complex electrical and software engineering systems ranging from small 28V batteries up to electronics for the Joint Strike Fighter 270V battery qualified to aviation standards.

In 1991, Saft bought NIFE in Greenville, North Carolina, along with the worldwide NIFE group, transferring pocket-plate cell and battery assembly, industrial primary battery recycling, and bring-back recycling for nickel-based batteries to Valdosta.
In 1993, Saft acquired Gates Aerospace Batteries, bringing on Annie Sennet, who later became Saft’s Executive Vice President for Space & Defense and Saft America’s President and CEO.
In 2001, Saft bought Hawker Eternacell, increasing Li-SO2 capabilities and making Saft a major world player in that market.
During the 1990s, the R&D Center performed funded research into molten-salt secondary batteries under contract with the US Advanced Battery Consortium backed by the US Department of Energy.
The molten-salt research did not lead to a rechargeable battery but maintained the capability to build thermal batteries.
Saft established a thermal-battery production operation in Cockeysville as part of a new subsidiary with EADS (now Airbus) called Advanced Thermal Batteries.

In 2011, Saft opened a state-of-the-art facility in Jacksonville, Florida, over 100 miles from the Valdosta plant, to enhance lithium-ion battery production for renewable energy, electric vehicles, and telecom applications.
In 2012, Valdese began producing lithium manganese dioxide (Li-MnO2) cells for new metering applications as demand in the sector grew.
Saft America currently represents 36 percent of the company's total sales and is a major contributor to its overall customer base.
The five US locations employ close to 1,000 people and produce batteries for all of Saft's major markets.

At the US R&D center in Cockeysville, 14 employees work on projects including lithium titanate (LTO) cells for 12V start-stop batteries, Formula One car cells operating at high rate and elevated temperature, and manganese phosphate providing safe and high energy density cells.
source_title: Saft America: half a century in the United States | Saft | Batteries to energize the world

facts: Sejong America, Inc. established Sejong Georgia, LLC in Georgia, U.S.A. to manufacture auto parts for Kia's transplant in Georgia.
The new subsidiary Sejong Georgia, LLC is capitalized at 974 million won, which was fully funded by Sejong America.
The Company will invest 27.8 million USD to establish Sejong Georgia LLC. on the former factory-site of Federal Mogul in LaGrange, Georgia, U.S.A.
The new plant will begin producing automotive muffler and exhaust systems in Oct. 2009 and supply them to Kia Motors' plant in Georgia.
The Georgia facility will become Sejong's second plant in North America, following the Sejong Alabama plant.
The Company acquired the remaining 50 percent equity stake that ArvinMeritor had in ArvinMeritor Sejong, located in Alabama U.S.A.
ArvinMeritor Sejong supplies exhaust systems to Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama (HMMA) and other companies.
Having acquired 100 percent of the equity in the joint-venture company, Seijong changed the name of the Alabama-based company to Sejong Alabama LLC.
The Company conducts research and development activities at its Sejong USA R&D Center in Detroit, Michigan, U.S.A.
Sejong Industrial Co., Ltd. supplies mufflers and catalytic converters for commercial vehicles as a Tier 1 supplier to the Hyundai and Kia Group.

The Company developed exhaust systems for multiple Hyundai and Kia vehicle models including Sonata, Tucson, Grandeur, Santa Fe, Libero, Genesis, Equus, Genesis Coupe, Avante, i30, Successor model to Tucson, Successor model to New Sonata, Semi large sedans, Santa Fe Facelift, Successor model to Avante HD, Strategic vehicles for the European market, Sorento, Grandeur TG, Opirus, Minivan, and Small car.
The Company developed exhaust gas purification catalysts and purifying devices compliant with Euro lV and Euro V emission standards under the G7 project.
The Company developed canning for plasma reaction for exhaust system development promotion projects.
The Company developed exhaust gas after-treatment devices for heavy-duty diesel engine vehicles compliant to Euro V standard under the Eco-Star project.
The Company developed technology to predict product life of automotive exhaust manifolds based on studying thermal fatigue levels.
The Company developed glucose sensors with electrodes made of copper, nickel, and gold.
The Company developed hydrogen gas leak detectors to detect gas leakage in fuel cell vehicles.
The Company developed silencers and flexible tubes for fuel cell vehicles, completing the first phase by Oct. 2009 and the second phase by Oct. 2011.
The Company developed technology to optimize the mounting position of the urea injection device to enhance exhaust purification performance of selective catalytic reduction systems.
The Company developed hydrogen supply systems based on heat and mass transfer characteristics, completing the first phase by Sep. 2011 and the second phase by Sep. 2013.
The Company developed variable valves to enhance engine performance and reduce noise, vibration, and harshness.
The Company developed non fusion joining technology for stainless steel.
The Company developed regenerated energy application systems to improve vehicle fuel efficiency.
The Company developed low-cost variable valves.
The Company developed low-cost hydrogen supply devices for fuel cell vehicles.
The Company developed sensor systems for exhaust aftertreatment technology.
The Company developed exhaust gas recirculation systems and exhaust gas purification systems for global automakers.
The Company is developing blower motors for rechargeable battery cooling.
The Company developed 6 degrees of freedom (DOF) load cells for exhaust systems.
The Company is studying electric pulse forming of tailor welded blanks aimed at developing lightweight and high-strength resonators.
The Company developed hydrogen sensors.
The Company is developing catalytic exhaust purification devices.
The Company is developing engine power generating systems for range-extended EVs.
The Company is developing system control units for hybrid power packs and power converters.
The Company is studying thermoelectric generation systems to develop automotive exhaust heat recovery systems.
The Company is developing turbochargers integrated with stainless exhaust manifolds.
The Company is developing injection modules for urea SCR systems used on clean diesel passenger vehicles.
The Company is developing converters that meet the Euro6 standards.
The Company is developing elemental technologies for high-performance green engines.
The Company is developing pumps for fuel-cell systems.
The Company announced it had established Sejong Czech S.R.O to supply auto parts to the local facilities of Hyundai Motor and Kia Motors, investing 3 million dollars in the new subsidiary.
The Company announced the establishment of Sejong Slovakia S.R.O in Slovakia, fully owned by the Company, to supply principally to Kia Motors.
The Company established a subsidiary, Sejong Rus LLC, in Russia in order to supply auto parts to Hyundai Motor's new assembly plant in the country, which is scheduled to launch production in 2010.
Sejong Rus started mass production in January 2011.

In July 2013, the Company established Sichuan Sejong Auto Parts Co., Ltd., a muffler production company, in Sichuan Province, China, capitalized at 1.67 million won with ownership split between SEWOOM (40%), Beijing Sejong Auto Parts (30%), and Yancheng Sejong Auto Parts (30%).

In July 2013, the Company and the Chongqing Qinchuan Industry Group jointly established Chongqing Qinchuan Sejong Auto Parts Co. Ltd., a muffler production company, in Chongqing City, China, capitalized at 2.22 million won with ownership split between Sejong (10%), Taicang Sejong Auto Part (39%), and Chongqing Qinchuan Sejong Auto Parts (51%).
Yancheng Sejong Auto Parts' new plant started operations in October 2011.
Beijing Sejong Auto Parts established its second plant in June 2011.

In April 2011, the Company established Taicang Sejong Auto Parts Co., Ltd., a joint-venture company to manufacture and sell automotive parts in China, capitalized at 852 million won with 50 percent from Beijing Sejong Auto Parts Co., Ltd. and 50 percent from a Chinese company.
In December 2013, the Company established Shanghai SJG R&D Center, a research base in China, capitalized at KRW 173 million, fully invested by Sejong (China) Auto Parts Technology R&D Co., Ltd.
In April 2012, the Company established Sejong (China) Auto Parts Technology R&D Co., Ltd. in Taicang, Jiangsu, China, wholly investing all of the 3,412 million won in capital.
The Company conducted research and development activities also at its Production Technology Research Center established in February 2009.
The Company established an R&D center in Suwon City, Gyeonggi-do in July 2008.
The Company established an R&D center in Ulsan City in June 1995.

Korea's Ministry of Knowledge Economy announced it will invest over the next year or two approximately 155 billion won in several growth areas, including green cars led by Hyundai Mobis, Korea Electric Terminal Co., Ltd., and Daewon Kangup Co., Ltd., with Sejong Industrial Co., Ltd. participating in application studies on renewable energy alongside Halla Climate Control Corp. and Inzi Controls Co., Ltd.

Ulsan City announced that the city will promote development of NOx sensors for clean diesel engines that meet the Euro 6 emissions standards, led by Sejong Industrial Co., Ltd., with an estimated investment of approximately 3,132 million won.

The Ministry of Knowledge Economy of Korea announced it has implemented eight strategic tasks in renewable energy, including commercializing polymer electrolyte fuel cells for transportation vehicles, led by Hyundai Motor with Sejong Industrial Co., Ltd. joining to develop this technology over three years with 14.9 billion won from the ministry and 13.6 billion won from the private sector.

The Company's FY ended Dec. 31, 2013 sales were 1,093,718 million won, with mufflers & catalytic converters accounting for 1,073,985 million won (98.2%) and body parts accounting for 3,978 million won (0.4%).
The Company's FY ended Dec. 31, 2013 operating income was 57,470 million won and net income was 46,464 million won.

In August 2013, the Company announced that its subsidiary SEWOOM will invest approximately 25 billion won to expand production capacity at its Jeongup Plant in Jeollabuk-do, adding 4,160 square meters of building area to be completed in March 2015.
The Company's R&D expenditure for FY ended Dec. 31, 2013 was 26,593 million won, representing 5.7% of sales.

facts: Seohan Auto Georgia, a subsidiary of South Korean automobile parts manufacturer Seohan Group, will construct a new facility in Midway, approximately 35 miles outside Savannah.
Seohan plans to invest $72 million into the development.
The facility will be situated within the Tradeport East Business Center, a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) site.
Production at the facility is scheduled to begin in late 2024.

Alyce Thornhill of the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), in partnership with the Liberty County Industrial Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Quick Start and Georgia Power, negotiated the terms of the project.

facts: Kamtec Auto USA announced it will open its first U.S. manufacturing facility in Auburn, Alabama.
The new operation represents a $9.2 million investment.
The facility will create about 50 jobs over the next three years.
The facility will provide advanced technical components for engine control and electric management in hybrid vehicles.
Kamtec was established in 2001 and is an affiliate of the Seohan Group headquartered in Seoul.
Seohan Group has operated in Auburn since 2007.
Kamtec will initially operate from a leased facility in Auburn.
Kamtec plans to relocate to a newly constructed dedicated manufacturing facility to be built by Seohan Auto USA.
Alabama Secretary of Commerce Ellen McNair welcomed Kamtec to Auburn, expressing confidence that the company will find the skilled workforce and ready community partners needed to be successful.
Ellen McNair stated this is a win for Alabama and an example of how automotive manufacturers in the state create opportunities by capturing follow-up investments from suppliers.
Auburn Mayor Ron Anders thanked Kamtec leadership for selecting Auburn, noting that private investment and quality jobs are essential to the community's long-term economic health.
Mayor Ron Anders noted Kamtec’s commitment to innovation and eco-friendly solutions aligns with Auburn residents’ sustainability values and the engineering expertise at Auburn University.

Kamtec Auto USA President Chang-Yeong Seo stated Auburn’s proximity to Hyundai Motor’s plant in Montgomery and Kia’s plant in West Point, Georgia, will allow the company to manufacture and supply its products locally for the U.S. market.
Kamtec plans to partner with Auburn University to identify talent for technical positions.
Kamtec expressed gratitude for the support of the City of Auburn and State of Alabama, aiming to contribute to balanced, sustainable growth for customers, partners, employees, and communities.

facts: Seohan Auto Georgia held a Grand Opening Ceremony for its new manufacturing facility located in the Tradeport East Business Center in Liberty County.

facts: Seoyon E-HWA announced it will invest $76 million in a new plant near Savannah to manufacture parts for Hyundai's upcoming plant in nearby Ellabell.
The company plans to hire more than 500 workers for the new facility.
The new plant will produce interior and exterior parts, including door trim, headlining, and tailgate trim.
Seoyon E-HWA plans to begin production at the Savannah plant in October 2024.
The company already operates a plant with 630 employees in LaGrange, located near Hyundai's plant in Montgomery, Alabama, and Kia's plant in West Point.
Seoyon E-HWA operates 32 manufacturing units worldwide.
Seoyon E-HWA is the fourth Hyundai supplier to announce plans for a southeast Georgia plant since Hyundai announced a $5.5 billion plant to assemble electric vehicles and batteries in May.
Hyundai's upcoming plant could grow to employ 8,100 workers and is supposed to begin producing vehicles in 2025.
Hyundai Mobis previously announced plans for a $926 million powertrain plant in Richmond Hill that will employ 1,500 people.

Governor Brian Kemp has proclaimed his goal for his second term is to make Georgia the leading state for electric-powered vehicles.

Seoyon E-HWA could qualify for more than $3.1 million in state income tax credits, calculated at $1,250 per job over five years, contingent on workers earning at least $31,300 annually.

facts: Sweden-based bearings maker SKF is set to divest its south-central Pennsylvania-based ring and seal business unit to exit non-core aerospace markets.
The Pennsylvania operation, based in Hanover, will be sold to Carco PRP Group subsidiary PCTI for $220 million.
The Hanover facility makes mechanical seals and rings and had 2023 annual sales of approximately $65 million.
The pending divestment deal was announced Oct. 29 and is expected to close in 1Q25.
SKF is also exploring options to divest its elastomeric device (PED) aerospace operation in Elgin, IL, which it considers non-strategic and smaller than the Hanover unit.
The company stated that the PED exit process is 'proceeding according to plan.'
On Oct. 31, SKF announced it completed the acquisition of the lubrication and flow management business of John Sample Group (JSG).
JSG is a lubrication system and full-service solutions provider with operations in Australia, New Zealand, Indonesia, and Singapore.
Terms for the JSG acquisition were not disclosed, but SKF noted JSG had approximately $51 million in its latest fiscal year sales and employs 85 people.
The JSG business will be integrated into SKF’s existing lubrication management business to strengthen offerings for customers in engineered solutions, heavy industries, and mobile equipment.
On Sept. 17, SKF announced its plan to separate its Automotive business as a standalone entity to become more industrial-focused.
As of an Oct. 31 report update, SKF has 'kick-started' separation planning and formed a dedicated project organization to list the Automotive business on Nasdaq Stockholm by the first half of 2026.
SKF shared its Q3 2024 financial results on Oct. 30, showing annual sales declines in both its Industrial and Automotive units.
The company posted total Q3 sales of $2.22 billion, down 4.4% year-over-year organically, with Industrial organic sales falling 4.6% and Automotive organic sales falling 4.0%.
SKF expects a mid-single-digit organic Q4 sales decline year-over-year, as well as for full-year organic sales.
source_title: SKF Selling Pennsylvania Aerospace Supply Unit for $220M - Modern Distribution Management

facts: SRG Global, Inc. was established in 2009.

Manufacturing processes include injection molding, painting, and plating to ensure meticulous finishes.
SRG Global's core client base includes major automotive manufacturers and suppliers across North America, Europe, and Asia.
The company operates manufacturing facilities in Mexico, Poland, Spain, Germany, Thailand, and China.
Revenue generation is primarily driven through B2B transactions with automotive manufacturers and suppliers, focusing on long-term partnerships.
The company engages in direct sales agreements that provide tailored solutions based on specific client needs.
Transaction structures involve contractual agreements facilitating bulk orders of automotive components to ensure efficient delivery and service.
SRG Global leverages its global manufacturing presence to manage inventory and logistics, aligning with client demands to optimize performance and profitability.

facts: Guardian Industries will invest $1.57 million to expand operations at its Consolidated Glass & Mirror facility in the City of Galax.
The expansion project will create 30 new jobs.
Guardian Industries is the world's largest manufacturer of float glass and fabricated glass products.
Governor Terry McAuliffe announced the investment, noting the company has been an important corporate citizen in Galax since 1979.
Maurice Jones, Virginia Secretary of Commerce and Trade, stated the expansion solidifies Guardian's commitment to the region and praised Galax's business environment and infrastructure.
Consolidated Glass & Mirror Corporation was founded in Galax in 1979 and became a subsidiary of Guardian Industries in 1992.
Guardian is a diversified global manufacturing company headquartered in Auburn Hills, Michigan.
Guardian holds leading positions in float glass, fabricated glass products, and building products for commercial, residential, and automotive applications.
Guardian's automotive trim group, SRG Global Inc., is one of the world’s largest suppliers of advanced, high-value coatings on plastics.
Through its Science & Technology Center, Guardian develops high-performance glass coatings and other advanced products.
Guardian, its subsidiaries, and affiliates employ 17,000 people and operate facilities throughout North America, Europe, South America, Asia, Africa, and the Middle East.
Plant Manager Dennis Carroll stated the company appreciates assistance from the Commonwealth, the City of Galax, and Virginia’s aCorridor in making the project a reality.
The Virginia Economic Development Partnership worked with the City of Galax and Virginia’s aCorridor to secure the project for Virginia.
The Virginia Tobacco Indemnification and Community Revitalization Commission approved $125,000 in Tobacco Region Opportunity Funds to assist Galax with the project.
Funding and services to support employee training activities will be provided through the Virginia Jobs Investment Program.
Galax Mayor C.M. Mitchell said helping local industry succeed is the surest way of revitalizing the economy and praised the 30 new jobs for recognizing citizens' production capabilities.
Senator Bill Carrico, a member of the Virginia Tobacco Indemnification and Community Revitalization Commission, congratulated Consolidated Glass and Mirror on bringing 30 new jobs to Galax.
The announcement highlights Consolidated's capital investment of $1.5 million, which will ultimately benefit the entire Twin County region.

facts: Suhner Manufacturing, Inc. is part of the Suhner corporation and was founded in 1976 as a flexible shaft manufacturer to provide for the automobile sector and industry.

facts: SUHNER Machining offers a comprehensive range of precision spindles that multiply production capacity for mechanical engineering and precision processes.
Spindles are required for drilling, milling, tapping, boring, cutting, and machining ferrous and non-ferrous metals.
Solutions are based on a modular system of feed tables, machine components, and drives designed and manufactured according to customer-specific requirements.
SUHNER MACHINING Spindles machining units are suitable for the production of vehicle types and automotive components, aerospace, and precision mechanics.
SUHNER spindles are characterized by their precision and durability, enabling products that function consistently and reliably under demanding conditions.
The simple handling of SUHNER machining units reduces familiarization times and facilitates day-to-day operation, leading to increased productivity and reduced operating costs.
Many metal components in vehicle construction are in continuous use around the world, whether in engine and gearbox production or in the machining of load-bearing structures.

SUHNER Machining products are known for outstanding quality and reliability, which is essential in automotive production where every component must function precisely to ensure overall performance and safety.

Metal components manufactured by SUHNER are particularly robust and durable, withstanding extreme conditions in engines and transmissions to guarantee a long service life that reduces maintenance costs.
Numerous renowned car manufacturers and their suppliers rely on SUHNER products based on many years of positive experience and adherence to high industry standards.

Specialized spindles play a key role in producing metal components such as airframes, cabin windows, structural parts, seats, and seat rails with a high degree of repeat accuracy for the aviation industry.

SUHNER Spindles machining units are used for the production of precision mechanical components in large quantities and with high quality requirements, including special production machines, sensors, bearings, and measuring devices.
Customized production machines designed for precise motor and transmission components utilize SUHNER spindles to manufacture complex geometries with high precision.
In sensor technology, SUHNER's precision spindles ensure every detail is correctly realized for functionality and reliability.
The manufacture of bearings requires extreme precision, especially when machining small and medium series, which SUHNER spindles provide through precision and repeatability.
For measuring instruments used in quality control and research, component accuracy is critical, and SUHNER's precision engineering ensures these components meet stringent standards.
Spindles are designed to offer high speeds and high precision simultaneously while being robustly built to absorb forces generated during machining and ensure a long service life.

The modular system allows users to combine and configure machining units individually according to specific machine construction requirements, enabling quick adaptation to changing production conditions.
Spindles function as rotating axes used to hold tools or workpieces, transmitting torque and speed for precision machining, especially in drilling, milling, and grinding.
SUHNER spindles are critical components in mechanical engineering, metalworking, automotive, aerospace, electronics, and micro manufacturing industries.
The core strategy focuses on the high flexibility of the SUHNER modular system to enable simultaneous completion of machining operations regardless of workpiece dimensions or number of operations.
SUHNER offers access to a comprehensive support network providing in-depth training, professional service, and support throughout the life of the products.
Marc Tschopp, Head of Engineering at KAWE AG, chose SUHNER because it is synonymous with skill and flexibility.

Alberto Hafner, CEO of Oldani AG, stated that SUHNER fulfilled high expectations in terms of robustness, accuracy, and a motor with a high brake force, enabling the construction of a high-performance system.

facts: Suhner Manufacturing, Inc. is located in Rome, Georgia.
The company introduced a line of standard and custom flexible shafts for rotary power transmission on medical, orthopedic, endoscopic, and laparoscopic instruments.
Torsion stable rotary flexible shafts are designed to provide rotary motion on surgical tools at the focal point for minimally invasive procedures.
The flexible shafts offer torsional strength and minimal deflection with uniformly smooth rotational behavior to ensure proper torsion and tactile feel on the distal end for surgeons.
Shafts are available with options such as passivation, electropolishing, and Teflon coating.
Products are provided as cleaned and assembled units to suit stringent medical procedure applications.
The shafts provide precisely controlled rotary power transmission in limited space.
A current application features a flexible shaft measuring only 0.024” in diameter for endoscopic surgeries.
Other applications include endovascular aneurysm/aortic repair (EVAR) and thoracic endovascular (TEVAR) surgery, stapling devices, orthopedic drills, surgical screwdrivers, and laparoscopic devices.
Shafts are available in clockwise, counterclockwise, and bi-directional motion designs.
Materials include Type 302, 304, 316, and 17-7 stainless steel, as well as high-carbon steel.
Suhner engineering works with OEMs to devise functional solutions for specific instrument, tool, and device applications.
Medical grade rotary flexible shafts are available in diameters from 0.024” to 0.500”.

facts: Delivers precision passive components including ceramic capacitors, inductors, power supplies, and sensors to keep networks running and equipment performing.

A major base station OEM consolidated its passive BOM for a 64T64R Massive MIMO radio unit requiring 1,200+ MLCCs per board, selecting TDK's C2012X7R1C475K capacitor after SEAT simulation confirmed 4.2 uF retained capacitance at operating voltage.
The base station project used 15 TDK inductor part numbers across 8 voltage rails, reducing the supplier count from four to one and cutting procurement lead time from 14 weeks to 6 weeks.

The Lambda HFE2500 rectifier shelf provided 96.2% efficiency at 50% load, reducing annual cooling costs by an estimated $18 per rack compared to the previous 94% efficient platform.

facts: NeoVolta has formed a joint venture to develop a U.S. battery energy storage system manufacturing platform in Pendergrass, Georgia.
The JV is named NeoVolta Power.
The JV partners are NeoVolta, system integrator PotisEdge, and solar PV company Longi.
In November 2025, Longi acquired approximately 62% of PotisEdge, gaining sole control.
The acquisition marks the Chinese solar manufacturer's entry into the energy storage space.
Longi and PotisEdge characterized the deal as a partnership to launch Longi's Energy Storage One-Stop Solution.
The solution is initially implemented in key markets including the UK, Germany, Italy, and Spain.
Also in November 2025, NeoVolta entered into agreements for a US$13 million private placement financing anchored by Infinite Grid Capital.
The financing will be used in part to advance the BESS manufacturing plant in Georgia.
The Georgia facility is built for an initial annual production capacity of 2GWh.
The facility has the potential to scale up to 8GWh.
It is scheduled to start mass production by mid-2026.
The plant will initially concentrate on assembling prismatic-cell battery packs and integrating DC containers.
The plant supports around 89 workers at its steady-state initial capacity.
NeoVolta holds a 60% controlling interest in the NeoVolta Power JV.
PotisEdge holds a 20% ownership interest in the JV.
The remaining 20% of the JV is held by a group of strategic investors providing additional technical and operational support.
Initial production is anticipated to focus more on utility-scale systems.
Commercial and industrial systems are expected to capture an increasing share as demand rises.
The platform is built to accommodate various system configurations and product formats.
The facility will not produce battery cells directly.
NeoVolta did not specify the source of the cells to be assembled.
PotisEdge does not manufacture battery cells.
Longi, as of Q3 2025, was unprofitable.

Under FEOC rules, U.S. downstream projects or upstream manufacturing cannot receive material support from prohibited foreign entities above specific thresholds if they wish to qualify for tax credits.
